judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a well-established prognostic factor for treatment response across oncology, including AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients flagged as unfit_for_intensive=1 have a different objective_response rate than fit patients.
judgments.rationale: Fitness assessment guiding intensive vs non-intensive therapy and its impact on response is standard in AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Secondary AML (secondary_aml=1) is associated with lower objective_response than de novo AML.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML is a well-recognized adverse prognostic factor with lower response rates documented in ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have higher objective_response rate than npm1_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable response/prognostic marker is established in ELN risk stratification and AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have lower objective_response rate than tp53_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation is a well-established adverse prognostic marker associated with poor response in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Complex karyotype (complex_karyotype=1) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a classic adverse-risk feature in ELN AML risk stratification with known lower response rates.

judgments.text: FLT3-ITD (flt3_itd=1) is associated with a different objective_response rate than flt3_itd=0.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD's impact on AML response and prognosis is a guideline-level established association.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age as a predictor of lower response in AML is standard knowledge in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher marrow blast percentage (blast_pct_marrow) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast burden's association with response is a textbook-level AML association.

judgments.text: Higher white blood cell count (wbc_k_per_ul) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis/high WBC as an adverse factor in AML response is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic marker correlating with treatment response is well-established across oncology including AML.

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor for treatment response is standard oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher C-reactive protein (crp_mg_l) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of inflammation predicting worse outcomes is well-established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a negative prognostic marker is a textbook association in AML and many cancers.

judgments.text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: NLR as a negative prognostic biomarker is widely recognized in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Routine chemistry panel variables (creatinine_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl) are not associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: The null association of routine chemistry panel values with response is the expected default and aligns with clinical consensus.

judgments.text: Treatment with venetoclax+azacitidine (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1) is associated with higher objective_response than not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax+azacitidine improving response rates in AML is the established standard-of-care finding from VIALE-A.

judgments.text: Treatment with 7+3 induction (treatment_7plus3=1) is associated with higher objective_response than not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction achieving high response rates is foundational AML treatment knowledge in every guideline.

judgments.text: Each targeted therapy (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) has a positive main effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Each targeted agent improving response in its mutation-defined subgroup (FLT3, IDH1, IDH2) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, the strongest independent predictors of objective_response include ecog_ps (negative), unfit_for_intensive (positive), npm1_mutation (positive), tp53_mutation (negative), complex_karyotype (negative), blast_pct_marrow (negative), wbc_k_per_ul (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), crp_mg_l (negative), and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive).
judgments.rationale: The specific multivariable combination asserting independent effects of CRP, weight loss, and albumin alongside genomic/treatment variables after adjustment is a cross-pathway claim beyond standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Midostaurin's effect on objective_response is more favorable in flt3_itd=1 patients than in flt3_itd=0 patients (clinically expected).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is established standard-of-care from RATIFY and appears in NCCN/ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: Gilteritinib's effect on objective_response is more favorable in flt3_itd=1 than in flt3_itd=0.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's preferential benefit in FLT3-mutated AML is the basis of its approval (ADMIRAL trial) and is guideline-standard.

judgments.text: Ivosidenib's effect on objective_response is more favorable in idh1_mutation=1 than in idh1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib is approved specifically for IDH1-mutant AML; differential benefit by IDH1 status is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Enasidenib's effect on objective_response is more favorable in idh2_mutation=1 than in idh2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is approved specifically for IDH2-mutant AML; differential benefit by IDH2 status is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The benefit of venetoclax+azacitidine on objective_response is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients (positive treatment-by-fitness interaction).
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza is guideline-recommended specifically for patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy (VIALE-A population); this is the established indication.

judgments.text: Within ven/aza-treated patients, tp53_mutation=1 abrogates the response benefit (negative ven/aza-by-tp53 interaction).
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as a poor prognostic/predictive marker that attenuates ven/aza benefit is well-recognized in current ELN/NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ven/aza-treated patients, npm1_mutation=1 dramatically amplifies the response benefit (positive ven/aza-by-npm1 interaction).
judgments.rationale: Strong ven/aza response in NPM1-mutant AML is established in VIALE-A subgroup analyses and reflected in current guideline commentary.

judgments.text: The ven/aza response benefit is concentrated in patients who are BOTH npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1; in either-or-neither subgroups the benefit is small or absent.
judgments.rationale: A specific multiplicative NPM1×unfitness interaction restricting ven/aza benefit to the joint subgroup is not a guideline claim.

judgments.text: Among npm1+/unfit+ patients on ven/aza, tp53_mutation=1 still suppresses the response benefit; the ven/aza effect is therefore concentrated in npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- patients.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional interaction (NPM1+ AND unfit AND TP53-wt) defining the ven/aza-responsive subset goes beyond guideline-level statements.

judgments.text: Within npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- patients on ven/aza, complex_karyotype=1 also suppresses the response benefit; the ven/aza-responsive subgroup is npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- AND complex_karyotype-.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conditional subgroup (NPM1+ AND unfit AND TP53-wt AND non-complex karyotype) as the ven/aza-responsive niche is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: The ven/aza-responsive subgroup is fully defined as npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; within this subgroup ven/aza dramatically increases objective_response, while outside it the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated AML without TP53 mutation or complex karyotype responding to venetoclax/azacitidine in unfit patients is well-established in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Each of the four predicates (npm1+, unfit+, tp53-, ck-) is necessary; relaxing any one of them substantially reduces the ven/aza response rate.
judgments.rationale: The strict necessity of all four predicates simultaneously, with substantial response loss when any one is relaxed, is a specific conjunctive claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the ven/aza-responsive subgroup, the ven/aza effect does not depend further on age, sex, ECOG, blasts, WBC, albumin, LDH, CRP, FLT3, IDH1/2, or secondary AML status (no remaining heterogeneity).
judgments.rationale: Claiming no residual heterogeneity from FLT3, IDH1/2, secondary AML, or other clinical covariates within this subgroup contradicts known modifiers and isn't a guideline statement.

judgments.text: Outside the ven/aza-responsive subgroup, no other treatment (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) shows a measurable benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that targeted agents like gilteritinib (FLT3), ivosidenib (IDH1), and enasidenib (IDH2) show no benefit outside this subgroup contradicts established mutation-targeted efficacy.

judgments.text: 7+3 induction has a positive effect on objective_response specifically in fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) patients.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction benefiting fit AML patients is the standard guideline-based paradigm.

judgments.text: Within the ven/aza-responsive subgroup, adding 7+3 does not further increase objective_response beyond ven/aza alone.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no incremental benefit from adding intensive induction to ven/aza in this responsive subgroup is a specific comparative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Continuous biomarkers (age, WBC, blasts, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin) do not meaningfully modify the ven/aza treatment effect after the four-predicate subgroup is accounted for.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no continuous biomarker modification of ven/aza effect beyond the four predicates is a specific heterogeneity claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: An exhaustive small-multifeature subgroup search of treatment-effect heterogeneity across all six treatments will not reveal another subgroup with a treatment-response benefit comparable to the ven/aza subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim about exhaustive subgroup search finding no comparable treatment-responsive subgroup is a speculative meta-claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Enasidenib has a sex-dependent effect: in sex_female=1 patients, enasidenib increases objective_response, while in male patients it does not (or trends downward).
judgments.rationale: Sex-dependent efficacy of enasidenib in female IDH2-mutant AML patients is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: The four-predicate ven/aza subgroup (npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- AND CK-) survives multivariable adjustment for all baseline covariates; the interaction is not explained by confounding with other prognostic features.
judgments.rationale: The claim that this specific four-predicate interaction survives full multivariable adjustment without confounding is a specific causal-inference claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Outside the ven/aza-responsive subgroup, npm1_mutation=1 is still associated with higher response, but the absolute increment is much smaller (favorable prognostic main effect that is amplified by ven/aza).
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is a well-established favorable prognostic marker in AML and its enhanced response to venetoclax/azacitidine is recognized in current AML guidelines and reviews.

judgments.text: tp53_mutation=1 has an unfavorable effect on response that is independent of treatment (true prognostic), whereas the npm1 favorable effect is treatment-dependent (predictive of ven/aza response).
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as an adverse prognostic factor independent of therapy and NPM1 as a predictor of ven/aza response are both established consensus findings in AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: Treatment with venetoclax+azacitidine (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1) increases objective_response substantially (absolute risk increase ~+0.63, ven=1 rate 0.79 vs ven=0 rate 0.16) ONLY in patients meeting all four predicates: npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0. In all other patients ven/aza has no measurable effect on objective_response. No comparable treatment-effect subgroup exists for any other treatment in the dataset.
judgments.rationale: The specific four-way conjunctive subgroup (NPM1+ AND unfit AND TP53-wt AND non-complex karyotype) defining a near-exclusive ven/aza benefit window with no effect elsewhere is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative association).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a well-established prognostic association across oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with npm1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable prognostic marker associated with higher response rates in AML is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with tp53_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as an adverse prognostic factor with lower response rates in AML is established in ELN/NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse-risk feature with lower response rates is a textbook AML risk stratification element.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage correlating with worse response is a long-recognized standard prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor performance/nutrition predicting worse outcomes is a well-known general oncology association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 have a higher overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine producing higher response rates than other regimens is established from VIALE-A and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_7plus3=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction's response rate profile is foundational AML guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's effect on response (in FLT3-mutated AML with chemotherapy) is established guideline content from RATIFY.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's effect on response in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML is established guideline knowledge from ADMIRAL.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's activity in AML is established and guideline-recognized, particularly in IDH1-mutant disease.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib=1 have a different overall probability of objective_response than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is an established IDH2-targeted therapy for relapsed/refractory IDH2-mutant AML and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin increases probability of objective_response relative to flt3_itd=1 patients not receiving midostaurin (positive interaction treatment_midostaurin x flt3_itd).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is a guideline-standard association (RATIFY trial).

judgments.text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_gilteritinib increases probability of objective_response relative to flt3_itd=1 patients not receiving gilteritinib (positive interaction treatment_gilteritinib x flt3_itd).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib benefit in FLT3-mutated (including ITD) AML is guideline-standard (ADMIRAL trial).

judgments.text: Among patients with idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib increases probability of objective_response relative to idh1_mutation=1 patients not receiving ivosidenib (positive interaction treatment_ivosidenib x idh1_mutation).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's enhanced activity in IDH1-mutant AML is the textbook indication.

judgments.text: Among patients with idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib increases probability of objective_response relative to idh2_mutation=1 patients not receiving enasidenib (positive interaction treatment_enasidenib x idh2_mutation).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's enhanced activity in IDH2-mutant AML is the textbook indication.

judgments.text: Across all 6 treatments x 29 candidate effect modifiers, at least one treatment-by-feature interaction surpasses Bonferroni significance for predicting objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A global Bonferroni-significant interaction claim across the full treatment-by-modifier grid is a speculative discovery claim, not guideline content.

judgments.text: Among patients with npm1_mutation=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine markedly increases probability of objective_response relative to npm1_mutation=1 patients not receiving venetoclax_azacitidine (positive interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation).
judgments.rationale: While venetoclax/azacitidine works in NPM1-mutant AML, a markedly amplified NPM1-specific interaction effect beyond the general unfit-AML indication is a subgroup-conditional claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients with unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine markedly increases probability of objective_response relative to unfit_for_intensive=1 patients not receiving venetoclax_azacitidine (positive interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x unfit_for_intensive).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is the guideline standard for unfit/older AML patients (VIALE-A).

judgments.text: In patients with npm1_mutation=0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has essentially no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming essentially no venetoclax/azacitidine effect in NPM1-wildtype patients contradicts guideline use across unfit AML broadly and is a speculative subgroup-restriction claim.

judgments.text: In patients with unfit_for_intensive=0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has essentially no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lack of VEN+AZA benefit in fit patients reflects the established guideline-based restriction of this regimen to unfit/older AML.

judgments.text: Among patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, the increase in probability of objective_response is reduced or abolished in patients with tp53_mutation=1 compared with tp53_mutation=0 (negative interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x tp53_mutation).
judgments.rationale: Reduced VEN+AZA response in TP53-mutated AML is well established and reflected in current AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, the increase in probability of objective_response is reduced or abolished in patients with complex_karyotype=1 compared with complex_karyotype=0 (negative interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x complex_karyotype).
judgments.rationale: A specific negative interaction between VEN+AZA and complex karyotype independent of TP53 is not standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: VEN+AZA increases objective_response only in patients meeting BOTH npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1; outside this joint subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Restricting VEN+AZA benefit exclusively to the joint NPM1+/unfit subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: VEN+AZA increases objective_response in the joint subgroup defined by npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this joint subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A four-way joint subgroup (NPM1+/unfit/TP53wt/non-complex) as the sole responder population is a speculative composite not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, lab values (WBC, blasts, albumin, LDH) and other mutations, the indicator (npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) still strongly modifies the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Persistence of this multi-feature interaction after adjustment for major covariates is a mechanistic cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_enasidenib increases probability of objective_response in female patients (sex_female=1) but not in male patients (positive interaction treatment_enasidenib x sex_female).
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific efficacy of enasidenib is not an established guideline finding for IDH2-mutant AML.

judgments.text: The treatment_enasidenib x sex_female positive interaction on objective_response persists after adjustment for major prognostic covariates (age, ecog, NPM1, TP53, IDH2, complex karyotype, lab values).
judgments.rationale: An adjusted sex-by-enasidenib interaction is a speculative subgroup claim absent from current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_7plus3 reduces probability of objective_response in patients with complex_karyotype=1 (negative interaction treatment_7plus3 x complex_karyotype).
judgments.rationale: Reduced response to 7+3 induction in complex karyotype AML is well-established adverse-risk biology in guidelines.

judgments.text: After exhaustive interaction screening, no clinically large (absolute risk-difference >0.05) treatment-by-feature interaction exists for treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, or treatment_ivosidenib on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim asserting no meaningful interactions exist for these targeted agents is a speculative cross-pathway statement beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Among all combinations of binary patient features (size 1-10) used to define subgroups, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is maximized by subgroups containing the predicates npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0 and complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: While venetoclax-azacitidine benefit in unfit NPM1-mutated AML without TP53/complex karyotype is plausible from known biology, the specific claim that benefit is restricted to this exact 4-predicate intersection (with no effect outside) is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL CONSOLIDATED HYPOTHESIS: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine substantially increases the probability of objective_response (absolute risk difference ~+0.63, ORR 0.79 vs 0.16) only in patients meeting all four of: npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0. Outside this 4-predicate subgroup the treatment has no detectable effect.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines recognize VEN+AZA efficacy in unfit AML and favorable NPM1 biology, but the precise quantitative claim of a ~63pp ORR difference confined to this 4-feature intersection with null effect elsewhere is a speculative heterogeneity claim not in standard references.

judgments.text: FINAL: For treatments other than venetoclax_azacitidine, no large or robust treatment-effect-heterogeneity subgroup exists for objective_response in this cohort. The strongest residual signal is treatment_enasidenib x sex_female=1 (~+2.5 pp boost in females, -2.3 pp in males); modifications by complex_karyotype on treatment_7plus3 are statistically detectable but small in magnitude.
judgments.rationale: A sex-by-enasidenib interaction (female benefit, male harm) for objective response is not an established guideline finding for IDH2-mutant AML and represents a novel cross-pathway/demographic heterogeneity claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: On main effects, ecog_ps, blast_pct_marrow, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, and wbc_k_per_ul all decrease objective_response, while npm1_mutation, albumin_g_dl, and unfit_for_intensive (entirely via VEN+AZA channeling) increase objective_response. Age, sex, secondary_aml, FLT3, IDH1/2, and most lab values have no detectable independent main effect.
judgments.rationale: These main-effect directions (TP53, complex karyotype, high WBC, high blasts, poor ECOG, low albumin as adverse; NPM1 as favorable; channeling of unfit patients to VEN+AZA) reflect well-established AML prognostic and treatment-allocation knowledge found in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Older age (higher age_years) is associated with lower objective_response in this AML cohort.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting worse AML response is a well-established prognostic factor in standard AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female==1) differs in objective_response from male sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in AML response are routinely examined as a baseline demographic variable, not a novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is textbook standard across oncology including AML.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary_aml==1 have lower objective_response than patients with de novo AML.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having worse response than de novo AML is established in ELN/NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive==1 have lower objective_response than fit patients (poor reserve hypothesis).
judgments.rationale: Unfit-for-intensive-therapy patients having poorer responses is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype==1 have lower objective_response than patients without.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse risk factor in AML is core ELN risk stratification.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation==1 have lower objective_response than TP53 wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation conferring poor response in AML is well-established and codified in ELN 2022 adverse risk.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation==1 have higher objective_response than NPM1 wild-type (favorable AML mutation).
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable AML marker is standard ELN risk classification.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_itd==1 have lower objective_response than FLT3-ITD-negative patients (adverse risk).
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD as adverse risk in AML is textbook-level established knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_tkd==1 have lower objective_response than FLT3-TKD-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD mutation status and its response association is a standard examined AML variable in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation==1 differ in objective_response from IDH1 wild-type.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status predicting response (e.g., to ivosidenib or hypomethylating agents) is well-established in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with idh2_mutation==1 differ in objective_response from IDH2 wild-type.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation status predicting response (e.g., to enasidenib) is established standard-of-care knowledge in AML.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response (hyperleukocytosis adverse).
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis as an adverse prognostic factor in AML is a textbook association.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage correlating with worse response/prognosis is standard hematologic oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Lower albumin_g_dl is associated with lower objective_response (poor nutrition / inflammation).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor performance/nutrition predicting worse outcomes is a well-known prognostic factor across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response (high tumor burden).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH reflecting tumor burden and predicting worse response is a guideline-level prognostic marker.

judgments.text: Larger weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response (cachexia/poor reserve).
judgments.rationale: Cachexia and weight loss predicting poor treatment outcomes is established clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response (inflammation).
judgments.rationale: CRP as an inflammation marker associated with worse cancer outcomes is a well-documented prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR as a poor prognostic marker is widely established across multiple cancer types including hematologic malignancies.

judgments.text: Liver/kidney chemistry, electrolytes (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, hemoglobin) have no meaningful association with objective_response after main-effect screening.
judgments.rationale: A negative/null claim that routine chemistries collectively lack association after screening is a non-trivial dataset-specific assertion not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response vs not receiving midostaurin (overall, unstratified).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's overall efficacy in AML is established in guidelines as a standard FLT3-mutant AML treatment.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is a guideline-standard FLT3 inhibitor with established response benefit in AML.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's response activity in IDH1-mutant AML is well-established in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's response activity in IDH2-mutant AML is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is a guideline-standard frontline AML regimen with documented response benefit.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 is the canonical induction regimen with well-documented response rates in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd==1 patients (canonical FLT3-targeted use).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin in FLT3-ITD AML is the canonical RATIFY-trial guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_tkd==1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's approval and guideline indication explicitly include FLT3-TKD mutated AML.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response in flt3_itd==1 patients (single-agent FLT3 inhibitor for relapsed/refractory FLT3+ AML).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib monotherapy for FLT3-ITD relapsed/refractory AML is the guideline-standard ADMIRAL-trial indication.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response in flt3_tkd==1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's FDA label and guideline indication explicitly include FLT3-TKD mutations.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib benefit is concentrated in FLT3-ITD+ patients who are unfit_for_intensive==1.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib benefit in FLT3-ITD+ AML is established standard-of-care, and concentrating benefit in unfit patients reflects guideline-driven use in this group.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response in idh1_mutation==1 patients (canonical IDH1-targeted use).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib for IDH1-mutated AML is canonical FDA-approved targeted therapy in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within IDH1-mutated patients, ivosidenib benefit is concentrated in tp53_mutation==0 / complex_karyotype==0 cytogenetically favorable subset.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning ivosidenib benefit on absence of TP53 mutation and non-complex karyotype within IDH1-mutated patients is a subgroup-specific mechanistic claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response in idh2_mutation==1 patients (canonical IDH2-targeted use).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib for IDH2-mutated AML is the canonical guideline-endorsed targeted indication.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib benefit is concentrated in unfit, IDH2-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is predominantly used in unfit/relapsed IDH2-mutated AML per guidelines, so this restriction reflects standard practice.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a substantially larger effect on objective_response in unfit_for_intensive==1 patients than in fit patients (canonical VIALE-A pattern).
judgments.rationale: VIALE-A established venetoclax/azacitidine as standard for unfit AML, making the larger effect in unfit patients a guideline-level expectation.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger effect on objective_response in npm1_mutation==1 patients than in NPM1 wild-type (NPM1+ is a known V/A super-responder marker).
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a marker of strong response to venetoclax/azacitidine is well-recognized and reflected in guideline discussion.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is attenuated (or abolished) in tp53_mutation==1 patients (TP53 mutation as resistance driver).
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as a driver of resistance to venetoclax/azacitidine is widely documented and guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is attenuated in complex_karyotype==1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Reduced venetoclax/azacitidine efficacy in complex karyotype AML is a well-established adverse-risk pattern noted in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 reduces objective_response in complex_karyotype==1 patients (chemo less effective in adverse cytogenetics).
judgments.rationale: Reduced response to 7+3 chemotherapy in complex karyotype AML is a foundational guideline-level prognostic association.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response does NOT differ between unfit_for_intensive and fit patients (negative-control: there should be no fit-only benefit signal).
judgments.rationale: Negative-control claim that 7+3 lacks a fit-only differential benefit is a methodological null, not a novel biological insight beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive==1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response further when also npm1_mutation==1 (joint subgroup, additive enhancement).
judgments.rationale: VEN/AZA producing high response rates in unfit NPM1-mutated AML is well-established in guidelines (VIALE-A subgroup analyses showing NPM1+ as a favorable responder group).

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive==1, npm1_mutation==1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is further amplified when tp53_mutation==0 (TP53 wild-type required).
judgments.rationale: Requiring TP53 wild-type as a further amplifier on top of NPM1+ for VEN/AZA response goes beyond standard guideline phrasing, which treats TP53 and NPM1 status as independently prognostic rather than as a joint conditional amplifier.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive==1, npm1_mutation==1, tp53_mutation==0, complex_karyotype==0 patients (full joint subgroup), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces objective_response in roughly 4-5x the rate seen off-treatment.
judgments.rationale: A specific 4-5x response multiplier confined to the full four-way joint subgroup (unfit + NPM1+ + TP53wt + no complex karyotype) is a quantitative cross-pathway claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Outside the joint subgroup unfit & NPM1+ & TP53wt & no complex karyotype, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has NO effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming VEN/AZA has zero effect outside this narrow joint subgroup contradicts guideline consensus that VEN/AZA benefits a broader unfit AML population, making it a strong novel speculative claim.

judgments.text: Each of the four predicates (unfit_for_intensive==1, npm1_mutation==1, tp53_mutation==0, complex_karyotype==0) is individually necessary to obtain the maximal V/A response benefit; dropping any one substantially shrinks the absolute effect.
judgments.rationale: Asserting each of four predicates is individually necessary for maximal benefit is a conjunctive mechanistic claim not reflected in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic regression adjusting for all baseline features and treatments simultaneously, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains the only treatment with an independent positive effect on objective_response, while the other five treatments are null.
judgments.rationale: Claiming all five non-VEN/AZA treatments (including targeted FLT3/IDH inhibitors) are null in adjusted analysis contradicts established guideline evidence supporting midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, and enasidenib in their indicated subgroups.

judgments.text: When the V/A interactions with unfit_for_intensive, npm1_mutation, tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype are added to the multivariable model, the V/A main effect becomes negative (i.e., V/A is harmful in the off-target subgroup) while the four interactions remain large and significant.
judgments.rationale: A sign-flipping interaction structure where VEN/AZA becomes harmful outside a four-predicate target subgroup is a speculative cross-pathway claim well beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Systematic 3-way binary subgroup screening (constrained to n_on>=200) identifies treatment-specific subgroups with treatment effects beyond V/A: 7+3 is harmful in complex_karyotype subgroups (esp. complex+FLT3-ITD+NPM1), enasidenib trends positive in NPM1+ female patients, and the targeted FLT3/IDH inhibitors fail to show benefit even in their canonical mutation-defined subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Claiming targeted FLT3/IDH inhibitors fail in their canonical mutation-defined subgroups while 7+3 is actively harmful in complex karyotype + FLT3-ITD + NPM1 contradicts established guideline evidence.

judgments.text: In NPM1-mutated female patients, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response (a sex-modified enasidenib effect not requiring IDH2 mutation).
judgments.rationale: A sex-modified enasidenib benefit in NPM1-mutated patients that does not require IDH2 mutation is a novel cross-pathway subgroup claim absent from guidelines, which restrict enasidenib to IDH2-mutated AML.

judgments.text: The 3-way interaction enasidenib × sex_female × npm1_mutation on objective_response is statistically significant.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction of enasidenib with female sex and NPM1 mutation is not an established paradigm — enasidenib's canonical indication is IDH2-mutant AML, not NPM1/sex-conditional response.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response specifically in patients meeting all four conditions: unfit_for_intensive==1 AND npm1_mutation==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0. Outside this 4-variable subgroup, V/A produces no detectable change in response. The unfavorable predicates that suppress the V/A treatment effect are tp53_mutation==1 and complex_karyotype==1; the necessary favorable predicates are unfit_for_intensive==1 and npm1_mutation==1. This is the dominant heterogeneous treatment effect in the cohort.
judgments.rationale: While venetoclax/azacitidine is guideline-standard for unfit AML and TP53/complex karyotype are known adverse modifiers, the specific 4-way conjunctive subgroup requiring NPM1+ AND TP53-wt AND non-complex karyotype as the sole responsive niche goes beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL secondary: For treatment_7plus3, the treatment-effect heterogeneity is concentrated in adverse cytogenetics — 7+3 reduces objective_response in complex_karyotype==1 patients and is essentially neutral elsewhere. No favorable subgroup in which 7+3 increases response was identified.
judgments.rationale: It is established oncology paradigm that 7+3 induction performs poorly in complex/adverse karyotype AML, so concentration of harm in that subgroup is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: FINAL secondary: For treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, and treatment_enasidenib, no clinically meaningful or statistically significant subgroup of objective_response benefit was identified — including their canonical mutation-targeted subgroups (FLT3-ITD/TKD, IDH1, IDH2). Enasidenib showed a borderline positive signal in NPM1+ females but the three-way interaction was not significant.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines explicitly endorse midostaurin/gilteritinib for FLT3-mutant AML and ivosidenib/enasidenib for IDH1/IDH2-mutant AML, so a claim that none of these targeted agents show benefit in their canonical biomarker-defined subgroups contradicts established paradigm and is non-standard.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's response benefit in FLT3-mutant AML is established in guidelines (RATIFY trial).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutant AML is standard guideline knowledge (ADMIRAL trial).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's response benefit in IDH1-mutant AML is established in NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's response benefit in IDH2-mutant AML is established in NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine response benefit in AML (especially unfit patients) is guideline standard (VIALE-A).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction's high response rate in AML is foundational textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: npm1_mutation=1 patients have a higher objective_response rate than npm1_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable prognostic marker with higher response rates is established in WHO/ELN classifications.

judgments.text: tp53_mutation=1 patients have a lower objective_response rate than tp53_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as an adverse marker with lower response rates is well-established in ELN risk stratification.

judgments.text: complex_karyotype=1 patients have a lower objective_response rate than complex_karyotype=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as adverse-risk with lower response is established ELN guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: unfit_for_intensive=1 patients have a higher objective_response rate than unfit_for_intensive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: That unfit patients have lower (not higher) response to standard therapy is established; the directional claim contradicts paradigm but isn't a novel mechanistic insight.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Poor performance status predicting worse response is a foundational, guideline-level prognostic factor across oncology including AML.

judgments.text: flt3_itd=1 alters objective_response rate vs flt3_itd=0 (test if direction is positive or negative).
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD as a prognostic/predictive marker in AML is well-established in guidelines (ELN, NCCN) and standard textbooks.

judgments.text: secondary_aml=1 patients have a different objective_response rate than secondary_aml=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having inferior response to therapy compared with de novo AML is a textbook prognostic distinction reflected in ELN/NCCN risk stratification.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting worse response in AML is a core, guideline-recognized prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast burden as an adverse prognostic feature is standard knowledge in AML risk assessment.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor nutritional/inflammatory status predicting worse outcomes is a well-known general oncology prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis/high WBC as an adverse prognostic factor in AML is standard, guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse treatment response is a long-established general oncology prognostic principle.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a systemic inflammation marker predicting worse cancer outcomes is widely recognized though not always guideline-specific; still a well-known association.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook association across hematologic malignancies including AML.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hemoglobin as a positive prognostic marker is a standard, well-established association in AML and other malignancies.

judgments.text: In multivariable logistic regression with all 35 baseline features and treatments, the strongest independent predictors of objective_response are ECOG (negative), NPM1 (positive), unfit_for_intensive (positive), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive), weight_loss (negative), blast_pct_marrow (negative), albumin (positive), complex_karyotype (negative), TP53 (negative), CRP (negative), and WBC (negative); other treatments will show no main effect.
judgments.rationale: The specific multivariable independent-predictor set with directions—including the claim that other treatments show no main effect—is a speculative joint claim that would not appear in a guideline.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin has a larger positive effect on objective_response in flt3_itd=1 patients than in flt3_itd=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's preferential benefit in FLT3-ITD/TKD AML is the basis of its approval and is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in flt3_itd=1 patients than in flt3_itd=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is approved specifically for FLT3-mutated relapsed/refractory AML; this interaction is canonical guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in idh1_mutation=1 patients than in idh1_mutation=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib is FDA-approved specifically for IDH1-mutated AML; this interaction is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in idh2_mutation=1 patients than in idh2_mutation=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is FDA-approved specifically for IDH2-mutated AML; this interaction is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in npm1_mutation=1 patients (positive interaction with NPM1).
judgments.rationale: While NPM1 mutation is associated with venetoclax response, an explicit positive interaction (differential effect) with venetoclax-azacitidine is a more nuanced subgroup claim beyond standard guideline phrasing.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients (positive interaction with unfit_for_intensive).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the standard-of-care regimen specifically indicated for patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy; this is core guideline content.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a smaller (or absent) effect on objective_response in tp53_mutation=1 patients (negative interaction with TP53).
judgments.rationale: Although TP53-mutated AML is known to have poor outcomes, the explicit claim of a diminished interaction effect specifically for venetoclax-azacitidine versus other treatments is a more specific subgroup-conditional claim than typical guideline statements.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a smaller (or absent) effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype=1 patients (negative interaction with CK).
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as a specific negative effect-modifier of venetoclax-azacitidine response (rather than a general poor prognostic marker) is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard guideline content.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine confers an exceptionally large positive effect on objective_response specifically in patients who are simultaneously npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 (super-additive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine producing high response rates in NPM1-mutated unfit AML is well-established in guidelines (VIALE-A, NCCN).

judgments.text: Within npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1, the venetoclax_azacitidine effect is further concentrated in patients who lack adverse cytogenetics; i.e. response benefit is largest when ALSO tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: A specific super-additive 4-way conditional benefit requiring simultaneous absence of TP53 mutation AND complex karyotype on top of NPM1+/unfit goes beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: A logistic model with the full 4-way interaction (ven_aza × npm1 × unfit × adverse-marker) will show a single dominant 3-way ven_aza×npm1×unfit positive interaction term, with negative ven_aza×npm1×TP53 and ven_aza×unfit×TP53 (and analogous CK) terms canceling the benefit when adverse markers are present.
judgments.rationale: A specific parametric claim about a dominant 3-way positive interaction with offsetting negative TP53/CK interaction terms is a mechanistic modeling prediction not in any guideline.

judgments.text: An 8-cell table of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × NPM1 × unfit × adverse(TP53|CK) will show responder rate ~78% only in the t=1, n=1, u=1, adverse=0 cell, with all other ven-aza-on cells indistinguishable from their respective off-treatment rates.
judgments.rationale: The quantitative 8-cell prediction that only one specific cell shows ~78% response while all other ven-aza cells equal their controls is a precise speculative claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Systematic 1-feature interaction screens for treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, and treatment_7plus3 against all baseline features will surface no significant biologically-canonical interaction (no FLT3, IDH1, IDH2 effects), and any interaction p<0.05 will be unstable / multiple-testing noise.
judgments.rationale: Predicting that canonical FLT3/IDH1/IDH2 interactions with midostaurin/gilteritinib/ivosidenib/enasidenib will fail to emerge contradicts guideline-established biomarker-drug pairings and is a non-standard meta-claim.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 reduces objective_response in patients with adverse cytogenetics/molecular markers (defined as tp53_mutation=1 OR complex_karyotype=1).
judgments.rationale: Reduced response to 7+3 induction in patients with TP53 mutation or complex karyotype is well-established adverse-risk AML biology in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all baseline features, only treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains a significant predictor of objective_response among the six treatments.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that after full baseline adjustment only ven-aza retains significance among six AML treatments is a dataset-specific comparative claim not derivable from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the ven-aza effect attenuates with older age_years (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Age-attenuation of ven-aza benefit specifically within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup is a subgroup-conditional modifier not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, ven-aza retains a large positive effect across ecog_ps strata 0, 1, and 2.
judgments.rationale: Preservation of ven-aza benefit across ECOG 0-2 within this specific quadruple-conditioned subgroup is a non-guideline subgroup-stability claim.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, no continuous baseline lab feature significantly modifies the ven-aza effect (no ven_aza:lab interaction p<0.05 after Bonferroni).
judgments.rationale: A null claim that no continuous lab modifies ven-aza effect within a narrowly defined molecular subgroup is a speculative dataset-level statement absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: An exhaustive search over fit×NPM1×adverse-marker subgroups will fail to locate any combination where treatment_7plus3 confers a clinically meaningful (>=5pp) response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Lack of 7+3 benefit in any fit/NPM1/adverse-marker subgroup is essentially a null/negative finding consistent with the well-known guideline view that 7+3 is reserved for fit patients without specific predictive subgroup advantages.

judgments.text: Within NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- patients NOT receiving ven-aza, none of the other treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) raise objective_response beyond baseline.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, and 7+3 all fail to improve response specifically within NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- non-ven-aza patients is a narrow subgroup-conditional negative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the ven-aza effect adjusted for other treatments and key covariates (age, ECOG, WBC, blast %, CRP) remains an OR > 15.
judgments.rationale: An adjusted OR>15 for ven-aza specifically within NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- patients is a quantitative subgroup-specific effect estimate beyond standard guideline statements that ven-aza benefits unfit AML patients.

judgments.text: Exhaustive search over all 1-3 binary-feature subgroups (min n=100 each arm) will identify the largest treatment effect as ven-aza in unfit/CK-/NPM1+ (or equivalents involving NPM1+/unfit with adverse marker excluded), with absolute effect >50 percentage points.
judgments.rationale: An exhaustive subgroup search identifying ven-aza in unfit/CK-/NPM1+ with >50pp absolute benefit as the largest treatment effect is a specific cross-feature heterogeneity claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: For patients NOT in the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, ven-aza confers no measurable response benefit (absolute diff < 1 percentage point).
judgments.rationale: Asserting that ven-aza confers essentially zero response benefit outside the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup contradicts the broader guideline view that ven-aza benefits unfit AML more generally.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 reduces objective_response in complex_karyotype=1 patients regardless of TP53 status.
judgments.rationale: Claiming 7+3 actively reduces response in complex karyotype patients independent of TP53 status is a specific harm claim beyond the standard guideline observation that CK AML responds poorly to intensive chemo.

judgments.text: All 4 predicates (npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0) are individually necessary for the ven-aza response benefit; flipping any one alone collapses the effect to ~0.
judgments.rationale: The claim that all four predicates are individually necessary and that flipping any one collapses the ven-aza effect to zero is a strong conjunctive interaction claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: The marginal enasidenib subgroup signal in females / NPM1+ / non-CK is real and independent of ven-aza; i.e. enasidenib confers a positive response effect in this subgroup after adjusting for ven-aza.
judgments.rationale: An independent enasidenib response benefit in females/NPM1+/non-CK after adjusting for ven-aza is a speculative subgroup-specific effect not supported by guidelines (enasidenib is indicated for IDH2-mutant AML, not NPM1).

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS for venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by ~63 percentage points (absolute) in the subgroup defined by npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this subgroup the effect is null. The complete subgroup definition includes the two adverse-marker exclusions (TP53-, CK-) because EITHER marker, when present, suppresses the otherwise-large ven-aza benefit.
judgments.rationale: A precise ~63pp ven-aza benefit confined to the four-way conjunctive subgroup with null effect elsewhere, gated by both TP53 and CK exclusions, is a highly specific heterogeneity claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS for 7+3 on objective_response: treatment_7plus3 reduces objective_response modestly (~1.5-2.3 pp absolute) in patients with complex_karyotype=1 (or more broadly tp53_mutation=1 OR complex_karyotype=1 adverse-genetics group), with no benefit subgroup identified.
judgments.rationale: A quantitative ~1.5-2.3pp absolute reduction in response from 7+3 specifically in CK or TP53/CK adverse-genetics patients with no benefit subgroup is a specific harm-magnitude heterogeneity claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL HYPOTHESIS for the targeted agents (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib): in this dataset, none confers a measurable response benefit in any biologically-plausible subgroup (FLT3-ITD/TKD for FLT3 inhibitors; IDH1 for ivosidenib; IDH2 for enasidenib); their adjusted main effects are null and no robust mutation-by-treatment interaction emerges.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines and textbooks affirm benefit of FLT3 and IDH inhibitors in their biomarker-defined AML subgroups, so a claim that none of these targeted agents confers benefit in any plausible subgroup contradicts established consensus and constitutes a novel (dataset-specific) assertion.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is an established favorable-risk marker in AML guidelines predicting better response to induction and venetoclax-based therapy.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation is a well-established adverse marker for response and survival in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a guideline-defined adverse cytogenetic risk category associated with poor response in AML.

judgments.text: Patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0 (paradoxical association in this cohort).
judgments.rationale: A paradoxical higher response in unfit patients contradicts standard prognostic teaching and would not appear in any guideline.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting lower response is a textbook prognostic association across oncology including AML.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast burden as an adverse predictor of response is standard AML prognostic teaching.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a general adverse prognostic/nutritional marker is well-established across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia predicting worse treatment response is a standard, guideline-level prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP/systemic inflammation predicting worse oncologic outcomes is an established general prognostic paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis is a recognized adverse prognostic factor in AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: age_years is associated with objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Older age is a well-established adverse prognostic factor for response and outcomes in AML and is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: sex_female is associated with objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Sex as a predictor of objective response in AML is not an established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: secondary_aml is associated with objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML is a well-known adverse-risk feature with lower response rates, reflected in ELN risk stratification and guidelines.

judgments.text: flt3_itd is associated with objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD is a canonical AML prognostic/predictive marker discussed in every guideline and textbook.

judgments.text: idh1_mutation or idh2_mutation is associated with objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: IDH1/IDH2 mutations are standard AML biomarkers with established prognostic and therapeutic implications in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response (positive main effect).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the guideline-endorsed standard for unfit AML with well-known response benefit over azacitidine alone.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction is the textbook standard intensive regimen for fit AML patients with established high CR rates.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin added to chemotherapy improves outcomes in FLT3-mutated AML per RATIFY and is standard of care in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML is established in guidelines based on the ADMIRAL trial.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's response activity in IDH1-mutant AML is well-established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's main-effect association with response in IDH2-mutant AML is established and appears in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable logistic adjustment for all 35 features and treatments, the strongest predictors of objective_response are ecog_ps (negative), npm1_mutation (positive), unfit_for_intensive (positive), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), blast_pct_marrow (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), complex_karyotype (negative), and tp53_mutation (negative).
judgments.rationale: These predictors (ECOG, NPM1, TP53, complex karyotype, blasts, albumin, fitness) are standard prognostic/predictive variables documented in AML guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin improves objective_response in patients with flt3_itd=1 (the canonical clinical indication: FLT3 inhibitor in FLT3-mutated AML).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is the canonical guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib improves objective_response in patients with flt3_itd=1.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib efficacy in FLT3-ITD AML is the established label indication and guideline standard.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib improves objective_response in patients with idh1_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib for IDH1-mutated AML is a guideline-endorsed targeted indication.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib improves objective_response in patients with idh2_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib for IDH2-mutated AML is a guideline-endorsed targeted indication.

judgments.text: None of the four targeted agents (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) shows a positive interaction with its canonical target mutation; therefore none of them produces a clinically meaningful improvement in objective_response in this cohort, even within mutation-positive subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Claiming all four canonical targeted agents fail to show benefit even in mutation-positive subgroups contradicts established paradigm and is a non-guideline, dataset-specific assertion.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a substantially larger positive effect on objective_response in patients with npm1_mutation=1 than in patients with npm1_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: A specific positive interaction making venetoclax-azacitidine differentially better in NPM1-mutant patients is a mechanistic subgroup claim beyond standard guideline statements about general efficacy.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a substantially larger positive effect on objective_response in patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 than in fit patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the standard-of-care regimen specifically indicated for unfit/older AML patients per guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is reduced (or eliminated) in patients with tp53_mutation=1 compared to tp53_mutation=0 (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Reduced venetoclax-azacitidine efficacy in TP53-mutated AML is well-documented in guidelines and recent literature as a known resistance pattern.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is reduced (or eliminated) in patients with complex_karyotype=1 compared to complex_karyotype=0 (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Reduced ven/aza response in complex karyotype is well-established in AML literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a markedly higher objective_response specifically in patients who are simultaneously unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1; in patients lacking either feature, the effect is small or absent.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutant AML's high sensitivity to venetoclax/azacitidine in unfit patients is a recognized guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Including a 3-way interaction term tx*unfit*npm1 in a logistic regression yields a large, highly significant positive coefficient, while the 2-way tx*unfit and tx*npm1 main interaction terms become small and non-significant — confirming that the response benefit requires both factors simultaneously.
judgments.rationale: A specific 3-way interaction structure where 2-way terms vanish is a statistical/mechanistic claim absent from any guideline.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a very large objective_response benefit (~60 percentage points absolute) specifically in patients who satisfy ALL of: unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, and complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: A precisely quantified ~60pp benefit in a 4-feature cross-pathway subgroup is far more specific than any guideline claim.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, no continuous laboratory feature (age, WBC, blast %, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, etc.) materially modifies the ven/aza response benefit beyond the four binary modifiers already identified.
judgments.rationale: A negative finding that no continuous lab covariate modifies effect within a defined favorable subgroup is not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a negative interaction with complex_karyotype: in CK+ patients, 7+3 reduces objective_response compared to no 7+3.
judgments.rationale: Poor response of complex-karyotype AML to 7+3 induction is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 improves objective_response in fit patients (unfit_for_intensive=0) compared to no 7+3.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 benefit in fit patients is the textbook indication for intensive induction.

judgments.text: Exhaustive 2-binary-feature stratified subgroup search across all six treatments will reveal the venetoclax_azacitidine effect in unfit AND npm1+ patients as by far the largest treatment effect, with no other treatment showing a comparable subgroup effect.
judgments.rationale: A claim that exhaustive cross-treatment search ranks this specific subgroup effect uniquely largest is a data-driven assertion beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: Exhaustive 3-binary-feature subgroup search for venetoclax_azacitidine will identify unfit_for_intensive=1 & complex_karyotype=0 & npm1_mutation=1 (or equivalently with tp53_mutation=0) as the highest-effect subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Identification of a specific 3-feature interaction subgroup as optimal via exhaustive search is a computational claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Combining all four key modifiers (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) yields an even larger ven/aza response benefit than any 3-feature subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A quantitative claim that adding a 4th modifier exceeds any 3-feature subgroup goes beyond consensus oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Adding a fifth binary feature (sex, secondary_aml, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1, idh2) to the 4-feature ven/aza subgroup does NOT meaningfully refine the treatment effect — i.e., the four-feature definition is parsimonious and complete among the binary modifiers.
judgments.rationale: Claim that a specific 4-feature binary definition is parsimonious and complete relative to other modifiers is a dataset-specific structural claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable ven/aza subgroup, additional targeted treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) do NOT further improve response on top of ven/aza.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that targeted agents like midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, or enasidenib provide no incremental benefit over ven/aza within a favorable subgroup contradicts standard mutation-directed treatment paradigms and is not guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Aside from venetoclax_azacitidine, no other treatment in the cohort produces a clinically meaningful (>10 pp) response benefit in any biomarker- or clinical-feature-defined subgroup with adequate sample size.
judgments.rationale: A sweeping negative claim that no other AML treatment yields >10pp benefit in any biomarker subgroup contradicts established subgroup benefits (e.g., FLT3, IDH1/2, core-binding factor) and is a non-standard, dataset-specific assertion.

judgments.text: For each of the five non-ven/aza treatments, a continuous-feature interaction screen will not reveal a clinically meaningful response-modifying subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A pre-specified negative finding across continuous-feature interaction screens for five treatments is a dataset-level analytic claim not contained in any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately +63 percentage points (78.6% vs 15.8%) specifically in the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this subgroup the average treatment effect is essentially zero. The unfavorable suppressors (tp53_mutation=1 and complex_karyotype=1) each independently abrogate the benefit.
judgments.rationale: A precise quadruple-conditional subgroup (unfit AND NPM1+ AND TP53- AND non-complex karyotype) with a quantified ~+63pp ven/aza effect and claimed independent abrogation by TP53 or complex karyotype goes well beyond guideline-level statements about ven/aza in unfit/NPM1 patients.

judgments.text: FINAL: No other treatment (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) shows a clinically meaningful, statistically robust positive effect on objective_response in this cohort, either as a main effect or in any reasonably-sized subgroup defined by mutations, performance status, fitness, or laboratory features.
judgments.rationale: Blanket denial of any robust effect for midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, or 7+3 in any reasonable subgroup contradicts established mutation-targeted indications and is a non-guideline, dataset-specific claim.

judgments.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have a higher objective_response rate than fit patients in this AML cohort.
judgments.rationale: Unfit patients generally have worse outcomes; a higher response rate in unfit patients would be a finding, but the hypothesis itself reflects no novel mechanism beyond standard fitness-based stratification.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than NPM1 wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is a well-established favorable prognostic marker in AML and appears in standard guidelines (ELN risk stratification).

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than TP53 wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation is a well-known adverse marker associated with poor response in AML per ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than non-complex-karyotype patients.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a standard adverse-risk feature in AML guidelines associated with poor response.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is an established standard-of-care regimen with known higher response rates, especially in unfit AML patients.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving 7+3 induction.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction's efficacy in fit AML patients is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin is guideline-recommended for FLT3-mutated AML with known response benefit.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib is approved and guideline-recommended for IDH1-mutated AML with known response activity.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is approved and guideline-recommended for IDH2-mutated AML with established response activity.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting lower response is a universally established prognostic factor in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Increasing age_years is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
