judgments.rationale: Older age associating with poorer AML treatment response is a well-established paradigm reflected in standard AML guidelines and risk stratification.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor nutritional/inflammatory status predicting worse cancer outcomes is textbook prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia with weight loss predicting worse treatment response and survival is a long-established clinical paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast burden predicting lower response in AML is a standard, guideline-level prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis predicting worse AML outcomes is a well-established prognostic factor in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a systemic inflammation marker associated with poorer cancer treatment response is a broadly recognized prognostic finding.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a different objective_response rate than de novo AML patients.
judgments.rationale: Secondary/therapy-related AML having inferior response compared to de novo AML is established in WHO/ELN classification and treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response rate than FLT3-ITD-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD status affecting AML response is a guideline-level biomarker incorporated into ELN risk stratification and treatment selection.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than IDH1 wild-type.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status as a response-relevant biomarker in AML is established in current treatment guidelines (e.g., ivosidenib eligibility).

judgments.text: Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than IDH2 wild-type.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation status as a prognostic/response factor in AML is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than male patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in AML objective response rate are not a recognized guideline factor.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for all baseline features, the strongest independent positive predictors of objective_response are unfit_for_intensive, npm1_mutation, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, and albumin_g_dl, while ecog_ps, weight_loss_pct_6mo, blast_pct_marrow, complex_karyotype, and tp53_mutation are independent negative predictors.
judgments.rationale: The specific independent contribution of nutritional/functional markers (albumin, weight loss) alongside molecular features in a multivariable response model goes beyond standard guideline summaries.

judgments.text: Treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd=1 patients (drug-target interaction).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's efficacy specifically in FLT3-ITD AML is the textbook/guideline indication.

judgments.text: Treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy specifically in FLT3-mutated AML is the textbook/guideline indication.

judgments.text: Treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response specifically in idh1_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's efficacy specifically in IDH1-mutated AML is the textbook/guideline indication.

judgments.text: Treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response specifically in idh2_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's efficacy specifically in IDH2-mutated AML is the textbook/guideline indication.

judgments.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more strongly in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in fit patients (positive treatment x unfit interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is the established standard-of-care regimen specifically for unfit AML patients per current guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more strongly in npm1_mutation=1 than NPM1 wild-type patients (positive treatment x NPM1 interaction).
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable predictor of venetoclax/azacitidine response is well-documented in current AML literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients who are BOTH npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1; the effect is small or zero in patients with only one or neither of these features.
judgments.rationale: The claim that ven/aza benefit is concentrated specifically in the NPM1+/unfit intersection with little effect otherwise is a subgroup-conditional interaction not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the npm1_mutation=1 & unfit_for_intensive=1 favorable subgroup, presence of tp53_mutation=1 abolishes the venetoclax_azacitidine treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutations are well-established to confer resistance/poor outcomes with venetoclax-azacitidine in AML, including in NPM1-mutated disease, and this is reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the npm1_mutation=1 & unfit_for_intensive=1 favorable subgroup, presence of complex_karyotype=1 abolishes the venetoclax_azacitidine treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype (often co-occurring with TP53) is a recognized adverse feature associated with poor response to venetoclax-azacitidine and is acknowledged in AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately +0.63 (78.6% vs 15.8%) specifically and only in patients meeting ALL FOUR predicates: npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; in any subgroup violating any one of these predicates, the treatment effect is not different from zero.
judgments.rationale: The specific four-predicate conjunction defining a uniquely responsive subgroup with a precise effect magnitude and the claim that violating any single predicate eliminates benefit is a granular cross-pathway claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the npm1=1 & unfit=1 & tp53=0 & CK=0 favorable subgroup, none of the other five treatments (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, treatment_7plus3) increases objective_response, ruling out treatment-confounding as the explanation for the venetoclax_azacitidine subgroup effect.
judgments.rationale: A subgroup-conditional comparative claim that none of midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, or 7+3 produce response in this exact NPM1+/unfit/TP53−/CK− subgroup is not a guideline statement and contradicts standard expectations for 7+3 and FLT3/IDH-targeted agents in their respective mutation contexts.

judgments.text: There is no clinically large interaction between any treatment and continuous baseline features (age, ECOG, WBC, blast %, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin) for objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A blanket null claim that no treatment meaningfully interacts with any of ten continuous baseline features for response is a strong, non-guideline assertion that goes beyond established prognostic/predictive knowledge.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, ECOG performance status remains a strong negative predictor of objective_response independent of age.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status being an independent negative predictor of response in unfit AML patients is standard, guideline-level prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Among AML patients, a constellation of cachexia/inflammation markers (low albumin, high CRP, high weight loss, high blast %) jointly predicts lower objective_response after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: The prognostic value of cachexia/inflammation markers (albumin, CRP, weight loss, blast %) for poorer outcomes in AML is well-recognized in the literature and clinical practice.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response (positive direction) specifically in patients with npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0. The other five treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) show no detectable effect on objective_response either as main effects or in mutation-matched subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Asserting a directional venetoclax-azacitidine benefit confined to this exact four-predicate subgroup while simultaneously claiming no main or mutation-matched effect for FLT3 inhibitors, IDH inhibitors, or 7+3 is a speculative cross-pathway claim well beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is a well-established favorable prognostic marker in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation is a well-known adverse prognostic marker with poor response in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a standard adverse-risk feature with poor response per ELN/NCCN AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a textbook clinical observation across oncology.

judgments.text: Patients labeled unfit_for_intensive=1 have a higher objective_response rate than those with unfit_for_intensive=0 (in this cohort, likely because they are preferentially given venetoclax-azacitidine).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine being preferentially used in unfit patients with high response rates is well documented in AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have an objective_response rate that differs from flt3_itd=0.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD is a guideline-recognized prognostic and predictive marker in AML.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have an objective_response rate that differs from idh1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status is a standard predictive/prognostic marker in AML guidelines, especially with targeted inhibitors.

judgments.text: Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have an objective_response rate that differs from idh2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation status is a standard predictive/prognostic marker in AML guidelines, especially with enasidenib.

judgments.text: Sex_female is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Sex as an independent predictor of objective response is not part of standard AML guideline consensus.

judgments.text: secondary_aml=1 is associated with a lower objective_response rate than secondary_aml=0.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having worse outcomes than de novo AML is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis is a well-established adverse prognostic factor in AML and appears in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage as an adverse prognostic feature in AML is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor performance status and worse outcomes is a well-known general oncology finding.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss predicting worse treatment response is a textbook-level association across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory marker associated with worse cancer outcomes is widely established.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio predicting poorer oncologic outcomes is well-documented in standard literature.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting lower response rates in AML and most cancers is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is established across hematologic malignancies and solid tumors.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 is associated with a higher objective_response rate than =0.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the guideline-endorsed standard for unfit AML patients with established response benefit.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with a higher objective_response rate than =0.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction is the standard-of-care intensive AML regimen with well-known higher response rates than less intensive options.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin=1 is associated with a different objective_response rate than =0.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's effect on response is established in guidelines for FLT3-mutated AML (RATIFY).

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with a different objective_response rate than =0.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's response benefit in FLT3-mutated R/R AML is standard guideline knowledge (ADMIRAL).

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with a different objective_response rate than =0.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib response activity in IDH1-mutated AML is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib=1 is associated with a different objective_response rate than =0.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib response activity in IDH2-mutated AML is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, unfit, complex karyotype, mutations, blood counts, and lab markers, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains positively associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is standard-of-care with established response benefit in AML (VIALE-A).

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, npm1_mutation remains a strong positive predictor and tp53_mutation/complex_karyotype remain negative predictors.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 favorable and TP53/complex karyotype adverse prognostic effects are core ELN risk stratification.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_midostaurin/gilteritinib/ivosidenib/enasidenib/7+3 each show no independent main effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming these targeted agents have no independent main effect after adjustment (i.e., effects are entirely mutation-conditional) is a specific multivariable inference not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd=1 patients (positive treatment-by-flt3_itd interaction).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit specifically in FLT3-ITD patients is the labeled indication and guideline standard.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_tkd=1 patients (positive treatment-by-flt3_tkd interaction).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin is approved/recommended for FLT3 mutations including TKD per RATIFY and guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is the established labeled indication.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response specifically in flt3_tkd=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in FLT3-mutated AML, including TKD mutations, is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response specifically in idh1_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib for IDH1-mutated AML is a guideline-endorsed targeted therapy.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response specifically in idh2_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib for IDH2-mutated AML is a guideline-endorsed targeted therapy.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a positive effect on objective_response that is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients (positive ven+aza × unfit interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax+azacitidine is the standard-of-care regimen specifically for unfit/older AML patients per guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a positive effect on objective_response that is larger in fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) patients than in unfit patients.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 intensive chemotherapy is established as the standard for fit AML patients, not unfit patients.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, the ven+aza ORR benefit is concentrated in npm1_mutation=1 patients and is essentially absent in npm1=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Restricting ven+aza benefit specifically to NPM1-mutant within the unfit subgroup is a more granular subgroup-conditional claim than guideline language.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, the ven+aza ORR benefit is essentially abolished in tp53_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutations are well-known to confer resistance to venetoclax-based regimens and are noted in guidelines/textbooks.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, the ven+aza ORR benefit is markedly attenuated in complex_karyotype=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: While TP53/adverse cytogenetics resistance is known, a specific quantitative attenuation tied to complex karyotype independent of TP53 is a more nuanced subgroup claim.

judgments.text: There is a 3-way ven+aza × unfit_for_intensive × npm1_mutation positive interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A formal 3-way ven+aza × fitness × NPM1 interaction is a mechanistic cross-pathway claim beyond standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: ven+aza has no benefit in unfit_for_intensive=0 patients regardless of npm1 status.
judgments.rationale: Asserting ven+aza has no benefit in fit patients regardless of NPM1 is a subgroup-conditional claim not addressed in current guidelines, which simply don't recommend it as first-line for fit patients.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 & npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=1 abolishes the ven+aza response.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutations are well-established as conferring resistance/poor outcomes to venetoclax+azacitidine in unfit AML patients, including NPM1-mutated cases.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 & npm1_mutation=1, complex_karyotype=1 abolishes the ven+aza response.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a recognized adverse marker that diminishes ven+aza efficacy in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a very large objective_response benefit specifically in patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (the complete responder subgroup), with effect sizes >0.5 in absolute ORR difference.
judgments.rationale: The specific four-way conjunction (unfit AND NPM1+ AND TP53- AND CK-) defining a 'complete responder' subgroup with >0.5 absolute ORR benefit is a granular subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: Outside the (unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck-) subgroup, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming ven+aza has no detectable effect outside this narrow four-way subgroup contradicts broader guideline endorsement of ven+aza in unfit AML and is a speculative cross-subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Beyond ven+aza × (unfit, npm1, tp53, ck), no other treatment × binary-feature pair shows a strong (|beta|>0.2 with p<0.001) interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim that no other treatment×binary-feature interaction reaches a strong threshold is a dataset-specific assertion not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Beyond ven+aza, no treatment × continuous-feature interaction reaches a robust effect (Bonferroni-style threshold p<0.001) on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim about absence of treatment×continuous-feature interactions at a Bonferroni threshold is a dataset-specific finding beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib has a positive effect in females (sex_female=1) and a negative effect in males (sex_female=0), independent of idh2 status.
judgments.rationale: Sex-dependent efficacy of enasidenib independent of IDH2 status is not part of established guideline paradigms, which tie enasidenib activity to IDH2 mutation.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib reduces objective_response specifically in npm1_mutation=1 patients (negative ivosidenib × npm1 interaction).
judgments.rationale: A negative ivosidenib×NPM1 interaction reducing response specifically in NPM1-mutated patients is a cross-pathway speculative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a negative effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype=1 patients (i.e., 7+3 is less effective in complex karyotype).
judgments.rationale: It is well established that 7+3 induction yields poorer responses in complex karyotype AML, a standard adverse-risk feature in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the (unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck-) responder subgroup, the absolute ven+aza ORR benefit declines with worsening ECOG performance status but remains very large at every ECOG level.
judgments.rationale: The claim of an ECOG-graded but uniformly large ven+aza benefit within a narrowly defined four-way responder subgroup is a granular subgroup-conditional pattern beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: ven+aza adds no detectable benefit in fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) patients, even when they carry npm1_mutation=1 with favorable cytogenetics (tp53=0, ck=0).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support ven+aza primarily in unfit/older patients but do not explicitly state fit NPM1-mutated patients derive no benefit from ven+aza, making this a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: In a fully adjusted model, the ven+aza × unfit × npm1 3-way interaction remains significant after controlling for tp53, complex_karyotype, ecog_ps, age, sex, wbc, blast %, albumin, weight loss, and crp; in addition, ven+aza × tp53 and ven+aza × complex_karyotype carry significant negative interactions.
judgments.rationale: A specific three-way ven+aza × unfit × NPM1 interaction surviving full adjustment alongside negative TP53 and complex karyotype interactions is a granular multivariable claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Final best-supported subgroup hypothesis: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response only in patients meeting ALL of the following conditions — unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0 — with an absolute ORR increase of approximately +0.63 (78.6% vs 15.8%); failure to meet any one of these four conditions reduces the effect to ~0.
judgments.rationale: Restricting ven+aza benefit to a precise four-condition intersection (unfit + NPM1 + TP53-wt + non-complex karyotype) with a ~63% absolute ORR gain is a speculative conjunctive subgroup claim beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: For the targeted agents midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, and enasidenib, no biologically expected mutation-based responder subgroup is supported in this cohort (no significant FLT3-by-FLT3-inhibitor or IDH-by-IDH-inhibitor interactions on objective_response).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines and trial data establish FLT3-inhibitor benefit in FLT3-mutant AML and IDH-inhibitor benefit in IDH-mutant AML, so a claim that these expected interactions are absent contradicts paradigm consensus and is non-standard.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 provides modest benefit in complex_karyotype=0 patients but appears slightly disadvantageous in complex_karyotype=1 patients (negative 7+3 × complex_karyotype interaction).
judgments.rationale: It is well established in guidelines that 7+3 induction yields poor outcomes in complex karyotype AML and better outcomes in non-complex karyotype disease, making this a standard known association.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rates (negative effect on response).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response is a foundational, guideline-level prognostic association in oncology including AML.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female==1) is associated with a different objective_response rate than male sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in AML treatment response are not an established guideline-level paradigm and would represent a novel subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting lower response rates in AML is a textbook prognostic factor reflected in standard risk stratification and guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation==1 have higher objective_response rates than NPM1-wildtype patients.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation conferring higher response rates is established in ELN/NCCN AML guidelines as a favorable-risk marker.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation==1 have lower objective_response rates than TP53-wildtype patients.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as an adverse predictor of response is well-established guideline consensus in AML risk stratification.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype==1 have lower objective_response rates than those with complex_karyotype==0.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse-risk feature predicting poor response is standard ELN/NCCN guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: FLT3-ITD (flt3_itd) main effect on objective_response is non-zero.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD having a non-zero effect on response is established guideline-level knowledge in AML risk stratification and therapy selection.

judgments.text: FLT3-TKD (flt3_tkd), idh1_mutation, idh2_mutation each have non-zero main effects on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD, IDH1, and IDH2 mutations are established AML drivers with recognized prognostic/therapeutic relevance in current guidelines.

judgments.text: secondary_aml is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having worse response than de novo AML is a textbook, guideline-level prognostic distinction.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow predicts lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage as an adverse prognostic feature is a standard, guideline-level association in AML.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl predicts higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a positive prognostic/predictive marker is well-established in AML and oncology generally.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo predicts lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia predicting worse treatment response is a standard prognostic association in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul predicts lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated WBC at AML diagnosis is a long-recognized adverse prognostic factor in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l predicts lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic marker reflecting systemic inflammation is well-established in oncology literature.

judgments.text: Hemoglobin, LDH, NLR, and other lab markers have no detectable association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no association for hemoglobin, LDH, and NLR contradicts established prognostic roles and is a non-obvious negative finding worth flagging.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response rate compared with patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response rates is the established standard of care for unfit AML patients.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response rate compared with patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction producing high response rates is the textbook standard for fit AML patients.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin and treatment_gilteritinib (FLT3 inhibitors) each increase objective_response rate overall.
judgments.rationale: FLT3 inhibitors improving response in AML is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib and treatment_enasidenib (IDH inhibitors) each increase objective_response rate overall.
judgments.rationale: IDH1/2 inhibitors improving response in IDH-mutant AML is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: unfit_for_intensive==1 patients have higher objective_response rates than unfit_for_intensive==0 patients (paradoxical association driven by treatment selection).
judgments.rationale: A paradoxical higher response rate in unfit patients driven by treatment selection (confounding by indication) is a non-obvious epidemiologic claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin's effect on objective_response is more positive in flt3_itd==1 patients than in flt3_itd==0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is established and reflected in guidelines (RATIFY trial).

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib's effect on objective_response is more positive in flt3_itd==1 patients than in flt3_itd==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is approved specifically for FLT3-mutated relapsed/refractory AML, a guideline-standard association.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin and treatment_gilteritinib have positive effects in flt3_tkd==1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin and gilteritinib activity in FLT3-TKD mutated AML is part of standard FLT3-inhibitor labeling and guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib's effect on objective_response is more positive in idh1_mutation==1 patients than IDH1-WT (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's efficacy in IDH1-mutant AML is its approved indication and guideline-recommended.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib's effect on objective_response is more positive in idh2_mutation==1 patients than IDH2-WT.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's efficacy in IDH2-mutant AML is its approved indication and guideline-recommended.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in unfit_for_intensive==1 patients than in unfit_for_intensive==0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the standard-of-care regimen for AML patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy (VIALE-A).

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in npm1_mutation==1 patients than in NPM1-WT patients.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutant AML is well known to have high response rates to venetoclax-azacitidine and is reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine's effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in tp53_mutation==1 patients than in TP53-WT patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutant AML's poor response to venetoclax-azacitidine is established and reflected in current ELN/NCCN guidance.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine's effect on objective_response is smaller in complex_karyotype==1 patients than non-complex (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype (often TP53-associated) is known to predict poor response to venetoclax-azacitidine per guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a worse effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype==1 patients than non-complex (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a well-established adverse predictor of response to 7+3 induction in standard AML risk stratification.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has heterogeneous effects across NPM1, TP53, FLT3-ITD, and unfit subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Heterogeneity of 7+3 efficacy across NPM1, TP53, FLT3-ITD, and fitness status is well-established AML guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In patients who are simultaneously npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a much larger increase in objective_response than in any other combination of these two markers.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is the guideline-endorsed standard for unfit AML patients, with NPM1-mutant unfit patients known to have particularly high response rates.

judgments.text: In patients who are npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 AND tp53_mutation==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an even larger increase in objective_response than in the broader NPM1+ unfit subgroup.
judgments.rationale: The specific claim that excluding TP53-mutant patients further amplifies ven/aza benefit beyond the NPM1+/unfit subgroup is a subgroup-conditional refinement not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients who are npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an even larger increase in objective_response than the same subgroup with complex karyotype mixed in.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conditional claim (NPM1+/unfit/TP53wt/non-complex karyotype) yielding even larger ven/aza benefit goes beyond standard guideline stratification.

judgments.text: Within the npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 subgroup, the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response remains strongly positive after adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, tp53_mutation, and complex_karyotype.
judgments.rationale: Robustness of ven/aza benefit in NPM1+ unfit patients after adjustment for standard prognostic covariates aligns with established consensus.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is modified by continuous biomarkers (age, blast %, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin, WBC).
judgments.rationale: Continuous biomarker effect modification of ven/aza response (by CRP, NLR, weight loss, albumin, etc.) is not part of standard guideline-based prediction.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib has a more positive effect on objective_response in females (sex_female==1) than males (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: A sex-based interaction favoring females for enasidenib response is not an established IDH2-inhibitor paradigm.

judgments.text: The absolute increase in objective_response from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine differs across ecog_ps strata.
judgments.rationale: Differential ven/aza response by ECOG performance status is consistent with well-known fitness-based treatment effect heterogeneity.

judgments.text: In tp53_mutation==1 patients, no available treatment (ven/aza, 7+3, midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) produces a meaningful increase in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: The poor response of TP53-mutant AML to all available therapies including ven/aza is well-established in current guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: Across all 60 (6 treatments x 10 binary features) treatment-by-feature interaction tests on objective_response, only ven/aza interactions and 7+3 x complex_karyotype survive multiple-testing correction.
judgments.rationale: The specific multiple-testing survival pattern (only ven/aza interactions plus 7+3 × complex karyotype) is a dataset-specific cross-pathway finding not in any guideline.

judgments.text: There exists a subgroup of idh1_mutation==1 patients (defined by additional clinical features) in which treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib benefit in IDH1-mutant AML is established and guideline-recommended, so positing a responder subgroup within IDH1+ patients restates known paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: among patients with npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately +63 percentage points (78.6% on treatment vs 15.8% off treatment); outside this subgroup the effect is essentially null.
judgments.rationale: The specific quantitative subgroup combining NPM1+, unfit, TP53 wild-type, and non-complex karyotype with a ~63pp response gain is a more granular, conditional claim than guideline statements that ven/aza benefits unfit AML broadly.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_7plus3: 7+3 has near-zero effect on objective_response overall (+0.26 pp); the only meaningful heterogeneity is a modest negative interaction with complex_karyotype (within complex_karyotype==1, 7+3 lowers response by ~2.3 pp; within complex_karyotype==0, +0.7 pp). No subgroup with substantial positive 7+3 benefit was found.
judgments.rationale: It is well established that 7+3 induction performs poorly in complex-karyotype/adverse-risk AML, so a modest negative interaction with complex karyotype reflects existing consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_midostaurin and treatment_gilteritinib: neither FLT3 inhibitor produces a significant increase in objective_response in any FLT3-ITD or FLT3-TKD subgroup tested. No responder subgroup identified.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse midostaurin and gilteritinib for FLT3-mutant AML based on survival benefit; claiming no FLT3 subgroup shows increased objective response contradicts the established paradigm and is a non-trivial negative claim.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_ivosidenib: in idh1_mutation==1 patients, ivosidenib's directional effect is weakly NEGATIVE (diff ~-4 pp; interaction p=0.077). No identified responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A weakly negative directional effect of ivosidenib in IDH1-mutant patients runs counter to the guideline-endorsed positive association and is therefore a non-standard finding.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_enasidenib: there is no benefit in idh2_mutation==1 patients; the only nominally significant interaction is enasidenib x sex_female (beta +0.315, p=2.2e-04), with female enasidenib-treated response 21.2% vs male 16.5%; this is plausibly multiplicity-driven and should be replicated.
judgments.rationale: A sex-by-enasidenib interaction favoring females, with no benefit in IDH2-mutant patients overall, is not part of guideline knowledge and represents a speculative cross-pathway/demographic claim.

judgments.text: Within NPM1+ unfit patients (the ven/aza responder population), tp53_mutation==1 and complex_karyotype==1 each independently suppress the ven/aza response (resistance markers).
judgments.rationale: While TP53 and complex karyotype are general adverse markers, the specific claim that they independently suppress ven/aza response within the NPM1+ unfit responder subgroup is a conditional mechanistic refinement beyond textbook statements.

judgments.text: Across all six recorded treatments, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is the ONLY drug in this cohort with a clinically meaningful, statistically robust subgroup of patients showing a large increase in objective_response; that subgroup is fully described by the simultaneous predicates npm1_mutation==1, unfit_for_intensive==1, tp53_mutation==0, complex_karyotype==0.
judgments.rationale: The cross-treatment claim that ven/aza is the ONLY drug with a robust responder subgroup—implicitly denying FLT3- and IDH-inhibitor benefits—goes beyond and partly contradicts current guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) have a lower objective_response rate than patients with lower ECOG performance status.
judgments.rationale: Worse performance status predicting lower response is a standard prognostic factor in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Older patients (age_years) have a different objective_response rate than younger patients.
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with lower response in AML is well-established in ELN risk stratification and treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than male patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in AML response are not established as a standard guideline-level association, but the hypothesis is generic and not a novel mechanistic claim—still, testing sex as a predictor goes slightly beyond paradigm; however, this is a routine demographic check, not a novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: Patients with NPM1 mutation (npm1_mutation=1) have a higher objective_response rate than patients without NPM1 mutation.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable-risk marker predicting better response is core ELN risk stratification.

judgments.text: Patients with TP53 mutation (tp53_mutation=1) have a lower objective_response rate than patients without TP53 mutation.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation predicting poor response is a well-established adverse-risk feature in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with complex karyotype (complex_karyotype=1) have a lower objective_response rate than patients without complex karyotype.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse-risk predictor of poor response is standard in ELN/NCCN AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary AML (secondary_aml=1) have a lower objective_response rate than patients with de novo AML.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having worse response than de novo AML is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with FLT3-ITD mutation (flt3_itd=1) have a different objective_response rate than patients without FLT3-ITD.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD as an adverse prognostic marker affecting response is established in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with FLT3-TKD mutation (flt3_tkd=1) have a different objective_response rate than patients without FLT3-TKD.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD is recognized in ELN risk stratification, though its prognostic impact is debated; testing it is standard, not novel.

judgments.text: Patients with IDH1 mutation (idh1_mutation=1) have a different objective_response rate than patients without IDH1 mutation.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status is a recognized AML biomarker in guidelines, particularly with targeted therapy implications.

judgments.text: Patients with IDH2 mutation (idh2_mutation=1) have a different objective_response rate than patients without IDH2 mutation.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation status is a well-established predictor of response to targeted therapy (enasidenib) and is part of standard AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients labeled unfit_for_intensive (unfit_for_intensive=1) have a different objective_response rate than fit patients.
judgments.rationale: Fitness assessment guiding treatment intensity and predicting response is a foundational concept in AML/MDS treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher marrow blast percentage (blast_pct_marrow) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage as an adverse prognostic factor is standard knowledge in hematologic malignancy guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher peripheral white blood cell count (wbc_k_per_ul) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis as an adverse prognostic factor in AML is well-established in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with a higher objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor performance/nutritional status predicting worse outcomes is a standard, textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as a component of cachexia/poor performance status predicting worse cancer outcomes is well-established textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher serum CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory marker predicting worse cancer outcomes is a widely recognized prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker in hematologic malignancies is standard textbook/guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: NLR as a prognostic inflammatory biomarker associated with cancer outcomes is widely established in the literature and increasingly in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine producing higher response rates in AML is established standard-of-care knowledge in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction is the standard AML chemotherapy backbone with well-established response rates, fully covered in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's response benefit in AML is established and guideline-endorsed (RATIFY trial).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML is standard guideline knowledge (ADMIRAL trial).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's activity in IDH1-mutated AML is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's activity in IDH2-mutated AML is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all available baseline features and treatments, the multivariable-adjusted association of NPM1 mutation with objective_response remains positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: A treatment-and-covariate-adjusted independent positive association of NPM1 with response across a mixed-therapy cohort is a multivariable claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all baseline features, the adjusted effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is positive.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response in AML is established standard-of-care knowledge (VIALE-A).

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, the targeted small-molecule treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) and treatment_7plus3 each have an adjusted main effect on objective_response that is not statistically distinguishable from zero.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that all these active targeted agents and 7+3 lose their main-effect signal after full multivariable adjustment is a non-obvious modeling-conditional finding not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients with FLT3-ITD mutation, the FLT3 inhibitor treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response, whereas in FLT3-ITD-negative patients it has no effect (i.e., midostaurin × flt3_itd interaction).
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD-restricted benefit of midostaurin (interaction with FLT3 status) is the established indication and guideline basis.

judgments.text: Among patients with FLT3-ITD mutation, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response, whereas in FLT3-ITD-negative patients it has no effect (gilteritinib × flt3_itd interaction).
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD-restricted benefit of gilteritinib is the established indication and guideline basis.

judgments.text: Among patients with IDH1 mutation, treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response (ivosidenib × idh1_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib for IDH1-mutated AML is FDA-approved and standard guideline-recommended therapy.

judgments.text: Among patients with IDH2 mutation, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response (enasidenib × idh2_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib for IDH2-mutated AML is FDA-approved and appears in standard AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in NPM1-mutated than in NPM1-wild-type patients (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enhanced venetoclax-azacitidine response in NPM1-mutated AML is well-established from VIALE-A and is reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the guideline-standard regimen specifically for AML patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is abolished or reduced in patients with TP53 mutation (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × tp53_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Reduced ven-aza efficacy in TP53-mutated AML is well-documented from VIALE-A subgroup analyses and noted in guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is abolished or reduced in patients with complex karyotype (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × complex_karyotype interaction).
judgments.rationale: Diminished ven-aza benefit in complex karyotype/adverse cytogenetics AML is established from VIALE-A and reflected in guideline risk stratification.

judgments.text: Joint subgroup hypothesis: among patients who are NPM1-mutated AND unfit_for_intensive AND TP53-wild-type, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a much larger increase in objective_response than in any other subgroup.
judgments.rationale: The specific composite three-way subgroup claim with quantified maximal benefit goes beyond guideline-level single-marker stratification.

judgments.text: Among NPM1-mutated AND unfit_for_intensive patients, the benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is suppressed both by TP53 mutation and by complex karyotype (each makes the treatment effect ≈ 0).
judgments.rationale: The claim that TP53 and complex karyotype each independently abolish ven-aza benefit within an NPM1+/unfit subgroup is a specific cross-pathway interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Joint subgroup hypothesis (refined): among patients who are NPM1-mutated AND unfit_for_intensive AND TP53-wild-type AND lack a complex karyotype, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an objective response in roughly 78% of patients vs ~16% off treatment (TE ≈ +0.63 absolute), and outside this composite subgroup the average treatment effect of ven-aza is ≈ 0.
judgments.rationale: The precise four-way composite subgroup with quantified ~78% vs ~16% response rates and near-zero effect elsewhere is a specific quantitative claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Within NPM1+ AND TP53- AND complex_karyotype-, the unfit_for_intensive=1 predicate is necessary for the ven-aza benefit (i.e., in fit/eligible patients with the same molecular profile, ven-aza confers no benefit).
judgments.rationale: The claim that unfitness is mechanistically necessary (not just an indication) for ven-aza benefit within a defined molecular subgroup contradicts guideline framing and is speculative.

judgments.text: Adding treatment_7plus3 on top of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine within the responder subgroup does NOT further increase objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Whether 7+3 adds to ven-aza in responders is a within-dataset analytic question, not a novel biological claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the responder subgroup, treatment_7plus3 alone (without ven-aza) does NOT increase objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A null finding about 7+3 in a responder subgroup is a descriptive negative result, not a novel mechanistic insight.

judgments.text: Formal logistic regression with full 4-way interaction among treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation × unfit_for_intensive × tp53_mutation will show a significant positive 3-way interaction (t × NPM1 × unfit) and a significant negative 4-way interaction (t × NPM1 × unfit × TP53), confirming TP53 cancels the joint NPM1+/unfit+ ven-aza response.
judgments.rationale: A specific TP53-cancels-the-NPM1+/unfit+ ven-aza benefit via a 4-way interaction is a speculative cross-pathway effect modification not stated in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment assignment is approximately random with respect to baseline biomarkers (i.e., the ven-aza × subgroup interactions reflect true effect modification rather than confounded treatment selection).
judgments.rationale: An assumption about randomization/treatment-confounding is a methodological premise, not a novel oncology hypothesis.

judgments.text: Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_midostaurin will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
judgments.rationale: A null subgroup-search result for midostaurin is a descriptive analytic claim, not novel biology beyond guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_gilteritinib will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
judgments.rationale: A null subgroup-search result for gilteritinib is a descriptive analytic claim, not a novel paradigm-extending finding.

judgments.text: Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_ivosidenib will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
judgments.rationale: A null subgroup-search result for ivosidenib is a descriptive analytic claim, not novel biology.

judgments.text: Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_enasidenib will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
judgments.rationale: A null subgroup-search result for enasidenib is a descriptive analytic claim, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_7plus3 will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
judgments.rationale: A null subgroup-search result for 7+3 is a descriptive analytic claim, not novel biology.

judgments.text: Within FLT3-ITD+ patients, neither treatment_midostaurin nor treatment_gilteritinib increases ORR beyond background — i.e., FLT3 inhibitors do not produce a measurable response benefit even in their canonical biomarker-matched subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Claiming FLT3 inhibitors fail to improve ORR even in FLT3-ITD+ patients directly contradicts established guideline consensus and would be a paradigm-challenging finding.

judgments.text: Within IDH1+ patients, treatment_ivosidenib does not increase ORR; within IDH2+ patients, treatment_enasidenib does not increase ORR.
judgments.rationale: Targeted IDH inhibitors (ivosidenib for IDH1, enasidenib for IDH2) producing responses in their respective mutated AML subgroups is established guideline knowledge, so claiming no benefit contradicts standard paradigm but the framing is about known targeted associations.

judgments.text: Within the venetoclax-azacitidine responder subgroup (NPM1+ AND unfit+ AND TP53- AND CK-), the response benefit is preserved across ECOG performance status strata 0/1/2.
judgments.rationale: A specific four-way conjunction (NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK-) defining a ven-aza responder class with preserved benefit across ECOG strata is a granular subgroup claim not articulated in guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for ECOG, blast %, weight loss, CRP, WBC, albumin, NPM1, TP53, complex karyotype, and other covariates, the four-way joint indicator I(ven-aza × NPM1 × unfit × ¬TP53 × ¬CK) has a large positive adjusted log-odds-ratio for objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A multivariable-adjusted four-way interaction indicator with a large positive log-odds for ORR is a specific quantitative subgroup-conditional claim beyond textbook content.

judgments.text: Sex (sex_female) does not modify the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine within the NPM1+/unfit+/TP53-/CK- responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Sex non-modification of ven-aza effect specifically within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup is a granular subgroup-effect-modifier claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Age (age_years dichotomized at 70) does not modify the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine within the NPM1+/unfit+/TP53-/CK- responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Age (≥/<70) non-modification of ven-aza within this specific four-way subgroup is a granular conditional claim beyond standard guideline statements that ven-aza is used in older/unfit patients.

judgments.text: Outside the responder subgroup, no other binary biomarker (FLT3-ITD, FLT3-TKD, IDH1, IDH2, secondary_aml) further amplifies the ven-aza effect to a clinically meaningful degree.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that no other common AML biomarker amplifies ven-aza outside this defined responder class is a cross-pathway negative claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, and calcium are NOT associated with objective_response after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Routine chemistry/CBC labs (LFTs, electrolytes, renal indices) are not established predictors of AML treatment response in guidelines, so non-association after adjustment is the textbook-consistent default.

judgments.text: Within the responder subgroup, the absolute response rate to treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is not further increased by IDH1, IDH2, or FLT3 mutations (i.e., the ~78% on-treatment ORR is the ceiling for this regimen in NPM1+/unfit/TP53-WT/non-CK patients regardless of additional mutation status).
judgments.rationale: Positing a ~78% ORR ceiling unmodified by IDH1/IDH2/FLT3 co-mutations within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-WT/non-CK subgroup is a specific quantitative ceiling claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL best-supported treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis for objective_response: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases ORR by approximately +63 absolute percentage points (from ~16% to ~79%) only in patients meeting all of: npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this subgroup the average treatment effect of venetoclax-azacitidine is indistinguishable from zero.
judgments.rationale: A precise +63-point absolute ORR effect of ven-aza confined to a four-way conjunction with null effect elsewhere is a speculative quantitative heterogeneity claim beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: FINAL hypothesis: none of the other treatments (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, treatment_7plus3) has a clinically or statistically meaningful positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup we could identify.
judgments.rationale: A blanket null claim that midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, and 7+3 produce no meaningful ORR benefit in any identifiable subgroup contradicts established targeted/intensive therapy paradigms and is a strong cross-pathway negative claim.

judgments.text: Sensitivity check: removing the responder subgroup from the data abolishes the marginal positive association of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine with objective_response and leaves the marginal effect of unfit_for_intensive close to zero.
judgments.rationale: This is a methodological sensitivity check about subgroup influence on marginal associations, not a substantive oncology claim that could be evaluated against guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and those not receiving it (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine response in AML is established and guideline-endorsed for older/unfit patients.

judgments.text: Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_7plus3 and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction response is the standard frontline AML benchmark covered in every guideline.

judgments.text: Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_midostaurin and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin added to chemotherapy improving response in FLT3-mutated AML is guideline standard.

judgments.text: Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib activity in FLT3-mutated relapsed/refractory AML is well-established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib response in IDH1-mutated AML is established and FDA-approved/guideline-listed.

judgments.text: Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_enasidenib and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib response in IDH2-mutated AML is established and guideline-listed.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have higher objective_response than those with npm1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable response/prognostic marker in AML is textbook ELN classification.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have lower objective_response than those with tp53_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation conferring poor response in AML is a well-established adverse marker.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have lower objective_response than those with complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse-risk feature with poor response is standard ELN guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a different objective_response from those with secondary_aml=0.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having inferior response compared to de novo AML is well-established and guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response from those with flt3_itd=0.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD status is a well-established prognostic and predictive biomarker in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients labelled unfit_for_intensive=1 have a different objective_response from those with unfit_for_intensive=0.
judgments.rationale: Fitness for intensive chemotherapy is a standard guideline-based stratifier for AML treatment response.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 or idh2_mutation=1 differ in objective_response from wild-type for those genes.
judgments.rationale: IDH1/IDH2 mutations are established predictive biomarkers with targeted therapies in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting lower response is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is well-established across oncology including AML.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High WBC count is a standard adverse prognostic factor in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage as an adverse prognostic factor is textbook AML knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as a poor prognostic indicator is a well-established general oncology principle.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of inflammation and poor prognosis is widely recognized in oncology.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting lower response in AML is a foundational guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Among flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is established guideline-level knowledge (RATIFY trial).

judgments.text: Among flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib efficacy in FLT3-ITD AML is standard guideline knowledge (ADMIRAL trial).

judgments.text: Among idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib for IDH1-mutant AML is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Among idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib for IDH2-mutant AML is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Among flt3_tkd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib activity extends to FLT3-TKD mutations per its label and guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is larger in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients than in unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the standard of care specifically for unfit/older AML patients (VIALE-A).

judgments.text: The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response is larger in unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit) patients than in unfit=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 intensive chemotherapy is reserved for fit patients by definition in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response in unfit AML is established standard of care.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit) patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine does NOT increase objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming venetoclax-azacitidine has NO benefit in fit patients is a subgroup-specific negative claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Targeted therapy/mutation match interactions are weak: treatment_midostaurin × flt3_itd, treatment_gilteritinib × flt3_itd, treatment_ivosidenib × idh1_mutation, treatment_enasidenib × idh2_mutation are not significant predictors of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that targeted therapy × matched mutation interactions are all weak/non-significant contradicts paradigm and is a non-standard cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: In the multivariable logistic model adjusted for demographics, mutations, labs and treatments, npm1_mutation is independently associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable prognostic/response factor in AML is established and appears in standard guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: In the same model, ecog_ps is independently associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response is a well-established, guideline-level clinical principle.

judgments.text: In the same model, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is independently associated with higher objective_response (main effect), while the four targeted therapies (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) and 7+3 are not.
judgments.rationale: Claiming ven/aza shows a main-effect response advantage while FLT3, IDH1, IDH2 targeted therapies and 7+3 do not is a specific cross-treatment comparative claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the same model, complex_karyotype and tp53_mutation are independently associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype and TP53 mutation conferring poor response in AML are textbook adverse-risk features per ELN/NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: When key targeted therapy × biomarker interactions are added to the multivariable model, only treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive is a strong significant interaction (positive); treatment_7plus3 × complex_karyotype is significantly negative; midostaurin/gilteritinib × flt3_itd, ivosidenib × idh1, enasidenib × idh2 are not significant.
judgments.rationale: The specific pattern that only ven/aza × unfit and 7+3 × complex karyotype interactions are significant — while canonical biomarker-targeted pairings (FLT3/IDH) are not — is a non-guideline, subgroup-conditional finding.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 6-treatment × 10-binary-modifier interaction screen (each model adjusted for age, sex, ECOG, secondary_aml, unfit, complex_karyotype, tp53, albumin) will surface treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation as the strongest positive heterogeneity term.
judgments.rationale: Identifying ven/aza × NPM1 as the single strongest positive heterogeneity term across an exhaustive interaction screen is a specific subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × tp53_mutation is a significant negative interaction (TP53 attenuates ven/aza benefit).
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation attenuating venetoclax/azacitidine benefit is increasingly recognized in current AML guidelines and reviews as an established adverse modifier.

judgments.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × complex_karyotype is a significant negative interaction.
judgments.rationale: A specifically significant negative ven/aza × complex karyotype interaction (independent of TP53) is a subgroup-conditional claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 × complex_karyotype is a significant negative interaction (complex karyotype attenuates 7+3 effect / makes it harmful).
judgments.rationale: Framing complex karyotype as making 7+3 actively harmful (negative interaction) rather than just lower-benefit goes beyond guideline phrasing of adverse-risk prognosis.

judgments.text: Treatment effects on objective_response are not strongly modified by continuous covariates age_years, ecog_ps (treated continuously), albumin_g_dl, wbc_k_per_ul, or ldh_u_l (no treatment-by-continuous covariate interaction has p<0.05).
judgments.rationale: A negative claim that no continuous covariate (age, ECOG, albumin, WBC, LDH) modifies treatment effect is a specific cross-pathway/global heterogeneity statement not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Stratified response in npm1_mutation × flt3_itd cells: response is highest in npm1=1 patients regardless of flt3_itd status (NPM1 prognostic, not modified by FLT3-ITD in this cohort).
judgments.rationale: Claim that FLT3-ITD does not modify NPM1 prognostic impact contradicts the established NPM1×FLT3-ITD interaction codified in ELN risk stratification.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation is a very large positive interaction (i.e., the within-unfit ven/aza benefit is concentrated in npm1_mutation=1 patients).
judgments.rationale: Enrichment of ven/aza benefit in NPM1-mutated unfit AML is well established from VIALE-A subgroup analyses and appears in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately 50 percentage points relative to no ven/aza.
judgments.rationale: Large ven/aza response in unfit, NPM1-mutated, TP53-wildtype AML is a guideline-recognized high-response subgroup.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=0 patients (regardless of tp53_mutation status), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claim that ven/aza has no benefit at all outside NPM1-mutated unfit patients contradicts guideline evidence of broad ven/aza activity across many non-NPM1 unfit AML subgroups.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response (TP53 abolishes ven/aza benefit).
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation abolishing ven/aza benefit is a well-known, guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by ~63 percentage points (the cleanest 'super-responder' subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Three-way conditioning that adds normal karyotype on top of NPM1+/TP53wt to define a ~63pp 'super-responder' subgroup is more granular than current guideline stratification.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect (complex karyotype suppresses the benefit even with otherwise-favorable NPM1+/TP53wt).
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype independently abolishing ven/aza benefit conditional on NPM1+/TP53-wildtype status is not an established guideline modifier once TP53 is held wildtype.

judgments.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit) AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no benefit (the unfit predicate is required for the ven/aza super-effect).
judgments.rationale: Asserting biological absence of ven/aza benefit specifically in fit NPM1+/TP53wt patients goes beyond guidelines, which simply prefer intensive chemo in fit patients without claiming ven/aza is biologically inert there.

judgments.text: In a single joint logistic model that includes treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interactions with all four candidate modifiers (unfit_for_intensive, npm1_mutation, tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype) plus baseline covariates, all four interaction terms remain significant with the expected signs (positive for unfit and npm1, negative for tp53 and complex_karyotype).
judgments.rationale: A joint four-way interaction model with simultaneous significance for unfit, NPM1, TP53, and complex karyotype modifiers is a composite cross-modifier claim not formalized in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response across all three ecog_ps strata (0, 1, 2), with similar magnitude (~+19–22 pp).
judgments.rationale: Uniform ven/aza benefit magnitude across ECOG 0/1/2 within the NPM1+/TP53wt subgroup is a performance-status-conditional claim more granular than guideline statements.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a small positive effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype=0 patients but a significantly negative effect in complex_karyotype=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Standard 7+3 is known to perform poorly in complex karyotype AML, but a small positive effect in non-complex-karyotype with a significantly negative effect specifically in complex-karyotype subgroup is a quantitative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline framing.

judgments.text: Within complex_karyotype=0 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response by ~0.8 percentage points.
judgments.rationale: A specific ~0.8 percentage point benefit of 7+3 conditional on the joint complex_karyotype=0 AND tp53=0 subgroup is a granular cross-biomarker conditional effect not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib does NOT increase objective_response and shows a marginal negative trend.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines and trial data support ivosidenib activity in IDH1-mutant AML, so a finding of no benefit (and marginal negative trend) contradicts established paradigm and is non-standard.

judgments.text: Among idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib does NOT increase objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is the guideline-endorsed targeted therapy for IDH2-mutant AML, so absence of benefit in this subgroup runs counter to established consensus.

judgments.text: Among flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_midostaurin does NOT increase objective_response (no benefit detected).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin added to chemotherapy is the guideline standard for FLT3-ITD AML based on RATIFY, so a finding of no benefit contradicts established paradigm.

judgments.text: Among flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib does NOT increase objective_response (no benefit detected).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is the established standard for relapsed/refractory FLT3-ITD AML (ADMIRAL trial), so absence of benefit in FLT3-ITD patients contradicts guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Across all 10 binary biomarker modifiers, treatment_7plus3 shows the strongest heterogeneity with complex_karyotype (negative); other 7+3 × biomarker interactions (npm1, tp53, secondary_aml, flt3_itd, idh1, idh2, sex_female, unfit_for_intensive) are not significant after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: A systematic interaction screen showing complex karyotype as the singular significant modifier of 7+3 across 10 biomarkers (with TP53, secondary AML, etc. not significant) is a comparative cross-pathway claim beyond guideline framing.

judgments.text: Across the full adjusted 6-treatment × 10-modifier screen (60 tests), no treatment_midostaurin × any modifier interaction has p<0.05.
