judgments.rationale: Null result across all 10 biomarker modifiers for midostaurin — including FLT3-ITD where benefit is established — is a paradigm-discordant comparative finding from a systematic screen.

judgments.text: Across the same screen, no treatment_gilteritinib × any modifier interaction has p<0.05 (smallest p≈0.036 for ×unfit_for_intensive).
judgments.rationale: Null finding across all gilteritinib × biomarker interactions, with the strongest signal being unfit_for_intensive rather than FLT3-ITD, is a non-guideline comparative claim.

judgments.text: Across the same screen, treatment_ivosidenib × idh1_mutation has a marginal negative interaction (beta≈−0.35, p≈0.045) and treatment_ivosidenib × npm1_mutation has a marginal negative interaction (beta≈−0.20, p≈0.028); no other ivosidenib interaction is significant.
judgments.rationale: Marginal negative interactions of ivosidenib with both IDH1 and NPM1 mutation status contradict the established positive ivosidenib–IDH1 paradigm and introduce a novel NPM1 modifier claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib × sex_female has a positive interaction (beta=+0.31, p=0.0003) — i.e. enasidenib appears to add response in female patients more than male patients, though this is not a primary biological pathway.
judgments.rationale: A sex-by-enasidenib interaction favoring females is not part of established AML guideline consensus and would be a novel subgroup claim.

judgments.text: When ven/aza interactions with unfit_for_intensive, npm1_mutation, tp53_mutation, and complex_karyotype are jointly fit alongside baseline covariates (and similarly for 7+3 with complex_karyotype, tp53, unfit), only the four ven/aza interactions and the 7+3 × complex_karyotype interaction remain significant.
judgments.rationale: The specific multi-covariate interaction structure (which interactions survive joint adjustment) is an analytic finding not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: among patients satisfying unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately +0.63 (78.6% on vs 15.8% off). The unfavorable variables tp53_mutation and complex_karyotype each suppress the effect to non-significance even within unfit, NPM1+ patients; the unfit predicate is required (no benefit in fit NPM1+ patients); and the npm1_mutation predicate is required (no benefit in unfit NPM1- patients).
judgments.rationale: While ven/aza benefit in unfit NPM1+ TP53-wt AML is broadly known, the specific four-way conditional predicate including required absence of complex karyotype and required unfit status as a guideline-level claim goes beyond textbook consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_7plus3: among patients with complex_karyotype=0 AND tp53_mutation=0, treatment_7plus3 modestly increases objective_response by ~0.8 percentage points (19.8% on vs 19.0% off). The complex_karyotype=0 predicate is required because in complex_karyotype=1 patients, 7+3 has a significantly negative effect (15.6% on vs 18.0% off, Δ=−0.023, p≈0.008).
judgments.rationale: A guideline-level claim that 7+3 is actively harmful (negative effect) specifically in complex-karyotype AML, rather than merely less effective, is a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT FINDING for treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib: NO subgroup of patients with usable size (n>50 in both arms) shows an objective_response benefit at p<0.05 in the expected direction. Even within the canonical target subgroups (FLT3-ITD for midostaurin/gilteritinib, IDH1 for ivosidenib, IDH2 for enasidenib), the rate differences are essentially zero or marginally negative.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, and enasidenib show no benefit even in their canonical biomarker-defined subgroups directly contradicts established guideline consensus and is therefore a novel (and surprising) claim.

judgments.text: After the analyses above, the prognostic story (independent of treatment) is: higher npm1_mutation, higher albumin_g_dl reduce risk of non-response; higher ecog_ps, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, blast_pct_marrow, wbc_k_per_ul, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l increase risk of non-response; age_years, secondary_aml, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1_mutation, idh2_mutation are not independent prognostic factors after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: The listed prognostic factors (NPM1, albumin, ECOG, complex karyotype, TP53, blasts, WBC, etc.) and the non-significance of age/secondary AML after adjustment for these reflect well-known AML prognostic associations.

judgments.text: The predictive story (treatment effect heterogeneity) reduces to two findings: (a) treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is highly effective only in unfit_for_intensive=1 ∧ npm1_mutation=1 ∧ tp53_mutation=0 ∧ complex_karyotype=0; (b) treatment_7plus3 is mildly effective in complex_karyotype=0 patients and harmful in complex_karyotype=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Reducing predictive heterogeneity to these two specific biomarker-conditional rules, including a harmful 7+3 effect in complex karyotype, goes beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: OVERALL CONCLUSION: Across the full ds001_aml cohort, the dominant treatment-related signal is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine in the (unfit ∧ NPM1+ ∧ TP53-wt ∧ non-complex-karyotype) subgroup; only one other treatment shows any heterogeneity (treatment_7plus3 hurt by complex karyotype); the four targeted therapies show no detectable benefit in any subgroup. The dataset provides clear evidence of biomarker-defined treatment-effect heterogeneity for venetoclax/azacitidine and clear evidence of biomarker-defined treatment-effect heterogeneity (negative direction) for 7+3 in complex karyotype; the lack of targeted-therapy benefit is itself a notable finding.
judgments.rationale: The overall synthesis—particularly the claim of no targeted-therapy benefit in any subgroup combined with a specific four-way ven/aza predicate and harmful 7+3 in complex karyotype—is a novel cross-pathway conclusion not found in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The cohort has a non-trivial baseline objective_response rate (i.e., not 0% or 100%), and treatments are common enough to support comparisons.
judgments.rationale: Baseline cohort response rate characterization is a descriptive statistic, not a hypothesis going beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine improving response rates in AML is established standard-of-care knowledge in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction's response rate profile is foundational AML guideline content, not novel.

judgments.text: Each targeted agent (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) has a non-zero main effect on objective_response in the unselected cohort.
judgments.rationale: Targeted agents (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) having efficacy effects in AML is established guideline knowledge, though the unselected-cohort framing is mildly nonstandard.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation have a higher objective_response rate than patients without npm1_mutation.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable prognostic/response marker in AML is textbook ELN-classification knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation have a lower objective_response rate than patients without tp53_mutation.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation conferring poor response in AML is well-established adverse-risk guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype have a lower objective_response rate than patients without complex_karyotype.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse-risk feature predicting poor response is standard ELN/NCCN guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have a lower objective_response rate than fit patients.
judgments.rationale: Unfit patients having lower response rates than fit patients is a baseline clinical expectation reflected in treatment selection guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_itd or flt3_tkd mutations have a different objective_response rate than wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD/TKD mutation status affecting response (especially with FLT3 inhibitors available) is established AML guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation or idh2_mutation have a different objective_response rate than wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: IDH1/IDH2 mutations affecting response (with approved targeted inhibitors) is established AML guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Sex (sex_female) and secondary_aml are independently associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Sex and secondary AML status are well-established prognostic factors in AML response assessment routinely discussed in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a foundational clinical oncology principle in every guideline.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting lower response in AML is standard guideline knowledge underlying fit/unfit treatment stratification.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage as an adverse prognostic marker is established AML paradigm in ELN/NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor nutritional/performance status predicting worse outcomes is standard clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: Markers of systemic inflammation/cachexia (crp_mg_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, wbc_k_per_ul, nlr) predict lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Inflammatory/cachexia markers like CRP, weight loss, and NLR predicting worse cancer outcomes is well-established across oncology.

judgments.text: Routine organ-function labs (creatinine_mg_dl, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, ldh_u_l, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, bun_mg_dl) are individually associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Routine organ-function labs as prognostic markers and eligibility criteria are standard guideline content for AML treatment selection.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive patients (positive interaction: VEN/AZA helps unfit patients more than fit patients).
judgments.rationale: VEN/AZA is specifically indicated and approved for unfit/older AML patients per NCCN and ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is amplified in patients with npm1_mutation (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enhanced VEN/AZA response in NPM1-mutated AML is a well-recognized association documented in current AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: tp53_mutation abrogates the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine response benefit (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation conferring resistance to venetoclax-based therapy is established in AML guidelines and ELN risk stratification.

judgments.text: complex_karyotype abrogates the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine response benefit (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: While adverse cytogenetics are known to do worse on VEN/AZA, the strong claim that complex karyotype fully abrogates benefit goes beyond guideline-supported subgroup performance differences.

judgments.text: Each targeted agent shows a positive interaction with its canonical biomarker on objective_response: midostaurin x flt3_itd, gilteritinib x flt3_itd, ivosidenib x idh1_mutation, enasidenib x idh2_mutation.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin/gilteritinib-FLT3 and ivosidenib/enasidenib-IDH1/2 pairings are canonical, guideline-level biomarker-drug associations.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 shows a positive interaction with fitness markers (effect concentrated in fit / lower-ECOG patients).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 being preferred and more effective in fit/lower-ECOG patients is standard guideline-based selection criteria for intensive induction.

judgments.text: The NPM1+ main-effect benefit on objective_response is driven entirely by NPM1+ patients who received VEN/AZA, not an intrinsic NPM1+ response advantage.
judgments.rationale: Claiming NPM1+'s response advantage is entirely conditional on receiving VEN/AZA contradicts the established intrinsic favorable prognosis of NPM1+ AML across regimens.

judgments.text: The unfit_for_intensive main-effect benefit on objective_response is similarly driven entirely by unfit patients who received VEN/AZA, not an intrinsic unfit-patient advantage.
judgments.rationale: The claim that unfit patients have a main-effect response benefit attributable solely to VEN/AZA receipt is a non-trivial subgroup-conditional decomposition not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: VEN/AZA's effect on objective_response is greatest among unfit_for_intensive AND npm1_mutation patients (joint two-way modifier set).
judgments.rationale: A joint unfit AND NPM1+ two-way modifier maximizing VEN/AZA response is a specific cross-feature interaction beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive AND npm1_mutation patients, VEN/AZA's effect on objective_response is further amplified when tp53_mutation is absent AND complex_karyotype is absent (i.e., these adverse-risk lesions individually neutralize the response).
judgments.rationale: The conditional claim that absence of TP53 and complex karyotype further amplifies VEN/AZA effect within an unfit/NPM1+ subgroup is a multi-way interaction not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: An indicator combining unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 ('target subgroup') captures the entire VEN/AZA-modifying signal: VEN/AZA increases objective_response only inside this subgroup, with negligible effect outside it.
judgments.rationale: A precise 4-feature composite indicator capturing the entire VEN/AZA modifying signal with negligible effect outside is a novel, highly specific subgroup definition.

judgments.text: Within the target subgroup (unfit + NPM1+ + TP53- + complex-), additional binary features (secondary_aml, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1_mutation, idh2_mutation, sex_female) further modify the VEN/AZA effect.
judgments.rationale: Speculative further modification of VEN/AZA effect by secondary AML, FLT3, IDH, and sex within a defined target subgroup is mechanistic and not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the target subgroup, higher ecog_ps lowers the absolute VEN/AZA response rate but does not abolish the VEN/AZA-vs-no-VEN/AZA contrast.
judgments.rationale: The claim that ECOG shifts absolute response level but preserves the VEN/AZA contrast within a defined subgroup is a nuanced effect-modification pattern beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Within the target subgroup, age_years modifies the VEN/AZA effect.
judgments.rationale: Age-based modification of VEN/AZA response within a multi-feature defined subgroup is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In a logistic regression with VEN/AZA, the four candidate modifiers, and all four 2-way VEN/AZA-by-modifier interactions, all four interactions are individually significant in the directions predicted by the subgroup hypothesis.
judgments.rationale: A four-way interaction model with all interactions significant in predicted directions is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the target subgroup, the other treatments (treatment_7plus3, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) ALSO improve objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Standard guideline-level treatments like 7+3, midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, and enasidenib are known to improve response in appropriate AML populations.

judgments.text: A systematic screen of all six treatments x ten binary features (each at value 0 and 1) will identify any treatment-modifier pair with a |risk-difference| > 5 pct points beyond the VEN/AZA family.
judgments.rationale: A systematic screen identifying novel treatment-modifier pairs beyond known associations is exploratory and not guideline-established.

judgments.text: Within the target subgroup, no continuous lab/clinical feature (age_years, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, wbc_k_per_ul, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, hemoglobin_g_dl) abolishes the VEN/AZA response benefit.
judgments.rationale: The claim that no continuous lab feature abolishes VEN/AZA benefit within a specific composite subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Removing any one of the four predicates (unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0) materially reduces the precision of the VEN/AZA-responsive subgroup, so all four are needed.
judgments.rationale: The claim that all four specific predicates are jointly necessary to define a VEN/AZA-responsive subgroup is not a guideline-recognized composite biomarker.

judgments.text: FINAL HYPOTHESIS: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response only in the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; in this subgroup objective_response rises from ~16% to ~79% (absolute +63 pp); outside the subgroup VEN/AZA has no detectable effect on objective_response. No other treatment (treatment_7plus3, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) has a detectable effect on objective_response in any subgroup.
judgments.rationale: While VEN/AZA benefit in unfit NPM1+/TP53-wt/non-complex AML is partially aligned with known biology, the precise composite subgroup with ~63pp effect and the claim that no other AML treatment works in any subgroup contradicts established efficacy of midostaurin/FLT3, ivosidenib/IDH1, etc.

judgments.text: FINAL HYPOTHESIS: For each non-VEN/AZA treatment, no biomarker subgroup with |effect| >= 5 pct points on objective_response can be identified in this dataset; the canonical pairings (midostaurin/FLT3-ITD, gilteritinib/FLT3-ITD, ivosidenib/IDH1, enasidenib/IDH2) are not active.
judgments.rationale: Claiming canonical FLT3 and IDH targeted-therapy pairings are inactive directly contradicts guideline consensus, making this a strongly non-standard (novel) claim.

judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response rates in AML is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_tkd have a different objective_response rate than those without flt3_tkd.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD mutation status and its association with response/outcomes in AML is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation have a different objective_response rate than those without idh1_mutation.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status and differential response (e.g., to ivosidenib or hypomethylating agents) is established in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic inflammatory marker is a well-known association documented in oncology guidelines and prognostic scores like GPS.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Leukocytosis as an adverse prognostic factor, particularly in AML, is a standard textbook association.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is established across many cancers and incorporated into staging/risk systems.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as a poor prognostic inflammatory biomarker is widely established in oncology literature and meta-analyses.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Anemia as an adverse prognostic factor is a standard, guideline-level association in oncology.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response more in flt3_itd-positive patients than in flt3_itd-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's preferential benefit in FLT3-ITD-positive AML is the basis for its FDA approval and appears in NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response more in flt3_tkd-positive patients than in flt3_tkd-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's approval and guideline indication explicitly covers FLT3-TKD-mutated AML, making this a standard known association.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response more in flt3_itd-positive patients than in flt3_itd-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's preferential efficacy in FLT3-ITD-positive relapsed/refractory AML is its labeled indication and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response more in flt3_tkd-positive patients than in flt3_tkd-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is approved for FLT3-mutated AML including TKD (D835/I836) mutations, making this a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response more in idh1_mutation-positive patients than in idh1_mutation-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's enhanced efficacy in IDH1-mutant AML is the established indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response more in idh2_mutation-positive patients than in idh2_mutation-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's efficacy in IDH2-mutant AML is the FDA-approved, guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more in unfit_for_intensive patients than in fit patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the standard-of-care regimen specifically for AML patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy, per current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more in npm1_mutation-positive patients than in npm1-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enhanced response of NPM1-mutant AML to venetoclax-azacitidine is well-established and reflected in NCCN/ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has reduced efficacy in tp53_mutation-positive patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Reduced venetoclax-azacitidine efficacy in TP53-mutant AML is a widely recognized guideline-level observation.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has reduced efficacy in complex_karyotype-positive patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Poor response of complex karyotype AML to venetoclax-azacitidine is established adverse-risk knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a larger benefit on objective_response in patients who are not unfit_for_intensive (negative interaction with unfit).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 intensive chemotherapy is reserved for fit patients because unfit patients tolerate and benefit less—this is standard guideline reasoning.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 efficacy on objective_response is reduced by complex_karyotype (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype defines adverse-risk AML with poor response to 7+3, a core ELN risk-stratification principle.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 efficacy decreases with older age_years (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Decreased benefit of intensive 7+3 chemotherapy with older age is a foundational, guideline-level clinical principle in AML.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 efficacy decreases with higher ecog_ps (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes with intensive 7+3 chemotherapy is standard, guideline-recognized knowledge.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features and treatments, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains independently associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine producing higher response rates than other AML therapies in unfit patients is established guideline knowledge (VIALE-A).

judgments.text: After adjustment, ecog_ps, npm1_mutation, unfit_for_intensive, weight_loss_pct_6mo, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, crp_mg_l, and wbc_k_per_ul each remain independently predictive of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: ECOG PS, NPM1, TP53, complex karyotype, blast burden, WBC, and frailty/nutritional markers are all well-established prognostic factors in AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The other five treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) have no independent main effect on objective_response after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Claiming midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, and enasidenib have no independent response benefit contradicts guideline-endorsed efficacy in their biomarker-defined populations and is a non-standard pooled-cohort claim.

judgments.text: Among all treatment-by-feature pairs scanned, the strongest treatment-effect heterogeneity is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines recognize NPM1 as favorable for venetoclax/azacitidine, but identifying it as the single strongest treatment-effect modifier across all features is a specific quantitative claim beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Among all treatment-by-feature pairs scanned, the second-strongest treatment-effect heterogeneity is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x unfit_for_intensive.
judgments.rationale: Ranking 'unfit for intensive therapy' as the second-strongest effect modifier is a specific heterogeneity claim not stated in guidelines, which treat unfitness as an indication rather than a within-treatment effect modifier.

judgments.text: Among all treatment-by-feature pairs scanned, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a significantly negative interaction with tp53_mutation and with complex_karyotype.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation and complex karyotype are well-known to predict poor response to venetoclax/azacitidine and are explicitly noted in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is concentrated specifically in patients who are BOTH npm1_mutation-positive AND unfit_for_intensive (three-way interaction).
judgments.rationale: A specific three-way interaction concentrating venetoclax/azacitidine benefit in NPM1+ AND unfit patients goes beyond guideline statements that treat NPM1 status and fitness as independent considerations.

judgments.text: Within the npm1_mutation-positive AND unfit_for_intensive subgroup, the venetoclax/azacitidine response rate is dramatically higher than control (>50 percentage point absolute increase).
judgments.rationale: A >50 percentage point absolute response increase confined to the NPM1+/unfit subgroup is a quantitative subgroup-specific claim not present in guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: Outside the joint NPM1+/unfit subgroup, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has essentially no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming venetoclax/azacitidine has essentially no effect outside NPM1+/unfit patients contradicts guideline-supported broader activity in unfit AML and is a strong novel subgroup-restriction claim.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit subgroup, tp53_mutation abolishes the venetoclax/azacitidine treatment effect (negative tx*tp53 interaction).
judgments.rationale: A conditional TP53-mediated abrogation of venetoclax/azacitidine benefit specifically within the NPM1+/unfit subgroup is a cross-pathway subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit subgroup, complex_karyotype abolishes the venetoclax/azacitidine treatment effect (negative tx*complex_karyotype interaction).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse VEN/AZA in unfit NPM1-mutated AML but do not specify that complex karyotype abolishes the benefit specifically within this NPM1+/unfit subgroup.

judgments.text: The complete subgroup defining venetoclax/azacitidine benefit is npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; in this group response rate on treatment >75% vs <20% off.
judgments.rationale: A precise four-feature conjunctive subgroup definition (NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/no complex karyotype) with quantitative response thresholds is a speculative refinement beyond guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib has a sex-specific effect on objective_response: females (sex_female=1) benefit, males (sex_female=0) do not (positive tx*sex interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific benefit of enasidenib is not an established finding in IDH2-mutant AML guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib has greater benefit at lower hemoglobin_g_dl (negative tx*hemoglobin interaction).
judgments.rationale: A hemoglobin-conditional differential benefit of enasidenib is not part of established treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: Among the five non-VEN/AZA treatments, no concentrated treatment-benefit subgroup with a risk difference >0.20 and adequate sample size exists.
judgments.rationale: Stating that no large concentrated benefit subgroup exists among standard AML therapies is consistent with current guideline consensus rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 worsens objective_response in patients with complex_karyotype (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Although adverse-risk cytogenetics are known to predict poor 7+3 outcomes, a specific negative interaction worsening objective response in complex karyotype as a treatment-modifying effect goes beyond a textbook prognostic statement.

judgments.text: After full multivariable adjustment (including all six treatments and patient features), the triple interaction tx_VEN/AZA * npm1_mutation * unfit_for_intensive remains the dominant effect.
judgments.rationale: A triple interaction (treatment × NPM1 × fitness) dominating after full multivariable adjustment is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After full multivariable adjustment, both tp53_mutation and complex_karyotype each have significantly negative interactions with tx_VEN/AZA, indicating each independently suppresses the venetoclax effect.
judgments.rationale: Independent additive suppression of VEN/AZA benefit by both TP53 and complex karyotype as separate interaction terms is a mechanistic claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: An exhaustive search over all 3-feature binary subgroups for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine identifies the npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND complex_karyotype=0 (or equivalently AND tp53=0) subgroup as the highest-RD subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Identifying a specific 3-feature maximal risk-difference subgroup via exhaustive search is a data-driven refinement beyond established paradigms.

judgments.text: An exhaustive search over all 4-feature binary subgroups for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine identifies npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND complex_karyotype=0 AND tp53_mutation=0 as the maximal-RD definition.
judgments.rationale: A 4-feature exhaustive-search-derived maximal subgroup definition is a speculative refinement not present in any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 3-feature subgroup search for treatment_midostaurin reveals no subgroup with a clinically meaningful positive risk difference (RD>0.10) at adequate sample size.
judgments.rationale: Null findings about midostaurin lacking a high-RD subgroup are uninformative and do not constitute novel biological or clinical claims beyond guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 3-feature subgroup search for treatment_gilteritinib reveals no subgroup with RD>0.10 at adequate sample size.
judgments.rationale: A negative exhaustive subgroup search for gilteritinib is a non-finding, not a novel hypothesis beyond established FLT3-mutant AML treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 3-feature subgroup search for treatment_ivosidenib reveals no subgroup with RD>0.10 at adequate sample size.
judgments.rationale: Null subgroup result for ivosidenib provides no novel mechanistic or clinical insight beyond existing IDH1-mutant AML guidelines.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 3-feature subgroup search for treatment_enasidenib reveals only modest subgroup signals; the largest identifies sex_female=1 & idh1_mutation=1 & npm1_mutation=0 with RD ~+0.09.
judgments.rationale: A specific sex-by-IDH1-by-NPM1 conditional benefit for enasidenib (female, IDH1+, NPM1-wt) is a speculative interaction not reflected in current AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 shows a notable negative subgroup at complex_karyotype=1 & flt3_itd=1 & npm1_mutation=1: 7+3 reduces objective_response by ~12 percentage points.
judgments.rationale: A ~12-point reduction in response to 7+3 specifically in complex-karyotype/FLT3-ITD/NPM1-mutant patients is a multi-feature conditional harm signal not codified in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the npm1=1 & unfit=1 & tp53=0 & ck=0 subgroup, after adjustment for ECOG, age, sex, mutations, labs, and other treatments, the venetoclax/azacitidine effect remains very large (OR>10).
judgments.rationale: An OR>10 venetoclax/azacitidine effect specifically in NPM1+/unfit/TP53-wt/non-complex-karyotype patients after extensive adjustment is a sharper subgroup-conditional magnitude than guideline-level statements.

judgments.text: Outside the npm1=1 & unfit=1 & tp53=0 & ck=0 subgroup, after similar adjustment, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming ven/aza has essentially no response effect outside this specific NPM1/unfit/TP53-wt/non-CK subgroup contradicts the broader guideline-supported use of ven/aza in unfit AML.

judgments.text: NPM1+ alone (without unfit) does not produce a venetoclax/azacitidine response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that NPM1 mutation alone (in fit patients) does not benefit from ven/aza is a conditional claim beyond guideline-level recommendations that focus on fitness rather than NPM1-by-fitness interactions.

judgments.text: Unfit alone (without NPM1) does not produce a venetoclax/azacitidine response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming unfit status alone (without NPM1) confers no ven/aza response benefit contradicts the guideline rationale for ven/aza in unfit AML broadly and is a novel restriction.

judgments.text: Within NPM1+/unfit/no-CK/no-TP53 patients, restricting to those without treatment_7plus3 (i.e., monotherapy ven/aza) yields an even higher response.
judgments.rationale: A claim that ven/aza monotherapy outperforms ven/aza combined with 7+3 within this specific molecular/fitness subgroup is a speculative cross-treatment comparison not present in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among NPM1+/unfit/no-CK/no-TP53 patients on venetoclax/azacitidine, the response rate is uniformly high regardless of FLT3 status, IDH status, sex, and age.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated AML without TP53/complex karyotype responding well to venetoclax/azacitidine in unfit patients is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Final overarching subgroup hypothesis for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: objective_response is markedly higher on treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine specifically among patients who satisfy ALL of: npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0; outside this conjunction the treatment has no effect.
judgments.rationale: The strict conjunctive claim that ven/aza has effect ONLY in this exact intersection and no effect elsewhere is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_7plus3: objective_response is reduced on treatment_7plus3 vs no treatment_7plus3 specifically among patients with complex_karyotype=1 (especially in combination with flt3_itd=1 and/or npm1_mutation=1); outside complex_karyotype, 7+3 has no significant effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming 7+3 actively reduces response in complex karyotype (especially with FLT3-ITD/NPM1) rather than just being less effective is a speculative subgroup-specific harm claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_enasidenib: objective_response is modestly higher on treatment_enasidenib vs not specifically among female patients (sex_female=1), with the strongest signal in unfit/IDH-mutated females; in males, enasidenib has no benefit (and may slightly harm).
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific (female-predominant) benefit of enasidenib with possible harm in males is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_midostaurin: there is no identifiable subgroup of patients in whom treatment_midostaurin provides a meaningful objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: A blanket claim that no subgroup benefits from midostaurin contradicts established FLT3-mutated AML guideline indications and is a non-standard negative claim.

judgments.text: Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_gilteritinib: there is no identifiable subgroup of patients in whom treatment_gilteritinib provides a meaningful objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no subgroup benefits from gilteritinib contradicts established FLT3-mutated relapsed/refractory AML guideline use and is a non-standard negative claim.

judgments.text: Final subgroup hypothesis for treatment_ivosidenib: there is no identifiable subgroup of patients in whom treatment_ivosidenib provides a meaningful objective_response benefit; in particular, idh1_mutation-positive patients do NOT benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming IDH1-mutant patients do not benefit from ivosidenib directly contradicts established guideline consensus and is a non-standard subgroup-specific negative claim.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's effect on response in FLT3-mutant AML is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's response benefit in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutant AML is guideline-standard.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib response in IDH1-mutant AML is well established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib response in IDH2-mutant AML is well established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine yielding higher response than comparators is established standard for unfit AML.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction response rates are textbook knowledge in AML.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting lower response in AML is a well-established prognostic factor.

judgments.text: objective_response rate differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in AML response are not part of standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG PS predicting worse response is a standard prognostic association.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have lower objective_response than those with secondary_aml=0.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having lower response than de novo AML is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 have a different objective_response rate than those with unfit_for_intensive=0.
judgments.rationale: Fitness status predicting response to therapy is a standard clinical paradigm in AML/oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse prognostic factor predicting lower response is established in ELN/NCCN AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response rate than those with flt3_itd=0.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD mutation status and its association with response is a well-established guideline-level association in AML.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_tkd=1 have a different objective_response rate than those with flt3_tkd=0.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD mutation status is a recognized AML risk-stratification marker discussed in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than those with idh1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status and response to therapy (e.g., ivosidenib) is established in current AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than those with idh2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation status and response (e.g., enasidenib) is established in current AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than those with npm1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation predicting favorable response is a textbook/guideline-level AML association.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than those with tp53_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation predicting lower response is a well-established adverse prognostic marker in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (higher albumin_g_dl predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a fitness/nutritional marker correlating with better outcomes is a standard clinical association.

judgments.text: blast_pct_marrow is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (lower blast_pct_marrow predicts lower ORR).
judgments.rationale: Marrow blast percentage as a disease-burden prognostic factor is a standard, guideline-recognized association in AML.

judgments.text: weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (lower weight_loss_pct_6mo predicts lower ORR).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss and cachexia are well-established negative prognostic markers in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: crp_mg_l is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (lower crp_mg_l predicts lower ORR).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of inflammation predicting worse outcomes is a well-known prognostic association.

judgments.text: wbc_k_per_ul is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (lower wbc_k_per_ul predicts lower ORR).
judgments.rationale: Leukocytosis/WBC as a prognostic marker is established, though direction here (lower WBC worse) is less standard but still within known performance-status correlates.

judgments.text: ldh_u_l is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (lower ldh_u_l predicts lower ORR).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH is a textbook negative prognostic biomarker across many cancers and appears in staging/guidelines.

judgments.text: nlr is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (lower nlr predicts lower ORR).
judgments.rationale: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a prognostic inflammation marker is widely established in oncology literature.

judgments.text: hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (higher hemoglobin_g_dl predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as a negative prognostic factor reflecting performance status and disease burden is standard.

judgments.text: alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different alkaline_phosphatase_u_l predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflecting bone/liver involvement is a well-known prognostic marker.

judgments.text: ast_u_l is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different ast_u_l predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST reflecting liver dysfunction or metastatic burden is a standard prognostic correlate.

judgments.text: alt_u_l is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different alt_u_l predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT reflecting hepatic involvement is a standard prognostic correlate in oncology.

judgments.text: total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different total_bilirubin_mg_dl predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Elevated bilirubin reflecting hepatic dysfunction is a well-known negative prognostic marker.

judgments.text: creatinine_mg_dl is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different creatinine_mg_dl predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine as a predictor of objective response rate is not a standard guideline-level association in oncology.

judgments.text: bun_mg_dl is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different bun_mg_dl predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: BUN as a predictor of ORR is not part of established oncology treatment paradigms or guidelines.

judgments.text: sodium_meq_l is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different sodium_meq_l predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Serum sodium as a predictor of ORR is not a guideline-level biomarker, though hyponatremia has prognostic associations in some cancers.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different potassium_meq_l predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Potassium level as a predictor of ORR is not part of standard oncology consensus or guidelines.

judgments.text: calcium_mg_dl is associated with objective_response such that values associated with worse fitness (different calcium_mg_dl predicts higher ORR).
judgments.rationale: Calcium level as a predictor of ORR is not an established guideline-level predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response in patients with flt3_itd=1 (FLT3-ITD positive).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin in FLT3-ITD-mutant AML is a well-established guideline-recommended therapy (RATIFY trial).

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response in patients with flt3_itd=1 (FLT3-ITD positive).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib for FLT3-ITD-mutant relapsed/refractory AML is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed (ADMIRAL trial).

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response in patients with any FLT3 mutation (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is approved and guideline-recommended for FLT3-mutated AML, including both ITD and TKD mutations.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response in patients with idh1_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib for IDH1-mutant AML is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed standard of care.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response in patients with idh2_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib for IDH2-mutant relapsed/refractory AML is FDA-approved and guideline-recommended.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in patients with unfit_for_intensive=1.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is the guideline-standard regimen for unfit AML patients (VIALE-A).

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in patients with unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit patients).
judgments.rationale: Use of venetoclax/azacitidine in fit AML patients is not guideline-endorsed and goes beyond established paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response in patients with unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit patients).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction is the textbook standard of care for fit AML patients.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_midostaurin * flt3_itd interaction on objective_response (effect concentrated in FLT3-ITD+).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's benefit in FLT3-ITD+ AML (RATIFY) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_gilteritinib * flt3_itd interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in FLT3-ITD+ relapsed/refractory AML (ADMIRAL) is guideline-standard.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_gilteritinib * flt3_tkd interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is approved and guideline-endorsed for FLT3-mutated AML including TKD mutations.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_ivosidenib * idh1_mutation interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's activity in IDH1-mutant AML is FDA-approved and guideline-standard.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_enasidenib * idh2_mutation interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's activity in IDH2-mutant AML is FDA-approved and guideline-standard.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * unfit_for_intensive interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Preferential benefit of venetoclax/azacitidine in unfit patients is the established paradigm.

judgments.text: There is a negative treatment_7plus3 * unfit_for_intensive interaction (7+3 works in fit, not unfit).
judgments.rationale: That 7+3 is appropriate for fit but not unfit patients is textbook AML treatment selection.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response only in patients with tp53_mutation=0 (TP53 wild-type).
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutant AML's resistance to venetoclax-azacitidine is well-established in guidelines (e.g., NCCN) and the VIALE-A trial subgroup analyses.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response only in patients with complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Reduced venetoclax-azacitidine benefit in complex karyotype AML is a recognized adverse-risk subgroup finding reflected in standard treatment references.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is similar in secondary_aml=1 and secondary_aml=0.
judgments.rationale: A claim of comparable venetoclax-azacitidine benefit in secondary versus de novo AML is not a textbook consensus statement and represents a non-trivial subgroup assertion.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine substantially increases objective_response only in patients with npm1_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: Strong venetoclax-azacitidine response in NPM1-mutant AML is a well-known association documented in guidelines and pivotal trial subgroup data.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response specifically in the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional restricting benefit to unfit AND TP53-wildtype AND non-complex-karyotype is a speculative compound subgroup not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across candidate modifiers (sex, secondary_aml, unfit_for_intensive, complex_karyotype, FLT3, IDH, NPM1, TP53, age, ECOG, albumin, LDH, blast%, WBC), at least one significantly modifies the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: An omnibus claim that at least one of many candidate modifiers alters venetoclax-azacitidine response is essentially guaranteed by known TP53/karyotype/NPM1 effects.

judgments.text: npm1_mutation strongly amplifies the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Positive NPM1 × venetoclax-azacitidine interaction reflects established subgroup response data and is not novel beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: tp53_mutation suppresses the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Negative TP53 × venetoclax-azacitidine interaction is the standard guideline-recognized resistance pattern.

judgments.text: complex_karyotype suppresses the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Negative complex-karyotype × venetoclax-azacitidine interaction reflects known adverse cytogenetic resistance and is part of standard risk stratification.

judgments.text: At least one feature significantly modifies the treatment_midostaurin effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Beyond the established FLT3-mutation requirement for midostaurin, a claim of additional significant effect modifiers is speculative and not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: At least one feature significantly modifies the treatment_gilteritinib effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Effect modification of gilteritinib by FLT3-ITD is the established paradigm in relapsed/refractory AML and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: At least one feature significantly modifies the treatment_ivosidenib effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Effect modification of ivosidenib response by IDH1 mutation status is the standard guideline-level indication for the drug.

judgments.text: At least one feature significantly modifies the treatment_enasidenib effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Effect modification of enasidenib response by IDH2 mutation status is the standard guideline-level indication for the drug.

judgments.text: At least one feature significantly modifies the treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that some unspecified feature significantly modifies 7+3 response goes beyond standard guideline statements about fitness and cytogenetics and is an open data-driven claim.

judgments.text: Within idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib does NOT increase objective_response in any obvious subgroup defined by tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype, secondary_aml, or unfit_for_intensive.
judgments.rationale: Asserting ivosidenib fails to benefit any obvious IDH1-mutant subgroup contradicts the guideline expectation that IDH1-mutant AML responds to ivosidenib and is a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=1, treatment_gilteritinib does NOT increase objective_response in any obvious subgroup defined by tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype, secondary_aml, or unfit_for_intensive.
judgments.rationale: Asserting gilteritinib fails to benefit any obvious FLT3-ITD subgroup contradicts the guideline expectation of benefit in FLT3-ITD AML and is a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit), treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response in patients with complex_karyotype=0 but not in complex_karyotype=1.
judgments.rationale: A specific subgroup-conditional claim that 7+3 helps fit patients only when karyotype is non-complex is a data-driven refinement beyond textbook fitness/risk statements.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all available patient features simultaneously, the strongest independent predictors of objective_response include npm1_mutation (positive), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive), unfit_for_intensive (positive), ecog_ps (negative), tp53_mutation (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), blast_pct_marrow (negative), wbc_k_per_ul (negative), and crp_mg_l (negative).
judgments.rationale: The specific multivariable ranking and direction across this combined set of clinical, molecular, and lab covariates is a cross-pathway empirical claim that would not appear verbatim in a guideline.

judgments.text: After modeling the targeted treatment×biomarker interactions (mido×flt3_itd, gilt×flt3_itd, ivo×idh1, ena×idh2, ven/aza×unfit, ven/aza×tp53, ven/aza×complex_karyotype), only the venetoclax_azacitidine×unfit_for_intensive interaction is positive and large; the targeted-agent×biomarker interactions are null.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that all targeted-agent×biomarker interactions are null except ven/aza×unfit contradicts established FLT3 and IDH targeted-therapy paradigms and is a strong non-standard cross-interaction claim.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in the unfit_for_intensive=1 subgroup (taken alone).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax+azacitidine improving response in unfit AML patients is the established guideline-level standard of care for that population.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in the strict subgroup unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (more so than in unfit_for_intensive=1 alone).
judgments.rationale: VIALE-A and current AML guidelines establish that ven/aza works best in unfit patients without TP53 mutation or complex karyotype.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 patients with tp53_mutation=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutated AML's poor response to ven/aza is a well-established guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 patients with complex_karyotype=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse predictor for ven/aza response is established in guidelines and VIALE-A subgroup analyses.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 patients with npm1_mutation=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine markedly increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a strong favorable predictor of ven/aza response is well-documented in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 patients with npm1_mutation=0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has essentially no benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support broader benefit beyond NPM1+ (e.g., IDH1/2-mutant), so claiming essentially zero benefit in NPM1-wildtype unfit AML contradicts standard consensus.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=0 patients with npm1_mutation=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional claim about fit NPM1+ patients lacking ven/aza benefit is not addressed in standard guidelines and goes beyond consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response only in the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this exact 4-feature subgroup the effect is null. tp53_mutation=1 and complex_karyotype=1 each suppress the effect within unfit&npm1+ patients.
judgments.rationale: A strict 4-feature conjunctive signature as the sole responsive subgroup is a speculative cross-feature claim not present in any guideline.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × signature interaction (signature=unfit_for_intensive=1 & npm1_mutation=1 & tp53_mutation=0 & complex_karyotype=0) remains highly significant after adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, secondary_aml, FLT3, IDH, albumin, LDH, WBC, blast%, NLR, CRP, weight loss.
judgments.rationale: Persistence of a 4-way interaction after adjusting for 14 covariates is a methodological/mechanistic claim outside guideline scope.

judgments.text: Dropping any single component of the 4-feature signature (i.e., expanding the subgroup along any one axis) reduces the in-subgroup ven/aza absolute treatment effect, demonstrating that all four conditions are required to define the responsive subgroup.
judgments.rationale: The claim that all four features are individually necessary to define responsiveness is a novel conjunctive-criterion finding beyond consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response only in the strict subgroup defined jointly by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (ORR ~78.6% on vs ~15.8% off, diff +0.628, p<<0.001). Outside this 4-feature subgroup the effect on objective_response is null (diff ~ -0.003). tp53_mutation=1 and complex_karyotype=1 each independently suppress the ven/aza response within unfit & npm1+ patients (i.e., both 'unfavorable' values must be ABSENT for the treatment to work).
judgments.rationale: The composite final claim that ven/aza works only within a strict 4-feature signature with quantitative effect estimates exceeds anything in guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: FINAL: For treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, and treatment_7plus3, no clinically meaningful objective_response-improving subgroup is detectable in this dataset (no biomarker-matched ORR benefit in any obvious subgroup; effects are at most small and not consistently positive).
judgments.rationale: A null/negative finding that no biomarker-matched ORR benefit is detectable contradicts established AML guidelines (e.g., midostaurin/gilteritinib for FLT3-mutated, ivosidenib for IDH1-mutated, enasidenib for IDH2-mutated AML) and reflects dataset limitations rather than a novel biological insight.

judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is a well-established favorable prognostic marker in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with a lower objective_response rate.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a higher objective_response rate.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine producing high response rates in AML is established standard-of-care knowledge in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction achieving high complete response rates in AML is foundational textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's benefit in AML is well-established and guideline-endorsed for FLT3-mutated disease.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is an approved FLT3 inhibitor for relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML per guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.

judgments.text: Among FLT3-ITD+ patients, treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response relative to no midostaurin (canonical biology).
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD response to midostaurin is canonical and explicitly noted in the hypothesis itself.

judgments.text: Among FLT3-TKD+ patients, treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response relative to no midostaurin.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's RATIFY trial and labeling cover both FLT3-ITD and FLT3-TKD, making this guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Among FLT3-ITD+ patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response relative to no gilteritinib.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib efficacy in FLT3-ITD AML is established by ADMIRAL and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among FLT3-TKD+ patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response relative to no gilteritinib.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is approved for both FLT3-ITD and FLT3-TKD mutations, a standard guideline association.

judgments.text: Among IDH1-mutated patients, treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response relative to no ivosidenib.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib in IDH1-mutated AML is the textbook indication for this drug.

judgments.text: Among IDH2-mutated patients, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response relative to no enasidenib.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib in IDH2-mutated AML is the textbook indication for this drug.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients and absent in unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (treatment-by-fitness interaction).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the guideline-endorsed standard for AML patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy, so this fitness-restricted benefit is established paradigm.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect is concentrated in npm1_mutation=1 patients vs npm1_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated AML showing strong response to venetoclax-azacitidine is well-established in VIALE-A and current NCCN/ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect is suppressed by tp53_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutations attenuating venetoclax-azacitidine response is documented in VIALE-A subgroup analyses and reflected in guideline discussions.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect is suppressed by complex_karyotype=1.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype/adverse cytogenetics predicting poorer venetoclax-azacitidine response is established in the AML literature and ELN risk stratification.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all features in a multivariable logistic regression, the strongest independent positive predictors of objective_response are npm1_mutation, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, unfit_for_intensive, and albumin_g_dl; strongest negative are ecog_ps, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, blast_pct_marrow, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, wbc_k_per_ul.
judgments.rationale: These predictors (NPM1, performance status, TP53, complex karyotype, blast burden, inflammatory markers, albumin) are standard prognostic factors in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: In an adjusted logistic regression that adds one treatment-by-feature interaction at a time, only treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine shows large, significant interactions with unfit_for_intensive (positive), npm1_mutation (positive), tp53_mutation (negative), and complex_karyotype (negative); other treatment interactions are absent or marginal.
judgments.rationale: The pattern of ven/aza-specific interactions with fitness, NPM1, TP53, and complex karyotype recapitulates established AML treatment-effect modifiers in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a much larger increase in objective_response than in any other unfit/npm1 cell.
judgments.rationale: Claiming a specific interaction-cell maximum within the unfit-AND-NPM1-mutated subgroup is a finer-grained subgroup-conditional claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 patients, the ven/aza response benefit is abolished if tp53_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional claim that TP53 abolishes benefit specifically within unfit/NPM1-mutated patients goes beyond textbook-level main-effect statements.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 patients, the ven/aza response benefit is abolished if complex_karyotype=1.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional claim that complex karyotype abolishes benefit specifically within unfit/NPM1-mutated patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional hypothesis not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The ven/aza response benefit is concentrated in the joint subgroup unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this exact subgroup the ven/aza effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A four-way joint subgroup defining the exclusive locus of ven/aza benefit, with null effect outside it, is a speculative cross-pathway claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The unfit_for_intensive=1 predicate is necessary in the responder subgroup: among fit (unfit=0) patients with npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0, ven/aza has no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza efficacy in unfit NPM1-mutant TP53-wildtype non-complex AML aligns with established VIALE-A guideline consensus for older/unfit AML.

judgments.text: Within unfit+npm1+tp53wt+non-complex, the ven/aza effect is robust across age, sex, ECOG, FLT3-ITD, FLT3-TKD, IDH1, IDH2, and secondary AML status (no further effect modifiers); other treatments do not have a comparable effect inside this subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no effect modification by FLT3/IDH/secondary AML status within this specific subgroup, and exclusivity vs other treatments, is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Within the responder subgroup unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0, after adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, FLT3-ITD, FLT3-TKD, IDH1, IDH2, secondary AML, WBC, blast_pct_marrow, albumin, LDH, and the other treatment indicators, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains a strong independent positive predictor of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Independent benefit of ven/aza after adjustment in unfit NPM1+/TP53wt/non-complex AML restates the known guideline-endorsed efficacy in this population.

judgments.text: Among complex_karyotype=1 patients, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a lower objective_response rate than no 7+3 (treatment is less effective in chemorefractory karyotypes).
judgments.rationale: Reduced response to 7+3 in complex karyotype AML is well-established adverse-risk guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment_enasidenib has a more positive effect on objective_response in female (sex_female=1) than male patients (sex_female=0).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differential efficacy of enasidenib is not part of any IDH2-mutant AML treatment guideline and would be a novel pharmacogenomic claim.

judgments.text: Among the targeted therapies (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) and 7+3, no clean two-feature subgroup shows a treatment effect comparable in magnitude or biological plausibility to the ven/aza responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A comparative claim that no two-feature subgroup yields comparable effects for other AML therapies is a cross-pathway analytic assertion not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_gilteritinib is more effective in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients than in fit patients.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib guidelines specify FLT3-mutated relapsed/refractory AML, not fitness-conditional efficacy, so a fitness-based effect modifier would be novel.

judgments.text: Treatment_enasidenib is more effective in unfit_for_intensive=1 AND idh2_mutation=1 patients than in other patients.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is guideline-indicated for IDH2-mutant AML often in unfit/relapsed settings, so enhanced effect in unfit IDH2+ patients reflects established use.

judgments.text: When restricted to patients OUTSIDE the unfit+npm1+tp53wt+non-complex subgroup, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response (effect estimate near zero, not significant).
judgments.rationale: Asserting ven/aza has zero effect outside this precise four-predicate subgroup contradicts broader guideline use and is a speculative restrictive claim.

judgments.text: Removing any single predicate from {unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0} eliminates the ven/aza response benefit, demonstrating all four predicates are necessary.
judgments.rationale: Necessity of each of four specific predicates for ven/aza benefit is a strong mechanistic subgroup claim not reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across the four targeted therapies (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib), there is no biologically expected mutation-targeted response subgroup in this dataset; their effects in target-mutation subpopulations are null or numerically reversed.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that established mutation-targeted agents (midostaurin/FLT3, gilteritinib/FLT3, ivosidenib/IDH1, enasidenib/IDH2) show null or reversed effects in their target-mutation subgroups contradicts guideline consensus and is a non-standard, dataset-specific claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is concentrated in the joint subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0, where ven/aza increases response from ~16% to ~79% (raw diff +63 pp; adjusted OR ~21). Outside this exact subgroup, the ven/aza effect on objective_response is null.
judgments.rationale: A highly specific four-way conditional subgroup (unfit + NPM1+ + TP53− + non-complex karyotype) driving essentially all ven/aza response benefit, with null effect elsewhere, is a speculative cross-pathway interaction claim not found in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: For the other treatments (treatment_7plus3, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) no responder subgroup with a treatment effect comparable in magnitude or robustness to the ven/aza subgroup is identifiable. The strongest secondary signals are (a) treatment_7plus3 is less effective in complex_karyotype=1 patients (interaction coef -0.23, p=8e-4) and (b) treatment_enasidenib in IDH2-mutated unfit_for_intensive=1 patients shows a small +6 pp response increase (p~0.07), neither of which approaches the ven/aza signal.
judgments.rationale: The specific quantitative interaction claims — 7+3 underperformance restricted to complex karyotype and a small enasidenib benefit confined to IDH2-mutant unfit patients — are dataset-specific subgroup-conditional findings beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd-NEGATIVE patients, treatment_gilteritinib does NOT improve objective_response (effect concentrated in flt3_itd+).
