judgments.text: Within flt3_tkd-NEGATIVE patients, treatment_gilteritinib does NOT improve objective_response (effect concentrated in flt3_tkd+).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's indication is FLT3-mutated AML (ITD or TKD), so this matches guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within idh1_mutation-NEGATIVE patients, treatment_ivosidenib does NOT improve objective_response (effect concentrated in idh1_mutation+).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's efficacy being concentrated in IDH1-mutant patients is the established label and guideline standard.

judgments.text: Within idh2_mutation-NEGATIVE patients, treatment_enasidenib does NOT improve objective_response (effect concentrated in idh2_mutation+).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's efficacy being concentrated in IDH2-mutant patients is the established label and guideline standard.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_midostaurin x flt3_itd interaction on objective_response (effect of treatment_midostaurin larger in flt3_itd+).
judgments.rationale: Positive midostaurin x FLT3-ITD interaction reflects the established RATIFY-based indication.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_midostaurin x flt3_tkd interaction on objective_response (effect of treatment_midostaurin larger in flt3_tkd+).
judgments.rationale: Positive midostaurin x FLT3-TKD interaction reflects the established FLT3-mutated AML indication.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_gilteritinib x flt3_itd interaction on objective_response (effect of treatment_gilteritinib larger in flt3_itd+).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's enhanced efficacy in FLT3-ITD+ AML is the established indication and appears in standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_gilteritinib x flt3_tkd interaction on objective_response (effect of treatment_gilteritinib larger in flt3_tkd+).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is FDA-approved and guideline-recommended for FLT3-mutated AML including TKD mutations, making this a known association.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_ivosidenib x idh1_mutation interaction on objective_response (effect of treatment_ivosidenib larger in idh1_mutation+).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's selective efficacy in IDH1-mutant AML is its labeled indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_enasidenib x idh2_mutation interaction on objective_response (effect of treatment_enasidenib larger in idh2_mutation+).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's selective efficacy in IDH2-mutant AML is its labeled indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, mutations, fitness, and labs, treatment_midostaurin has an independent effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's benefit in AML (in FLT3-mutated disease with chemotherapy) is established and appears in guidelines as a standard treatment effect.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, mutations, fitness, and labs, treatment_gilteritinib has an independent effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's independent efficacy in AML is established in guidelines for FLT3-mutated relapsed/refractory disease.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, mutations, fitness, and labs, treatment_ivosidenib has an independent effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's independent efficacy is established in guidelines for IDH1-mutated AML.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, mutations, fitness, and labs, treatment_enasidenib has an independent effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's independent efficacy is established in guidelines for IDH2-mutated AML.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, mutations, fitness, and labs, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has an independent effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine's efficacy in AML (especially older/unfit patients) is a guideline-standard regimen.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, mutations, fitness, and labs, treatment_7plus3 has an independent effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction chemotherapy's efficacy in AML is the longstanding standard of care reflected in all guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by age_years (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Age is a standard stratification factor for venetoclax-azacitidine in AML and appears in guidelines for older/unfit patients.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by ecog_ps (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a standard eligibility and prognostic factor used in venetoclax-azacitidine treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by sex_female (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of venetoclax-azacitidine response is not an established guideline association in AML.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by tp53_mutation (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation is a well-known adverse predictor reducing benefit from venetoclax-azacitidine and appears in NCCN/ELN guidance.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by complex_karyotype (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a recognized adverse-risk feature associated with diminished venetoclax-azacitidine response in guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by npm1_mutation (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is an established favorable predictor of response to venetoclax-azacitidine reflected in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by flt3_itd (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD is a known modifier (generally less favorable) of venetoclax-azacitidine response reflected in guideline risk stratification.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by flt3_tkd (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD specifically as a response modifier for venetoclax-azacitidine is not established in guidelines, which typically lump FLT3 or focus on ITD.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by idh1_mutation (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation is a recognized favorable predictor of venetoclax-azacitidine response reflected in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by idh2_mutation (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation is a recognized favorable predictor of venetoclax-azacitidine response reflected in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by secondary_aml (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML is a well-established negative prognostic factor for venetoclax-azacitidine response and is discussed in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by unfit_for_intensive (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is specifically indicated for patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy per guidelines, so this subgroup framing reflects standard paradigm.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by albumin_g_dl (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as an effect modifier of venetoclax-azacitidine response is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by wbc_k_per_ul (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Baseline WBC modifying venetoclax-azacitidine response (beyond cytoreduction practice) is not an established predictive marker in guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by blast_pct_marrow (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Marrow blast burden is a routinely considered prognostic feature in AML and its influence on venetoclax-azacitidine outcomes is part of standard clinical understanding.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by crp_mg_l (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: CRP as a predictive effect modifier of venetoclax-azacitidine response is not part of established AML treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by hemoglobin_g_dl (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Baseline hemoglobin as an effect modifier of venetoclax-azacitidine efficacy is not a recognized predictive biomarker in guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is modified by ldh_u_l (interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: LDH as a predictive effect modifier specifically for venetoclax-azacitidine response is not established in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Within patients with ecog_ps==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response in fit/good-performance-status AML patients is consistent with established efficacy data.

judgments.text: Within patients with ecog_ps==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response in ECOG 1 patients reflects the standard guideline-supported indication.

judgments.text: Within patients with ecog_ps==2, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the guideline-endorsed standard for unfit AML patients including those with ECOG PS 2, so improved response is expected.

judgments.text: Within patients with tp53_mutation==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: TP53-wildtype AML responds well to venetoclax-azacitidine per standard guidelines and the VIALE-A trial.

judgments.text: Within patients with tp53_mutation==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Although TP53-mutant AML has poorer outcomes, venetoclax-azacitidine is still the guideline-recommended option for unfit patients with TP53 mutations and produces responses.

judgments.text: Within patients with complex_karyotype==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improves response in non-complex karyotype AML, consistent with guideline-level evidence from VIALE-A.

judgments.text: Within patients with complex_karyotype==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is recommended even in complex karyotype/adverse-risk AML and produces responses, per current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Within patients with npm1_mutation==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Improved response with venetoclax-azacitidine in NPM1-wildtype AML is consistent with VIALE-A and standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Within patients with npm1_mutation==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated AML is a well-established highly responsive subgroup to venetoclax-azacitidine per guidelines.

judgments.text: Within patients with unfit_for_intensive==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is not guideline-endorsed for fit patients eligible for intensive chemotherapy, so a response benefit in this subgroup would be a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Within patients with unfit_for_intensive==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response in unfit AML patients is the guideline-defined standard-of-care indication.

judgments.text: Within patients with secondary_aml==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improves response in de novo (non-secondary) AML per VIALE-A and standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Within patients with secondary_aml==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is established standard for unfit AML including secondary AML per VIALE-A and current NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Within patients with flt3_itd==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine benefit in FLT3-ITD wild-type AML is well-established in VIALE-A subgroup analyses and guidelines.

judgments.text: Within patients with flt3_itd==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine activity in FLT3-ITD-mutated AML is documented in VIALE-A and reflected in guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: Within patients with idh1_mutation==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Benefit of venetoclax-azacitidine in IDH1-wildtype AML is established in pivotal trial subgroup data.

judgments.text: Within patients with idh1_mutation==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: IDH1-mutant AML response to venetoclax-azacitidine is a well-known guideline-level association with high response rates.

judgments.text: Within patients with idh2_mutation==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Benefit in IDH2-wildtype AML is part of established VIALE-A evidence and guideline use.

judgments.text: Within patients with idh2_mutation==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine raises objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: IDH2-mutant AML response to venetoclax-azacitidine is well-established with notably high response rates in guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients who are unfit_for_intensive==1 AND npm1_mutation==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine markedly increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated unfit AML responding well to venetoclax-azacitidine is a guideline-recognized high-response subgroup.

judgments.text: In patients who are unfit_for_intensive==1 AND npm1_mutation==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine markedly increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: This specific four-way conditional subgroup (unfit + NPM1+ + TP53wt + non-complex karyotype) defining markedly enhanced response is more granular than guideline-level statements.

judgments.text: Outside the unfit & npm1+ subgroup, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine provides no objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no benefit outside the unfit/NPM1+ subgroup contradicts guideline consensus that venetoclax-azacitidine benefits a broader unfit AML population.

judgments.text: In TP53-mutated patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine does NOT increase objective_response (TP53 suppresses ven/aza benefit).
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutated AML having poor response to venetoclax/azacitidine is well-established in guidelines and recent literature.

judgments.text: In complex_karyotype+ patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine does NOT increase objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype (often co-occurring with TP53) predicting poor ven/aza response is established consensus.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by age_years (interaction logit term).
judgments.rationale: Age modifying response to 7+3 induction is a foundational, guideline-level concept in AML treatment selection.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by ecog_ps (interaction logit term).

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by flt3_tkd (interaction logit term).

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by idh1_mutation (interaction logit term).

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by idh2_mutation (interaction logit term).

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by secondary_aml (interaction logit term).
judgments.rationale: Secondary/therapy-related AML having worse response to 7+3 (favoring CPX-351) is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by unfit_for_intensive (interaction logit term).
judgments.rationale: Unfit-for-intensive patients having worse outcomes with 7+3 induction is foundational guideline-level knowledge driving venetoclax-based regimen selection.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by albumin_g_dl (interaction logit term).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a specific modifier of 7+3 response (beyond general prognostic value) is not a guideline-level treatment-effect modifier.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by wbc_k_per_ul (interaction logit term).
judgments.rationale: WBC count specifically modifying 7+3 response effect is not a standard guideline treatment-selection criterion beyond hyperleukocytosis management.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_7plus3 is modified by blast_pct_marrow (interaction logit term).
judgments.rationale: Marrow blast percentage as an interaction modifier of 7+3 efficacy is not established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutant AML responsiveness to venetoclax-azacitidine is a well-recognized association in current AML guidelines and reviews.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by unfit==1 & npm1==1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Combined unfit and NPM1+ subgroup response to venetoclax-azacitidine reflects the established guideline indication overlapping a known favorable molecular marker.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by unfit & npm1+ & tp53-, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: The specific triple-conditional subgroup (unfit, NPM1+, TP53-) defining differential ven-aza response is a refined cross-pathway claim beyond guideline-level statements.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & CK-, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conditional subgroup adding absence of complex karyotype to NPM1+/TP53-/unfit is a speculative refinement not present in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by NOT(unfit & npm1+) [complement], treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Asserting differential ven-aza efficacy in the complement of unfit-and-NPM1+ patients is a non-standard subgroup framing not addressed in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by unfit & tp53+ (suppressor present), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutant unfit AML is generally considered ven-aza-resistant, so claiming a distinct response signal in this subgroup is a non-guideline, speculative claim.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by unfit & CK+ (suppressor present), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine in unfit patients with complex karyotype plus a suppressor mutation is a specific subgroup-conditional claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by fit & npm1+ (no benefit expected), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate vs no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Suggesting venetoclax-azacitidine has a different response rate in fit NPM1+ patients (where intensive chemo is standard) is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within CK==0, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 efficacy in non-complex-karyotype AML is the standard guideline-endorsed setting where intensive induction is expected to work.

judgments.text: Within CK==1, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: While complex karyotype is known to be adverse, a specific differential response signal for 7+3 within CK+ as a treatment-effect modifier is a non-trivial subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Within CK==0 & fit, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Fit, non-complex-karyotype AML is the canonical guideline population for 7+3 induction.

judgments.text: Within CK==0 & fit & npm1+, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Fit NPM1+ non-CK patients are a textbook favorable-risk group expected to respond to 7+3 induction.

judgments.text: Within CK==0 & tp53==0, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Non-CK, TP53 wild-type AML is the standard favorable setting for 7+3 response per guidelines.

judgments.text: Within CK==1 & tp53==1 (very poor risk), treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: A specific treatment-effect signal for 7+3 in the CK+/TP53-mutant ultra-poor-risk subgroup (where guidelines generally discourage intensive chemo) is a non-trivial subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: In flt3_itd+ patients, after adjustment for case-mix, treatment_midostaurin has no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-ITD+ AML is established guideline standard; claiming no effect contradicts but still concerns the canonical paradigm rather than offering a novel mechanism.

judgments.text: In flt3_tkd+ patients, after adjustment for case-mix, treatment_midostaurin has no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD is a less-emphasized midostaurin indication and a claim of no adjusted effect specifically in TKD+ patients goes beyond standard guideline framing that lumps ITD and TKD together.

judgments.text: In flt3_itd+ patients, after adjustment for case-mix, treatment_gilteritinib has no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in FLT3-ITD+ AML is established guideline standard; testing this association is not novel.

judgments.text: In flt3_tkd+ patients, after adjustment for case-mix, treatment_gilteritinib has no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is FDA-approved and guideline-recommended for FLT3-TKD+ AML; this is a standard known association.

judgments.text: In idh1_mutation+ patients, after adjustment for case-mix, treatment_ivosidenib has no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib in IDH1-mutant AML is a guideline-level established association.

judgments.text: In idh2_mutation+ patients, after adjustment for case-mix, treatment_enasidenib has no effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib in IDH2-mutant AML is a guideline-level established association.

judgments.text: In patients with albumin_g_dl ABOVE median (3.80), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Albumin-stratified differential response to venetoclax/azacitidine is not a guideline-level paradigm and represents a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: In patients with albumin_g_dl AT-OR-BELOW median (3.80), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Differential ven/aza response in low-albumin patients is not established in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients with blast_pct_marrow ABOVE median (60.00), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.

judgments.text: In patients with blast_pct_marrow AT-OR-BELOW median (60.00), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Differential ven/aza efficacy by marrow blast percentage is not a guideline-level established predictor.

judgments.text: In patients with wbc_k_per_ul ABOVE median (12.20), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.

judgments.text: In patients with wbc_k_per_ul AT-OR-BELOW median (12.20), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Differential ven/aza response in low-WBC patients is not a guideline-recognized predictive subgroup.

judgments.text: In patients with crp_mg_l ABOVE median (3.31), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-specific response to venetoclax-azacitidine based on elevated CRP is not in current AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients with crp_mg_l AT-OR-BELOW median (3.31), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Differential response to venetoclax-azacitidine in patients with low/normal CRP is not an established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: In patients with hemoglobin_g_dl ABOVE median (12.50), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Hemoglobin-stratified response to venetoclax-azacitidine is not part of standard AML treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: In patients with hemoglobin_g_dl AT-OR-BELOW median (12.50), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Low-hemoglobin subgroup-conditional response to venetoclax-azacitidine is not described in guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients with ldh_u_l ABOVE median (210.26), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: LDH-stratified differential response to venetoclax-azacitidine is not a guideline-recognized predictor.

judgments.text: In patients with ldh_u_l AT-OR-BELOW median (210.26), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Response to venetoclax-azacitidine conditioned on low/normal LDH is not part of established AML treatment consensus.

judgments.text: In patients with nlr ABOVE median (3.00), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: NLR-stratified response to venetoclax-azacitidine is not an established biomarker in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients with nlr AT-OR-BELOW median (3.00), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Low-NLR conditional response to venetoclax-azacitidine is not a guideline-level association.

judgments.text: In patients with calcium_mg_dl ABOVE median (9.40), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Calcium-stratified response to venetoclax-azacitidine is not described in standard AML treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: In patients with calcium_mg_dl AT-OR-BELOW median (9.40), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Low/normal calcium conditional response to venetoclax-azacitidine is not part of guideline-based AML care.

judgments.text: Within fit & CK- & tp53-, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Fit, non-complex-karyotype, TP53-wildtype AML responding to 7+3 induction is the textbook favorable/intermediate-risk standard of care.

judgments.text: Within fit & CK- & tp53- & npm1+, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated, TP53-wildtype, non-complex karyotype AML in fit patients receiving 7+3 is established favorable-risk guideline therapy.

judgments.text: Within fit & CK- & tp53- & npm1-, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning on fit/CK-/TP53-/NPM1- isolates an intermediate-risk slice where differential 7+3 response versus alternatives is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: Within fit & ECOG<=1, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Fit patients with ECOG<=1 receiving 7+3 induction is the canonical eligible population in AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within CK==1 (poor risk — 7+3 may harm), treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype AML having poor response to 7+3 is well-established adverse-risk guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within tp53==1 (poor risk), treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutant AML's poor response to intensive 7+3 chemotherapy is established in current ELN/NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Within age<60, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Younger (<60) AML patients receiving 7+3 induction is the standard guideline population with expected response.

judgments.text: Within age>=70, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Reduced benefit/tolerability of 7+3 in patients >=70 is well-known and drives guideline preference for hypomethylating-agent-based regimens.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x treatment_midostaurin interaction on objective_response (combination differs from sum of individual effects).
judgments.rationale: A specific synergistic or antagonistic interaction between venetoclax/azacitidine and midostaurin on response is not guideline-established and represents a speculative cross-regimen claim.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x treatment_gilteritinib interaction on objective_response (combination differs from sum of individual effects).
judgments.rationale: An interaction effect between venetoclax/azacitidine and gilteritinib on response goes beyond guideline consensus, which treats these as separate FLT3-directed strategies rather than a characterized combination.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x treatment_ivosidenib interaction on objective_response (combination differs from sum of individual effects).
judgments.rationale: Pairwise interaction between ven/aza and ivosidenib on response is not a standard guideline claim; ven/aza vs IDH1 inhibitor comparisons exist but explicit interaction effects are not paradigm.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x treatment_enasidenib interaction on objective_response (combination differs from sum of individual effects).
judgments.rationale: Interaction between ven/aza and enasidenib on response goes beyond guideline consensus, which treats them as separate options without specifying interaction direction.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x treatment_7plus3 interaction on objective_response (combination differs from sum of individual effects).
judgments.rationale: An explicit interaction effect between ven/aza and 7+3 on response is not a guideline statement; guidelines stratify by fitness rather than model cross-regimen interactions.

judgments.text: In subgroup [unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & CK-], treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine adjusted effect (logit OR) on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza benefit in unfit, NPM1-mutated, TP53-wildtype, non-complex-karyotype AML is well-established favorable subgroup behavior reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: In subgroup [complement of clean+favorable], treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine adjusted effect (logit OR) on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Effect of ven/aza in the complement of a custom 'clean+favorable' subgroup is a non-standard slice not addressed by guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive 3-way interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation x unfit_for_intensive on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A formal positive 3-way interaction among ven/aza, NPM1, and unfit status is a mechanistic/statistical claim beyond the guideline-level observation that NPM1-mutated unfit patients respond well.

judgments.text: Negative 3-way interaction treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation x tp53_mutation on objective_response (TP53 wipes out NPM1+ benefit).
judgments.rationale: While TP53 mutation is known to predict poor ven/aza response, an explicit negative 3-way interaction wiping out NPM1+ benefit is a more specific cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Define S = (unfit_for_intensive==1 & npm1_mutation==1 & tp53_mutation==0 & complex_karyotype==0). In an adjusted logistic regression, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interacts positively with S on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A positive interaction between ven/aza and a composite favorable subgroup S in adjusted logistic regression is a specific statistical interaction claim beyond guideline phrasing.

judgments.text: Within S=1, adjusted treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is positive.
judgments.rationale: Positive ven/aza response in unfit NPM1+/TP53-wt/non-CK AML aligns with established guideline expectations (e.g., VIALE-A subgroup data).

judgments.text: Within S=0, adjusted treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is null.
judgments.rationale: A null adjusted effect of ven/aza outside this favorable composite subgroup is a stronger claim than guidelines make, since ven/aza shows benefit broadly in unfit AML beyond just NPM1+/TP53-wt.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is a well-established predictor of favorable response to venetoclax+azacitidine and appears in NCCN/ELN AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x unfit_for_intensive interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax+azacitidine is the guideline-standard regimen specifically indicated for unfit AML patients (VIALE-A), so this association is the core indication.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x tp53_mutation interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation is widely recognized in guidelines as conferring poor response to venetoclax+azacitidine.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x complex_karyotype interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is an established adverse-risk feature associated with poor venetoclax+azacitidine response and is reflected in ELN risk stratification.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_enasidenib x sex_female interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based heterogeneity in enasidenib response is not described in guidelines or standard treatment paradigms for IDH2-mutant AML.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_7plus3 x complex_karyotype interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a guideline-established adverse predictor of poor response to 7+3 induction chemotherapy.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_ivosidenib x npm1_mutation interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's predictive biomarker is IDH1 mutation; an NPM1-conditional response effect for ivosidenib is not part of guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_enasidenib x unfit_for_intensive interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's response paradigm is driven by IDH2 mutation status, not fitness; differential effect by fitness status is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_gilteritinib x unfit_for_intensive interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's predictive biomarker is FLT3 mutation; differential efficacy stratified by fitness status is not a guideline-recognized moderator.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: treatment_ivosidenib x unfit_for_intensive interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib (with or without azacitidine) is specifically indicated for unfit/newly diagnosed IDH1-mutant AML, making this interaction tautological with its indication.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in patients with unfit_for_intensive==1 AND npm1_mutation==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0 (and provides essentially no benefit outside this subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support venetoclax+azacitidine broadly in unfit AML; the narrow conditional restricting benefit to NPM1+/TP53wt/non-complex karyotype and claiming no benefit elsewhere is more specific than guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_7plus3 has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response in any subgroup tested; the only consistent signal is a small detrimental association in complex_karyotype==1 patients.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction producing meaningful CR rates is textbook standard of care; claiming no clinically meaningful effect in any subgroup contradicts established paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_midostaurin does NOT meaningfully change objective_response, even in the flt3_itd+ subgroup.
judgments.rationale: RATIFY-based guidelines establish midostaurin benefit in FLT3-ITD+ AML; claiming no meaningful effect contradicts guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_gilteritinib does NOT meaningfully change objective_response, even in the flt3_itd+ subgroup.
judgments.rationale: ADMIRAL trial and guidelines establish gilteritinib efficacy in FLT3-ITD+ relapsed/refractory AML; the null claim contradicts paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_ivosidenib does NOT meaningfully change objective_response, even in the idh1_mutation+ subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib has FDA approval and guideline endorsement for IDH1-mutant AML based on response data; the null claim contradicts established paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_enasidenib does NOT meaningfully change objective_response, even in the idh2_mutation+ subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib has guideline-supported activity in IDH2-mutant AML; claiming no meaningful response effect contradicts established consensus.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with lower probability of objective_response in this AML cohort.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting worse response in AML is well-established and appears in standard guidelines and risk stratification.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than males (sex_female=0).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in AML response are commonly examined as a baseline covariate and not a novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have lower objective_response than de novo AML patients (secondary_aml=0).
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having worse response than de novo AML is well-established and reflected in ELN risk stratification and guidelines.

judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse prognostic marker with lower response is a guideline-level ELN risk classification element.

judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD's association with AML response/prognosis is a textbook biomarker incorporated in ELN risk and targeted therapy decisions.

judgments.text: flt3_tkd=1 is associated with a different objective_response than flt3_tkd=0.

judgments.text: idh1_mutation=1 is associated with a different objective_response than idh1_mutation=0.

judgments.text: idh2_mutation=1 is associated with a different objective_response than idh2_mutation=0.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with HIGHER objective_response.

judgments.text: Higher ast_u_l (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST as a marker of poor performance/disease burden predicting worse treatment response is a standard clinical observation.

judgments.text: Higher alt_u_l (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT as a marker of poor performance/disease burden predicting worse treatment response is a standard clinical observation.

judgments.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hyperbilirubinemia indicating hepatic dysfunction and worse oncologic outcomes is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.
