judgments.text: Higher bun_mg_dl (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflecting renal impairment/dehydration and worse outcomes is a standard clinical association.

judgments.text: Higher sodium_meq_l (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hyponatremia is a well-known negative prognostic marker in oncology, so higher (normal) sodium associating with better response is established.

judgments.text: Higher potassium_meq_l (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A directional association between higher potassium and lower response is not a standard guideline-level claim and would require subgroup/mechanistic justification.

judgments.text: Higher calcium_mg_dl (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hypercalcemia is typically a poor prognostic sign in cancer, so higher calcium predicting BETTER response contradicts paradigm and would be novel if true.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo (a marker of disease burden / poor nutrition / organ stress) is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse treatment response is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_midostaurin=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_midostaurin=0 (univariate, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's effect on response in its approved indications (e.g., FLT3-mutant AML, advanced systemic mastocytosis) is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_gilteritinib=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_gilteritinib=0 (univariate, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in FLT3-mutated AML is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_ivosidenib=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_ivosidenib=0 (univariate, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's activity in IDH1-mutant AML is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_enasidenib=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_enasidenib=0 (univariate, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's activity in IDH2-mutant AML is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0 (univariate, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine response in AML is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_7plus3=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_7plus3=0 (univariate, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction response rates in AML are textbook standard.

judgments.text: After adjustment for demographics, comorbidity flags, mutations and labs, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 retains an independent positive association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Independent positive association of ven-aza with response is consistent with guideline-level evidence.

judgments.text: After adjustment for the same covariates, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, and treatment_7plus3 each have NO independent main-effect association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming targeted agents and 7+3 each lack independent main effects after adjustment is a non-obvious multivariable claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment for confounders, the unfit_for_intensive=1 main effect on objective_response BECOMES POSITIVE (sign flips), suggesting venetoclax-azacitidine confounding.
judgments.rationale: A sign-flip of unfit_for_intensive after confounder adjustment attributed to ven-aza confounding is a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Adjusted positive effect of npm1_mutation=1 on objective_response remains.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable response predictor in AML is textbook.

judgments.text: Adjusted negative effect of tp53_mutation=1 on objective_response remains.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as an adverse response predictor in AML is textbook.

judgments.text: Adjusted negative effect of higher ecog_ps on objective_response remains.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a well-established prognostic factor in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: In flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_midostaurin=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0; the treatment_midostaurin × flt3_itd interaction term in a logistic model with covariates is positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is established standard of care reflected in NCCN/ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: In flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response; logit interaction term treatment_gilteritinib:flt3_itd is positive.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib efficacy in FLT3-ITD AML is guideline-endorsed standard of care for relapsed/refractory disease.

judgments.text: In flt3_tkd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response; logit interaction term treatment_gilteritinib:flt3_tkd is positive.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib activity in FLT3-TKD mutated AML is established and reflected in its FDA label and guidelines.

judgments.text: In idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response; logit interaction term treatment_ivosidenib:idh1_mutation is positive.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib efficacy in IDH1-mutant AML is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed standard of care.

judgments.text: In idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response; logit interaction term treatment_enasidenib:idh2_mutation is positive.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib efficacy in IDH2-mutant AML is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed standard of care.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is LARGER in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in unfit_for_intensive=0 (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the established standard of care specifically for unfit/older AML patients per VIALE-A and guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is LARGER in npm1_mutation=1 than in npm1_mutation=0 (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: While NPM1-mutant AML responds well to venetoclax-azacitidine, a positive interaction effect specifically larger than in NPM1-wildtype is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard guideline framing.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is SMALLER (or absent) in tp53_mutation=1 than in tp53_mutation=0 (interaction term is negative).
judgments.rationale: Reduced venetoclax-azacitidine efficacy in TP53-mutated AML is well-recognized in guidelines and post-hoc VIALE-A analyses.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is SMALLER in complex_karyotype=1 than in complex_karyotype=0 (interaction term is negative).
judgments.rationale: Differential venetoclax-azacitidine effect by complex karyotype as an interaction (independent of TP53) is not a standard guideline claim.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × age_years interaction on objective_response is non-zero (effect size varies with age).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax+azacitidine is the standard regimen for older/unfit AML patients per VIALE-A; age-dependence aligns with guideline-level use.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is similar across sex_female and secondary_aml strata.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza is approved and effective in secondary AML and across sexes; lack of stratum-specific modification matches existing guideline expectations.

judgments.text: The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response declines with older age_years (treatment_7plus3 × age_years interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: Declining benefit of 7+3 induction with advancing age is established textbook knowledge driving fitness-based regimen selection.

judgments.text: The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response is smaller in unfit_for_intensive=1 than fit patients.
judgments.rationale: Reduced 7+3 benefit in unfit patients is essentially definitional and the basis for current fitness-based treatment algorithms.

judgments.text: The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response is smaller in complex_karyotype=1 than complex_karyotype=0 (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a guideline-recognized adverse cytogenetic risk factor predicting poor response to intensive chemotherapy.

judgments.text: The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response varies by npm1_mutation status.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is an ELN favorable-risk marker known to enhance response to intensive induction chemotherapy.

judgments.text: The treatment_7plus3 × {flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1, idh2, tp53, secondary_aml, sex_female, ecog_ps} interactions are not significant.
judgments.rationale: Null interactions with TP53 and FLT3-ITD contradict guideline-recognized adverse modifiers of 7+3 response, making this finding non-paradigmatic.

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 confers a positive response benefit specifically in npm1_mutation=1 patients (and possibly nowhere else).
judgments.rationale: Restricting midostaurin's FLT3-ITD benefit to the NPM1-comutated subset is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond RATIFY-based guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: Combining FLT3 mutations (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1), treatment_gilteritinib=1 yields HIGHER objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0 in this FLT3-mutated subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's superior response in FLT3-mutated AML is the established ADMIRAL-trial-based labeled indication.

judgments.text: Within idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib=1 confers a positive benefit at least in some sub-subset (e.g., npm1_mutation=0, tp53_mutation=0, unfit_for_intensive=1).
judgments.rationale: Confining ivosidenib benefit to a specific NPM1-/TP53-/unfit sub-subset within IDH1-mutant AML is a speculative subgroup claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib=1 confers a positive benefit at least in some sub-subset (e.g., unfit_for_intensive=1).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib benefit in IDH2-mutant AML, especially in unfit patients, is established and reflected in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: In tp53_mutation=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has NO objective_response benefit (rate diff ≈ 0); the benefit is concentrated in tp53_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines actually endorse venetoclax-azacitidine for unfit AML including TP53-mutant in many contexts; claiming zero benefit in TP53-mutant and concentration in TP53-wildtype is a stronger subgroup-restricted claim beyond standard consensus.

judgments.text: Even within tp53_mutation=0, the venetoclax-azacitidine response benefit is further attenuated when complex_karyotype=1.
judgments.rationale: A further attenuation of VEN-AZA benefit specifically in TP53-wildtype patients with complex karyotype is a conditional subgroup claim not part of standard guideline teaching.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine objective_response benefit is concentrated specifically in patients meeting the conjunction (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) (the "VEN-AZA supergroup"); outside this subgroup the benefit is zero.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conjunction defining a 'VEN-AZA supergroup' with zero benefit outside it is a speculative subgroup-restricted effect well beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In the simpler subgroup (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1), the venetoclax-azacitidine effect on objective_response is already very large; further restricting to tp53_mutation=0 and complex_karyotype=0 increases it further.
judgments.rationale: Claiming additional incremental benefit from layering TP53-wildtype and non-complex karyotype on top of unfit+NPM1 is a fine-grained subgroup interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In unfit_for_intensive=1 but npm1_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has NO objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Restricting VEN-AZA benefit to NPM1-mutant within the unfit population (no benefit in NPM1-wildtype unfit patients) contradicts the broad guideline indication for unfit AML.

judgments.text: In npm1_mutation=1 but unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has NO objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no VEN-AZA benefit in fit NPM1-mutant patients is a subgroup-conditional claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The triple-interaction term treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive × npm1_mutation in a logistic regression of objective_response (with main effects, two-way interactions, and other covariates) is positive and highly significant — formally confirming the synergistic subgroup structure observed in stratified analyses.
judgments.rationale: A formally significant three-way interaction among treatment, fitness, and NPM1 status in logistic regression is a mechanistic/statistical claim beyond standard guideline content.

judgments.text: In the full triple-interaction model, the marginal treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine main effect (i.e., for unfit=0, npm1=0) is no longer significant — the apparent overall main effect arose entirely from the unfit=1 ∩ npm1=1 subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the overall VEN-AZA main effect is entirely driven by the unfit∩NPM1 subgroup is a non-standard reattribution of treatment effect not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: The non-VEN-AZA treatments (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, treatment_7plus3) have NO objective_response benefit even when stratified by unfit_for_intensive.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, and 7+3 confer no response benefit even stratified by fitness contradicts established guideline-endorsed efficacy of these agents.

judgments.text: The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response is similar across age tertiles (no age-driven heterogeneity).
judgments.rationale: Null finding consistent with standard expectation that 7+3 efficacy is not strongly age-stratified within tertiles; not a guideline-departing claim.

judgments.text: Across the 6 treatments × 10 binary feature combinations, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × {npm1_mutation, unfit_for_intensive} interactions remain by far the largest positive (and tp53/complex_karyotype the largest negative) — a comprehensive interaction screen confirms VEN-AZA is the dominant heterogeneous-effect treatment.
judgments.rationale: A systematic interaction-screen claim that VEN-AZA is the singularly dominant heterogeneous-effect treatment across a full grid goes beyond guideline statements about VEN-AZA in unfit/NPM1 patients.

judgments.text: Targeted treatments (mido, gilt, ivo, ena) and 7+3 produce no major significant Tx × marker interactions in this systematic screen (apart from 7+3:complex_karyotype negative and a couple of marginal/spurious associations).
judgments.rationale: A blanket negative claim that targeted agents (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) show no meaningful Tx×marker interactions contradicts guideline expectations that these drugs act specifically in their biomarker-defined subgroups.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × blast_pct_marrow interaction on objective_response is positive (effect attenuates less, or even amplifies, at higher marrow blast burden).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not state that VEN-AZA benefit amplifies with higher marrow blast burden; this is a mechanistic subgroup claim beyond consensus.

judgments.text: Other continuous-lab × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interactions (wbc_k_per_ul, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl) on objective_response are not significant.
judgments.rationale: A systematic null across multiple continuous lab × VEN-AZA interactions (LDH, CRP, NLR, albumin, etc.) is not a guideline statement and constitutes a non-trivial cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: All four predicates of the VEN-AZA supergroup definition (unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0) are necessary: violating any single predicate (e.g., flipping unfit→0 OR npm1→0 OR tp53→1 OR complex_karyotype→1) eliminates the venetoclax-azacitidine objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: The conjunctive necessity of all four predicates (unfit AND NPM1+ AND TP53− AND non-CK) to obtain VEN-AZA benefit is a speculative supergroup definition beyond guideline indications.

judgments.text: Dropping a single predicate from the supergroup definition (e.g., {npm1=1, tp53=0, ck=0} without the unfit predicate) yields a smaller (but still positive) average venetoclax-azacitidine effect, because the dropped subgroup includes some NPM1+ patients who are not unfit and where VEN-AZA does not work.
judgments.rationale: The specific decompositional claim that dropping the unfit predicate dilutes but does not abolish VEN-AZA benefit is a subgroup-conditional finding not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In complex_karyotype=1 patients, treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with LOWER objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0 (paradoxical/futility signal in adverse cytogenetics).
judgments.rationale: While 7+3 is known to perform poorly in complex karyotype, an explicit paradoxical/futility signal where 7+3=1 yields lower response than 7+3=0 within CK+ patients is a stronger claim than guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 patients (favorable cytogenetics), treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with marginally HIGHER objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.
judgments.rationale: It is established that 7+3 works best in favorable-risk cytogenetics (TP53−, non-CK); higher response with 7+3 in this subgroup matches textbook expectation.

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_midostaurin=1 has NO independent positive effect on objective_response after adjusting for age, ECOG, and other key predictors (i.e., the textbook FLT3-targeting indication is not detectable in this dataset).
judgments.rationale: A negative finding that midostaurin shows no independent benefit in FLT3-ITD+ patients after adjustment directly contradicts guideline-endorsed RATIFY-based standard of care and is therefore a non-consensus claim.

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 has NO independent positive effect on objective_response after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse gilteritinib for FLT3-ITD AML based on response/survival benefit; claiming no independent positive effect after adjustment contradicts paradigm and is a non-standard speculative claim.

judgments.text: Within idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib=1 has NO positive (and possibly slightly negative) adjusted effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Standard paradigm holds ivosidenib produces responses in IDH1-mutant AML; asserting no/negative adjusted effect is a non-guideline, speculative reversal.

judgments.text: Within idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib=1 has NO independent positive effect on objective_response after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is guideline-endorsed for IDH2-mutant relapsed/refractory AML; claiming no independent benefit after adjustment is a speculative counter-paradigm claim.

judgments.text: Within the (unfit_for_intensive=1 ∩ npm1_mutation=1) subgroup, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response does NOT vary materially with age_years (no further age-driven heterogeneity).
judgments.rationale: Absence of age-driven heterogeneity within an unfit∩NPM1 subgroup for ven/aza is a subgroup-conditional, mechanistic claim not addressed in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the same subgroup, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × tp53_mutation interaction on objective_response is strongly NEGATIVE — TP53 mutation acts as a within-subgroup suppressor.
judgments.rationale: A within-subgroup negative TP53 interaction modifying ven/aza response is a stratified mechanistic claim beyond general TP53-poor-prognosis statements in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the same subgroup, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × complex_karyotype interaction on objective_response is strongly NEGATIVE — complex karyotype acts as a within-subgroup suppressor.
judgments.rationale: A within-subgroup complex-karyotype suppressor interaction with ven/aza is a stratified, conditional effect not standard in guideline text.

judgments.text: In npm1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with LOWER objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0 (i.e., the marginal ivosidenib × npm1 interaction observed in the screen reflects a small but real negative ivosidenib effect in NPM1+ patients).
judgments.rationale: A negative ivosidenib effect specifically in NPM1-mutant patients is a cross-pathway subgroup claim not part of consensus.

judgments.text: The marginal enasidenib × sex_female interaction reflects directionally opposite stratified effects: enasidenib lowers response in males (sex_female=0) and raises it in females (sex_female=1) — but both effect sizes are small and likely not clinically meaningful.
judgments.rationale: Sex-stratified opposite-direction enasidenib effects are a non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: In a logistic regression of objective_response on treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × supergroup (where supergroup = (unfit_for_intensive=1 & npm1_mutation=1 & tp53_mutation=0 & complex_karyotype=0)) plus other patient covariates, the venetoclax-azacitidine × supergroup interaction is positive and the venetoclax-azacitidine main effect (in the non-supergroup) is essentially zero — confirming the supergroup definition formally.
judgments.rationale: A formal composite-supergroup × ven/aza interaction with null main effect outside the supergroup is a multi-marker conditional model not in guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine objective_response benefit is similar in secondary_aml=1 and secondary_aml=0 (no secondary-AML modifier, in contrast to npm1 / tp53 / complex_karyotype / unfit_for_intensive).
judgments.rationale: Equivalence of ven/aza benefit across secondary vs de novo AML, contrasted against other modifiers, is a stratified comparative claim beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: In complex_karyotype=0 patients, after adjustment for age, ECOG, fitness, key mutations and labs, treatment_7plus3=1 has at most a small positive (and statistically marginal) effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: The claim that 7+3 chemotherapy has only a marginal effect on response in non-complex-karyotype AML reflects standard practice, as 7+3 is the established induction backbone with well-known modest benefit in this group.

judgments.text: Within the supergroup (unfit_for_intensive=1 ∩ npm1_mutation=1 ∩ tp53_mutation=0 ∩ complex_karyotype=0), the adjusted log-odds-ratio for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is large positive (≥ +2.5; OR ≥ ~12), with very small p-value, after controlling for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, FLT3/IDH mutations, and key labs.
judgments.rationale: A specific four-way conditional supergroup (unfit + NPM1+ + TP53wt + non-complex karyotype) defining a very large adjusted OR ≈12 for VEN-AZA response goes beyond guideline statements that simply recommend VEN-AZA for unfit AML.

judgments.text: Outside the supergroup (i.e., in the complement, n≈45,000), the adjusted treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is essentially ZERO (|β| < 0.05 on the logit scale, p > 0.4) — final negative-control confirmation that the entire VEN-AZA benefit is contained inside the (unfit_for_intensive=1 ∩ npm1_mutation=1 ∩ tp53_mutation=0 ∩ complex_karyotype=0) supergroup.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the entire VEN-AZA response benefit is contained within that exact four-feature supergroup with a true null effect outside is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup-exclusivity claim not present in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP CALL: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine improves objective_response substantially and exclusively in the subgroup defined by (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) — response 78.6% on vs 15.8% off (Δ ≈ +62.8 percentage points, adjusted OR ≈ 21). Outside this subgroup the effect is null. The two suppressors are tp53_mutation=1 and complex_karyotype=1; the two required positive predicates are unfit_for_intensive=1 and npm1_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: A composite 'final call' identifying NPM1 and unfit status as required positive predicates and TP53/complex karyotype as exclusive suppressors of VEN-AZA benefit, with quantitative response gap, exceeds guideline-level knowledge that merely notes TP53/complex karyotype as poor-prognosis.

judgments.text: Patients carrying npm1_mutation have a higher rate of objective_response than patients without npm1_mutation.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation predicting favorable response is well-established AML guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients carrying tp53_mutation have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without tp53_mutation.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as a poor-response marker in AML is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without complex_karyotype.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as adverse-risk with poor response is established ELN/NCCN guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have a higher rate of objective_response than fit patients (because the unfit subgroup is preferentially treated with venetoclax-based therapy).
judgments.rationale: Claiming unfit patients have higher response due to venetoclax selection is a treatment-allocation confounding mechanism not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary_aml have a different rate of objective_response than de novo patients.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having worse response than de novo is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Female sex is associated with a different rate of objective_response than male sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in AML response are not part of standard guideline-level paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG predicting worse response/outcomes is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage predicting lower response is not a standard guideline-level predictor in AML.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Quantitative 6-month weight loss as a response predictor is not in standard AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a continuous response predictor is not a guideline-endorsed AML biomarker.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated WBC at AML diagnosis as an adverse prognostic factor for response and survival is well-established in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: CRP as a predictor of objective response in AML is not part of standard treatment guideline consensus and goes beyond routine prognostic paradigms.

judgments.text: FLT3-ITD is associated with a different rate of objective_response than FLT3-ITD-negative status.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD's prognostic and predictive impact on response is a foundational, guideline-level association in AML.

judgments.text: IDH1 mutation is associated with a different rate of objective_response than IDH1-wild-type.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status as a response-relevant biomarker (e.g., for ivosidenib and prognostication) is established in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: IDH2 mutation is associated with a different rate of objective_response than IDH2-wild-type.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation status as a response-relevant biomarker (e.g., for enasidenib) is established in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Routine chemistries (sodium, potassium, calcium, creatinine, BUN, AST, ALT, total bilirubin) show no clinically meaningful association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A null claim that all routine chemistries lack meaningful association with response is a specific cross-panel assertion not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine producing higher response rates than no such treatment is standard guideline knowledge for AML.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 is associated with higher objective_response than no treatment_7plus3.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction yielding higher response than no induction chemotherapy is textbook AML treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin is associated with a different rate of objective_response than no treatment_midostaurin.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's effect on response in FLT3-mutated AML is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib is associated with a different rate of objective_response than no treatment_gilteritinib.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's effect on response in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib is associated with a different rate of objective_response than no treatment_ivosidenib.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's main effect on response is established/approved for IDH1-mutant AML and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib is associated with a different rate of objective_response than no treatment_enasidenib.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's main effect on response is established/approved for IDH2-mutant AML and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment assignment is independent of biomarker status (i.e., treatments were assigned approximately at random with respect to NPM1, TP53, FLT3, IDH1, IDH2, complex karyotype, secondary AML, and unfit_for_intensive).
judgments.rationale: This is a methodological balance claim about the dataset rather than a clinical oncology hypothesis beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation interaction on objective_response: ven_aza increases response substantially more in NPM1+ than in NPM1- patients.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutant AML's strong response to venetoclax/azacitidine is well-established in VIALE-A and current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x unfit_for_intensive interaction on objective_response: ven_aza increases response substantially more in unfit-for-intensive than in fit patients.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza's preferential benefit in unfit-for-intensive patients is essentially its labeled indication and standard guideline recommendation.

judgments.text: There is a negative treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x tp53_mutation interaction on objective_response: ven_aza fails to improve response in TP53+ patients while it does improve response in TP53- patients.
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutant AML's poor response to venetoclax/azacitidine is well-recognized in current AML guidelines and consensus.

judgments.text: There is a negative treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x complex_karyotype interaction on objective_response: ven_aza's benefit is markedly attenuated in complex_karyotype patients.
judgments.rationale: Attenuated ven/aza benefit in complex karyotype (often co-occurring with TP53) is documented in VIALE-A subgroup analyses and guideline discussion.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x flt3_itd interaction on objective_response is null (no differential benefit by FLT3-ITD status).
judgments.rationale: A specific null interaction claim for FLT3-ITD with ven/aza is a subgroup-conditional claim that goes beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x idh1_mutation interaction on objective_response is null.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines highlight high response in IDH1-mutant AML to ven/aza; a specific null-interaction claim is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional hypothesis.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x idh2_mutation interaction on objective_response is null.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines highlight high response in IDH2-mutant AML to ven/aza; a specific null-interaction claim is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional hypothesis.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x secondary_aml interaction on objective_response is null.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza efficacy in secondary AML is established (VIALE-A subgroup analyses); a null interaction is consistent with the current guideline view that ven/aza works across AML subtypes.

judgments.text: Joint logistic model: ven_aza interactions with npm1_mutation (positive), unfit_for_intensive (positive), tp53_mutation (negative), and complex_karyotype (negative) are simultaneously significant after mutual adjustment, indicating four independent modifiers.
judgments.rationale: Claiming four simultaneously independent biomarker modifiers of ven/aza after mutual adjustment is a multi-pathway, cross-covariate claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has no benefit on objective_response in any biomarker subgroup (no main effect, no positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse 7+3 as standard induction for fit AML across most subgroups; a blanket 'no benefit anywhere' claim contradicts paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 may be harmful in complex_karyotype patients (negative subgroup effect).
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a known adverse prognostic marker, but a directional claim that 7+3 is actively harmful (not just less effective) goes beyond guideline language.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin improves objective_response in FLT3-mutant patients (FLT3-ITD or FLT3-TKD).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin + chemo for FLT3-mutant AML (RATIFY) is a standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib improves objective_response in FLT3-mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib for FLT3-mutant relapsed/refractory AML (ADMIRAL) is a standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib improves objective_response in IDH1-mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib for IDH1-mutant AML is an FDA-approved, guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib improves objective_response in IDH2-mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib for IDH2-mutant relapsed/refractory AML is an FDA-approved, guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow positively modifies the ven_aza treatment effect (treatment effect grows with blast burden).
judgments.rationale: A positive blast-burden × ven/aza interaction (effect grows with blast %) is not a guideline-recognized effect modifier and is a speculative mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: ECOG performance status does not modify the ven_aza treatment effect on the absolute scale.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza was developed for unfit patients, so a guideline-level claim that ECOG does not modify its absolute treatment effect is a non-standard subgroup-conditional assertion.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo positively modifies the ven_aza treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as a treatment effect modifier for ven/aza is not established; however the directional claim that weight loss enhances ven/aza benefit is implausible and not a guideline concept—but framed as a generic prognostic/effect-modifier claim it is speculative rather than guideline consensus, yet it does not represent meaningful novel oncology insight beyond noise-level subgroup analysis.

judgments.text: Adjusted for treatments and other features in a single multivariable logistic model, ven_aza retains a strong positive effect on objective_response while none of the other five treatments shows an independent effect.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza superiority in unfit AML versus azacitidine alone is established by VIALE-A and reflected in NCCN/ELN guidelines, so a multivariable model recapitulating this is standard paradigm.

judgments.text: In NPM1+ AND unfit_for_intensive patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an extreme positive treatment effect on objective_response (>40 percentage points absolute).
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated unfit AML responding very well to venetoclax/azacitidine is explicitly recognized in ELN 2022 and NCCN guidelines as a favorable molecular subgroup for ven/aza.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+ AND unfit subgroup, restricting further to TP53- AND complex_karyotype- yields an even larger positive ven_aza effect (>=60 percentage points).
judgments.rationale: The specific claim that excluding TP53 and complex karyotype within NPM1+ unfit patients pushes response rates to ≥60 percentage point absolute treatment effect is a quantitative cross-pathway subgroup claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the inverse subgroup (TP53+ OR complex_karyotype+), ven_aza has no detectable benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutated and complex karyotype AML having poor/absent benefit from ven/aza is well-established in guidelines and the VIALE-A subgroup analyses.

judgments.text: In TP53+ patients alone, ven_aza has no detectable benefit.
judgments.rationale: Lack of meaningful ven/aza benefit in TP53-mutated AML is a guideline-level consensus finding.

judgments.text: In NPM1- AND fit (i.e., neither favorable modifier present) patients, ven_aza has no benefit (or a slight negative trend).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not state that fit, NPM1-wildtype patients derive no benefit from ven/aza—standard practice gives such patients intensive chemotherapy but the comparative claim of negligible/negative ven/aza effect in this subgroup is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional assertion.

judgments.text: Within NPM1+ patients, complex_karyotype suppresses the ven_aza effect (i.e., the NPM1+ benefit holds only when CK is absent).
judgments.rationale: The interaction claim that complex karyotype abrogates the favorable NPM1+ ven/aza response is a cross-pathway effect-modification claim not codified in guidelines, which generally treat NPM1 and CK as independent risk features.

judgments.text: Within NPM1+ patients, tp53_mutation suppresses the ven_aza effect.
judgments.rationale: While TP53 generally portends poor ven/aza response, the specific claim that TP53 mutation abolishes the NPM1+ ven/aza benefit (an interaction within the favorable NPM1 subgroup) is a mechanistic cross-pathway claim beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: Within NPM1+ AND TP53- AND CK- patients, ven_aza produces a large response benefit even without the unfit_for_intensive flag (so unfit augments but does not gate the effect).
judgments.rationale: The claim that fitness status is not gating—i.e., NPM1+/TP53-/CK- fit patients also derive large ven/aza benefit—contradicts the guideline framing that ven/aza is reserved for unfit patients and is a non-standard subgroup-conditional assertion.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive patients, the absence of TP53 and complex_karyotype mutations is required for full ven_aza benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines already restrict ven-aza to unfit patients and recognize TP53/complex karyotype as adverse modifiers blunting benefit.

judgments.text: Among the 64 cells of the joint screening (6 treatments x 9 binary biomarkers, on each side), the only treatment with any subgroup showing a positive treatment effect on objective_response is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: A dataset-specific claim that ven-aza is the *only* one of six treatments with any positive subgroup signal for response is a cohort-level finding not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Tree/rule-based subgroup discovery (decision tree on treatment-effect proxy) identifies the same modifiers (npm1_mutation, unfit_for_intensive, tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype) for ven_aza.
judgments.rationale: A methodological convergence claim about decision-tree-identified modifiers in this specific analysis is not a guideline-level statement.

judgments.text: Final best-supported subgroup hypothesis (objective_response, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine): patients with npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0 derive the largest absolute response benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine; the treatment effect is positive (response rate higher on ven_aza than off).
judgments.rationale: The conjunctive subgroup NPM1+ AND unfit AND TP53− AND complex-karyotype− as the maximal-benefit cell is a specific cross-feature claim beyond standard NPM1/TP53 prognostic teaching.

judgments.text: Final null subgroup hypothesis (objective_response, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine): patients carrying tp53_mutation==1 OR complex_karyotype==1 derive no detectable benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutated and complex-karyotype AML deriving little/no benefit from venetoclax-azacitidine is now standard guideline/textbook teaching.

judgments.text: For all other treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) there is no biomarker-defined subgroup with a robust positive treatment effect on objective_response in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: A negative cohort-wide claim that midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, and 7+3 show no biomarker-defined responder subgroup contradicts established FLT3/IDH biomarker-driven responses and is a dataset-specific assertion.

judgments.text: Older age (higher age_years) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting worse response in AML is well-established and appears in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status correlating with worse response is standard prognostic knowledge in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response rate differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in response are commonly explored and not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with secondary_aml=0.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having lower response rates than de novo AML is established consensus in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0.
judgments.rationale: Unfit-for-intensive-therapy status predicting lower response is a guideline-level standard observation.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse-risk feature predicting poor response is core ELN/NCCN guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation predicting poor response in AML is well-established adverse-risk biology in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation as a favorable-risk marker predicting higher response is standard ELN guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with flt3_itd=0.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD as a prognostic/predictive marker is established in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_tkd=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with flt3_tkd=0.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD is a recognized AML mutation routinely tested and discussed in guidelines, not novel.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with idh1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status as a predictor of response is established AML knowledge tied to ivosidenib targeting.

judgments.text: Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with idh2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation status as a predictor of response is established AML knowledge tied to enasidenib targeting.

judgments.text: Receiving treatment_midostaurin=1 is associated with higher overall objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's benefit in FLT3-mutated AML is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Receiving treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with higher overall objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib improves response in FLT3-mutated relapsed/refractory AML per established guidelines.

judgments.text: Receiving treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with higher overall objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's efficacy in IDH1-mutant AML is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Receiving treatment_enasidenib=1 is associated with higher overall objective_response than treatment_enasidenib=0.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's efficacy in IDH2-mutant AML is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 is associated with higher overall objective_response than treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax+azacitidine improving response over comparators is standard AML guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Receiving treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with higher overall objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction's response rate in AML is foundational textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis as an adverse prognostic factor in AML is well-established.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Marrow blast percentage as a response/prognostic correlate in AML is established baseline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic/predictive marker for response and survival is well-established in oncology guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH is a classic, guideline-recognized poor prognostic marker associated with reduced response across many cancers.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss are textbook predictors of poor treatment response and survival.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: CRP as an inflammatory marker predicting poor oncologic outcomes is well-established in the literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: High NLR predicting poor response and survival is a widely recognized and well-published prognostic biomarker across tumor types.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Anemia as a negative prognostic factor and higher hemoglobin associating with better outcomes is standard oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase typically reflects bone/liver metastases and is a known marker of worse outcomes in standard practice.

judgments.text: Higher ast_u_l is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST commonly reflects liver involvement or dysfunction and is a recognized marker of poor prognosis.

judgments.text: Higher alt_u_l is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT similarly reflects hepatic involvement/dysfunction and is a known correlate of worse oncologic outcomes.

judgments.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated bilirubin reflecting hepatic dysfunction or biliary obstruction predicting worse response is established clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Renal dysfunction (elevated creatinine) is a well-known adverse prognostic factor in AML and broadly across oncology, routinely noted in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher bun_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflects renal dysfunction/poor performance status, a standard negative prognostic marker recognized in oncology practice.

judgments.text: Lower sodium_meq_l is associated with lower objective_response rate (i.e., higher sodium = higher response).
judgments.rationale: While hyponatremia is a general poor prognostic sign, a specific quantitative association between sodium level and objective response rate in AML is not standard guideline content.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is associated with objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Potassium level as a predictor of objective response in AML is not part of standard treatment guidelines or paradigm.

judgments.text: calcium_mg_dl is associated with objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Calcium level as a predictor of objective response in AML is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: After multivariable logistic adjustment for demographics, fitness, mutations and labs, treatment_midostaurin is independently associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's efficacy in FLT3-mutated AML (improving response/survival) is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_gilteritinib is independently associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's superior response in relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_ivosidenib is independently associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's efficacy in IDH1-mutated AML is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_enasidenib is independently associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's efficacy in IDH2-mutated AML is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is independently associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax plus azacitidine is the established standard of care for unfit AML with well-known high response rates per guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_7plus3 is independently associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 chemotherapy as standard AML induction with established response is textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_midostaurin and flt3_itd: midostaurin's effect on objective_response is larger in flt3_itd=1 than in flt3_itd=0.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is established and guideline-endorsed (RATIFY trial).

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin is guideline-recommended for FLT3-ITD mutated AML.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_midostaurin and flt3_tkd: midostaurin's effect is larger in flt3_tkd=1 than in flt3_tkd=0.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's approval and guideline use explicitly covers FLT3-TKD mutations alongside ITD.

judgments.text: Within flt3_tkd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin is guideline-recommended for FLT3-TKD mutated AML per RATIFY.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_gilteritinib and flt3_itd: gilteritinib's effect is larger in flt3_itd=1 than in flt3_itd=0.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is FDA-approved and guideline-recommended for FLT3-ITD mutated AML (ADMIRAL trial).

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=1, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib efficacy in FLT3-ITD AML is established standard of care.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_gilteritinib and flt3_tkd: gilteritinib's effect is larger in flt3_tkd=1 than in flt3_tkd=0.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's approval covers FLT3-TKD mutations and is reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within flt3_tkd=1, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is guideline-recommended for FLT3-TKD mutated AML.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_ivosidenib and idh1_mutation: ivosidenib's effect is larger in idh1_mutation=1 than in idh1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib is FDA-approved and guideline-recommended specifically for IDH1-mutated AML.

judgments.text: Within idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib efficacy in IDH1-mutant AML is established and guideline-recommended.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_ivosidenib and idh2_mutation (cross-target activity).
judgments.rationale: Cross-target activity of ivosidenib in IDH2-mutant disease is not part of standard paradigm.

judgments.text: Within idh2_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib benefit specifically in IDH2-mutant patients is off-label/cross-target and not guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_enasidenib and idh2_mutation: enasidenib's effect is larger in idh2_mutation=1 than in idh2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's preferential activity in IDH2-mutant AML is the established, guideline-recognized indication.

judgments.text: Within idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_enasidenib=0.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib response in IDH2-mutant AML is standard known association.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_enasidenib and idh1_mutation (cross-target activity).

judgments.text: Within idh1_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_enasidenib=0.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib benefit specifically in IDH1-mutant patients would be cross-target and not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a smaller (more-negative interaction) effect on objective_response in tp53_mutation=1 vs tp53_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Poor response to 7+3 induction in TP53-mutant AML is well-established consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a smaller effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype=1 vs complex_karyotype=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Reduced 7+3 efficacy in complex karyotype AML is a well-known established association.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a smaller effect on objective_response in tp53_mutation=1 vs tp53_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Diminished venetoclax/azacitidine benefit in TP53-mutant AML is established and reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a smaller effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype=1 vs complex_karyotype=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Reduced efficacy of venetoclax-azacitidine in complex karyotype/TP53-altered AML is well-established in guidelines and VIALE-A subgroup analyses.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3 has a larger positive effect on objective_response in unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit) vs unfit_for_intensive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Standard guideline knowledge: 7+3 intensive chemotherapy is indicated for fit patients and contraindicated/less effective in unfit patients.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in unfit_for_intensive=1 vs unfit_for_intensive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the guideline-endorsed standard for unfit/older AML patients based on VIALE-A, so larger benefit in this group is established consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3's effect on objective_response is larger in age_years<75 (age_ge75=0) than in age_ge75=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Better outcomes with intensive 7+3 in younger (<75) versus older patients is a long-standing, guideline-level observation in AML.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine's effect on objective_response is larger in age_years>=75 (age_ge75=1) than in age_ge75=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Ven-aza's preferential use and benefit in patients ≥75 is the basis for its FDA approval and guideline recommendation.

judgments.text: treatment_7plus3's effect on objective_response is larger in ecog_ps<2 (ecog_ge2=0) than in ecog_ge2=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Poorer 7+3 outcomes with higher ECOG performance status is standard clinical knowledge reflected in fitness-based treatment selection guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine's effect on objective_response is larger in ecog_ge2=1 than in ecog_ge2=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Ven-aza being preferred for patients with poor performance status (ECOG ≥2) is established guideline consensus for unfit AML.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin's effect on objective_response varies between ecog_ge2 strata.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib's effect on objective_response varies between ecog_ge2 strata.

judgments.text: Within FLT3-mutated patients (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1), treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit) patients, treatment_7plus3=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction improving response in fit AML patients is the textbook standard-of-care recommendation.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=0 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_7plus3=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.

judgments.text: There exists a 1-3 binary feature combination defining a subgroup where treatment_midostaurin produces the largest positive difference in objective_response (on - off).
judgments.rationale: A data-mined 1-3 feature subgroup defining maximal midostaurin response goes beyond the guideline indication of FLT3-mutant AML.

judgments.text: There exists a 1-3 binary feature combination defining a subgroup where treatment_gilteritinib produces the largest positive difference in objective_response (on - off).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's benefit in FLT3-mutated AML is a guideline-established standard association, not novel.

judgments.text: There exists a 1-3 binary feature combination defining a subgroup where treatment_ivosidenib produces the largest positive difference in objective_response (on - off).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's benefit in IDH1-mutated AML is a guideline-established standard association.

judgments.text: There exists a 1-3 binary feature combination defining a subgroup where treatment_enasidenib produces the largest positive difference in objective_response (on - off).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's benefit in IDH2-mutated AML is a guideline-established standard association.

judgments.text: There exists a 1-3 binary feature combination defining a subgroup where treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces the largest positive difference in objective_response (on - off).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine benefit in unfit AML patients is a guideline-established standard of care.

judgments.text: There exists a 1-3 binary feature combination defining a subgroup where treatment_7plus3 produces the largest positive difference in objective_response (on - off).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction benefit in fit, favorable-risk AML is textbook standard, not novel.

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0 (final restated).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib superiority in FLT3-ITD AML is guideline-established (ADMIRAL trial).

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0 (final restated).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin plus chemotherapy in fit FLT3-mutated AML is guideline-standard (RATIFY trial).

judgments.text: Within idh1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 patients, treatment_ivosidenib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0 (final restated).
judgments.rationale: Conditioning ivosidenib benefit on absence of TP53 mutation and complex karyotype is a speculative subgroup refinement beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: Within idh2_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_enasidenib=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_enasidenib=0 (final restated).
judgments.rationale: Restricting enasidenib benefit to TP53-wildtype IDH2-mutant AML is a subgroup-conditional refinement not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0 (final restated).
judgments.rationale: Conditioning ven/aza benefit on TP53-wildtype and absence of complex karyotype is a speculative refinement beyond guideline-level recommendations.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=0 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 patients, treatment_7plus3=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0 (final restated).
judgments.rationale: Standard 7+3 induction in fit, favorable-risk AML (no TP53 mutation, no complex karyotype) is the guideline-endorsed standard of care.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model fit only within flt3_itd=1 (adjusting for age_years, sex_female, ecog_ps, wbc_k_per_ul, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l), treatment_gilteritinib has positive log-odds for objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib benefit in FLT3-ITD AML is established and guideline-recommended.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model fit within flt3_itd=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_midostaurin has positive log-odds for objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin added to intensive chemotherapy in fit FLT3-mutated AML is the established standard per RATIFY and current guidelines.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model fit within idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib has positive log-odds for objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib for IDH1-mutant AML is a guideline-endorsed targeted therapy.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model fit within idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib has positive log-odds for objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib for IDH2-mutant AML is a guideline-endorsed targeted therapy.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model fit within unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has positive log-odds for objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax + azacitidine in unfit AML is the established VIALE-A standard of care.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model fit within unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_7plus3 has positive log-odds for objective_response.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction in fit AML patients is the textbook standard intensive regimen.

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=0 AND flt3_tkd=0 (FLT3-wt) patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 does NOT produce a meaningful difference in objective_response vs treatment_gilteritinib=0 (negative control).
judgments.rationale: Lack of gilteritinib benefit in FLT3-wildtype AML is the expected mechanistic null and reflected in its FLT3-restricted indication.

judgments.text: Within flt3_itd=0 AND flt3_tkd=0 (FLT3-wt) patients, treatment_midostaurin=1 does NOT produce a meaningful difference in objective_response vs treatment_midostaurin=0 (negative control).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's indication is restricted to FLT3-mutated AML, so absence of benefit in FLT3-wt is the expected guideline-consistent null.

judgments.text: Within idh1_mutation=0 patients, treatment_ivosidenib=1 does NOT produce a meaningful difference in objective_response vs treatment_ivosidenib=0 (negative control).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's indication is restricted to IDH1-mutant AML, so absence of benefit in IDH1-wildtype is the expected guideline-consistent null.

judgments.text: Within idh2_mutation=0 patients, treatment_enasidenib=1 does NOT produce a meaningful difference in objective_response vs treatment_enasidenib=0 (negative control).
judgments.rationale: Negative control confirming enasidenib lacks activity in IDH2-wildtype AML aligns with established mechanism-based guideline restriction.

judgments.text: Within npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_7plus3=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 efficacy in fit NPM1-mutated AML is standard guideline-endorsed induction therapy.

judgments.text: Within npm1_mutation=0 AND unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_7plus3=1 produces higher objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction in fit AML patients regardless of NPM1 status is the established standard of care.

judgments.text: There is a non-zero interaction between treatment_midostaurin and treatment_7plus3 in their joint effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A specific statistical interaction (synergy/antagonism) between midostaurin and 7+3 beyond their additive guideline-based combination is not a textbook claim.

judgments.text: There is a non-zero interaction between treatment_gilteritinib and treatment_7plus3 in their joint effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is guideline-endorsed for relapsed/refractory FLT3+ AML, not as part of induction with 7+3, so an interaction effect is speculative and beyond consensus.

judgments.text: There is a non-zero interaction between treatment_ivosidenib and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine in their joint effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cross-pathway interaction between IDH1 inhibitor ivosidenib and venetoclax/azacitidine on response is an investigational combination not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: In a full multivariable logistic model with all targeted-therapy x mutation interactions, the treatment_midostaurin:flt3_itd interaction term is positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin added to 7+3 improving outcomes in FLT3-ITD AML is the established RATIFY-trial-based guideline standard.

judgments.text: In the full multivariable model, the treatment_gilteritinib:flt3_itd interaction term is positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib benefit specifically conditioned on FLT3-ITD within a multivariable induction model is beyond its guideline-approved relapsed/refractory monotherapy indication.

judgments.text: In the full multivariable model, the treatment_gilteritinib:flt3_tkd interaction term is positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: Differential gilteritinib benefit by FLT3-TKD subtype is a mechanistic subgroup claim not codified in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In the full multivariable model, the treatment_ivosidenib:idh1_mutation interaction term is positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib efficacy restricted to IDH1-mutant AML is the established biomarker-defined guideline indication.

judgments.text: In the full multivariable model, the treatment_enasidenib:idh2_mutation interaction term is positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's benefit in IDH2-mutant AML is the established indication and appears in guidelines, so a positive interaction is the known paradigm.

judgments.text: After adjusting for demographics, ecog_ps and other labs, higher albumin_g_dl is independently associated with higher odds of objective_response (positive coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin as a favorable prognostic/response marker is a well-established, guideline-recognized association across oncology.

judgments.text: After adjustment, higher ldh_u_l is independently associated with lower odds of objective_response (negative coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker associated with worse response is standard textbook oncology.

judgments.text: After adjustment, higher crp_mg_l is independently associated with lower odds of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP/inflammation predicting worse response is a widely recognized prognostic association in oncology.

judgments.text: After adjustment, higher nlr is independently associated with lower odds of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as an adverse prognostic/response marker is well-established in the oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is independently associated with lower odds of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting poorer treatment response is a classic, guideline-level prognostic factor.

judgments.text: After adjustment, higher hemoglobin_g_dl is independently associated with higher odds of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as an adverse prognostic factor for response is standard oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: FINAL PINNED: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response within the subgroup defined by flt3_itd=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (TP53 mutation and complex karyotype hypothesised to suppress benefit).
judgments.rationale: Conditioning gilteritinib benefit on the joint absence of TP53 mutation AND complex karyotype within FLT3-ITD AML is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL PINNED: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response within the subgroup defined by flt3_itd=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=0 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (unfitness, TP53 mutation and complex karyotype hypothesised to suppress benefit).
judgments.rationale: Restricting midostaurin benefit to fit, FLT3-ITD, TP53-wildtype, non-complex-karyotype patients is a speculative multi-factor subgroup hypothesis not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL PINNED: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response within the subgroup defined by idh1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (TP53 and complex karyotype hypothesised to suppress benefit).
judgments.rationale: Conditioning ivosidenib benefit in IDH1-mutant AML on absence of TP53 mutation and complex karyotype is a speculative cross-pathway modifier claim beyond standard guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: FINAL PINNED: treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response within the subgroup defined by idh2_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (TP53 and complex karyotype hypothesised to suppress benefit).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is the guideline-endorsed therapy for IDH2-mutant relapsed/refractory AML, and TP53 mutation/complex karyotype are widely recognized adverse prognostic factors that blunt response to most AML therapies.

judgments.text: FINAL PINNED (intermediate): treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response within the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; this captures most of the venetoclax-azacitidine benefit but the effect is further concentrated when npm1_mutation=1 (see h158).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the standard of care for AML patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy, and TP53 mutation and complex karyotype are well-established predictors of poor response that appear in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL PINNED: treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response within the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=0 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (unfitness, TP53 and complex karyotype hypothesised to suppress benefit).
judgments.rationale: Standard 7+3 induction in fit patients without TP53 mutation or complex karyotype reflects established AML treatment paradigms documented in NCCN/ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL PINNED (most refined): treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine dramatically increases objective_response within the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 AND npm1_mutation=1. Suppressors of benefit: TP53 mutation, complex karyotype; NPM1-wildtype within the otherwise-eligible group also abolishes benefit (h159).
judgments.rationale: While NPM1 mutation is known to associate with favorable venetoclax-azacitidine response, the strong claim that NPM1 mutation is *required* for benefit (NPM1-wildtype abolishes it) within the otherwise-eligible unfit subgroup is a guideline-exceeding subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 AND npm1_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 does NOT produce a meaningful difference in objective_response vs treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0 (negative control showing NPM1+ is required for benefit).
judgments.rationale: The negative-control claim that NPM1-wildtype unfit patients (TP53-WT, non-complex karyotype) derive no meaningful benefit from venetoclax-azacitidine contradicts current guideline recommendations that broadly endorse the regimen for unfit AML regardless of NPM1 status.

judgments.text: Older age (higher age_years) is associated with lower probability of objective_response in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting worse response in AML is well-established in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with a different probability of objective_response than male sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in AML objective response are not part of standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a standard established prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Patients with complex_karyotype have lower objective_response rate than those without.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype as an adverse-risk factor with lower response is established in ELN/NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with tp53_mutation have lower objective_response rate than those without.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation predicting poor response in AML is well-established adverse-risk biology in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with secondary_aml have lower objective_response rate than those with de novo AML.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML having worse outcomes than de novo AML is established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have lower objective_response rate than fit patients.
judgments.rationale: Unfit patients having lower response rates is the rationale for fitness stratification in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_itd mutation have a different (likely lower) overall objective_response rate than wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD as an adverse prognostic marker affecting response is established in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with flt3_tkd mutation have a different overall objective_response rate than wild-type.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-TKD is a recognized mutation in AML risk stratification and response considerations in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with idh1_mutation have a different overall objective_response rate than wild-type.
judgments.rationale: IDH1 mutation status and its response implications are addressed in current AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with idh2_mutation have a different overall objective_response rate than wild-type.
judgments.rationale: IDH2 mutation status is a well-established prognostic and predictive biomarker in AML, with targeted therapy (enasidenib) and known response associations in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with npm1_mutation have a higher overall objective_response rate than wild-type (favorable mutation).
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation is a textbook favorable-risk marker in AML associated with higher response rates, included in ELN risk stratification guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor performance status/nutrition predicting worse treatment response is well-established across oncology.
