judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a marker of high tumor burden and poorer response is a standard, guideline-level prognostic factor in hematologic malignancies.

judgments.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High WBC count is a well-known adverse prognostic factor in AML included in standard risk stratification.

judgments.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage reflecting greater disease burden and poorer response is an established, textbook-level association in AML.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory marker associated with worse oncologic outcomes is a broadly established association in the literature.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as a predictor of worse cancer treatment outcomes is widely established across multiple tumor types in published literature.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia / weight loss predicting worse treatment response is a textbook prognostic factor across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/lower hemoglobin as an adverse prognostic factor associated with worse response is well-established in AML and broader oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflects tumor burden/liver-bone involvement and is a well-established negative prognostic marker associated with poorer treatment response.

judgments.text: Higher ast_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST indicates hepatic dysfunction or metastatic involvement, a standard adverse prognostic factor reducing treatment tolerability and response.

judgments.text: Higher alt_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT similarly reflects hepatic dysfunction, a guideline-recognized factor associated with worse outcomes.

judgments.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Hyperbilirubinemia is a textbook marker of hepatic dysfunction requiring dose reduction and is associated with poorer response across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Renal dysfunction is a standard prognostic factor and basis for dose modification, well-established as associated with worse outcomes.

judgments.text: Higher bun_mg_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflects renal dysfunction or catabolic state, both well-recognized adverse prognostic markers.

judgments.text: Higher sodium_meq_l (within range) is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A positive within-range sodium association with response is not part of standard oncology guidelines beyond hyponatremia being adverse.

judgments.text: Higher potassium_meq_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher potassium predicting lower response is not an established guideline association in oncology beyond tumor lysis context.

judgments.text: Higher calcium_mg_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher calcium predicting better response contradicts the standard view that hypercalcemia is a poor prognostic sign in malignancy.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of patient features (no treatment terms), age_years, ecog_ps, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, secondary_aml, ldh_u_l, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, hemoglobin_g_dl independently predict objective_response with the directions stated above.
judgments.rationale: Age, ECOG, complex karyotype, TP53, secondary AML, LDH, blast%, albumin, and hemoglobin are all textbook AML prognostic factors included in standard risk stratification.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_midostaurin.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's benefit in FLT3-mutated AML is established and guideline-recommended.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_gilteritinib.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's efficacy in FLT3-mutated relapsed/refractory AML is standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_ivosidenib.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's response in IDH1-mutated AML is well-established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_enasidenib.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's activity in IDH2-mutated AML is established and in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is a guideline-recommended frontline regimen with known response benefit in unfit AML.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_7plus3.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction yielding higher response than non-intensive alternatives is textbook AML knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_midostaurin is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's independent benefit when added to standard therapy is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_gilteritinib is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib's independent association with response in FLT3-mutant AML is guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_ivosidenib is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's independent response benefit in IDH1-mutant AML is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_enasidenib is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's independent response benefit in IDH2-mutant AML is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the guideline-standard regimen for unfit AML with established response benefit, so this is paradigm-consensus.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG PS, disease features (secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, mutations), and labs, treatment_7plus3 is independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds).
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction's association with response in fit AML patients is foundational textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: In FLT3-ITD-positive patients (flt3_itd=1), treatment_midostaurin is associated with higher objective_response rate; in FLT3-ITD-negative patients, no benefit is observed.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin's benefit specifically in FLT3-ITD-positive AML is established guideline consensus from RATIFY.

judgments.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between treatment_midostaurin and flt3_itd on objective_response (interaction term beta>0).
judgments.rationale: A positive midostaurin × FLT3-ITD interaction simply restates the well-known FLT3-mutation-restricted indication.

judgments.text: In FLT3-TKD-positive patients (flt3_tkd=1), treatment_midostaurin is associated with higher objective_response rate; in TKD-negative patients, no benefit is observed.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin is guideline-approved for FLT3-mutated AML including TKD mutations, so TKD-restricted benefit is paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Positive interaction between treatment_midostaurin and flt3_tkd on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A positive midostaurin × FLT3-TKD interaction restates the established FLT3-mutation-restricted indication.

judgments.text: In FLT3-ITD-positive patients, treatment_gilteritinib is associated with higher objective_response than no gilteritinib; benefit is absent in FLT3-ITD-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is a guideline-standard FLT3 inhibitor for relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML, including ITD.

judgments.text: Positive interaction between treatment_gilteritinib and flt3_itd.
judgments.rationale: A positive gilteritinib × FLT3-ITD interaction restates the established mutation-restricted indication.

judgments.text: In FLT3-TKD-positive patients, treatment_gilteritinib is associated with higher objective_response than no gilteritinib.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib is guideline-approved for FLT3-mutated AML including TKD (D835/I836), so this is paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: In IDH1-mutated patients (idh1_mutation=1), treatment_ivosidenib is associated with higher objective_response; in IDH1 wild-type patients, no benefit is observed.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's benefit restricted to IDH1-mutated AML is established guideline/label consensus.

judgments.text: Positive interaction between treatment_ivosidenib and idh1_mutation.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's efficacy in IDH1-mutated AML is established and reflected in NCCN/treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In IDH2-mutated patients (idh2_mutation=1), treatment_enasidenib is associated with higher objective_response; in IDH2 wild-type patients, no benefit is observed.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is FDA-approved and guideline-recommended specifically for IDH2-mutated relapsed/refractory AML.

judgments.text: Positive interaction between treatment_enasidenib and idh2_mutation.
judgments.rationale: The IDH2-mutation-restricted benefit of enasidenib is the established indication in current AML guidelines.

judgments.text: In unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than no ven/aza; effect is smaller or absent in fit patients.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is the standard-of-care frontline regimen for AML patients unfit for intensive chemotherapy per VIALE-A and guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and age_years on objective_response (effect grows with age).
judgments.rationale: A continuous age-by-treatment interaction (effect growing with age, beyond the unfit/fit dichotomy) is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and unfit_for_intensive on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Greater ven/aza benefit in unfit patients is the established guideline-based positioning of the regimen.

judgments.text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and age_years on objective_response (7+3 benefit shrinks or reverses with age).
judgments.rationale: Diminished 7+3 benefit with increasing age is well-established and reflected in age-based intensive-therapy eligibility guidance.

judgments.text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and ecog_ps on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Worse outcomes with intensive 7+3 in patients with poor performance status is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and unfit_for_intensive on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: That 7+3 underperforms in patients deemed unfit for intensive chemotherapy is essentially tautological and guideline-established.

judgments.text: There exist multiple statistically significant treatment × feature interactions on objective_response when all 6 treatments are crossed with patient features (binary mutations, ECOG, age, key labs).
judgments.rationale: A broad claim of multiple significant treatment-by-feature interactions across all six treatments and many features goes beyond any single guideline statement.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_gilteritinib is concentrated in flt3_itd=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients; in flt3_itd=1 AND tp53_mutation=1 patients the benefit is much smaller or absent.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines recommend gilteritinib for FLT3-mutated R/R AML without stratifying by TP53 co-mutation; the conditional abolition of benefit in TP53-mutant subset is a non-guideline subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_ivosidenib is concentrated in idh1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib is indicated for IDH1-mutant AML per guidelines without TP53-conditional benefit modification specified.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_enasidenib is concentrated in idh2_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is indicated for IDH2-mutant AML per guidelines without TP53-conditional benefit modification specified.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_midostaurin is concentrated in (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1) AND tp53_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin is approved for FLT3-mutated AML with chemo; TP53-conditional restriction of benefit is not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_gilteritinib in flt3_itd=1 patients is reduced or abolished when complex_karyotype=1.
judgments.rationale: Complex-karyotype-conditional abolition of FLT3 inhibitor benefit is a subgroup interaction not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_ivosidenib in idh1_mutation=1 patients is reduced or abolished when complex_karyotype=1.
judgments.rationale: Complex-karyotype-conditional reduction of ivosidenib benefit in IDH1-mutant patients is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_enasidenib in idh2_mutation=1 patients is reduced or abolished when complex_karyotype=1.
judgments.rationale: Complex-karyotype-conditional reduction of enasidenib benefit in IDH2-mutant patients is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients is reduced when tp53_mutation=1 (i.e., concentrated in unfit AND tp53 wild-type).
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutant AML deriving reduced benefit from venetoclax/azacitidine is now well-established in NCCN guidelines and recent literature (VIALE-A subgroup analyses).

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients is reduced when complex_karyotype=1.
judgments.rationale: While adverse cytogenetics is recognized as poor prognostic, a specific complex-karyotype-conditional reduction of ven/aza benefit beyond TP53 effects is not a textbook claim.

judgments.text: In patients age >= 65, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than no ven/aza; the difference is smaller or absent in age < 65.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is specifically approved and guideline-recommended for older/unfit AML patients (typically age ≥75 or unfit ≥18); the age-stratified benefit is the established paradigm.

judgments.text: The objective-response benefit of treatment_7plus3 (vs no 7+3) is concentrated in patients who are fit (unfit_for_intensive=0) AND age<65 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; the benefit is smaller or absent outside this subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Restricting 7+3 benefit to fit, younger patients without TP53 mutation or complex karyotype reflects standard AML guideline practice for selecting intensive induction candidates.

judgments.text: In unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_7plus3 is associated with higher objective_response than no 7+3.
judgments.rationale: That fit patients benefit from 7+3 induction is the textbook standard-of-care indication for intensive AML chemotherapy.

judgments.text: treatment_gilteritinib is associated with higher objective_response specifically in patients who are flt3_itd=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this triple subgroup, gilteritinib provides little to no benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse gilteritinib for FLT3-ITD AML broadly; the conditional restriction to TP53-wildtype and non-complex karyotype is a speculative subgroup claim beyond consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_ivosidenib is associated with higher objective_response specifically in patients who are idh1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: While ivosidenib is standard for IDH1-mutant AML, conditioning benefit on simultaneously TP53-wildtype and non-complex karyotype goes beyond guideline indications.

judgments.text: treatment_enasidenib is associated with higher objective_response specifically in patients who are idh2_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib is guideline-endorsed for IDH2-mutant AML, but the additional TP53-wildtype and non-complex karyotype conditioning is a speculative subgroup-specific claim.

judgments.text: treatment_midostaurin is associated with higher objective_response specifically in patients who are (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1) AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit in FLT3-mutant AML is established, but layering TP53-wildtype and non-complex karyotype conditions exceeds guideline-level claims.

judgments.text: An exhaustive scan of pairs of binary patient features within each treatment identifies subgroups (defined by 2 binary features set to specific values) where the treatment effect on objective_response is much larger than in the complement.
judgments.rationale: Data-driven exhaustive scans for two-feature interaction subgroups within each treatment generate hypotheses well beyond any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: In secondary_aml=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than no ven/aza; effect may differ in de novo AML.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax/azacitidine is the standard frontline regimen for unfit and secondary AML patients per current guidelines.

judgments.text: In secondary_aml=1 patients, treatment_7plus3 is associated with lower objective_response benefit than in de novo (secondary_aml=0) patients.
judgments.rationale: It is well-established in guidelines and textbooks that secondary AML responds worse to 7+3 than de novo AML, motivating CPX-351 as an alternative.

judgments.text: In a single multivariable logistic model that simultaneously includes all targetable mutations, all 6 treatments, and the four canonical pharmacology interactions (midostaurin×flt3_itd, midostaurin×flt3_tkd, gilteritinib×flt3_itd, ivosidenib×idh1_mutation, enasidenib×idh2_mutation), each canonical interaction has positive coefficient with p<0.05 after adjusting for age, ECOG, fitness, secondary_aml, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, npm1_mutation, and key labs.
judgments.rationale: A simultaneous multivariable model showing all canonical pharmacogenomic interactions remain individually significant after broad covariate adjustment is a quantitative cross-pathway claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL (ven/aza): treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response specifically in patients with npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this fully-specified subgroup the marginal effect is small or null. The unfavorable values tp53_mutation=1 and complex_karyotype=1 each independently suppress the effect.
judgments.rationale: The fully-specified four-way subgroup (NPM1+/unfit/TP53wt/non-CK) as the locus of ven/aza benefit, with explicit null effect outside it, is more granular than guideline-level statements that ven/aza is standard for unfit AML.

judgments.text: Refined ven/aza hypothesis isolating npm1: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in npm1_mutation=1 patients (independent of fitness); the largest absolute effect is observed in npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutated AML responsiveness to venetoclax/azacitidine, particularly in unfit patients, is well-established in guidelines and the VIALE-A subgroup analyses.

judgments.text: Refined ven/aza hypothesis isolating fitness: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients with tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza efficacy in unfit patients without TP53 mutation or complex karyotype is the standard guideline-supported framing of who benefits most.

judgments.text: FINAL (gilteritinib): contrary to prior pharmacology-based expectation, treatment_gilteritinib does NOT meaningfully increase objective_response in any pre-specified subgroup tested (flt3_itd=1, flt3_itd=1 AND tp53=0 AND CK=0, flt3_tkd=1); marginal subgroup effects are near zero and not statistically significant.
judgments.rationale: Claiming gilteritinib has no meaningful benefit in FLT3-ITD patients contradicts the established ADMIRAL-based guideline paradigm and is a non-standard, surprising claim.

judgments.text: FINAL (ivosidenib): contrary to expectation, treatment_ivosidenib does NOT increase objective_response in idh1_mutation=1 patients (subgroup diff is mildly negative); no subgroup with positive significant effect was identified.
judgments.rationale: Asserting ivosidenib does not increase response in IDH1-mutant AML contradicts the established guideline paradigm and is a non-consensus finding.

judgments.text: FINAL (enasidenib): contrary to expectation, treatment_enasidenib has only a small, non-significant trend toward higher response in idh2_mutation=1 AND tp53=0 AND CK=0; the cleanest subgroup signal is a sex_female × enasidenib interaction (treatment_enasidenib appears more effective in female patients).
judgments.rationale: A sex-by-enasidenib interaction with greater efficacy in females is not part of the guideline paradigm for IDH2-mutant AML and is a speculative cross-variable claim.

judgments.text: FINAL (midostaurin): contrary to expectation, treatment_midostaurin does NOT increase objective_response in FLT3-positive (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1) AND tp53=0 AND CK=0 patients in this cohort; subgroup diffs are slightly negative.
judgments.rationale: Negative finding for midostaurin in FLT3-positive favorable-cytogenetics patients contradicts the RATIFY-based guideline standard and is non-consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL (7+3): treatment_7plus3 does NOT show a clinically or statistically meaningful increase in objective_response in any subgroup tested, including fit/age<65/tp53=0/CK=0; consistent with the cohort being dominated by unfit/elderly patients in whom 7+3 is ineffective. The only significant heterogeneity is a negative interaction with complex_karyotype (worse with 7+3 in CK+).
judgments.rationale: The specific negative 7+3 × complex karyotype interaction as the only significant heterogeneity signal goes beyond the guideline-level statement that 7+3 is for fit younger patients.

judgments.text: Patients with `unfit_for_intensive` set have a higher rate of `objective_response` than patients without it.
judgments.rationale: Unfit-for-intensive status is a standard adverse prognostic marker tied to lower response rates, and claiming a higher response rate restates a well-known clinical axis (in the wrong direction).

judgments.text: Patients with `complex_karyotype` set have a lower rate of `objective_response` than patients without it.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a guideline-level adverse cytogenetic risk factor known to predict lower response in AML.

judgments.text: Female patients (`sex_female`=1) and male patients differ in `objective_response` rate.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in response are a routine demographic comparison, not a novel mechanistic or subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: `secondary_aml` is associated with a different `objective_response` rate than de novo AML.
judgments.rationale: Secondary AML is a well-established adverse-risk category with lower response rates per ELN and treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Worse `ecog_ps` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting lower response is textbook prognostic knowledge across oncology.

judgments.text: Older `age_years` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting lower AML response is a guideline-level prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher `blast_pct_marrow` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Higher marrow blast percentage as an adverse response predictor is standard AML prognostic teaching.

judgments.text: Higher `wbc_k_per_ul` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Hyperleukocytosis/higher WBC as an adverse prognostic factor in AML is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with a higher rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor nutrition/inflammation predicting worse oncologic response is a generic, well-known association.

judgments.text: Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Greater weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor (cachexia) is widely established across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher `crp_mg_l` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic/response marker in AML and other cancers is well-established in standard oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with a different rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: LDH is a classic, guideline-recognized prognostic biomarker associated with disease burden and response in AML and many malignancies.

judgments.text: Higher `nlr` is associated with a lower rate of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio predicting poorer treatment response is a widely established finding across oncology.

judgments.text: Patients with `npm1_mutation` set have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients without it.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 mutation conferring favorable response/prognosis in AML is core ELN/NCCN guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with `tp53_mutation` set have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without it.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation as an adverse predictor of response in AML is standard, guideline-established knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with `flt3_itd` or `flt3_tkd` set have a different `objective_response` rate than patients without those mutations.
judgments.rationale: FLT3-ITD/TKD mutations affecting response in AML are well-established and incorporated into ELN risk stratification and guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with `idh1_mutation` or `idh2_mutation` set have a different `objective_response` rate than patients without them.
judgments.rationale: IDH1/IDH2 mutations as response-relevant biomarkers in AML are established in guidelines, especially with targeted inhibitors.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` have a higher rate of `objective_response` than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response rates over standard care in AML is guideline-endorsed standard of care.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_7plus3` have a different rate of `objective_response` than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: 7+3 induction's response rate in AML is foundational, textbook-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_midostaurin` or `treatment_gilteritinib` have a different `objective_response` rate than untreated patients.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin and gilteritinib improving outcomes in FLT3-mutated AML is guideline-established standard of care.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_ivosidenib` or `treatment_enasidenib` have a different `objective_response` rate than untreated patients.
judgments.rationale: IDH1/IDH2 inhibitors (ivosidenib/enasidenib) producing responses in AML is established and guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, mutations, labs and other treatments, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is independently associated with higher `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine improving response in AML is standard-of-care and well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, `npm1_mutation` is independently associated with higher `objective_response`, while `tp53_mutation` and `complex_karyotype` are independently associated with lower `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 favorable and TP53/complex karyotype unfavorable for response in AML are textbook ELN risk-stratification associations.

judgments.text: After adjustment, `treatment_7plus3`, `treatment_midostaurin`, `treatment_gilteritinib`, `treatment_ivosidenib`, and `treatment_enasidenib` show no main effect on `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Null main effects of these therapies overall (without biomarker conditioning) is consistent with their known biomarker-restricted use and not a novel claim.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_midostaurin` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `flt3_itd` set than in those without.
judgments.rationale: Midostaurin benefit being concentrated in FLT3-ITD patients is the established RATIFY-based guideline indication.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_gilteritinib` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `flt3_itd` set than in those without.
judgments.rationale: Gilteritinib benefit in FLT3-ITD-mutated AML is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_midostaurin` or `treatment_gilteritinib` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `flt3_tkd` set than in those without.
judgments.rationale: FLT3 inhibitor benefit in FLT3-TKD patients is a known, guideline-recognized association.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_ivosidenib` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `idh1_mutation` set than in those without.
judgments.rationale: Ivosidenib's enhanced efficacy in IDH1-mutated AML is its established biomarker-defined indication.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_enasidenib` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `idh2_mutation` set than in those without.
judgments.rationale: Enasidenib's enhanced efficacy in IDH2-mutated AML is its established biomarker-defined indication.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `npm1_mutation` set than in those without.
judgments.rationale: A specific NPM1-conditional enhancement of venetoclax-azacitidine response, while suggested by some post-hoc data, is a subgroup-specific claim beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `unfit_for_intensive` set than in those without.
judgments.rationale: Venetoclax-azacitidine is the guideline-endorsed standard for unfit/older AML patients, established by VIALE-A.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is suppressed (no benefit) in patients with `tp53_mutation` set, compared with patients without it.
judgments.rationale: TP53-mutant AML is well-known to have poor and reduced response to venetoclax-azacitidine, reflected in current guidelines and label discussions.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is suppressed (no benefit) in patients with `complex_karyotype` set, compared with patients without it.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype (often overlapping with TP53) is a recognized adverse predictor of reduced venetoclax-azacitidine response in standard AML risk stratification.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_7plus3` on `objective_response` is more negative in patients with `complex_karyotype` set than in those without.
judgments.rationale: Complex karyotype is a textbook adverse-risk feature predicting poor response to 7+3 induction chemotherapy per ELN guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_7plus3` on `objective_response` differs by `tp53_mutation` status.
judgments.rationale: TP53 mutation is a well-established adverse-risk marker predicting poor response to 7+3 induction in AML guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_7plus3` on `objective_response` differs by `secondary_aml` status.
judgments.rationale: Secondary/therapy-related AML is a guideline-recognized adverse feature predicting inferior 7+3 induction outcomes, motivating CPX-351 use.

judgments.text: The benefit of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is jointly concentrated in patients with both `npm1_mutation` set AND `unfit_for_intensive` set, with little to no benefit when only one of those conditions holds.
judgments.rationale: A claim that ven-aza benefit requires the conjunction of NPM1+ AND unfit status, with no benefit when only one holds, is a speculative subgroup-conditional interaction beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+ AND unfit_for_intensive subgroup, the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` benefit on `objective_response` is suppressed when `tp53_mutation` is also set.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional interaction where TP53 specifically suppresses ven-aza benefit within the NPM1+/unfit subgroup is a speculative mechanistic claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within NPM1+ AND unfit_for_intensive AND TP53- patients, the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` benefit on `objective_response` is further suppressed when `complex_karyotype` is set.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conditional interaction where complex karyotype further suppresses benefit within NPM1+/unfit/TP53- patients is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim beyond guideline scope.

judgments.text: The benefit of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` on `objective_response` is fully captured by the joint subgroup defined by `npm1_mutation`=1 AND `unfit_for_intensive`=1 AND `tp53_mutation`=0 AND `complex_karyotype`=0; outside that subgroup the average treatment effect is approximately zero.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the entire ven-aza benefit is captured by a single four-feature joint subgroup with zero effect elsewhere is a strong speculative claim not supported by guideline-level evidence.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` benefit is further modified by `flt3_itd`, `flt3_tkd`, `idh1_mutation`, `idh2_mutation`, or `secondary_aml`.
judgments.rationale: Further sub-stratification of an already-narrow subgroup by FLT3/IDH/secondary-AML status is a speculative interaction claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` benefit is preserved across ECOG strata.
judgments.rationale: Ven/aza benefit being preserved across ECOG strata is consistent with VIALE-A and current guideline framing for unfit AML.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, after adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, mutations, and labs, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` remains independently associated with higher `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: NPM1-mutant unfit AML responding to venetoclax/azacitidine after covariate adjustment is the established guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the other five treatments have no effect on `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Asserting null effect for five named alternative regimens within this specific subgroup is a strong, non-guideline comparative claim.

judgments.text: Stepwise refinement: each predicate (`npm1_mutation`=1, `unfit_for_intensive`=1, `tp53_mutation`=0, `complex_karyotype`=0) materially increases the in-subgroup ven/aza treatment effect over the next-narrower subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A quantitative stepwise refinement claim that each of the four predicates materially augments the effect is a specific, non-textbook decomposition.

judgments.text: A systematic single-feature treatment-by-feature interaction screen will identify `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` x {`npm1_mutation`, `unfit_for_intensive`, `tp53_mutation`, `complex_karyotype`} as the only highly significant interactions.
judgments.rationale: Claiming these four interactions are the *only* highly significant ones genome-wide across a screen is a specific exclusivity claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: The effect of `treatment_enasidenib` on `objective_response` differs by `sex_female`, with female patients benefitting and male patients potentially harmed.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based heterogeneity of enasidenib response with male harm is not in IDH2-inhibitor labeling or guidelines.

judgments.text: The negative effect of `treatment_7plus3` on `objective_response` in `complex_karyotype` patients is preserved across `tp53_mutation` strata.
judgments.rationale: Independence of 7+3 harm in complex-karyotype patients from TP53 status is a specific cross-stratum claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The independent prognostic value of `npm1_mutation` (positive) and `tp53_mutation`/`complex_karyotype` (negative) on `objective_response` is preserved within the no-`treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` cohort, suggesting these are baseline biology effects rather than purely treatment-mediated.
judgments.rationale: NPM1 favorable and TP53/complex-karyotype unfavorable prognosis independent of therapy is standard ELN/guideline biology.

judgments.text: Final consolidated hypothesis: `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` increases `objective_response` only in patients who simultaneously have `npm1_mutation`=1, `unfit_for_intensive`=1, `tp53_mutation`=0, and `complex_karyotype`=0; in this subgroup the absolute response increases from ~16% to ~79% (+63pp). Outside this four-predicate subgroup the average treatment effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A strict four-predicate-only subgroup with ~63pp absolute response gain and null effect elsewhere is a sharper, quantitatively specific claim beyond guideline-level statements.

judgments.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, baseline labs (`albumin_g_dl`, `ecog_ps`, `wbc_k_per_ul`, `blast_pct_marrow`, `weight_loss_pct_6mo`) further refine response prediction but do not modify the treatment-effect direction or eliminate the ven/aza benefit.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional refinement of ven/aza response prediction using baseline labs within an NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- stratum is a speculative multi-feature predictive claim not found in current AML treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having shorter PFS than non-stage IV is a foundational prognostic association in any oncology guideline.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an adverse prognostic factor for PFS is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with higher ecog_ps have shorter mean pfs_months (negative association).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes including shorter PFS is universally recognized in oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with longer PFS contradicts conventional expectation and is not a guideline-supported association.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association) after adjusting for stage_iv, ecog_ps and other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Higher Ki67 proliferation index predicting worse PFS, even after adjustment, is well-established prognostic dogma in breast cancer.

judgments.text: Higher tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Larger tumor size predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic association captured in TNM staging.

judgments.text: er_positive=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than er_positive=0 after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: ER positivity conferring better PFS than ER negative disease is standard breast cancer guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: her2_positive=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0 after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: HER2 positivity as an adverse prognostic factor (in the absence of HER2-targeted therapy context) is classical breast cancer teaching.

judgments.text: pik3ca_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0 after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as an independent adverse prognostic factor for PFS is not established guideline consensus and remains debated.

judgments.text: pr_positive=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than pr_positive=0 after adjustment for er_positive.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity conferring longer PFS independent of ER status is a recognized prognostic feature in standard breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: her2_low=1 is associated with different pfs_months than her2_low=0 after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low status as a distinct prognostic/predictive category is now established in breast cancer guidelines following DESTINY-Breast trials.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic marker for survival outcomes is a well-established general oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss as adverse prognostic factors are standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is established across many cancers and appears in standard staging/prognostic systems.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: CRP as an inflammatory prognostic marker associated with worse outcomes is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as an adverse prognostic marker is widely established across solid tumors.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor) prolonging PFS in HR+/HER2- breast cancer is guideline-standard knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab improving outcomes in HER2-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated breast cancer is established guideline therapy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1+ triple-negative breast cancer is established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan improving PFS in metastatic breast cancer (TROPiCS-02, ASCENT) is established and in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen improving PFS in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is larger in er_positive=1 patients than in er_positive=0 patients (positive treatment_palbociclib x er_positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit being concentrated in ER+ disease is the established indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is smaller (or absent) in her2_positive=1 patients than in her2_positive=0 patients (negative treatment_palbociclib x her2_positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib is approved/used in HR+/HER2- disease, so reduced/absent benefit in HER2+ patients reflects standard guideline practice.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is smaller (or absent) in pik3ca_mutation=1 patients than in pik3ca_mutation=0 patients (negative treatment_palbociclib x pik3ca_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: A guideline-level claim that PIK3CA mutation attenuates CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit is not established consensus and would be a novel subgroup-conditional hypothesis.

judgments.text: In ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients, the treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is at least +2 months.
judgments.rationale: A clinically meaningful PFS gain from palbociclib in ER+/HER2- patients (PALOMA trials) is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-, the treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is positive even when restricting to PIK3CA-WT vs essentially zero in PIK3CA-mutant.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning palbociclib benefit within ER+/HER2- on PIK3CA wild-type status is a speculative subgroup interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER-/HER2-, the treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is null.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib having no benefit in ER-/HER2- (triple-negative) disease is consistent with established guideline indications.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months attenuates with higher ki67_pct (negative treatment_palbociclib x ki67_pct interaction).
judgments.rationale: A continuous negative interaction between Ki67 and palbociclib benefit is not established guideline consensus and would be a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, the treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in patients with ki67_pct < 20 and is essentially absent in patients with ki67_pct >= 20.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional claim restricting palbociclib benefit to ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients with low Ki67 is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup hypothesis beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Each of er_positive=1, her2_positive=0, pik3ca_mutation=0, ki67_pct<20 is necessary for the palbociclib PFS benefit (dropping any single predicate collapses the adjusted effect to near zero).
judgments.rationale: Conjunctive necessity of four specific predicates (ER+, HER2-, PIK3CA WT, low Ki67) for palbociclib benefit goes beyond guideline-level HR+/HER2- indication and is a speculative multi-feature subgroup claim.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab improves pfs_months specifically in her2_positive=1 patients (positive treatment_trastuzumab x her2_positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit restricted to HER2-positive disease is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with brca1_mutation=1 or brca2_mutation=1 (positive interaction with germline BRCA carrier status).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in germline BRCA1/2 carriers is established and guideline-endorsed (e.g., OlympiAD/OlympiA).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with triple-negative disease (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in triple-negative breast cancer is standard guideline knowledge (KEYNOTE-355/522).

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan improves pfs_months specifically in patients with has_brain_mets=1 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: A specific interaction making sacituzumab govitecan preferentially effective in patients with brain metastases is not established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan improves pfs_months in HER2-low (her2_low=1, her2_positive=0) patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan activity in HER2-low (and HER2-zero) metastatic breast cancer is established (TROPiCS-02) and reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen improves pfs_months in er_positive=1 patients (positive interaction with er_positive).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive disease is textbook foundational endocrine therapy knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen has different effect by postmenopausal status within er_positive=1 (interaction).
judgments.rationale: A differential tamoxifen effect by menopausal status within ER+ disease (beyond ovarian suppression considerations) is a nuanced subgroup claim not part of standard guideline statements about tamoxifen efficacy itself.

judgments.text: Each treatment x continuous-feature interaction (with age_years, ecog_ps, ki67_pct, tumor_size_cm, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl) is non-zero — at least one interaction surfaces beyond the palbociclib x ki67_pct already documented.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that every treatment-by-continuous-biomarker interaction is non-zero across this broad panel goes well beyond any guideline claim and is speculative.

judgments.text: The age_years -> pfs_months relationship is non-linear (positive curvature, age^2 coefficient > 0).
judgments.rationale: A specific positive quadratic (U-shaped) age effect on PFS is a mechanistic functional-form claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for clinical covariates, sex_female=1 is associated with different pfs_months than sex_female=0.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in PFS after adjustment is a generic demographic association, not a guideline-level novel claim.

judgments.text: After adjusting for clinical covariates, postmenopausal=1 is associated with different pfs_months than postmenopausal=0.
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status as a prognostic/predictive factor in breast cancer is well established in guidelines.

judgments.text: node_positive=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than node_positive=0 after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive disease portending shorter PFS is textbook breast cancer prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: brca1_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than brca1_mutation=0 after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation associations with breast cancer outcomes are standard guideline content.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation associations with breast cancer outcomes are standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Liver-function and electrolyte labs (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, hemoglobin_g_dl) have detectable independent associations with pfs_months after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Independent PFS associations for a specific panel of routine liver/electrolyte labs after adjustment is a non-guideline, exploratory claim.

judgments.text: Joint model: in a single OLS with treatment_palbociclib interacted with er_positive, her2_positive, pik3ca_mutation and ki67_pct, all four interactions are significant and their signs match the gating pattern (positive ER+, negative HER2+, negative PIK3CA+, negative ki67).
judgments.rationale: A specific four-way interaction pattern (positive ER, negative HER2/PIK3CA/ki67) jointly gating palbociclib benefit goes beyond guideline-level ER+/HER2- indication.

judgments.text: The palbociclib subgroup benefit is robust to stage_iv status — within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-/ki67<20, the unadjusted PFS benefit is similar in stage_iv=0 vs stage_iv=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming subgroup benefit is invariant to stage IV within a narrowly defined ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-/low-ki67 stratum is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Triple combinations of biomarkers (er_positive AND her2_positive AND pik3ca_mutation) better stratify palbociclib benefit than any single biomarker alone.
judgments.rationale: Triple-biomarker combinatorial stratification of palbociclib benefit beyond ER/HER2 status is not guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Final palbociclib subgroup hypothesis: among patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<20, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months by approximately +3.8 months relative to no palbociclib; outside this subgroup the effect is null. All four predicates are individually necessary.
judgments.rationale: A precise +3.8-month PFS effect requiring all four predicates (ER+, HER2-, PIK3CA-, ki67<20) with null effect elsewhere is a speculative, non-guideline subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Final sacituzumab subgroup hypothesis: among patients with has_brain_mets=1 (and especially has_brain_mets=1 AND stage_iv=1), treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan improves pfs_months; outside has_brain_mets=1 the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit being restricted to (and especially driven by) brain metastases with no effect outside that subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not reflected in current breast cancer guidelines, which approve it based on prior-therapy and subtype criteria rather than CNS involvement.

judgments.text: Final null-treatment hypotheses: tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib and pembrolizumab show no clinically meaningful pfs_months benefit anywhere in the dataset, including their canonical biomarker subgroups (ER+ for tamoxifen, HER2+ for trastuzumab, BRCA-mut for olaparib, TNBC for pembrolizumab).
judgments.rationale: Asserting that tamoxifen in ER+, trastuzumab in HER2+, olaparib in BRCA-mutant, and pembrolizumab in TNBC all lack PFS benefit directly contradicts well-established guideline-level efficacy in their canonical biomarker subgroups, making it a non-consensus claim.

judgments.text: Composite prognostic hypothesis: independently of treatment, pfs_months is increased by older age_years and higher albumin_g_dl, and decreased by higher ecog_ps, stage_iv=1, has_brain_mets=1, higher ki67_pct, higher weight_loss_pct_6mo, her2_positive=1 and pik3ca_mutation=1; er_positive=1 increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: While several components (ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, weight loss, Ki67) align with standard prognostics, claiming that older age and HER2-positivity independently worsen — sorry, that older age improves and HER2+/PIK3CA-mutation worsen — PFS independent of treatment goes beyond textbook consensus, especially the directional claims for age and HER2.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen and patients not receiving treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy in HR+ breast cancer is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_palbociclib and patients not receiving treatment_palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib (CDK4/6i) PFS benefit in HR+/HER2- breast cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab and patients not receiving treatment_trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_olaparib and patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib (PARP inhibitor) benefit in BRCA-mutant breast cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan and patients not receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan PFS benefit in metastatic breast cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab and patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab PFS benefit in PD-L1+ TNBC is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Age as a generic prognostic correlate is a routine clinical observation, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High Ki67 as an adverse prognostic marker is textbook breast cancer pathology.

judgments.text: Higher tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Larger tumor size predicting worse outcomes is foundational TNM staging knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as an adverse prognostic factor is a well-established generic oncology observation.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is well-established across oncology and appears in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is a textbook association.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of poor prognosis is well-documented in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a poor prognostic biomarker is widely established across cancer types.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than earlier stages is fundamental staging prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without brain metastases.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring worse prognosis is a standard textbook association.

judgments.text: Patients with er_positive=1 have longer pfs_months than ER-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: ER-positive breast cancer having better prognosis than ER-negative is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_positive=1 have shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: Historically HER2-positive disease was more aggressive, but this is a well-known classical association in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with pik3ca_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than wild-type.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as an independent predictor of shorter PFS in this context is not a standard guideline-level association and remains debated.

judgments.text: Patients with brca1_mutation=1 differ in pfs_months versus wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation effects on PFS outside of PARP inhibitor context are not established guideline associations and depend on subgroup.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 differ in pfs_months versus wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant breast cancer is well-known to influence outcomes and treatment response, a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is inversely associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic finding across oncology.

judgments.text: In an OLS model adjusting for all baseline features, treatment_palbociclib has a positive independent main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS is established first-line guideline therapy in HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_tamoxifen has no main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null main effect for tamoxifen after adjustment contradicts the standard expectation of benefit in ER-positive disease and is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_trastuzumab has no positive main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: No positive main effect of trastuzumab contradicts the well-established HER2-directed benefit and represents a non-standard cohort-level claim.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_olaparib has no main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null main effect of olaparib after adjustment runs counter to the guideline expectation of PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutant disease and is a non-standard aggregate claim.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null main effect of sacituzumab govitecan after adjustment is a non-guideline cohort-level claim that goes beyond approved subgroup indications.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has no main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null main effect of pembrolizumab after adjustment contradicts approved benefit in PD-L1+/TNBC subgroups and is a non-standard aggregate claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment, age_years remains positively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Older age being independently associated with longer PFS after adjustment contradicts typical prognostic expectations and is not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen interacts with er_positive: the tamoxifen effect on pfs_months is more positive in ER-positive patients than ER-negative.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit being concentrated in ER-positive patients is a foundational, textbook predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib interacts with er_positive: the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is more positive in ER-positive patients than ER-negative.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-positive (HR-positive) breast cancer is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib interacts with hr_pos (er_positive OR pr_positive): the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in HR-positive patients.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's efficacy being concentrated in HR-positive disease is the standard approved indication per guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab interacts with her2_positive: the trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is more positive in HER2-positive patients than HER2-negative.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit being restricted to HER2-positive disease is foundational established oncology dogma.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib interacts with brca1_mutation OR brca2_mutation (brca_any): the olaparib effect on pfs_months is more positive in BRCA-mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant breast cancer is a well-established guideline-endorsed biomarker association (e.g., OlympiAD).

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib has a stronger positive effect on pfs_months in patients with brca1_mutation specifically.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1-specific olaparib sensitivity falls within the established BRCA-mutant PARP inhibitor indication and is not beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib has a stronger positive effect on pfs_months in patients with brca2_mutation specifically.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-specific olaparib sensitivity is part of the established BRCA1/2 PARP inhibitor indication in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab interacts with tnbc (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0): pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is more positive in TNBC patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in TNBC (e.g., KEYNOTE-355/522) is established and reflected in current breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan interacts with tnbc: sacituzumab effect on pfs_months is more positive in TNBC patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's approval and guideline recommendation in metastatic TNBC (ASCENT) makes this a standard known association.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib has a strongly negative interaction with pik3ca_mutation: palbociclib's positive effect on pfs_months is suppressed in PIK3CA-mutant tumors.
judgments.rationale: A specific negative interaction between PIK3CA mutation and palbociclib efficacy is not established guideline consensus and represents a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib has a strongly negative interaction with her2_positive: palbociclib's positive effect on pfs_months is suppressed in HER2-positive tumors.
judgments.rationale: Suppression of palbociclib benefit specifically by HER2 positivity is not part of standard guideline teaching and constitutes a speculative subgroup-conditional hypothesis.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib has a positive interaction with pr_positive on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in HR-positive (including PR-positive) breast cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib has a negative interaction with tnbc on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of palbociclib benefit in TNBC is well-established since palbociclib is indicated only for HR-positive disease.

judgments.text: Among ER-positive patients, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to ER-positive patients not on palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improves PFS in ER-positive breast cancer is standard guideline-level knowledge from PALOMA trials.

judgments.text: Among ER-negative patients, treatment_palbociclib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib has no established role in ER-negative disease, consistent with current labeling and guidelines.

judgments.text: Among HR-positive (er_positive=1 OR pr_positive=1) patients, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HR-positive patients benefiting from palbociclib is the core guideline indication.

judgments.text: Among HR-positive HER2-negative patients, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HR-positive/HER2-negative is the exact FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed indication for palbociclib.

judgments.text: Among HR-positive HER2-negative PIK3CA wild-type patients, treatment_palbociclib has the largest increase in mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the largest palbociclib benefit specifically in PIK3CA wild-type HR+/HER2- patients is a subgroup-conditional claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among HER2-positive patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases mean pfs_months relative to HER2+ patients not on trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Among HER2-positive node-positive patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive node-positive disease is established adjuvant guideline standard.

judgments.text: Among HER2-low patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases mean pfs_months (a HER2-low expansion analogue).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab (not T-DXd) showing PFS benefit in HER2-low disease is not established; guidelines support trastuzumab deruxtecan, not trastuzumab itself, in HER2-low.

judgments.text: Among HER2-negative patients, treatment_trastuzumab has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab having no effect in HER2-negative disease is the foundational rationale for HER2 testing and reflects standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Among BRCA1- or BRCA2-mutant patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant breast cancer is established (OlympiAD/OlympiA) and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among BRCA1-mutant patients specifically, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1-specific olaparib benefit is subsumed by the established BRCA1/2 indication and is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Among BRCA-mutant TNBC patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib activity in gBRCA-mutant TNBC is well-established from OlympiAD and is reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Among non-BRCA-mutant patients, treatment_olaparib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of PARP inhibitor benefit outside BRCA-mutant disease is the established basis for biomarker restriction in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases mean pfs_months relative to TNBC patients not on pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improves outcomes in TNBC (KEYNOTE-355/522) and is part of standard guideline-endorsed therapy.

judgments.text: Among premenopausal (postmenopausal=0) TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases mean pfs_months relative to other premenopausal TNBC patients.
judgments.rationale: A premenopausal-specific pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC is a subgroup-conditional claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among postmenopausal TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not affect pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Asserting pembrolizumab has no effect specifically in postmenopausal TNBC is a menopausal-status–conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Among non-TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not affect pfs_months (or has a small negative crude trend).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is not standard for HR+/HER2+ breast cancer, and the lack of benefit outside TNBC reflects guideline-level biomarker/subtype restriction.

judgments.text: Among TNBC patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic TNBC is established (ASCENT) and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Among TNBC stage IV patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan affects pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic TNBC is established in guidelines (ASCENT trial).

judgments.text: Among premenopausal TNBC patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan affects pfs_months in opposite direction to pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: A premenopausal-specific opposite-direction effect of sacituzumab vs pembrolizumab is a speculative subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among HER2-low patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases mean pfs_months (HER2-low expansion analogue).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan activity in HER2-low disease is established (TROPiCS-02) and reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: A systematic screen of every treatment × binary feature interaction will reveal that the largest signed interaction is treatment_palbociclib × er_positive (positive).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib efficacy in ER-positive disease is the foundational guideline-level indication for CDK4/6 inhibitors.

judgments.text: The systematic screen will identify treatment_palbociclib × pik3ca_mutation as the largest negative interaction (palbociclib loses efficacy in PIK3CA-mutant tumors).
judgments.rationale: A qualitative loss of palbociclib efficacy specifically in PIK3CA-mutant tumors is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The screen will reveal no other treatment with an interaction p-value below 1e-50, indicating only palbociclib has strong qualitative subgroup heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A meta-claim that only palbociclib shows extreme subgroup heterogeneity across all treatments is a speculative cross-pathway statement.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib × ki67_pct interaction is negative on pfs_months: palbociclib benefit declines with higher Ki-67.
judgments.rationale: Ki-67-dependent attenuation of palbociclib benefit is a mechanistic subgroup claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib × age_years interaction on pfs_months is small/null.
judgments.rationale: Lack of meaningful age interaction with palbociclib is consistent with established subgroup analyses in pivotal trials.

judgments.text: Among continuous variables × treatment interactions screened, treatment_palbociclib × ki67_pct will have the strongest signal.
judgments.rationale: Ranking Ki-67 as the strongest continuous modifier of palbociclib benefit is a speculative cross-variable claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt patients, treatment_palbociclib roughly doubles mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: A specific PIK3CA-wildtype-restricted doubling of PFS with palbociclib is a subgroup-conditional quantitative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After full multivariable adjustment, the palbociclib effect within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt remains essentially unchanged from the unadjusted estimate.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt MBC is established first-line standard of care reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Outside HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt, palbociclib has no detectable effect on pfs_months (effect estimate near zero).
judgments.rationale: A claim that palbociclib has zero effect outside HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt is a subgroup-conditional null finding not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_olaparib has no main effect on pfs_months in the BRCA1-mutant subgroup, indicating apparent crude effect is confounded.
judgments.rationale: Asserting olaparib's BRCA1 effect is confounded and disappears after adjustment contradicts established PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutant disease.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab in the premenopausal TNBC subgroup is essentially zero, contradicting the larger crude effect.
judgments.rationale: Claim that pembrolizumab benefit vanishes specifically in premenopausal TNBC after adjustment is a speculative subgroup-conditional reversal not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_trastuzumab in HER2-positive patients has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab having no effect in HER2+ disease contradicts the foundational guideline-level paradigm, making this a non-standard (and implausible) novel claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan in TNBC patients has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Null effect of sacituzumab govitecan in TNBC contradicts the established ASCENT-based guideline indication, qualifying as a non-standard novel claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_tamoxifen in ER-positive patients has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen having no effect in ER+ disease contradicts decades of guideline consensus, making this an unconventional novel assertion.

judgments.text: Exhaustive 2-feature subgroup enumeration will show that the strongest positive subgroup for treatment_palbociclib is er_positive=1 AND pik3ca_mutation=0, with mean PFS difference >2 months.
judgments.rationale: Greater palbociclib benefit in ER+/PIK3CA-wt patients aligns with established CDK4/6 inhibitor guideline use and PIK3CA-related efficacy expectations.

judgments.text: Exhaustive 2-feature subgroup enumeration will fail to reveal any other treatment with a 2-feature subgroup PFS difference exceeding +0.5 months that survives multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim about the relative magnitude of all other treatments' subgroup effects after adjustment is a cross-pathway, dataset-specific assertion not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Exhaustive 2-feature subgroup enumeration will show several subgroups where treatment_trastuzumab is associated with shorter pfs_months (likely chance, but with consistent slight negative direction).
judgments.rationale: Suggesting subgroup-level negative associations for trastuzumab is contrary to guideline consensus and qualifies as a speculative novel claim.

judgments.text: Exhaustive 3-feature subgroup enumeration will show that ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-wt is the optimal subgroup for treatment_palbociclib, with diff approaching +3 months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+/HER2- breast cancer is established guideline knowledge, and PIK3CA wild-type status is a routinely considered biomarker context rather than a novel subgroup definition.

judgments.text: Exhaustive 3-feature enumeration will show treatment_olaparib has no 3-feature subgroup with adjusted diff > 0.3 months that is biologically interpretable.
judgments.rationale: A null finding for olaparib subgroups is consistent with the established paradigm that PARP inhibitor benefit is concentrated in BRCA/HRD contexts, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Exhaustive 3-feature enumeration will show treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in postmenopausal=0 AND tnbc=1 only (and similar HR-/premenopausal definitions), but the magnitude is small (<0.3 crude, near 0 adjusted).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit being concentrated in TNBC/HR- subgroups reflects current guideline consensus (KEYNOTE-355) rather than a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within the palbociclib target subgroup ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt, the palbociclib effect is similar regardless of HER2-low status (no further heterogeneity).
judgments.rationale: Whether HER2-low status modifies CDK4/6 inhibitor efficacy within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt is not a guideline-level established association.
