judgments.text: Tamoxifen produces longer pfs_months in ER-positive patients (er_positive=1) than in ER-negative patients (treatment×er interaction).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy being restricted to ER-positive disease is the foundational principle of endocrine therapy.

judgments.text: Tamoxifen produces longer pfs_months in postmenopausal patients than in premenopausal patients.
judgments.rationale: A menopausal-status interaction favoring postmenopausal patients on tamoxifen contradicts the conventional paradigm where tamoxifen is preferred premenopausally and AIs postmenopausally, making it a non-guideline subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Tamoxifen produces longer pfs_months in PR-positive patients than in PR-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity predicting tamoxifen benefit is standard endocrine therapy doctrine in breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Palbociclib produces longer pfs_months in ER-positive patients than in ER-negative patients (palbociclib×ER interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib is approved and guideline-recommended specifically for HR/ER-positive metastatic breast cancer, so ER-positive benefit is established.

judgments.text: Palbociclib's PFS benefit is reduced or abolished in HER2-positive patients (palbociclib×HER2 interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not establish a negative palbociclib×HER2 interaction; palbociclib is studied in HER2-negative disease but a subgroup-conditional reduction in HER2-positive is a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Palbociclib's PFS benefit is reduced or abolished in PIK3CA-mutant patients (palbociclib×PIK3CA interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as a negative modifier of palbociclib benefit is not established in guidelines and represents a speculative pathway-interaction claim.

judgments.text: Palbociclib produces longer pfs_months in postmenopausal patients than in premenopausal patients.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib trials and labels (PALOMA) emphasize postmenopausal/endocrine-combination use, making greater postmenopausal benefit a well-known paradigm.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab produces longer pfs_months in HER2-positive patients than in HER2-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit restricted to HER2-positive disease is foundational textbook oncology.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab produces longer pfs_months in HER2-low patients than in HER2-non-low patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab outperforming in HER2-low versus HER2-non-low contradicts established paradigm (trastuzumab is for HER2-positive; HER2-low is the trastuzumab-deruxtecan story), making this a novel/speculative claim.

judgments.text: Olaparib produces longer pfs_months in BRCA1-mutated patients than in BRCA1 wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1-mutant tumors responding preferentially to olaparib is established PARP inhibitor guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Olaparib produces longer pfs_months in BRCA2-mutated patients than in BRCA2 wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant tumors responding preferentially to olaparib is established PARP inhibitor guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Olaparib produces longer pfs_months in BRCA1-or-BRCA2-mutated (brca_any=1) patients than in BRCA wild-type patients (combined gBRCA modifier).
judgments.rationale: Combined gBRCA1/2 status as a predictor of olaparib benefit is the standard companion-diagnostic indication in guidelines.

judgments.text: Sacituzumab govitecan produces longer pfs_months in triple-negative breast cancer (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) than in non-TNBC.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's efficacy in TNBC is established (ASCENT trial) and reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Sacituzumab govitecan produces longer pfs_months in HER2-low patients than in HER2-non-low patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan activity in HER2-low metastatic breast cancer is a well-known guideline-level association (TROPiCS-02 and related data).

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab produces longer pfs_months in TNBC patients than in non-TNBC (pembrolizumab×TNBC interaction positive).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC (KEYNOTE-355/522) is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab produces longer pfs_months in high-Ki67 (≥20%) patients than in low-Ki67 patients.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning pembrolizumab benefit on Ki67 ≥20% is a speculative subgroup claim not part of current breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab produces a HARMFUL effect (shorter pfs_months) in ER+ disease relative to controls.
judgments.rationale: A harmful pembrolizumab effect specifically in ER+ disease is a non-guideline, speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable OLS model adjusting for demographics, biology, labs, and other treatments, palbociclib retains a large positive coefficient and PIK3CA, ER, HER2 remain among the dominant biology covariates.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit and ER/HER2/PIK3CA dominance among biology covariates restate well-established guideline-level biology.

judgments.text: After adjustment, ECOG performance status, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, age_years, albumin, weight_loss are independent prognostic factors for pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, age, albumin, and weight loss are textbook prognostic factors in oncology.

judgments.text: Tamoxifen produces longer pfs_months in the joint subgroup of ER+ AND postmenopausal patients than the same patients without tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy in ER+ postmenopausal patients is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Palbociclib produces a particularly large pfs_months benefit in the joint subgroup ER+ AND HER2- AND postmenopausal.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+/HER2-/postmenopausal disease is the labeled, guideline-endorsed indication (PALOMA trials).

judgments.text: Trastuzumab produces longer pfs_months in HER2+ patients than the same patients without trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ disease is a textbook standard-of-care association.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab produces longer pfs_months in TNBC patients than the same patients without pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC is established (KEYNOTE-355) and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Across all six treatments and 19 binary candidate modifiers (incl. age≥65, sex_female, stage_iv, brain_mets, node+, postmeno, ER, PR, HER2, HER2-low, BRCA1, BRCA2, BRCA-any, PIK3CA, TNBC, ki67_high, albumin_low, ldh_high, weight_loss_5p), the strongest treatment×modifier interactions involve palbociclib with PIK3CA (negative), ER (positive), HER2 (negative), Ki67-high (negative), TNBC (negative), and PR (positive).
judgments.rationale: Specific ranking of palbociclib×modifier interactions across 19 candidates, including negative PIK3CA/Ki67/TNBC and positive ER/PR effects, is a dataset-specific multi-modifier claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Continuous Ki-67 percentage is a quantitative modifier of palbociclib effect on pfs_months: each 1-point increase in ki67_pct reduces palbociclib's PFS gain.
judgments.rationale: A quantitative dose-response of continuous Ki-67% attenuating palbociclib PFS gain is not a guideline-endorsed predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: Continuous albumin_g_dl is a positive modifier of sacituzumab govitecan effect on pfs_months: higher albumin amplifies sacituzumab benefit.
judgments.rationale: Continuous albumin as a positive quantitative effect modifier specifically amplifying sacituzumab benefit is not in standard oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/postmeno, the palbociclib PFS benefit is RESTRICTED to PIK3CA wild-type patients; PIK3CA mutation eliminates the benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines recommend palbociclib in ER+/HER2-/postmeno regardless of PIK3CA; restricting benefit to PIK3CA wild-type contradicts standard paradigm.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-, the palbociclib PFS benefit holds in both pre- and postmenopausal patients (postmeno is not a critical effect modifier).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit across pre- and postmenopausal ER+/HER2- patients (with OFS in premeno) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab's TNBC interaction reflects qualitative reversal: pembro slightly hurts non-TNBC patients (especially ER+) but is null/slightly helpful in TNBC; no further sub-modifier within TNBC produces a significant pembro PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Qualitative reversal harming ER+ non-TNBC patients plus the claim that no sub-modifier within TNBC identifies responders is a speculative cross-pathway pattern not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Olaparib's PFS benefit is concentrated in BRCA-mutated AND ER-negative patients; ER+ status attenuates olaparib benefit even in BRCA mutation carriers.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support olaparib in BRCA-mutated HER2- breast cancer regardless of ER status; ER-negativity as required co-modifier is novel/contradictory.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab is null/inert across every plausible subgroup (HER2+, HER2+/ER+, HER2+/ER-, HER2+/non-stage-IV, HER2+/stage-IV, HER2-low) — i.e., the dataset does not support trastuzumab efficacy as ordinarily expected.
judgments.rationale: Claiming trastuzumab is inert across all HER2+ subgroups contradicts well-established guideline-level efficacy and is a dataset-specific anomaly.

judgments.text: Tamoxifen does not produce a PFS benefit in ANY plausible mechanism-driven subgroup (ER+, ER+/postmeno, ER+/premeno, ER+/HER2-, ER+/HER2-/premeno, ER+/HER2-/postmeno, ER+/PR+, PR+).
judgments.rationale: Claiming tamoxifen yields no PFS benefit in any ER+ subgroup contradicts foundational guideline consensus and is a dataset-specific negative finding.

judgments.text: Sacituzumab govitecan does not produce a PFS benefit in TNBC, HER2-low, HER2-low/ER+, HER2-low/TNBC, ER-/HER2-, or HER2- subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Contradicts ASCENT/TROPiCS-02 guideline consensus that sacituzumab provides PFS benefit in TNBC and HR+/HER2-low — a null-everywhere claim is not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/postmeno, the palbociclib PFS benefit does not depend further on stage_iv, has_brain_mets, or ECOG performance status — i.e., other prognostic factors do not modify the treatment effect after the core biology is fixed.
judgments.rationale: Asserting palbociclib benefit is homogeneous across stage, brain mets, and ECOG within the indicated ER+/HER2-/postmeno subgroup mirrors PALOMA subgroup forest plots and standard guideline framing.

judgments.text: Patients with the joint phenotype ER+ AND HER2-negative AND PIK3CA-wild-type form the complete subgroup in which palbociclib produces a large PFS benefit (~+2.9 months); outside this subgroup palbociclib is not associated with longer PFS.
judgments.rationale: Restricting palbociclib benefit to PIK3CA-wild-type is not in NCCN/ESMO guidelines; PIK3CA status governs alpelisib/inavolisib selection, not CDK4/6 inhibitor eligibility.

judgments.text: Patients with the joint phenotype BRCA1-or-BRCA2-mutated AND ER-negative form the subgroup with the largest detectable olaparib PFS benefit (~+0.83 months); BRCA mutation alone is sufficient for a smaller benefit (~+0.35 months) but ER+ status attenuates it.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines (OlympiAD/OlympiA) endorse olaparib for germline BRCA-mutated breast cancer regardless of ER status; conditioning benefit on ER-negative is a non-standard subgroup claim.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for age, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ki67_pct, albumin, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and ldh, the palbociclib PFS effect within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA- remains large (+2.9 months) and outside the subgroup is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: Same PIK3CA-WT-conditional palbociclib effect as H3, now claimed to survive multivariable adjustment — still beyond guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: Within TNBC, no joint sub-phenotype (TNBC + PIK3CA-, TNBC + ki67_low, TNBC + no-brain-mets, TNBC + non-stage-IV, TNBC + BRCA-mut) produces a statistically significant pembrolizumab PFS benefit individually; the pembro×TNBC interaction reflects mainly an absence of the small harmful effect seen in non-TNBC.
judgments.rationale: Contradicts KEYNOTE-355, which establishes pembrolizumab PFS benefit in PD-L1+ TNBC; claiming no TNBC sub-phenotype benefits and that the interaction is driven by ER+ harm is a non-guideline mechanistic reframing.

judgments.text: FINAL palbociclib subgroup: pfs_months is longer (~+2.9 months) for palbociclib-treated patients, and only for patients meeting the joint criteria er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0; outside this subgroup the palbociclib effect on PFS is null.
judgments.rationale: Same PIK3CA-WT-restricted palbociclib subgroup as H3/H5; not reflected in any current breast cancer guideline.

judgments.text: FINAL olaparib subgroup: pfs_months is longer (~+0.83 months) for olaparib-treated patients meeting the joint criteria (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) AND er_positive=0; outside this subgroup the effect is small or null.
judgments.rationale: Same ER-negative-restricted olaparib claim as H4; goes beyond established BRCA-only indication.

judgments.text: FINAL pembrolizumab heterogeneity: pfs_months trends UP (+0.07) on pembrolizumab in TNBC and DOWN (-0.11, p=0.005) in ER+ disease; the qualitative reversal across triple_neg is significant (interaction p=0.015) but no positive subgroup reaches per-stratum significance for pembrolizumab benefit.
judgments.rationale: The qualitative direction (pembro favorable in TNBC, unfavorable in ER+) matches established paradigm; reporting that no per-stratum estimate reached significance is a study-specific descriptive finding, not a new biological claim.

judgments.text: FINAL trastuzumab/tamoxifen/sacituzumab: none of these three treatments produces a detectable PFS benefit in any plausible subgroup of this dataset, including their canonical mechanism-of-action subgroups (HER2+, ER+/postmeno, TNBC/HER2-low). All adjusted main effects are null or marginally negative; no subgroup analysis recovers a benefit.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no benefit for trastuzumab in HER2+, tamoxifen in ER+/postmeno, or sacituzumab in TNBC/HER2-low contradicts foundational guideline-level efficacy and would not appear in any textbook.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_tamoxifen (unadjusted population-level effect).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_palbociclib (unadjusted population-level effect).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's PFS benefit in HR+ breast cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_trastuzumab (unadjusted population-level effect).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab's efficacy in HER2+ breast cancer is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (unadjusted population-level effect).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's efficacy in BRCA-mutated breast cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan (unadjusted population-level effect).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's PFS benefit in metastatic triple-negative/HR+ breast cancer is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (unadjusted population-level effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's efficacy in PD-L1+ TNBC is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: age_years is positively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: A positive population-level correlation between age and PFS is not a standard guideline-level claim and would be unexpected.

judgments.text: ki67_pct is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: High Ki-67 as a marker of worse prognosis/shorter PFS is established textbook knowledge in breast cancer.

judgments.text: tumor_size_cm is positively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: A positive correlation between tumor size and PFS contradicts standard prognostic teaching, making the directional claim non-standard.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl is positively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin as a favorable prognostic factor is well-established across oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: ldh_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a negative prognostic marker is a well-established standard in oncology guidelines across many tumor types.

judgments.text: weight_loss_pct_6mo is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia is a guideline-recognized adverse prognostic factor in oncology.

judgments.text: crp_mg_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of poor prognosis is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: nlr is positively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: High NLR is consistently associated with worse outcomes; the hypothesis claims the opposite direction, which contradicts established consensus rather than offering a novel insight beyond it.

judgments.text: hemoglobin_g_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Anemia (low hemoglobin) is a recognized adverse prognostic factor in standard oncology practice.

judgments.text: alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase, often reflecting bone/liver involvement, is a recognized poor prognostic marker.

judgments.text: ast_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST commonly reflects hepatic involvement or dysfunction and is a known adverse prognostic indicator.

judgments.text: alt_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT similarly reflects hepatic dysfunction/involvement and is a recognized adverse prognostic factor.

judgments.text: total_bilirubin_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Elevated bilirubin reflecting hepatic dysfunction is a guideline-recognized poor prognostic marker.

judgments.text: creatinine_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Elevated creatinine indicating renal dysfunction is a standard adverse prognostic and treatment-tolerance factor.

judgments.text: bun_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflecting renal dysfunction or poor performance status correlating with worse outcomes is a standard prognostic observation.

judgments.text: sodium_meq_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Hyponatremia as a poor prognostic marker in advanced cancer is well-established in oncology guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is positively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: A direct positive association between serum potassium and PFS is not an established prognostic finding in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: calcium_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months (continuous main effect).
judgments.rationale: Hypercalcemia of malignancy as an adverse prognostic factor is a textbook oncology concept.

judgments.text: Patients with sex_female=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with sex_female=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in PFS are well-documented in many cancers and routinely discussed in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring worse PFS than earlier stages is the foundational basis of cancer staging.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with has_brain_mets=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an adverse prognostic factor is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with node_positive=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with node_positive=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Node-positive disease portending worse outcomes than node-negative is a textbook staging principle.

judgments.text: Patients with postmenopausal=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with postmenopausal=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status as a prognostic/treatment-relevant factor in breast cancer is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients with er_positive=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with er_positive=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: ER-positive status conferring better PFS than ER-negative breast cancer is established textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with pr_positive=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with pr_positive=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: PR positivity as a favorable prognostic factor in breast cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_positive=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with her2_positive=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Historically HER2-positive disease was associated with worse prognosis, a well-established paradigm in textbooks.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_low=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with her2_low=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: HER2-low as a prognostic (not just predictive for T-DXd) factor for PFS is not established guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients with brca1_mutation=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with brca1_mutation=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation prognostic associations in breast/ovarian cancer are standard textbook material.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with brca2_mutation=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation prognostic associations are standard textbook material.

judgments.text: Patients with pik3ca_mutation=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with pik3ca_mutation=0 (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as a generally adverse prognostic factor in breast cancer is widely discussed in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps (0/1/2) is associated with shorter mean pfs_months (one-way ANOVA across ECOG levels).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is a foundational oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: A multivariable OLS regression of pfs_months on all 31 patient features and 6 treatment indicators yields an adjusted prognostic model with substantial explanatory power (R^2>0.5), and the per-feature signs will match their univariate directions for strongly prognostic features.
judgments.rationale: A specific quantitative multivariable prognostic model with R^2>0.5 across 31 features and 6 treatments is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, age_years is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: Age as an independent prognostic factor adjusted for other variables is standard prognostic modeling content.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, stage_iv is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease being independently associated with shorter PFS is a foundational oncology principle.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, has_brain_mets is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an independent negative prognostic factor for PFS is well-established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, ecog_ps is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a textbook independent prognostic factor for PFS across cancer types.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, er_positive is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: ER positivity as a favorable prognostic/predictive marker in breast cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, her2_positive is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: HER2 status as a prognostic/predictive factor (especially in the era of HER2-targeted therapy) is established guideline content.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, pik3ca_mutation is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: An independent PFS effect of PIK3CA mutation after adjustment for treatment and other features is not a settled guideline-level prognostic claim.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, albumin_g_dl is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as an independent adverse prognostic factor is well-documented across oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, weight_loss_pct_6mo is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as an independent adverse prognostic factor is standard textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all other features and treatments in a multivariable OLS, treatment_palbociclib is independently associated with pfs_months (signed coefficient on the natural scale of months per unit).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS in HR+ metastatic breast cancer is established guideline-level evidence.

judgments.text: Pre-specified clinical pairing: tamoxifen benefits ER+ more than ER- patients (treatment x biomarker interaction on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefiting ER+ over ER- patients is the foundational predictive biomarker relationship in breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Pre-specified clinical pairing: tamoxifen benefits postmenopausal more than premenopausal patients (treatment x biomarker interaction on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Differential tamoxifen benefit by menopausal status (favoring postmenopausal) is not a standard guideline claim and contradicts typical teaching that tamoxifen works in both, with AIs preferred postmenopausally.

judgments.text: Pre-specified clinical pairing: palbociclib benefits ER+ more than ER- patients (treatment x biomarker interaction on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+ disease is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Pre-specified clinical pairing: trastuzumab benefits HER2+ more than HER2- patients (treatment x biomarker interaction on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit restricted to HER2+ disease is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Pre-specified clinical pairing: olaparib benefits BRCA1-mutated more than BRCA1-wildtype patients (treatment x biomarker interaction on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1-mutated cancers is established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Pre-specified clinical pairing: olaparib benefits BRCA2-mutated more than BRCA2-wildtype patients (treatment x biomarker interaction on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated cancers is established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Pre-specified clinical pairing: sacituzumab govitecan benefits HER2-low more than HER2-not-low patients (treatment x biomarker interaction on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Differential sacituzumab govitecan benefit specifically by HER2-low vs not-low status is not established guideline consensus (its Trop-2 ADC mechanism is HER2-agnostic).

judgments.text: Pre-specified clinical pairing: pembrolizumab effect on PFS differs in patients with vs without brain mets (treatment x biomarker interaction on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Brain-metastasis-conditional differential efficacy of pembrolizumab on PFS is not standard guideline consensus across tumor types.

judgments.text: There exists at least one patient feature whose interaction with treatment_tamoxifen significantly modifies the treatment effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: An open-ended search for any feature modifying tamoxifen effect goes beyond guideline-level predictive biomarker claims.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by stage_iv (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + stage_iv + treatment_tamoxifen:stage_iv).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV status as a quantitative effect modifier of tamoxifen PFS benefit is not a guideline-established predictive interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by brca1_mutation (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + brca1_mutation + treatment_tamoxifen:brca1_mutation).
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation status as a modifier of tamoxifen efficacy is not part of established endocrine therapy guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by ki67_pct (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + ki67_pct + treatment_tamoxifen:ki67_pct).
judgments.rationale: Ki67 as a quantitative effect modifier of tamoxifen PFS benefit is not established guideline-level predictive consensus.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by nlr (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + nlr + treatment_tamoxifen:nlr).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy modification by neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio is a speculative inflammation-based interaction not in breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by ast_u_l (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + ast_u_l + treatment_tamoxifen:ast_u_l).
judgments.rationale: AST-modified tamoxifen efficacy is a speculative hepatic-function interaction not described in standard treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by total_bilirubin_mg_dl (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + total_bilirubin_mg_dl + treatment_tamoxifen:total_bilirubin_mg_dl).
judgments.rationale: Bilirubin-modified tamoxifen efficacy is a speculative pharmacokinetic/hepatic interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by er_positive (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + er_positive + treatment_tamoxifen:er_positive).
judgments.rationale: ER status modifying tamoxifen response is the foundational established paradigm of endocrine therapy in breast cancer.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by has_brain_mets (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + has_brain_mets + treatment_tamoxifen:has_brain_mets).
judgments.rationale: Brain metastasis status modifying tamoxifen PFS effect is a speculative subgroup interaction not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by postmenopausal (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + postmenopausal + treatment_tamoxifen:postmenopausal).
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status modifying tamoxifen efficacy is well-established (e.g., AI superiority in postmenopausal women) and appears in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by sex_female (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + sex_female + treatment_tamoxifen:sex_female).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of tamoxifen efficacy is not a standard guideline-level association in breast cancer treatment.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by crp_mg_l (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + crp_mg_l + treatment_tamoxifen:crp_mg_l).
judgments.rationale: CRP-modified tamoxifen efficacy is a speculative systemic inflammation interaction not in established treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by creatinine_mg_dl (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + creatinine_mg_dl + treatment_tamoxifen:creatinine_mg_dl).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine/renal-function-modified tamoxifen efficacy is a speculative interaction not described in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by her2_positive (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + her2_positive + treatment_tamoxifen:her2_positive).
judgments.rationale: HER2 status modifying tamoxifen PFS effect beyond ER context is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not codified as a guideline-level predictive biomarker for tamoxifen specifically.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by age_years (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + age_years + treatment_tamoxifen:age_years).
judgments.rationale: Age-dependent modification of tamoxifen PFS effect is not a standard guideline finding; tamoxifen indication is based on HR status, not age-treatment interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by alt_u_l (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + alt_u_l + treatment_tamoxifen:alt_u_l).
judgments.rationale: ALT as an effect modifier of tamoxifen efficacy is not described in guidelines, which use ALT only for safety monitoring.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + ecog_ps + treatment_tamoxifen:ecog_ps).
judgments.rationale: ECOG PS is broadly prognostic but is not established in guidelines as a specific modifier of tamoxifen efficacy.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by weight_loss_pct_6mo (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_tamoxifen:weight_loss_pct_6mo).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as a modifier of tamoxifen response is not part of standard endocrine therapy guidance.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + treatment_tamoxifen:alkaline_phosphatase_u_l).
judgments.rationale: Alkaline phosphatase as an effect modifier of tamoxifen is not in guidelines, even though it's a bone-met marker.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is modified by hemoglobin_g_dl (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_tamoxifen + hemoglobin_g_dl + treatment_tamoxifen:hemoglobin_g_dl).

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by brca2_mutation (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + brca2_mutation + treatment_pembrolizumab:brca2_mutation).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 status modifying pembrolizumab benefit is a speculative cross-pathway claim not reflected in current immunotherapy guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by albumin_g_dl (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + albumin_g_dl + treatment_pembrolizumab:albumin_g_dl).
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a predictive (not just prognostic) modifier of pembrolizumab PFS benefit is not a guideline-established interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by has_brain_mets (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + has_brain_mets + treatment_pembrolizumab:has_brain_mets).
judgments.rationale: HER2 status modifying pembrolizumab efficacy on PFS is not a guideline-endorsed predictive interaction (KEYNOTE-811 concerns response, not a HER2 interaction term).

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by her2_positive (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + her2_positive + treatment_pembrolizumab:her2_positive).
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status as an effect modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is not described in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by postmenopausal (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + postmenopausal + treatment_pembrolizumab:postmenopausal).
judgments.rationale: Sex-by-pembrolizumab interaction on PFS is a debated research-level question, not a guideline-level predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by sex_female (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + sex_female + treatment_pembrolizumab:sex_female).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a predictive modifier (beyond prognostic) of pembrolizumab efficacy is not in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by nlr (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + nlr + treatment_pembrolizumab:nlr).
judgments.rationale: NLR as a predictive biomarker modifying pembrolizumab's PFS benefit is a speculative interaction beyond guideline consensus, though prognostic associations are described.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by bun_mg_dl (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + bun_mg_dl + treatment_pembrolizumab:bun_mg_dl).
judgments.rationale: BUN as an effect modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not part of established guideline paradigms.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by age_years (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + age_years + treatment_pembrolizumab:age_years).
judgments.rationale: Age-by-pembrolizumab interaction on PFS is not a guideline-level established predictive relationship.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by tumor_size_cm (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + tumor_size_cm + treatment_pembrolizumab:tumor_size_cm).
judgments.rationale: Tumor size as a continuous effect modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is not a standard guideline-level predictive paradigm.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by ast_u_l (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + ast_u_l + treatment_pembrolizumab:ast_u_l).
judgments.rationale: AST as a predictive biomarker modifying pembrolizumab efficacy is not part of established consensus.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by total_bilirubin_mg_dl (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + total_bilirubin_mg_dl + treatment_pembrolizumab:total_bilirubin_mg_dl).
judgments.rationale: Bilirubin as an effect modifier of pembrolizumab PFS benefit is not in any guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by alt_u_l (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + alt_u_l + treatment_pembrolizumab:alt_u_l).
judgments.rationale: ALT as a predictive modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is speculative and not guideline-established.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by creatinine_mg_dl (interaction term in OLS pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + creatinine_mg_dl + treatment_pembrolizumab:creatinine_mg_dl).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine as an effect modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is not a recognized predictive biomarker in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [all], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS in an unselected pooled population reflects its established CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit and is guideline-consistent at a general level.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [ER+], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS in ER+ disease is the established, guideline-endorsed indication for CDK4/6 inhibitors.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [PIK3CA-WT], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in PIK3CA-WT HR+ disease is established; subgroup analyses in PALOMA trials show benefit irrespective of PIK3CA status, making this within-guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [HER2-], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's PFS benefit in HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer is the foundational guideline-level indication from PALOMA-2/3.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [ER+ AND HER2-], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: ER+/HER2- is the standard guideline-approved indication for palbociclib combined with endocrine therapy.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [ER+ AND PIK3CA-WT], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+ disease regardless of PIK3CA status is established from PALOMA trial subgroup analyses.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-WT], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: This is essentially the guideline-approved ER+/HER2- indication with a PIK3CA-WT restriction that doesn't alter the established benefit.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-WT AND ki67<med], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Differential palbociclib benefit conditioned on low Ki67 within PIK3CA-WT ER+/HER2- disease is a speculative biomarker-stratified claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-WT AND ki67>=med], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Ki67-high stratified palbociclib benefit within PIK3CA-WT ER+/HER2- is a mechanistic subgroup hypothesis beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [ER- (negative control)], patients on treatment_palbociclib have shorter mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Lack of palbociclib benefit in ER-negative disease is established/expected since the drug is indicated only for HR+ disease.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [PIK3CA-mut (negative control)], patients on treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Claiming palbociclib still confers PFS benefit in PIK3CA-mutant disease as a directional prediction is a non-trivial cross-pathway claim about CDK4/6-PI3K interaction.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by [HER2+ (negative control)], patients on treatment_palbociclib have shorter mean pfs_months than patients off palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Absence of palbociclib monotherapy/endocrine-combo benefit in HER2+ disease aligns with guideline restriction to HER2-negative tumors.

judgments.text: In an OLS with treatment_palbociclib, a triple-marker indicator (ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-WT), and their interaction predicting pfs_months, the interaction coefficient is positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT benefit from palbociclib aligns with established CDK4/6 inhibitor guideline use in HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer.

judgments.text: Adding low Ki67 (ki67_pct < median) to the triple marker, forming a quadruple-marker indicator (ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-WT AND ki67<median), produces an even larger and more significant palbociclib interaction term.
judgments.rationale: Adding low Ki67 as a fourth conjunctive modifier to amplify palbociclib benefit is a speculative subgroup-conditional refinement not in guidelines, which actually emphasize CDK4/6i benefit even in high-Ki67 luminal B disease.

judgments.text: Among all 1-, 2-, and 3-modifier subgroups of binary features, the subgroup with the largest positive treatment effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is [NOT_stage_iv AND NOT_postmenopausal AND NOT_brca2_mutation].
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit being maximal specifically in non-stage-IV, premenopausal, BRCA2-wildtype patients is a speculative three-way conjunction not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among the same exhaustive subgroup search for treatment_tamoxifen, the subgroup with the largest negative (worst) treatment effect on pfs_months is [sex_female AND brca2_mutation AND pik3ca_mutation].
judgments.rationale: A claim that female BRCA2/PIK3CA co-mutated patients have the worst tamoxifen response is a cross-pathway mechanistic subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Among all 1-, 2-, and 3-modifier subgroups of binary features, the subgroup with the largest positive treatment effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months is [er_positive AND NOT_her2_positive AND NOT_pik3ca_mutation].
judgments.rationale: Largest palbociclib benefit in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT recapitulates the standard CDK4/6 inhibitor indication in HR+/HER2- disease.

judgments.text: Among the same exhaustive subgroup search for treatment_palbociclib, the subgroup with the largest negative (worst) treatment effect on pfs_months is [NOT_stage_iv AND NOT_er_positive AND NOT_pik3ca_mutation].
judgments.rationale: Worst palbociclib effect in non-stage-IV, ER-negative, PIK3CA-wildtype patients is a speculative subgroup; ER-negative non-use is known but the specific 3-way conjunction with stage and PIK3CA status is not guideline content.

judgments.text: Among all 1-, 2-, and 3-modifier subgroups of binary features, the subgroup with the largest positive treatment effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is [stage_iv AND node_positive AND her2_low].
judgments.rationale: Maximal trastuzumab benefit in HER2-low (not HER2-positive) stage IV node-positive disease contradicts the guideline paradigm restricting trastuzumab to HER2-positive disease.

judgments.text: Among the same exhaustive subgroup search for treatment_trastuzumab, the subgroup with the largest negative (worst) treatment effect on pfs_months is [node_positive AND her2_low AND NOT_stage_iv].
judgments.rationale: Worst trastuzumab effect specifically in non-stage-IV HER2-low node-positive patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim outside guideline paradigms.

judgments.text: Among all 1-, 2-, and 3-modifier subgroups of binary features, the subgroup with the largest positive treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months is [brca1_mutation AND NOT_sex_female AND NOT_has_brain_mets].
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit being maximal in male BRCA1-mutated patients without brain metastases is a speculative sex-conditional subgroup claim not in guidelines, which focus on BRCA1/2 status irrespective of sex.

judgments.text: Among the same exhaustive subgroup search for treatment_olaparib, the subgroup with the largest negative (worst) treatment effect on pfs_months is [stage_iv AND has_brain_mets AND pik3ca_mutation].
judgments.rationale: Worst olaparib effect in stage IV brain-met PIK3CA-mutated patients is a cross-pathway speculative subgroup claim not codified in PARP inhibitor guidelines.

judgments.text: Among all 1-, 2-, and 3-modifier subgroups of binary features, the subgroup with the largest positive treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is [stage_iv AND has_brain_mets AND pr_positive].
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit specifically in stage IV brain mets PR-positive subgroup is a speculative cross-feature subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among the same exhaustive subgroup search for treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, the subgroup with the largest negative (worst) treatment effect on pfs_months is [NOT_postmenopausal AND NOT_er_positive AND NOT_her2_positive].
judgments.rationale: Worst sacituzumab govitecan effect in premenopausal ER-/HER2- patients is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Among all 1-, 2-, and 3-modifier subgroups of binary features, the subgroup with the largest positive treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is [NOT_postmenopausal AND NOT_er_positive AND NOT_pr_positive].
judgments.rationale: Largest pembrolizumab benefit specifically in premenopausal ER-/PR- subgroup is a speculative subgroup claim beyond guideline TNBC/PD-L1 framing.

judgments.text: Among the same exhaustive subgroup search for treatment_pembrolizumab, the subgroup with the largest negative (worst) treatment effect on pfs_months is [pr_positive AND NOT_node_positive AND NOT_her2_low].
judgments.rationale: Worst pembrolizumab effect in PR+ node-negative non-HER2-low subgroup is a speculative cross-feature claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [all], patients on treatment_olaparib have shorter pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib lacking benefit (or harming) in unselected breast cancer is consistent with guideline restriction to BRCA-mutated disease.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA1+], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1-mutant breast cancer is established guideline knowledge (OlympiA/OlympiAD).

judgments.text: Within [BRCA2+], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant breast cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA1+ or BRCA2+], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in germline BRCA1/2-mutant breast cancer is the standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA1- AND BRCA2-], patients on treatment_olaparib have shorter pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Lack of olaparib benefit in BRCA wild-type disease is consistent with guideline restriction to BRCA-mutated patients.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND ER+], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Specifically conditioning olaparib benefit on BRCA-mutant AND ER+ subgroup is a refinement beyond standard guideline framing.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND HER2-], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer is established (OlympiAD) and in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND TNBC], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in germline BRCA-mutated triple-negative breast cancer is a guideline-endorsed standard indication.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND no brain mets], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Restricting to BRCA-mutant patients without brain metastases is just the standard olaparib-responsive population, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: In OLS pfs ~ olaparib + brca_any + olaparib:brca_any, the interaction coefficient is positive (olaparib effect larger in BRCA1+/BRCA2+ patients than in BRCA-wildtype patients).
judgments.rationale: A positive olaparib-by-BRCA interaction simply restates the well-known synthetic-lethality paradigm and PARP-inhibitor labeling.

judgments.text: Within [all], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Claiming trastuzumab shortens PFS in an unselected population contradicts standard oncology knowledge but is a trivially expected negative-control result, not a novel scientific insight.

judgments.text: Within [HER2+], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab shortening PFS in HER2+ disease would directly contradict guideline consensus and is not an established or speculative paradigm-consistent claim.

judgments.text: Within [HER2- (negative control)], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Lack/harm of trastuzumab benefit in HER2-negative disease is the textbook negative-control expectation underlying HER2 testing.

judgments.text: Within [HER2+ AND ER+], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Asserting trastuzumab worsens PFS specifically in HER2+/ER+ disease contradicts established benefit and is not a recognized subgroup caveat in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [HER2+ AND ER-], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Claiming trastuzumab harms PFS in HER2+/ER- disease runs counter to strong guideline-endorsed benefit and is not an accepted subgroup finding.

judgments.text: Within [HER2+ AND postmenopausal], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: A postmenopausal-specific harm from trastuzumab in HER2+ disease is not part of guideline consensus or standard mechanistic teaching.

judgments.text: Within [HER2+ AND stage_iv], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ metastatic disease is established guideline consensus; this restates known efficacy framing.

judgments.text: Within [HER2+ AND no brain mets], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab efficacy in HER2+ disease without brain metastases is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within [HER2+ AND node positive], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ node-positive disease is foundational adjuvant guideline content.

judgments.text: Within [HER2+ AND PIK3CA-WT], patients on treatment_trastuzumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA-WT–conditional modulation of trastuzumab PFS is a mechanistic subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within [all], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is not broadly indicated across all breast cancer, so a population-wide PFS effect restates known non-utility rather than a novel finding.

judgments.text: Within [TNBC: ER- AND HER2-], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC (KEYNOTE-355/522) is established guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: Within [TNBC AND ki67_high], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Ki67-high TNBC predicting worse pembrolizumab PFS is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [TNBC AND PIK3CA-WT], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA-WT TNBC as a pembrolizumab-responsive subgroup is a mechanistic cross-pathway claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within [HER2 low], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy framing specific to HER2-low disease is not part of established guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: Within [HER2 low AND ER-], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low ER- subgroup as pembrolizumab-responsive is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within [PD-like: high nlr], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Speculative claim of pembrolizumab harm conditional on high NLR — not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [Visceral: brain mets], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional claim of pembrolizumab harm in brain-mets patients goes beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within [Stage IV], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Generic 'drug-on patients do worse in stage IV' is the canonical confounding-by-indication pattern, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Within [Stage IV AND TNBC], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV TNBC is the indicated population; an apparent harm signal is the standard confounding-by-indication artifact rather than a new biological hypothesis.

judgments.text: Within [PIK3CA mutation], patients on treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA-conditional pembrolizumab harm is a cross-pathway speculative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [all], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan is given to heavily pretreated/advanced patients, so 'on-drug worse overall' is generic confounding, not novel.

judgments.text: Within [TNBC: ER- AND HER2-], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: TNBC is the on-label population for sacituzumab govitecan; the apparent harm signal reflects standard indication confounding, not a guideline-departing insight.

judgments.text: Within [TNBC AND ki67_high], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Ki67-high–conditional sacituzumab harm in TNBC is a speculative subgroup claim outside guideline statements.

judgments.text: Within [TNBC AND PIK3CA-WT], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA-WT–conditional sacituzumab harm is a cross-pathway speculative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [HER2 low], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab activity stratified by HER2-low status (vs HER2-0) is an active research question, not a settled guideline association.

judgments.text: Within [HER2 low AND ER-], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan is approved for HER2-low and TNBC settings; this restates the labeled indication context without novel subgroup biology.

judgments.text: Within [PD-like: high nlr], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Differential sacituzumab govitecan efficacy conditioned on high neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio is not part of guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Within [Visceral: brain mets], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Enhanced sacituzumab govitecan benefit specifically in patients with brain metastases is a speculative CNS-activity claim beyond current guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within [Stage IV], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan is indicated and demonstrated to improve PFS in metastatic (stage IV) breast cancer per ASCENT/TROPiCS-02.

judgments.text: Within [Stage IV AND TNBC], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have shorter pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Claiming sacituzumab govitecan shortens PFS in stage IV TNBC contradicts pivotal ASCENT data and is not a standard guideline finding.

judgments.text: Within [PIK3CA mutation], patients on treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than patients off treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA-mutation-conditioned benefit from sacituzumab govitecan is not an established predictive biomarker in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the joint subgroup [er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<median (=15.0)], treatment_palbociclib produces a substantially longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+/HER2- breast cancer (with low Ki67 and PIK3CA-WT being favorable hormone-sensitive features) is core guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: In the relaxed subgroup that drop ER+ requirement (only require: HER2-, PIK3CA-WT, low_Ki67), treatment_palbociclib still produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (but smaller than in the full quad).
judgments.rationale: Asserting palbociclib benefit without the ER+ requirement contradicts its established ER-positive-only indication and is speculative.

judgments.text: In the relaxed subgroup that drop HER2- requirement (only require: ER+, PIK3CA-WT, low_Ki67), treatment_palbociclib still produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (but smaller than in the full quad).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit without HER2-negativity is not a guideline-supported claim and represents a speculative cross-subgroup extension.

judgments.text: In the relaxed subgroup that drop PIK3CA-WT requirement (only require: ER+, HER2-, low_Ki67), treatment_palbociclib still produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (but smaller than in the full quad).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+/HER2- low-Ki67 disease regardless of PIK3CA status is consistent with established guideline use across PIK3CA strata.

judgments.text: In the relaxed subgroup that drop low_Ki67 requirement (only require: ER+, HER2-, PIK3CA-WT), treatment_palbociclib still produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (but smaller than in the full quad).
judgments.rationale: Claims palbociclib benefit persists in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT without requiring low Ki67, which is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: When the other 3 quad markers are satisfied, the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is much larger when ER+=1 than when ER+=0 (each marker is necessary).
judgments.rationale: ER+ being necessary for palbociclib (a CDK4/6 inhibitor approved for HR+/HER2- disease) benefit is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: When the other 3 quad markers are satisfied, the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is much larger when HER2-=1 than when HER2-=0 (each marker is necessary).
judgments.rationale: HER2- status being necessary for palbociclib benefit reflects the standard HR+/HER2- indication in guidelines.

judgments.text: When the other 3 quad markers are satisfied, the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is much larger when PIK3CA-WT=1 than when PIK3CA-WT=0 (each marker is necessary).
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA wild-type as a necessary condition for palbociclib benefit is not part of standard guideline criteria for CDK4/6 inhibitor use.

judgments.text: When the other 3 quad markers are satisfied, the palbociclib effect on pfs_months is much larger when low_Ki67=1 than when low_Ki67=0 (each marker is necessary).
judgments.rationale: A low-Ki67 requirement for palbociclib benefit contradicts guideline use, which favors CDK4/6 inhibitors broadly in HR+/HER2- disease regardless of Ki67.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients, the palbociclib PFS benefit is large when ki67_pct < ~14 and essentially absent when ki67_pct >= ~14 (a sharp Ki67 threshold).
judgments.rationale: A sharp Ki67 ~14% threshold dichotomizing palbociclib benefit is a speculative cutoff not endorsed in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment for prognostic covariates (age, ECOG, stage_iv, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH), the treatment_palbociclib x quad-marker (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<median) interaction on pfs_months remains large and significant, while the palbociclib main effect outside the quad is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: A covariate-adjusted four-way interaction defining palbociclib benefit only within a specific quad-marker subgroup goes beyond guideline-level claims.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA1+ only (not BRCA2)], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1-mutant cancers is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA2+ only (not BRCA1)], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant cancers is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant cancers is the standard guideline-endorsed indication for PARP inhibitors.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND HER2-], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated HER2-negative breast cancer is established (OlympiAD/OlympiA) and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND HER2+], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit specifically in BRCA-mutated HER2-positive breast cancer is not a guideline-endorsed indication and would be a novel subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND ER+], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: BRCA-mutated HR+/HER2- disease responding to olaparib is a standard guideline-supported indication.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND ER- (TNBC-leaning)], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: BRCA-mutated TNBC response to olaparib is well-established in guidelines (OlympiAD).

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND no brain mets], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib activity in BRCA-mutated breast cancer without brain metastases reflects the standard trial population and is not novel.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA neg control: not BRCA either], patients on treatment_olaparib have shorter pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Lack of olaparib benefit (or potential harm) in BRCA-wildtype breast cancer is the established negative-control expectation consistent with guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for major prognostic and biomarker covariates (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH, ER, HER2, PIK3CA, Ki67), the treatment_olaparib x brca_any interaction on pfs_months remains positive and significant.
judgments.rationale: A positive olaparib-by-BRCA interaction on PFS after covariate adjustment recapitulates the established predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: After adjusting for the full set of prognostic features and biomarkers (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH, ER, HER2, PIK3CA, Ki67, BRCA1, BRCA2, HER2-low, postmenopausal, PR), treatment_trastuzumab has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab having a main effect on PFS in HER2-directed breast cancer treatment is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for the full set of prognostic features and biomarkers (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH, ER, HER2, PIK3CA, Ki67, BRCA1, BRCA2, HER2-low, postmenopausal, PR), treatment_pembrolizumab has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A non-zero adjusted main effect of pembrolizumab across an unselected breast cancer population (not restricted to PD-L1+ TNBC or early-stage TNBC) goes beyond current guideline indications.

judgments.text: After adjusting for the full set of prognostic features and biomarkers (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH, ER, HER2, PIK3CA, Ki67, BRCA1, BRCA2, HER2-low, postmenopausal, PR), treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A main-effect PFS benefit of sacituzumab govitecan adjusted across a broad mixed population (rather than its guideline-defined pretreated metastatic TNBC/HR+ settings) would extend beyond current guideline scope.

judgments.text: After adjusting for the full set of prognostic features and biomarkers (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH, ER, HER2, PIK3CA, Ki67, BRCA1, BRCA2, HER2-low, postmenopausal, PR), treatment_tamoxifen has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen having a main effect on PFS in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer is a foundational guideline-level finding, not novel.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by ldh_u_l (cont).
judgments.rationale: LDH as an effect modifier of trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive disease is not part of standard guideline biomarker selection.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by nlr (cont).
judgments.rationale: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio modifying trastuzumab efficacy is a speculative immune-inflammatory interaction not in HER2 treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps (cont).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status as a quantitative effect modifier of trastuzumab benefit (beyond eligibility) is not a guideline-endorsed predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by albumin_g_dl (cont).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a predictive (not just prognostic) modifier of trastuzumab efficacy is not in HER2-positive treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by tumor_size_cm (cont).
judgments.rationale: Tumor size modifying trastuzumab benefit beyond stage-based selection is a speculative subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by potassium_meq_l (cont).
judgments.rationale: Serum potassium as an effect modifier of trastuzumab efficacy has no established mechanistic or guideline basis.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by has_brain_mets (binary).
judgments.rationale: Brain metastasis status modifying trastuzumab PFS benefit (beyond CNS-penetrant agent selection) is a speculative subgroup interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by sex_female (binary).
judgments.rationale: HER2-positive breast cancer is overwhelmingly female and trastuzumab benefit is established regardless of sex; sex as modifier here is not a meaningful novel claim.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by hemoglobin_g_dl (cont).
judgments.rationale: Hemoglobin as a predictive modifier of trastuzumab efficacy is not established in HER2-positive treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is modified by postmenopausal (binary).
judgments.rationale: Postmenopausal status as an effect modifier of trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ disease is not an established guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, after adjusting for prognostic features (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH, ER, PIK3CA, Ki67, postmenopausal, PR, HER2-low), trastuzumab has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive breast cancer is a foundational, guideline-established association.

judgments.text: Within [er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<12], treatment_palbociclib produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (refining the threshold).
judgments.rationale: A specific Ki67<12 threshold combined with ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT to predict palbociclib benefit is a speculative subgroup refinement not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<13], treatment_palbociclib produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (refining the threshold).
judgments.rationale: Conditioning palbociclib benefit on Ki67<13 plus PIK3CA-wildtype status is a speculative cross-marker refinement absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<14], treatment_palbociclib produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (refining the threshold).
judgments.rationale: A Ki67<14 threshold combined with PIK3CA-WT as a palbociclib-response subgroup is not part of established CDK4/6 inhibitor guidance.

judgments.text: Within [er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<15], treatment_palbociclib produces a longer mean pfs_months than no palbociclib (refining the threshold).
judgments.rationale: Ki67<15 plus PIK3CA-WT as a palbociclib benefit-defining subgroup is a speculative refinement beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: After adjusting for an extended covariate set (age, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin, weight_loss, LDH, PR, HER2-low, BRCA1, BRCA2, postmenopausal, tumor size, CRP), the treatment_palbociclib x quad-marker (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<14) interaction on pfs_months remains very large (~+5 months) and the palbociclib main effect outside the quad is near zero.
judgments.rationale: A large adjusted quad-marker interaction with near-zero palbociclib effect outside the subgroup is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Outside the ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<14 quad subgroup, the unadjusted treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
judgments.rationale: Claiming palbociclib has no effect outside a PIK3CA-WT/low-Ki67 subgroup contradicts broad HR+/HER2- guideline endorsement and is a novel restrictive claim.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND ER- AND HER2- (TNBC+BRCA)], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit is established in BRCA-mutant HER2- disease broadly; restricting to TNBC+BRCA as a distinct subgroup claim is a speculative refinement.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND ER- AND HER2- AND PR-], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Adding PR-negativity to BRCA/TNBC as an olaparib-response-defining subgroup is a speculative refinement beyond guideline-based BRCA-mutation indication.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either AND ER- AND HER2- AND no brain mets], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in gBRCA-mutated HER2- metastatic breast cancer is established by OlympiAD and reflected in NCCN/ESMO guidelines.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA1 only AND ER- AND HER2-], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1-mutant TNBC benefit from olaparib is a standard guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA2 only AND ER- AND HER2-], patients on treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant HER2- breast cancer benefit from olaparib is guideline-standard.

judgments.text: Within [BRCA either control: not BRCA], patients on treatment_olaparib have shorter pfs_months than patients off olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Lack of (or harm from) PARP inhibition in BRCA-wildtype disease is the established rationale for restricting olaparib to BRCA-mutated patients.
