judgments.text: In TNBC patients (er_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0), the treatment_olaparib x brca_any interaction on pfs_months survives adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH, PIK3CA, Ki67, and PR.
judgments.rationale: An olaparib×BRCA interaction in TNBC surviving covariate adjustment merely reaffirms the well-known biomarker-defined effect.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [all] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_trastuzumab has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: An all-comers trastuzumab main effect is expected as a reflection of the established HER2+ benefit and is consistent with guideline reasoning.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [HER2+] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_trastuzumab has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab efficacy in HER2+ disease is the foundational guideline-endorsed paradigm.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [all] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_pembrolizumab has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines restrict pembrolizumab to TNBC (PD-L1+ metastatic or high-risk early-stage); a non-zero all-comers effect would extend beyond consensus.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [TNBC] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_pembrolizumab has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in TNBC is established by KEYNOTE-355/522 and is in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [all] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan is approved/guideline-endorsed in both TNBC (ASCENT) and HR+/HER2- (TROPiCS-02), so an all-comers effect is consistent with consensus.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [TNBC] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic TNBC is established (ASCENT trial) and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [HER2-low] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in HER2-low metastatic breast cancer is established (TROPiCS-02) and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [all] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_tamoxifen has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen having a PFS effect in an unselected breast cancer population is a standard expectation reflecting the predominance of ER+ disease, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Restricted to the [ER+] population, after adjusting for the full prognostic covariate set, treatment_tamoxifen has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy in ER+ breast cancer is foundational guideline/textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: FINAL: The treatment effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months is concentrated in the joint subgroup [er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 (i.e., HER2-) AND pik3ca_mutation=0 (i.e., PIK3CA-WT) AND ki67_pct<14], where palbociclib lengthens mean PFS by ~+5 months versus no palbociclib. Outside this subgroup the effect of palbociclib on PFS is statistically indistinguishable from zero. Each of the 4 markers is necessary for the full effect — relaxing any single requirement materially reduces the magnitude (drop ER+ → +3.5; drop HER2- → +4.1; drop PIK3CA-WT → +3.2; drop low-Ki67 → +2.9), and the within-complement test confirms each marker is required.
judgments.rationale: A four-marker conjunction (ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-WT AND Ki67<14) as the necessary subgroup for palbociclib benefit, with each marker required, goes beyond the guideline indication of CDK4/6 inhibitors in HR+/HER2- disease.

judgments.text: FINAL: Of the six treatments studied, only treatment_palbociclib shows a robust, biomarker-defined causal-looking effect on pfs_months. After adjustment for prognostic covariates, the population-level effects of treatment_tamoxifen, treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_olaparib, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months are all statistically indistinguishable from zero, and biomarker-defined subgroup analyses (trastuzumab in HER2+, tamoxifen in ER+, olaparib in BRCA1+/BRCA2+, sacituzumab in HER2-low / TNBC, pembrolizumab in TNBC / brain mets) fail to reveal a subgroup in which any of these treatments has a clinically meaningful, adjustment-robust PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming null effects for trastuzumab in HER2+, tamoxifen in ER+, olaparib in BRCA-mutant, and pembrolizumab in TNBC contradicts established guideline-level efficacy and constitutes a non-standard cross-treatment claim.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years, continuous) is associated with shorter pfs_months overall (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic factor in oncology is a standard, well-established association in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than male sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in cancer outcomes are commonly discussed in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps, ordinal 0/1/2) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a foundational, guideline-level prognostic variable across virtually all solid tumors.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage IV.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease portending worse outcomes is the most basic tenet of oncologic staging and is in every guideline.

judgments.text: Brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than no brain mets.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases being associated with worse prognosis is a textbook, guideline-recognized adverse prognostic feature.

judgments.text: Node-positive disease (node_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than node-negative.
judgments.rationale: Nodal involvement as an adverse prognostic factor is a fundamental staging principle in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: Postmenopausal status (postmenopausal=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than premenopausal.
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status as a prognostic/treatment-stratifying variable is well established in breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Larger tumor size (tumor_size_cm, continuous) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Larger tumor size predicting worse outcomes is a core T-stage principle present in all standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher Ki-67 proliferation index (ki67_pct, continuous) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Higher Ki-67 indicating more aggressive disease and worse prognosis is standard textbook breast cancer pathology.

judgments.text: ER-positive disease (er_positive=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than ER-negative.
judgments.rationale: ER-positive disease being associated with better prognosis than ER-negative is a long-established guideline-level fact.

judgments.text: PR-positive disease (pr_positive=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than PR-negative.
judgments.rationale: PR-positive (hormone receptor-positive) breast cancer having better outcomes than PR-negative is a well-established prognostic association in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: HER2-positive disease (her2_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative (without targeted therapy as a univariate effect).
judgments.rationale: HER2-positive disease historically having worse prognosis than HER2-negative in the absence of targeted therapy is established textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: HER2-low disease (her2_low=1) is associated with different pfs_months than HER2-not-low.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low as a clinically relevant category with prognostic and predictive (trastuzumab deruxtecan) implications is now established in current breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: BRCA1 mutation (brca1_mutation=1) is associated with different pfs_months than wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation status as a prognostic and predictive biomarker (PARP inhibitor sensitivity) is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: BRCA2 mutation (brca2_mutation=1) is associated with different pfs_months than wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status as a prognostic and predictive biomarker is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: PIK3CA mutation (pik3ca_mutation=1) is associated with different pfs_months than wild-type.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as a recurrent driver with prognostic/predictive implications (alpelisib eligibility) is established in current breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor across cancers is a textbook-level association.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a long-established, guideline-recognized association across many cancers.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP/systemic inflammation as an adverse prognostic marker is a well-established association in oncology literature and prognostic scores.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR as an adverse prognostic marker is well-established across solid tumors and routinely cited in guidelines and prognostic literature.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
judgments.rationale: Anemia (low hemoglobin) is a standard adverse prognostic factor included in widely used risk models such as IMDC and IPI.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase is a textbook adverse prognostic marker, often reflecting bone or liver metastases.

judgments.text: Higher ast_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST is a recognized marker of hepatic involvement and poor prognosis, included in standard prognostic assessments.

judgments.text: Higher alt_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT similarly reflects hepatic dysfunction or metastatic burden and is a conventional adverse prognostic indicator.

judgments.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Hyperbilirubinemia is a standard marker of hepatic dysfunction and poor prognosis, routinely incorporated into organ-function and dosing guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated creatinine reflecting renal dysfunction is a well-known adverse prognostic and treatment-tolerance factor in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher sodium_meq_l is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
judgments.rationale: Hyponatremia is an established adverse prognostic factor across many cancers (e.g., included in IMDC-adjacent models and lung cancer prognostic literature).

judgments.text: Higher potassium_meq_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Potassium level is not an established prognostic biomarker for PFS in standard oncology guidelines, making a directional association with outcome non-standard.

judgments.text: Higher calcium_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (hypercalcemia is unfavorable).
judgments.rationale: Hypercalcemia of malignancy is a textbook adverse prognostic finding associated with advanced disease and shorter survival.

judgments.text: Higher bun_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflects renal dysfunction/poor performance status, a well-known nonspecific prognostic factor in oncology.

judgments.text: In a multivariable OLS adjusting for ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, age_years, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl, ki67_pct, tumor_size_cm, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, calcium_mg_dl, ECOG remains negatively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a textbook independent prognostic factor for PFS across virtually all solid tumors.

judgments.text: In the same multivariable model, albumin_g_dl remains positively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a positive prognostic marker independent of other factors is well-established in oncology guidelines and prognostic indices.

judgments.text: In the same multivariable model, weight_loss_pct_6mo remains negatively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss is a canonical adverse prognostic factor and a core component of cachexia-related prognostic scores in oncology.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy in hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer is a foundational guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's PFS benefit in HR+/HER2- breast cancer is established first-line guideline therapy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab efficacy in HER2-positive breast cancer is a textbook standard-of-care association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's benefit in BRCA-mutated breast cancer is an established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's PFS benefit in metastatic triple-negative/HR+ breast cancer is an approved guideline indication.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it (unadjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's benefit in PD-L1-positive triple-negative breast cancer is an established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Adjusting for prognostic features (ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, age, albumin, LDH, weight_loss, CRP, NLR, Hb, Ki67, tumor size, ALP, Ca, ER/PR/HER2/HER2-low, BRCA1/2, PIK3CA, node, postmenopausal, sex), treatment_tamoxifen remains independently associated with pfs_months (signed effect).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in breast cancer is a foundational guideline-level association, not novel.

judgments.text: Adjusting for prognostic features (ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, age, albumin, LDH, weight_loss, CRP, NLR, Hb, Ki67, tumor size, ALP, Ca, ER/PR/HER2/HER2-low, BRCA1/2, PIK3CA, node, postmenopausal, sex), treatment_palbociclib remains independently associated with pfs_months (signed effect).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor) PFS benefit in HR+ metastatic breast cancer is established guideline standard.

judgments.text: Adjusting for prognostic features (ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, age, albumin, LDH, weight_loss, CRP, NLR, Hb, Ki67, tumor size, ALP, Ca, ER/PR/HER2/HER2-low, BRCA1/2, PIK3CA, node, postmenopausal, sex), treatment_trastuzumab remains independently associated with pfs_months (signed effect).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab PFS benefit in HER2+ breast cancer is a textbook, guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Adjusting for prognostic features (ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, age, albumin, LDH, weight_loss, CRP, NLR, Hb, Ki67, tumor size, ALP, Ca, ER/PR/HER2/HER2-low, BRCA1/2, PIK3CA, node, postmenopausal, sex), treatment_olaparib remains independently associated with pfs_months (signed effect).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib PFS benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant breast cancer is an established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Adjusting for prognostic features (ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, age, albumin, LDH, weight_loss, CRP, NLR, Hb, Ki67, tumor size, ALP, Ca, ER/PR/HER2/HER2-low, BRCA1/2, PIK3CA, node, postmenopausal, sex), treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan remains independently associated with pfs_months (signed effect).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan PFS benefit in metastatic breast cancer (TNBC/HR+) is now guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Adjusting for prognostic features (ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, age, albumin, LDH, weight_loss, CRP, NLR, Hb, Ki67, tumor size, ALP, Ca, ER/PR/HER2/HER2-low, BRCA1/2, PIK3CA, node, postmenopausal, sex), treatment_pembrolizumab remains independently associated with pfs_months (signed effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab PFS benefit in PD-L1+ TNBC (KEYNOTE-355) is established guideline care.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months only in ER-positive (er_positive=1) patients; the er_positive × treatment_tamoxifen interaction is positive (treatment effect larger in ER+).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy restricted to ER-positive disease is a foundational textbook predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: Among ER-positive patients, treatment_tamoxifen has a positive effect on pfs_months; among ER-negative patients, the effect is null or negative.
judgments.rationale: Restated form of the canonical ER-positivity → tamoxifen response paradigm; not novel.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen × pr_positive interaction: tamoxifen effect is larger when pr_positive=1.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity as an ER-pathway co-marker enhancing endocrine therapy response is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab × her2_positive interaction is positive: trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is larger in HER2-positive patients.
judgments.rationale: HER2 positivity predicting trastuzumab benefit is the canonical predictive biomarker in oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Among HER2-positive patients, treatment_trastuzumab has a positive effect on pfs_months; among HER2-negative patients, the effect is null or negative.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit restricted to HER2-positive disease is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab × her2_low interaction: trastuzumab is not expected to benefit HER2-low patients (interaction with her2_low ≈ 0).
judgments.rationale: Lack of trastuzumab monotherapy benefit in HER2-low disease (which is treated with T-DXd, not trastuzumab) is established consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib × brca1_mutation interaction is positive: olaparib prolongs pfs_months selectively in BRCA1-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1-mutated breast/ovarian cancer is a standard guideline-level companion-diagnostic indication.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction is positive: olaparib prolongs pfs_months selectively in BRCA2-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated cancers is an established guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: Among patients carrying any BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation, treatment_olaparib has a positive effect on pfs_months; among non-carriers the effect is null or negative.
judgments.rationale: Selective olaparib efficacy in BRCA1/2 carriers versus non-carriers is the textbook synthetic-lethality paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib × er_positive interaction is positive: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months preferentially in ER-positive (HR+) patients.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's preferential efficacy in HR+/ER+ disease is the basis of its guideline-approved indication.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib × her2_positive interaction is negative: palbociclib has reduced or null effect in HER2-positive patients.
judgments.rationale: CDK4/6 inhibitors are not indicated in HER2+ disease per current guidelines, making reduced effect there expected.

judgments.text: In ER-positive AND HER2-negative patients (i.e., HR+/HER2-), treatment_palbociclib has a positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib efficacy in HR+/HER2- breast cancer is the standard FDA/guideline-approved use.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab × er_positive interaction: pembrolizumab effect is more favorable in ER-negative patients (negative interaction with er_positive).
judgments.rationale: Greater immunotherapy benefit in ER-negative (TNBC-enriched) breast cancer versus ER+ disease is established consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab has a positive effect on pfs_months in triple-negative breast cancer (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in TNBC is guideline-endorsed (KEYNOTE-355/522) standard of care.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a positive effect on pfs_months in triple-negative breast cancer.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan improving PFS in TNBC is established standard-of-care from ASCENT and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across all six treatments and 12 binary modifiers, at least one significant treatment×modifier interaction on pfs_months exists beyond the obvious receptor matches.
judgments.rationale: Claiming a significant treatment×modifier interaction beyond canonical receptor matches is a speculative cross-pathway discovery not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment effects are blunted in patients with ecog_ps>=2: every treatment × ecog_ps interaction is non-positive (treatment effect smaller as ECOG rises).
judgments.rationale: A universal claim that every treatment's benefit is non-positively modified by ECOG≥2 is a speculative cross-treatment generalization not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib's PFS benefit shrinks or disappears in patients with ecog_ps==2.
judgments.rationale: Specific attenuation of palbociclib PFS benefit at ECOG=2 is a subgroup-conditional claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab benefit (the trastuzumab × her2_positive effect) is preserved even in patients with brain mets (no negative trastuzumab × has_brain_mets interaction restricted to HER2+).
judgments.rationale: Preservation of trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ patients with brain metastases (no negative interaction) is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib benefit (in BRCA carriers) is attenuated by high LDH (negative olaparib × ldh_u_l interaction within BRCA carriers).
judgments.rationale: LDH-dependent attenuation of olaparib benefit within BRCA carriers is a mechanistic biomarker-modifier hypothesis not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among ER-positive AND HER2-negative AND ECOG<=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib produces a clear positive PFS effect.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+/HER2−/good-PS patients is the established guideline indication (PALOMA trials).

judgments.text: For each treatment, the patient subgroup with the largest adjusted PFS benefit is defined by 1-2 binary biomarkers that match canonical breast-cancer biology (e.g., olaparib in BRCA carriers, trastuzumab in HER2+, tamoxifen/palbociclib in ER+).
judgments.rationale: Recovering canonical biomarker-defined subgroups (BRCA/olaparib, HER2+/trastuzumab, ER+/endocrine+CDK4/6) restates established guideline biology.

judgments.text: Greedy 3-feature conjunctions search recovers, for each treatment, a small subgroup definition where the adjusted PFS benefit is maximal — matching canonical biomarker-defined subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Recovering canonical biomarker subgroups via conjunction search is a methodological restatement of established guideline associations.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib produces a positive PFS effect specifically in patients carrying brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; in non-carriers the adjusted effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy restricted to BRCA1/2 carriers with null effect in non-carriers is the established guideline indication (OlympiAD/OlympiA).

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab produces a positive PFS effect in HER2-positive (her2_positive=1) patients only; in HER2-negative patients the effect is null or negative.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit restricted to HER2-positive disease is the foundational paradigm for HER2-targeted therapy and appears in every breast cancer guideline.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib produces a positive PFS effect specifically in ER-positive AND HER2-negative patients; in HER2-positive or ER-negative patients the effect is null or negative.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor) benefit in ER-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer is the standard-of-care indication and core guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen produces a positive PFS effect specifically in ER-positive patients; in ER-negative patients the effect is null or negative.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy restricted to ER-positive disease is one of the oldest and most established predictive biomarker relationships in oncology.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan produces a positive PFS effect in ER-negative AND HER2-negative (i.e., TNBC-leaning) patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed for metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, making this association established consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab produces a positive PFS effect in ER-negative AND HER2-negative (TNBC-leaning) patients; in ER-positive disease the effect is null or negative.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's preferential benefit in TNBC (vs ER-positive disease) is established via KEYNOTE-355/522 and is in current breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: In a joint multivariable model that includes all six treatments and the canonical treatment-by-biomarker interactions, each canonical interaction is positive and significant: tamoxifen×er_positive, palbociclib×er_positive (with negative palbociclib×her2_positive), trastuzumab×her2_positive, olaparib×brca_any, sacituzumab×(er_neg & her2_neg), pembrolizumab×(er_neg & her2_neg).
judgments.rationale: This is a composite restatement of six individually canonical treatment-biomarker pairings, each of which is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months specifically in patients with brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; in BRCA1/2 wild-type patients the adjusted effect is not positive.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit restricted to BRCA1/2-mutated breast cancer is the FDA-approved, guideline-endorsed indication (OlympiAD/OlympiA).

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_trastuzumab increases pfs_months specifically in her2_positive=1 patients; in her2_positive=0 the adjusted effect is not positive.
judgments.rationale: Restating the canonical HER2-positive trastuzumab indication that defines HER2-targeted therapy.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months specifically in ER-positive patients without HER2 amplification (er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0); HER2-positivity suppresses any palbociclib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's indication in ER-positive/HER2-negative disease (and lack of role in HER2-positive disease where anti-HER2 therapy dominates) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_tamoxifen increases pfs_months specifically in er_positive=1 patients; in er_positive=0 the adjusted effect is not positive.
judgments.rationale: ER-positivity as the predictive biomarker for tamoxifen benefit is foundational, textbook-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months specifically in ER-negative AND HER2-negative patients (er_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0); ER-positivity suppresses any benefit.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic triple-negative (ER-/HER2-) breast cancer is established in guidelines (ASCENT trial, FDA approval), so restricting benefit to ER-/HER2- patients is standard paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months specifically in ER-negative AND HER2-negative patients (er_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0); ER-positivity suppresses any benefit.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in triple-negative (ER-/HER2-) breast cancer is established guideline consensus (KEYNOTE-355/522, FDA approval in TNBC), making ER-/HER2- restriction a known association rather than novel.

judgments.text: PFS distribution in ds001_breast is unimodal with mean near 5 months and a long right tail; baseline PFS varies in a clinically meaningful way (>1 month) across ECOG performance status (0/1/2).
judgments.rationale: Describing PFS distribution shape and ECOG-based variation is descriptive epidemiology consistent with well-known prognostic effect of performance status.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope) in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic factor for shorter PFS is a standard clinical observation, not novel.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in PFS in breast cancer cohorts is a basic descriptive comparison without novel mechanistic insight.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is textbook prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease being associated with shorter PFS than earlier stage is established staging prognosis.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without brain metastases.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring shorter PFS is well-established in guideline-level breast cancer prognosis.

judgments.text: Patients with node_positive=1 have shorter pfs_months than node-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive disease predicting worse outcomes is a canonical prognostic factor in breast cancer.

judgments.text: Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Larger tumor size correlating with shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic relationship.

judgments.text: er_positive=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than ER- patients.
judgments.rationale: ER status differences in PFS are foundational to breast cancer prognosis and guideline classification.

judgments.text: pr_positive=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than PR- patients.
judgments.rationale: PR status as a prognostic marker for PFS in breast cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: her2_positive=1 patients have shorter mean pfs_months than HER2-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: HER2-positive status as a prognostic/predictive factor in breast cancer is a foundational guideline-level association.

judgments.text: her2_low=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than non-HER2-low patients.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low as a distinct PFS-prognostic category beyond its predictive role for trastuzumab deruxtecan is not established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: postmenopausal=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than premenopausal patients.
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status as a stratifier of outcomes and treatment selection is standard textbook breast cancer knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct (proliferation) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Ki-67 as a proliferation marker linked to worse prognosis is well-established in breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: brca1_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than non-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation effects on breast cancer outcomes and treatment response are guideline-established.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than non-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation prognostic/predictive associations are standard guideline content.

judgments.text: pik3ca_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than non-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as a recognized biomarker (e.g., for alpelisib eligibility) and prognostic factor appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl (better nutrition/liver synthetic function) is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a PFS predictor in breast cancer is a general prognostic concept but not a guideline-endorsed disease-specific biomarker.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l (tumor burden marker) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: LDH is guideline-standard in melanoma/lymphoma but not an established PFS biomarker in breast cancer treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (often a marker of bone/liver involvement) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Alkaline phosphatase as a quantitative PFS predictor is not a guideline-endorsed breast cancer biomarker beyond nonspecific metastasis screening.

judgments.text: Liver enzymes (ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl) are inversely associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated liver enzymes reflecting hepatic dysfunction or metastatic burden are well-established negative prognostic markers in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Renal markers (creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl) are inversely associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Renal dysfunction as a negative prognostic factor is standard textbook knowledge in oncology performance status assessment.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia as a negative prognostic factor (and conversely higher hemoglobin associated with better outcomes) is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher inflammatory markers (crp_mg_l, nlr) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP and NLR as adverse prognostic markers are well-established in oncology literature and increasingly in guidelines.

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss as adverse prognostic factors are foundational textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Electrolyte derangements (sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) have detectable associations with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Electrolyte abnormalities (e.g., hyponatremia, hypercalcemia of malignancy) as adverse prognostic markers are standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen improving outcomes vs untreated in hormone-responsive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor) improving PFS vs untreated control in HR+ breast cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab improving outcomes in HER2+ breast cancer vs untreated is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS in BRCA-mutated breast cancer vs untreated is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Unadjusted PFS effect of sacituzumab govitecan vs untreated is a basic descriptive comparison, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab (vs untreated) changes mean pfs_months in the unadjusted comparison.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_tamoxifen effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen having a residual PFS effect after adjusting for standard prognostic covariates is consistent with established endocrine therapy benefit.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib retaining a PFS effect after standard covariate adjustment reflects established CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit in HR+ breast cancer.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab retaining a PFS effect after adjustment is the well-established HER2-directed therapy benefit.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib retaining a PFS effect after adjustment aligns with known PARP inhibitor activity in BRCA-mutant disease.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan retaining a PFS effect after adjustment matches established ADC activity in metastatic TNBC/HR+ disease.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, tumor size, albumin, LDH, and ki67, the treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months remains nonzero.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab retaining a PFS effect after adjustment is consistent with established immunotherapy benefit in selected breast cancer subsets.

judgments.text: A multivariable OLS over all features and treatments will show ECOG, stage IV, has_brain_mets, albumin (positive), LDH (negative), HER2 status, ER status, and treatment_palbociclib as among the largest-magnitude predictors of pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, LDH, HER2/ER status, and CDK4/6 inhibitor use being top PFS predictors is textbook prognostic/predictive knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen interacts with er_positive: tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months only in er_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit being restricted to ER-positive patients is foundational, guideline-level breast cancer biology.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib interacts with er_positive: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months only in er_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit restricted to ER-positive breast cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab interacts with her2_positive: trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months only in her2_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab efficacy limited to HER2-positive disease is foundational guideline consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib interacts with brca1_mutation: olaparib prolongs pfs_months in brca1_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1-mutated cancer is a standard guideline-endorsed association.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib interacts with brca2_mutation: olaparib prolongs pfs_months in brca2_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated cancer is a standard guideline-endorsed association.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan interacts with her2_low: sacituzumab govitecan prolongs pfs_months in her2_low=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan activity in HER2-low breast cancer is now reflected in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab interacts with her2_positive: pembrolizumab pfs_months effect differs by her2_positive status.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive disease is textbook standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit within ER-positive patients is established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit within HER2-positive patients is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1/2-mutated patients is standard guideline-endorsed practice.

judgments.text: Within her2_low=1 patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan recipients have longer pfs_months than non-recipients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in HER2-low metastatic breast cancer is established (TROPiCS-02) and reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment-by-ECOG interactions exist for at least one treatment, indicating performance-status-dependent benefit.
judgments.rationale: Performance-status-dependent treatment benefit is a generic, guideline-acknowledged consideration across oncology.

judgments.text: Treatment-by-stage_iv interactions exist (treatment effect varies between stage IV and earlier stage).
judgments.rationale: Stage-dependent treatment effects (metastatic vs. earlier-stage) are foundational to guideline indications and not novel.

judgments.text: Treatment-by-postmenopausal interactions exist for hormonally targeted therapies (tamoxifen, palbociclib).
judgments.rationale: Menopausal-status considerations for tamoxifen and palbociclib (e.g., AI partner choice, ovarian suppression) are explicit in current breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment-by-brain_mets interactions exist (treatment effect varies in patients with vs. without brain metastases).
judgments.rationale: Differential treatment efficacy in patients with brain metastases (CNS penetration) is well-established and routinely discussed in guidelines.

judgments.text: For treatment_tamoxifen, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: An agnostic interaction screen proposing an unspecified modifier of tamoxifen benefit beyond established ER/menopausal biology would not appear in current guidelines.

judgments.text: For treatment_palbociclib, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: Discovery-mode search for a non-prespecified palbociclib effect modifier across 31 features goes beyond guideline-level knowledge of CDK4/6i predictors.

judgments.text: For treatment_trastuzumab, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: An exhaustive screen for trastuzumab effect heterogeneity beyond HER2 status is speculative and not represented in guideline consensus.

judgments.text: For treatment_olaparib, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: Searching agnostically for olaparib effect modifiers beyond BRCA/HRD status is speculative subgroup-conditional discovery not in guidelines.

judgments.text: For treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: Agnostic interaction screening for sacituzumab govitecan modifiers beyond HER2-low/TNBC status is speculative and goes beyond current guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: For treatment_pembrolizumab, an exhaustive treatment × feature interaction screen across all 31 candidate features will identify a top modifier with interaction p < 0.10 indicating biologically meaningful effect heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: Generic methodological claim about a screen finding any interaction at p<0.10; makes no specific biological assertion beyond consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is positive and large only in er_positive=1 patients (effect ~+1.6 months) and ~zero in er_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib is approved and indicated specifically for HR+/HER2- breast cancer; ER-restricted benefit is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months becomes more negative as ecog_ps increases (worse PS, more harm/no benefit).
judgments.rationale: Reduced immunotherapy benefit with worsening performance status is a well-established general oncology principle reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan effect on pfs_months differs by creatinine_mg_dl (interaction p<0.05 in screen).
judgments.rationale: Renal function (creatinine) as an effect modifier for sacituzumab govitecan is not a recognized predictive biomarker in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months is more positive in postmenopausal=1 patients than in postmenopausal=0 patients (top modifier from screen).
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status is not an established modifier of olaparib benefit; PARP inhibitor use is guided by BRCA/HRD status, not menopausal state.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib benefit is concentrated where her2_positive=0 (i.e., the ER+/HER2- subgroup).
judgments.rationale: ER+/HER2- is the standard guideline-defined indication for palbociclib; this is textbook consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated patients is similar in BRCA1+ and BRCA2+ subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Comparable olaparib efficacy across BRCA1 and BRCA2 carriers is the established consensus from pivotal trials and guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen benefit, if present, is restricted to ER+ postmenopausal patients.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit is well-established in premenopausal ER+ patients; restricting it to postmenopausal contradicts standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan benefit is concentrated in HER2-low AND ER- (TNBC-like) patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's TNBC (ER-/HER2-low) indication is a guideline-endorsed standard from ASCENT and subsequent approvals.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC is established by KEYNOTE-355 and is part of standard guideline recommendations for PD-L1+ TNBC.

judgments.text: After adjusting for prognostic confounders within ER+ patients, treatment_palbociclib still increases pfs_months by ~1.5 months on average.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS in ER+ breast cancer is established guideline knowledge from PALOMA trials.

judgments.text: After adjustment within ER+ patients, treatment_tamoxifen has an effect on pfs_months that is not distinguishable from zero.
judgments.rationale: Claiming tamoxifen has no effect in ER+ patients contradicts established guideline consensus, making it a novel (and likely dataset-specific) finding.

judgments.text: After adjustment within HER2+ patients, treatment_trastuzumab has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab having no PFS benefit in HER2+ disease contradicts standard-of-care guidelines and would be a novel claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment within BRCA-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib has a small positive effect on pfs_months whose adjusted p>0.05.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib showing modest non-significant PFS benefit in BRCA-mutated patients aligns with known guideline-level efficacy expectations.

judgments.text: After adjustment within HER2-low patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no detectable effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan having no benefit in HER2-low patients is contrary to emerging guideline support and would be a non-standard finding.

judgments.text: After adjustment within TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab has no detectable beneficial effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab having no benefit in TNBC contradicts KEYNOTE-355/522 guideline-level evidence and is a novel/contrary claim.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib benefit is similar in BRCA1+ and BRCA2+ patients (no statistically detectable difference between the two genes).
judgments.rationale: Direct equivalence of olaparib benefit between BRCA1 and BRCA2 carriers is a subgroup-specific comparison not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen has no benefit in ER+/PR+ or ER+/PR- after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen having no benefit across PR+ and PR- ER+ subgroups contradicts standard endocrine therapy guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib retains its large adjusted positive effect on pfs_months in the ER+/HER2- subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+/HER2- is precisely the guideline-endorsed indication, not novel.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab has no benefit in HER2+ regardless of ECOG.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ regardless of ECOG contradicts guideline standard of care and is a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no benefit in HER2-low/ER- after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's activity in HER2-low/ER- (TNBC) is established TROPiCS-2/ASCENT-derived guideline knowledge, so claiming no benefit there is a restatement (negation) of standard paradigm rather than a novel cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit (or harm) in TNBC does not differ by has_brain_mets.
judgments.rationale: Whether pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC is modified by brain metastases is not a guideline-level established fact and represents a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib has a three-way interaction with er_positive and her2_positive: benefit is largest in ER+/HER2- and smaller (or absent) when HER2+.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's benefit being restricted to ER+/HER2- disease is the standard guideline indication and not novel.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a three-way interaction with her2_low and er_positive (largest benefit in HER2-low/ER-).
judgments.rationale: Largest sacituzumab govitecan benefit in HER2-low/ER- restates the established TNBC/HER2-low indication from ASCENT/TROPiCS-2.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab has a three-way interaction with er_positive and her2_positive consistent with TNBC-restricted benefit.
judgments.rationale: TNBC-restricted pembrolizumab benefit (KEYNOTE-355/522) is the established guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib has no three-way BRCA1 × BRCA2 interaction (effect is through either gene independently).
judgments.rationale: Whether olaparib effect requires a BRCA1×BRCA2 interaction versus independent single-gene effects is a mechanistic claim not stated in guidelines, which simply lump gBRCA1/2 together.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab has no three-way interaction with her2_positive and node_positive.
judgments.rationale: Absence of a three-way trastuzumab × HER2 × nodal-status interaction is a specific mechanistic/subgroup claim beyond the guideline statement that HER2+ disease benefits from trastuzumab regardless of nodal status.

judgments.text: An exhaustive scan of two-binary-feature subgroups will confirm that treatment_palbociclib has its largest, most significant subgroup effects within er_positive=1 and pr_positive=1 (and within pik3ca_mutation=0).
judgments.rationale: Largest palbociclib effect within ER+/PR+ (and PIK3CA wild-type) tracks the established endocrine-sensitive CDK4/6i indication.

judgments.text: For treatment_olaparib, the strongest two-feature subgroups by p-value involve brca1_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's strongest signals arising in BRCA1-mutant subgroups recapitulates the established gBRCA-directed indication.

judgments.text: For treatments without true biology-driven targets in this dataset (tamoxifen, trastuzumab, sacituzumab_govitecan, pembrolizumab), the smallest-p subgroups will not be biologically aligned and likely reflect chance.
judgments.rationale: A meta-claim that smallest-p subgroups for non-targeted agents reflect chance rather than biology is an analytical/statistical assertion not found in clinical guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+ patients, treatment_palbociclib benefit is approximately uniform across age tertiles, ECOG categories, albumin tertiles, and LDH tertiles (i.e., effect is driven by ER status, not these continuous prognostics).
judgments.rationale: That CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit is driven by ER status rather than age/ECOG/labs is consistent with guideline-level understanding of palbociclib's indication.

judgments.text: Within HER2+ patients, treatment_trastuzumab effect remains ~zero across all clinical strata.
judgments.rationale: Claiming trastuzumab has ~zero effect across all strata in HER2+ disease contradicts established guideline consensus that HER2-targeted therapy improves outcomes.

judgments.text: Within BRCA-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib effect remains modest (<1 month) across age, ECOG, albumin, and LDH strata.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines describe substantial PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutated disease, so a uniformly modest (<1 month) effect across strata would contradict standard expectations.

judgments.text: Within HER2-low patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan does not show benefit in any age/ECOG/albumin/LDH stratum.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan is guideline-endorsed in HER2-low metastatic breast cancer, so absence of benefit in any stratum would go beyond established consensus.

judgments.text: Within TNBC patients, treatment_pembrolizumab benefit does not vary materially by age/ECOG/albumin/LDH strata (i.e., no further enriched subgroup is evident).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines treat pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC as driven by PD-L1/TNBC status without further age/ECOG/lab-based subgroup enrichment, matching this claim.

judgments.text: An exhaustive search across all ordered pairs of binary features for treatment_palbociclib will identify er_positive=1 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 (or pr_positive=1 AND pik3ca_mutation=0) as the single largest-effect subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Specifying a PIK3CA-wildtype-conditional largest-effect subgroup for palbociclib is a cross-pathway interaction not part of guideline prescribing criteria.

judgments.text: An exhaustive search across all binary feature pairs will not identify any subgroup with a >0.5-month positive treatment_trastuzumab effect at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: A pipeline-wide negative result asserting no binary-pair subgroup shows trastuzumab benefit contradicts the guideline expectation of HER2+ benefit.

judgments.text: An exhaustive search across all binary feature pairs will identify brca1_mutation=1 AND node_positive=1 as a positive-effect olaparib subgroup with effect >0.5 months.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive status is not a guideline-level effect modifier for olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated disease, making this a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: For treatment_pembrolizumab, exhaustive subgroup search yields predominantly negative-effect (harm) subgroups in non-TNBC strata.
judgments.rationale: A claim that pembrolizumab is predominantly harmful across non-TNBC subgroups goes beyond guideline statements, which simply restrict its breast cancer indication rather than asserting harm.

judgments.text: Final candidate: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months (positive effect) in patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0; effect is essentially zero outside this subgroup; treatment-by-subgroup interaction is highly significant.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit confined to ER+/HER2- disease with a strong treatment-by-subgroup interaction mirrors the standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: Final candidate: treatment_olaparib has a small positive PFS effect in patients with brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; effect is much weaker (and not significant after adjustment) outside the BRCA+ subgroup.
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit restricted to BRCA1/2-mutant tumors is a foundational, guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Final candidate: treatment_tamoxifen has no detectable PFS benefit in er_positive=1 patients in this cohort; the unadjusted within-ER+ effect is ~0 (p>0.4) and the interaction with ER status is not significant.
judgments.rationale: A cohort-specific null for tamoxifen in ER+ disease is a negative observation against established paradigm, not a new mechanistic or subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Final candidate: treatment_trastuzumab has no detectable PFS benefit in her2_positive=1 patients in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Null effect of trastuzumab in HER2+ is simply absence of the expected guideline effect, not a novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: Final candidate: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has no detectable PFS benefit in her2_low=1 patients (or in HER2-low/ER- subgroup) in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: A null finding for sacituzumab in HER2-low is just a cohort-level negative result, not a new biological claim.

judgments.text: Final candidate: treatment_pembrolizumab has no detectable PFS benefit in TNBC patients (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0); the overall pembrolizumab main effect is slightly negative and concentrated in non-TNBC, ECOG>=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming a negative pembrolizumab effect concentrated in non-TNBC, ECOG>=1 patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional pattern not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In a joint model that includes all main effects, all six treatments, and all six biology-driven treatment × biomarker interaction terms (palbociclib × er_positive; tamoxifen × er_positive; trastuzumab × her2_positive; olaparib × BRCA1-or-BRCA2; sacituzumab × her2_low; pembrolizumab × TNBC), only the palbociclib × er_positive interaction will remain robustly significant (p<0.001) with a coefficient near +1.5 months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib × ER+ being the dominant interaction restates the established CDK4/6 inhibitor + HR+ paradigm.

judgments.text: In the same joint model, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets (negative), and albumin_g_dl (positive) will be the dominant prognostic main effects, each shifting expected pfs_months by >0.5 month per unit/level.
judgments.rationale: ECOG, stage IV, brain metastases, and albumin are textbook prognostic factors across solid tumors.

judgments.text: In the joint model, none of the other five treatments has a significant marginal main effect on pfs_months at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: A joint-model null for the other treatments is a model-specific negative result, not a guideline-departing mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Best-supported subgroup hypothesis for treatment_palbociclib: PFS benefit is concentrated in patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0; in this triple-defined subgroup the unadjusted effect is approximately +2.9 months. The pik3ca_mutation=1 status appears to suppress the benefit even within ER+/HER2- patients.
judgments.rationale: Asserting PIK3CA mutation suppresses palbociclib benefit even within ER+/HER2- disease is a subgroup-conditional predictive biomarker claim beyond current guidelines.

judgments.text: Best-supported subgroup hypothesis for treatment_olaparib: small positive PFS effect (~+0.35 months) in patients with brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; effect is not robust to adjustment but is the only directionally biologically plausible subgroup signal in the cohort.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib activity in BRCA1/2-mutant tumors is the canonical, guideline-established PARP-inhibitor indication.

judgments.text: Best-supported subgroup hypothesis for treatment_tamoxifen, treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, and treatment_pembrolizumab: NO biology-aligned subgroup with statistically supported PFS benefit was found. For each, the within-target-biomarker effect is ≤|0.1| months and not significant after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: A null finding asserting that biomarker-aligned subgroups (HER2 for trastuzumab, ER for tamoxifen, TROP2/PD-L1 contexts) show no PFS benefit contradicts established guideline-level efficacy paradigms and would not appear in a textbook.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having shorter PFS than non-stage IV is a fundamental staging principle in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases predicting shorter PFS is a well-established prognostic factor in standard oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status correlating with shorter PFS is a foundational prognostic association in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor for PFS is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: er_positive=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than er_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: ER-positive breast cancer having longer PFS than ER-negative is a well-established prognostic and predictive marker.

judgments.text: pr_positive=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than pr_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity conferring better prognosis is a standard hormone receptor association in breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: her2_positive=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: While HER2-positive historically had worse prognosis, in the modern anti-HER2 therapy era this directionality is paradigm-consistent and guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: her2_low=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than her2_low=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low as a distinct prognostic category associated with longer PFS (vs. HER2-zero) is not established guideline consensus and remains an area of active investigation.

judgments.text: brca1_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than brca1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation association with more aggressive (often triple-negative) disease and shorter PFS is recognized in standard breast cancer references.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutations are not typically associated with shorter PFS in guidelines—often linked to luminal/ER+ disease with comparable or better outcomes—so this directionality goes beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: pik3ca_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as an adverse prognostic factor in breast cancer is well-established and appears in standard references.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher Ki-67 indicating worse prognosis and shorter PFS is a textbook association in breast cancer.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is a standard, guideline-level association across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a well-known textbook finding.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with shorter PFS is a conventional and expected prognostic relationship.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all features and treatments simultaneously, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ecog_ps, her2_positive, pik3ca_mutation, ki67_pct, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and ldh_u_l independently shorten pfs_months, while er_positive, age_years, and albumin_g_dl independently lengthen it.
judgments.rationale: A multivariable claim specifying the independent direction of all these covariates simultaneously goes beyond any single guideline statement.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_palbociclib has a positive coefficient on pfs_months while the other treatments have null coefficients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming palbociclib is the only treatment with an independent PFS benefit after full adjustment is a specific cross-treatment comparative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_palbociclib=0 in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib prolonging PFS is established from PALOMA trials and is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The other five treatments (tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab_govitecan, pembrolizumab) have small or null overall effects on pfs_months when assessed univariately.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab govitecan, and pembrolizumab all have null univariate PFS effects in this cohort contradicts guideline-level expectations and is a dataset-specific claim.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_palbociclib is larger in er_positive=1 patients than in er_positive=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's benefit being concentrated in ER-positive patients is the established indication and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_palbociclib is smaller (or reversed) in her2_positive=1 patients than in her2_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Reduced endocrine/CDK4/6 efficacy in HER2-positive disease aligns with the standard paradigm that HER2-positive tumors require HER2-directed therapy, not novel.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_palbociclib is smaller in pik3ca_mutation=1 patients than in pik3ca_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: A negative PIK3CA interaction with palbociclib is not part of guideline consensus; PIK3CA status does not currently dictate CDK4/6 inhibitor selection.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_palbociclib is larger in pr_positive=1 patients than in pr_positive=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Greater CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit in hormone receptor-positive (PR+) disease reflects established endocrine-sensitivity paradigm.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_palbociclib decreases as ki67_pct increases (negative interaction with ki67_pct).
judgments.rationale: A continuous Ki-67-dependent attenuation of palbociclib benefit is not established in guidelines, which do not use Ki-67 to select CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_trastuzumab is larger in her2_positive=1 patients than in her2_positive=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: HER2-positivity predicting trastuzumab benefit is the canonical, guideline-defining biomarker association.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_olaparib is larger in brca1_mutation=1 patients than in brca1_mutation=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation predicting PARP inhibitor (olaparib) benefit is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_olaparib is larger in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation predicting olaparib benefit is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_olaparib is larger in patients with brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 than in BRCA-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: Pooled BRCA1/2 mutation status predicting olaparib benefit is the established indication.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab is larger in triple-negative patients (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) than in non-TNBC patients.
judgments.rationale: Greater pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC versus non-TNBC reflects current guideline-endorsed use (e.g., KEYNOTE-355/522).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in TNBC patients with high ki67_pct (above median).
judgments.rationale: Conditioning pembrolizumab benefit on high Ki-67 within TNBC is a subgroup-specific claim not reflected in guidelines, which use PD-L1 (not Ki-67) for selection.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is larger in has_brain_mets=1 patients than in has_brain_mets=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit being larger in brain-metastasis patients is a speculative subgroup-specific claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit from treatment_tamoxifen is larger in er_positive=1 than in er_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive vs ER-negative breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Among er_positive=1 patients, the tamoxifen pfs_months benefit is larger in postmenopausal=1 than in postmenopausal=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Greater tamoxifen benefit in postmenopausal ER+ patients reflects long-established adjuvant endocrine therapy data.

judgments.text: In the joint subgroup defined by er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by more than 2 months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+/HER2- breast cancer (with PIK3CA-wt subset) is standard guideline-based CDK4/6 inhibitor practice.

judgments.text: Adding pr_positive=1 to ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt further increases the palbociclib pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity within ER+/HER2- disease as a luminal marker associated with endocrine/CDK4/6 benefit is well-recognized.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/PR+ patients, restricting to low ki67_pct (below median) further increases the palbociclib pfs_months benefit, identifying a luminal-A-like subgroup with the maximal effect.
judgments.rationale: Luminal A-like (ER+/PR+/HER2-/low Ki67) tumors having maximal endocrine-based therapy benefit is textbook breast cancer biology.

judgments.text: Within has_brain_mets=1 patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in patients with low ki67_pct (below median).
judgments.rationale: Concentrating sacituzumab govitecan benefit in brain-met patients with low Ki67 is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: A 2- or 3-feature binary subgroup exists in which treatment_trastuzumab significantly increases pfs_months by more than 0.5 months.
judgments.rationale: An unspecified 2-/3-feature subgroup driving trastuzumab PFS benefit beyond HER2 status would be a novel non-guideline finding.

judgments.text: Beyond the BRCA-mutated subgroup, an additional 2-/3-feature binary subgroup exists where treatment_olaparib substantially increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in a non-BRCA-defined multi-feature subgroup goes beyond established HRD/BRCA-based guideline indications.

judgments.text: A multi-feature subgroup involving has_brain_mets=1 AND low ki67_pct exists in which treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months by ~0.4-0.7 months.
judgments.rationale: A brain-mets plus low-Ki67 subgroup defining sacituzumab govitecan benefit is a speculative compound predictor not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: No 2-/3-feature subgroup exists in which treatment_pembrolizumab significantly increases pfs_months; effects are null or slightly negative across all candidate subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab lacking benefit in HR+/HER2- breast cancer is consistent with guideline consensus that ICIs are not standard outside TNBC/PD-L1+ settings.

judgments.text: The exhaustive 2-/3-feature search confirms that treatment_palbociclib produces its largest pfs_months benefits (diff > 3 mo) in subgroups that combine pik3ca_wt AND low ki67_pct, with additional gain from er_positive=1, pr_positive=1, and her2_negative.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER+/PR+/HER2- with low Ki67 and PIK3CA-wt is essentially the canonical CDK4/6i indication and matches guideline expectations.

judgments.text: A small positive subgroup for treatment_tamoxifen exists in brca1_mutation=1 patients (diff ~0.4-0.5 mo), though it is borderline after multiple testing.
judgments.rationale: A tamoxifen-specific PFS benefit restricted to BRCA1-mutant patients is not a guideline-endorsed predictive biomarker and would be a novel subgroup claim.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in HER2+/ER- (HER2-driven, non-luminal) patients than in HER2+/ER+ patients.
judgments.rationale: Although HER2-directed therapy is standard, claiming differential trastuzumab benefit favoring HER2+/ER- over HER2+/ER+ (luminal vs non-luminal HER2) is a mechanistic subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by ~5 months relative to no palbociclib in the joint subgroup defined by er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND pr_positive=1 AND ki67_pct < median (~14.6%). Outside this subgroup the palbociclib effect is small (~+0.35 mo).
judgments.rationale: Conditioning palbociclib's large benefit specifically on PIK3CA-wt plus low Ki67 within ER+/PR+/HER2- is a finer-grained predictive claim than current guidelines, which recommend CDK4/6i broadly in HR+/HER2- regardless of PIK3CA or Ki67.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months by ~0.4 months in the subgroup defined by has_brain_mets=1 AND ki67_pct < median; effect is null elsewhere.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit confined to brain-mets-positive plus low-Ki67 patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months by ~0.35 months in the subgroup defined by brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; effect is null in BRCA-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit limited to BRCA1/2-mutant patients with no effect in BRCA wild-type is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_trastuzumab has no clinically meaningful main effect or subgroup effect on pfs_months in this cohort, including in the her2_positive=1 subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no trastuzumab benefit even within HER2-positive patients contradicts established guideline consensus and is therefore a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab has no positive subgroup effect on pfs_months in this cohort; in subgroups defined by frailty markers (ecog_ps=2 + low_alb + low_ki67) the association is mildly negative.
judgments.rationale: A specific frailty-marker subgroup (ECOG 2 + low albumin + low Ki67) showing mildly negative pembrolizumab association is a speculative cross-feature claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_tamoxifen has no robust pfs_months benefit on the cohort or in canonical ER+/postmenopausal subgroups; a borderline positive signal in BRCA1+ patients is likely chance after multiple-testing correction.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen lacking robust benefit in this cohort with a borderline BRCA1 signal dismissed as chance aligns with standard guideline framing and offers no novel predictive claim.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: Older age correlating with outcomes is a standard prognostic consideration, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association).
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic association across oncology.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv = 0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than non-stage IV is foundational staging-guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with has_brain_mets = 0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring worse PFS is a well-established adverse prognostic factor in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with er_positive = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with er_positive = 0.
judgments.rationale: ER-positive breast cancer having more favorable outcomes than ER-negative is standard guideline/textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with pik3ca_mutation = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without the mutation.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as a negative PFS prognostic marker is not an established guideline association and direction is debated.
