judgments.text: Patients with her2_positive = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without HER2 amplification.
judgments.rationale: In the modern anti-HER2 therapy era, HER2-positive disease is not guideline-established as having shorter PFS, making this directional claim non-standard.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as an adverse prognostic marker is a well-established standard oncology finding.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: High Ki67 proliferation index predicting worse outcomes is standard pathology/guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with pr_positive = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with pr_positive = 0.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity as a favorable prognostic marker in breast cancer is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_low = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with her2_low = 0.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low as an independent prognostic factor (separate from T-DXd response) is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with brca1_mutation = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without the mutation.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation as a marginal predictor of shorter PFS is not a standard guideline claim; BRCA status is typically discussed for PARP/platinum sensitivity rather than as a prognostic marker.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is universally established across oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, age_years remains positively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Older age being positively associated with PFS after multivariable adjustment is counterintuitive and not a guideline finding.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, ecog_ps remains negatively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is foundational guideline/textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, stage_iv remains negatively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease predicting shorter PFS is core staging-based prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_palbociclib has a positive main-effect coefficient on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS is the established basis for its approval in HR+ breast cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Marginal PFS benefit from palbociclib aligns directly with guideline-endorsed efficacy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have shorter pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Marginal shorter PFS with pembrolizumab contradicts guideline expectations of benefit and is not a standard textbook claim (likely reflects confounding by indication, but the hypothesis itself goes beyond consensus).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have different pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Marginal PFS difference with trastuzumab is the established HER2-targeted therapy benefit found in any breast cancer guideline.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have different pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Marginal PFS difference with olaparib reflects the well-known PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutant/HRD breast cancer per standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have different pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Marginal PFS difference with tamoxifen is the foundational endocrine therapy effect in HR+ breast cancer documented in every textbook.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have different pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Marginal PFS difference with sacituzumab govitecan reflects its established benefit in metastatic TNBC/HR+ breast cancer per current guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, treatment_palbociclib retains a positive main-effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A persistent positive whole-cohort palbociclib effect after adjusting for biomarkers like LDH, CRP, NLR, and brain mets goes beyond the guideline-restricted HR+/HER2- indication.

judgments.text: After covariate adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has a negative or null marginal effect on pfs_months across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: A null-or-negative whole-cohort pembrolizumab effect after covariate adjustment contradicts the guideline narrative of benefit in PD-L1+/TNBC subsets and is a non-obvious cross-cohort claim.

judgments.text: After covariate adjustment, treatment_trastuzumab has a null marginal effect on pfs_months across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: A null whole-cohort trastuzumab effect after adjustment contradicts the established HER2-driven benefit and implies the marginal signal is confounded, which is not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: After covariate adjustment, treatment_olaparib has a null marginal effect on pfs_months across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: A null whole-cohort olaparib effect after adjustment would contradict the guideline-level PARP benefit and suggests confounding by covariates rather than a true biomarker-restricted effect.

judgments.text: After covariate adjustment, treatment_tamoxifen has a null marginal effect on pfs_months across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: A null whole-cohort tamoxifen effect after adjustment contradicts the canonical endocrine-therapy benefit and is a non-guideline, confounding-driven claim.

judgments.text: After covariate adjustment, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a null marginal effect on pfs_months across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: A null whole-cohort sacituzumab govitecan effect after adjustment contradicts its established PFS benefit and implies covariate confounding rather than a guideline-consistent finding.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab improves pfs_months specifically in her2_positive=1 patients (positive interaction term: trastuzumab x her2_positive > 0).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive breast cancer is the foundational guideline-level indication.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab improves pfs_months in her2_low=1 (HER2-low) patients beyond the her2_positive=0 group baseline (positive interaction trastuzumab x her2_low > 0).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab monotherapy benefit in HER2-low disease goes beyond guideline consensus (T-DXd is the HER2-low standard, not trastuzumab).

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen improves pfs_months specifically in er_positive=1 patients (positive interaction term: tamoxifen x er_positive > 0).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit restricted to ER-positive disease is a textbook association.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1, treatment_tamoxifen has a positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy within ER-positive patients is established standard-of-care.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib has a strongly positive effect on pfs_months specifically in er_positive=1 patients (palbociclib x er_positive interaction > 0).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-positive (HR+) disease is the guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by ~1.5 months.
judgments.rationale: A positive palbociclib effect in ER-positive patients reflects established CDK4/6 inhibitor efficacy.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=0 patients, treatment_palbociclib has a null effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of palbociclib benefit outside HR+ disease is consistent with guideline restriction to ER-positive patients.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months differs by postmenopausal status (palbociclib x postmenopausal interaction != 0).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse palbociclib in HR+ disease regardless of menopausal status (with OFS in premenopausal women), so a differential effect by menopausal status is not standard consensus.

judgments.text: The palbociclib benefit in er_positive=1 patients is concentrated in those who are postmenopausal=1; effect is null in er_positive=1 + postmenopausal=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming palbociclib benefit is null in premenopausal ER-positive patients contradicts guideline use across menopausal status and is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in BRCA1- or BRCA2-mutated patients (olaparib x brca_any interaction > 0).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA1/2-mutated breast cancer is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within brca1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1-mutant breast cancer is established in guidelines (OlympiAD/OlympiA).

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant breast cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Within patients with neither brca1_mutation nor brca2_mutation, treatment_olaparib has a null effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of olaparib benefit in BRCA wild-type patients is the standard guideline rationale for restricting PARP inhibitors to BRCA carriers.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs between triple_negative=1 (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) and non-triple-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's preferential benefit in TNBC versus hormone receptor-positive disease is established (KEYNOTE-355/522).

judgments.text: Within triple_negative=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab prolongs PFS in TNBC per KEYNOTE-355 and is in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within non-triple-negative patients, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A claim that pembrolizumab actively shortens PFS in non-TNBC goes beyond a null/no-benefit guideline statement and is a speculative harm claim.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan effect on pfs_months is greater in triple_negative=1 patients than in non-triple-negative patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's approved indication and pivotal benefit (ASCENT) is in metastatic TNBC, making greater benefit there standard.

judgments.text: Within triple_negative=1 patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan prolongs PFS in metastatic TNBC per ASCENT and is guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no trastuzumab.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=0 AND her2_low=1 patients (HER2-low), treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab monotherapy benefit in HER2-low disease is not guideline-supported (HER2-low is treated with trastuzumab deruxtecan, not trastuzumab); this is a novel cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=0 AND her2_low=0 patients (HER2-zero), treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-zero patients contradicts standard guideline indication restricted to HER2-positive (and now HER2-low) disease.

judgments.text: Across an exhaustive screen of treatment x binary-feature interactions on pfs_months, the largest signal is treatment_palbociclib x er_positive (positive direction).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-positive breast cancer is the established guideline indication for CDK4/6 inhibitors.

judgments.text: After palbociclib x er_positive, the next strongest treatment-effect heterogeneity signals on pfs_months are treatment_palbociclib with HR-related markers and treatment_olaparib with brca_any.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib with HR markers and olaparib with BRCA mutations are textbook biomarker-treatment pairings.

judgments.text: Patients with albumin_g_dl below the cohort median (albumin_low=1) have shorter pfs_months than those above the median.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor is a well-established general oncology finding.

judgments.text: Patients with ldh_u_l above the cohort median (ldh_high=1) have shorter pfs_months than those at/below.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is standard oncology knowledge across many tumor types.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab x ldh_high interaction is non-zero on pfs_months (treatment-effect heterogeneity by LDH).
judgments.rationale: Treatment-effect heterogeneity of pembrolizumab specifically by LDH status is not a guideline-level established predictive biomarker in breast cancer.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1 + postmenopausal=1, treatment_palbociclib retains a strong positive effect on pfs_months after adjusting for age, ECOG, stage IV, albumin, LDH, CRP.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib efficacy in ER-positive postmenopausal patients after standard covariate adjustment reflects the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within brca_any=1 (BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutated), treatment_olaparib retains a positive effect on pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA-mutated patients is the established guideline-based indication for PARP inhibitors.

judgments.text: Within triple_negative=1, treatment_pembrolizumab retains a positive effect on pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in triple-negative breast cancer is an established guideline indication (e.g., KEYNOTE-355).

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=1, treatment_trastuzumab does NOT show a positive effect on pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Absence of trastuzumab benefit within HER2-positive disease contradicts the well-established guideline standard of care.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is similar in stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Whether palbociclib's PFS benefit is uniform across stage IV vs earlier stage is not an established guideline claim.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: A stage-dependent modification of pembrolizumab's PFS effect is not a standard guideline statement.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib benefit is present in BOTH er_positive=1 + postmenopausal=1 AND er_positive=1 + postmenopausal=0 patients (concentrated in er_positive=1 regardless of menopausal status).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in HR+/HER2- disease across both pre- and postmenopausal patients is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=0, treatment_palbociclib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of palbociclib benefit in ER-negative disease is established guideline consensus (drug indicated only for HR+).

judgments.text: The pembrolizumab x triple_negative interaction on pfs_months is concentrated in postmenopausal=0 patients (3-way interaction pembrolizumab*triple_negative*postmenopausal is negative).
judgments.rationale: A 3-way pembrolizumab*TNBC*menopausal-status interaction is a speculative subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, the pembrolizumab effect within triple_negative=1 + postmenopausal=0 collapses toward null, suggesting the unadjusted interaction is driven by confounding by other prognostic features.
judgments.rationale: Claim that the TNBC/premenopausal pembrolizumab effect is driven by confounding is a speculative mechanistic/methodologic claim beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=1 + ecog_ps=0, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ patients with good performance status is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=1 + stage_iv=1, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ metastatic disease is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months interacts with ecog_ps (smaller benefit at higher ECOG).
judgments.rationale: A specific palbociclib*ECOG interaction on PFS is not codified in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months interacts with ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: A specific pembrolizumab*ECOG interaction on PFS is not an established guideline claim.

judgments.text: Within brca_any=1 + postmenopausal=0, treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months more than within brca_any=1 + postmenopausal=1.
judgments.rationale: Premenopausal status modifying olaparib benefit within BRCA carriers is a subgroup-conditional claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within brca_any=1 + stage_iv=1, treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib for BRCA-mutated metastatic breast cancer is a standard guideline indication (OlympiAD).

judgments.text: Treatment_palbociclib best-supported predictive subgroup is er_positive=1 (PFS gain ~+1.58 months).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's predictive benefit in ER+ disease is the established guideline indication for CDK4/6 inhibitors.

judgments.text: Treatment_olaparib best-supported predictive subgroup is brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 (PFS gain ~+0.35 months unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's benefit in BRCA1/2-mutated breast cancer is a standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: Treatment_pembrolizumab best-supported predictive subgroup is triple_negative=1 (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0); positive direction within TNBC, slight harm outside.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's predictive benefit in TNBC is established guideline knowledge (KEYNOTE-355).

judgments.text: Treatment_trastuzumab has NO predictive subgroup with a clear PFS benefit in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no trastuzumab predictive subgroup contradicts the well-established HER2+ benefit and is a notable cohort-specific finding.

judgments.text: Treatment_tamoxifen has NO predictive subgroup with a clear PFS benefit in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no tamoxifen predictive subgroup contradicts the well-established ER+ benefit and is a notable cohort-specific finding.

judgments.text: Treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has NO predictive subgroup with a clear PFS benefit in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no sacituzumab govitecan predictive subgroup contradicts the established TNBC benefit (ASCENT) and is a notable cohort-specific finding.

judgments.text: The complete predictive subgroup for treatment_palbociclib's positive effect on pfs_months is er_positive=1, with no other unfavorable feature acting as a suppressor; the effect is +1.502 months in postmenopausal=1 ER+ and +1.690 months in postmenopausal=0 ER+, similar magnitudes.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib working in ER+ regardless of menopausal status is essentially the standard indication framework in guidelines.

judgments.text: The complete predictive subgroup for treatment_olaparib's positive effect on pfs_months is brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; the effect is positive in both BRCA1 carriers (+0.398) and BRCA2 carriers (+0.295), and even larger in BRCA-positive premenopausal (+0.547) and BRCA-positive stage_iv=1 (+0.538) sub-strata.
judgments.rationale: Quantitative claims that olaparib effect is larger in premenopausal and stage IV BRCA sub-strata are speculative subgroup-conditional claims beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: The complete predictive subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab's positive treatment-effect heterogeneity on pfs_months is triple_negative=1 + postmenopausal=0 (premenopausal TNBC), with postmenopausal=1 acting as a suppressor; unadjusted effect +0.275 months in this subgroup, attenuating to ~+0.02 after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Restricting pembrolizumab PFS benefit specifically to premenopausal TNBC with postmenopausal status acting as a suppressor is a subgroup-conditional claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Final treatment-by-biomarker map for pfs_months: (i) treatment_palbociclib improves PFS by ~+1.58 months in er_positive=1, null (-0.02) in er_positive=0; (ii) treatment_olaparib improves PFS by ~+0.35 months in brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 (significant unadjusted, attenuated after adjustment); (iii) treatment_pembrolizumab shows positive PFS within triple_negative=1 (especially premenopausal TNBC, +0.28 months unadjusted) and slight harm outside TNBC (-0.11 mo); (iv) treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_tamoxifen, and treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan show NO predictive subgroup with statistically significant PFS benefit in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: This map largely restates established guideline associations (palbociclib in ER+, olaparib in BRCA1/2, pembrolizumab in TNBC) with the only novel element being the premenopausal sub-restriction already covered in hypothesis 1.

judgments.text: In ds001_breast (n=50000), the marginal mean of pfs_months is approximately 4.7 months with appreciable patient heterogeneity in stage_iv (~30%), has_brain_mets (~10%), er_positive (~70%), her2_positive (~18%), and brca1_mutation/brca2_mutation (~2.5% each).
judgments.rationale: Descriptive summary of cohort marginal distributions, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with stage_iv=1 than stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than non-stage IV is established prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with has_brain_mets=1 than has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring worse PFS is a well-known adverse prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with node_positive=1 than node_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive disease portending worse outcomes than node-negative is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with postmenopausal=1 than postmenopausal=0.
judgments.rationale: A direct postmenopausal vs premenopausal PFS difference independent of subtype/treatment is not a guideline-level paradigm.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is differs in patients with sex_female=1 than sex_female=0.
judgments.rationale: A sex-based PFS difference in a breast cancer cohort is not addressed in standard guidelines and would be an unusual finding.

judgments.text: pfs_months is positively associated with age_years as a continuous predictor.
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with longer PFS contradicts the typical expectation that older patients fare worse, making this a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: pfs_months is negatively associated with ecog_ps as a continuous predictor.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse PFS is established prognostic dogma.

judgments.text: pfs_months is negatively associated with ki67_pct as a continuous predictor.
judgments.rationale: High Ki-67 proliferation index correlating with worse outcomes is well-established in breast cancer.

judgments.text: pfs_months is negatively associated with weight_loss_pct_6mo as a continuous predictor.
judgments.rationale: Cancer-related weight loss predicting worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: pfs_months is negatively associated with tumor_size_cm as a continuous predictor.
judgments.rationale: Larger tumor size correlating with worse PFS is a standard prognostic finding in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients with er_positive=1 vs er_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: ER-positive status conferring better outcomes in breast cancer is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients with pr_positive=1 vs pr_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: PR-positive status as a favorable prognostic/predictive marker is standard breast cancer textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with her2_positive=1 vs her2_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2-positive disease historically associated with worse outcomes (without trastuzumab) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients with her2_low=1 vs her2_low=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low as an independent PFS prognostic marker beyond its predictive role for T-DXd is not established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is differs in patients with brca1_mutation=1 vs brca1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation status influencing breast cancer outcomes and treatment response is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is differs in patients with brca2_mutation=1 vs brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status as a prognostic/predictive factor in breast cancer is established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with pik3ca_mutation=1 vs pik3ca_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutations associated with worse outcomes/endocrine resistance is standard breast cancer guideline content.

judgments.text: pfs_months is positively associated with albumin_g_dl.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin correlating with better cancer outcomes is a well-known general prognostic association.

judgments.text: pfs_months is negatively associated with ldh_u_l.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is a standard, textbook oncology association.

judgments.text: pfs_months is negatively associated with crp_mg_l.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic marker is well established in oncology literature.

judgments.text: pfs_months is negatively associated with nlr.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio predicting worse outcomes is a widely recognized prognostic finding.

judgments.text: pfs_months is positively associated with hemoglobin_g_dl.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as a negative prognostic factor is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: pfs_months is negatively associated with alkaline_phosphatase_u_l.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflects bone/liver involvement and is a known adverse prognostic marker.

judgments.text: pfs_months is differs associated with calcium_mg_dl.
judgments.rationale: Calcium derangements (especially hypercalcemia) are established markers of advanced disease and poor prognosis.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen=1 than treatment_tamoxifen=0 (unadjusted comparison across the full cohort).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in breast cancer is guideline-standard, and an unadjusted positive association is the expected paradigm finding.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients receiving treatment_palbociclib=1 than treatment_palbociclib=0 (unadjusted comparison across the full cohort).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's PFS benefit in HR+/HER2- breast cancer is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab=1 than treatment_trastuzumab=0 (unadjusted comparison across the full cohort).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab's benefit in HER2+ breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 than treatment_olaparib=0 (unadjusted comparison across the full cohort).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA-mutant breast cancer is a guideline-recognized standard association.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=1 than treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=0 (unadjusted comparison across the full cohort).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's PFS benefit is established in approved breast cancer indications per current guidelines.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months is higher in patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (unadjusted comparison across the full cohort).
judgments.rationale: Unadjusted overall pembrolizumab effect on PFS is a standard treatment-effect comparison, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Adjusted for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and tumor_size_cm, treatment_tamoxifen is positively associated with pfs_months in the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen improving PFS in a breast cancer cohort is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Adjusted for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and tumor_size_cm, treatment_palbociclib is positively associated with pfs_months in the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS is established in HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Adjusted for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and tumor_size_cm, treatment_trastuzumab is positively associated with pfs_months in the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab improving PFS is a textbook association in HER2-positive breast cancer.

judgments.text: Adjusted for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and tumor_size_cm, treatment_olaparib is positively associated with pfs_months in the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS is established in BRCA-mutated breast/ovarian cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Adjusted for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and tumor_size_cm, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is positively associated with pfs_months in the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan improving PFS is established in metastatic triple-negative and HR+ breast cancer per guidelines.

judgments.text: Adjusted for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and tumor_size_cm, treatment_pembrolizumab is positively associated with pfs_months in the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is established in multiple guideline indications including TNBC.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_tamoxifen differs between patients with er_positive=1 and er_positive=0; specifically, patients with er_positive=1 (the canonical hormone-receptor-positive group) should derive larger PFS benefit from treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: Greater tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive disease is the canonical, guideline-defining biomarker association.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_tamoxifen differs between patients with pr_positive=1 and pr_positive=0; specifically, patients with pr_positive=1 (the canonical hormone-receptor-positive group) should derive larger PFS benefit from treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: Greater tamoxifen benefit in PR-positive (hormone receptor-positive) disease is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_trastuzumab differs between patients with her2_positive=1 and her2_positive=0; specifically, treatment_trastuzumab, a HER2-targeted antibody, should produce larger PFS benefit when her2_positive=1.
judgments.rationale: Greater trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive disease is the textbook predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_trastuzumab differs between patients with her2_low=1 and her2_low=0; specifically, treatment_trastuzumab, a HER2-targeted antibody, should produce larger PFS benefit when her2_low=1.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab is guideline-standard for HER2-positive disease, not HER2-low; claiming larger benefit in HER2-low than HER2-low=0 (which includes HER2-positive) contradicts paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_olaparib differs between patients with brca1_mutation=1 and brca1_mutation=0; specifically, treatment_olaparib (a PARP inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit when brca1_mutation=1 (synthetic-lethality biology).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA1-mutant disease via synthetic lethality is canonical guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_olaparib differs between patients with brca2_mutation=1 and brca2_mutation=0; specifically, treatment_olaparib (a PARP inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit when brca2_mutation=1 (synthetic-lethality biology).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA2-mutant disease via synthetic lethality is canonical guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_olaparib differs between patients with brca_any=1 and brca_any=0; specifically, treatment_olaparib (a PARP inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit when brca_any=1 (synthetic-lethality biology).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in any BRCA-mutated disease is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_palbociclib differs between patients with er_positive=1 and er_positive=0; specifically, treatment_palbociclib (a CDK4/6 inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit when er_positive=1 (HR+/HER2- biology).
judgments.rationale: CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit in ER-positive disease is the canonical HR+/HER2- guideline indication.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_palbociclib differs between patients with her2_positive=1 and her2_positive=0; specifically, treatment_palbociclib (a CDK4/6 inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit when her2_positive=1 (HR+/HER2- biology).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib is indicated for HER2-negative disease; claiming larger benefit in HER2-positive directly contradicts current guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_palbociclib differs between patients with postmenopausal=1 and postmenopausal=0; specifically, treatment_palbociclib (a CDK4/6 inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit when postmenopausal=1 (HR+/HER2- biology).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support palbociclib in both pre- and postmenopausal HR+/HER2- patients without specifying differential efficacy by menopausal status.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_palbociclib differs between patients with er_pos_her2_neg=1 and er_pos_her2_neg=0; specifically, treatment_palbociclib (a CDK4/6 inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit when er_pos_her2_neg=1 (HR+/HER2- biology).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in the ER+/HER2- subgroup is the precise canonical guideline indication.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan differs between patients with tnbc=1 and tnbc=0; specifically, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan (a TROP2-directed ADC) should produce larger PFS benefit when tnbc=1 (or, for er_positive=1, smaller benefit).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in TNBC (ASCENT trial) is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan differs between patients with er_positive=1 and er_positive=0; specifically, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan (a TROP2-directed ADC) should produce larger PFS benefit when er_positive=1 (or, for er_positive=1, smaller benefit).
judgments.rationale: Although sacituzumab is approved in HR+/HER2- (TROPiCS-02), guidelines do not establish that ER-positive patients derive larger PFS benefit than TNBC patients.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan differs between patients with her2_low=1 and her2_low=0; specifically, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan (a TROP2-directed ADC) should produce larger PFS benefit when her2_low=1 (or, for er_positive=1, smaller benefit).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in HER2-low (and HR+) breast cancer is established in guidelines based on TROPiCS-02 and ASCENT subgroup data.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_pembrolizumab differs between patients with tnbc=1 and tnbc=0; specifically, treatment_pembrolizumab (an immune checkpoint inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit in tnbc=1 patients (typical TNBC/advanced indications).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's larger PFS benefit in TNBC (KEYNOTE-355/522) is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_pembrolizumab differs between patients with stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0; specifically, treatment_pembrolizumab (an immune checkpoint inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit in stage_iv=1 patients (typical TNBC/advanced indications).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's approved breast cancer indications concentrate in advanced/metastatic (stage IV) and high-risk early disease, a guideline-standard pattern.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_pembrolizumab differs between patients with has_brain_mets=1 and has_brain_mets=0; specifically, treatment_pembrolizumab (an immune checkpoint inhibitor) should produce larger PFS benefit in has_brain_mets=1 patients (typical TNBC/advanced indications).
judgments.rationale: Differential pembrolizumab PFS benefit specifically by brain metastasis status in breast cancer is not a guideline-established subgroup effect.

judgments.text: Across the full grid of treatment x biomarker/clinical-feature interactions, any treatment-effect heterogeneity should concentrate in biologically plausible modifiers (HR+ for tamoxifen/palbociclib, HER2+ for trastuzumab, BRCA for olaparib, TNBC for sacituzumab/pembrolizumab); we expect strongly non-zero interactions only for the matched indications.
judgments.rationale: This is a restatement of the canonical biomarker-matched indication paradigm taught in oncology textbooks and guidelines.

judgments.text: Within er_positive==1, treatment_tamoxifen is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive==1, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within (brca1_mutation==1 OR brca2_mutation==1), treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant breast cancer (OlympiA/OlympiAD) is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HR+/HER2- (i.e. (er_positive==1 OR pr_positive==1) AND her2_positive==0), treatment_palbociclib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in HR+/HER2- breast cancer (PALOMA trials) is standard first-line guideline therapy.

judgments.text: Within TNBC (er_positive==0 AND pr_positive==0 AND her2_positive==0), treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in TNBC (ASCENT) is an established guideline-level indication.

judgments.text: Within TNBC, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC is established and reflected in current guidelines (KEYNOTE-355/522).

judgments.text: In a model with the three-way interaction treatment_tamoxifen x er_positive x postmenopausal, the three-way term is non-zero, indicating that the treatment-by-canonical-modifier effect itself depends on the secondary modifier.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction where tamoxifen's ER-dependent effect is further modified by menopausal status is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: In a model with the three-way interaction treatment_palbociclib x hr_pos x postmenopausal, the three-way term is non-zero, indicating that the treatment-by-canonical-modifier effect itself depends on the secondary modifier.
judgments.rationale: While palbociclib is used in HR+ postmenopausal patients, a non-zero three-way interaction term implying menopausal status modifies the HR-dependent treatment effect goes beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: In a model with the three-way interaction treatment_palbociclib x er_pos_her2_neg x postmenopausal, the three-way term is non-zero, indicating that the treatment-by-canonical-modifier effect itself depends on the secondary modifier.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction of palbociclib with ER+/HER2- and postmenopausal status as a secondary modifier is a speculative cross-modifier claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In a model with the three-way interaction treatment_trastuzumab x her2_positive x node_positive, the three-way term is non-zero, indicating that the treatment-by-canonical-modifier effect itself depends on the secondary modifier.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines recommend trastuzumab for HER2+ regardless of nodal status; a HER2 x node-positive interaction modifying trastuzumab effect is not standard.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months differs by stage_iv status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in stage_iv=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential tamoxifen efficacy specifically by stage IV status is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in has_brain_mets=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy modification by brain metastases status is speculative and not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months differs by node_positive status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in node_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential tamoxifen efficacy by nodal status is not a guideline-established interaction.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months differs by stage_iv status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in stage_iv=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential palbociclib efficacy by stage IV status (vs. earlier-stage) is not a guideline-stated interaction.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in has_brain_mets=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential palbociclib efficacy by brain metastases status is speculative and not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months differs by node_positive status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in node_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Heterogeneity of palbociclib PFS benefit by nodal status is not a guideline-established subgroup effect in HR+ breast cancer.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months differs by stage_iv status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in stage_iv=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab efficacy in metastatic (stage IV) HER2+ breast cancer is a foundational, guideline-established indication.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in has_brain_mets=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential trastuzumab PFS benefit specifically conditioned on brain metastasis status is not a standard guideline claim, as trastuzumab has limited CNS penetration and brain mets are typically managed with HER2-directed TKIs.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months differs by node_positive status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in node_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup modification of trastuzumab effect by node-positive status is not an established guideline-level predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by stage_iv status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in stage_iv=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's labeled indications are based on BRCA/HRD status, not stage IV per se as an effect modifier, so a stage-conditional benefit claim goes beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in has_brain_mets=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy modification by brain metastasis status is not a guideline-established subgroup effect.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by node_positive status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in node_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib effect heterogeneity by nodal status is not part of standard predictive biomarker guidance.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months differs by stage_iv status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in stage_iv=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan is approved and guideline-endorsed in metastatic (stage IV) TNBC and HR+/HER2- disease, so a stage IV benefit is established.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in has_brain_mets=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential sacituzumab govitecan PFS benefit conditioned on brain metastasis status is not a guideline-established subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months differs by node_positive status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in node_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan effect modification by nodal status is not an established guideline-level predictive subgroup.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by stage_iv status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in stage_iv=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV status as a modifier of pembrolizumab benefit reflects standard guideline-level stratification by disease stage.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in has_brain_mets=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential pembrolizumab PFS effect specifically conditioned on brain metastasis status is a subgroup-mechanistic claim not codified in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by node_positive status; specifically, the treatment is more (or less) effective in node_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive status as a modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not an established guideline-level predictive biomarker for ICI response.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_palbociclib varies with the continuous predictor ki67_pct; we expect a non-zero treatment_palbociclib:ki67_pct interaction term.
judgments.rationale: A continuous Ki67-by-palbociclib interaction predicting differential PFS benefit goes beyond guideline consensus, which does not use Ki67 to select CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_pembrolizumab varies with the continuous predictor ecog_ps; we expect a non-zero treatment_pembrolizumab:ecog_ps interaction term.
judgments.rationale: While ECOG is prognostic, a continuous ECOG-by-pembrolizumab interaction as a predictive modifier is not established in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_palbociclib varies with the continuous predictor age_years; we expect a non-zero treatment_palbociclib:age_years interaction term.
judgments.rationale: A continuous age-by-palbociclib interaction on PFS is not an established predictive relationship in CDK4/6 inhibitor guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_palbociclib varies with the continuous predictor albumin_g_dl; we expect a non-zero treatment_palbociclib:albumin_g_dl interaction term.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a continuous predictive modifier of palbociclib efficacy is not part of guideline-level patient selection.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan varies with the continuous predictor albumin_g_dl; we expect a non-zero treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan:albumin_g_dl interaction term.
judgments.rationale: Albumin-by-sacituzumab govitecan interaction as a predictive biomarker is speculative and not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan varies with the continuous predictor ldh_u_l; we expect a non-zero treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan:ldh_u_l interaction term.
judgments.rationale: LDH as a continuous predictive modifier of sacituzumab govitecan efficacy is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan varies with the continuous predictor crp_mg_l; we expect a non-zero treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan:crp_mg_l interaction term.
judgments.rationale: CRP-by-sacituzumab govitecan interaction as a predictive biomarker is not part of standard oncology guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_trastuzumab varies with the continuous predictor ki67_pct; we expect a non-zero treatment_trastuzumab:ki67_pct interaction term.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit modification by Ki67 is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_palbociclib varies with the continuous predictor nlr; we expect a non-zero treatment_palbociclib:nlr interaction term.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib efficacy modification by neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio is speculative and not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_olaparib varies with the continuous predictor crp_mg_l; we expect a non-zero treatment_olaparib:crp_mg_l interaction term.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit modification by CRP is a speculative inflammation-PARP cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_tamoxifen varies with the continuous predictor nlr; we expect a non-zero treatment_tamoxifen:nlr interaction term.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit modification by NLR is not established in guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: The pfs_months effect of treatment_trastuzumab varies with the continuous predictor tumor_size_cm; we expect a non-zero treatment_trastuzumab:tumor_size_cm interaction term.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit modification by tumor size as a continuous interaction is not a guideline association.

judgments.text: Within HR+/HER2- AND postmenopausal=1, treatment_palbociclib is associated with longer pfs_months than in patients outside this subgroup (positive joint subgroup effect).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in HR+/HER2- postmenopausal patients is the standard guideline indication (PALOMA trials).

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2- AND postmenopausal=1, treatment_palbociclib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib in ER+/HER2- postmenopausal patients is the established first-line guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=1 AND stage_iv=1, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ stage IV disease is the foundational guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1), treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant breast cancer is an established guideline indication (OlympiAD).

judgments.text: Within TNBC AND stage_iv=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic TNBC is the established guideline indication (ASCENT).

judgments.text: Within TNBC AND stage_iv=1, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in metastatic TNBC is established (KEYNOTE-355) and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+ AND PR+ AND postmenopausal=1, treatment_tamoxifen is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER+/PR+ postmenopausal breast cancer is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months on age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and tumor_size_cm, the dominant prognostic variables are age_years (positive), ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), has_brain_mets (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), and weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative).
judgments.rationale: ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, low albumin, and weight loss as negative prognostic factors are standard textbook prognostic variables.

judgments.text: In a single OLS model that includes each treatment crossed with its canonical indication (treatment_tamoxifen*er_positive, treatment_trastuzumab*her2_positive, treatment_olaparib*brca_any, treatment_palbociclib*er_pos_her2_neg, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan*tnbc, treatment_pembrolizumab*tnbc), only the treatment_palbociclib:er_pos_her2_neg interaction is meaningfully positive; the other treatment-by-canonical-indication interactions are near zero.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that only palbociclib shows a canonical-indication interaction while trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab, and pembrolizumab effects are null in their indications contradicts guideline consensus and is a non-standard cross-treatment claim.

judgments.text: Within the HR+/HER2- subgroup, the treatment_palbociclib treatment effect on pfs_months is further modified by ki67_pct (a non-zero treatment_palbociclib:ki67_pct interaction within the indication subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Ki67 as a quantitative modifier of palbociclib benefit within HR+/HER2- is not established in guidelines (Ki67 guides abemaciclib adjuvant use, not palbociclib effect modification).

judgments.text: Within the HER2+ subgroup, the treatment_trastuzumab treatment effect on pfs_months is further modified by her2_low (a non-zero treatment_trastuzumab:her2_low interaction within the indication subgroup).
judgments.rationale: HER2-low status modifying trastuzumab efficacy within HER2+ disease is not a guideline concept; HER2-low is a separate category relevant to T-DXd, not trastuzumab effect modification.

judgments.text: Within the BRCA1/2 mut subgroup, the treatment_olaparib treatment effect on pfs_months is further modified by weight_loss_pct_6mo (a non-zero treatment_olaparib:weight_loss_pct_6mo interaction within the indication subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as a modifier of olaparib efficacy in BRCA-mutant disease is a speculative cachexia-PARP interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the TNBC subgroup, the treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan treatment effect on pfs_months is further modified by age_years (a non-zero treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan:age_years interaction within the indication subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Age as a modifier of sacituzumab govitecan benefit in TNBC is not a guideline-recognized effect modifier.

judgments.text: Within the TNBC subgroup, the treatment_pembrolizumab treatment effect on pfs_months is further modified by weight_loss_pct_6mo (a non-zero treatment_pembrolizumab:weight_loss_pct_6mo interaction within the indication subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as a modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy in TNBC is a speculative cachexia-immunotherapy interaction not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the ER+ subgroup, the treatment_tamoxifen treatment effect on pfs_months is further modified by ldh_u_l (a non-zero treatment_tamoxifen:ldh_u_l interaction within the indication subgroup).
judgments.rationale: LDH as a modifier of tamoxifen efficacy in ER+ disease is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker for endocrine therapy.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=1 AND has_brain_mets=0, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within HR+/HER2- AND postmenopausal=1 AND ecog_ps<2, treatment_palbociclib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in HR+/HER2- postmenopausal patients is standard first-line guideline therapy (PALOMA trials).

judgments.text: Within TNBC AND stage_iv=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic TNBC is established guideline therapy from ASCENT.

judgments.text: Within TNBC AND stage_iv=1 AND has_brain_mets=0, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in metastatic TNBC is standard guideline therapy (KEYNOTE-355), and brain mets exclusion mirrors trial criteria.

judgments.text: Within (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) AND ecog_ps<2, treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant breast cancer is established guideline therapy (OlympiAD/OlympiA).

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1 AND pr_positive=1 AND postmenopausal=1, treatment_tamoxifen is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER+/PR+ postmenopausal breast cancer is textbook endocrine therapy.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib PFS benefit is concentrated in patients who are HR+ ((er_positive=1 OR pr_positive=1)) AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0; in HR+/HER2- patients with pik3ca_mutation=1 the palbociclib benefit is essentially zero, so PIK3CA mutation defines the unfavorable secondary feature that suppresses the treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming PIK3CA mutation abolishes CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit contradicts guideline view that palbociclib retains efficacy regardless of PIK3CA status.

judgments.text: Within HR+/HER2- AND pik3ca_mutation=1, treatment_palbociclib shows no PFS benefit (adjusted coefficient near zero, non-significant).
judgments.rationale: Asserting zero palbociclib PFS benefit in PIK3CA-mutant HR+/HER2- disease is a speculative subgroup claim not reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: In a model with the three-way interaction treatment_palbociclib * (HR+/HER2- indicator) * (PIK3CA wild-type indicator), the triple-interaction coefficient is large and positive (~+2.7 months PFS), while the constituent two-way interactions and the palbociclib main effect are near zero — i.e. the entire palbociclib effect lives in the HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt cell.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction localizing the entire palbociclib effect to the HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt cell is a novel mechanistic subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within HR+/HER2-, there is a large negative treatment_palbociclib x pik3ca_mutation interaction on pfs_months: the palbociclib benefit (~+2.7 months in PIK3CA-wt) is fully neutralized when pik3ca_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: A large negative palbociclib x PIK3CA interaction fully neutralizing benefit is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for treatment_tamoxifen: across the entire ds001_breast cohort, treatment_tamoxifen has no detectable PFS benefit, even within er_positive=1 AND pr_positive=1 AND postmenopausal=1 (the canonical hormone-receptor-positive subgroup). Direction near zero (slight negative point estimate, non-significant).
judgments.rationale: Claiming tamoxifen has no PFS benefit even in ER+/PR+/postmenopausal patients contradicts guideline consensus that tamoxifen benefits hormone-receptor-positive disease.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for treatment_trastuzumab: even within her2_positive=1 (the canonical indication, with or without restricting to has_brain_mets=0 / ecog_ps<2 / stage_iv=1), treatment_trastuzumab has no detectable PFS benefit (adjusted coefficients near zero, non-significant).
judgments.rationale: Claiming trastuzumab has no PFS benefit in HER2+ disease contradicts established guideline consensus and is a non-standard, surprising claim.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for treatment_olaparib: within (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) — with or without restricting to ecog_ps<2 — treatment_olaparib shows no significant PFS benefit; the point estimate inside this subgroup is small and the formal interaction tx x BRCA is non-significant.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib has no PFS benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant patients contradicts the established guideline indication for PARP inhibitors in this subgroup.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for treatment_palbociclib: the PFS benefit of treatment_palbociclib is concentrated in patients who are HR+ ((er_positive=1 OR pr_positive=1)) AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0. In this complete subgroup, adjusted PFS is ~+2.74 months longer with palbociclib (p<<0.001). Two unfavorable predicates suppress the effect: her2_positive=1 (eliminates benefit, tx x HER2+ interaction strongly negative), and pik3ca_mutation=1 (eliminates benefit, tx x PIK3CA interaction strongly negative). Within the favorable subgroup, higher ki67_pct further reduces benefit (ki67 tx-interaction = -0.13 months per percent ki67, p<<0.001), but does not abolish it.
judgments.rationale: The specific multi-predicate conditioning (HR+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-wildtype, with a quantitative Ki67 interaction attenuating but not abolishing benefit) is a cross-pathway, subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard guideline language.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan: within TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0), with or without further restriction to stage_iv=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan shows no significant PFS benefit (adjusted coefficient near zero).
judgments.rationale: Claiming sacituzumab govitecan has no PFS benefit in TNBC contradicts its established guideline indication in metastatic TNBC.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for treatment_pembrolizumab: within TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0), with or without further restriction to stage_iv=1 AND has_brain_mets=0, treatment_pembrolizumab shows no significant PFS benefit (adjusted coefficient ~+0.01 to +0.03, non-significant). The unadjusted negative association outside TNBC reflects channelling of pembrolizumab into more advanced disease, not a true harm.
judgments.rationale: The claim that pembrolizumab shows no PFS benefit in TNBC and that observed harm outside TNBC reflects channelling bias is a non-guideline, mechanism/confounding-level claim beyond standard consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months differs by stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: Differential trastuzumab PFS benefit specifically by stage IV vs earlier stage is not a guideline-established effect modifier beyond standard indication.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastasis status modifying systemic trastuzumab PFS benefit is a speculative subgroup claim not codified in guidelines (which instead favor tucatinib/T-DXd for CNS disease).

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months differs by postmenopausal.
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status as a modifier of trastuzumab efficacy is not an established guideline-level predictor for HER2-directed therapy.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months differs by brca_any.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit modification by BRCA status is not an established guideline concept; trastuzumab selection is based on HER2, not BRCA.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by er_positive.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy differing by ER status within BRCA-mutant disease is a subgroup-conditional claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by pr_positive.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by her2_positive.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib in metastatic breast cancer is guideline-restricted to HER2-negative disease, so differential effect by HER2 status is established paradigm.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by her2_low.
judgments.rationale: Differential olaparib benefit by HER2-low versus HER2-zero status is not an established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by brca1_mutation.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines and the OlympiAD/OlympiA trials establish olaparib efficacy in germline BRCA1-mutant breast cancer, making this a known association.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by brca2_mutation.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines establish olaparib efficacy in germline BRCA2-mutant breast cancer, making this a known association.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by pik3ca_mutation.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months differs by pik3ca_mutation.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months differs by stage_iv.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months differs by postmenopausal.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months differs by brca_any.
judgments.rationale: BRCA mutation status as a predictor of sacituzumab govitecan benefit is not part of standard guideline paradigm, which focuses on TROP2 expression and HR/HER2 status.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by er_positive.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit by ER status reflects the established TNBC vs HR+ paradigm where pembrolizumab is approved for TNBC, making ER-negative subgroup benefit guideline-consistent.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by pr_positive.
judgments.rationale: PR status tracks closely with ER and HR-status-based pembrolizumab indication (TNBC), so differential effect by PR positivity is consistent with established paradigm.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by her2_positive.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy modification by HER2 status is not part of established breast cancer guideline paradigm, which centers immunotherapy use on TNBC and PD-L1.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by her2_low.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low as an effect modifier for pembrolizumab response is a speculative cross-pathway claim not established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by brca1_mutation.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation as a predictor of pembrolizumab benefit in breast cancer is not a guideline-endorsed effect modifier and represents a mechanistic subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by brca2_mutation.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by pik3ca_mutation.

judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a specific effect modifier of pembrolizumab PFS is not codified in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by brca_any.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by nlr (continuous).
judgments.rationale: NLR as a treatment-effect modifier specifically for sacituzumab govitecan PFS is speculative and not in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by hemoglobin_g_dl (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Hemoglobin as a predictive (not just prognostic) modifier of sacituzumab govitecan effect is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Alkaline phosphatase as a predictive modifier of sacituzumab govitecan benefit goes beyond standard prognostic interpretation in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by ast_u_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: AST as a treatment-effect modifier for sacituzumab govitecan PFS is not a guideline-established predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by alt_u_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: ALT as a predictive modifier of sacituzumab govitecan efficacy is not established in current oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by total_bilirubin_mg_dl (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Total bilirubin influences sacituzumab govitecan dosing per label, but its role as a continuous PFS effect modifier is not a guideline-established predictive claim.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by creatinine_mg_dl (continuous).

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by bun_mg_dl (continuous).
judgments.rationale: BUN as a treatment-effect modifier of sacituzumab govitecan PFS is not an established guideline biomarker.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by sodium_meq_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Sodium as a predictive modifier of sacituzumab govitecan PFS effect is not in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by potassium_meq_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Potassium as a continuous effect modifier of sacituzumab govitecan PFS benefit is not part of any guideline or established paradigm.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is modified by calcium_mg_dl (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Calcium level modifying sacituzumab govitecan efficacy is a speculative biomarker interaction not found in current oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by age_years (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Age as a modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is a well-discussed clinical factor in immunotherapy guidelines and meta-analyses.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps (continuous).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status as a modifier of immunotherapy benefit is a standard, guideline-level consideration.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by ki67_pct (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Ki67 as a continuous modifier of pembrolizumab PFS effect is not an established predictive biomarker in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by tumor_size_cm (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Tumor size as a continuous effect modifier of pembrolizumab PFS benefit goes beyond standard guideline paradigms.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by albumin_g_dl (continuous).

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by ldh_u_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: LDH as a predictive (effect-modifying) rather than purely prognostic biomarker for pembrolizumab is a speculative cross-paradigm claim.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by weight_loss_pct_6mo (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss percentage as a continuous modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not established in guidelines beyond general performance considerations.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by crp_mg_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: CRP as a continuous predictive modifier of pembrolizumab PFS benefit is a speculative inflammatory biomarker hypothesis not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by nlr (continuous).
judgments.rationale: NLR as a prognostic and predictive biomarker for immune checkpoint inhibitor response, including pembrolizumab, is well-established in the oncology literature and increasingly cited in practice.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by hemoglobin_g_dl (continuous).

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Alkaline phosphatase as a predictive modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy (rather than a generic prognostic marker reflecting metastatic burden) is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by ast_u_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: AST as an effect modifier specifically of pembrolizumab treatment benefit on PFS is speculative and not in guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by alt_u_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: ALT as a predictive modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not established in oncology guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by total_bilirubin_mg_dl (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Total bilirubin as a predictive (not just prognostic/safety) modifier of pembrolizumab PFS benefit is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by creatinine_mg_dl (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine as an effect modifier of pembrolizumab PFS benefit is not established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by bun_mg_dl (continuous).
judgments.rationale: BUN as a predictive modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not part of established oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by sodium_meq_l (continuous).

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by potassium_meq_l (continuous).
judgments.rationale: Serum potassium as an effect modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is modified by calcium_mg_dl (continuous).

judgments.text: In ER-positive patients, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib improving PFS in ER-positive disease is the established CDK4/6 inhibitor indication (PALOMA trials).

judgments.text: In patients who are ER-positive AND HER2-negative, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ER+/HER2- is the exact guideline-endorsed indication for palbociclib in metastatic breast cancer.

judgments.text: In patients who are ER-positive AND HER2-negative AND PIK3CA wild-type (pik3ca_mutation=0), treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months substantially more than in unselected patients.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not stratify palbociclib benefit by PIK3CA status; a substantially larger effect in PIK3CA-WT is a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Outside the ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT subgroup (i.e., ER-negative, OR HER2-positive, OR PIK3CA-mutated), treatment_palbociclib has no meaningful effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: While ER- and HER2+ exclusions are standard, asserting no benefit in PIK3CA-mutated ER+/HER2- disease contradicts/extends current guidance.

judgments.text: In the cell er+/her2-/pik3ca_wt, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: This is essentially the standard ER+/HER2- indication; PIK3CA-WT is a subset where palbociclib is already expected to work.

judgments.text: In the cell er+/her2-/pik3ca_mut, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse palbociclib in ER+/HER2- regardless of PIK3CA status, so benefit in PIK3CA-mutant patients is established.

judgments.text: In the cell er+/her2+/pik3ca_wt, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib is not guideline-indicated in HER2-positive disease, so claiming PFS benefit there is novel.

judgments.text: In the cell er+/her2+/pik3ca_mut, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in HER2-positive (and PIK3CA-mutant) disease is outside guideline-endorsed use.

judgments.text: In the cell er-/her2-/pik3ca_wt, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-negative disease contradicts the established hormone-receptor-dependent mechanism and indication.
