judgments.text: In the cell er-/her2-/pik3ca_mut, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-negative/HER2-negative/PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer is not part of guideline consensus, which restricts palbociclib to HR+/HER2- disease.

judgments.text: In the cell er-/her2+/pik3ca_wt, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib is not standard for ER-/HER2+ disease, so a PFS benefit in this subgroup would go beyond guideline paradigms.

judgments.text: In the cell er-/her2+/pik3ca_mut, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-/HER2+/PIK3CA-mutant disease is outside the established HR+/HER2- indication and represents a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients, higher ki67_pct attenuates the palbociclib benefit on pfs_months (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: A specific negative interaction between Ki67 and palbociclib benefit within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT is a subgroup-conditional mechanistic claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients with ki67_pct < 20, palbociclib increases pfs_months even more.
judgments.rationale: Enhanced palbociclib benefit in low-Ki67 (<20) ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients is a speculative biomarker-stratified claim beyond standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients with ki67_pct < 15, palbociclib increases pfs_months even more.
judgments.rationale: Enhanced palbociclib benefit at Ki67 <15 in this molecular subgroup is a speculative threshold-based prediction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients with ki67_pct < 10, palbociclib increases pfs_months even more.
judgments.rationale: Enhanced palbociclib benefit at Ki67 <10 in this molecular subgroup is a speculative threshold-based prediction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients with ki67_pct >= 30, the palbociclib benefit is attenuated (smaller diff).
judgments.rationale: Attenuated palbociclib benefit at Ki67 >=30 in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In HER2-positive patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2-positive disease is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: In HER2-negative patients, treatment_trastuzumab does not improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of trastuzumab benefit in HER2-negative disease is established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by er_positive.
judgments.rationale: ER status modifying trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ disease (dual HER2/ER blockade considerations) is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by pr_positive.
judgments.rationale: PR-specific modification of trastuzumab efficacy independent of ER is not a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by node_positive.
judgments.rationale: Nodal status as an effect modifier of trastuzumab benefit on PFS is not an established guideline claim beyond prognostic value.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV (metastatic) versus earlier stage altering trastuzumab PFS benefit is reflected in standard treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by has_brain_mets.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases modifying systemic trastuzumab PFS effect (CNS sanctuary considerations) is a recognized but speculative subgroup claim, going beyond simple guideline statements about HER2-directed therapy efficacy.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by postmenopausal.
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status as a modifier of trastuzumab effect in HER2+ disease is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by pik3ca_mutation.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutations attenuating trastuzumab benefit is a well-described mechanism appearing in current HER2+ literature and emerging guidelines.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by brca1_mutation.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation status modifying trastuzumab efficacy in HER2+ disease is not a guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by brca2_mutation.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status modifying trastuzumab efficacy in HER2+ disease is not a guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within HER2-positive patients, the treatment_trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is modified by brca_any.
judgments.rationale: Any BRCA mutation modifying trastuzumab efficacy in HER2+ disease is not established in standard oncology paradigms.

judgments.text: In BRCA1-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1-mutated breast/ovarian cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In BRCA2-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated patients is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In BRCA1-or-BRCA2-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA1/2-mutated patients is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: In BRCA-wild-type patients, treatment_olaparib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of olaparib benefit in BRCA-wild-type patients reflects established label and guideline restriction.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by her2_positive=1, has_brain_mets=1, treatment_pembrolizumab changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab effect specifically in HER2-positive patients with brain metastases is a speculative subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by stage_iv=0, has_brain_mets=1, treatment_pembrolizumab changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab effect in non-stage-IV patients with brain metastases is an unusual subgroup combination not covered by standard guidelines.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by postmenopausal=0, brca_any=1, treatment_pembrolizumab changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab effect conditional on premenopausal status and BRCA mutation is a cross-pathway subgroup claim outside guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by has_brain_mets=1, node_positive=1, treatment_pembrolizumab changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab effect specifically in node-positive patients with brain metastases is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by her2_low=0, brca_any=1, treatment_pembrolizumab changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab effect conditional on HER2-low-negative and BRCA-mutated status is a cross-pathway subgroup hypothesis not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by er_positive=1, has_brain_mets=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan effect specifically in ER-positive patients with brain metastases is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond current guidelines.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by her2_low=0, brca_any=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan effect specifically in HER2-low-negative, BRCA-mutated breast cancer is not an established guideline subgroup.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by stage_iv=1, has_brain_mets=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: SG efficacy specifically in stage IV patients with brain metastases is not a guideline-level subgroup claim.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by her2_positive=1, pik3ca_mutation=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: SG is not standard in HER2-positive disease, and a PIK3CA-mutant HER2+ subgroup effect is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by pik3ca_mutation=0, has_brain_mets=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA-wildtype with brain metastases as a defining subgroup for SG response is not in established guidelines.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by pik3ca_mutation=0, brca_any=1, treatment_tamoxifen changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA-wildtype, BRCA-mutant subgroup for tamoxifen response is not a guideline-recognized predictive context.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by her2_low=1, brca_any=1, treatment_tamoxifen changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low and BRCA-mutant subgroup-specific tamoxifen effect is not described in guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by postmenopausal=1, brca_any=1, treatment_tamoxifen changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: Postmenopausal BRCA-mutant subgroup as distinct predictor of tamoxifen effect goes beyond standard guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by her2_positive=0, brca_any=1, treatment_tamoxifen changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: HER2-negative, BRCA-mutant subgroup-specific tamoxifen effect is a subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by node_positive=1, brca_any=1, treatment_tamoxifen changes mean pfs_months relative to no treatment_tamoxifen.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive, BRCA-mutant subgroup as a specific tamoxifen effect modifier is not a guideline-level distinction.

judgments.text: In the three-way model pfs_months ~ palbociclib * er_positive * pik3ca_mutation, the term 'treatment_palbociclib' is non-zero, indicating it contributes to determining the palbociclib effect.
judgments.rationale: A three-way palbociclib × ER × PIK3CA interaction governing the palbociclib effect is not a guideline-recognized predictive framework.

judgments.text: In the three-way model pfs_months ~ palbociclib * er_positive * pik3ca_mutation, the term 'treatment_palbociclib:er_positive' is non-zero, indicating it contributes to determining the palbociclib effect.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit in ER-positive disease is the foundational guideline-established indication for CDK4/6 inhibitors.

judgments.text: In the three-way model pfs_months ~ palbociclib * er_positive * pik3ca_mutation, the term 'treatment_palbociclib:pik3ca_mutation' is non-zero, indicating it contributes to determining the palbociclib effect.
judgments.rationale: A PIK3ca-mutation-specific modifier of palbociclib effect is not an established guideline concept and would be a mechanistic cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: In the three-way model pfs_months ~ palbociclib * er_positive * pik3ca_mutation, the term 'treatment_palbociclib:er_positive:pik3ca_mutation' is non-zero, indicating it contributes to determining the palbociclib effect.
judgments.rationale: A three-way ER × PIK3CA interaction modifying palbociclib benefit is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond current guidelines.

judgments.text: In ER-negative AND HER2-negative (TNBC-like) patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC (with PD-L1 positivity) is established in guidelines (KEYNOTE-355).

judgments.text: In ER-negative AND HER2-negative (TNBC-like) patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic TNBC is guideline-established (ASCENT trial).

judgments.text: In ER-positive AND postmenopausal patients, treatment_tamoxifen increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge, including postmenopausal use.

judgments.text: In HER2-low patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan activity in HER2-low disease is now reflected in guidelines following TROPiCS-02 and related data.

judgments.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable model, the term treatment_palbociclib is non-zero (i.e., contributes to determining palbociclib effect on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: That palbociclib has a treatment effect on PFS is the established guideline finding from PALOMA trials.

judgments.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable model, the term treatment_palbociclib:er_positive is non-zero (i.e., contributes to determining palbociclib effect on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: ER-positivity modifying palbociclib effect is the standard guideline indication for CDK4/6 inhibitors.

judgments.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable model, the term treatment_palbociclib:her2_positive is non-zero (i.e., contributes to determining palbociclib effect on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: A HER2-positive-specific modifier of palbociclib effect is not part of standard guideline indications and would be a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable model, the term treatment_palbociclib:pik3ca_mutation is non-zero (i.e., contributes to determining palbociclib effect on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation status is not an established effect modifier for palbociclib in HR+/HER2- breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable model, the term treatment_palbociclib:ki67_pct is non-zero (i.e., contributes to determining palbociclib effect on pfs_months).
judgments.rationale: Ki67 is not a guideline-endorsed predictive biomarker for palbociclib benefit.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by er_positive=1 & her2_positive=0 & pik3ca_mutation=0, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months by approximately +2.908.
judgments.rationale: Restricting palbociclib benefit to PIK3CA-wildtype within the standard ER+/HER2- population is not a guideline criterion.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by er_positive=1 & pik3ca_mutation=0, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months by approximately +2.376.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning palbociclib benefit on PIK3CA-wildtype status goes beyond standard ER+ selection criteria.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by pr_positive=1 & pik3ca_mutation=0, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months by approximately +2.246.
judgments.rationale: PR-positivity (rather than ER) plus PIK3CA-wildtype is not a standard palbociclib selection paradigm.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by her2_low=1 & pik3ca_mutation=0, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months by approximately +2.044.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low combined with PIK3CA-wildtype is not a recognized palbociclib selection subgroup.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by her2_positive=0 & pik3ca_mutation=0, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months by approximately +2.036.
judgments.rationale: Adding PIK3CA-wildtype to the HER2-negative criterion is not standard guideline practice.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by er_positive=1 & her2_low=1, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months by approximately +1.944.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low status is not an established stratifier for palbociclib benefit in ER+ disease.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months in the subgroup defined by er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0; outside this subgroup, the effect is null. The unfavorable values that suppress the effect are her2_positive=1 (HER2-positive) and pik3ca_mutation=1 (PIK3CA mutation).
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation suppressing palbociclib benefit (effective only in PIK3CA-wildtype) is not established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months in the subgroup defined by brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1; outside this subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1/2-mutated breast cancer is the established guideline indication (OlympiAD/OlympiA).

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_trastuzumab affects mean pfs_months only in HER2-positive (her2_positive=1) patients. (Effect direction and significance to be verified by the test.)
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit being restricted to HER2-positive breast cancer is the foundational guideline-level paradigm for HER2-targeted therapy.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab affects mean pfs_months only in ER-negative AND HER2-negative (TNBC-like) patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's PFS benefit in TNBC (ER-negative/HER2-negative) is established in guidelines (e.g., KEYNOTE-355) for metastatic TNBC.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan affects mean pfs_months only in HER2-low (her2_low=1) patients.
judgments.rationale: Restricting sacituzumab govitecan benefit to HER2-low specifically (rather than the guideline-endorsed TNBC/HR+ HER2-negative populations from ASCENT/TROPiCS-02) is a non-standard subgroup claim not in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_tamoxifen affects mean pfs_months only in ER-positive AND postmenopausal (er_positive=1 AND postmenopausal=1) patients.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy in ER-positive postmenopausal patients is a textbook-level established association in endocrine therapy guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope of pfs_months on age_years).
judgments.rationale: Older age correlating with shorter PFS is a standard prognostic association covered in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between female (sex_female=1) and male (sex_female=0) patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in PFS are a routine demographic comparison and not beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative trend).
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic factor across oncology.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV disease.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having shorter PFS than earlier stages is a foundational staging-based prognostic principle.

judgments.text: Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring shorter PFS is well-established in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Node-positive disease (node_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than node-negative disease.
judgments.rationale: Node-positive disease being associated with worse outcomes is a core textbook prognostic finding.

judgments.text: Postmenopausal patients (postmenopausal=1) have different mean pfs_months than premenopausal patients.
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status differences in PFS reflect standard breast cancer prognostic stratification.

judgments.text: ER-positive patients (er_positive=1) have longer pfs_months than ER-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: ER-positive disease being associated with longer PFS is an established hormone-receptor prognostic paradigm.

judgments.text: PR-positive patients (pr_positive=1) have longer pfs_months than PR-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: PR-positive disease conferring longer PFS is a standard hormone receptor prognostic association.

judgments.text: HER2-positive patients (her2_positive=1) have shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: HER2-positive disease historically associated with worse PFS (absent targeted therapy) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: HER2-low patients (her2_low=1) have different pfs_months than HER2-not-low patients.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low status as a prognostic/predictive category is now established in breast cancer guidelines following trastuzumab deruxtecan approval.

judgments.text: BRCA1-mutation carriers (brca1_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than non-carriers.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1-mutant tumors having distinct prognosis is well-established standard oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: BRCA2-mutation carriers (brca2_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than non-carriers.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutation prognostic associations are standard guideline-level knowledge in breast and ovarian cancer.

judgments.text: PIK3CA-mutation carriers (pik3ca_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than non-carriers.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation prognostic and predictive value (e.g., for alpelisib) is established in current breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct (proliferation index) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High Ki-67 proliferation index as a poor prognostic marker is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Larger tumor size predicting worse outcomes is fundamental TNM staging principle in every guideline.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Hypoalbuminemia as a poor prognostic marker is well-established across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is standard textbook knowledge across many cancers.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss as adverse prognostic factors are textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher NLR as a poor prognostic marker is well-established across oncology, including breast cancer.

judgments.text: Among hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, no clinically meaningful linear associations with pfs_months will be detected.
judgments.rationale: A null hypothesis that routine labs lack linear associations with PFS is not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (in the marginal, unadjusted comparison).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen prolonging PFS is standard endocrine therapy knowledge in breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor) prolonging PFS is established in HR+ metastatic breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit in HER2+ breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant breast cancer is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic TNBC/HR+ breast cancer is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (marginal).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit (with chemo) in PD-L1+ TNBC is guideline-established.

judgments.text: After adjustment for demographics, performance status, stage, brain mets, ER/PR/HER2/HER2-low, BRCA1/BRCA2/PIK3CA, Ki67, tumor size, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin, and alkaline phosphatase, only treatment_palbociclib retains a clinically meaningful adjusted PFS benefit; the other five treatments do not.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that only palbociclib retains adjusted PFS benefit while five other guideline-approved therapies lose effect contradicts established paradigm and is a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Tamoxifen × er_positive interaction: tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months more in ER-positive than ER-negative patients (positive interaction term on PFS).
judgments.rationale: Greater tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive vs ER-negative disease is the textbook predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: Palbociclib × er_positive interaction: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months more in ER-positive than ER-negative patients (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: ER-positive disease responding to CDK4/6 inhibitors like palbociclib is established first-line guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Palbociclib × her2_positive interaction: palbociclib effect on pfs_months is larger in HER2-negative than HER2-positive patients (negative interaction term with HER2 positivity).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's benefit being confined to HER2-negative disease is standard guideline indication (HR+/HER2- breast cancer).

judgments.text: Palbociclib × (er_positive AND her2_negative) joint subgroup: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months substantially in patients who are simultaneously ER-positive and HER2-negative, but not otherwise.
judgments.rationale: ER+/HER2- is the canonical guideline-endorsed indication for palbociclib, not a novel subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab × her2_positive interaction: trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months more in HER2-positive than HER2-negative patients (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab efficacy restricted to HER2-positive disease is foundational textbook oncology.

judgments.text: Sacituzumab govitecan × TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0): sacituzumab prolongs pfs_months more in TNBC than non-TNBC patients (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan is guideline-approved for metastatic TNBC, a well-established indication.

judgments.text: Olaparib × any-BRCA-mutation (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1): olaparib prolongs pfs_months more in BRCA-mutated than BRCA-WT patients (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1/2-mutated breast cancer is a standard guideline-based biomarker-drug pairing.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab × TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0): pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months more in TNBC than non-TNBC patients (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is guideline-indicated for TNBC (KEYNOTE-355/522), an established association.

judgments.text: Within ER-positive HER2-negative patients, palbociclib × pik3ca_mutation interaction is negative: palbociclib prolongs pfs_months in PIK3CA wild-type but not in PIK3CA-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming PIK3CA mutation abrogates palbociclib benefit within HR+/HER2- disease contradicts/extends guideline consensus, which does not stratify CDK4/6i use by PIK3CA status.

judgments.text: Within ER-positive HER2-negative patients, palbociclib effect on pfs_months declines with rising ki67_pct (negative tx*ki67 interaction).
judgments.rationale: A continuous Ki67-dependent attenuation of palbociclib benefit within HR+/HER2- disease is not part of standard guideline decision-making.

judgments.text: Palbociclib prolongs pfs_months substantially (~+4 months) and exclusively in patients who are simultaneously er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct < 20; outside this subgroup palbociclib provides no PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: A narrow four-way conjunction (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/low Ki67) as the exclusive responder subgroup is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, the palbociclib PFS benefit is large and approximately constant for ki67_pct < ~14 and falls toward zero as Ki67 approaches ~20%, consistent with a continuous Ki67 modifier rather than a sharp threshold.
judgments.rationale: A continuous Ki67 modifier with effect attenuating between 14-20% within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT is a finer-grained subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Outside palbociclib, no other treatment shows a reproducible biomarker-defined subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>=0.5 month) treatment-effect on pfs_months in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: A null finding stating no other treatment shows a meaningful biomarker subgroup effect is a dataset-level negative result, not a novel biological claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within BRCA-mutated patients, olaparib × node_positive interaction is positive: olaparib prolongs pfs_months more in node-positive BRCA-mutated patients than in node-negative BRCA-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: A node-positive × BRCA interaction enhancing olaparib PFS benefit is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The olaparib pfs_months benefit is concentrated in BRCA-mutated patients (no further single binary modifier robustly refines this beyond BRCA status given limited N=239 olaparib-treated BRCA carriers).
judgments.rationale: Restricting olaparib benefit to BRCA-mutated patients is the established guideline-level indication.

judgments.text: Within TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0), pembrolizumab × postmenopausal interaction is negative: pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months in premenopausal TNBC but not in postmenopausal TNBC.
judgments.rationale: A menopausal-status × pembrolizumab interaction within TNBC (benefit only premenopausal) is a speculative subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL palbociclib subgroup: treatment_palbociclib=1 prolongs pfs_months by approximately +3.8 to +4.7 months (depending on the Ki67 cutoff used) in patients defined by er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct < 20%; effect is null outside this complete four-variable subgroup. Each of the four predicates is required: ER negativity, HER2 positivity, PIK3CA mutation, and high Ki67 each independently abolishes the effect.
judgments.rationale: A precise four-variable conjunctive subgroup (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<20) where each predicate is individually necessary goes well beyond guideline-level CDK4/6 indications.

judgments.text: Across all six treatments, the rank ordering of subgroup-conditional PFS benefit is: palbociclib (in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<20) ≈ +3.8–4.7 m  >>  olaparib (in any-BRCA-mutated) ≈ +0.35 m ≈ pembrolizumab (in premenopausal TNBC) ≈ +0.28 m  >  tamoxifen ≈ trastuzumab ≈ sacituzumab ≈ 0 (null in any tested subgroup, including their canonical indications).
judgments.rationale: A specific cross-treatment rank ordering of subgroup-conditional effect sizes including null results in canonical indications (trastuzumab, sacituzumab, tamoxifen) is a novel cross-pathway empirical claim.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for biomarkers, ECOG, stage, brain mets, labs, and treatments, age_years remains a positive linear predictor of pfs_months (older → longer PFS) — consistent with what is seen in the unadjusted regression, despite being clinically counter-intuitive in real-world oncology.
judgments.rationale: Older age as a positive linear predictor of longer PFS after multivariable adjustment is clinically counter-intuitive and not a guideline-endorsed association.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: Older age as a prognostic factor in oncology is a well-established clinical consideration covered in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association).
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a foundational, textbook prognostic association across nearly all cancers.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring worse PFS than earlier-stage disease is the basis of TNM staging and is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without brain metastases.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a negative prognostic factor for PFS is a well-established, guideline-level association in breast and other cancers.

judgments.text: er_positive=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than er_positive=0 (positive prognostic).
judgments.rationale: ER positivity as a favorable prognostic and predictive biomarker in breast cancer is textbook consensus.

judgments.text: her2_positive=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0 (negative prognostic).
judgments.rationale: While HER2 positivity was historically negative, in the modern anti-HER2 therapy era this remains a standard, well-known prognostic/predictive marker discussed in guidelines.

judgments.text: pik3ca_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: A direct claim that PIK3CA mutation shortens PFS (independent of subtype/treatment context) is not a clean guideline-level statement and remains debated, making it a non-standard association.

judgments.text: pr_positive=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than pr_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity as a favorable prognostic marker in breast cancer is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: her2_low=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than her2_low=0.
judgments.rationale: A general prognostic PFS advantage for HER2-low versus HER2-zero disease is not established guideline consensus and goes beyond the predictive role for trastuzumab deruxtecan.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin as a favorable prognostic marker is a well-established, textbook association across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as a poor prognostic marker for shorter PFS is well-established in oncology guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High Ki-67 as a marker of proliferation predicting worse outcomes/shorter PFS is standard textbook knowledge in breast oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker associated with shorter PFS is a guideline-level established association across many cancers.

judgments.text: Other lab values (hemoglobin, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, CRP, NLR, alkaline phosphatase, tumor_size_cm) are not strongly associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null-association statement across routine labs is a descriptive negative finding, not a novel mechanistic or subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: node_positive, postmenopausal, sex_female, brca1_mutation, brca2_mutation are not associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Reporting that classical clinicopathologic factors (nodal status, menopausal status, sex, BRCA status) lack association is a descriptive null result, not a novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than =0 in the unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor) prolonging PFS in HR+ breast cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than =0 (unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen prolonging PFS in hormone receptor-positive breast cancer is foundational textbook oncology.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than =0 (unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab prolonging PFS in HER2-positive breast cancer is established guideline-level standard of care.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than =0 (unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib prolonging PFS in BRCA-mutated breast/ovarian cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than =0 (unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan prolonging PFS in metastatic triple-negative/HR+ breast cancer is established guideline-supported knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than =0 (unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in oncology is a well-established paradigm reflected in numerous guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment for prognostic features, treatment_palbociclib retains a substantial positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's PFS benefit in metastatic breast cancer is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_tamoxifen, _trastuzumab, _olaparib, and _pembrolizumab show no overall main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of overall main effect for biomarker-targeted agents in unselected populations is the expected, guideline-consistent finding.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan shows a small positive main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's modest PFS benefit is established in guideline-endorsed indications such as TNBC and HR+/HER2- breast cancer.

judgments.text: The palbociclib effect on pfs_months is positively modified by er_positive (larger benefit in ER+).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib is approved and guideline-recommended specifically for ER+ disease, so larger benefit in ER+ is standard knowledge.

judgments.text: The palbociclib effect on pfs_months is negatively modified by her2_positive (no/negative benefit in HER2+).
judgments.rationale: CDK4/6 inhibitors are indicated in HR+/HER2- disease, so reduced benefit in HER2+ is consistent with current guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: The palbociclib effect on pfs_months is negatively modified by pik3ca_mutation (no benefit in PIK3CA-mut).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not exclude palbociclib in PIK3CA-mutant ER+ breast cancer, so a claim of no benefit in PIK3CA-mut is a non-standard subgroup-conditional assertion.

judgments.text: Palbociclib provides large PFS benefit (>2 months) only in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt patients; no benefit elsewhere.
judgments.rationale: The conjunction restricting meaningful palbociclib benefit to ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt patients goes beyond guideline consensus, which endorses use across PIK3CA status.

judgments.text: trastuzumab effect on pfs_months is positively modified by her2_positive (benefit concentrated in HER2+).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit concentrated in HER2+ disease is a foundational, textbook-level oncology fact.

judgments.text: olaparib effect on pfs_months is positively modified by brca1_mutation or brca2_mutation.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's enhanced benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant tumors is an established, guideline-endorsed biomarker association.

judgments.text: pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is positively modified by triple-negative status (er-, pr-, her2-).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC is established (KEYNOTE-355/522) and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: tamoxifen effect on pfs_months is positively modified by er_positive.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive disease is foundational textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: sacituzumab govitecan effect on pfs_months is positively modified by her2_low.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan targets Trop-2, not HER2; HER2-low as a positive effect modifier for SG is not a guideline-endorsed predictive biomarker (that role belongs to T-DXd).

judgments.text: Among the six therapies, only palbociclib has a clinically meaningful main effect; trastuzumab, tamoxifen, olaparib, pembrolizumab show no benefit even in their canonical molecular subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Claiming trastuzumab, tamoxifen, olaparib, and pembrolizumab provide no benefit in their canonical subgroups directly contradicts established guideline efficacy.

judgments.text: Within the ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt palbociclib responder subgroup, the benefit is further modified by ki67_pct (smaller benefit in high ki67).
judgments.rationale: Ki67 as a quantitative effect modifier within an ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt palbociclib responder subgroup is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker for CDK4/6 inhibitors.

judgments.text: The complete palbociclib responder subgroup is ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/ki67<15; in this group palbociclib provides ~+4.7 mo PFS benefit, with ~0 benefit elsewhere.
judgments.rationale: A four-feature conjunctive responder definition (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/Ki67<15) with a precise ~4.7-month PFS benefit is a speculative compound subgroup not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Sacituzumab govitecan effect is positively modified by has_brain_mets (benefit only in patients with brain metastases).
judgments.rationale: Restricting sacituzumab govitecan benefit to patients with brain metastases is contrary to its established label/indications and not a recognized predictive subgroup.

judgments.text: Within the brain-metastases subgroup, sacituzumab benefit is further concentrated in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/low-ki67 patients.
judgments.rationale: A doubly-conditional brain-mets + ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/low-Ki67 sacituzumab responder subgroup is highly speculative and absent from any guideline.

judgments.text: Within brain mets, the sacituzumab benefit is independent of ER status (i.e., has_brain_mets alone defines the subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Asserting brain-metastasis status alone (independent of ER) defines sacituzumab benefit is a novel subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Comprehensive treatment-by-feature interaction screen across binary and median-split continuous features identifies palbo and sacituzumab as the only treatments with strong heterogeneity signals.
judgments.rationale: A data-driven claim that only palbociclib and sacituzumab show treatment-effect heterogeneity across features is an analytic finding, not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: Adjusted multivariable model: pfs_months = age (+), ecog (-), stage_iv (-), has_brain_mets (-), er+ (+), her2+ (-), pik3ca-mut (-), ki67 (-), albumin (+), ldh (-), weight_loss (-), with palbociclib the main treatment with positive effect.
judgments.rationale: Standard prognostic/predictive associations in breast cancer (ECOG, stage, brain mets, ER/HER2 status, LDH, albumin, palbociclib benefit) are textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Older age_years is a strong positive prognostic factor for pfs_months even after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Older age as a strong positive prognostic factor for PFS after adjustment contradicts standard oncology teaching where older age is typically neutral or adverse.

judgments.text: node_positive, postmenopausal, sex_female, brca1, brca2, tumor_size_cm, hemoglobin, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, CRP, NLR, alkaline phosphatase have null or trivial effects on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming null effects for node positivity, tumor size, BRCA1/2, and PR-related variables on PFS contradicts established prognostic paradigms in breast cancer.

judgments.text: Adjusted treatment effect of trastuzumab in HER2+ patients is null.
judgments.rationale: Null trastuzumab effect in HER2+ disease directly contradicts one of the most established treatment paradigms in oncology.

judgments.text: Adjusted treatment effect of olaparib in BRCA-mutated patients is null.
judgments.rationale: Null olaparib effect in BRCA-mutated patients contradicts the OlympiAD/OlympiA guideline-level evidence supporting PARP inhibition.

judgments.text: Adjusted treatment effect of pembrolizumab in TNBC patients is null.
judgments.rationale: Null pembrolizumab effect in TNBC contradicts KEYNOTE-355/522 guideline-endorsed benefit of checkpoint inhibition in TNBC.

judgments.text: Adjusted treatment effect of tamoxifen in ER+ patients is null.
judgments.rationale: Null tamoxifen effect in ER+ disease contradicts decades of guideline-established endocrine therapy benefit.

judgments.text: Within the palbociclib responder subgroup ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/ki67<15, the magnitude of the palbociclib benefit is independent of stage_iv, brain mets, postmenopausal status, PR, her2_low, and BRCA mutations.
judgments.rationale: A claim that palbociclib benefit within a defined responder subgroup is invariant to stage IV, brain mets, and BRCA status is a specific cross-pathway invariance claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit is concentrated in patients with brain metastases; outside brain mets the effect is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit being concentrated in brain mets patients with no effect elsewhere contradicts ASCENT/TROPiCS-02 guideline indications and is a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Within brain mets the sacituzumab benefit is amplified in ER+/HER2-/low-ki67 patients but the simplest defensible subgroup is has_brain_mets=1 alone.
judgments.rationale: Amplified sacituzumab benefit in ER+/HER2-/low-Ki67 patients with brain mets is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup-conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: When combining all palbociclib subgroup-defining variables, the responder definition is: er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<15. Outside this definition palbociclib has no benefit.
judgments.rationale: Restricting palbociclib benefit to ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/Ki67<15 with zero benefit outside is a speculative multi-marker subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus, which endorses palbociclib broadly in HR+/HER2- disease.

judgments.text: Olaparib has no measurable PFS benefit in BRCA-mutated patients (no signal even unadjusted in the sample once basic prognostics are controlled).
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib has no PFS benefit in BRCA-mutated patients contradicts established guideline consensus from OlympiAD/OlympiA, making this a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab has no measurable PFS benefit in TNBC patients in adjusted analysis.
judgments.rationale: Asserting pembrolizumab has no PFS benefit in TNBC contradicts KEYNOTE-355 guideline-supported use in PD-L1+ mTNBC, making this a non-canonical claim.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab provides no detectable benefit in HER2+ patients in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: Claiming trastuzumab confers no benefit in HER2+ disease contradicts foundational guideline consensus and is therefore a non-standard, novel claim about this dataset.

judgments.text: ECOG performance status, stage_iv and brain mets exhibit additive (not multiplicative) prognostic effects on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: An additive (vs multiplicative) joint prognostic structure for ECOG, stage IV, and brain mets is a specific statistical-interaction claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: The dominant treatment-effect heterogeneity in this cohort is the palbociclib effect, which is concentrated in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/ki67<15 patients (≈+4.7 month PFS benefit).
judgments.rationale: Identifying palbociclib heterogeneity as concentrated specifically in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/Ki67<15 with a quantified +4.7 month effect goes beyond guideline-level subgroup descriptions.

judgments.text: The secondary treatment-effect heterogeneity is the sacituzumab govitecan effect in patients with brain metastases (+0.24 month PFS benefit, smaller magnitude but reproducible).
judgments.rationale: A reproducible sacituzumab govitecan benefit specifically conditioned on brain metastases is not part of guideline consensus, which bases its use on prior-line metastatic TNBC/HR+ status, not CNS involvement.

judgments.text: Final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for palbociclib: in patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<15, palbociclib increases pfs_months by approximately +4.7 months relative to no-palbociclib (effectively zero benefit outside this subgroup).
judgments.rationale: The quantitative four-marker conditional palbociclib effect estimate is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: Final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for sacituzumab govitecan: in patients with has_brain_mets=1, sacituzumab govitecan increases pfs_months by approximately +0.24 months relative to no-sacituzumab (no benefit outside this subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Restricting sacituzumab govitecan benefit to brain-mets patients with a specific effect size is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, and pembrolizumab show no detectable PFS benefit in this cohort, including within their canonical molecular subgroups (ER+, HER2+, BRCA-mut, TNBC respectively).
judgments.rationale: A blanket null-benefit claim across tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, and pembrolizumab within their canonical biomarker subgroups contradicts established guideline efficacy and is therefore non-standard.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's PFS benefit in HR+/HER2- breast cancer is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy in ER+ breast cancer is a foundational, guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab improving outcomes in HER2+ breast cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA-mutant breast cancer is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's PFS benefit in metastatic TNBC and HR+ disease is guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's PFS benefit in PD-L1+ TNBC is established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: er_positive=1 patients have longer pfs_months than er_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: ER positivity conferring better prognosis/PFS is textbook breast cancer knowledge.

judgments.text: pr_positive=1 patients have longer pfs_months than pr_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity as a favorable prognostic marker is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: her2_positive=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2 positivity historically associated with worse prognosis is textbook (though modified by anti-HER2 therapy).

judgments.text: her2_low=1 patients have different pfs_months than her2_low=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low as an independent prognostic category for PFS (separate from its predictive role for T-DXd) is not established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: brca1_mutation=1 patients have different pfs_months than brca1_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1 mutation status as a predictor of outcome is well-established in guidelines, particularly for breast/ovarian cancer.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 patients have different pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation prognostic/predictive value is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: pik3ca_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation as an adverse prognostic marker is established in oncology guidelines (e.g., breast cancer).

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic fact.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an adverse prognostic factor is established consensus.

judgments.text: node_positive=1 patients have different pfs_months than node_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: Nodal status as a prognostic factor is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: postmenopausal=1 patients have different pfs_months than postmenopausal=0.
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status influencing outcomes (especially in breast cancer) is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: sex_female=1 patients have different pfs_months than sex_female=0.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in outcomes are well-documented in standard oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (monotone gradient).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is a foundational textbook association.

judgments.text: Higher age_years correlates with pfs_months (direction unknown a priori).
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic factor is a standard, well-known clinical variable in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl correlates with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic factor for cancer outcomes is well-established in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct correlates with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Ki-67 as a marker of poor prognosis and shorter PFS is standard breast cancer paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo correlates with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is textbook oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l correlates with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is established across many cancers.

judgments.text: Inflammatory markers crp_mg_l and nlr correlate with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: CRP and NLR as inflammatory prognostic markers are well-documented in oncology literature and increasingly in guidelines.

judgments.text: Routine labs (hemoglobin, alk phos, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, tumor_size_cm) have negligible association with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim that tumor size and standard labs have negligible PFS association contradicts conventional staging-based prognostication.

judgments.text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months on all features and treatments, treatment_palbociclib retains a substantial positive coefficient while tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab govitecan, and pembrolizumab do not.
judgments.rationale: A claim that palbociclib uniquely retains effect while other targeted/IO agents do not in a pooled multivariable model is a non-guideline cross-pathway assertion.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months more in er_positive=1 patients than in er_positive=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib benefit being concentrated in ER-positive disease is the established indication and guideline standard.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months differs by postmenopausal status.
judgments.rationale: Differential palbociclib efficacy by menopausal status beyond endocrine partner choice is not a standard guideline claim.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is reduced in her2_positive=1 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: A negative interaction between palbociclib and HER2-positive status on PFS is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen increases pfs_months more in er_positive=1 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit in ER-positive breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab increases pfs_months more in her2_positive=1 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab efficacy in HER2-positive disease is standard-of-care textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months more in brca1_mutation=1 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA1-mutant cancer is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months more in brca2_mutation=1 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant cancer is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months more in her2_low=1 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan activity in HER2-low breast cancer is recognized in current guidelines following ASCENT and related data.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs by pik3ca_mutation status.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutation status is not a guideline-established predictor of pembrolizumab benefit, making this a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Treatment assignment is approximately balanced across biomarker subgroups (no channeling), so observed treatment effects are not confounded by biomarker-driven indication.
judgments.rationale: This is a methodological observation about study balance, not a biological hypothesis about the disease.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen prolonging PFS in ER-positive patients is core guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=1 AND postmenopausal=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen efficacy in postmenopausal ER-positive breast cancer is long-established standard care.

judgments.text: Within her2_positive=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab prolonging PFS in HER2-positive disease is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib prolongs pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA1/2-mutant breast cancer is established guideline knowledge (OlympiAD/OlympiA).

judgments.text: Within triple-negative (er_positive=0, pr_positive=0, her2_positive=0) patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan prolongs pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan benefit in metastatic triple-negative breast cancer is standard of care (ASCENT trial, NCCN guidelines).

judgments.text: Within triple-negative patients, treatment_pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in triple-negative breast cancer is established (KEYNOTE-355/522) and in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is reduced in pik3ca_mutation=1 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: A negative interaction between PIK3CA mutation and CDK4/6 inhibitor efficacy is not established guideline consensus and would be a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is greater in pr_positive=1 patients (positive interaction, secondary to ER+ correlation).
judgments.rationale: PR positivity as a specific positive modifier of palbociclib benefit beyond ER status is not in guidelines and represents a speculative subgroup interaction.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib effect is enhanced in her2_low=1 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: HER2-low status as an enhancer of CDK4/6 inhibitor efficacy is not an established or guideline-endorsed predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib markedly prolongs pfs_months in the joint subgroup er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0, with little or no benefit outside it.
judgments.rationale: A three-way joint subgroup (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wildtype) defining palbociclib benefit with no effect elsewhere is a speculative cross-feature claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across all pairs of binary features, exhaustive search will reveal whether tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab_govitecan, or pembrolizumab has any 2-feature subgroup with a meaningful (>0.3 month) and significant (p<0.01) effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Exhaustive 2-feature subgroup mining for unexpected drug-by-feature interactions across multiple agents is hypothesis-generating and not guideline-derivable.

judgments.text: Within er_positive=0 AND (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) patients, treatment_olaparib prolongs pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit specifically conditioned on the joint ER-negative AND BRCA-mutant subgroup goes beyond the BRCA-only indication in guidelines.

judgments.text: The unadjusted olaparib benefit in the BRCAany & ER- subgroup is driven by confounding (treated patients are older), and disappears after adjusting for age and prognostic covariates.
judgments.rationale: A claim that an apparent olaparib benefit is an age/confounding artifact that vanishes after adjustment is a methodological/causal hypothesis not present in guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The strong positive association between age_years and pfs_months is robust within strata of ecog_ps and stage_iv (i.e., not an artifact of sicker young patients).
judgments.rationale: A positive age-PFS association persisting after adjustment for ECOG and stage is a descriptive epidemiologic observation, not a guideline-relevant novel claim.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt, treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months decreases monotonically across ki67_pct quartiles (largest in low-ki67, vanishing in high-ki67).
judgments.rationale: A monotonic decline of palbociclib benefit across Ki67 quartiles within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt is a specific subgroup-conditional effect-modification claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt, the treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months declines linearly with ki67_pct (negative interaction coefficient).
judgments.rationale: A linear negative Ki67-by-palbociclib interaction within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt is a quantitative effect-modification hypothesis beyond standard CDK4/6 guideline language.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib produces a very large pfs_months gain (~5 months) in the joint subgroup er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<=14, and a negligible effect outside it.
judgments.rationale: Restricting palbociclib benefit to a four-way joint subgroup (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CAwt/Ki67<=14) with negligible effect elsewhere is a speculative cross-biomarker stratification not endorsed in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusted univariate, biomarker-interaction, and exhaustive 2-way subgroup screens, no subgroup definition yields a significant adjusted positive PFS benefit for treatment_tamoxifen, treatment_trastuzumab, treatment_olaparib, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan, or treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim that no adjusted subgroup yields PFS benefit for tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab govitecan, or pembrolizumab contradicts established guideline-endorsed indications and is a non-trivial dataset-specific assertion.

judgments.text: The cohort has unusual demographic features for a labeled breast cancer dataset: only ~45% female, with PFS improving with age - both consistent with synthetic data rather than real EHR breast oncology.
judgments.rationale: Flagging the dataset as likely synthetic based on sex distribution and age-PFS direction is a meta-level data-provenance claim, not an oncology guideline statement.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: Older age correlating with outcomes is a standard prognostic consideration in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor is well-established in oncology textbooks and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: High Ki-67 as a marker of poor prognosis is a standard textbook association.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is a guideline-level established association.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is standard in oncology guidelines (e.g., melanoma, lymphoma staging).

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established across malignancies.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as a poor prognostic marker is widely established in oncology literature and increasingly in guidelines.

judgments.text: Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Larger tumor size predicting worse outcomes is a foundational staging/prognostic principle.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than non-stage IV is a definitional guideline-level fact.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring worse prognosis is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (monotonic).
judgments.rationale: Worse performance status predicting shorter PFS is a universal prognostic factor in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: er_positive=1 patients have longer pfs_months than er_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: ER-positive breast cancer having better outcomes/longer PFS than ER-negative is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: pr_positive=1 patients have longer pfs_months than pr_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity as a favorable prognostic marker in breast cancer is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: her2_positive=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: HER2 positivity as an adverse prognostic factor (in absence of HER2-targeted therapy) is well-established paradigm.

judgments.text: pik3ca_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: PIK3CA mutations as adverse prognostic markers in breast cancer is a commonly described association in current guidelines.

judgments.text: brca1_mutation or brca2_mutation positivity is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRCA1/2 mutations are not consistently associated with shorter PFS in breast cancer guidelines; outcomes depend on treatment context (e.g., PARP inhibitors).

judgments.text: postmenopausal status is associated with different pfs_months relative to pre-menopausal status.
judgments.rationale: Menopausal status differences in breast cancer outcomes are standard prognostic considerations in guidelines.

judgments.text: node_positive=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than node_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Node positivity predicting worse outcomes is foundational breast cancer staging/prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: sex_female has a main effect on pfs_months in this breast cohort.
judgments.rationale: Sex effect within a breast cancer cohort (male vs female breast cancer) is not a standard guideline-level prognostic claim and would be unusual.

judgments.text: In a multivariable OLS for pfs_months including all features and treatments, age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss_pct_6mo, albumin_g_dl, ki67_pct, er_positive, her2_positive, pik3ca_mutation, ldh_u_l, and treatment_palbociclib will each have independent effects with the directions seen in univariate analysis.
judgments.rationale: The claim that all listed covariates retain independent effects with univariate directions in a fully adjusted multivariable model is a specific multivariable assertion not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: her2_low=1 has only a marginal independent association with pfs_months once HER2-positive and other features are adjusted for.
judgments.rationale: HER2-low having marginal independent prognostic effect after adjustment is consistent with current understanding that HER2-low is primarily a therapeutic marker rather than an independent prognostic factor.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_tamoxifen=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen prolonging PFS in appropriate (ER+) breast cancer patients is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_palbociclib=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_palbociclib=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib prolonging PFS in HR+ metastatic breast cancer is established standard-of-care from PALOMA trials.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_trastuzumab=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab prolonging PFS in HER2+ breast cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib prolonging PFS in BRCA-mutant breast cancer is established guideline knowledge from OlympiAD.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan prolonging PFS in metastatic TNBC/HR+ breast cancer is established from ASCENT/TROPiCS-02 trials.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab prolonging PFS in PD-L1+ TNBC is established guideline knowledge from KEYNOTE-355.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in er_positive=1 patients (positive interaction palbociclib×er_positive).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's benefit being concentrated in ER-positive patients is the defining indication in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is abolished in her2_positive=1 patients (negative interaction palbociclib×her2_positive).
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib is not indicated/effective in HER2+ disease per current guidelines, so abolished effect in HER2+ patients matches established paradigm.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is abolished by pik3ca_mutation=1 (negative interaction palbociclib×pik3ca_mutation).
judgments.rationale: A PIK3CA mutation abolishing palbociclib benefit is a speculative biomarker-conditional claim not reflected in current guidelines, which still recommend CDK4/6 inhibitors regardless of PIK3CA status.

judgments.text: The treatment_palbociclib effect on pfs_months is larger in pr_positive=1 than in pr_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: PR positivity as a modifier of palbociclib benefit within HR+ disease is broadly known and not a novel cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Within the ER+/HER2- subgroup, treatment_palbociclib has a much larger PFS benefit (>1.5 mo) than in the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Palbociclib's PFS benefit in ER+/HER2- is the established guideline indication (PALOMA trials).

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by ~3 months on average; PIK3CA mutation negates the benefit even within ER+/HER2-.
judgments.rationale: Claiming PIK3CA mutation negates CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit within ER+/HER2- contradicts guideline consensus that palbociclib benefit is preserved regardless of PIK3CA status.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen has a larger PFS benefit in er_positive=1 than in er_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Tamoxifen benefit being concentrated in ER-positive disease is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_tamoxifen has a larger PFS benefit in postmenopausal=1 than in pre-menopausal patients (within er_positive=1).
judgments.rationale: Differential tamoxifen effects by menopausal status within ER+ disease are well-established and discussed in standard adjuvant endocrine therapy guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab has a positive PFS effect that is concentrated in her2_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab benefit confined to HER2-positive disease is the canonical biomarker-drug pairing.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab has a positive PFS effect in her2_low=1 patients (extension to HER2-low biology).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab (monotherapy-style) PFS benefit in HER2-low patients goes beyond guidelines, which reserve HER2-low indications for trastuzumab deruxtecan, not trastuzumab itself.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib has a positive PFS effect concentrated in patients with brca1_mutation=1 or brca2_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit concentrated in BRCA1/2-mutant patients is the established companion-diagnostic indication.

judgments.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan has a positive PFS effect concentrated in TNBC (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) patients.
judgments.rationale: Sacituzumab govitecan's PFS benefit in metastatic TNBC is the guideline-approved indication.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab has a positive PFS effect concentrated in TNBC patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's PFS benefit in TNBC (especially PD-L1+ or early-stage) is established in current breast cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, the treatment_palbociclib PFS effect declines as ki67_pct increases (negative palbociclib×ki67_pct interaction in the adjusted model).
judgments.rationale: Ki67-dependent attenuation of palbociclib benefit within PIK3CA-WT ER+/HER2- is a subgroup-conditional interaction not codified in guidelines, which recommend CDK4/6i broadly across ER+/HER2- regardless of Ki67.

judgments.text: In the full cohort, treatment_palbociclib effect declines with rising ki67_pct (adjusted T×ki67 interaction is negative and significant).
judgments.rationale: A negative palbociclib×Ki67 interaction in the full cohort (across non-ER+ patients too) is a cross-population claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, treatment_palbociclib produces large PFS gains in the lowest ki67_pct tertile, intermediate gains in the middle tertile, and no detectable gain in the highest tertile.
judgments.rationale: Tertile-stratified loss of palbociclib benefit at the highest Ki67 tertile is a specific dose-response pattern not in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, the treatment_palbociclib PFS benefit is concentrated below ki67_pct≈14, with effect ≥+4.5 months below this cutoff and essentially zero above it.
judgments.rationale: A discrete Ki67≈14 cutoff demarcating palbociclib benefit (≥4.5 mo below, none above) is not a guideline-endorsed selection criterion for CDK4/6 inhibitors.

judgments.text: All four predicates — er_positive=1, her2_positive=0, pik3ca_mutation=0, ki67_pct<14 — are individually binding for the maximal palbociclib effect (~+4.94 mo); dropping any one materially reduces the estimated effect.
judgments.rationale: Requiring PIK3CA-WT and Ki67<14 (in addition to ER+/HER2-) as jointly necessary for maximal palbociclib effect goes beyond standard ER+/HER2- selection in guidelines.

judgments.text: The palbociclib PFS benefit in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/ki67<14 survives adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss_pct_6mo, albumin_g_dl, and ki67_pct.
judgments.rationale: Demonstrating robustness of palbociclib benefit in a narrow PIK3CA-WT/Ki67-low subgroup after multivariable adjustment is a non-guideline subgroup-specific claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment within their canonical biomarker subgroups, none of trastuzumab (HER2+), olaparib (BRCA1/2+), sacituzumab (TNBC), pembrolizumab (TNBC), or tamoxifen (ER+) show a meaningful (≥0.2 mo) PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Asserting near-zero adjusted PFS benefit for trastuzumab/olaparib/sacituzumab/pembrolizumab/tamoxifen in their canonical biomarker subgroups directly contradicts established guideline paradigms — paradigm-violating, hence novel.

judgments.text: A systematic adjusted treatment-by-binary-biomarker interaction screen will find significant modifiers ONLY for treatment_palbociclib (with er_positive, pr_positive, her2_positive, her2_low, pik3ca_mutation), and none for the other treatments.
judgments.rationale: A systematic claim that only palbociclib shows biomarker-interaction modifiers (across multiple binary markers) while other agents show none is an exploratory cross-treatment pattern not represented in guidelines.

judgments.text: An adjusted treatment-by-continuous-biomarker interaction screen will identify ki67_pct as a (negative) modifier of the palbociclib effect and find no comparable modifier for the other treatments.
judgments.rationale: Selectively identifying Ki67 as a negative continuous modifier for palbociclib alone, with no analogous continuous modifier for other agents, is a comparative interaction finding beyond guideline content.
