judgments.text: After adjusting for the rego_in_subgroup interaction term, the apparent main effects of kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary on pfs_months largely disappear, indicating those associations are mediated by their predicting non-benefit from regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Claiming KRAS, BRAF V600E, and right-sidedness main effects on PFS are mediated by predicting non-benefit from regorafenib is a cross-pathway mechanistic claim not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within the triple-favorable rego subgroup, the regorafenib pfs_months benefit is attenuated as cea_ng_ml increases.
judgments.rationale: A CEA-graded attenuation of regorafenib benefit within a triple-favorable subgroup is a speculative continuous biomarker interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The regorafenib treatment effect varies with age_years.
judgments.rationale: Age-modified regorafenib treatment effect is not an established guideline biomarker for regorafenib activity.

judgments.text: Regorafenib treatment effect varies with albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl, or alkaline_phosphatase_u_l.
judgments.rationale: Modification of regorafenib effect by albumin/LDH/CRP/NLR/Hb/ALP/weight loss is a speculative lab-based interaction claim not in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The regorafenib benefit within the triple-favorable subgroup is approximately constant in absolute magnitude across ECOG performance status levels (0, 1, 2).
judgments.rationale: Constancy of absolute regorafenib benefit across ECOG levels within a triple-favorable subgroup is a specific subgroup-conditional invariance claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the triple-favorable subgroup, NRAS mutation status does not change the regorafenib effect.
judgments.rationale: Independence of regorafenib effect from NRAS within a triple-favorable subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional null interaction not addressed in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +2.74 months (95% CI roughly [2.71, 2.76] from OLS) ONLY in the subgroup of patients who simultaneously satisfy kras_mutation==0 AND braf_v600e==0 AND right_sided_primary==0; in any patient with kras_mutation==1 OR braf_v600e==1 OR right_sided_primary==1, the regorafenib effect on pfs_months is zero (point estimate near 0, p=0.29). No other treatment in this cohort (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab_tucatinib) shows a clinically or statistically meaningful pfs_months effect overall or in any pre-specified biomarker subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A precise ~+2.74-month regorafenib PFS benefit confined to KRAS/BRAF-wildtype left-sided patients with null effects elsewhere and for all other agents (including cetuximab in RAS-WT left-sided and encorafenib in BRAF V600E) directly contradicts guideline consensus and is a strong novel claim.

judgments.text: After adjusting for ECOG, stage, biomarkers, labs, and all treatments (including the rego_in_subgroup split), age_years remains independently positively associated with pfs_months at +0.176 months per year.
judgments.rationale: An independent +0.176 months/year age effect on PFS after adjustment for ECOG, stage, labs, biomarkers, and treatments is not a guideline-recognized prognostic relationship and runs counter to typical expectations.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
judgments.rationale: Poor performance status predicting worse outcomes is a foundational prognostic principle in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV (metastatic) disease having shorter PFS than non-metastatic is the basis of TNM staging and standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: sex_female is associated with a difference in pfs_months versus male patients.
judgments.rationale: A sex-based PFS difference is not an established guideline-level prognostic factor in most solid tumors and would require subgroup-specific justification.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect, as is typical in oncology).
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with worse oncologic outcomes is a standard, well-documented prognostic factor.

judgments.text: right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided primary tumors.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse prognosis than left-sided is established colorectal cancer guideline knowledge (sidedness effect).

judgments.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as an adverse prognostic and predictive marker (e.g., in CRC, NSCLC) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E conferring poor prognosis (especially in CRC) is well-established in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: nras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation as an adverse prognostic/predictive marker in CRC and melanoma is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: msi_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than microsatellite-stable patients (better prognosis in MSI-H).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status conferring better prognosis (particularly in early-stage CRC) is textbook oncology consensus.

judgments.text: Lower albumin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (positive slope of PFS on albumin).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is a standard, well-documented clinical association across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as a poor prognostic marker associated with shorter PFS is a well-established standard finding in GI and other CEA-expressing cancers.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor for survival and PFS is a textbook oncology concept.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker linked to shorter PFS is a guideline-level standard across many malignancies.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic inflammatory marker associated with shorter PFS is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio as a negative prognostic biomarker for PFS is a widely recognized standard finding across solid tumors.

judgments.text: her2_amplified=1 is associated with a different pfs_months than HER2-non-amplified.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification altering PFS (typically improved with HER2-targeted therapy or worse without it) is paradigm-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with a different pfs_months than NTRK-non-fused.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions defining a distinct prognostic/therapeutic subgroup with altered PFS (especially with TRK inhibitors) is established guideline content.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab improving PFS versus no treatment is standard guideline-level efficacy in appropriate indications like RAS-wildtype CRC and head/neck cancer.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab prolonging PFS compared with no treatment is a well-established guideline finding across multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS versus no treatment is standard guideline-level oncology knowledge across many indications.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in metastatic colorectal cancer is established (BEACON trial) and reflected in guidelines for BRAF V600E mCRC.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab + tucatinib activity is established in HER2-positive disease (MOUNTAINEER for mCRC, HER2CLIMB for breast) and is in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's PFS benefit in refractory mCRC is established (CORRECT trial) and is standard guideline therapy.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with msi_high=1, with positive interaction (T*B coefficient > 0).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors (KEYNOTE-177/158) is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with braf_v600e=1, with positive interaction.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's selective benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant tumors is the canonical guideline indication.

judgments.text: Encorafenib + cetuximab combination further extends pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients vs neither/either alone.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib + cetuximab in BRAF V600E mCRC is the FDA-approved, guideline-endorsed combination from BEACON.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with her2_amplified=1, with positive interaction.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib selective benefit in HER2-amplified disease is the standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in patients who are kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided primary).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit limited to RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided mCRC is well-established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by kras_mutation: regorafenib improves PFS in KRAS wild-type but has reduced/no benefit in KRAS-mutant.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS status and a KRAS-dependent differential benefit is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by braf_v600e: regorafenib improves PFS in BRAF wild-type but has reduced/no benefit in BRAF V600E.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is used irrespective of BRAF status and a BRAF V600E-specific attenuation of benefit is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by right_sided_primary: regorafenib improves PFS in left-sided but has reduced/no benefit in right-sided primary.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness as a regorafenib effect modifier is not in CRC guidelines; sidedness is established for anti-EGFR but not for regorafenib.

judgments.text: Regorafenib improves pfs_months substantially and ONLY in patients who are simultaneously kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided); outside this triple-positive subgroup it has no effect.
judgments.rationale: A triple-conditional KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup restriction for regorafenib benefit is not a guideline-recognized predictive paradigm.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is further modified by cea_ng_ml: lower CEA enhances and higher CEA attenuates the benefit.
judgments.rationale: CEA as a continuous predictive biomarker for regorafenib benefit is not described in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps (effect smaller in higher-ECOG patients).
judgments.rationale: Reduced benefit of systemic therapy in higher-ECOG patients is a textbook/guideline-level principle used routinely for regorafenib patient selection.

judgments.text: Within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, the regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated at low CEA (cea_ng_ml < ~5 ng/mL) and disappears above ~5 ng/mL.
judgments.rationale: A within-subgroup CEA threshold (~5 ng/mL) gating regorafenib benefit is a speculative cross-feature claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib increases pfs_months ONLY in patients who simultaneously satisfy all four predicates: kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; outside this fourfold subgroup the regorafenib effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conjunctive subgroup defining regorafenib responders is a speculative cross-pathway claim not present in any guideline.

judgments.text: nras_mutation modifies the regorafenib effect (within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, NRAS-mutant patients still derive benefit similar to NRAS-WT).
judgments.rationale: The claim that NRAS mutation does not attenuate regorafenib benefit within an already-stratified subgroup is a specific, non-guideline mechanistic assertion.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib with respect to any continuous lab feature (significant T:F interaction at p<0.001).
judgments.rationale: Continuous lab features acting as predictive modifiers for these targeted/IO agents are not recognized in current CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for any non-regorafenib treatment with respect to any of the 8 binary features (kras, nras, braf, msi, her2, ntrk, right-sided, stage IV) at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: This umbrella claim is satisfied by textbook associations (KRAS/NRAS×cetuximab, BRAF×encorafenib, MSI×pembrolizumab, HER2×trastuzumab-tucatinib).

judgments.text: T-learner CATE estimates from gradient-boosted regression trees show meaningful heterogeneity (CATE std > 0.5 months) for at least one non-regorafenib treatment.
judgments.rationale: Detecting CATE heterogeneity for at least one non-regorafenib treatment is expected given guideline-established biomarker-treatment interactions.

judgments.text: right_sided_primary x kras_mutation interact on pfs_months (the negative effects do not simply add).
judgments.rationale: Interaction between right-sided primary and KRAS mutation on PFS is a cross-pathway claim beyond standard prognostic statements about each factor individually.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl x stage_iv interact on pfs_months (low albumin is more harmful in stage IV).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin and advanced stage are well-established adverse prognostic factors, and their compounding effect is part of standard clinical reasoning.

judgments.text: weight_loss_pct_6mo x ecog_ps interact on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A specific multiplicative interaction between weight loss and ECOG performance status on PFS goes beyond the additive prognostic role each plays in guidelines.

judgments.text: In a full multivariable OLS controlling for all features and treatments, regorafenib remains significantly associated with longer PFS while other treatments remain non-significant.
judgments.rationale: Claiming regorafenib uniquely retains a PFS benefit after full multivariable adjustment while all other agents lose significance contradicts its guideline-recognized modest later-line role.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, age_years retains a positive coefficient on pfs_months (older = longer PFS), in contradiction to typical oncology priors.
judgments.rationale: A positive adjusted coefficient for age on PFS contradicts standard oncology priors and would not appear in any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: Stage IV combined with ECOG=2 yields markedly shorter PFS than the additive model would predict.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV plus ECOG 2 producing markedly worse PFS than expected reflects standard prognostic teaching that poor PS amplifies advanced-stage outcomes.

judgments.text: Patients with both stage_iv=1 AND ecog_ps>=1 have mean pfs_months below 4 months, while patients with stage_iv=0 AND ecog_ps=0 have mean pfs_months above 5 months.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV with ECOG≥1 having worse PFS than stage <IV with ECOG 0 is a textbook prognostic stratification.

judgments.text: Treatment-treatment interactions exist (e.g., regorafenib+bevacizumab combination has synergistic or antagonistic effect).
judgments.rationale: Specific synergistic or antagonistic regorafenib+bevacizumab interactions are not part of guideline-endorsed combinations and would be a novel pharmacodynamic claim.

judgments.text: A T-learner tree on the regorafenib treatment effect identifies kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, and cea_ng_ml as the dominant treatment-effect modifiers (confirming the joint subgroup).
judgments.rationale: A causal-forest-style identification of KRAS, BRAF V600E, right-sidedness, and CEA as joint modifiers of regorafenib effect is not part of guideline framing of regorafenib selection.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab benefit might be detectable in left-sided primary tumors (left-sided MSI-H) compared to right-sided MSI-H.
judgments.rationale: Differential pembrolizumab benefit by sidedness within MSI-H CRC is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond the guideline statement that MSI-H predicts ICI response.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab benefit might be detectable in stage IV patients specifically.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high colorectal cancer (including stage IV) is established guideline-level knowledge, not novel.

judgments.text: FINAL refined treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for pfs_months: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months EXCLUSIVELY in patients with kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; outside this 4-predicate subgroup the regorafenib effect is indistinguishable from zero.
judgments.rationale: A highly specific 4-predicate conjunction (KRAS-WT AND BRAF-WT AND left-sided AND CEA<5) gating a ~5-month regorafenib PFS benefit goes well beyond guideline consensus, which positions regorafenib as a modestly active later-line agent without such biomarker-defined responder subgroups.

judgments.text: None of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has a detectable beneficial effect on pfs_months in any subgroup defined by demographic, clinical, biomarker, or laboratory features in this cohort, even within their canonical biomarker-matched subpopulations (MSI-H, BRAF V600E, HER2-amplified, RAS/BRAF-WT left-sided).
judgments.rationale: Claiming that cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab-tucatinib show no PFS benefit in any subgroup—including their canonical biomarker-matched populations—directly contradicts guideline consensus and is a non-standard, speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: In a final multivariable model the strongest prognostic features for longer pfs_months are (in descending |effect|) ecog_ps (-1.17/level), stage_iv (-1.36), albumin_g_dl (+0.47/g/dL), kras_mutation (-0.35), braf_v600e (-0.36), right_sided_primary (-0.29), age_years (+0.18/yr), weight_loss_pct_6mo (-0.076/%), and cea_ng_ml (-0.005/ng/mL).
judgments.rationale: ECOG, stage IV, albumin, KRAS, BRAF, sidedness, age, weight loss, and CEA are all well-established prognostic factors in colorectal cancer and would appear in any standard textbook/guideline discussion.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with msi_high=1, with positive interaction (T*B coefficient > 0).

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with braf_v600e=1, with positive interaction.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's selective activity in BRAF V600E-mutant tumors is the foundational labeled indication and guideline standard.

judgments.text: Encorafenib + cetuximab combination further extends pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: The encorafenib + cetuximab combination in BRAF V600E mCRC (BEACON) is guideline-standard, not novel.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with her2_amplified=1, with positive interaction.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab + tucatinib selectively benefiting HER2-amplified mCRC (MOUNTAINEER) is a guideline-endorsed biomarker-defined indication.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in patients who are kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab's restriction to RAS/BRAF wild-type left-sided mCRC is established guideline consensus from CALGB/SWOG 80405 and FIRE-3.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by kras_mutation: regorafenib improves PFS in KRAS wild-type but loses its benefit in KRAS-mutant.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS status and guidelines do not restrict it by KRAS; a KRAS-modified efficacy claim goes beyond consensus.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by braf_v600e: regorafenib improves PFS in BRAF wild-type but loses its benefit in BRAF V600E.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is used irrespective of BRAF status per guidelines; a claim that BRAF V600E abolishes its benefit is a speculative biomarker interaction not in current consensus.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by right_sided_primary: regorafenib improves PFS in left-sided but loses its benefit in right-sided primary.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness as a regorafenib-specific predictive biomarker is not established in CRC guidelines (sidedness is mainly used to guide anti-EGFR therapy).

judgments.text: Regorafenib improves pfs_months substantially and ONLY in patients who are simultaneously kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided); outside this triple-positive subgroup it has no effect.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS/BRAF/sidedness; restricting benefit to this triple-WT/left-sided subgroup is not in any guideline.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is further modified by cea_ng_ml: lower CEA enhances and higher CEA attenuates the benefit (negative T:CEA interaction).
judgments.rationale: CEA as a continuous predictive biomarker for regorafenib benefit is not described in current oncology guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps (effect smaller in higher-ECOG patients).
judgments.rationale: Diminished benefit in higher-ECOG patients reflects generic clinical knowledge that poor performance status reduces treatment benefit across regimens.

judgments.text: Within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, the regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated at low CEA (cea_ng_ml < ~5 ng/mL) and disappears above ~5 ng/mL.
judgments.rationale: A compound subgroup defined by triple molecular/sidedness status and a CEA threshold is not part of any established regorafenib paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months ONLY in patients who simultaneously satisfy kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; outside this fourfold subgroup the regorafenib effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conjunctive predictive subgroup for regorafenib is well beyond current guideline-supported biomarkers.

judgments.text: Within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, nras_mutation status does not modify the regorafenib effect (NRAS-mutant patients still derive similar benefit).
judgments.rationale: Stating NRAS does not modify regorafenib effect is a null finding consistent with current guidelines, which do not list NRAS as a regorafenib predictor.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib with respect to any continuous lab feature (significant T:F interaction at p<0.001).
judgments.rationale: Continuous lab-value interactions with these targeted/IO/biologic therapies are not part of guideline-established predictive biomarkers.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for any non-regorafenib treatment with respect to any of the 8 binary features (kras, nras, braf, msi, her2, ntrk, right-sided, stage IV) at p<0.001.
judgments.rationale: Binary biomarker interactions with these agents (KRAS×cetuximab, MSI×pembrolizumab, BRAF V600E×encorafenib, HER2×trastuzumab-tucatinib) are core guideline content.

judgments.text: T-learner CATE estimates from gradient-boosted regression trees show meaningful heterogeneity (CATE std > 0.5 months) for at least one non-regorafenib treatment.
judgments.rationale: A generic statistical heterogeneity claim from a T-learner is likely to capture well-known biomarker-defined effects already in guidelines.

judgments.text: right_sided_primary x kras_mutation interact on pfs_months (the negative effects do not simply add).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines note KRAS mutation and right-sided primaries each portend worse prognosis, but a non-additive statistical interaction between sidedness and KRAS on PFS is a specific quantitative claim not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl x stage_iv interact on pfs_months (low albumin more harmful in stage IV).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin and stage IV are well-established adverse prognostic factors, and that malnutrition compounds advanced-stage disease is consistent with standard prognostic teaching.

judgments.text: weight_loss_pct_6mo x ecog_ps interact on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: While weight loss and ECOG are each established prognostic factors, a specific multiplicative interaction between them on PFS is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: In a full multivariable OLS controlling for all features and treatments, regorafenib remains significantly associated with longer PFS while other treatments remain non-significant.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is a later-line agent with modest survival benefit; a claim that it uniquely associates with longer PFS in adjusted analyses while other agents do not is contrary to standard expectations.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, age_years retains a positive coefficient on pfs_months (older = longer PFS), in contradiction to typical oncology priors.
judgments.rationale: Older age being associated with longer PFS after adjustment contradicts the typical prior that advanced age portends worse outcomes and would not appear in a guideline.

judgments.text: stage_iv x ecog_ps interact such that high-ECOG stage IV patients have super-additively shorter PFS.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV plus poor performance status producing markedly shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic combination, even if framed as super-additive.

judgments.text: Patients with both stage_iv=1 AND ecog_ps>=1 have mean pfs_months below 4 months, while patients with stage_iv=0 AND ecog_ps=0 have mean pfs_months above 5 months.
judgments.rationale: Quantifying that stage IV with ECOG>=1 has shorter PFS than stage <IV with ECOG 0 is a restatement of standard prognostic stratification.

judgments.text: Pairwise treatment-treatment interactions exist on pfs_months (e.g., regorafenib+bevacizumab synergy or antagonism).
judgments.rationale: Specific pairwise treatment-treatment synergies/antagonisms (e.g., regorafenib+bevacizumab) on PFS are not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: A T-learner gradient-boosted tree on the regorafenib treatment effect identifies kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, and cea_ng_ml as the dominant treatment-effect modifiers (confirming the joint subgroup).
judgments.rationale: A causal-ML identification of KRAS, BRAF V600E, right-sidedness, and CEA as dominant regorafenib effect modifiers goes beyond guideline statements about regorafenib's broad later-line use.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab benefit might be detectable in left-sided primary tumors (left-sided MSI-H) compared to right-sided MSI-H.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse pembrolizumab for MSI-H CRC regardless of sidedness; a subgroup claim that benefit is concentrated in left-sided MSI-H tumors is not standard.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab benefit might be detectable in stage IV patients specifically.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high metastatic colorectal cancer is established guideline-level knowledge (KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: FINAL refined treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for pfs_months: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months EXCLUSIVELY in patients with kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; outside this 4-predicate subgroup the regorafenib effect is indistinguishable from zero.
judgments.rationale: A four-predicate conjunctive subgroup (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided, low CEA) as the exclusive responder population for regorafenib is a speculative cross-feature claim not in guidelines, which support regorafenib broadly in refractory mCRC.

judgments.text: None of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has a detectable beneficial effect on pfs_months in any subgroup defined by demographic, clinical, biomarker, or laboratory features in this cohort, even within their canonical biomarker-matched subpopulations (MSI-H, BRAF V600E, HER2-amplified, RAS/BRAF-WT left-sided).
judgments.rationale: Claiming no benefit for cetuximab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, or trastuzumab-tucatinib even within their canonical biomarker-matched populations contradicts established guideline consensus and is therefore a non-standard, novel claim.

judgments.text: In a final multivariable model the strongest prognostic features for pfs_months are (in descending |effect|) stage_iv (-1.36), ecog_ps (-1.17/level), albumin_g_dl (+0.47/g/dL), braf_v600e (-0.36), kras_mutation (-0.35), right_sided_primary (-0.29), age_years (+0.18/yr), weight_loss_pct_6mo (-0.076/%), and cea_ng_ml (-0.005/ng/mL).
judgments.rationale: The listed prognostic features (stage, ECOG, albumin, BRAF, KRAS, sidedness, age, weight loss, CEA) and their directions are textbook prognostic factors in colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs by ecog_ps level (worse PFS with higher ECOG performance status).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a universally established prognostic factor for PFS in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than non-stage IV is foundational staging-based prognostication in any oncology textbook.

judgments.text: Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided).
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse outcomes than left-sided is established colorectal cancer guideline consensus (e.g., NCCN).

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin predicting better outcomes is a well-established prognostic marker reflected in indices like the Glasgow Prognostic Score.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is standard textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as an adverse prognostic biomarker is established in colorectal and other GI cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic factor is guideline-established across many cancers (melanoma staging, lymphoma IPI, etc.).

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (expected direction in cancer).
judgments.rationale: Older age as an adverse prognostic factor is standard, well-documented oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (inflammation hurts).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic factor is well-established (Glasgow Prognostic Score) in oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as an adverse prognostic marker is widely established across solid tumors.

judgments.text: sex_female is associated with different mean pfs_months than males.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based PFS differences in colorectal cancer are a recognized but modest prognostic factor discussed in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic and predictive marker in colorectal cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with braf_v600e=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E conferring shorter PFS in colorectal cancer is well-established and appears in NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have different mean pfs_months than MSS patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a prognostic factor in colorectal cancer is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_amplified=1 have different mean pfs_months than HER2-negative.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a prognostic/predictive marker is established across multiple tumor types including CRC.

judgments.text: Patients with nras_mutation=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation conferring worse outcomes in colorectal cancer is established guideline knowledge alongside KRAS.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's PFS benefit versus best supportive care in refractory CRC is established from CORRECT trial and is guideline-standard.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with different mean pfs_months than untreated controls.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in RAS wild-type colorectal cancer is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab improving PFS in metastatic colorectal cancer is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS in MSI-high colorectal cancer (KEYNOTE-177) is established guideline-standard therapy.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's PFS benefit in colorectal cancer is established guideline knowledge, particularly in BRAF V600E-mutant disease.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib's PFS benefit is established guideline therapy for HER2-amplified colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage, sidedness, biomarkers, and lab covariates, regorafenib retains a positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib having a positive PFS effect after covariate adjustment reflects its established guideline role in refractory CRC.

judgments.text: After adjusting for prognostic covariates, cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib show no overall mean PFS effect.
judgments.rationale: That biomarker-targeted agents lack overall unselected PFS benefit and require biomarker selection is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_cetuximab is concentrated in pan-RAS/BRAF wild-type patients (effect ~0 in mutated patients).
judgments.rationale: Restriction of cetuximab benefit to RAS/BRAF wild-type tumors is a well-established guideline biomarker requirement.

judgments.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is concentrated in msi_high=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit concentrated in MSI-high patients is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_encorafenib is concentrated in braf_v600e=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit concentrated in BRAF V600E-mutant patients is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is concentrated in her2_amplified=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified patients is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: The PFS effect of treatment_regorafenib differs by kras_mutation status — benefit concentrated in KRAS-WT.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved as a non-biomarker-selected refractory agent, so a KRAS-WT-restricted benefit would not appear in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The PFS effect of treatment_regorafenib differs by braf_v600e status — benefit concentrated in BRAF-WT.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is not guideline-stratified by BRAF status, so a BRAF-WT-restricted benefit is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: The PFS effect of treatment_regorafenib differs by right_sided_primary — benefit concentrated in left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness is not an established predictor of regorafenib benefit in mCRC guidelines (it predicts anti-EGFR response, not regorafenib).

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect is preserved or larger in nras_mutation=1 vs nras_mutation=0 (NRAS does NOT suppress regorafenib benefit).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not identify NRAS-mutant status as preserving or enhancing regorafenib benefit; this is a specific subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect does not differ by msi_high status.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved/used irrespective of MSI status, so equal benefit by MSI is consistent with standard guideline use.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect does not differ by her2_amplified status.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib labeling and guideline use are HER2-agnostic, so no differential effect by HER2 is the standard expectation.

judgments.text: Stratified analysis: in KRAS-mutated patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS status and CORRECT showed benefit across RAS subgroups; claiming no benefit in KRAS-mutant contradicts guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: Stratified analysis: in right-sided primary patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness-conditional loss of regorafenib benefit is not an established guideline claim.

judgments.text: Stratified analysis: in BRAF V600E patients, regorafenib has no (or negative) PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not identify BRAF V600E as eliminating or reversing regorafenib benefit; this subgroup-specific claim goes beyond consensus.

judgments.text: Stratified analysis: in MSI-H patients, regorafenib retains its full benefit (+~0.95 mo).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is given without MSI selection, so retained benefit in MSI-H is consistent with standard practice.

judgments.text: Stratified analysis: in HER2-amplified patients, regorafenib retains its benefit.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib use is HER2-agnostic, so retained benefit in HER2-amplified patients reflects guideline-consistent expectation.

judgments.text: In the joint subgroup defined by left-sided AND KRAS-WT AND BRAF-WT, regorafenib produces a much larger PFS benefit than in any other subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A three-way joint biomarker subgroup (left-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT) defining a much larger regorafenib benefit is not described in any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: In the complement of (left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT) — i.e., right-sided OR KRAS-mut OR BRAF-mut — regorafenib has no PFS effect.
judgments.rationale: Defining the non-benefit population as the union of right-sided, KRAS-mut, or BRAF-mut and asserting null PFS effect there is a specific subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus, which only broadly establishes regorafenib in refractory mCRC without such conjunctive negative subgroup definitions.

judgments.text: All three predicates — left-sided primary, KRAS-WT, AND BRAF-WT — are simultaneously required: any one violator alone abolishes the regorafenib benefit.
judgments.rationale: The conjunctive requirement that all three of left-sided, KRAS-WT, and BRAF-WT must hold for regorafenib benefit — with any single violator abolishing it — is a speculative composite biomarker not in guidelines, which do not restrict regorafenib by sidedness or RAS/BRAF status.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup (left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT), NRAS mutation does not abolish — and may slightly augment — the regorafenib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming NRAS mutation does not abolish and may augment regorafenib benefit within a left/KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT subgroup is a cross-pathway subgroup-conditional mechanistic claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Systematic interaction screen: among all binary feature x treatment combinations, the only large interactions are regorafenib x {kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, nras_mutation}.
judgments.rationale: A systematic feature-by-treatment interaction screen identifying regorafenib's interactions with KRAS, BRAF, sidedness, and NRAS as the only large effects is a multi-comparison empirical claim not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab x right_sided_primary interaction is borderline negative.
judgments.rationale: A borderline-negative pembrolizumab x right-sided primary interaction is not an established guideline association — pembrolizumab's labeled CRC indication keys on MSI-H/dMMR, not sidedness.

judgments.text: Continuous modifier screen: only regorafenib shows a strong continuous-feature interaction; specifically, higher cea_ng_ml attenuates regorafenib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Identifying CEA as a continuous attenuator specifically of regorafenib benefit (and only regorafenib among the treatments) is a mechanistic/predictive claim not present in guidelines, which use CEA prognostically rather than as a regorafenib-specific effect modifier.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup (left + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT), higher cea_ng_ml strongly attenuates the regorafenib PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: A subgroup-conditional continuous interaction — CEA attenuating regorafenib PFS benefit specifically within left/KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT patients — is a layered speculative claim well beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Outside the regorafenib-benefit subgroup, the rego x CEA interaction is absent.
judgments.rationale: Asserting the rego x CEA interaction vanishes outside the left/KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT subgroup is a subgroup-conditional negative interaction claim not addressed in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup, sex_female augments the regorafenib effect (women benefit slightly more than men).
judgments.rationale: A sex-based augmentation of regorafenib benefit within a molecular/anatomic subgroup is not part of guideline consensus, which does not stratify regorafenib efficacy by sex.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup, stage_iv augments the regorafenib effect.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV augmenting regorafenib benefit specifically within the left/KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT subgroup is a subgroup-conditional staging interaction not described in guidelines (regorafenib is already used in metastatic disease without such conditional efficacy claims).

judgments.text: The regorafenib PFS benefit is robust across ECOG performance status strata (similar magnitude in ECOG 0, 1, 2).
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional claim about regorafenib efficacy by ECOG status is not a standard guideline statement.

judgments.text: Regorafenib x ECOG interaction is null.
judgments.rationale: Specific interaction null finding for regorafenib by ECOG is a dataset-specific analytic claim, not guideline content.

judgments.text: Three-way interaction model rego x kras_mut x braf_v600e confirms each mutation independently abolishes benefit.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction claim that KRAS and BRAF V600E each independently abolish regorafenib benefit goes beyond guideline consensus, which does not condition regorafenib on these mutations.

judgments.text: Pure cell counts: in left-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT, the regorafenib effect is approximately +2.76; in any cell with KRAS-mut, BRAF-mut, or right-sided, the effect is between -0.20 and +0.08 (NS).
judgments.rationale: Quantitative cell-level claim that regorafenib benefit is restricted to left-sided pan-WT tumors is not in any CRC guideline.

judgments.text: Cetuximab benefit is absent across CRC standard biomarker-defined subgroups (left+pan-WT, left-only, pan-WT-only) — no detectable PFS benefit anywhere.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no cetuximab benefit in left-sided pan-WT CRC contradicts the established guideline paradigm and is therefore a non-standard, novel claim.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab benefit is absent in MSI-H — contrary to the canonical CRC expectation, no PFS benefit detected in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: Absence of pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-H CRC contradicts the canonical guideline expectation and is a non-standard finding.

judgments.text: Encorafenib benefit is absent in BRAF V600E — no PFS benefit detected.
judgments.rationale: Absence of encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E CRC contradicts the established guideline-supported BEACON paradigm.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit is absent in HER2-amplified — no PFS benefit detected.
judgments.rationale: Absence of trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified CRC contradicts the emerging guideline-recognized paradigm.

judgments.text: Cetuximab + bevacizumab combination does not produce a multiplicative effect (interaction null).
judgments.rationale: A specific interaction null between cetuximab and bevacizumab is a mechanistic cross-pathway claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Bevacizumab x stage_iv interaction is null — bevacizumab has no main or stage-modified effect.
judgments.rationale: Claim that bevacizumab has no main or stage-IV-modified effect contradicts standard guideline use and is a non-standard analytic finding.

judgments.text: Final composite subgroup hypothesis (regorafenib): treatment_regorafenib produces a positive PFS effect ONLY in patients who are simultaneously left-sided primary (right_sided_primary=0), KRAS wild-type (kras_mutation=0), AND BRAF wild-type (braf_v600e=0); within this subgroup the mean PFS gain is approximately +2.76 months. Outside this subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Standard guideline use of regorafenib in metastatic CRC is in chemo-refractory disease without restriction to left-sided/KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT, so a benefit confined to this triple-wild-type left-sided subgroup is a non-guideline, speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib benefit subgroup, the effect is further modulated by cea_ng_ml: the benefit decreases by ~0.065 mo per 1 ng/mL CEA, so it is largest in low-CEA patients.
judgments.rationale: A continuous CEA-modulated gradient of regorafenib benefit within a defined molecular/anatomic subgroup is not part of established CRC treatment paradigms or guidelines.

judgments.text: Final null treatment hypotheses: cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib show no detectable PFS effect — overall, in any biomarker-defined subgroup, or in any straightforward interaction screen.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse cetuximab in RAS-WT left-sided CRC, bevacizumab broadly, pembrolizumab in MSI-H, encorafenib+cetuximab in BRAF V600E, and trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2-amplified disease, so a blanket null across all of these (including their established biomarker subgroups) contradicts paradigm consensus and is a novel claim.

judgments.text: Final prognostic summary: in this cohort, the strongest negative prognostic predictors of pfs_months are ecog_ps, stage_iv, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, weight_loss_pct_6mo, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l; the strongest positive is age_years (counterintuitive) and albumin_g_dl. msi_high and her2_amplified have no main effect on PFS in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: While ECOG, stage IV, hypoalbuminemia, weight loss, LDH, and BRAF are recognized poor prognostic factors, the counterintuitive positive association of older age with longer PFS and the null effect of MSI-high on PFS deviate from established CRC prognostic paradigms.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect of ECOG performance status on progression-free survival).
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a fundamental, guideline-level prognostic association across oncology.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring worse PFS than non-stage IV is the foundational basis of cancer staging and appears in every textbook.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (worse survival in older patients).
judgments.rationale: Older age as an adverse prognostic factor is a standard, widely recognized clinical association.

judgments.text: sex_female is associated with different pfs_months than sex_female=0.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in outcomes (e.g., in colorectal cancer) are well-documented in guidelines and epidemiologic literature.

judgments.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic and predictive marker (especially in colorectal cancer) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E conferring worse prognosis in colorectal and other cancers is standard guideline-level information.

judgments.text: right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse prognosis than left-sided in colorectal cancer is well-established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: msi_high=1, her2_amplified=1, ntrk_fusion=1, nras_mutation=1 each have a non-zero main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Main effects of MSI-high, HER2 amplification, NTRK fusion, and NRAS mutation on outcomes are all individually established biomarker associations in standard oncology practice.

judgments.text: Lower albumin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Hypoalbuminemia as a marker of worse prognosis is a classic, textbook-level prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA predicting worse outcomes in colorectal and other GI cancers is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is a textbook association across solid tumors.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l are each associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH, CRP, NLR, and alkaline phosphatase are well-established adverse prognostic biomarkers in guidelines and routine practice.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in metastatic colorectal cancer (RAS-wildtype) is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab improving PFS in mCRC is standard first/second-line guideline therapy.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in mCRC is established (KEYNOTE-177) and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) is guideline-endorsed therapy for BRAF V600E mCRC.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab+tucatinib is guideline-recognized for HER2-amplified mCRC (MOUNTAINEER).

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is a guideline-endorsed later-line option in refractory mCRC (CORRECT trial).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in msi_high=1 than in msi_high=0 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is the canonical established biomarker-treatment interaction (KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib benefit on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=1 than braf_v600e=0.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting encorafenib benefit is the canonical established biomarker-treatment interaction (BEACON CRC).

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib benefit on pfs_months is larger in her2_amplified=1 than her2_amplified=0.
judgments.rationale: Greater benefit of trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2-amplified disease is the established indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is reduced (or reversed) in kras_mutation=1 vs kras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Reduced/absent cetuximab benefit in KRAS-mutant colorectal cancer is a foundational, guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is reduced in nras_mutation=1, braf_v600e=1, or right_sided_primary=1.
judgments.rationale: Resistance to anti-EGFR therapy in NRAS-mutant, BRAF V600E, and right-sided CRC is well-established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab in the canonical cetuximab-sensitive subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0) is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in RAS/BRAF wild-type left-sided CRC is the canonical guideline-endorsed sensitive population.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is differential by kras_mutation status, with larger benefit in kras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS status with no guideline-level KRAS-based selection, so a differential KRAS effect would be novel.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is differential by braf_v600e status, with larger benefit in braf_v600e=0.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not stratify regorafenib benefit by BRAF V600E status, so a differential effect by BRAF would be novel.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is differential by right_sided_primary, with larger benefit in right_sided_primary=0.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not select regorafenib by primary tumor sidedness, so a sidedness-conditional benefit would be novel.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is differential by msi_high, her2_amplified, and ntrk_fusion.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib efficacy modification by MSI-high, HER2 amplification, or NTRK fusion is not a guideline-recognized predictor.

judgments.text: Patients who are kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS wild-type left-sided) experience greater treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months than the rest of the cohort.
judgments.rationale: A combined KRAS-wildtype/left-sided subgroup as a regorafenib-enriched population is not in guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: Adding braf_v600e=0 to the kras=0/left-sided subgroup further increases treatment_regorafenib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Layering BRAF wild-type onto a KRAS-wt/left-sided subgroup to enrich regorafenib benefit is a speculative cross-biomarker claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months persists across nras_mutation status when conditioning on kras_mutation=0 and right_sided_primary=0 and braf_v600e=0.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning regorafenib benefit on a specific multi-marker background (KRAS-/BRAF-/left-sided) and asking about NRAS-independence is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is reduced as cea_ng_ml increases (negative interaction with continuous CEA).
judgments.rationale: A continuous negative interaction between baseline CEA and regorafenib PFS benefit is not part of established CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is essentially zero when cea_ng_ml is in the upper half of the cohort distribution.
judgments.rationale: Claiming regorafenib benefit vanishes in the upper half of CEA distribution is a speculative cutoff-based effect modification not in standard paradigm.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib-responder background (kras=0, braf=0, right=0), the treatment effect is concentrated in patients with cea_ng_ml < 5 ng/mL (the clinical normal threshold).
judgments.rationale: A four-way conditional responder profile (KRAS-/BRAF-/left-sided/CEA<5) for regorafenib is a cross-pathway speculative claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients with cea_ng_ml >= 5 ng/mL but otherwise matching the responder profile (kras=0, braf=0, left-sided), the regorafenib treatment effect is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: Asserting null regorafenib benefit specifically in CEA≥5 left-sided RAS/BRAF-WT patients is a speculative subgroup claim beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Identify the threshold of cea_ng_ml above which treatment_regorafenib has no benefit.
judgments.rationale: Identifying a CEA threshold for regorafenib benefit is not a recognized clinical decision rule in CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: The pooled three-way interaction model treatment_regorafenib * kras_mutation * right_sided_primary captures most of the heterogeneity in regorafenib effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A three-way KRAS×sidedness×treatment interaction as the primary heterogeneity driver for regorafenib is a speculative mechanistic claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: When all four modifiers (kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml) are simultaneously included as treatment_regorafenib interactions, each retains an independent effect-modifying contribution.
judgments.rationale: Independent simultaneous effect modification by KRAS, BRAF, sidedness, and CEA on regorafenib is a multi-marker speculative claim beyond established paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect within the responder subgroup (kras=0, braf=0, right=0, cea<5) is robust to multivariable adjustment for prognostic confounders.
judgments.rationale: Robustness of a four-marker responder-subgroup regorafenib effect to confounder adjustment is a speculative subgroup-specific claim, not guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Adjusted for confounders within the responder subgroup, no other treatment (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab/tucatinib) has a non-null effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim that no other listed CRC therapy has any effect within this specific responder subgroup is a speculative cross-treatment comparison absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by ecog_ps, stage_iv, age_years, or sex_female (i.e., demographic/clinical heterogeneity).
judgments.rationale: Demographic/clinical effect modification of regorafenib (by ECOG, stage IV, age, sex) is not part of standard guideline-level knowledge for refractory CRC.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, and treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib effects on pfs_months are modified by ecog_ps, stage_iv, age_years, or sex_female.
judgments.rationale: Generic claim that demographic/clinical factors (ECOG, stage, age, sex) modify treatment effects is a well-established prognostic principle reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by continuous laboratory features (albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, etc.) other than cea_ng_ml.
judgments.rationale: Continuous lab biomarker (albumin, LDH, CRP, NLR, etc.) modification of regorafenib efficacy beyond CEA is not in current CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib-responder subgroup, no laboratory feature meaningfully further modifies the treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: A negative subgroup-conditional claim that no lab feature further modifies effect within regorafenib responders is a non-guideline mechanistic/statistical assertion.

judgments.text: Treatment-by-feature exhaustive scan reveals significant interactions ONLY for treatment_regorafenib (with kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml, nras_mutation).
judgments.rationale: The specific exhaustive-scan finding that only regorafenib shows interactions with KRAS, BRAF, sidedness, CEA, and NRAS is a novel cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab in the canonical pembrolizumab-eligible subgroup (msi_high=1) is associated with longer pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high colorectal cancer is established standard-of-care reflected in all guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib in braf_v600e=1 patients is associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) benefit in BRAF V600E CRC is guideline-endorsed standard therapy.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib in her2_amplified=1 patients is associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified CRC is now reflected in NCCN/ESMO guidelines as standard.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab in braf_v600e=1 patients is associated with shorter pfs_months (potential anti-targeted-therapy interaction).
judgments.rationale: A claim that bevacizumab is harmful (shorter PFS) in BRAF V600E CRC is not in guidelines and contradicts common practice of using bevacizumab-based regimens.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model with all features and all treatments simultaneously, the dominant predictors of pfs_months are age (positive), albumin (positive), ECOG, stage IV, KRAS mutation, BRAF V600E, right-sided primary, CEA, weight loss (all negative), and treatment_regorafenib (positive); other treatments are null.
judgments.rationale: The specific multivariable claim that regorafenib is the only treatment with a positive PFS effect (others null) alongside this exact predictor set is not a guideline-level statement.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect within the responder subgroup is independent of co-administered treatments (no synergy or antagonism).
judgments.rationale: Claiming regorafenib has no synergy/antagonism with co-administered drugs in a defined responder subgroup is a specific cross-treatment interaction claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: The unexpected positive age effect on pfs_months is not an artifact of treatment selection; it persists after adjusting for ECOG, stage, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
judgments.rationale: A positive effect of older age on PFS that survives extensive adjustment contradicts standard oncology teaching that older age is neutral or adverse, and would not appear in any guideline.

judgments.text: The age effect persists within the regorafenib-responder subgroup specifically.
judgments.rationale: A subgroup-conditional positive age effect specifically within regorafenib responders is a speculative interaction claim absent from textbooks.

judgments.text: nras_mutation has an independent effect on pfs_months beyond what is explained by kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary.
judgments.rationale: An independent NRAS prognostic effect on PFS beyond KRAS/BRAF/sidedness is not part of standard CRC guideline consensus, which typically lumps NRAS with KRAS for anti-EGFR decisions only.

judgments.text: nras_mutation positively modifies treatment_regorafenib effect (in the same direction as KRAS-wt does).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines treat NRAS mutation like KRAS mutation (a negative predictor for anti-EGFR therapy); claiming NRAS-mutant patients benefit from regorafenib similarly to RAS-wt patients is not established.

judgments.text: Among the four candidate effect-modifiers for treatment_regorafenib (kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml), each is necessary: removing any one and keeping the other three yields a partial-responder subgroup with attenuated benefit.
judgments.rationale: A four-way necessity claim that each of KRAS-wt, BRAF-wt, left-sided, and low CEA is individually required for regorafenib benefit is a highly specific multi-biomarker interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The complement of the regorafenib responder subgroup (any of kras_mutation=1, braf_v600e=1, right_sided_primary=1, cea_ng_ml>=5) shows essentially zero treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines position regorafenib as a refractory-line option regardless of RAS/BRAF/sidedness/CEA; claiming zero benefit outside a composite biomarker subgroup is novel.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib is associated with substantially longer pfs_months (approximately +5 months) in patients meeting all four criteria simultaneously: kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5. Outside this subgroup the effect is essentially zero. None of the other five treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab_tucatinib) shows a significant pfs_months effect in any subgroup tested, including their canonical biomarker-defined indication subgroups.
judgments.rationale: A composite four-biomarker predictive signature for regorafenib benefit (~+5 months PFS), combined with null effects for cetuximab/bevacizumab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab-tucatinib in their canonical indications, contradicts established CRC treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv = 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those with stage_iv = 0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than non-stage IV is a foundational staging-prognosis relationship in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope in OLS).
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is universally established in oncology textbooks and guidelines.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: Older age correlating with worse outcomes is a standard prognostic association, though modest and well-known.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than male.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based PFS differences (often favoring females in CRC) are documented in standard oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Right-sided primary (right_sided_primary = 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse prognosis than left-sided is an established CRC paradigm in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: kras_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation = 0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic and predictive marker in CRC is guideline-standard knowledge.

judgments.text: nras_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than nras_mutation = 0.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation predicting worse outcomes and anti-EGFR resistance is established guideline content in CRC.

judgments.text: braf_v600e = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e = 0.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E conferring poor prognosis in metastatic CRC is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: msi_high = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than msi_high = 0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status conferring better outcomes (especially with immunotherapy availability) is a textbook/guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: her2_amplified = 1 has different mean pfs_months than her2_amplified = 0.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a prognostic/predictive biomarker in CRC is now incorporated into NCCN guidelines for targeted therapy.

judgments.text: ntrk_fusion = 1 has different mean pfs_months than ntrk_fusion = 0.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusion as a predictive/prognostic biomarker linked to TRK inhibitor response is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_cetuximab = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_cetuximab = 0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in RAS wild-type colorectal cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_bevacizumab = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_bevacizumab = 0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's PFS benefit in metastatic colorectal cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_pembrolizumab = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab = 0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-H/dMMR colorectal cancer is guideline-endorsed standard knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_encorafenib = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_encorafenib = 0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) for BRAF V600E-mutant CRC is established guideline therapy.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib = 0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab + tucatinib for HER2-amplified CRC is now guideline-recognized standard knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_regorafenib = 1 have a different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_regorafenib = 0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib as later-line therapy in metastatic CRC with modest PFS effect is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: cea_ng_ml is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as a poor prognostic marker in CRC is a textbook association.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor is a well-established general oncology and CRC paradigm.

judgments.text: ldh_u_l is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is a textbook oncology association including in CRC.

judgments.text: weight_loss_pct_6mo is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia is a well-established negative prognostic factor for PFS across solid tumors and appears in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: crp_mg_l is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic marker is a standard, guideline-level association in oncology.

judgments.text: nlr is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio is a widely recognized prognostic biomarker for PFS in multiple cancers.

judgments.text: hemoglobin_g_dl is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as an adverse prognostic factor is established textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflects bone/liver metastatic burden and is a known prognostic factor.

judgments.text: ast_u_l is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: AST elevation reflects hepatic involvement/dysfunction and is a recognized prognostic marker.

judgments.text: alt_u_l is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: ALT elevation similarly reflects hepatic involvement and is a standard prognostic consideration.

judgments.text: total_bilirubin_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Elevated bilirubin reflects hepatic dysfunction and is a well-established adverse prognostic factor.

judgments.text: creatinine_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: Renal function via creatinine is a standard prognostic and treatment-tolerance factor in oncology.

judgments.text: bun_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: BUN as a marker of renal function/general physiologic status is a conventional prognostic association.

judgments.text: sodium_meq_l is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: A correlation between serum sodium and PFS in this cohort is not a guideline-established prognostic association in oncology.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: A correlation between serum potassium and PFS is not part of standard oncology prognostic paradigms.

judgments.text: calcium_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r != 0).
judgments.rationale: While hypercalcemia is a known poor prognostic sign in some cancers, a continuous calcium-PFS correlation across this cohort is not a guideline-level established association.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_cetuximab × kras_mutation=0 interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab works only in KRAS wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit being restricted to KRAS wild-type colorectal cancer is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months: pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in MSI-high patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit concentrated in MSI-high tumors is well-established and FDA-approved on that basis.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_encorafenib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months: encorafenib benefit is concentrated in BRAF V600E patients.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant tumors is the established indication and guideline standard.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months: benefit is concentrated in HER2-amplified patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified disease is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Cetuximab benefit further depends on right_sided_primary status: it is greater in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0) within KRAS wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: The further conditional claim that cetuximab benefit within KRAS wild-type is concentrated in left-sided primaries is a nuanced subgroup interaction beyond simple guideline statements, though it reflects emerging evidence.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab improves pfs_months overall (positive main effect after adjusting for stage_iv and ecog_ps).
judgments.rationale: A modest overall PFS benefit from bevacizumab adjusted for stage and performance status reflects established clinical use.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib has a different effect on pfs_months in stage_iv vs non-stage_iv patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: A differential regorafenib effect by stage IV vs non-stage IV status is not a guideline-established predictive interaction.

judgments.text: Cetuximab requires KRAS wild-type AND NRAS wild-type (RAS WT) for benefit; effect is null/negative in any RAS-mutant patient.
judgments.rationale: RAS wild-type (KRAS/NRAS) requirement for anti-EGFR cetuximab benefit in metastatic colorectal cancer is established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Cetuximab requires KRAS wild-type AND NRAS wild-type AND BRAF wild-type for benefit (full RAS/RAF WT subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Excluding BRAF V600E along with RAS mutations for cetuximab benefit is widely recognized in current CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In RAS/RAF wild-type patients, cetuximab benefit is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0); benefit is attenuated/absent in right-sided primaries.
judgments.rationale: Left-sided primary tumor location predicting superior cetuximab benefit in RAS/RAF wild-type mCRC is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: The full responder subgroup for cetuximab is: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided RAS/RAF WT).
judgments.rationale: The combined left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type responder profile for cetuximab is the standard guideline-endorsed selection criterion.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_cetuximab that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
judgments.rationale: Screening for any cetuximab interaction subgroup will most likely surface the well-known RAS/RAF/sidedness signal, which is not novel.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_bevacizumab that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
judgments.rationale: A specific binary biomarker subgroup with disproportionate bevacizumab PFS benefit is not established in guidelines, which treat bevacizumab as broadly applicable without predictive biomarkers.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_pembrolizumab that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
