judgments.rationale: Beyond MSI-H/dMMR or high TMB, novel binary-feature subgroups predicting pembrolizumab benefit in this dataset would go beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_encorafenib that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant colorectal cancer is the established guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified/positive disease is the established guideline-supported subgroup.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_×_feature interaction on pfs_months for treatment_regorafenib that identifies a subgroup with disproportionate benefit (screen across binary features).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib has no established predictive biomarker in guidelines, so identifying a subgroup with disproportionate benefit would be novel.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab benefit (in MSI-high patients) is modified by ecog_ps: patients with poorer performance status get less benefit.
judgments.rationale: Performance status modifying pembrolizumab benefit specifically within MSI-high patients is a subgroup-conditional claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Encorafenib benefit (in BRAF V600E patients) is modified by stage_iv: benefit is smaller/absent in stage IV.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV attenuating encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E disease is a subgroup-conditional effect modifier not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab+tucatinib benefit (in HER2-amplified patients) is modified by ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: ECOG PS modifying trastuzumab+tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified disease is a speculative subgroup interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In RAS/RAF WT left-sided patients, cetuximab benefit is further modified by stage_iv (smaller in stage IV).
judgments.rationale: A stage IV attenuation of cetuximab benefit specifically within RAS/RAF WT left-sided CRC is a finer subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: After joint modeling, the treatment_cetuximab × kras_mutation, × nras_mutation, × braf_v600e, and × right_sided_primary interactions remain jointly significant predictors of pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: KRAS/NRAS/BRAF mutations and right-sided primary are well-established negative predictors of cetuximab benefit in guidelines.

judgments.text: After joint modeling, the treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction remains significant after adjusting for other treatment×biomarker terms.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting pembrolizumab benefit is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After joint modeling, the treatment_encorafenib × braf_v600e interaction remains significant.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting encorafenib benefit is established guideline-level knowledge in CRC and melanoma.

judgments.text: After joint modeling, the treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × her2_amplified interaction remains significant.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification predicting trastuzumab+tucatinib benefit is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The complete cetuximab responder subgroup is: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided RAS/RAF WT). Within this subgroup, mean pfs_months is higher in cetuximab-treated patients.
judgments.rationale: Left-sided RAS/RAF WT defining the cetuximab-responsive subgroup is standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Removing any of these four predicates (KRAS WT, NRAS WT, BRAF WT, left-sided) substantially reduces cetuximab's PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: That each of KRAS WT, NRAS WT, BRAF WT, and left-sidedness is required for cetuximab benefit is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The complete pembrolizumab responder subgroup is msi_high=1; within MSI-H, pembrolizumab-treated patients have markedly higher pfs_months than untreated.
judgments.rationale: MSI-H predicting pembrolizumab response is a well-established guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: Within MSI-H, pembrolizumab benefit is modulated by stage_iv (smaller in stage IV) — refining the subgroup to msi_high=1 AND stage_iv=0 may show an even larger effect.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV-conditional attenuation of pembrolizumab benefit within MSI-H is a speculative subgroup refinement not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab benefit is strongest in MSI-H AND ecog_ps≤1 (excluding very poor PS).
judgments.rationale: ECOG ≤1 eligibility is standard practice for immunotherapy and not a novel biomarker-conditional claim.

judgments.text: The encorafenib responder subgroup is braf_v600e=1; within BRAF V600E, encorafenib-treated patients have higher pfs_months than untreated.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting encorafenib response is established CRC/melanoma guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within BRAF V600E, encorafenib benefit is further modulated by msi_high status (BRAF V600E + MSI-H may behave differently).
judgments.rationale: MSI-H modulation of encorafenib benefit within BRAF V600E is a cross-pathway interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The trastuzumab+tucatinib responder subgroup is her2_amplified=1; within HER2+, treated patients have higher pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification predicting trastuzumab+tucatinib benefit is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within HER2+, the benefit is similar regardless of RAS status (no further refinement needed).
judgments.rationale: Claim that RAS status does not modulate HER2-directed benefit contradicts emerging data and is a non-guideline cross-pathway assertion.

judgments.text: Bevacizumab benefit on pfs_months is broad and not concentrated in any single biomarker subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Absence of any biomarker-defined responder subgroup for bevacizumab is a meaningful negative claim beyond guideline statements.

judgments.text: Bevacizumab benefit may be larger in stage_iv patients (more vasculogenic disease).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV-specific enhancement of bevacizumab benefit is a speculative subgroup-conditional mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is broad with no clear biomarker-defined responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Explicit assertion that regorafenib lacks any biomarker-defined responder subgroup is a non-guideline negative claim.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect differs by performance status (ecog_ps).
judgments.rationale: Performance status as a prognostic/predictive modifier for regorafenib in refractory mCRC is well-established and reflected in guideline patient-selection criteria.

judgments.text: In a multivariable OLS, lower albumin_g_dl, higher ldh_u_l, higher crp_mg_l, higher nlr, higher cea_ng_ml, higher ecog_ps, and stage_iv=1 each independently shorten pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Each of these markers (albumin, LDH, CRP, NLR, CEA, ECOG, stage IV) is a textbook prognostic factor for shorter PFS/OS in advanced cancer.

judgments.text: Outside MSI-H, pembrolizumab is associated with shorter or no-better pfs_months (potential confounding/treatment in unselected patients yielding null/negative effect).
judgments.rationale: Restriction of pembrolizumab benefit to MSI-H/dMMR CRC is established guideline consensus; lack of benefit in MSS disease is standard knowledge.

judgments.text: Outside BRAF V600E, encorafenib is associated with no benefit on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's benefit being confined to BRAF V600E-mutant disease is a basic biomarker-restricted indication in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Outside HER2 amplification, trastuzumab+tucatinib has no benefit on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HER2-directed therapy (trastuzumab + tucatinib) requiring HER2 amplification/overexpression is standard guideline-level biomarker selection.

judgments.text: In KRAS-mutant or NRAS-mutant or BRAF-mutant patients, cetuximab has no PFS benefit (or negative effect).
judgments.rationale: Lack of anti-EGFR (cetuximab) benefit in RAS- or BRAF-mutant CRC is a foundational guideline rule.

judgments.text: Within MSI-H, lower albumin_g_dl reduces pembrolizumab benefit (interaction term negative if albumin enters multiplicatively with treatment).
judgments.rationale: A within-MSI-H interaction where low albumin specifically attenuates pembrolizumab benefit is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within MSI-H, higher crp_mg_l reduces pembrolizumab benefit.
judgments.rationale: CRP as a quantitative modifier of immunotherapy benefit within MSI-H CRC is a mechanistic/inflammatory-axis hypothesis beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within MSI-H, weight_loss_pct_6mo modifies pembrolizumab benefit.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as a treatment-effect modifier (not just prognostic) for pembrolizumab within MSI-H is a speculative interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within left-sided RAS/RAF WT, cetuximab benefit is unchanged by sex_female (no further refinement on sex).
judgments.rationale: An explicit null-interaction claim that sex does not further refine cetuximab benefit within left-sided RAS/RAF WT CRC is a non-standard, hypothesis-level statement absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within left-sided RAS/RAF WT, cetuximab benefit is unchanged by msi_high.
judgments.rationale: Restating that left-sided RAS/RAF WT defines cetuximab benefit irrespective of MSI status reflects standard guideline consensus, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: After adjusting for ecog_ps, stage_iv, age_years, sex_female, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, treatment_bevacizumab has a positive independent effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab improving PFS in metastatic colorectal cancer after adjustment for prognostic covariates is a well-established, guideline-level association.

judgments.text: After the same adjustment, treatment_regorafenib has a non-positive (null or negative) independent effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's modest/neutral PFS effect in unselected refractory mCRC is established in guidelines (CORRECT trial), so this is not novel.

judgments.text: In a joint OLS that includes all six treatments and their key biomarker matches, each targeted treatment's biomarker-matched interaction (cetuximab×KRAS WT proxy via -kras, pembrolizumab×msi_high, encorafenib×braf_v600e, trastuzumab+tucatinib×her2_amplified) is positive and the matching simple effects on the rest of the population are smaller.
judgments.rationale: Each of these biomarker-matched targeted therapy interactions (cetuximab/RAS-WT, pembrolizumab/MSI-H, encorafenib/BRAF V600E, trastuzumab+tucatinib/HER2-amplified) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Exhaustive search of small multi-feature subgroups confirms that left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type defines cetuximab's responder population.
judgments.rationale: Confirming left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type as cetuximab's responder population recapitulates current CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Exhaustive search confirms msi_high defines pembrolizumab's responder population, with no further enhancement from any other binary feature.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high as the defining responder population for pembrolizumab in CRC is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in a subgroup that includes kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type); within KRAS-mutant patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not restrict regorafenib benefit to KRAS wild-type; claiming no PFS benefit in KRAS-mutant patients is a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0); within right-sided primaries, regorafenib has no benefit (or harmful effect).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not condition regorafenib benefit on primary tumor sidedness, so concentrating benefit in left-sided disease is a novel subgroup hypothesis.

judgments.text: Regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in BRAF wild-type (braf_v600e=0); within BRAF V600E patients, regorafenib has no benefit.
judgments.rationale: Restricting regorafenib PFS benefit to BRAF wild-type is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker relationship and is therefore novel.

judgments.text: Final cetuximab responder subgroup: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0; treatment_cetuximab=1 increases pfs_months (positive direction).
judgments.rationale: Defining cetuximab responders as KRAS/NRAS/BRAF wild-type and left-sided is the established guideline-level selection criterion.

judgments.text: Final pembrolizumab responder subgroup: msi_high=1; treatment_pembrolizumab=1 increases pfs_months (positive direction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high colorectal cancer is established guideline-level knowledge (KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: Final encorafenib responder subgroup: braf_v600e=1; treatment_encorafenib=1 increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E colorectal cancer is established guideline standard (BEACON trial).

judgments.text: Final trastuzumab+tucatinib responder subgroup: her2_amplified=1; treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab plus tucatinib for HER2-amplified colorectal cancer is established (MOUNTAINEER trial, NCCN guidelines).

judgments.text: Final regorafenib responder subgroup: kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND braf_v600e=0 (left-sided KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT). Within this subgroup, treatment_regorafenib=1 increases pfs_months substantially (signed positive).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib benefit conditioned on left-sided, KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT subgroup is not a guideline-recognized predictive signature; regorafenib is approved regardless of these markers.

judgments.text: Within RAS WT (KRAS=0 & NRAS=0) AND BRAF WT AND left-sided, treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months (the most refined responder definition).
judgments.rationale: Refining regorafenib response to RAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided primary is a speculative subgroup claim not reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Removing the KRAS-WT predicate, the BRAF-WT predicate, OR the left-sided predicate from the regorafenib responder subgroup definition substantially reduces (or abolishes) the regorafenib treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming each of KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, and left-sidedness is individually necessary for regorafenib benefit is a novel mechanistic/subgroup-interaction claim.

judgments.text: Outside the responder subgroup defined in h24.1, treatment_cetuximab does NOT increase pfs_months (null or negative).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab lacking benefit outside left-sided RAS/BRAF-WT is established guideline knowledge for colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Outside MSI-H, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT increase pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab inefficacy outside MSI-H colorectal cancer is well-established and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Outside BRAF V600E, treatment_encorafenib does NOT increase pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's lack of benefit outside BRAF V600E is established and reflected in its labeled indication.

judgments.text: Outside HER2-amplified, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does NOT increase pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HER2-targeted therapy lacking benefit outside HER2-amplified disease is an established biomarker-driven principle.

judgments.text: Outside the regorafenib responder subgroup defined in h24.5 (i.e., right-sided OR KRAS-mutant OR BRAF-V600E), treatment_regorafenib has no PFS benefit (or negative effect).
judgments.rationale: The claim that regorafenib lacks PFS benefit specifically outside a right-sided/KRAS-mutant/BRAF-V600E subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional efficacy claim not established in CRC treatment guidelines, where regorafenib is approved for refractory mCRC regardless of these features.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
judgments.rationale: Age effects on PFS are well-established prognostic considerations covered in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between female (sex_female=1) and male (sex_female=0) patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in PFS are a standard demographic comparison routinely reported in trial subgroup analyses.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease being associated with shorter PFS is a foundational tenet of cancer staging covered in every guideline.

judgments.text: Worse performance status (higher ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is well-established in oncology guidelines and prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as an adverse prognostic marker is a standard tumor marker association in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is established in standard staging systems and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is a well-known textbook association.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic inflammatory marker is well-documented in standard prognostic scores like GPS.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as an adverse prognostic marker is widely established across oncology literature and reviews.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/lower hemoglobin as a poor prognostic factor is well-established in oncology guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflecting bone/liver metastases as a poor prognostic marker is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Other labs (ast, alt, bilirubin, creatinine, bun, electrolytes) have detectable univariate associations with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Routine LFTs, renal function, and electrolytes as prognostic markers in advanced cancer are well-documented in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Right-sided primary tumors (right_sided_primary=1) have shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse prognosis than left-sided is established consensus in metastatic colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic and predictive biomarker is standard guideline knowledge in colorectal and other cancers.

judgments.text: nras_mutation=1 is associated with different pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation status as a predictive biomarker for anti-EGFR therapy is established in CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E mutation as a poor prognostic marker is well-established consensus in colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: msi_high=1 is associated with different pfs_months than microsatellite-stable.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a prognostic and predictive biomarker (especially for immunotherapy) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: her2_amplified=1 is associated with different pfs_months than non-amplified.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a predictive biomarker is established in breast, gastric, and increasingly colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with different pfs_months than non-fusion.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions as actionable targets with TRK inhibitors are established in tumor-agnostic treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Marginally, treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months (positive main effect).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab's PFS benefit in mCRC is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Marginally, treatment_bevacizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's PFS benefit in mCRC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Marginally, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's PFS benefit (especially in MSI-H mCRC) is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Marginally, treatment_encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's PFS benefit in BRAF V600E mCRC is established standard-of-care.

judgments.text: Marginally, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib for HER2+ mCRC is a guideline-recognized regimen.

judgments.text: Marginally, treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's modest PFS benefit in refractory mCRC is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: After mutually adjusting for all features, the strongest predictors of pfs_months are ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), age_years (positive), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), kras_mutation (negative), right_sided_primary (negative), braf_v600e (negative), cea_ng_ml (negative), and ldh_u_l (negative). Other lab values have negligible adjusted effects.
judgments.rationale: ECOG, stage, albumin, weight loss, KRAS, right-sidedness, BRAF, CEA, and LDH are all canonical mCRC prognostic factors.

judgments.text: After adjusting for prognostic features, treatment_regorafenib retains a substantial positive main effect on pfs_months, while no other treatment shows a non-zero adjusted main effect.
judgments.rationale: The claim that only regorafenib retains an adjusted treatment effect while cetuximab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib do not contradicts guideline expectations and would not appear in a textbook.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type) patients but not in kras_mutation=1 (negative interaction with KRAS mutation).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy restricted to KRAS wild-type is the textbook predictive biomarker finding in mCRC.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in braf_v600e=0 patients but not in braf_v600e=1 (negative interaction with BRAF mutation).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting poor response to anti-EGFR monotherapy is established in mCRC guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in msi_high=1 patients (positive interaction with MSI-high).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is established standard-of-care across multiple guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in braf_v600e=1 patients (positive interaction with BRAF V600E).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant disease is the established indication in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in her2_amplified=1 patients (positive interaction with HER2 amplification).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib activity is anchored to HER2 amplification/overexpression in standard treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type), with little or no benefit in kras_mutation=1 (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines position regorafenib as a refractory-line agent without KRAS-status restriction, so a KRAS wild-type-restricted benefit goes beyond consensus.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in braf_v600e=0 (BRAF wild-type), with little or no benefit in braf_v600e=1 (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is not guideline-stratified by BRAF V600E status, so a BRAF wild-type-only benefit is non-standard.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0), with little or no benefit in right-sided (negative interaction with right-sided).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness predicts EGFR-antibody benefit in guidelines, but regorafenib is not stratified by left- vs right-sided primary.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is concentrated in patients with low cea_ng_ml (negative interaction with CEA above the median).
judgments.rationale: CEA-level-conditional efficacy of regorafenib is not part of guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: Comprehensive interaction screen: for every non-regorafenib treatment, no biomarker, sidedness, ECOG, age, or lab marker produces a clinically meaningful interaction with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A blanket null-interaction claim across all non-regorafenib agents contradicts established predictive biomarker effects and is a non-standard cross-pathway assertion.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is jointly defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided): in patients meeting all three criteria, regorafenib substantially increases PFS, while in patients failing any criterion, regorafenib has no detectable effect.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conjunctive predictor (KRAS WT AND BRAF WT AND left-sided) for regorafenib benefit is not in any guideline.

judgments.text: The full pfs_months benefit of treatment_regorafenib is defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml below ~5 ng/mL: in patients meeting all four criteria, regorafenib increases mean PFS by ~5 months; in patients failing any criterion, regorafenib has no clinically meaningful effect.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conjunctive selection rule including a CEA<5 threshold for regorafenib benefit is a speculative composite signature absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Across the 6 listed treatments, only treatment_regorafenib has a non-trivial effect on pfs_months, and that effect is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously kras_mutation=0, braf_v600e=0, right_sided_primary=0, and cea_ng_ml<5; cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib show no meaningful PFS benefit in this cohort, even within their canonical biomarker-defined indications.
judgments.rationale: Claiming regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided, low-CEA patients while denying benefit from cetuximab/bevacizumab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab-tucatinib even in their guideline-defined biomarker subgroups is a speculative, subgroup-conditional claim that contradicts standard CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with PFS is a routine demographic covariate analysis without novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with different mean pfs_months than male.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based PFS differences are a standard demographic comparison, not a guideline-changing novel claim.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG predicting shorter PFS is a well-established prognostic association in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV being associated with shorter PFS is foundational staging knowledge taught in textbooks.

judgments.text: right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided colorectal primary predicting worse outcomes is established consensus reflected in current CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS-wt.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive marker in CRC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: nras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than NRAS-wt.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation conferring worse prognosis and anti-EGFR resistance is established guideline content.

judgments.text: braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF-wt.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a poor prognostic marker in CRC is well-established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: msi_high=1 is associated with different pfs_months than MSS.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high prognostic/predictive significance in CRC is standard textbook and guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: her2_amplified=1 is associated with different pfs_months than HER2-neg.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification's prognostic/predictive role is well-established in guideline-directed oncology care.

judgments.text: ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with different pfs_months than non-fusion.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions are an established actionable alteration with known prognostic and predictive implications widely covered in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Albumin is a well-established prognostic biomarker associated with better outcomes across virtually all cancers.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia is a textbook adverse prognostic factor across solid tumors.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA is a standard adverse prognostic marker in CEA-expressing cancers, especially colorectal.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH is a classical adverse prognostic biomarker incorporated into staging systems (e.g., melanoma AJCC).

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP is a well-known systemic inflammation marker associated with worse cancer prognosis.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio is a widely established adverse prognostic marker across solid tumors.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit (in appropriate biomarker-selected populations) is standard guideline knowledge for CRC and H&N cancers.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab providing PFS benefit is established across multiple tumor types in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is standard guideline knowledge across many indications.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) is established standard for BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic colorectal cancer per guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab plus tucatinib is a guideline-recognized regimen for HER2-positive metastatic colorectal and breast cancers.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is an established later-line therapy with documented PFS benefit in metastatic colorectal cancer per guidelines.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs by kras_mutation status (cetuximab benefit larger in KRAS-wt).
judgments.rationale: KRAS wild-type status as a predictor of cetuximab benefit is a well-established guideline-level biomarker.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs by nras_mutation status (cetuximab benefit larger in NRAS-wt).
judgments.rationale: Extended RAS testing including NRAS to predict cetuximab benefit is standard guideline practice.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs by braf_v600e status (cetuximab benefit larger in BRAF-wt).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E mutation as a negative predictor of anti-EGFR benefit is well-established in CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs by right_sided_primary status (cetuximab benefit larger in left-sided).
judgments.rationale: Left-sided primary tumor location predicting greater cetuximab benefit is established in CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months within RAS/BRAF-wt patients (kras=0 & nras=0 & braf=0).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in RAS/BRAF wild-type metastatic CRC is the standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months within RAS/BRAF-wt left-sided patients.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in RAS/BRAF wild-type left-sided CRC is the guideline-preferred first-line population.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs by msi_high (benefit larger in MSI-H).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's enhanced efficacy in MSI-high tumors is a well-established, guideline-endorsed biomarker association.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months within msi_high=1 subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high colorectal cancer is established and guideline-endorsed (KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months differs by braf_v600e (benefit larger in BRAF-V600E).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's enhanced benefit in BRAF-V600E-mutant CRC is the basis for its approval (BEACON trial) and is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months within braf_v600e=1 subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) is guideline-recommended for BRAF-V600E-mutant metastatic CRC.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months differs by her2_amplified (benefit larger in HER2-amp).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit restricted to HER2-amplified tumors is established (MOUNTAINEER) and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months within her2_amplified=1 subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Tucatinib + trastuzumab is guideline-recommended for HER2-amplified metastatic CRC.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months within HER2-amp & RAS-wt subgroup.
judgments.rationale: MOUNTAINEER and current NCCN guidance specifically restrict tucatinib/trastuzumab to HER2-amplified, RAS wild-type CRC, so this is standard.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs by stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: Differential bevacizumab benefit specifically by stage IV vs earlier stage is not a standard guideline-level claim and would be a subgroup-conditional finding.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs by braf_v600e.
judgments.rationale: BRAF-V600E status as a modifier of bevacizumab efficacy is not established in guidelines and would be a novel interaction claim.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab is associated with longer pfs_months within stage_iv=1 subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is standardly used in stage IV/metastatic CRC, so benefit in this subgroup is guideline-consistent.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is larger in KRAS-wt than KRAS-mutant (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS status, and a KRAS-wt-specific enhanced benefit is not part of standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months in kras_mutation=0 subgroup; effect is absent in kras_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved in refractory mCRC regardless of KRAS status; a KRAS-wt-only benefit is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is larger in left-sided primaries than right-sided (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness is predictive for anti-EGFR therapy, not regorafenib; differential effect by side is not guideline-supported.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months in right_sided_primary=0; effect absent in right_sided_primary=1.
judgments.rationale: Left-sided-only benefit of regorafenib is not an established guideline claim.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is larger in BRAF-wt than BRAF-V600E (interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E is prognostic but not an established regorafenib effect modifier in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months in braf_v600e=0; effect absent in braf_v600e=1.
judgments.rationale: Restricting regorafenib benefit to BRAF-wt is not in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs by nras_mutation.
judgments.rationale: NRAS as a regorafenib effect modifier is not established in oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months in NRAS-mutant patients (and in NRAS-wt).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib working in both NRAS subgroups aligns with the standard view that it is approved irrespective of RAS status.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup defined by kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND braf_v600e=0 (favorable), treatment_regorafenib substantially increases pfs_months; outside this subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A three-way KRAS/sidedness/BRAF interaction defining regorafenib responders is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, age_years independently predicts longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Age as an independent prognostic factor for PFS is textbook-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjustment, ecog_ps independently predicts shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is one of the most established prognostic factors in oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: After adjustment, stage_iv independently predicts shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease predicting shorter PFS is a foundational prognostic fact in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, albumin_g_dl independently predicts longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as an adverse prognostic marker is well-established in oncology and standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: After adjustment for all features, treatment_regorafenib independently predicts longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's modest PFS benefit in refractory mCRC is established from CORRECT/CONCUR trials and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, alkaline_phosphatase, AST, ALT, sodium, potassium, calcium, creatinine, BUN, total_bilirubin are not independent predictors of pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Routine LFTs/electrolytes/renal labs not being independent PFS predictors after adjustment is consistent with standard prognostic understanding.

judgments.text: Within the favorable molecular/sidedness subgroup, the regorafenib pfs_months benefit decreases as cea_ng_ml rises.
judgments.rationale: A CEA-dependent attenuation of regorafenib benefit specifically within RAS/BRAF-wildtype left-sided mCRC is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: Outside the favorable subgroup, cea_ng_ml does NOT modify the (already null) regorafenib effect.
judgments.rationale: The subgroup-conditional absence of CEA effect modification on regorafenib is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, regorafenib increases pfs_months substantially when cea_ng_ml <= 5 ng/mL.
judgments.rationale: Restricting substantial regorafenib benefit to RAS/BRAF-wildtype, left-sided patients with low CEA is a novel multi-feature predictive claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup with cea_ng_ml > 5, regorafenib does NOT increase pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Loss of regorafenib benefit at CEA >5 within the favorable molecular subgroup is a novel threshold-based predictive claim.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the regorafenib effect is essentially null in the highest CEA quartile (Q4: cea_ng_ml > ~10 ng/mL).
judgments.rationale: A null regorafenib effect specifically in the highest CEA quartile within the favorable subgroup is a novel biomarker-stratified finding.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib substantially increases pfs_months only in the subgroup defined by ALL of: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml <= 5 ng/mL.
judgments.rationale: A composite five-feature predictive signature (RAS/BRAF wildtype, left-sided, CEA ≤5) for regorafenib benefit is well beyond current guideline-level evidence.

judgments.text: Outside that subgroup (any unfavorable predicate present), treatment_regorafenib does not increase pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Stating that regorafenib does not improve PFS outside a favorable subgroup restates its known modest, broadly applied benefit in refractory mCRC without offering a guideline-divergent claim.

judgments.text: Across all binary features (sex, stage, sidedness, KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/MSI/HER2/NTRK), no feature significantly modifies the cetuximab pfs_months effect at p<0.05 after a full screen.
judgments.rationale: A null finding that no standard binary biomarker modifies cetuximab effect in a full screen aligns with—not beyond—the established RAS/BRAF-centric paradigm and is not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Within the RAS/BRAF-wt cetuximab-eligible subgroup, no continuous feature (age, ECOG, CEA, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin) modifies cetuximab effect at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that within RAS/BRAF-wt patients no continuous clinical/lab feature (e.g., CEA, LDH, NLR, albumin) modifies cetuximab benefit goes beyond guideline content, which does not address these continuous modifiers.

judgments.text: No binary feature significantly modifies the pembrolizumab pfs_months effect (full screen across sex/stage/sidedness/KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/MSI/HER2/NTRK) in a way that yields a meaningful (>0.2 mo) subgroup benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines already restrict pembrolizumab benefit in CRC to MSI-H/dMMR; a screen finding no meaningful binary modifier is consistent with established paradigm rather than novel.

judgments.text: No binary feature significantly modifies the encorafenib pfs_months effect at p<0.05 in a full screen.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib in CRC is guideline-indicated specifically for BRAF V600E with cetuximab; a claim that no binary feature (including BRAF) modifies its PFS effect contradicts/extends the guideline framework and is non-standard.

judgments.text: No binary feature significantly modifies the trastuzumab/tucatinib pfs_months effect in a full screen.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib is guideline-directed for HER2-amplified CRC; claiming no binary feature (including HER2) modifies its effect departs from established subgroup-conditional paradigm.

judgments.text: No binary feature meaningfully modifies the bevacizumab pfs_months effect (only marginal BRAF interaction with very small absolute effect).
judgments.rationale: A specific subgroup-conditional claim that only a marginal BRAF interaction modifies bevacizumab PFS effect is not part of guideline consensus, which treats bevacizumab benefit as biomarker-agnostic.

judgments.text: FINAL: The only treatment with a clinically meaningful pfs_months benefit in this cohort is treatment_regorafenib, and its benefit is concentrated in patients meeting ALL of: kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml <= 5 ng/mL. Within that subgroup the mean PFS gain is ~5 months; outside it the mean PFS gain is statistically and clinically null.
judgments.rationale: A multi-feature conjunctive predictor (RAS/BRAF-wt AND left-sided AND CEA≤5) selecting a ~5-month regorafenib PFS gain is a cross-pathway, subgroup-conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, and treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib show no clinically meaningful PFS benefit overall or within any biomarker- or feature-defined subgroup tested in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Asserting null benefit for cetuximab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and HER2-directed therapy across all tested biomarker subgroups directly contradicts established guideline-endorsed benefits in RAS/BRAF-wt, MSI-H, BRAF V600E, and HER2+ subsets.

judgments.text: FINAL prognostic drivers of pfs_months (independent of treatment): older age (longer PFS, +0.18 mo/yr adjusted), higher ECOG (shorter PFS), stage IV (shorter PFS), right-sided primary (shorter PFS), KRAS or BRAF mutation (shorter PFS), greater weight loss (shorter PFS), higher CEA (shorter PFS), higher LDH (shorter PFS), lower albumin (shorter PFS).
judgments.rationale: The listed prognostic factors (ECOG, stage, sidedness, RAS/BRAF, weight loss, CEA, LDH, albumin, age) are textbook prognostic variables in mCRC and not novel.

judgments.text: Patient age (age_years) is associated with progression-free survival (pfs_months) in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic factor for PFS is a standard clinical consideration, not novel.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have different mean pfs_months than male patients (sex_female=0).
judgments.rationale: Sex as a prognostic variable in oncology is a standard demographic comparison, not novel.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps 0 vs 1 vs 2) is associated with shorter pfs_months — ECOG 2 patients have lower mean PFS than ECOG 0.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a well-established prognostic factor in all oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Stage IV patients (stage_iv=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than non-stage-IV patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than earlier stages is foundational oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: Right-sided primary tumors (right_sided_primary=1) are associated with shorter mean pfs_months than left-sided tumors.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse prognosis is established consensus in colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: KRAS-mutant patients (kras_mutation=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than KRAS-wildtype.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive marker is standard guideline knowledge in colorectal and lung cancer.

judgments.text: NRAS-mutant patients (nras_mutation=1) have different mean pfs_months than NRAS-wildtype.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation status is a standard biomarker assessed in colorectal cancer treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: BRAF V600E-mutant patients (braf_v600e=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than BRAF-wildtype.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E mutation as a poor prognostic marker is established in colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) have different mean pfs_months than MSS patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status is a well-established prognostic and predictive biomarker in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1) have different mean pfs_months than non-amplified.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a prognostic/predictive biomarker is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: NTRK-fusion patients (ntrk_fusion=1) have different mean pfs_months than NTRK-fusion-negative.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions are an established actionable target with TRK inhibitors and known prognostic/predictive significance, covered in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher CEA (cea_ng_ml) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as a marker of higher tumor burden and worse prognosis is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker (reflecting nutritional/inflammatory status) is well-established in oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook association across many solid tumors.

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss as adverse prognostic factors are standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP reflecting systemic inflammation as a poor prognostic marker is well-established in oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as an adverse prognostic biomarker is widely recognized and appears in standard prognostic discussions.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia as a poor prognostic factor (and higher hemoglobin as favorable) is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline phosphatase is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase often reflects bone/liver metastases and is a known adverse prognostic marker.

judgments.text: Higher AST is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST often reflects liver involvement/metastases and is a recognized adverse prognostic indicator.

judgments.text: Higher ALT is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT reflects hepatic dysfunction broadly associated with worse cancer outcomes, a well-established prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher total bilirubin is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Hyperbilirubinemia indicating liver dysfunction is a standard adverse prognostic factor across oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Renal dysfunction reflected by creatinine is a recognized adverse prognostic marker in standard oncology practice.

judgments.text: Higher BUN is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflecting renal impairment or catabolic state is a known general prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Sodium is associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Hyponatremia is a well-established adverse prognostic marker in many solid tumors per standard oncology references.

judgments.text: Potassium is associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Potassium abnormalities reflecting general illness severity are commonly recognized prognostic correlates.

judgments.text: Calcium is associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Hypercalcemia of malignancy is a textbook adverse prognostic indicator across multiple cancers.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_cetuximab=0 (overall, ignoring biomarker status).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy on PFS is a guideline-level established treatment effect in approved indications.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_bevacizumab=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab improving PFS is a well-established guideline finding across multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is a standard guideline-level established treatment effect.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_encorafenib=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's PFS benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant colorectal cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib activity in HER2-amplified colorectal cancer is well-established and guideline-supported.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_regorafenib=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's modest PFS benefit in refractory mCRC is established from CORRECT trial and standard guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves mean pfs_months in RAS/BRAF wildtype patients (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0); cetuximab patients have higher PFS than non-cetuximab patients within this subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit restricted to RAS/BRAF wildtype mCRC is core guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves mean pfs_months in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in left-sided primary mCRC is established guideline-level knowledge from CALGB/SWOG 80405 and similar studies.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab is harmful or neutral in KRAS-mutant patients (kras_mutation=1).
judgments.rationale: Lack of cetuximab benefit (or harm) in KRAS-mutant mCRC is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_cetuximab and right_sided_primary on pfs_months (cetuximab benefit differs by sidedness).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness-by-cetuximab interaction is established in current mCRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_cetuximab and kras_mutation on pfs_months (cetuximab benefit reduced in KRAS mutant).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation negating cetuximab benefit is a textbook predictive biomarker interaction.

judgments.text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_cetuximab and braf_v600e on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E reducing anti-EGFR monotherapy benefit is established guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves mean pfs_months in MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) vs no pembrolizumab in the same subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high mCRC is established first-line guideline standard from KEYNOTE-177.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high colorectal cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in right-sided tumors (right_sided_primary=1) more than in left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Differential pembrolizumab benefit by primary tumor sidedness within MSI-agnostic populations is not standard guideline content.

judgments.text: There is an interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and braf_v600e on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy modification specifically by BRAF V600E status is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves mean pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients (braf_v600e=1) vs no encorafenib in the same subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant colorectal cancer is established guideline knowledge (BEACON trial).

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_encorafenib and braf_v600e on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: The biomarker-treatment interaction of encorafenib with BRAF V600E is the established mechanistic basis for its guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within BRAF V600E mutant patients, the combination of treatment_encorafenib and treatment_cetuximab produces additional pfs_months benefit beyond either alone (positive 3-way interaction encor × cetux × braf).
judgments.rationale: The encorafenib + cetuximab combination in BRAF V600E mCRC is the guideline-endorsed regimen from BEACON.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves mean pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1) vs no trast+tuc in the same subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab + tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified colorectal cancer is established guideline knowledge (MOUNTAINEER).

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib and her2_amplified on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: The HER2-amplification biomarker interaction underlies the guideline indication for trastuzumab + tucatinib.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves mean pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients who are also RAS/BRAF wildtype (her2_amplified=1 AND kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0).
judgments.rationale: RAS/BRAF wildtype restriction for HER2-targeted therapy in mCRC is the standard guideline-eligible subgroup.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in patients with ECOG 0 (ecog_ps=0) vs no regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib benefit specifically conditional on ECOG 0 performance status is not an established guideline-level predictive claim.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in patients with ECOG 1 (ecog_ps=1) vs no regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib benefit in good-performance-status (ECOG 0-1) refractory mCRC patients is the standard guideline indication from CORRECT trial.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in patients with ECOG 2 (ecog_ps=2) vs no regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is generally restricted to ECOG 0-1 in guidelines due to toxicity; a PFS benefit specifically in ECOG 2 patients is not established and would be a non-standard subgroup claim.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in stage IV patients (stage_iv=1) vs no regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved/used in metastatic (stage IV) refractory CRC, so PFS benefit in stage IV is the on-label, guideline-consistent expectation.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × ecog_ps interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A formal regorafenib × ECOG interaction effect on PFS is a mechanistic/subgroup-conditional claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab improves mean pfs_months in right-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=1).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines and CALGB/SWOG 80405 suggest right-sided mCRC tends to favor bevacizumab over anti-EGFR for OS, but a specific PFS benefit of bevacizumab restricted to right-sided tumors is not a standard guideline statement.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab improves mean pfs_months in KRAS-mutant patients (kras_mutation=1).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is used regardless of KRAS status; a KRAS-mutant-specific PFS benefit is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab improves mean pfs_months in stage IV patients (stage_iv=1).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab improving PFS in stage IV (metastatic) CRC is the established guideline indication from pivotal trials.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, sidedness, biomarkers, key labs, and other treatments, treatment_regorafenib remains independently associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Independent association of regorafenib with longer PFS after multivariable adjustment recapitulates the established trial-based efficacy signal.

judgments.text: After adjustment, age_years is independently associated with pfs_months (signed effect: positive).
judgments.rationale: A signed, independent positive association of older age with longer PFS after adjustment contradicts typical prognostic expectations and is not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: After adjustment, ecog_ps is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status being independently associated with shorter PFS is a well-established prognostic finding reflected in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: After adjustment, stage_iv is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease portending shorter PFS is a foundational prognostic principle in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: After adjustment, kras_mutation is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as an adverse prognostic/predictive marker (especially in colorectal cancer) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjustment, braf_v600e is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E conferring poor prognosis in colorectal and other cancers is well-established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, albumin_g_dl is independently associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is textbook oncology and standard in prognostic indices.

judgments.text: After adjustment, cea_ng_ml is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA correlating with worse outcomes is a long-established tumor marker principle in GI oncology.

judgments.text: After adjustment, weight_loss_pct_6mo is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is foundational clinical oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_cetuximab has no main effect on pfs_months (coefficient ~0).
judgments.rationale: Claiming cetuximab has no independent PFS effect after adjustment contradicts guideline-endorsed benefit in RAS-wildtype CRC and would be a notable subgroup-conditional finding.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has no main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null main effect for pembrolizumab after adjustment is non-standard given established benefit in MSI-H/dMMR and other biomarker-defined populations, suggesting effect modification beyond guideline simplifications.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_encorafenib has no main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib showing no main PFS effect after adjustment is non-trivial given its guideline-endorsed activity in BRAF V600E CRC/melanoma and implies the benefit is conditional on covariates.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has no main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null main effect for trastuzumab-tucatinib contradicts guideline-supported efficacy in HER2-positive disease and implies a subgroup-conditional rather than universal effect.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_bevacizumab has no main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab having a modest or no main effect after adjustment in mCRC is consistent with guideline-level understanding that its benefit is small and biomarker-agnostic.

judgments.text: A composite risk score combining low albumin, high LDH, high NLR, high CRP, and weight loss is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A specific composite of albumin, LDH, NLR, CRP, and weight loss as a unified prognostic score is not part of standard mCRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months — KRAS mutation suppresses regorafenib benefit (negative interaction coefficient).
judgments.rationale: A KRAS-mutation-driven suppression of regorafenib benefit is not an established guideline biomarker interaction for regorafenib, which is approved without biomarker selection.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months — right-sided primary suppresses regorafenib benefit (negative interaction coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness as a negative predictor of regorafenib benefit is not part of guideline recommendations, which use regorafenib regardless of primary tumor side.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months — BRAF V600E mutation suppresses regorafenib benefit (negative interaction coefficient).
judgments.rationale: A BRAF V600E-specific suppression of regorafenib benefit is not an established guideline biomarker interaction for regorafenib.

judgments.text: Interactions of cetuximab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab+tucatinib with their canonical biomarkers are NOT statistically significant on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Stating that canonical biomarker interactions are not significant contradicts well-established guideline associations and is essentially a negative restatement of standard paradigms.

judgments.text: Refined: there is a (canonical-direction) interaction between cetuximab × kras_mutation on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation predicting lack of cetuximab benefit is a textbook, guideline-level biomarker association in mCRC.

judgments.text: Refined: there is a (canonical-direction) interaction between pembrolizumab × msi_high on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is a textbook, guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: Refined: there is a (canonical-direction) interaction between encorafenib × braf_v600e on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting encorafenib benefit is a guideline-endorsed biomarker association in mCRC.

judgments.text: Refined: there is a (canonical-direction) interaction between trast+tuc × her2_amplified on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification predicting trastuzumab+tucatinib benefit is a guideline-endorsed biomarker association in mCRC.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves mean pfs_months in the most-favorable subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in RAS/BRAF wild-type left-sided mCRC is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab does NOT improve mean pfs_months in (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=1).
judgments.rationale: Lack of cetuximab benefit in right-sided RAS/BRAF wild-type mCRC is well-established and reflected in NCCN/ESMO guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves mean pfs_months in MSI-high left-sided patients (msi_high=1 AND right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Restricting pembrolizumab benefit to MSI-high left-sided tumors is a sidedness-conditional refinement not in guidelines, which recommend pembrolizumab for all MSI-H mCRC.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves mean pfs_months in MSI-high BRAF-wildtype patients (msi_high=1 AND braf_v600e=0).
judgments.rationale: Conditioning pembrolizumab benefit on BRAF wild-type within MSI-H is a subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus, which endorses pembrolizumab regardless of BRAF status.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves mean pfs_months in MSI-high ECOG≤1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-H mCRC with good performance status is consistent with the standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves mean pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients receiving cetuximab (braf_v600e=1 AND treatment_cetuximab=1).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib plus cetuximab in BRAF V600E-mutant mCRC is the established BEACON-based standard of care.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves mean pfs_months in BRAF V600E mutants without cetuximab.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit without cetuximab in BRAF V600E mCRC contradicts guideline consensus that the cetuximab combination is required.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves mean pfs_months in HER2-amplified left-sided patients (her2_amplified=1 AND right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Restricting trastuzumab-tucatinib benefit to left-sided HER2-amplified mCRC is a sidedness-conditional claim beyond current HER2-targeted guideline indications.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves mean pfs_months in HER2-amplified ECOG≤1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib in HER2-amplified mCRC with good performance status reflects the established MOUNTAINEER-based indication.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in ECOG 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Restricting regorafenib benefit specifically to ECOG 0 patients is a narrower performance-status-conditional claim than guideline language, which permits ECOG 0–1.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in ECOG 0 patients with albumin ≥ 3.5.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is a guideline-endorsed later-line option in mCRC and good performance status/adequate organ function (e.g., albumin) is standard selection criteria, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in ECOG ≤ 1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is indicated for refractory mCRC patients with ECOG 0-1, which is standard guideline language.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months in ECOG ≥ 1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming benefit specifically in ECOG ≥1 (including poor performance status) contradicts the standard restriction to good PS and would not appear in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among NTRK-fusion-positive patients (ntrk_fusion=1), treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is a multikinase inhibitor not selected by NTRK fusion status; a fusion-conditional benefit claim is not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Among NTRK-fusion-positive patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not change mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A subgroup-conditional null for pembrolizumab specifically in NTRK-fusion-positive patients is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a three-way interaction treatment_cetuximab × kras_mutation × right_sided_primary on pfs_months (cetuximab benefit varies by KRAS and side jointly).
judgments.rationale: A three-way KRAS × sidedness × cetuximab interaction goes beyond the standard guideline rule of RAS-wildtype + left-sided eligibility into a joint-modifier mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab main effect at the reference (KRAS=0, left-sided) is not significantly different from zero.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in RAS-wildtype left-sided mCRC is established; a non-zero main effect at that reference is consensus, and noting it as 'not significant' is a statistical artifact, not a novel biological claim.

judgments.text: Among RAS/BRAF wildtype patients (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0), treatment_regorafenib markedly improves mean pfs_months vs no regorafenib in the same subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib efficacy is not known to depend on RAS/BRAF wildtype status; a marked benefit specifically in this subgroup is a novel biomarker-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Among RAS/BRAF wildtype patients, treatment_cetuximab does NOT improve mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab is guideline-endorsed precisely for RAS/BRAF wildtype mCRC, so a claim of no benefit in that subgroup contradicts established consensus and is non-standard.

judgments.text: Among RAS/BRAF wildtype patients, treatment_bevacizumab does NOT improve mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab benefit is not selected by RAS/BRAF status in guidelines; the absence of a RAS-wildtype-specific bevacizumab effect is consistent with standard understanding.

judgments.text: Among RAS/BRAF wildtype patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT improve mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab lacking PFS benefit in unselected RAS/BRAF wildtype CRC (without MSI-H selection) reflects standard guideline knowledge that immunotherapy benefit is restricted to MSI-H/dMMR disease.

judgments.text: Among RAS/BRAF wildtype patients, treatment_encorafenib does NOT improve mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's lack of benefit in BRAF wildtype CRC is established guideline knowledge — its indication is restricted to BRAF V600E-mutant disease.

judgments.text: Among RAS/BRAF wildtype patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does NOT improve mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib not benefiting RAS/BRAF wildtype unselected patients aligns with guidelines restricting this regimen to HER2-amplified, RAS wildtype CRC.

judgments.text: Among MSI-high patients (msi_high=1), treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT significantly improve mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines establish pembrolizumab as standard-of-care with PFS benefit in MSI-high CRC (KEYNOTE-177); a null finding contradicts paradigm consensus and is a novel/surprising claim.

judgments.text: Among MSI-high patients, treatment_regorafenib improves mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib benefit specifically enriched in MSI-high CRC is not an established guideline association — regorafenib is a later-line option without MSI-based selection.

judgments.text: Among MSI-high patients, treatment_cetuximab does not significantly change mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab's lack of effect in MSI-high CRC is consistent with established knowledge that anti-EGFR therapy is selected by RAS/BRAF status, not MSI, and MSI-H tumors are often right-sided/BRAF-mutant with poor anti-EGFR response.

judgments.text: After adjustment for prognostic covariates and other treatments, treatment_cetuximab has no independent effect on pfs_months in (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support cetuximab benefit in left-sided RAS/BRAF wildtype CRC; a null independent effect after adjustment in this exact biomarker-defined subgroup contradicts paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has no independent effect on pfs_months in MSI-high patients.
judgments.rationale: Null independent effect of pembrolizumab in MSI-high CRC after adjustment contradicts the KEYNOTE-177 guideline-endorsed benefit and would be a surprising finding.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_encorafenib has no independent effect on pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Null independent effect of encorafenib in BRAF V600E-mutant CRC contradicts the BEACON-trial-based guideline recommendation and is a paradigm-challenging claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has no independent effect on pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients.
judgments.rationale: Null independent effect of trastuzumab-tucatinib in HER2-amplified CRC contradicts MOUNTAINEER-based guideline endorsement and would be a novel, paradigm-challenging finding.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_regorafenib remains strongly associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is established as providing PFS benefit in refractory mCRC per CORRECT trial and guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_bevacizumab has no independent effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's modest/variable PFS effect after adjustment is consistent with known guideline-level uncertainty about its independent magnitude.

judgments.text: Conditional average treatment effects (CATEs) from a T-learner regression tree show that treatment_regorafenib has substantial positive heterogeneity (q90 - q10 wide and centered well above 0 for many patients), implying a strong responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Identifying a specific strong-responder subgroup for regorafenib via CATE heterogeneity goes beyond guideline statements, which treat it as broadly applicable in refractory disease.

judgments.text: CATEs for treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, and treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib are centered near zero with narrow spread, implying no strong responder subgroup detectable on this feature set.
judgments.rationale: That standard targeted agents show heterogeneous but bounded effects is unsurprising; guidelines already acknowledge biomarker-defined responder groups exist for these drugs.

judgments.text: FINAL: The complete responder subgroup for treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months is defined by ALL THREE predicates (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0); within this subgroup treatment_regorafenib increases mean pfs_months by approximately 2.7 months relative to no regorafenib, and the effect collapses to ~0 if any one of these predicates is unfavorable.
judgments.rationale: A regorafenib responder subgroup specifically defined by KRAS-wt AND BRAF-wt AND left-sided primary is not a guideline-recognized predictive signature.

judgments.text: FINAL: There is no detectable responder subgroup for treatment_cetuximab — even in the canonical (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0) subgroup the effect on pfs_months is null/very slightly negative.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines explicitly recommend cetuximab in RAS/BRAF-wt left-sided mCRC, so finding null effect even in that canonical subgroup contradicts established paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: There is no detectable responder subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab — even in MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) the effect on pfs_months is null.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is guideline-standard for MSI-high mCRC with established PFS benefit (KEYNOTE-177), so a null effect in MSI-high contradicts consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: There is no detectable responder subgroup for treatment_encorafenib — even in BRAF V600E-mutant patients with cetuximab (braf_v600e=1 AND treatment_cetuximab=1) the effect on pfs_months is null.
