judgments.rationale: Encorafenib + cetuximab is the guideline standard for BRAF V600E mCRC (BEACON), so a null effect in that subgroup contradicts established paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: There is no detectable responder subgroup for treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib — even in HER2-amplified RAS/BRAF wildtype patients (her2_amplified=1 AND kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0) the effect on pfs_months is null.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab + tucatinib has guideline-supported activity in HER2-amplified RAS/BRAF-wt mCRC (MOUNTAINEER), so null effect there contradicts consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_bevacizumab has no detectable benefit on pfs_months in any subgroup tested (right-sided, KRAS-mutant, stage IV).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's lack of clear subgroup-specific predictive biomarker is already the guideline-level understanding.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear effect).
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic factor in oncology is a standard clinical consideration appearing in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a well-established prognostic factor for PFS across virtually all solid tumors and appears in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV (metastatic) disease having shorter PFS than non-stage IV is foundational oncology staging knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic biomarker reflecting nutritional status is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor (cachexia) is standard oncology paradigm and incorporated into prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as an adverse prognostic marker is standard knowledge in GI and other CEA-relevant malignancies.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic factor is well-established across many cancers and incorporated into staging systems (e.g., melanoma AJCC).

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase as an adverse prognostic marker, often reflecting bone/liver metastases, is standard oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in cancer outcomes are widely discussed in guidelines and represent established epidemiologic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker (e.g., Glasgow Prognostic Score) is well-established in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as an adverse prognostic marker is well-established across solid tumors including colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive marker (especially for anti-EGFR therapy resistance) in colorectal cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with braf_v600e=1 have shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E is a well-known poor prognostic marker in metastatic colorectal cancer and appears in NCCN/ESMO guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with nras_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than nras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation, like KRAS, is established as conferring resistance to anti-EGFR therapy and is in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have longer pfs_months than msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a favorable prognostic marker (and predictor of immunotherapy response) in CRC is paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_amplified=1 have shorter pfs_months than her2_amplified=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification's prognostic and predictive role (and anti-EGFR resistance) in CRC is increasingly guideline-recognized standard knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with ntrk_fusion=1 have a different pfs_months than ntrk_fusion=0.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions are a guideline-recognized actionable alteration with established treatment implications (larotrectinib/entrectinib).

judgments.text: Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumor location is a well-established adverse prognostic and anti-EGFR predictive factor in metastatic CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_cetuximab and patients off treatment_cetuximab (overall, no specific direction).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in RAS-wild-type CRC is foundational guideline knowledge, not novel.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_bevacizumab and patients off treatment_bevacizumab (overall, no specific direction).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's effect on PFS in metastatic CRC is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_pembrolizumab and patients off treatment_pembrolizumab (overall, no specific direction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in colorectal cancer is well-established for MSI-high disease and broadly known across oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_encorafenib and patients off treatment_encorafenib (overall, no specific direction).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib is a guideline-endorsed targeted therapy in BRAF V600E-mutant colorectal cancer, so an overall PFS difference is expected.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients on treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib and patients off treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib (overall, no specific direction).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib is an established HER2-directed regimen with known PFS benefit in HER2-amplified disease.

judgments.text: Patients on treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than patients off treatment_regorafenib (overall positive effect).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is a guideline-recommended later-line therapy with established PFS benefit in refractory colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Treatment assignment for every treatment column is independent of every biomarker (kras_mutation, nras_mutation, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified, ntrk_fusion); biomarker frequencies do not differ between treated and untreated for any treatment.
judgments.rationale: Independence of treatment assignment from biomarkers is a baseline statistical/design assumption, not a novel oncology claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, and laboratory features in a single OLS model, the dominant prognostic signals on pfs_months are age_years (positive), ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), cea_ng_ml (negative), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), and ldh_u_l (negative).
judgments.rationale: These prognostic associations (ECOG, stage IV, albumin, CEA, LDH, weight loss) are textbook prognostic factors in oncology.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months (longer PFS) in kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 patients (the canonical RAS/RAF wild-type indication for anti-EGFR therapy).
judgments.rationale: Anti-EGFR therapy benefit restricted to RAS/RAF wild-type colorectal cancer is the canonical guideline-defined indication.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months (longer PFS) in msi_high=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is a guideline-endorsed, FDA-approved tissue-agnostic indication.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months (longer PFS) in braf_v600e=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E-mutant disease is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months (longer PFS) in her2_amplified=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified disease is a guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in kras_mutation=0 than in kras_mutation=1 (regorafenib benefit concentrated in KRAS wild-type).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved for refractory mCRC regardless of KRAS status; CORRECT trial subgroup analyses did not establish KRAS-WT restriction in any guideline.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=0 than in braf_v600e=1.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not restrict regorafenib by BRAF V600E status; differential benefit by BRAF is not standard knowledge.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided primaries) than in right_sided_primary=1.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness is predictive for anti-EGFR therapy, not regorafenib; no guideline conditions regorafenib use on primary tumor location.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months differs between msi_high=1 and msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is used regardless of MSI status; differential efficacy by MSI-high is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in nras_mutation=1 than in nras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: No guideline supports enhanced regorafenib benefit in NRAS-mutant tumors; this contradicts the typical poor-prognosis interpretation of NRAS mutation.

judgments.text: Within the joint stratification of kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary, the treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit is restricted to the cell with kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided primary); in every other cell the benefit is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional restriction of regorafenib benefit to KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided patients is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: The kras_mutation main effect on pfs_months that we detected in iteration 3 is entirely explained by differential regorafenib benefit; among patients with treatment_regorafenib=0, kras_mutation has no independent prognostic effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming KRAS has no independent prognostic effect outside regorafenib treatment contradicts its established prognostic role in mCRC.

judgments.text: The braf_v600e main effect on pfs_months disappears among patients with treatment_regorafenib=0.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E is a well-established poor-prognosis marker in mCRC independent of treatment; claim that its prognostic effect vanishes without regorafenib is contrary to guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The right_sided_primary main effect on pfs_months disappears among patients with treatment_regorafenib=0.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary is an established prognostic factor in mCRC regardless of therapy; claim that this effect disappears without regorafenib is novel.

judgments.text: After fitting an adjusted OLS model with prognostic covariates plus indicators for regorafenib_within_eligible (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0) and regorafenib_outside_eligible, the within-eligible regorafenib coefficient is large and positive (~+2.7 months) while the outside-eligible regorafenib coefficient is essentially zero. The kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary main-effect coefficients become non-significant.
judgments.rationale: A model partitioning regorafenib effect into within-/outside-eligible subgroups defined by triple-marker eligibility is a speculative interaction-driven biomarker construct not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Across all patients, lower cea_ng_ml predicts a larger treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months (negative regorafenib x cea interaction).
judgments.rationale: CEA as a prognostic/predictive biomarker for response in metastatic CRC is well-established and would appear in guidelines/textbooks.

judgments.text: Across all patients, the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months varies with age_years.
judgments.rationale: Age-based effect modification of regorafenib benefit is a standard subgroup analysis routinely reported in trials and guidelines.

judgments.text: Across all patients, the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months varies with albumin_g_dl.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a specific effect modifier of regorafenib PFS benefit is not part of standard guideline consensus, though albumin is prognostic generally.

judgments.text: Across all patients, the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months varies with ldh_u_l.
judgments.rationale: LDH as a predictive effect modifier specifically for regorafenib PFS benefit is not established in CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 patients, lower cea_ng_ml predicts a larger treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit; the interaction between regorafenib and CEA is negative inside this eligibility subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A subgroup-conditional claim restricted to KRAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided CRC where low CEA predicts greater regorafenib benefit is a speculative cross-pathway hypothesis not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the eligibility subgroup, treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does not vary by age_years.
judgments.rationale: Lack of age-based heterogeneity in regorafenib effect within a subgroup is consistent with standard trial subgroup findings and not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Within the eligibility subgroup, treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does not vary by ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: ECOG PS not modifying regorafenib effect within the subgroup is a standard, non-novel observation aligned with trial subgroup analyses.

judgments.text: Within the eligibility subgroup, treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does not vary by stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV not modifying regorafenib benefit is unsurprising given regorafenib is used in metastatic disease and is not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Within the eligibility subgroup, treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does not differ between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0.
judgments.rationale: Sex not modifying regorafenib benefit is a standard non-significant subgroup observation, not novel.

judgments.text: Within the eligibility subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0) there is a sharp threshold on cea_ng_ml at approximately 5 ng/mL: patients with cea_ng_ml < 5 derive a treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit of approximately +5 months, while patients with cea_ng_ml >= 5 derive essentially zero benefit.
judgments.rationale: A sharp 5 ng/mL CEA threshold producing ~5 months PFS benefit vs zero benefit in KRAS/BRAF-WT left-sided CRC on regorafenib is a highly specific, speculative claim not in any guideline.

judgments.text: The complete regorafenib responder subgroup is kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; within this subgroup treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +5.0 months, while outside the subgroup the effect is approximately zero.
judgments.rationale: A specific four-feature subgroup (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided, low CEA) defining a large regorafenib PFS benefit is not a guideline-recognized predictor of regorafenib response.

judgments.text: Across two-feature subgroups defined by binary clinical/biomarker variables, no subgroup yields a clinically meaningful (>=0.3 month) or statistically significant treatment_cetuximab pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit being limited in unselected mCRC and concentrated in KRAS-WT/left-sided disease is established guideline knowledge, so a null finding across arbitrary two-feature subgroups is not novel.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in patients with kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS-WT, left-sided).
judgments.rationale: KRAS wild-type, left-sided primary predicting cetuximab benefit is standard guideline-level knowledge in metastatic colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Across two-feature subgroups of binary clinical/biomarker variables, treatment_bevacizumab does not produce a meaningful pfs_months benefit; the only nominally significant signal is a small NEGATIVE effect of treatment_bevacizumab in braf_v600e=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: A specifically negative bevacizumab PFS effect confined to BRAF V600E-mutant patients is a subgroup-conditional harm signal not reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1 patients, no continuous lab feature (cea, albumin, ldh, weight_loss, crp, nlr, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) defines a subgroup with a meaningful treatment_pembrolizumab pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines treat MSI-high as the predictor for pembrolizumab benefit without further lab-based stratification, so a claim that no continuous lab refines benefit within MSI-high is a non-guideline mechanistic/stratification claim.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1 patients, the treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months does not differ between cea_ng_ml above vs below the median.
judgments.rationale: CEA-based stratification of pembrolizumab effect within MSI-high disease is not part of guideline paradigm, making a null interaction claim a non-trivial mechanistic finding.

judgments.text: Within braf_v600e=1 patients, no second feature (msi_high, kras_mutation, right_sided_primary, sex_female, stage_iv, ecog_ps, age_years, albumin_g_dl, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo) defines a subgroup with a meaningful treatment_encorafenib pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse encorafenib (with cetuximab) for BRAF V600E mCRC without further subgroup refinement, so a claim that no second feature defines additional benefit goes beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Within her2_amplified=1 patients, no additional binary or continuous feature defines a subgroup with a meaningful treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2-amplified disease without further refinement, so a claim of no additional modifier is a non-guideline stratification finding.

judgments.text: Among patients receiving treatment_regorafenib, additionally receiving treatment_bevacizumab does not modify the pfs_months effect of regorafenib (no synergistic interaction).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib + bevacizumab combination interaction is not addressed in guidelines, so a no-synergy claim is a novel cross-therapy interaction finding.

judgments.text: Among patients receiving treatment_regorafenib, additionally receiving treatment_cetuximab does not modify the pfs_months effect of regorafenib (no synergistic interaction).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib + cetuximab combination interaction is not part of guideline paradigm, so a no-synergy claim is a novel cross-therapy interaction finding.

judgments.text: When pfs_months is regressed on (treatment_regorafenib, eligibility_subgroup, regorafenib*eligibility), the regorafenib*eligibility interaction coefficient is approximately +2.7 months and dwarfs the regorafenib main effect, confirming that the entire regorafenib signal is concentrated in the eligibility subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A regorafenib benefit concentrated in a specific eligibility subgroup with a ~+2.7 month interaction effect is not a guideline-level claim; regorafenib is established as a later-line option without such subgroup-conditional efficacy in standard texts.

judgments.text: After adjustment for ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, cea_ng_ml, and ldh_u_l, age_years remains POSITIVELY associated with pfs_months (beta > 0, statistically significant).
judgments.rationale: A positive independent association of older age with longer PFS after adjustment for performance status, stage, and metabolic/tumor burden markers contradicts the usual textbook expectation that age is neutral or adverse, making this a non-guideline claim.

judgments.text: Refined consolidated statement: the only treatment in this cohort that confers a positive pfs_months effect is treatment_regorafenib, and that effect is fully concentrated in the four-feature subgroup defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; in this subgroup the regorafenib pfs_months effect is approximately +5.0 months, while outside the subgroup the effect is approximately zero. The other five treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab_tucatinib) show no clinically or statistically meaningful pfs_months benefit anywhere, including in their canonical biomarker-defined indications.
judgments.rationale: Restricting regorafenib's PFS benefit to a four-feature subgroup (KRAS WT, BRAF WT, left-sided, CEA<5) with ~+5 months gain, and simultaneously claiming null effects for cetuximab/bevacizumab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab-tucatinib in their guideline-endorsed biomarker indications, goes well beyond textbook consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for pfs_months. Treatment: treatment_regorafenib. Direction: increases pfs_months. Subgroup predicates (must all hold): kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5. Within this subgroup the treatment effect on pfs_months is approximately +5.0 months. If any one of the four predicates fails (i.e. kras_mutation=1, OR braf_v600e=1, OR right_sided_primary=1, OR cea_ng_ml>=5) the regorafenib treatment effect on pfs_months is suppressed to approximately 0. Each of the four predicates is necessary; none alone is sufficient.
judgments.rationale: A conjunctive four-predicate necessary-and-jointly-sufficient subgroup definition for regorafenib efficacy is a speculative cross-pathway interaction claim absent from any treatment guideline.

judgments.text: FINAL FINDING for the other treatments: there is no patient subgroup in this cohort within which treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib produces a meaningful (>=0.3 month) and statistically defensible pfs_months benefit; their canonical biomarker-targeted indications (RAS/RAF wild-type, MSI-high, BRAF V600E, HER2-amplified) all show null effects in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: Asserting null efficacy for cetuximab in RAS/RAF WT, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, encorafenib in BRAF V600E, and trastuzumab-tucatinib in HER2-amplified disease directly contradicts established guideline-level biomarker-driven indications, making the claim non-standard.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin (`albumin_g_dl`) is associated with longer progression-free survival (`pfs_months`); the OLS slope of pfs_months on albumin_g_dl is positive.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic factor for survival outcomes is well-established in oncology and appears in standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Worse performance status (higher `ecog_ps`) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; the OLS slope is negative.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a foundational prognostic variable in every oncology guideline and textbook.

judgments.text: Patients with `stage_iv` = 1 have shorter mean `pfs_months` than those with `stage_iv` = 0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse survival than earlier stages is the most basic staging principle in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher tumor burden marker CEA (log1p(`cea_ng_ml`)) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as a marker of higher tumor burden and worse prognosis is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is well-established and incorporated into staging systems (e.g., melanoma AJCC, lymphoma IPI).

judgments.text: Higher `nlr` (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as an adverse prognostic marker is widely reported and increasingly cited in guidelines and reviews across tumor types.

judgments.text: Higher `crp_mg_l` (inflammation) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP/systemic inflammation as a poor prognostic factor (e.g., Glasgow Prognostic Score) is established oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (`weight_loss_pct_6mo`) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss as adverse prognostic factors are textbook oncology concepts.

judgments.text: Older age (`age_years`) is associated with shorter `pfs_months`; OLS slope is negative.
judgments.rationale: Older age as a general adverse prognostic factor is standard and appears in most prognostic models.

judgments.text: Mean `pfs_months` differs between female (`sex_female`=1) and male patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in cancer outcomes are broadly recognized, though direction varies; the generic claim of a difference is not beyond guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with `right_sided_primary`=1 have shorter mean `pfs_months` than those with left-sided primary.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse prognosis/shorter PFS in colorectal cancer is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment with cetuximab (`treatment_cetuximab`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab improving PFS in metastatic colorectal cancer is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment with bevacizumab (`treatment_bevacizumab`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab improving PFS in metastatic colorectal cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment with pembrolizumab (`treatment_pembrolizumab`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS in MSI-H/dMMR colorectal cancer is established guideline knowledge (KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: Treatment with encorafenib (`treatment_encorafenib`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) improving PFS in BRAF V600E-mutant CRC is established guideline knowledge (BEACON).

judgments.text: Treatment with trastuzumab/tucatinib (`treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib activity in HER2-amplified CRC is established and guideline-recognized (MOUNTAINEER).

judgments.text: Treatment with regorafenib (`treatment_regorafenib`=1) is associated with longer `pfs_months` overall.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib improving PFS in refractory metastatic CRC is established guideline knowledge (CORRECT trial).

judgments.text: Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is restricted to KRAS wild-type patients: in `kras_mutation`=0 the cetuximab-vs-no-cetuximab PFS difference is positive, and the treatment_cetuximab×kras_mutation interaction is negative.
judgments.rationale: Restriction of anti-EGFR (cetuximab) benefit to KRAS wild-type CRC is a foundational guideline-level biomarker fact.

judgments.text: In `kras_mutation`=1 patients, cetuximab does not improve `pfs_months` (effect ~ 0).
judgments.rationale: Lack of cetuximab benefit in KRAS-mutant CRC is established guideline knowledge and the basis for biomarker testing.

judgments.text: Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is restricted to NRAS wild-type patients (interaction with `nras_mutation` negative).
judgments.rationale: Extended RAS testing including NRAS to restrict anti-EGFR therapy to NRAS wild-type is established guideline practice.

judgments.text: Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is restricted to BRAF wild-type patients (interaction with `braf_v600e` negative).
judgments.rationale: Restriction of anti-EGFR (cetuximab) benefit to BRAF wild-type CRC is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is greater in left-sided primary (`right_sided_primary`=0) than right-sided.
judgments.rationale: Greater cetuximab benefit in left-sided primary tumors is well-established in NCCN/ESMO CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: In RAS/RAF wild-type patients (`kras_mutation`=0 AND `nras_mutation`=0 AND `braf_v600e`=0), cetuximab is associated with longer `pfs_months` than no cetuximab.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in RAS/RAF wild-type CRC is a foundational guideline-level association.

judgments.text: In RAS/RAF wild-type AND left-sided (`right_sided_primary`=0) patients, cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is even larger than in the broader RAS/RAF WT group.
judgments.rationale: Combined left-sided plus RAS/RAF wild-type as the optimal anti-EGFR population is current guideline-endorsed practice.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on `pfs_months` is restricted to MSI-high (`msi_high`=1) patients; the treatment_pembrolizumab×msi_high interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab restriction to MSI-high CRC is established (KEYNOTE-177) and codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Encorafenib benefit on `pfs_months` is concentrated in BRAF V600E (`braf_v600e`=1) patients; the treatment_encorafenib×braf_v600e interaction is positive (i.e., encorafenib effect more positive in BRAF mut).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib targeting BRAF V600E CRC is an FDA-approved, guideline-endorsed indication (BEACON).

judgments.text: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit on `pfs_months` is concentrated in HER2-amplified (`her2_amplified`=1) patients; the treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib×her2_amplified interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib activity in HER2-amplified CRC is guideline-recognized (MOUNTAINEER).

judgments.text: Within HER2-amplified, the trastuzumab/tucatinib effect on `pfs_months` is greater when patients are also RAS wild-type (`kras_mutation`=0 AND `nras_mutation`=0).
judgments.rationale: A within-HER2-amplified interaction with RAS wild-type status modifying tucatinib/trastuzumab benefit is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Bevacizumab effect on `pfs_months` differs by KRAS mutation status (treatment_bevacizumab×kras_mutation interaction is non-zero).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is given regardless of KRAS status; a KRAS-modified bevacizumab effect is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: Bevacizumab effect on `pfs_months` differs by tumor sidedness (treatment_bevacizumab×right_sided_primary interaction is non-zero).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is recommended regardless of sidedness; a sidedness-modified bevacizumab effect itself is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by KRAS mutation status (interaction non-zero).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not specify KRAS mutation status as a predictive biomarker modifying regorafenib benefit.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by tumor sidedness (interaction non-zero).
judgments.rationale: Tumor sidedness as a predictive modifier of regorafenib (rather than anti-EGFR) efficacy is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by BRAF V600E status (interaction non-zero).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a predictive biomarker for differential regorafenib benefit is not part of standard treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by MSI-high status (interaction non-zero).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status modifying regorafenib efficacy is not a guideline-recognized predictive interaction.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` differs by stage_iv status (interaction non-zero).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved in the refractory metastatic (stage IV) setting, so stage-based effect modification is essentially baseline indication context rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by HER2 amplification (interaction non-zero).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a predictive modifier of regorafenib benefit is not established in current CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by ECOG performance status.
judgments.rationale: Poorer ECOG performance status is a well-known prognostic and treatment-benefit modifier across refractory therapies including regorafenib, and dose/benefit considerations by PS appear in guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by age.
judgments.rationale: Age-related differences in tolerability and benefit of regorafenib are well documented and reflected in dosing guidance.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by log(CEA); higher CEA reduces benefit (interaction negative).
judgments.rationale: A specific quantitative interaction whereby higher log(CEA) attenuates regorafenib PFS benefit is not a guideline-level established predictive relationship.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by albumin.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a predictive (treatment-effect modifying) rather than purely prognostic biomarker for regorafenib is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by LDH.
judgments.rationale: LDH as a quantitative effect modifier of regorafenib PFS benefit is not a guideline-level established predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is modified by NLR.
judgments.rationale: NLR as a predictive (not just prognostic) modifier of regorafenib efficacy is not established in CRC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, stage_iv, sidedness, RAS/RAF/MSI/HER2 biomarkers, and labs, the adjusted association of cetuximab with `pfs_months` is positive.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in RAS/BRAF wild-type colorectal cancer after multivariable adjustment is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, bevacizumab is associated with longer `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab improving PFS in metastatic CRC is well-established guideline-supported knowledge.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, pembrolizumab is associated with longer `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS (in MSI-H mCRC) is established first-line guideline therapy.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, encorafenib is associated with longer `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) improving outcomes in BRAF V600E mCRC is established guideline therapy.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, trastuzumab/tucatinib is associated with longer `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified mCRC is now reflected in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, regorafenib remains associated with longer `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's PFS benefit in refractory mCRC is established from CORRECT trial and in guidelines.

judgments.text: An exhaustive treatment-by-feature interaction screen across {age, sex, ECOG, stage_iv, sidedness, KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, MSI, HER2, NTRK, log_cea, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin} will identify at least one feature whose interaction with cetuximab is significant at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: An agnostic interaction screen identifying novel non-RAS/BRAF modifiers of cetuximab effect goes beyond guideline-established predictors.

judgments.text: The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of bevacizumab effect on `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Identifying any significant clinical/lab modifier of bevacizumab efficacy is novel since no predictive biomarker for bevacizumab is guideline-established.

judgments.text: The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of pembrolizumab effect on `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Generic claim that an interaction screen will find any modifier of pembrolizumab is not a specific novel mechanistic hypothesis.

judgments.text: The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of encorafenib effect on `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Generic claim about finding any modifier of encorafenib effect is not a specific novel biological hypothesis.

judgments.text: The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of trastuzumab/tucatinib effect on `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: Generic claim about finding any modifier of trastuzumab/tucatinib effect is not a specific novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: The same interaction screen will identify at least one significant modifier of regorafenib effect on `pfs_months`; the strongest will involve KRAS.
judgments.rationale: Predicting KRAS as the strongest modifier of regorafenib benefit is a specific cross-pathway claim not established in guidelines for this multikinase inhibitor.

judgments.text: Best subgroup for cetuximab benefit: KRAS WT AND NRAS WT AND BRAF WT AND left-sided primary; in this subgroup cetuximab improves `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: RAS/BRAF wild-type left-sided CRC predicting cetuximab benefit is established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Best subgroup for pembrolizumab benefit: MSI-high; in this subgroup pembrolizumab improves `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit in CRC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Best subgroup for encorafenib benefit: BRAF V600E mutation; in this subgroup encorafenib improves `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting encorafenib benefit is established guideline consensus in CRC.

judgments.text: Best subgroup for trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit: HER2-amplified AND RAS WT; in this subgroup it improves `pfs_months`.
judgments.rationale: HER2-amplified RAS-wild-type CRC responding to trastuzumab/tucatinib is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Bevacizumab provides a small adjusted positive effect on `pfs_months` in the overall cohort (no clear subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Modest overall PFS benefit of bevacizumab without a clear biomarker subgroup reflects established guideline understanding.

judgments.text: Regorafenib provides a positive effect on `pfs_months` in patients with ECOG performance status 0.
judgments.rationale: Restricting regorafenib benefit specifically to ECOG 0 patients is a subgroup-conditional clinical claim beyond standard guideline framing.

judgments.text: Cetuximab benefit on `pfs_months` is concentrated specifically in KRAS WT AND left-sided patients; the effect is largest in (kras_mutation=0, right_sided_primary=0) and minimal in the other three cells.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit being restricted to KRAS WT left-sided mCRC is established guideline consensus (NCCN/ESMO recommend anti-EGFR only for RAS WT left-sided tumors).

judgments.text: Within RAS wild-type (KRAS WT and NRAS WT), the cetuximab×BRAF V600E interaction on `pfs_months` is negative — BRAF V600E suppresses cetuximab benefit even after restricting to RAS WT.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting lack of cetuximab benefit within RAS WT is established and reflected in guidelines that exclude/qualify anti-EGFR use in BRAF-mutant mCRC.

judgments.text: Within BRAF V600E, the encorafenib effect on `pfs_months` is concentrated in KRAS WT (BRAF V600E and KRAS mutation are typically mutually exclusive but may have an effect modifier here).
judgments.rationale: Although BRAF V600E and KRAS mutations are typically mutually exclusive, claiming KRAS WT specifically modifies encorafenib efficacy within BRAF V600E is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab effect on `pfs_months` is modified by sidedness, KRAS, stage_iv, or ECOG (test each).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines treat MSI-high as a uniform predictor of pembrolizumab benefit; modification by sidedness, KRAS, stage IV, or ECOG within MSI-H is a speculative subgroup hypothesis.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit on `pfs_months` is concentrated in HER2-amplified AND fully RAS/RAF wild-type patients (KRAS WT, NRAS WT, BRAF WT).
judgments.rationale: While HER2 amplification predicts trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit and RAS/RAF WT is typically required, the specific compound restriction to fully KRAS+NRAS+BRAF WT as a heterogeneity-of-effect claim goes beyond routine guideline phrasing.

judgments.text: Bevacizumab effect on `pfs_months` is modified by log(CEA), albumin, LDH, ECOG, or age (test each interaction).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is given broadly without biomarker selection in guidelines; effect modification by CEA, albumin, LDH, ECOG, or age is exploratory and not established.

judgments.text: Regorafenib improves `pfs_months` in KRAS wild-type (`kras_mutation`=0) patients but has no effect (or is null) in KRAS mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved/used regardless of KRAS status in refractory mCRC; KRAS WT-restricted benefit contradicts current guideline practice and would be a novel finding.

judgments.text: Mean `pfs_months` differs between patients with `ntrk_fusion`=1 and those without.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions are vanishingly rare in CRC and treated with TRK inhibitors; a generic PFS difference by NTRK fusion status without specifying treatment is not a standard guideline claim.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, none of the canonical biomarker × treatment interactions (cetuximab×KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/sidedness, pembrolizumab×MSI, encorafenib×BRAF, trastuzumab/tucatinib×HER2) are statistically significant in this dataset (i.e., the dataset's only strong heterogeneity is for regorafenib).
judgments.rationale: A negative meta-claim that all canonical biomarker×treatment interactions fail except regorafenib in this dataset is a dataset-specific, non-guideline assertion.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on `pfs_months` requires KRAS WT: in KRAS WT × BRAF WT it is large and positive; KRAS mutation suppresses the effect; BRAF V600E further suppresses it.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional structure where regorafenib requires KRAS WT and is further suppressed by BRAF V600E is not an established regorafenib biomarker pattern in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS WT × BRAF WT, regorafenib benefit is concentrated in left-sided primary patients (sidedness suppresses the effect when right-sided).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support regorafenib in refractory mCRC regardless of RAS/BRAF/sidedness; concentrating benefit in left-sided KRAS/BRAF WT patients is not an established paradigm.

judgments.text: Within KRAS WT × BRAF WT × left-sided, regorafenib benefit is further concentrated in patients with low CEA (`cea_ng_ml` < 5 ng/mL); higher CEA suppresses benefit.
judgments.rationale: Low CEA as a predictive biomarker for regorafenib benefit within a molecularly and anatomically defined subgroup is not part of standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: FINAL: Regorafenib produces a large positive effect on `pfs_months` (~+5 months) specifically in patients with `kras_mutation`=0 AND `nras_mutation`=0 AND `braf_v600e`=0 AND `right_sided_primary`=0 AND `cea_ng_ml` < 5 ng/mL; outside this subgroup the effect is essentially null. Each of {KRAS mutation, NRAS mutation (weakly), BRAF V600E, right-sided primary, elevated CEA} suppresses the regorafenib treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: A composite predictive signature (all-RAS/BRAF WT + left-sided + low CEA) defining a ~5-month PFS benefit from regorafenib goes well beyond current refractory-mCRC treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: Joint multivariable interaction model confirms negative interactions of regorafenib with `kras_mutation`, `braf_v600e`, `right_sided_primary`, and `log_cea`, while NRAS mutation is not an independent suppressor.
judgments.rationale: A multivariable interaction framework identifying KRAS, BRAF, sidedness, and log-CEA as independent suppressors of regorafenib effect (with NRAS non-contributory) is not reflected in current guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a well-established universal prognostic factor for PFS across virtually all cancers and appears in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV disease.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than earlier stages is foundational oncology staging dogma found in any textbook.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is standard knowledge incorporated into prognostic scores like GPS/mGPS in guidelines.

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss as adverse prognostic indicators is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Right-sided primary tumor (right_sided_primary=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumor location conferring worse prognosis is a well-established finding in colorectal cancer guidelines (e.g., NCCN, ESMO).

judgments.text: Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic factor is standard, incorporated into staging systems (e.g., AJCC melanoma) and guideline-level prognostication.

judgments.text: Higher CEA (cea_ng_ml) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA predicting worse outcomes in colorectal and other cancers is textbook-level standard tumor marker knowledge.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with different pfs_months than male sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in cancer outcomes are widely recognized and discussed in standard oncology references, though direction varies by disease.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Older age as a general adverse prognostic factor is universally established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of systemic inflammation and poor prognosis is standard, incorporated into Glasgow Prognostic Score in guideline-level use.

judgments.text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR as a poor prognostic marker is well-established across solid tumors and appears in standard prognostic discussions.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin (hemoglobin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as an adverse prognostic factor is a textbook association in oncology.

judgments.text: KRAS-mutated patients (kras_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive factor (especially in CRC and against anti-EGFR therapy) is guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: NRAS-mutated patients (nras_mutation=1) have shorter pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation conferring worse outcomes and anti-EGFR resistance in CRC is established guideline content.

judgments.text: BRAF V600E-mutated patients (braf_v600e=1) have shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a poor prognostic marker in CRC and other tumors is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) have different pfs_months than MSI-low patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status affecting prognosis and treatment response is a well-established guideline-level biomarker.

judgments.text: HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1) have different pfs_months than HER2 non-amplified.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a prognostic and predictive biomarker is textbook oncology across multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: NTRK fusion-positive patients (ntrk_fusion=1) have different pfs_months than NTRK fusion-negative.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusion as a targetable driver with prognostic/predictive implications is established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have different pfs_months than those not receiving cetuximab.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy (tied to RAS/BRAF status in CRC, EGFR in HNSCC) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving bevacizumab.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab providing PFS benefit across multiple tumor types is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in oncology (e.g., MSI-high CRC, NSCLC) is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving encorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (typically with cetuximab) is guideline-standard for BRAF V600E mCRC.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab plus tucatinib is established for HER2-positive disease (breast and HER2-amplified CRC).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is a guideline-recommended later-line therapy in mCRC.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs across ECOG performance status categories (0, 1, 2) with monotonically shorter PFS at higher ECOG.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic finding.

judgments.text: In MSI-high (msi_high=1) patients, the effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is more positive than in MSI-low patients (i.e., pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in MSI-high).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit concentrated in MSI-high tumors is the canonical guideline indication (e.g., KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: In BRAF V600E-mutated (braf_v600e=1) patients, treatment_encorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in BRAF wild-type patients (encorafenib benefit concentrated in BRAF V600E).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit restricted to BRAF V600E-mutant tumors is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: In HER2-amplified (her2_amplified=1) patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib produces longer pfs_months than in HER2 non-amplified.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit confined to HER2-amplified tumors is the standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: In KRAS wild-type (kras_mutation=0) patients, treatment_cetuximab produces longer pfs_months than in KRAS-mutated patients (cetuximab benefit concentrated in KRAS WT).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit restricted to KRAS wild-type CRC is a long-established guideline biomarker.

judgments.text: In all-RAS-and-BRAF wild-type and left-sided primary patients, treatment_cetuximab produces longer pfs_months than in those without cetuximab (the canonical benefit subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Anti-EGFR benefit in RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided mCRC is the canonical guideline-endorsed subgroup.

judgments.text: Treatment_bevacizumab produces longer pfs_months in right-sided primary tumor patients than in left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness effects in CRC are well-established for anti-EGFR therapy but bevacizumab benefit by sidedness is not a guideline-standard predictive marker.

judgments.text: Treatment_bevacizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in stage IV patients than non-stage-IV.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is indicated and used predominantly in stage IV/metastatic CRC, so larger benefit in stage IV patients reflects standard practice, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Treatment_regorafenib produces a larger pfs_months benefit in patients with low CEA (cea_ng_ml at or below median) than in patients with high CEA.
judgments.rationale: CEA-stratified differential benefit of regorafenib is not part of any treatment guideline or established predictive paradigm.

judgments.text: Treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months differs across ECOG performance status categories.
judgments.rationale: While ECOG PS gates eligibility for regorafenib, a true differential treatment effect across PS categories is not a guideline-recognized predictive interaction.

judgments.text: Treatment_regorafenib effect differs by albumin (low <3.5 vs ≥3.5 g/dL).
judgments.rationale: Albumin is prognostic generally, but a regorafenib-specific differential benefit by albumin status is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a significant negative interaction between treatment_regorafenib and continuous cea_ng_ml on pfs_months — the regorafenib benefit attenuates as CEA rises.
judgments.rationale: A continuous CEA × regorafenib interaction attenuating benefit is a speculative biomarker claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: In KRAS wild-type (kras_mutation=0) patients, treatment_regorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in KRAS-mutated patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS status; differential benefit by KRAS wild-type is not guideline-established.

judgments.text: In BRAF wild-type (braf_v600e=0) patients, treatment_regorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in BRAF-V600E patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of BRAF status; differential benefit by BRAF V600E status is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In left-sided primary (right_sided_primary=0) patients, treatment_regorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in right-sided patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness as a predictor of regorafenib benefit is not a guideline-recognized predictive marker, unlike for anti-EGFR therapy.

judgments.text: Treatment_regorafenib × msi_high interaction on pfs_months is significant.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status guides immunotherapy decisions, but a regorafenib × MSI-high interaction is not part of established treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: Treatment_regorafenib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months is significant.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib benefit conditional on HER2 amplification in colorectal cancer is not part of guideline consensus and represents a speculative subgroup-specific effect.

judgments.text: Treatment_regorafenib × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months is significant.
judgments.rationale: An NRAS-mutation-specific modifier of regorafenib PFS benefit is not an established guideline finding for this multi-kinase inhibitor.

judgments.text: None of age_years, ecog_ps, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl modify the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months once cea_ng_ml is removed (i.e., these continuous features are not strong heterogeneity drivers for regorafenib).
judgments.rationale: A negative claim that standard prognostic continuous covariates (ECOG, albumin, LDH, NLR, etc.) do not modify regorafenib effect once CEA is removed is a speculative heterogeneity claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In a multivariable regression of pfs_months on all features and treatments, treatment_regorafenib retains a positive significant coefficient and remains the only treatment with a clinically meaningful main effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming regorafenib is the only treatment with a clinically meaningful positive PFS coefficient in adjusted multivariable analysis contradicts standard CRC guideline framing where regorafenib is a later-line option with modest benefit.

judgments.text: In a multivariable regression, ecog_ps, stage_iv, age_years, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml retain significant adjusted associations with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG, stage IV, albumin, weight loss, KRAS, BRAF V600E, sidedness, and CEA being prognostic for PFS in colorectal cancer are well-established guideline/textbook associations.

judgments.text: In low-CEA + KRAS wild-type patients, treatment_regorafenib produces a much larger pfs_months benefit than in the broader low-CEA group, while in low-CEA + KRAS-mutated patients regorafenib has no detectable benefit.
judgments.rationale: A three-way conditional benefit (low CEA + KRAS wild-type amplifies regorafenib effect, abolished by KRAS mutation) is not part of regorafenib's guideline indication, which is biomarker-agnostic.

judgments.text: In low-CEA + left-sided primary patients, treatment_regorafenib produces a much larger pfs_months benefit than in low-CEA + right-sided patients (where the effect is null).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness-conditional regorafenib benefit within a low-CEA subgroup is a speculative cross-pathway claim not reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Albumin, ECOG, and stage_iv do not further refine the low-CEA subgroup's regorafenib benefit appreciably.
judgments.rationale: A negative refinement claim about albumin/ECOG/stage IV not modifying regorafenib benefit within low-CEA patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional finding beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Even in the canonical cetuximab benefit subgroup (kras_wt & nras_wt & braf_wt & left-sided & msi_low & her2_neg), treatment_cetuximab does not produce a meaningfully positive pfs_months difference in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: Asserting cetuximab fails to benefit even the canonical all-RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided, MSI-low, HER2-negative subgroup contradicts established guideline consensus and is a notable dataset-specific claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment for clinical features, the treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months remains non-significant.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy being tied to MSI-high status is a core guideline-level association in colorectal/solid tumor oncology, so a non-significant interaction restates the standard paradigm framing.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs by tumor side (right_sided_primary), with right-sided patients potentially deriving relatively more benefit (or less harm) than left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Tumor sidedness as a predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is not a guideline-established interaction (sidedness predicts anti-EGFR response, not PD-1 blockade).

judgments.text: In BRAF V600E + treatment_cetuximab patients, treatment_encorafenib produces longer pfs_months than in BRAF V600E patients without cetuximab (clinical encorafenib + cetuximab combination interaction).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib + cetuximab is the established guideline-endorsed regimen for BRAF V600E mutant metastatic colorectal cancer (BEACON CRC).

judgments.text: In HER2-amplified + RAS wild-type patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib produces longer pfs_months than in HER2-amplified + RAS-mutated patients (canonical clinical eligibility).
judgments.rationale: HER2-amplified, RAS wild-type is the canonical guideline eligibility criterion for trastuzumab + tucatinib (MOUNTAINEER) in mCRC.

judgments.text: Treatment_bevacizumab does not produce a clinically meaningful pfs_months benefit in any clinical subgroup (ECOG, side, CRP, LDH).
judgments.rationale: A blanket claim that bevacizumab provides no benefit in any clinical subgroup contradicts guideline consensus that bevacizumab adds PFS benefit broadly in mCRC.

judgments.text: Within the low-CEA subgroup, the regorafenib pfs_months benefit is essentially the same regardless of additional clinical modifiers (ECOG low, albumin high, low weight loss, high hemoglobin, low NLR).
judgments.rationale: Claiming regorafenib benefit is invariant to multiple clinical modifiers within a low-CEA subgroup is a specific cross-covariate stability claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: As the count of 'unfavorable' clinical features (high CEA, low albumin, ECOG=2, high weight loss, high LDH) increases from 0 to 5, the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months declines monotonically (linear interaction).
judgments.rationale: A monotonic linear decline in regorafenib benefit with increasing count of unfavorable features is a specific quantitative interaction not described in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the most narrowly defined cetuximab-favorable subgroup (triple WT + left-sided + msi_low + her2_neg + ecog<2), treatment_cetuximab still does NOT produce a positive pfs_months difference; if anything, the point estimate is slightly negative.
judgments.rationale: Asserting cetuximab provides no PFS benefit even in the canonically favorable triple-WT/left-sided subgroup contradicts established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In patients who are simultaneously LEFT-SIDED (right_sided_primary=0), KRAS WILD-TYPE (kras_mutation=0), BRAF WILD-TYPE (braf_v600e=0), and have LOW CEA (cea_ng_ml at or below the cohort median), treatment_regorafenib produces a substantially LONGER pfs_months than in those without regorafenib; outside this subgroup the regorafenib effect is essentially null. The full subgroup definition is: (right_sided_primary=0) AND (kras_mutation=0) AND (braf_v600e=0) AND (cea_ng_ml ≤ median). The unfavorable values that suppress the regorafenib effect are: right-sided primary, KRAS mutation, BRAF V600E mutation, and high CEA.
judgments.rationale: A four-way subgroup (left-sided + KRAS WT + BRAF WT + low CEA) defining where regorafenib uniquely works is not a guideline-recognized predictive signature.

judgments.text: None of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib produces a clinically meaningful pfs_months benefit in any biomarker-defined subgroup in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: A sweeping null claim across all five targeted/immunotherapy agents in their biomarker-selected populations contradicts established guideline efficacy.

judgments.text: Treatment_regorafenib's pfs_months benefit shrinks linearly as cea_ng_ml increases (continuous interaction), even after adjusting for clinical and molecular covariates.
judgments.rationale: A continuous linear CEA-by-regorafenib interaction on PFS is not an established guideline-level predictive relationship.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope of pfs on ecog_ps).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a well-established prognostic factor for survival outcomes including PFS across virtually all oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV (metastatic) disease having worse PFS than non-stage IV is foundational staging-prognosis dogma in every oncology textbook.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Older age as a negative prognostic factor is a standard clinical consideration, though effect size varies; this is not novel.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have different pfs_months than male patients (sex_female=0).
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in PFS are commonly examined and reported in colorectal/oncology guidelines as a standard demographic variable.

judgments.text: Right-sided primary tumors (right_sided_primary=1) have shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse prognosis than left-sided is established consensus in colorectal cancer guidelines (e.g., NCCN, ESMO).

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as a negative prognostic marker is standard textbook knowledge in colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin (hypoalbuminemia) as a negative prognostic factor is well-established across oncology and reflected in indices like GPS/mGPS.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is standard guideline-level knowledge in many cancers.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor is foundational oncology knowledge and incorporated in standard assessments.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a negative prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established and embedded in scores like the Glasgow Prognostic Score.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with longer pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Contradicts established prognostic consensus that high NLR predicts worse, not longer, PFS.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Contradicts standard teaching that anemia (low hemoglobin) is associated with worse outcomes.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase as a marker of poor prognosis is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ast_u_l is associated with longer pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Contradicts standard view that elevated AST reflects hepatic dysfunction/metastasis and worse prognosis.

judgments.text: Higher alt_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT reflecting hepatic dysfunction and worse outcomes is consistent with established clinical understanding.

judgments.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Hyperbilirubinemia indicating hepatic compromise and worse prognosis is standard clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Renal dysfunction (elevated creatinine) predicting worse outcomes is established baseline prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher bun_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflecting renal dysfunction or volume status with worse prognosis is established.

judgments.text: Higher sodium_meq_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Contradicts the well-known association of hyponatremia (not hypernatremia) with worse cancer outcomes.

judgments.text: Higher potassium_meq_l is associated with longer pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: Potassium level is not an established directional prognostic marker for PFS in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher calcium_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (linear slope hypothesis: nonzero).
judgments.rationale: A linear association between serum calcium and PFS is not part of standard oncology guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with kras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive marker (especially in CRC and lung) is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with nras_mutation=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with nras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: A directional claim that NRAS mutation predicts longer PFS contradicts standard paradigm and is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with braf_v600e=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with braf_v600e=0.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as an adverse prognostic marker associated with shorter PFS is well-established in CRC and melanoma guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high is generally associated with better outcomes (especially with immunotherapy), so claiming shorter PFS is contrary to guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_amplified=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with her2_amplified=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as an adverse prognostic factor (without targeted therapy) is established textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with ntrk_fusion=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with ntrk_fusion=0.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusion predicting better outcomes via TRK inhibitor responsiveness is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months on average (positive main effect).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit (in RAS-wildtype CRC or HNSCC) is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab is associated with longer pfs_months on average (positive main effect).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab providing PFS benefit is established across multiple tumor types in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months on average across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab providing survival/PFS benefit is well-established guideline knowledge across many indications.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib is established standard-of-care for BRAF V600E-mutated colorectal cancer and melanoma, a well-known guideline association.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab plus tucatinib is an established guideline-endorsed regimen for HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer and HER2-positive colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months across the whole cohort.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is an established later-line therapy in metastatic colorectal cancer and other tumors per current guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs between KRAS-mutated and KRAS-wildtype patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation status as a predictor of cetuximab benefit is foundational guideline knowledge in metastatic colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs between NRAS-mutated and NRAS-wildtype patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation status as a predictor of (lack of) cetuximab benefit is established RAS-testing guideline knowledge in metastatic CRC.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs between BRAF V600E-mutated and BRAF wildtype patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting reduced benefit from anti-EGFR monotherapy is well-established in colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months differs between right-sided and left-sided primary tumors (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Primary tumor sidedness as a predictor of cetuximab benefit in RAS-wildtype mCRC is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is much larger (more positive) in MSI-high patients than in MSI-stable patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting pembrolizumab benefit is a guideline-level, tumor-agnostic FDA-approved indication.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs between BRAF V600E and BRAF wildtype patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: A BRAF V600E-specific differential effect of pembrolizumab beyond MSI status is a speculative subgroup interaction not reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months is positive and larger in BRAF V600E-mutated patients than in BRAF wildtype patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's positive efficacy specifically in BRAF V600E-mutated tumors is the textbook indication for the drug.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months is positive and larger in HER2-amplified patients than in HER2-non-amplified patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib benefit being larger in HER2-amplified colorectal/breast cancer is the established indication and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs between kras_mutation=1 and kras_mutation=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: While KRAS status is well-known to predict anti-EGFR response, a KRAS-modified bevacizumab effect is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs between right_sided_primary=1 and right_sided_primary=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness predicts anti-EGFR but not bevacizumab benefit in guidelines, so a sidedness-by-bevacizumab interaction would go beyond consensus.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is approved/used in metastatic (stage IV) colorectal cancer, so a stage-dependent effect reflects standard indication rather than novel science.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs between msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status guides immunotherapy decisions, not bevacizumab benefit, so an MSI-by-bevacizumab interaction is not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months differs between braf_v600e=1 and braf_v600e=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E informs targeted/EGFR therapy decisions; a BRAF-modified bevacizumab effect is not a guideline-established association.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between kras_mutation=1 and kras_mutation=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved as a refractory line agnostic to KRAS status, so KRAS-conditional benefit is a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between nras_mutation=1 and nras_mutation=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: NRAS-conditional regorafenib benefit is not described in guidelines, which use regorafenib without biomarker selection.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between braf_v600e=1 and braf_v600e=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRAF-conditional regorafenib effect is not part of guideline consensus, which treats regorafenib as biomarker-agnostic.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high modification of regorafenib effect is not an established guideline association and would represent a novel cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between her2_amplified=1 and her2_amplified=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a predictive biomarker for regorafenib response is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between ntrk_fusion=1 and ntrk_fusion=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusion modifying regorafenib efficacy is not a guideline-recognized interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between right_sided_primary=1 and right_sided_primary=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Primary tumor sidedness as a predictive biomarker specifically for regorafenib is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Stage is a standard prognostic/effect-modifying variable expected in any treatment context.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differential efficacy of regorafenib is not a guideline-established interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs between msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting pembrolizumab benefit is well-established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs between braf_v600e=1 and braf_v600e=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E modifying pembrolizumab efficacy independent of MSI status is not guideline-established.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs between right_sided_primary=1 and right_sided_primary=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness as a predictive biomarker for pembrolizumab response is not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs between kras_mutation=1 and kras_mutation=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation status as a modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not guideline-established.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs between nras_mutation=1 and nras_mutation=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation status as a modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not guideline-established.
