judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy by stage IV vs non-stage IV is not a guideline-established interaction in colorectal cancer outside of MSI status.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months differs between her2_amplified=1 and her2_amplified=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification modifying pembrolizumab efficacy is not an established guideline interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months differs between braf_v600e=1 and braf_v600e=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib efficacy in BRAF V600E-mutant colorectal cancer is the established indication per guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months differs between her2_amplified=1 and her2_amplified=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification modifying encorafenib efficacy is a cross-pathway interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months differs between msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status modifying encorafenib response is a speculative subgroup interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months differs between kras_mutation=1 and kras_mutation=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation modifying encorafenib (BRAF inhibitor) response is a cross-pathway interaction not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months differs between right_sided_primary=1 and right_sided_primary=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Tumor sidedness modifying encorafenib efficacy beyond BRAF status is not a guideline-established interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV status modifying encorafenib efficacy is not a guideline-established interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months differs between braf_v600e=1 and braf_v600e=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E modifying trastuzumab-tucatinib response is a cross-pathway interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months differs between her2_amplified=1 and her2_amplified=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib efficacy in HER2-amplified disease is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months differs between msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab-tucatinib benefit modification by MSI-high status in CRC is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months differs between kras_mutation=1 and kras_mutation=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: KRAS-mutation interaction with trastuzumab-tucatinib efficacy is not part of standard HER2+ CRC treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months differs between right_sided_primary=1 and right_sided_primary=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness as a modifier of anti-HER2 therapy response in CRC is increasingly recognized but tucatinib-trastuzumab efficacy is broadly accepted regardless; however right-sided vs left-sided differential effect for HER2-targeted therapy in CRC is discussed in current guidelines/consensus.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 patients (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV-specific interaction with tucatinib-trastuzumab benefit beyond the metastatic indication itself is not a guideline concept.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously KRAS-wildtype (kras_mutation=0) AND left-sided (right_sided_primary=0), i.e., the effect is much larger in this joint subgroup than outside it.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib benefit being concentrated in joint KRAS-wildtype + left-sided subgroup is not an established guideline concept; regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS/sidedness.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously KRAS-wildtype (kras_mutation=0) AND left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) AND BRAF-wildtype (braf_v600e=0); regorafenib substantially prolongs PFS in this triple-negative wildtype subgroup but not elsewhere.
judgments.rationale: Triple-wildtype (KRAS/BRAF-wt + left-sided) restriction of regorafenib benefit is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect is essentially absent (close to zero) in KRAS-mutated patients regardless of side.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is currently used irrespective of KRAS status; absence of effect in KRAS-mutated patients would contradict standard practice.

judgments.text: A regression tree fit on per-patient regorafenib treatment-effect estimates (tau-hat from a T-learner with gradient boosting) identifies KRAS-wildtype + left-sided primary as the dominant rule for higher regorafenib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Causal-forest/T-learner-derived rule identifying KRAS-wt + left-sided as primary regorafenib responders is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among all two-feature subgroups (using binary clinical/molecular features), the largest regorafenib treatment-effect subgroups jointly involve KRAS-wildtype and left-sided primary.
judgments.rationale: The claim that the largest two-feature regorafenib benefit subgroup involves KRAS-wt and left-sidedness is novel and not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In KRAS-mutated and right-sided (and BRAF-mutated) subgroups, the regorafenib treatment effect is small or absent.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not stratify regorafenib use by KRAS/BRAF/sidedness; absent benefit in these subgroups would be a novel biomarker-based restriction.

judgments.text: Regorafenib substantially prolongs pfs_months in patients who are KRAS-wildtype (kras_mutation=0), BRAF-wildtype (braf_v600e=0), AND left-sided (right_sided_primary=0); outside this triple-wildtype/left-sided subgroup, the effect is small or null.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved for refractory mCRC regardless of KRAS/BRAF/sidedness; a triple-wildtype/left-sided-restricted benefit is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model with all three regorafenib interactions (KRAS, BRAF, side), each individual interaction term is significantly negative, confirming that all three unfavorable variables independently attenuate the regorafenib effect.
judgments.rationale: Independent additive attenuation of regorafenib by KRAS, BRAF, and sidedness as separate interaction terms is a specific multivariable claim absent from any guideline.

judgments.text: In MSI-high patients alone, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT prolong pfs_months (null effect).
judgments.rationale: Null pembrolizumab effect in MSI-high CRC contradicts KEYNOTE-177 and current guideline consensus, making it a non-standard finding.

judgments.text: In BRAF V600E-mutated patients alone, treatment_encorafenib does NOT prolong pfs_months (null effect, contrary to BEACON-style expectation).
judgments.rationale: Null encorafenib effect in BRAF V600E mCRC contradicts BEACON CRC and established guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: In HER2-amplified patients alone, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does NOT prolong pfs_months (null effect).
judgments.rationale: Null trastuzumab/tucatinib effect in HER2-amplified mCRC contradicts MOUNTAINEER and HER2-directed guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In KRAS-wildtype patients alone, treatment_cetuximab does NOT prolong pfs_months (null effect).
judgments.rationale: Null cetuximab effect in KRAS-wildtype mCRC contradicts the foundational anti-EGFR biomarker paradigm in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the classic anti-EGFR best-responder subgroup (RAS-wildtype + BRAF-wildtype + left-sided), treatment_cetuximab does NOT prolong pfs_months in this dataset (null effect).
judgments.rationale: Null cetuximab effect in the canonical RAS-wt/BRAF-wt/left-sided best-responder subgroup contradicts the strongest guideline-level anti-EGFR indication.

judgments.text: FINAL HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib substantially prolongs pfs_months ONLY in patients who are simultaneously KRAS-wildtype (kras_mutation=0) AND BRAF V600E-wildtype (braf_v600e=0) AND left-sided primary (right_sided_primary=0) AND have low baseline CEA (cea_ng_ml <= 5). Inside this 4-feature subgroup the regorafenib effect is large (~+5 months PFS gain). Outside this subgroup (any of: KRAS-mutated, BRAF V600E-mutated, right-sided primary, or CEA > 5) the regorafenib effect is essentially null. The unfavorable values that suppress the regorafenib treatment effect are: kras_mutation=1, braf_v600e=1, right_sided_primary=1, and cea_ng_ml>5.
judgments.rationale: A four-feature (KRAS-wt + BRAF-wt + left-sided + low CEA) responder subgroup for regorafenib with ~5-month PFS gain is not a recognized clinical paradigm.

judgments.text: In contrast to regorafenib, the other five treatments in this dataset (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab/tucatinib) do not show a meaningful effect on pfs_months — neither overall nor inside the canonical biomarker-defined responder subgroup (RAS-wt+BRAF-wt+left for cetuximab; MSI-high for pembrolizumab; BRAF V600E for encorafenib; HER2-amplified for trastuzumab/tucatinib).
judgments.rationale: A blanket null for cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib even in their canonical biomarker subgroups contradicts multiple guideline-level associations.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Older age as a generic prognostic factor for shorter PFS is a standard, well-established clinical observation.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have different mean pfs_months than male patients in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in PFS are commonly explored and described in standard oncology literature, not beyond guideline-level discussion.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is textbook prognostic knowledge across virtually all cancers.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease being associated with worse PFS is a foundational staging concept in any oncology guideline.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as an adverse prognostic marker is a well-established, guideline-level association in GI and other cancers.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as an adverse prognostic factor is a standard, textbook observation across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic biomarker is a classical, guideline-level finding across many tumor types.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting poor outcomes is a well-established, textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of systemic inflammation and poor prognosis is widely recognized in standard oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR as an adverse prognostic marker is broadly established across solid tumors and routinely cited in the literature.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as a poor prognostic factor is well-established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Right-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse prognosis than left-sided is established consensus in colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: KRAS-mutated tumors (kras_mutation=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive marker is standard guideline knowledge in colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: NRAS-mutated tumors (nras_mutation=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than NRAS wild-type in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation predicting worse outcomes and resistance to anti-EGFR therapy is established guideline content.

judgments.text: BRAF V600E-mutant tumors (braf_v600e=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a poor prognostic marker is well-established in colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: MSI-high tumors (msi_high=1) have different mean pfs_months than MSS tumors in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a prognostic and predictive biomarker is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: HER2-amplified tumors (her2_amplified=1) have different mean pfs_months than non-amplified in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a prognostic/predictive biomarker is established across multiple tumor types in guidelines.

judgments.text: NTRK-fusion-positive tumors (ntrk_fusion=1) have different mean pfs_months than NTRK-negative in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusion as an actionable target with prognostic implications is established guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy (particularly in RAS wild-type colorectal cancer) is standard guideline-based knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's effect on PFS is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Generic treatment effect on PFS for an established agent; not a novel mechanistic or subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib is an established treatment (typically with cetuximab for BRAF V600E CRC); a generic PFS difference is guideline-level.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab + tucatinib is an established HER2-directed regimen; a bare PFS difference is not novel.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have LONGER mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is an established later-line therapy with known PFS benefit vs best supportive care; guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab benefit is larger in KRAS wild-type than in KRAS mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: KRAS wild-type predicting greater cetuximab benefit is the canonical textbook/guideline finding in CRC.

judgments.text: Within KRAS wild-type patients (kras_mutation=0), treatment_cetuximab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in KRAS wild-type CRC is a foundational guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within KRAS mutant patients (kras_mutation=1), treatment_cetuximab has no effect or a detrimental effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of cetuximab benefit (and potential harm) in KRAS-mutant CRC is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab benefit is greater in NRAS wild-type.
judgments.rationale: Extended RAS testing including NRAS is guideline-standard; greater EGFR-antibody benefit in NRAS wild-type is established.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab benefit is greater in BRAF V600E wild-type.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting reduced cetuximab benefit is reflected in current CRC guidelines and biomarker testing.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months: cetuximab benefit is greater for left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Left-sided primary tumors deriving greater anti-EGFR benefit is an established guideline-level finding in metastatic CRC.

judgments.text: In the canonical cetuximab-eligible subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 [left-sided]), treatment_cetuximab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in left-sided RAS/BRAF wild-type metastatic colorectal cancer is established guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: In pan-RAS/BRAF wild-type, RIGHT-sided patients (kras=0, nras=0, braf=0, right_sided_primary=1), treatment_cetuximab has no benefit on pfs_months (a contrast subgroup to h7.1).
judgments.rationale: Lack of cetuximab benefit in right-sided RAS/BRAF wild-type mCRC is well-established in guidelines and major trials (e.g., CALGB/SWOG 80405, FIRE-3 sidedness analyses).

judgments.text: In any-RAS/BRAF mutant patients, treatment_cetuximab is associated with no benefit (or harm) on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Absence of anti-EGFR benefit (and potential harm) in RAS/BRAF mutant mCRC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months: pembrolizumab benefit is larger in MSI-high than MSS patients.
judgments.rationale: Larger pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high vs MSS colorectal cancer is established consensus (KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients (msi_high=1), treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high mCRC is guideline-standard first-line therapy.

judgments.text: Within MSS patients (msi_high=0), treatment_pembrolizumab has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of pembrolizumab monotherapy benefit in MSS mCRC is well-established.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months: encorafenib benefit is concentrated in BRAF V600E mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit limited to BRAF V600E mutant mCRC is guideline-level knowledge (BEACON CRC).

judgments.text: Within BRAF V600E patients (braf_v600e=1), treatment_encorafenib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) efficacy in BRAF V600E mCRC is FDA-approved and in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within BRAF wild-type patients (braf_v600e=0), treatment_encorafenib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib is mechanistically and clinically restricted to BRAF V600E tumors; no benefit in wild-type is standard knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months: benefit is concentrated in HER2-amplified tumors.
judgments.rationale: Tucatinib + trastuzumab benefit confined to HER2-amplified mCRC is guideline-established (MOUNTAINEER trial).

judgments.text: Within HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1), treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HER2-targeted therapy (trastuzumab + tucatinib) benefit in HER2-amplified disease is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within HER2 non-amplified patients (her2_amplified=0), treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of HER2-targeted therapy benefit in HER2-non-amplified disease is the standard biomarker paradigm.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A KRAS-mutation-conditional bevacizumab effect is not part of guideline consensus, which generally treats bevacizumab as biomarker-agnostic.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a bevacizumab effect modifier is not standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high as a modifier of bevacizumab efficacy goes beyond established paradigm; MSI is tied to immunotherapy, not VEGF blockade.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a bevacizumab effect modifier is not part of standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × kras_mutation interaction: regorafenib benefit is larger in KRAS wild-type than KRAS mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is generally considered biomarker-agnostic in refractory CRC; a KRAS-conditional benefit is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × braf_v600e interaction: regorafenib benefit is larger in BRAF wild-type than BRAF V600E patients.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × msi_high interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib efficacy by MSI-high status is not an established guideline-level predictive biomarker in colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × right_sided_primary interaction: regorafenib benefit is larger in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) than right-sided primaries.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness as a predictive biomarker is well-established for anti-EGFR therapy, but regorafenib sidedness effect is debated; however, left-sided benefit framing aligns with established sidedness paradigm and is discussed in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A stage IV-specific modification of regorafenib effect beyond its standard refractory mCRC indication is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.rationale: Cetuximab is indicated in stage IV (metastatic) CRC by definition, so this 'interaction' restates the standard indication.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Reduced cetuximab benefit in right-sided primaries is well-established and explicitly reflected in NCCN/ESMO guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation predicting lack of cetuximab benefit is the canonical guideline biomarker for anti-EGFR therapy.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation predicting resistance to cetuximab is established RAS-testing guideline content.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting poor response to cetuximab monotherapy is established and in current guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status modifying cetuximab benefit is not part of standard anti-EGFR guideline criteria (which focus on RAS/BRAF and sidedness).

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a mechanism of anti-EGFR (cetuximab) resistance in CRC is now reflected in NCCN/guideline testing recommendations.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_cetuximab × ntrk_fusion interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: An NTRK-fusion–conditional cetuximab effect is not described in oncology guidelines; NTRK fusions guide TRK inhibitor use, not anti-EGFR therapy.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × sex_female interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: A sex-by-bevacizumab interaction on PFS is not part of guideline-level prescribing criteria.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's indication is essentially metastatic/stage IV disease, so a stage-IV interaction reflects its established use case rather than a novel finding.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Tumor sidedness is a guideline biomarker for anti-EGFR therapy, not bevacizumab; a sidedness-by-bevacizumab interaction would go beyond standard paradigm.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines recommend bevacizumab regardless of KRAS status, so a KRAS-conditional bevacizumab benefit would be a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab efficacy is considered RAS-agnostic per guidelines, so an NRAS-conditional interaction is not established.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E is prognostic and guides BRAF-targeted combinations, not a recognized modifier of bevacizumab benefit in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high primarily directs immunotherapy decisions; an MSI-conditional bevacizumab effect is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab benefit modified by HER2 amplification is not part of standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_bevacizumab × ntrk_fusion interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab efficacy modulated by NTRK fusion status is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × sex_female interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of pembrolizumab efficacy is a speculative subgroup claim not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is standardly used in advanced/stage IV disease, so a stage IV interaction reflects established practice.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary as a modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is a speculative tumor-sidedness claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation as a modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not part of standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E modifying pembrolizumab benefit (beyond MSI context) is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting pembrolizumab benefit is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification modifying pembrolizumab efficacy is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × ntrk_fusion interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in NTRK-fusion tumors is not a guideline-recognized predictive interaction; NTRK fusions guide TRK inhibitors, not PD-1 therapy.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × sex_female interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of encorafenib efficacy is not part of established BRAF-mutant CRC treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV-specific modification of encorafenib effect beyond its standard metastatic CRC indication is not a guideline-level predictive interaction.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Primary tumor sidedness as a modifier of encorafenib benefit is not codified in guidelines for BRAF-mutant CRC.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a modifier of encorafenib effect is speculative since encorafenib targets BRAF V600E and KRAS/BRAF mutations are typically mutually exclusive.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation modifying encorafenib response is not a guideline-level interaction in CRC.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E is the established predictive biomarker for encorafenib efficacy in colorectal cancer per current guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × msi_high interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a modifier of encorafenib benefit is not part of guideline paradigm; MSI-H predicts immunotherapy response, not BRAF inhibitor benefit.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a modifier of encorafenib efficacy is not a guideline-level predictive interaction.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_encorafenib × ntrk_fusion interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusion as a modifier of encorafenib benefit is not a guideline-recognized predictive interaction.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × sex_female interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differential efficacy of tucatinib/trastuzumab in HER2+ CRC is not a guideline-established predictor.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: A stage IV-conditional differential effect of tucatinib/trastuzumab beyond its metastatic indication is not a guideline claim.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Primary tumor sidedness as an effect modifier specifically for HER2-directed tucatinib/trastuzumab is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: RAS mutation status (including KRAS) is an established eligibility/efficacy modifier for HER2-directed therapy in CRC per NCCN guidance.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation, like KRAS, is a guideline-recognized exclusion/negative predictor for HER2-directed therapy in CRC.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a specific effect modifier for tucatinib/trastuzumab response in HER2+ CRC is not a standard guideline statement.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × msi_high interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status modifying tucatinib/trastuzumab efficacy is not an established paradigm beyond MSI-H favoring immunotherapy.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification is the canonical, guideline-established biomarker selecting patients for tucatinib/trastuzumab.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib × ntrk_fusion interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusion status as an effect modifier of tucatinib/trastuzumab is not a recognized paradigm.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × sex_female interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differential efficacy of regorafenib in refractory CRC is not an established guideline predictor.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib benefit being modified by stage IV status is not a guideline-established predictive interaction.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Sidedness as a predictive biomarker for regorafenib (rather than for anti-EGFR therapy) is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation status is not a guideline-established predictor of regorafenib efficacy, unlike its role with anti-EGFR agents.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × nras_mutation interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation as a predictive modifier of regorafenib benefit is not part of standard treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a predictive biomarker for regorafenib response is not in guidelines (BRAF directs encorafenib-based therapy, not regorafenib).

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × msi_high interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a predictive modifier of regorafenib benefit is not guideline-established (MSI-H directs immunotherapy choice instead).

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × her2_amplified interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a predictive biomarker for regorafenib is not established; HER2 directs HER2-targeted regimens, not regorafenib selection.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_regorafenib × ntrk_fusion interaction on pfs_months (binary-feature interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusion as a modifier of regorafenib benefit is not guideline-established; NTRK directs TRK inhibitor therapy.

judgments.text: In any progressively narrower cetuximab-eligible subgroup (left-sided + KRAS wt; left+KRAS+NRAS wt; left+pan-RAS/BRAF wt; left+pan-wt+MSS), treatment_cetuximab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in left-sided, progressively RAS/BRAF wild-type CRC is the established guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: In the right-sided pan-RAS/BRAF wild-type subgroup (right_sided_primary=1, kras=0, nras=0, braf=0), treatment_cetuximab does NOT improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of cetuximab benefit in right-sided tumors even when pan-RAS/BRAF wild-type is the established guideline-recognized sidedness effect.

judgments.text: In KRAS-mutant left-sided patients (right_sided_primary=0, kras_mutation=1), treatment_cetuximab does NOT improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of cetuximab benefit in KRAS-mutant colorectal cancer is established guideline knowledge regardless of sidedness.

judgments.text: In BRAF V600E left-sided patients (right_sided_primary=0, braf_v600e=1), treatment_cetuximab does NOT improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab monotherapy lacking benefit in BRAF V600E mCRC is well-established and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within BRAF V600E patients further restricted to KRAS wild-type, treatment_encorafenib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) is the guideline-standard for BRAF V600E mCRC, typically used in KRAS wild-type patients, so this is established.

judgments.text: Within BRAF V600E patients further restricted to MSS (msi_high=0), treatment_encorafenib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E mCRC is overwhelmingly MSS, and encorafenib benefit in this population is the standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within BRAF V600E patients further restricted to ECOG 0-1 (ecog_ps<=1), treatment_encorafenib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E patients with good performance status reflects standard trial-eligible populations and guideline use.

judgments.text: Within BRAF V600E patients further restricted to left-sided, treatment_encorafenib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Restricting encorafenib benefit specifically to left-sided BRAF V600E tumors is a sidedness-conditional claim not established in guidelines, since BRAF V600E mCRC is predominantly right-sided.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients further restricted to KRAS wild-type, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high mCRC is guideline-standard and not restricted by KRAS status.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients further restricted to BRAF wild-type, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high mCRC is established regardless of BRAF status per KEYNOTE-177 and guidelines.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients further restricted to left-sided, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A sidedness-conditional pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is not part of guideline consensus, since MSI-high CRC skews right-sided yet immunotherapy benefit is not stratified by sidedness.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients further restricted to ECOG 0-1, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high mCRC patients with good performance status reflects standard trial-eligible guideline use.

judgments.text: Within HER2-amplified patients further restricted to KRAS wild-type, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Restricting trastuzumab-tucatinib benefit to HER2-amplified KRAS-wild-type is a subgroup-conditional refinement beyond the standard HER2-amplified, RAS/BRAF-wildtype guideline statement, but overlaps heavily with current MOUNTAINEER-derived guidance.

judgments.text: Within HER2-amplified patients further restricted to BRAF wild-type, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: HER2-amplified, BRAF-wild-type benefit from trastuzumab plus tucatinib reflects established MOUNTAINEER-based guideline criteria for this regimen.

judgments.text: Within HER2-amplified patients further restricted to left-sided, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A sidedness-conditional benefit of trastuzumab-tucatinib within HER2-amplified disease is not part of standard guideline language, which selects on HER2/RAS/BRAF status rather than primary tumor side.

judgments.text: Within HER2-amplified patients further restricted to ECOG 0-1, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning HER2-directed benefit specifically on ECOG 0-1 performance status is a subgroup claim beyond the molecularly defined indication in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_bevacizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months in left-sided patients (right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab benefit in left-sided metastatic CRC is a well-known guideline-level consideration, particularly relative to anti-EGFR alternatives.

judgments.text: Treatment_bevacizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months in KRAS-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab activity in KRAS-wild-type disease is established and reflected in standard first-line chemotherapy-plus-bevacizumab recommendations.

judgments.text: Treatment_bevacizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months in fit patients (ecog_ps<=1).
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab use in fit (ECOG 0-1) patients receiving systemic therapy is standard guideline practice.

judgments.text: Treatment_bevacizumab is associated with LONGER pfs_months in stage IV patients.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's PFS benefit in stage IV CRC is a foundational, guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit (treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months) is LARGER in patients with low cea_ng_ml (below the cohort median) than in patients with high CEA.
judgments.rationale: A CEA-stratified differential PFS benefit from regorafenib is a biomarker-conditional claim not present in current guidelines, which recommend regorafenib based on prior-line exposure rather than CEA.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is similar across albumin_g_dl strata (above vs below median).
judgments.rationale: Claiming homogeneity of regorafenib benefit across albumin strata is a subgroup-level mechanistic statement absent from guidelines, which do not stratify regorafenib indication by serum albumin.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is similar across ECOG 0-1 vs ECOG 2.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup consistency of regorafenib benefit across ECOG 0-1 vs 2 reflects standard prognostic stratification reported in CORRECT-style trials and guideline discussions.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is similar across ldh_u_l strata.
judgments.rationale: Claim of homogeneous regorafenib PFS benefit across LDH strata is a subgroup-conditional assertion not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is similar across weight_loss_pct_6mo strata.
judgments.rationale: Equivalent regorafenib benefit across weight-loss strata is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline-level evidence.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is larger when restricted to ECOG<=1 AND high albumin (>= median) than in the complement.
judgments.rationale: A composite ECOG-and-albumin-defined subgroup with enhanced regorafenib benefit is a speculative cross-marker interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is largest in good-prognostic patients defined by ECOG<=1 AND high albumin AND low LDH AND low CEA simultaneously.
judgments.rationale: Maximal regorafenib benefit in a four-marker good-prognosis subgroup (ECOG, albumin, LDH, CEA) is a speculative multi-marker interaction beyond standard practice.

judgments.text: In any patient with at least one unfavorable prognostic value (ECOG=2 OR low albumin OR high LDH), regorafenib still has a meaningful PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Asserting preserved regorafenib benefit in any patient with at least one unfavorable marker is a subgroup-conditional claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Adjusting for age, ECOG, stage, sidedness, CEA, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, and hemoglobin, treatment_cetuximab still does not improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab lacking PFS benefit in an unselected/RAS-unspecified mCRC population aligns with established guideline restrictions to RAS wild-type left-sided disease.

judgments.text: Adjusting for the same covariates, treatment_bevacizumab does not improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab providing only modest/uncertain PFS gains after covariate adjustment is consistent with established guideline-level expectations.

judgments.text: Adjusting for the same covariates, treatment_pembrolizumab does not improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab not improving PFS in an unselected (likely MSS-dominant) CRC cohort matches established guideline knowledge restricting benefit to MSI-H/dMMR.

judgments.text: Adjusting for the same covariates, treatment_encorafenib does not improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib lacking PFS benefit in an unselected population without BRAF V600E selection and cetuximab pairing aligns with guideline-established use.

judgments.text: Adjusting for the same covariates, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does not improve pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Contradicts established efficacy of trastuzumab+tucatinib in HER2-amplified disease (e.g., MOUNTAINEER in mCRC, HER2CLIMB in breast), so a null PFS finding is unexpected.

judgments.text: Adjusting for the same covariates, treatment_regorafenib still has a strong positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's PFS benefit in refractory mCRC (CORRECT trial) is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_cetuximab on pfs_months across binary biomarkers and continuous-lab median splits: at least one subgroup will show a clinically meaningful effect direction or magnitude that diverges from the marginal effect.
judgments.rationale: Speculative subgroup-conditional heterogeneity claim for cetuximab beyond the standard RAS/BRAF/sidedness predictors documented in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months across binary biomarkers and continuous-lab median splits: at least one subgroup will show a clinically meaningful effect direction or magnitude that diverges from the marginal effect.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab guidelines do not endorse predictive biomarkers, so any subgroup-divergent effect is a novel heterogeneity claim.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months across binary biomarkers and continuous-lab median splits: at least one subgroup will show a clinically meaningful effect direction or magnitude that diverges from the marginal effect.
judgments.rationale: Beyond MSI/PD-L1/TMB, additional biomarker-defined heterogeneity for pembrolizumab is speculative and not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_encorafenib on pfs_months across binary biomarkers and continuous-lab median splits: at least one subgroup will show a clinically meaningful effect direction or magnitude that diverges from the marginal effect.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-divergent effects of encorafenib beyond BRAF V600E status would go beyond current guideline framing.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib on pfs_months across binary biomarkers and continuous-lab median splits: at least one subgroup will show a clinically meaningful effect direction or magnitude that diverges from the marginal effect.
judgments.rationale: Heterogeneity of trastuzumab+tucatinib across non-HER2 biomarkers/lab splits is speculative and beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months across binary biomarkers and continuous-lab median splits: at least one subgroup will show a clinically meaningful effect direction or magnitude that diverges from the marginal effect.
judgments.rationale: Predictive biomarker-defined heterogeneity for regorafenib is not established in any guideline.

judgments.text: Even with progressively narrower restrictions (left-sided, then KRAS wt, then NRAS wt, then BRAF wt, then MSS), treatment_cetuximab does NOT improve pfs_months in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Contradicts established efficacy of cetuximab in left-sided RAS/BRAF wild-type MSS mCRC, an unexpected null in the optimal subset.

judgments.text: Treatment_encorafenib does NOT improve pfs_months in BRAF V600E patients (braf_v600e=1), even when further restricted to KRAS wild-type, in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Contradicts BEACON CRC and guideline-endorsed activity of encorafenib in BRAF V600E mCRC, particularly in KRAS wild-type.

judgments.text: Treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT improve pfs_months in MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit being established in MSI-high tumors is guideline-level consensus, so a null finding restates a known paradigm rather than offering a novel claim.

judgments.text: Treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does NOT improve pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified=1) in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab plus tucatinib activity in HER2-amplified disease is a standard guideline-endorsed association, so this is not novel.

judgments.text: Treatment_regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated almost entirely in patients who are simultaneously KRAS-wild-type (kras_mutation=0), BRAF-V600E-wild-type (braf_v600e=0), left-sided primary (right_sided_primary=0), and have low pre-treatment CEA (cea_ng_ml < cohort median ≈ 4.44 ng/mL). In that subgroup the effect is dramatic (~+5 months PFS). In the COMPLEMENT (any unfavorable value: KRAS-mutant OR BRAF-V600E OR right-sided OR high CEA), the regorafenib effect on PFS is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conditional subgroup (KRAS-WT AND BRAF-WT AND left-sided AND low CEA) driving essentially all regorafenib PFS benefit is a speculative cross-pathway claim that goes beyond guideline statements about regorafenib in refractory mCRC.

judgments.text: Adding NRAS wild-type (nras_mutation=0) to the regorafenib responsive subgroup definition does not materially change the magnitude of the regorafenib benefit, because NRAS mutation is rare (~3%) in the cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claiming NRAS status is immaterial to regorafenib benefit due to low NRAS prevalence is a cohort-specific mechanistic/quantitative assertion not found in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation between continuous age and PFS).
judgments.rationale: Older age and PFS associations are routinely discussed in oncology guidelines and prognostic discussions, not novel.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have different mean pfs_months than male patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in PFS are a standard demographic comparison reported in trials and guidelines.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having shorter PFS than non-stage IV is a foundational oncology paradigm, not novel.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse PFS is well-established prognostic knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: right_sided_primary=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than left-sided patients (right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse outcomes (e.g., in colorectal cancer) is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with kras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation status as a prognostic/predictive biomarker is standard in current oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with nras_mutation=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with nras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation status is a well-established biomarker in colorectal cancer guidelines for anti-EGFR therapy decisions.

judgments.text: Patients with braf_v600e=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with braf_v600e=0.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a poor prognostic marker and therapeutic target is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a prognostic and immunotherapy-predictive biomarker is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_amplified=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with her2_amplified=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a prognostic and predictive biomarker is foundational guideline-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with ntrk_fusion=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with ntrk_fusion=0.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions are well-established actionable drivers with known prognostic/predictive implications for TRK inhibitor therapy in guidelines.

judgments.text: cea_ng_ml is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: CEA is a standard tumor marker whose association with disease burden and outcomes is well-established in GI/oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker for survival/PFS is textbook oncology consensus.

judgments.text: ldh_u_l is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic biomarker is well-established across multiple tumor types in guidelines.

judgments.text: weight_loss_pct_6mo is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: crp_mg_l is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory marker associated with worse oncologic outcomes is well-established in the literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: nlr is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a prognostic marker for survival/PFS is widely established across solid tumors.

judgments.text: hemoglobin_g_dl is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as a negative prognostic factor in cancer is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflecting bone/liver metastases and worse prognosis is established oncology consensus.

judgments.text: ast_u_l is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST as a marker of liver involvement/dysfunction associated with worse outcomes is standard clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: alt_u_l is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: ALT as a routine liver function marker correlating with PFS reflects general performance/organ function status, a standard clinical consideration.

judgments.text: total_bilirubin_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Bilirubin correlating with PFS reflects standard hepatic function/performance status considerations already in guidelines.

judgments.text: creatinine_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine correlating with PFS reflects standard renal function/performance status considerations in oncology practice.

judgments.text: bun_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: BUN correlating with PFS reflects standard renal function/performance status considerations already established.

judgments.text: sodium_meq_l is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Sodium correlating with PFS (hyponatremia as a poor prognostic marker) is a well-established association in oncology.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Potassium level as a PFS predictor is not a standard guideline-level association in oncology.

judgments.text: calcium_mg_dl is correlated with pfs_months (any direction).
judgments.rationale: Calcium correlating with PFS (hypercalcemia of malignancy as poor prognosis) is a well-established oncology association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_cetuximab.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy on PFS is a standard guideline-level association in EGFR-pathway-driven cancers.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_bevacizumab.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab efficacy on PFS is a standard, guideline-established association across multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy on PFS is a standard, guideline-established association in immunotherapy-eligible cancers.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib=1 have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_encorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's PFS benefit in BRAF V600E mCRC is established guideline knowledge, and a marginal association is not novel.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib activity in HER2-amplified mCRC is established (MOUNTAINEER) and reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib=1 have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's PFS effect in refractory mCRC is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all features simultaneously (OLS), age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l and treatment_regorafenib remain independently associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A specific multivariable claim that this exact set of clinical/molecular/lab covariates remain independently associated with PFS is not a guideline-level statement.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in patients with KRAS wild-type, NRAS wild-type, BRAF wild-type tumors (i.e., kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0).
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in RAS/BRAF wild-type mCRC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in msi_high=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is standard guideline knowledge (KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months in braf_v600e=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E mCRC is standard guideline knowledge (BEACON).

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months in her2_amplified=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified mCRC is now established in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided tumors (kras=0, nras=0, braf_v600e=0, right_sided_primary=0).
judgments.rationale: Preferential cetuximab benefit in left-sided RAS/BRAF wild-type mCRC is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab is harmful or neutral in kras_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Lack of benefit/harm of anti-EGFR therapy in KRAS-mutant mCRC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is neutral or harmful in msi_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's lack of benefit in MSI-stable/MMR-proficient CRC is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for clinical covariates, the canonical targeted-therapy associations (cetuximab in RAS-WT, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, encorafenib in BRAF V600E, trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2+) remain non-significant.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the canonical biomarker-targeted associations fail to replicate after adjustment contradicts established paradigm and is not in any guideline.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab has no effect on pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse bevacizumab for PFS benefit in mCRC, so a null overall effect would be a paradigm-contradicting finding.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab has no effect on pfs_months in left-sided tumors.
judgments.rationale: A sidedness-conditional null for bevacizumab is a subgroup-specific claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months in HER2+, RAS/BRAF wild-type tumors (her2_amplified=1, kras=0, nras=0, braf_v600e=0).
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified, RAS/BRAF wild-type mCRC is the established label/guideline indication (MOUNTAINEER).

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months versus no regorafenib in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's modest PFS improvement in refractory mCRC is established from CORRECT and is in guidelines.

judgments.text: An exhaustive treatment-by-biomarker interaction screen will reveal that treatment_regorafenib has highly significant interactions with kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary (its benefit is modified by these features), while other treatments will lack strong interactions.
judgments.rationale: A specific pattern of regorafenib effect modification by KRAS, BRAF, and sidedness (with other drugs lacking interactions) is not guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months in kras_mutation=0 patients, but not in kras_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: KRAS-status-conditional regorafenib benefit is a speculative subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) patients, but not in right-sided patients.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness-conditional regorafenib benefit is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months in braf_v600e=0 patients, but not in braf_v600e=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRAF-V600E-conditional regorafenib benefit is a speculative subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is abolished in the highest tertile of cea_ng_ml.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib selection by CEA tertile is not part of any mCRC guideline; CEA is prognostic but not a predictive biomarker for regorafenib.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab harms pfs_months in braf_v600e=1 patients (i.e., negative effect in this subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not state that bevacizumab is harmful in BRAF V600E mCRC; bevacizumab is still used across mutation statuses.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab has no significant effect on pfs_months in any of the major clinical subgroups (sidedness, KRAS status, MSI, HER2, sex, stage).
judgments.rationale: Claiming bevacizumab has no benefit in any major subgroup contradicts the guideline-level expectation of modest, broadly distributed benefit.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months in patients who are kras_mutation=0, nras_mutation=0, braf_v600e=0, AND right_sided_primary=0 (the joint RAS/BRAF-WT, left-sided subgroup), with a larger effect than in either single-modifier subgroup.
judgments.rationale: RAS/BRAF-WT left-sided is the predictive profile for anti-EGFR therapy, not regorafenib; this conditional, super-additive regorafenib effect is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) patients regardless of mutation status.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness is not an established predictive biomarker for regorafenib benefit in any current mCRC guideline.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months most strongly in kras_mutation=0, nras_mutation=0, braf_v600e=0, right_sided_primary=0, low-CEA (cea_ng_ml below median) patients.
judgments.rationale: A four-feature joint subgroup (RAS/BRAF-WT, left-sided, low CEA) selecting regorafenib responders is not found in any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: Within the RAS/BRAF-WT, left-sided subgroup, the treatment_regorafenib effect is much smaller in patients with cea_ng_ml in the highest tertile than in those with low/medium cea_ng_ml.
judgments.rationale: Within-subgroup attenuation of regorafenib effect by high CEA is a speculative cross-feature interaction not reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is abolished if EITHER kras_mutation=1 OR right_sided_primary=1; the effect is restored only when both are zero. Tested as a three-way interaction treatment_regorafenib × kras_mutation × right_sided_primary, where the three-way coefficient is positive (indicating that 'KRAS+ AND right-sided' brings the rego effect back to zero rather than further negative).
judgments.rationale: A three-way KRAS × sidedness × regorafenib interaction with non-monotone restoration of effect is a mechanistic claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: In a joint multivariable model with covariate adjustment and interactions of treatment_regorafenib with kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, and continuous cea_ng_ml, all four interaction terms remain negative and highly significant, confirming that each independently attenuates the regorafenib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Independent attenuation of regorafenib benefit by KRAS, BRAF, sidedness, and CEA in a joint model is not established; guidelines apply regorafenib without such stratification.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months in the subgroup defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml below the upper tertile (~14.5 ng/mL); the unfavorable values of any of these four features (KRAS mutation, BRAF V600E, right-sided primary, or high CEA) suppress the benefit. This is the best-supported treatment-effect subgroup for the regorafenib–pfs_months pair.
judgments.rationale: A composite four-feature predictive subgroup for regorafenib PFS benefit is well beyond current guideline-level paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: The regorafenib benefit in the kras=0 AND braf=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND CEA-non-high subgroup remains large and significant after adjusting for age_years, sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, nlr, crp_mg_l, hemoglobin_g_dl.
judgments.rationale: A regorafenib benefit conditional on the specific four-way subgroup (KRAS-wt, BRAF-wt, left-sided, non-high CEA) that persists after adjustment for clinical/lab covariates is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not found in mCRC guidelines, which position regorafenib as a later-line option without such predictive biomarker stratification.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab does NOT improve pfs_months in the same kras=0, braf=0, right_sided_primary=0, CEA-non-high subgroup, confirming that the regorafenib benefit is treatment-specific rather than an artifact of patient selection.
judgments.rationale: Although cetuximab's lack of benefit in right-sided or RAS/BRAF-mutant disease is guideline-established, the specific contrast claiming cetuximab fails to improve PFS in the left-sided KRAS/BRAF-wildtype non-high-CEA subgroup contradicts standard guideline expectations (where this subgroup is the canonical responder population) and is therefore a non-standard, novel claim.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect of ecog_ps on PFS).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic association across oncology.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring shorter PFS than non-stage IV is standard staging-based prognostication.

judgments.text: right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided primaries.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse outcomes than left-sided is well-established in colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: sex_female has no meaningful association with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of meaningful sex association with PFS aligns with guideline-level consensus and is not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Older age predicting longer PFS contradicts standard prognostic expectations and is not a guideline-endorsed association.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA predicting shorter PFS is a well-established prognostic marker in colorectal and other GI cancers.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin associating with longer PFS reflects the established prognostic value of nutritional status.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Greater weight loss predicting shorter PFS is standard cachexia/prognostic teaching in oncology.

judgments.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation conferring worse PFS is a well-known negative prognostic and predictive biomarker in CRC and NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting shorter PFS is a textbook poor-prognosis biomarker in colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: msi_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting better outcomes (especially with immunotherapy) is well-established guideline-level knowledge in colorectal and other cancers.

judgments.text: her2_amplified=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than her2_amplified=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a poor prognostic marker (in the absence of HER2-targeted therapy) is standard textbook oncology.

judgments.text: ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than ntrk_fusion=0.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions predicting response to TRK inhibitors and association with prolonged PFS on targeted therapy is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: nras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than nras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutations as a negative prognostic/predictive marker (e.g., resistance to anti-EGFR therapy in CRC) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_regorafenib=0 (univariate).
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is a guideline-approved later-line therapy in CRC with established PFS benefit versus placebo.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab has well-established efficacy in RAS-wildtype colorectal cancer per standard guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's PFS benefit in colorectal cancer is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high/dMMR colorectal cancer is established first-line guideline therapy.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) for BRAF V600E-mutant CRC is a standard guideline-recommended regimen.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 has a non-zero univariate effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab plus tucatinib for HER2-amplified CRC is now an established guideline-supported regimen (MOUNTAINEER).

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for all baseline features and treatments, age_years remains independently associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Older age is conventionally associated with worse, not longer, PFS, but the claim of an independent age effect after adjustment is a basic prognostic observation, not a novel mechanistic insight beyond guideline-level discussion.

judgments.text: After adjustment, ecog_ps, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and braf_v600e remain independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Poor ECOG, stage IV, right-sided primary, KRAS, and BRAF V600E mutations are all well-established adverse prognostic factors documented in CRC guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: After adjustment, albumin_g_dl, cea_ng_ml, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and ldh_u_l remain independently associated with pfs_months in their expected directions.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin, elevated CEA, weight loss, and elevated LDH are standard guideline/textbook prognostic markers in advanced CRC.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_regorafenib remains the only systemic-therapy variable with a non-zero main effect on pfs_months; the other five treatments have null adjusted main effects.
judgments.rationale: Claiming regorafenib is the only treatment with a non-null main effect while five other standard regimens have null adjusted effects contradicts guideline expectations and is a non-standard, dataset-specific assertion.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in the kras_mutation=0 (RAS-WT) subgroup; cetuximab x kras_mutation interaction is significant with positive effect in KRAS-WT and null/negative effect in KRAS-mutant.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit restricted to RAS wild-type tumors is a cornerstone guideline-level biomarker selection rule in metastatic CRC.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in braf_v600e=0; cetuximab x braf_v600e interaction is negative.
judgments.rationale: Lack of cetuximab benefit (and worse outcomes) in BRAF V600E-mutant CRC is established in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab is more effective in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) tumors than right-sided.
judgments.rationale: Greater anti-EGFR (cetuximab) efficacy in left-sided versus right-sided CRC is a well-established guideline-endorsed predictive factor.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in msi_high=1; pembro x msi_high interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy specifically in MSI-high/dMMR CRC is a guideline-level, FDA-approved biomarker-based indication.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect varies by ecog_ps with greater benefit in ECOG 0.
judgments.rationale: An ECOG-PS-conditional differential benefit of pembrolizumab in CRC is a speculative subgroup interaction not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in braf_v600e=1; encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's efficacy is restricted to BRAF V600E-mutant CRC per its labeled, guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib effect is modified by kras_mutation status.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib's activity in BRAF V600E mCRC and the role of KRAS/RAS status as a known modifier of EGFR-pathway-directed therapy is established; KRAS as effect modifier of BRAF-targeted therapy is discussed in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in her2_amplified=1.
judgments.rationale: Tucatinib plus trastuzumab is guideline-endorsed specifically for HER2-amplified/positive mCRC, so benefit in HER2-amplified patients is standard consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib effect varies with ldh_u_l.
judgments.rationale: LDH as a continuous effect modifier of tucatinib/trastuzumab efficacy is not part of established guideline criteria for this regimen.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab effect is modified by kras_mutation status.
judgments.rationale: KRAS status is a well-known consideration in anti-angiogenic versus anti-EGFR selection, and KRAS-related modification of bevacizumab benefit has been extensively discussed in guidelines/textbooks.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab effect is modified by braf_v600e status.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a specific effect modifier of bevacizumab benefit is not an established guideline-level predictive biomarker for bevacizumab.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab effect is modified by right_sided_primary.
