judgments.rationale: While sidedness modifies anti-EGFR benefit per guidelines, sidedness as a predictive modifier specifically for bevacizumab is not established consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by kras_mutation: larger benefit in KRAS-WT than KRAS-mut.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is approved regardless of KRAS status and KRAS is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker for regorafenib benefit.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by braf_v600e: larger benefit in BRAF-WT than BRAF V600E.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E is not an established predictive biomarker for regorafenib efficacy in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by right_sided_primary: larger benefit in left-sided than right-sided.
judgments.rationale: Primary tumor sidedness is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker for regorafenib benefit.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by nras_mutation.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation status is not an established predictive biomarker for regorafenib efficacy in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect varies with cea_ng_ml: smaller (or no) benefit at higher CEA levels.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib efficacy modulation by baseline CEA level is not an established guideline-level biomarker for this agent.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect varies with albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, or nlr.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib effect modification by albumin/LDH/CRP/NLR goes beyond standard prognostic interpretation into predictive heterogeneity not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients who are right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided) AND kras_mutation=0 (KRAS-WT) AND braf_v600e=0 (BRAF-WT), treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months relative to no regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is positioned as a later-line agent regardless of sidedness/RAS/BRAF; restricting benefit to the left-sided, KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT triple-negative-for-unfavorable-features subgroup is not guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Among patients who are right-sided OR KRAS-mutant OR BRAF-mutant (any single one of these unfavorable features), treatment_regorafenib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming complete absence of regorafenib effect in any right-sided OR RAS/BRAF-mutant patient is a stronger, non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within the left-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT subgroup, regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in patients with cea_ng_ml at or below ~5 ng/mL and is absent in patients with cea_ng_ml > ~5.
judgments.rationale: A CEA≤5 threshold within an already-restricted molecular/anatomic subgroup as a regorafenib response predictor is speculative and not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the four-way subgroup (right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND cea_ng_ml<=5), treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately 5 months even after adjustment for all other covariates and concomitant treatments.
judgments.rationale: A ~5-month covariate-adjusted PFS gain in a four-way-defined subgroup is a specific, non-guideline magnitude claim for regorafenib.

judgments.text: The regorafenib benefit in this four-way subgroup is independent of how many other treatments are co-administered.
judgments.rationale: Independence of regorafenib benefit from co-administered therapies within this subgroup is a cross-treatment interaction claim not in standard sources.

judgments.text: Within the four-way regorafenib responder subgroup, the regorafenib effect varies meaningfully with ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: ECOG-PS-based heterogeneity within this specific four-way subgroup is not a guideline-recognized predictive interaction.

judgments.text: Within the four-way regorafenib responder subgroup, the regorafenib effect varies meaningfully with stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV-based heterogeneity within the four-way regorafenib responder subgroup is a speculative subgroup interaction beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Decision-tree-based heterogeneity (T-learner with shallow trees) confirms that regorafenib has a wide CATE distribution while cetuximab/bevacizumab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab+tucatinib have narrow CATE distributions and no meaningful subgroup with substantial benefit.
judgments.rationale: A comparative CATE-distribution claim ranking regorafenib as uniquely heterogeneous versus cetuximab/bevacizumab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab+tucatinib is a methods-driven cross-drug claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, age_years has a strong positive effect on pfs_months (+0.176 mo per year of age) that is not driven by confounding by performance status, stage, biomarkers, or labs.
judgments.rationale: A clinically meaningful positive effect of older age on PFS independent of performance status, stage, biomarkers, and labs contradicts standard oncology teaching and would not appear in a guideline.

judgments.text: There is no treatment x age_years interaction for any of the six systemic therapies.
judgments.rationale: Absence of treatment-by-age interactions is a null finding consistent with standard practice, where age alone is not a guideline-level effect modifier for these agents.

judgments.text: Joint single-feature heterogeneity scan: across all 18 candidate baseline features, the only treatment with a single-feature split that creates a >1 month difference in treatment effect is regorafenib (with cea, kras, or right_sided_primary as the splitter).
judgments.rationale: Identifying regorafenib as the sole therapy with single-feature heterogeneity driven by CEA, KRAS, or sidedness is a speculative subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: The classic cetuximab/RAS-WT, encorafenib/BRAF V600E, pembrolizumab/MSI-high, and trastuzumab+tucatinib/HER2-amplified targeted-therapy hypotheses are NOT supported in this dataset; none of these biomarker x targeted-therapy interactions reach p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: Refuting the canonical cetuximab/RAS-WT, encorafenib/BRAF, pembrolizumab/MSI-H, and HER2-targeted interactions overturns guideline-level paradigms and is a strongly novel claim.

judgments.text: Whole-cohort CEA cutoff alone (without restricting to left-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT) does not isolate the full regorafenib benefit, indicating that all four predicates are needed to define the responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that CEA alone fails to isolate regorafenib benefit without sidedness/KRAS/BRAF predicates is a non-guideline, dataset-specific subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The minimal subgroup definition for the regorafenib PFS benefit consists of all four predicates: right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND cea_ng_ml<=5. Removing any one predicate substantially attenuates the effect.
judgments.rationale: A four-predicate minimal responder definition for regorafenib (left-sided, KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, low CEA) is not in any guideline and is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim.

judgments.text: There is no joint or interaction effect of multiple co-administered treatments (cetuximab+bevacizumab, pembrolizumab+others, etc.) on pfs_months beyond the sum of their main effects.
judgments.rationale: Asserting additivity of co-administered treatment effects is a default null assumption, not a novel guideline-departing finding.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately 5 months specifically in patients defined by ALL FOUR predicates: right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided primary) AND kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type) AND braf_v600e=0 (BRAF wild-type) AND cea_ng_ml<=5 (low/normal CEA). In any patient who is right-sided OR KRAS-mutant OR BRAF V600E mutant OR has CEA>5, the regorafenib effect on pfs_months is essentially zero. No other treatment in the dataset shows clinically meaningful subgroup heterogeneity for pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A ~5-month regorafenib PFS benefit confined to a four-predicate subgroup with zero effect elsewhere directly contradicts the guideline view of regorafenib as a modest later-line option without such biomarker selection.

judgments.text: FINAL PROGNOSTIC HYPOTHESIS: pfs_months is determined primarily by age_years (positive), ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), kras_mutation (negative), braf_v600e (negative), and right_sided_primary (negative), in approximate decreasing order of contribution to multivariable R^2 (~0.86).
judgments.rationale: The specific ranked multivariable prognostic model with age as a positive driver and R^2~0.86 is a dataset-specific quantitative claim beyond standard prognostic teaching.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is an established later-line therapy in metastatic colorectal cancer per NCCN/ESMO guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in RAS wild-type colorectal cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab added to chemotherapy improving outcomes in metastatic colorectal cancer is textbook standard.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-H/dMMR colorectal cancer is established first-line guideline therapy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) is the guideline standard for BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab plus tucatinib is the established regimen for HER2-amplified metastatic colorectal cancer per current guidelines.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with longer PFS contradicts typical expectations and is not a guideline-endorsed prognostic claim.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a textbook prognostic association across oncology.

judgments.text: Stage IV (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring shorter PFS is fundamental staging-prognosis knowledge in any oncology textbook.

judgments.text: Right-sided primary (right_sided_primary=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse outcomes than left-sided in colorectal cancer is established guideline-level prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: kras_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a poor prognostic marker in colorectal cancer is well-established in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: braf_v600e=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a marker of poor prognosis in metastatic CRC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA correlating with worse outcomes is a textbook tumor marker association in CRC.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor is a well-known general oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is established across multiple cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: msi_high=1 patients differ in mean pfs_months vs msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status affecting prognosis/treatment response in CRC is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: her2_amplified=1 patients differ in mean pfs_months vs her2_amplified=0.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a prognostic/predictive biomarker is well-established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: nras_mutation=1 patients differ in mean pfs_months vs nras_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation as a poor prognostic factor in CRC alongside KRAS/BRAF is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In a multivariable OLS model with all features and treatments, treatment_regorafenib has an independent positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: An independent positive PFS effect of regorafenib after adjusting for all biomarkers and treatments is a multivariable-conditional claim not stated in guidelines, which typically position regorafenib as a modest later-line option.

judgments.text: In the same multivariable model, none of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has a non-zero independent main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Testing whether established targeted/biologic therapies have main effects on PFS reflects standard guideline-level efficacy claims, not a novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: Cetuximab has a larger positive PFS effect in kras_mutation=0 patients than in kras_mutation=1 patients (interaction kras x cetuximab).
judgments.rationale: KRAS wild-type predicting cetuximab benefit in colorectal cancer is a well-established guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: Cetuximab benefit is larger in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) than right-sided (right_sided_primary=1) tumors.
judgments.rationale: Left-sided primary predicting greater anti-EGFR (cetuximab) benefit is established in CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab has a larger positive PFS effect in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is a standard guideline-level association across tumor types including CRC.

judgments.text: Encorafenib has a larger positive PFS effect in braf_v600e=1 patients than in braf_v600e=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E predicting encorafenib benefit is the established indication in metastatic CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: Trastuzumab/tucatinib has a larger positive PFS effect in her2_amplified=1 than her2_amplified=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification predicting trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit is a standard guideline-level biomarker indication.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on PFS is modified by stage_iv (stronger in metastatic vs non-metastatic).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV-specific modification of regorafenib PFS benefit is not a standard guideline claim and represents a subgroup-conditional hypothesis.

judgments.text: Regorafenib effect on PFS is modified by ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status modifying regorafenib's PFS effect (beyond general fitness considerations) is a speculative interaction not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in patients with kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided KRAS-WT).
judgments.rationale: A regorafenib benefit concentrated specifically in left-sided KRAS-WT tumors is a cross-pathway subgroup claim not part of established regorafenib indications.

judgments.text: Among kras_mutation=1 patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming KRAS-mutant patients derive no regorafenib benefit contradicts its tumor-agnostic refractory CRC indication and is a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Among right_sided_primary=1 patients, regorafenib has no PFS benefit.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional claim that regorafenib lacks PFS benefit specifically in right-sided primaries is not part of guideline consensus, which recommends regorafenib broadly in refractory mCRC regardless of sidedness.

judgments.text: Bevacizumab harms PFS in braf_v600e=1 patients (negative subgroup effect).
judgments.rationale: A negative PFS effect of bevacizumab specifically in BRAF V600E-mutant CRC is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not reflected in guidelines, which still endorse bevacizumab-containing regimens in this population.

judgments.text: Adding braf_v600e=0 to the regorafenib subgroup further refines (or preserves) the benefit; presence of braf_v600e=1 abolishes the regorafenib benefit even in left-sided KRAS-WT patients.
judgments.rationale: A multi-feature conditional rule (BRAF V600E abolishing regorafenib benefit even within left-sided KRAS-WT) is a cross-pathway speculative claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib benefits patients with nras_mutation=1 even when KRAS is wild-type and primary is left-sided.
judgments.rationale: Claiming regorafenib benefit in NRAS-mutant, KRAS-WT, left-sided CRC is a speculative subgroup-conditional effect not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit requires the simultaneous combination right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0; nras_mutation status is not part of the qualifying subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A precise three-feature conjunction (left-sided + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT) as the qualifying subgroup for regorafenib benefit is not part of guideline consensus, which uses regorafenib broadly in refractory disease.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib responsive subgroup defined in h7.1, the magnitude of benefit is roughly constant regardless of stage_iv and ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: Claim that benefit magnitude is invariant across stage IV and ECOG within a defined molecular subgroup is a speculative quantitative invariance not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Two-feature subgroup search (across all binary features and median-split continuous variables) will identify additional treatment x feature combinations with adjusted PFS benefits beyond regorafenib's subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Predicting additional treatment-by-feature interactions from a two-feature search is an exploratory cross-pathway claim beyond guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all features, every treatment x feature interaction other than regorafenib's interactions with right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and braf_v600e is non-significant.
judgments.rationale: A global null on all other treatment-feature interactions after full adjustment is a strong speculative claim not covered by guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the canonical biomarker-positive subgroup (msi_high=1) plus an additional modifier, pembrolizumab improves PFS.
judgments.rationale: While pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high CRC is established, conditioning on an additional modifier on top of MSI-high is a speculative subgroup refinement beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: Within braf_v600e=1 plus an additional modifier, encorafenib improves PFS.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib benefit in BRAF V600E CRC is established, but requiring an additional modifier beyond BRAF status is a speculative subgroup-conditional refinement not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within her2_amplified=1 plus an additional modifier, trastuzumab/tucatinib improves PFS.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib benefit in HER2-amplified colorectal cancer is established in current guidelines (e.g., MOUNTAINEER trial) and represents standard paradigm rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: Within the canonical anti-EGFR-eligible subgroup (KRAS-WT, NRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided), cetuximab improves PFS.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab benefit in KRAS/NRAS/BRAF wild-type left-sided metastatic colorectal cancer is the canonical guideline-endorsed indication and therefore not novel.

judgments.text: Three-modifier subgroup search reveals additional treatment effects for cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, or trastuzumab/tucatinib beyond what 2-modifier search found.
judgments.rationale: Claiming additional three-way subgroup-conditional treatment effects beyond standard biomarker pairs is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Final refined subgroup hypothesis: regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately 2.7 months in patients with right_sided_primary=0 AND kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0; outside this subgroup the regorafenib effect is null (~0 months).
judgments.rationale: A specific ~2.7-month PFS benefit of regorafenib confined to left-sided, KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT patients is not part of guideline regorafenib indications, which are biomarker-agnostic in refractory CRC.

judgments.text: Adjusted main treatment effects of cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib are essentially zero in this dataset, with no robust subgroups of benefit identified.
judgments.rationale: Asserting null adjusted effects for cetuximab and trastuzumab/tucatinib in their established biomarker-defined indications contradicts guideline consensus and is therefore a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have different mean pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in RAS-wild-type colorectal cancer is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have different mean pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's PFS benefit in metastatic colorectal cancer is well-established first-line guideline therapy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have different mean pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high colorectal cancer is established guideline-level standard of care.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have different mean pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib (with cetuximab) for BRAF V600E colorectal cancer is established guideline therapy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have different mean pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib for HER2-amplified colorectal cancer is established guideline-recognized therapy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have higher mean pfs_months than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is a guideline-established later-line therapy in refractory colorectal cancer.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_mutation have lower mean pfs_months than KRAS-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive marker in colorectal cancer is textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with nras_mutation have different mean pfs_months than NRAS-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: NRAS mutation status affecting outcomes and anti-EGFR eligibility is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with braf_v600e have lower mean pfs_months than BRAF-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: BRAF V600E as a poor prognostic marker in colorectal cancer is well-established textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high have different mean pfs_months than MSS patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a prognostic and predictive biomarker in colorectal cancer is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Patients with her2_amplified have different mean pfs_months than HER2-non-amplified patients.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification is a well-established prognostic and predictive biomarker in colorectal and other cancers, routinely covered in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with ntrk_fusion have different mean pfs_months than NTRK-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: NTRK fusions are established actionable alterations with known prognostic/predictive implications documented in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv have lower mean pfs_months than non-stage-IV patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than earlier stages is foundational oncology staging knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with right_sided_primary have lower mean pfs_months than left-sided patients.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary tumors having worse outcomes than left-sided is established colorectal cancer guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: sex_female is associated with mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sex as a prognostic factor is a standard demographic covariate routinely examined and reported in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is textbook oncology consensus.

judgments.text: age_years is linearly associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic covariate is a standard, guideline-level demographic association.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CEA as a poor prognostic marker is established in colorectal cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor is well-established in oncology and reflected in standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is textbook oncology and appears in standard prognostic scores.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss are well-established adverse prognostic factors documented in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of systemic inflammation predicting worse cancer outcomes is a well-established prognostic association in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio is a widely recognized adverse prognostic biomarker across multiple cancer types in standard oncology practice.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia is a well-established adverse prognostic factor in cancer, included in standard prognostic indices like IPI and IMDC.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase, often reflecting bone or liver metastases, is a standard adverse prognostic marker in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ast_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST, often indicating liver involvement or metastases, is a recognized adverse prognostic factor in standard oncology assessment.

judgments.text: Higher alt_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT reflecting hepatic dysfunction or metastases is a standard adverse prognostic indicator in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated bilirubin indicating hepatic dysfunction is a well-recognized adverse prognostic marker and treatment-limiting factor in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated creatinine reflecting renal dysfunction is a standard adverse prognostic and treatment-modifying factor recognized in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher bun_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflecting renal dysfunction or catabolic state is a recognized adverse prognostic factor in standard oncology practice.

judgments.text: sodium_meq_l is associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sodium as a continuous prognostic marker for PFS in this disease is not a guideline-established association.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Potassium level as a PFS prognostic factor is not part of established oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: calcium_mg_dl is associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Calcium as a continuous PFS predictor (outside hypercalcemia of malignancy) is not a guideline-recognized prognostic marker.

judgments.text: After adjusting jointly for age_years, sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, cea_ng_ml, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, and hemoglobin_g_dl, ecog_ps, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l and weight_loss_pct_6mo independently shorten pfs_months while age_years and albumin_g_dl independently lengthen it.
judgments.rationale: This is a standard composite of well-known prognostic factors (ECOG, stage IV, CEA, LDH, weight loss, albumin, age) routinely cited in colorectal cancer prognosis.

judgments.text: Among msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases mean pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab; among msi_high=0 patients there is no such effect, yielding a positive treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit restricted to MSI-high tumors is established guideline knowledge (KEYNOTE-177).

judgments.text: Among kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 (RAS/RAF wild-type) patients, treatment_cetuximab increases mean pfs_months; the cetuximab x ras_raf_wt interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab efficacy in RAS/RAF wild-type CRC is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Within RAS/RAF wild-type AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided), treatment_cetuximab increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anti-EGFR benefit in left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type mCRC is established guideline recommendation.

judgments.text: Among braf_v600e=1 patients, treatment_encorafenib increases mean pfs_months; in braf_v600e=0 patients there is no effect; treatment_encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib efficacy restricted to BRAF V600E mutant CRC is guideline-established (BEACON trial).

judgments.text: Among her2_amplified=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib increases mean pfs_months; in her2_amplified=0 patients there is no effect; treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib x her2_amplified interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Trastuzumab/tucatinib efficacy in HER2-amplified CRC is established (MOUNTAINEER) and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within her2_amplified=1 AND kras_mutation=0 (HER2+ KRAS-wild-type), treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib increases mean pfs_months relative to within her2_amplified=1 AND kras_mutation=1.
judgments.rationale: A KRAS-wild-type-conditional modifier of HER2-targeted therapy benefit within HER2+ disease is a speculative subgroup interaction beyond current guideline consensus.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l and cea_ng_ml, treatment_bevacizumab independently changes mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab's independent PFS benefit in metastatic CRC after adjustment for standard prognostic covariates is a well-established guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab interacts with kras_mutation, right_sided_primary, msi_high, stage_iv, ecog_ps, or braf_v600e on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Interactions of bevacizumab efficacy with KRAS, BRAF, MSI, sidedness, stage, and performance status have been extensively studied and discussed in standard CRC guidelines/textbooks.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, and cea_ng_ml, treatment_regorafenib independently increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib's modest independent PFS benefit in refractory mCRC after standard covariate adjustment reflects the established CORRECT trial finding in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is suppressed (negative interaction) by kras_mutation.
judgments.rationale: A specific negative interaction between KRAS mutation and regorafenib efficacy is not part of guideline consensus, since regorafenib is approved regardless of RAS status.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is enhanced (positive interaction) in nras_mutation patients.
judgments.rationale: Enhanced regorafenib benefit specifically in NRAS-mutant patients is a speculative subgroup claim not reflected in guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is suppressed by braf_v600e.
judgments.rationale: Differential suppression of regorafenib efficacy by BRAF V600E specifically is not a guideline-level claim, since regorafenib is used agnostic to BRAF status.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is suppressed by right_sided_primary.
judgments.rationale: Right-sided primary as a negative effect modifier of regorafenib efficacy is not in standard guidelines, which apply regorafenib regardless of sidedness.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is suppressed by her2_amplified.
judgments.rationale: HER2 amplification as a suppressor of regorafenib efficacy is a speculative cross-pathway claim not appearing in current CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is suppressed by higher ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: Worse performance status attenuating benefit of late-line therapies including regorafenib is a well-known clinical principle reflected in guidelines and labels.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is not modified by msi_high, ntrk_fusion, stage_iv, or sex_female.
judgments.rationale: A specific null claim that MSI, NTRK, stage IV, and sex do not modify regorafenib effect is a speculative subgroup-conditional assertion beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Among kras_mutation=0 patients, treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months; among kras_mutation=1 patients, the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib in mCRC is approved and used regardless of KRAS status; KRAS-wt-only benefit is a known/expected speculation but not established consensus — leaning to established null hypothesis territory, however the explicit subgroup-conditional claim of null effect in KRAS-mutant goes beyond guideline language.

judgments.text: Among braf_v600e=0 patients, treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months; among braf_v600e=1 patients, the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not stratify regorafenib benefit by BRAF V600E status; claiming benefit is abolished in BRAF-mutant is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Among right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided) patients, treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months; among right_sided_primary=1 patients, the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Sidedness predicts anti-EGFR benefit in mCRC, but is not an established effect modifier for regorafenib in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the joint subgroup kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0, treatment_regorafenib substantially increases pfs_months relative to non-regorafenib patients in the same subgroup; outside this subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A triple-conjunction biomarker subgroup (KRAS-wt AND BRAF-wt AND left-sided) as the sole responder population for regorafenib is not in any guideline.

judgments.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months at every ecog_ps stratum (0, 1, 2), so ecog_ps modifies baseline pfs_months but does not abolish the regorafenib effect.
judgments.rationale: Stating ECOG is prognostic but not predictive of regorafenib effect is the default/standard expectation, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Within kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND ecog_ps=0, treatment_regorafenib produces an even larger absolute pfs_months gain than in the same biomarker subgroup overall.
judgments.rationale: Claiming an even larger absolute regorafenib gain in a 4-way conjunction subgroup (triple-negative biomarker plus ECOG 0) is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher cea_ng_ml suppresses the treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: CEA as a quantitative effect modifier suppressing regorafenib PFS benefit is not an established guideline predictor.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl suppresses the treatment_regorafenib pfs_months benefit (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin suppressing regorafenib benefit is counterintuitive (albumin is usually favorable) and absent from guidelines as an effect modifier.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, nlr or sodium_meq_l enhance the treatment_regorafenib effect; higher crp_mg_l, bun_mg_dl suppress it; remaining labs (age_years, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) show no interaction with treatment_regorafenib on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A specific multi-lab interaction signature (LDH/weight loss/NLR/sodium enhance, CRP/BUN suppress, others null) for regorafenib effect is a detailed mechanistic hypothesis far beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: In a joint regression including treatment_regorafenib x kras_mutation, treatment_regorafenib x braf_v600e, and treatment_regorafenib x right_sided_primary plus age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, cea_ng_ml, the baseline regorafenib effect (KRAS-wt, BRAF-wt, left-sided) is large and positive, and each of the three interactions is significantly negative and roughly equal in magnitude to the baseline effect — i.e., each unfavorable predicate fully neutralises the regorafenib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming three covariates (KRAS, BRAF, sidedness) each independently and equally fully neutralize the regorafenib effect in a joint model is a specific quantitative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, msi_high, albumin_g_dl, cea_ng_ml, treatment_pembrolizumab does not change pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tests overall pembrolizumab effect adjusted for MSI-high; aligns with established guideline knowledge that benefit is restricted to MSI-high subset.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab does not interact with msi_high, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, stage_iv, ecog_ps, or her2_amplified on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Concerns standard textbook association (MSI-high predicts pembrolizumab benefit); restating/testing this established interaction is not novel.

judgments.text: treatment_cetuximab does not interact with kras_mutation, nras_mutation, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified, right_sided_primary, stage_iv, ecog_ps or sex_female on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cetuximab interactions with KRAS/NRAS/BRAF and sidedness are textbook predictive biomarkers in mCRC guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib does not interact with kras_mutation, nras_mutation, braf_v600e, her2_amplified, right_sided_primary, stage_iv, ecog_ps, treatment_cetuximab, or treatment_bevacizumab on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Encorafenib + cetuximab is guideline-approved specifically for BRAF V600E mCRC; this addresses the known established interaction.

judgments.text: treatment_encorafenib has a negative interaction with msi_high on pfs_months (effect more negative when MSI-high).
judgments.rationale: Cross-pathway claim that MSI-high attenuates BRAF inhibitor benefit is speculative and not in current CRC guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib does not interact with any of kras_mutation, nras_mutation, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified, right_sided_primary, stage_iv, or ecog_ps on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Tucatinib+trastuzumab is guideline-indicated for HER2-amplified RAS-wt mCRC; the HER2 interaction is established.

judgments.text: treatment_bevacizumab does not interact with nras_mutation, her2_amplified, ntrk_fusion, sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, or right_sided_primary on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Bevacizumab is recognized as biomarker-agnostic in CRC guidelines, so absence of interaction with these factors reflects established consensus.

judgments.text: Across the 2x2x2 grid of kras_mutation x braf_v600e x right_sided_primary, treatment_regorafenib has a strong positive pfs_months effect ONLY in the (kras_mutation=0, braf_v600e=0, right_sided_primary=0) cell; in every other cell with patients available the effect is statistically null.
judgments.rationale: Regorafenib is guideline-approved as biomarker-agnostic refractory therapy; restricting benefit to a specific KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/left-sided cell is a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Removing any one of the three predicates (kras_mutation=0, braf_v600e=0, right_sided_primary=0) from the regorafenib subgroup definition reduces the estimated treatment_regorafenib pfs_months effect, confirming that all three predicates contribute to defining the responsive subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Multi-predicate compositional subgroup definition for regorafenib responsiveness goes beyond any guideline characterization.

judgments.text: Among nras_mutation=1 patients, treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months; the estimated effect is enhanced when the additional KRAS-wt + BRAF-wt + left-sided predicates also hold, but NRAS status itself is not required to define the responsive subgroup (because nras_mutation=1 is essentially a subset of kras_mutation=0).
judgments.rationale: Speculative cross-pathway/subset reasoning linking NRAS-mutant status to regorafenib benefit via overlap with KRAS-wt is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL SUBGROUP: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months relative to no regorafenib in the subgroup defined by (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0); the unfavorable values of any of these three predicates (kras_mutation=1, braf_v600e=1, or right_sided_primary=1) suppress the regorafenib benefit toward zero.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines position regorafenib as a later-line option in metastatic CRC without restricting use to KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/left-sided tumors, so a triple-negative biomarker subgroup uniquely benefiting is a non-standard, speculative claim.

judgments.text: Within the regorafenib responder subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0), none of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib changes pfs_months — i.e., the subgroup-specific benefit is unique to regorafenib.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab-tucatinib all lack PFS benefit specifically in the KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/left-sided subgroup contradicts established guideline expectations (e.g., cetuximab benefit in left-sided RAS/BRAF-wt CRC) and is a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: In a regression with treatment_regorafenib interactions for kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary plus adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps and stage_iv, the baseline (KRAS-wt + BRAF-wt + left-sided) regorafenib effect on pfs_months is approximately +2.3 months and is highly significant.
judgments.rationale: A quantitative ~+2.3-month PFS gain for regorafenib restricted to a KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/left-sided subgroup with covariate adjustment is not a guideline-level association and represents a speculative subgroup-conditional effect.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (marginal main effect, all-comers).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy on PFS is well-established and appears in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_sotorasib (marginal main effect, all-comers).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant disease is established in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (marginal main effect, all-comers).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_osimertinib (marginal main effect, all-comers).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is standard first-line guideline recommendation.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation), reflecting typical worse prognosis with age.
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with worse prognosis is a textbook prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status as a prognostic factor for PFS is universally established in oncology.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 (metastatic disease) is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Metastatic (stage IV) disease having worse PFS than non-metastatic is foundational staging knowledge.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a poor prognostic factor is well-established in oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is a standard, textbook association.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer-related weight loss/cachexia predicting worse outcomes is a classical prognostic finding.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker shortening PFS is a well-established standard association across solid tumors including lung cancer.

judgments.text: Squamous histology is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
judgments.rationale: Worse outcomes for squamous versus adenocarcinoma histology in NSCLC is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a prognostic factor with current smokers faring worse is a textbook association in lung cancer.

judgments.text: sex_female=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than sex_female=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in NSCLC outcomes (with females generally faring better) are well-documented in standard oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher nlr and crp_mg_l are associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR and CRP as adverse inflammatory prognostic markers shortening PFS is a widely established association.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than egfr_mutation=0 patients (marginal).
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation status as a predictor of differential PFS (typically better with TKI therapy) is the canonical guideline-level NSCLC biomarker.

judgments.text: kras_g12c=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 patients (marginal).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C as a prognostic/predictive biomarker in NSCLC is now standard with sotorasib/adagrasib in guidelines.

judgments.text: alk_fusion=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 patients (marginal).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion status driving differential PFS with TKI therapy is a textbook NSCLC biomarker association.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than stk11_mutation=0 patients (marginal).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations as adverse prognostic markers, particularly with immunotherapy resistance, are now established in NSCLC literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 patients (marginal).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as a marginal predictor of PFS differences in NSCLC is not a standard guideline-level association in this disease context.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting better outcomes is a well-established biomarker association in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: tmb_high=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high status as a predictor of differential outcomes is an established biomarker concept in current oncology practice.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months more in patients with higher pdl1_tps than in those with lower pdl1_tps (positive treatment×pdl1_tps interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit increasing with higher PD-L1 expression is a foundational guideline-level finding (e.g., KEYNOTE trials).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months more in tmb_high=1 patients than tmb_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Greater immunotherapy benefit in TMB-high patients is established and reflected in FDA approvals and guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months substantially more in kras_g12c=1 patients than kras_g12c=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's selective benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the basis for its approval and standard guideline use.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months more in brca2_mutation=1 patients than brca2_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant patients is a textbook synthetic lethality association in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months more in egfr_mutation=1 patients than egfr_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's benefit in EGFR-mutant patients is the canonical targeted therapy paradigm in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months more in alk_fusion=1 patients than alk_fusion=0 patients (off-label/unexpected).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in ALK-fusion patients would be off-label and contrary to established mechanism, making this a novel cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: The age–pfs_months relationship is monotonically increasing across the age range (no inverted-U shape).
judgments.rationale: A strictly monotonic increasing age-PFS relationship contradicts typical clinical expectations and isn't a guideline statement.

judgments.text: Each treatment is balanced across baseline covariates (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, sex), suggesting random or RCT-like assignment rather than confounded EHR-driven assignment.
judgments.rationale: Claiming RCT-like balance across covariates in an EHR dataset is a non-standard methodological assertion not found in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib treatment effect on pfs_months is heterogeneous across baseline features (some features modify the effect).
judgments.rationale: Heterogeneity of sotorasib effect across baseline features within KRAS G12C+ is a generic, vague claim consistent with standard understanding that treatment effects vary.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+, the sotorasib effect on pfs_months is markedly attenuated in sex_female=1 patients compared to sex_female=0 patients (negative treatment×sex interaction).
judgments.rationale: A specific sex-based attenuation of sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C+ NSCLC is not established in current guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+, the sotorasib effect on pfs_months is attenuated in alk_fusion=1 patients (negative treatment×alk interaction).
judgments.rationale: Co-occurrence of ALK fusion with KRAS G12C is biologically unusual and a specific ALK-driven attenuation of sotorasib effect is not part of guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+, the sotorasib effect on pfs_months is attenuated in brca2_mutation=1 patients (negative treatment×brca2 interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status modifying sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C+ disease is not an established paradigm.

judgments.text: The 'clean' sotorasib responder subgroup is defined by kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; in this subgroup, treatment_sotorasib increases mean pfs_months by ~5 months over no-sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: Defining a 'clean responder' subgroup by joint absence of female sex, ALK fusion, and BRCA2 mutation with a quantified ~5 month PFS gain is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: Presence of any one of {sex_female=1, alk_fusion=1, brca2_mutation=1} fully suppresses the sotorasib effect (mean PFS difference ~0) within KRAS G12C+ patients.
judgments.rationale: Complete suppression of sotorasib benefit by any one of these three unrelated features is a strong cross-pathway interaction claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across binary biomarker pairs, treatment_pembrolizumab has at least one subgroup where it produces a meaningful pfs_months gain.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab having at least one biomarker-defined subgroup with meaningful PFS gain is consistent with established PD-L1/TMB-based selection in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across binary biomarker pairs, treatment_olaparib has at least one subgroup where it produces a meaningful pfs_months gain.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib producing meaningful PFS gain in at least one biomarker subgroup (e.g., BRCA-mutant) is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Across binary biomarker pairs, treatment_osimertinib has at least one subgroup where it produces a meaningful pfs_months gain.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib producing meaningful PFS gain in at least one biomarker subgroup (EGFR-mutant) is core guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for prognostic factors and including a 3-way treatment_sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex_female interaction plus treatment_sotorasib×alk_fusion and treatment_sotorasib×brca2_mutation interactions, the model explains the great majority of variance in pfs_months and confirms that the only treatment with a non-null PFS effect is sotorasib in the targeted subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A combined model asserting sotorasib is the sole effective treatment and only within a specific 3-way interaction subgroup, with other guideline-endorsed therapies showing null effects, contradicts established paradigms and is a speculative composite claim.

judgments.text: In the multivariable model, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib have no significant pfs_months effect (coefficients near zero).
judgments.rationale: A null finding that established targeted/immunotherapy agents lack PFS effect in an unselected multivariable model is a methodological observation, not a novel biological claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is no joint subgroup defined by sex/ECOG/histology/smoking that rescues a meaningful treatment effect of osimertinib in egfr_mutation=1 patients, of olaparib in brca2_mutation=1 patients, or of pembrolizumab in pdl1_tps high patients.
judgments.rationale: Stating that no demographic/clinical subgroup rescues treatment effect in biomarker-positive patients is a negative result consistent with current guideline expectations that biomarker status itself drives benefit.

judgments.text: The final, fully-specified treatment-effect subgroup with a clinically meaningful pfs_months gain is: kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; in this subgroup, treatment_sotorasib produces a +5 month gain in mean pfs_months versus no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: A specific multi-gene conditional subgroup (KRAS G12C positive AND male AND ALK-negative AND BRCA2-negative) predicting a +5-month sotorasib PFS gain is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not found in current NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy on PFS is well-established across multiple guideline-endorsed indications.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_sotorasib and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established in NCCN guidelines.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_olaparib and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers is a textbook association.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_osimertinib and those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is guideline-standard first-line therapy.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_g12c=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with kras_g12c=0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C is a well-known targetable driver associated with treatment-defined PFS outcomes in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with egfr_mutation=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with egfr_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting better PFS on TKI therapy is canonical guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with alk_fusion=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with alk_fusion=0.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion status and its PFS implications under ALK TKIs are standard guideline content; an unconditioned worse-PFS claim is still within established paradigm framing.

judgments.text: Patients with tmb_high=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with tmb_high=0.
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting improved PFS on immunotherapy is an established biomarker association in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS correlating with better PFS on checkpoint inhibitors is textbook oncology.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation conferring better PFS on PARP inhibitor-containing regimens is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with stk11_mutation=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with stk11_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive marker in NSCLC is well-established in current literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months is larger in patients with kras_g12c=1 than in those with kras_g12c=0 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's enhanced efficacy in KRAS G12C-mutant tumors is the basis of its FDA approval and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months increases with pdl1_tps (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS as a positive predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is a guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months is larger in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in those with brca2_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation predicting olaparib benefit is a well-established synthetic lethality paradigm in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_osimertinib on pfs_months is larger in patients with egfr_mutation=1 than in those with egfr_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting osimertinib benefit is the textbook indication for the drug.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is larger in patients with tmb_high=1 than in those with tmb_high=0.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high status predicting pembrolizumab benefit underlies the tissue-agnostic FDA approval and is in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months substantially.
judgments.rationale: Substantial sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ patients is the established on-label effect.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C- patients, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of sotorasib activity outside KRAS G12C is the standard targeted-therapy paradigm.

judgments.text: Sex modifies the sotorasib effect within KRAS G12C+ patients: the benefit is concentrated in males (sex_female=0).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of sotorasib efficacy within KRAS G12C+ patients is not a recognized guideline claim and is a speculative subgroup interaction.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ male (sex_female=0) patients, treatment_sotorasib raises pfs_months by >4 months on average.
judgments.rationale: A specific >4-month PFS gain restricted to KRAS G12C+ males is a speculative subgroup-conditional quantitative claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ female (sex_female=1) patients, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific absence of sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ NSCLC is not an established guideline finding.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males, alk_fusion=1 (rare overlap) eliminates the benefit of sotorasib on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Concurrent ALK fusion abolishing sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ males is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males, brca2_mutation=1 eliminates the benefit of sotorasib on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation eliminating sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ males is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males without alk_fusion and without brca2_mutation, sotorasib confers a PFS benefit of approximately +5 months.
judgments.rationale: Quantified PFS benefit conditional on male sex plus absence of ALK/BRCA2 alterations is a novel subgroup-specific claim.

judgments.text: ALK fusion attenuates the sotorasib×kras_g12c interaction effect on pfs_months in a multivariable interaction model.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion attenuating the sotorasib×KRAS G12C interaction is a novel mechanistic interaction claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: BRCA2 mutation attenuates the sotorasib×kras_g12c interaction effect on pfs_months in a multivariable interaction model.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation attenuating the sotorasib×KRAS G12C interaction is a novel cross-pathway interaction claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is inversely associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is well-established prognostic dogma in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 patients have lower pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring shorter PFS than earlier stage is standard staging-prognosis consensus.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 patients have lower pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases predicting worse PFS in NSCLC is established guideline-level prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with squamous histology have lower pfs_months than adenocarcinoma patients.
judgments.rationale: Squamous versus adenocarcinoma histology prognostic differences are described in standard NSCLC references.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have lower pfs_months than male patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based PFS differences are not a standard guideline-level association and this simple direction (female worse) is not established consensus, but the claim is a generic demographic comparison without mechanism — treated as a routine descriptive finding, not novel.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is well-established across oncology and appears in standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is textbook oncology (e.g., cachexia, ECOG performance status correlates).

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is standard and appears in guideline staging/prognostic systems.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established in oncology literature and prognostic scores.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with lower pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as an adverse prognostic marker is widely established across solid tumors.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with lower pfs_months (higher age, worse outcomes is the standard direction).
judgments.rationale: Older age associated with worse outcomes is the conventional default assumption in oncology prognostication.

judgments.text: Current smokers have lower pfs_months than former or never smokers.
judgments.rationale: Current smoking status worsening outcomes is a standard clinical association, particularly in lung cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for clinical/laboratory features, pdl1_tps remains independently associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS as an independent prognostic/predictive biomarker is guideline-level standard in NSCLC and other tumors.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, kras_g12c remains independently associated with pfs_months (positive direction) due to sotorasib treatment effects in G12C+ males.
judgments.rationale: A sex-conditional (male-specific) independent KRAS G12C benefit attributed to sotorasib is a speculative subgroup-by-treatment interaction not reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months (pembrolizumab harm in localized but not stage IV disease).
judgments.rationale: Stage-conditional harm of pembrolizumab in localized disease is not standard guideline content.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab × weight_loss_pct_6mo interaction (pembrolizumab is less harmful or more beneficial in cachectic patients).
judgments.rationale: Cachexia-conditional pembrolizumab benefit is a speculative subgroup interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps × tmb_high three-way interaction is non-zero on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A three-way PD-L1 × TMB × pembrolizumab interaction goes beyond standard single-biomarker guideline use.

judgments.text: The pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction is positive within adenocarcinoma histology (pembrolizumab benefits high-PDL1 adeno).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in high-PD-L1 adenocarcinoma is established first-line guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within high-PDL1 (≥0.5) adenocarcinoma patients, treatment_pembrolizumab raises pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS in high-PD-L1 (≥50%) adenocarcinoma NSCLC is standard guideline-based knowledge.

judgments.text: Within never-smokers with adenocarcinoma, treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Specifically restricting the PD-L1 × pembrolizumab interaction to never-smoker adenocarcinoma is a non-guideline subgroup claim.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib × crp_mg_l interaction on pfs_months is positive (more benefit in inflammatory states).
judgments.rationale: CRP/inflammation-conditional olaparib benefit is a mechanistic claim not in PARP inhibitor guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib × bun_mg_dl interaction on pfs_months is positive.
judgments.rationale: Renal function (BUN)-conditional olaparib efficacy is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2 mutation positive patients, treatment_olaparib raises pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS in BRCA2-mutant patients is standard, well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib × alk_fusion interaction on pfs_months is non-zero (off-target ALK effect).
judgments.rationale: An off-target osimertinib effect modified by ALK fusion status is a cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within EGFR mutation positive patients, treatment_osimertinib raises pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is the textbook standard-of-care association.

judgments.text: Across all four treatments, no PFS benefit is detectable in the sex_female=1, kras_g12c=0 stratum.
judgments.rationale: A claim that no PFS benefit is detectable across all four treatments specifically in KRAS-G12C–negative females is a speculative subgroup-conditional null not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Sotorasib effect on pfs_months in KRAS G12C+ males is approximately additive in the linear scale (no further interaction with stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ECOG, smoking, or histology).
judgments.rationale: Asserting strict additivity of sotorasib in KRAS G12C+ males with no higher-order interactions across multiple clinical covariates is a mechanistic modeling claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males (alk-, brca2-), sotorasib confers a benefit ≥ 4 months across every ECOG stratum.
judgments.rationale: A uniform ≥4-month sotorasib benefit across every ECOG stratum within KRAS G12C+ ALK−/BRCA2− males is a granular subgroup-conditional effect-size claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ females, no continuous lab feature (age, albumin, NLR, LDH, CRP, weight loss) recovers a sotorasib benefit (interaction p>0.10 for all).
judgments.rationale: Claiming no continuous lab biomarker modifies sotorasib response in KRAS G12C+ females is a cross-feature negative interaction claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, albumin, weight_loss, ldh, sex, histology, smoking, and all biomarkers/treatments, no significant pembrolizumab main effect remains.
judgments.rationale: After heavy multivariable adjustment, the disappearance of a pembrolizumab main effect would be a dataset-specific covariate-conditional finding rather than guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for the same covariates, no significant olaparib main effect remains.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib lacks a guideline-supported main PFS effect outside HRD/BRCA-selected populations, so a null adjusted main effect aligns with established consensus.

judgments.text: After adjusting for the same covariates, no significant osimertinib main effect remains.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's main effect being driven by EGFR-mutant patients (and thus attenuating after adjustment for biomarkers) is consistent with the standard paradigm.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months: a screen of pembrolizumab × X interactions over all clinical/biomarker features yields no signal that survives Bonferroni correction.
judgments.rationale: A Bonferroni-survival negative interaction screen for pembrolizumab across all clinical/biomarker features is a specific cross-pathway heterogeneity claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_olaparib: same screen yields no signal that survives Bonferroni correction.
judgments.rationale: It is established that olaparib's benefit concentrates in HRD/BRCA-selected subgroups, so a broad null after multiple-testing correction matches guideline expectations.

judgments.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_osimertinib: same screen yields no signal that survives Bonferroni correction.
judgments.rationale: Null finding from a heterogeneity screen makes no new biological claim beyond established osimertinib-EGFR paradigm.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 2-feature subgroup search (binary feature pairs) for treatment_pembrolizumab does not identify a subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically robust PFS gain.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result asserts no new biology, only absence of signal.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_olaparib does not identify a subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically robust PFS gain.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result; no claim beyond established BRCA-PARP paradigm.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_osimertinib does not identify a subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically robust PFS gain.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result; no novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Joint multivariable model for pfs_months: ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, histology, smoking, age, albumin, weight_loss, ldh, sex, and the sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex three-way interaction together capture the systematic structure; other lab features (sodium, potassium, calcium, hemoglobin, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, alkaline_phosphatase, NLR, CRP) add no independent signal.
judgments.rationale: Methodological/statistical sufficiency claim about lab covariates; not a clinical biology assertion absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Refined: The maximum-effect treatment subgroup in the dataset is patients with kras_g12c=1, sex_female=0, alk_fusion=0, and brca2_mutation=0, where treatment_sotorasib raises pfs_months by ≈ +5 months.
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific sotorasib benefit conditional on absence of ALK fusion and BRCA2 mutation is a cross-pathway, subgroup-conditional claim absent from any guideline.

judgments.text: Within the kras_g12c=1, sex_female=0 subgroup, neither alk_fusion=1 nor brca2_mutation=1 alone leaves any residual sotorasib benefit (both fully abolish the effect).
judgments.rationale: Claim that ALK fusion or BRCA2 mutation each fully abolishes sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C males is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: There are NO additional treatment-effect heterogeneities of comparable magnitude (>1 mo PFS) for any of the four treatments hidden in the data; the sotorasib-in-G12C+-males-without-ALK-or-BRCA2 effect is unique.
judgments.rationale: Negative uniqueness claim; asserts absence of other heterogeneities rather than proposing new biology.

judgments.text: After conditioning on the sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex three-way interaction, KRAS G12C is associated with WORSE baseline PFS (negative main effect on pfs_months in non-sotorasib-treated patients).
judgments.rationale: Conditional negative prognostic effect of KRAS G12C in non-sotorasib patients after a three-way interaction adjustment is a non-guideline mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: The negative TMB-high effect on pfs_months survives multivariable adjustment for ECOG, stage, brain mets, histology, smoking, age, sex, and biomarkers.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high associated with WORSE PFS after multivariable adjustment contradicts the standard paradigm that TMB-high predicts better immunotherapy outcomes.

judgments.text: Albumin × ECOG interaction on pfs_months is non-zero (high albumin partially rescues poor ECOG patients).
judgments.rationale: An interaction between serum albumin and ECOG performance status on PFS, with albumin rescuing poor-ECOG patients, is a cross-pathway prognostic claim not found in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Females in this cohort have lower pfs_months than males primarily because females cannot benefit from sotorasib in KRAS G12C+ disease (i.e. the sex effect on PFS is concentrated in the kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib=1 subgroup).
judgments.rationale: A sex-specific lack of sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant disease is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not established in current NSCLC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypotheses for each treatment on pfs_months: (a) treatment_sotorasib confers a +5 mo PFS gain in patients with kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; (b) treatment_pembrolizumab provides no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup; (c) treatment_olaparib provides no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup, including BRCA2-mutant patients; (d) treatment_osimertinib provides no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup, including EGFR-mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab, olaparib, and osimertinib provide no benefit in any subgroup (including EGFR-mutant and BRCA2-mutant patients) directly contradicts guideline consensus and is a non-standard, novel claim.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (positive main effect of pembrolizumab on PFS).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit on PFS is established standard-of-care knowledge in multiple oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established and guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA/HRD-positive cancers is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse PFS is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv == 1 have shorter pfs_months than those with stage_iv == 0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease predicting shorter PFS is established staging-based prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets == 1 have shorter pfs_months than those with has_brain_mets == 0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases predicting shorter PFS is a well-known adverse prognostic factor in guidelines.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female == 1) have different mean pfs_months than males.
