judgments.rationale: Sex differences in PFS as a generic main effect is a standard demographic covariate, not a novel finding.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation in this cohort).
judgments.rationale: Older age correlating with longer PFS contradicts typical prognostic expectations and would not appear in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin predicting longer PFS reflects established nutritional/prognostic biomarker knowledge.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as a negative prognostic factor in advanced cancer is well-established in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting longer PFS on immunotherapy is standard guideline-level knowledge in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Squamous histology is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
judgments.rationale: Histology-based prognostic differences between squamous and adenocarcinoma are standard textbook content.

judgments.text: Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a prognostic/predictive factor in NSCLC is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation == 1 is associated with different (longer) pfs_months than egfr_mutation == 0.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting better PFS (typically with TKI therapy) is a foundational guideline-level association in NSCLC.

judgments.text: kras_g12c == 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than kras_g12c == 0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C being associated with longer PFS (rather than shorter or neutral) is not standard guideline consensus and would be a non-obvious directional claim.

judgments.text: alk_fusion == 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion == 0.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion status and its prognostic/predictive implications are standard NSCLC guideline content.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a negative prognostic marker (especially for immunotherapy resistance) is emerging research not yet firmly in standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as a prognostic factor for PFS in lung cancer is not part of established NSCLC guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: tmb_high is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high predicting longer PFS on immunotherapy is established guideline-level knowledge with FDA-approved tumor-agnostic indications.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with kras_g12c == 1, with little or no effect in kras_g12c == 0 (positive sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's FDA-approved indication is KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC; this is the textbook target-drug pairing.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with brca2_mutation == 1 (positive olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant tumors is a guideline-level established indication.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with egfr_mutation == 1 (positive osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is the canonical first-line guideline standard.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit increases with pdl1_tps (positive pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS as a continuous predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is a textbook-level KEYNOTE-established association.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is greater in tmb_high == 1 patients (positive pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: TMB-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is a guideline-recognized pan-tumor biomarker.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib has a positive interaction with alk_fusion: patients with alk_fusion == 1 get more PFS benefit from osimertinib than alk_fusion == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib targets EGFR, not ALK; predicting benefit conditional on ALK fusion is contrary to established mechanism and would not appear in any guideline.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in stk11_mutation == 1 patients (negative pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction), reflecting reported IO resistance.
judgments.rationale: STK11-associated immunotherapy resistance in NSCLC is a well-described, textbook-level reported association.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit varies by smoking_status (e.g., greater benefit in current/former smokers than never).
judgments.rationale: Greater IO benefit in smokers (linked to higher TMB) is a well-established textbook observation in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit at PD-L1 TPS >=50% is the literal KEYNOTE-024 guideline-level indication.

judgments.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.7, treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: A higher PD-L1 threshold is a trivial restriction of the established >=50% guideline cutoff and reflects the same paradigm.

judgments.text: Among adenocarcinoma never-smokers with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 (a canonical IO-favorable subgroup), pembrolizumab improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 high never-smoker adenocarcinoma benefit from pembrolizumab is established first-line guideline practice.

judgments.text: Among patients with brca2_mutation == 1 and not receiving any other targeted therapy, olaparib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA2-mutant cancers is a well-established guideline-endorsed association.

judgments.text: The olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction differs by sex_female (e.g., olaparib helps BRCA2+ females specifically).
judgments.rationale: Sex-modified olaparib efficacy in BRCA2+ patients is not part of guideline consensus and would be a speculative interaction.

judgments.text: Among EGFR+ patients with adenocarcinoma histology (or never smokers), osimertinib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant adenocarcinoma/never-smokers is standard first-line guideline practice.

judgments.text: The sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction is itself modified by sex_female: among kras_g12c+ patients, sotorasib improves pfs_months in males (sex_female == 0) but NOT in females (sex_female == 1).
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific modification of sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C+ patients is not established in guidelines and represents a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction is reduced in patients with has_brain_mets == 1 (i.e., brain metastases attenuate sotorasib's KRAS-specific benefit).
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases attenuating sotorasib's KRAS G12C-specific benefit is a speculative cross-pathway/subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among males with kras_g12c == 1 (sex_female == 0 & kras_g12c == 1), treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by approximately 4-5 months relative to no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: A male-specific magnitude estimate for sotorasib in KRAS G12C+ patients is a speculative sex-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Among females with kras_g12c == 1 (sex_female == 1 & kras_g12c == 1), treatment_sotorasib has NO effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no sotorasib effect in female KRAS G12C+ patients contradicts the guideline-endorsed pan-sex indication and is speculative.

judgments.text: The male-KRAS-G12C-specific sotorasib effect persists after adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, histology, smoking status, and other treatments.
judgments.rationale: Persistence of a male-specific sotorasib effect after multivariable adjustment is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within male KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib PFS benefit is similar across all clinical strata (ECOG 0/1/2, stage IV vs not, brain mets vs not, adeno vs squamous, STK11 mut vs WT).
judgments.rationale: Uniform male-specific sotorasib benefit across all clinical strata including STK11 status is speculative and contradicts known STK11 resistance signals.

judgments.text: Within female KRAS G12C+ patients, no clinical or genomic subgroup recovers a meaningful sotorasib effect.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim that no subgroup of female KRAS G12C+ patients benefits from sotorasib is a speculative subgroup-conditional finding not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab × kras_g12c interaction is negative (pembro is less helpful in KRAS+ patients).
judgments.rationale: A negative interaction between pembrolizumab and KRAS G12C status is a cross-pathway speculative claim beyond standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab is associated with worse PFS specifically in patients with stage_iv == 0 (non-stage-IV KRAS+ subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab worsening PFS specifically in non-stage-IV KRAS+ patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: There exists at least one clinically interpretable subgroup defined by 1-2 features in which pembrolizumab significantly improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: The existence of some subgroup where pembrolizumab improves PFS is consistent with established immunotherapy benefit and not novel.

judgments.text: There exists at least one clinically interpretable subgroup defined by 1-2 features in which olaparib significantly improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: The existence of some subgroup where olaparib improves PFS aligns with established BRCA/HRD-based PARP inhibitor benefit.

judgments.text: There exists at least one clinically interpretable subgroup defined by 1-2 features in which osimertinib significantly improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: The existence of some subgroup where osimertinib improves PFS aligns with the established EGFR-mutant benefit and is guideline-standard.

judgments.text: Among patients with alk_fusion == 1, treatment_osimertinib is associated with a small but real positive effect on pfs_months (~+0.2 to +0.25 months crude).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in ALK fusion-positive patients is a cross-pathway claim contrary to established EGFR-specific indication.

judgments.text: The osimertinib × alk_fusion effect does not differ by sex_female (i.e., it is present in both sexes).
judgments.rationale: A sex-invariant osimertinib × ALK fusion effect is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among male KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib effect is larger in older patients (positive sotorasib × age_years interaction).
judgments.rationale: An age-modified sotorasib effect restricted to male KRAS G12C+ patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional interaction beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Among male KRAS G12C+ patients, concomitant pembrolizumab attenuates the sotorasib benefit (negative sotorasib × pembrolizumab interaction).
judgments.rationale: A negative sotorasib × pembrolizumab interaction confined to male KRAS G12C+ patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional cross-drug claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab has no statistically supported interaction with any single feature on the natural scale (no subgroup-defining modifier survives a strict screen).
judgments.rationale: Null finding restating that pembrolizumab lacks a robust single-feature predictor mirrors the known absence of a single guideline-endorsed biomarker beyond PD-L1/TMB and is not novel.

judgments.text: Olaparib has no statistically supported interaction with any single feature.
judgments.rationale: Restates the established paradigm that olaparib's benefit is anchored to BRCA/HRD status without surprising new modifiers, consistent with current guidelines.

judgments.text: Osimertinib has no statistically supported interaction with any single feature except a small positive interaction with alk_fusion.
judgments.rationale: A positive osimertinib interaction with ALK fusion is biologically unexpected and not part of any guideline, since osimertinib is an EGFR-selective TKI.

judgments.text: Sotorasib's strongest single-feature interaction is with kras_g12c (huge positive); the next strongest is with sex_female (negative), driven entirely through the kras_g12c subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A sex-specific (female-negative) modifier of sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C+ patients is not part of any treatment guideline and would be a novel subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The complete subgroup definition for sotorasib's PFS benefit is `kras_g12c == 1 AND sex_female == 0` (male KRAS G12C+); no additional predicate (ECOG, stage, brain mets, histology, smoking, STK11, age) is required to capture the heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: Defining sotorasib's PFS benefit as restricted to male KRAS G12C+ patients goes well beyond guideline criteria, which require only KRAS G12C positivity.

judgments.text: Treatments do not interact strongly with each other on PFS in the overall cohort (no large joint-treatment synergy or antagonism).
judgments.rationale: The absence of large treatment-treatment synergy/antagonism on PFS is the expected default and would not appear as a novel claim in any guideline.

judgments.text: The strong positive correlation between age_years and pfs_months (r ~ 0.76) is not explained by treatment differences across age — it reflects baseline cohort structure.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that an r~0.76 age-PFS correlation reflects baseline cohort structure rather than treatment effects is a non-trivial cohort-level claim not found in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Independent baseline predictors of shorter pfs_months in this cohort include: higher ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, squamous histology, current smoking, lower albumin, greater weight loss, sex_female, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation. Independent positive predictors include: higher age_years, kras_g12c (largely via sotorasib effect), egfr_mutation.
judgments.rationale: While many of these prognostic features are guideline-known, claims that BRCA2 mutation, ALK fusion, and female sex are independent negative PFS predictors and that EGFR mutation and KRAS G12C are independent positive predictors are non-standard and partly counterintuitive.

judgments.text: A systematic treatment-effect heterogeneity search (treatment × feature interaction screen plus 2-variable subgroup search) for treatment_pembrolizumab finds NO subgroup with a meaningful, robust positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null subgroup-search result for pembrolizumab restates the known difficulty of identifying robust ICI-benefit subgroups beyond established biomarkers and is not novel.

judgments.text: A systematic treatment-effect heterogeneity search for treatment_olaparib finds NO subgroup with a meaningful, robust positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A null subgroup result for olaparib aligns with the established paradigm that benefit tracks HRD/BRCA status and offers no new guideline-relevant claim.

judgments.text: A systematic treatment-effect heterogeneity search for treatment_osimertinib finds at most a small (~+0.03 to +0.25 months) positive effect confined to alk_fusion == 1 patients; no other subgroup shows benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib benefit is confined to ALK-fusion patients (not EGFR-mutant) contradicts guideline consensus and is a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: A systematic treatment-effect heterogeneity search for treatment_sotorasib confirms one large, robust subgroup: kras_g12c == 1 AND sex_female == 0 (male KRAS G12C+), with ~+4.5 to +4.7 months PFS benefit. No additional subgroup-defining features further refine this.
judgments.rationale: Restricting sotorasib benefit to male KRAS G12C+ patients is a sex-conditional subgroup claim not present in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab has no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup of this NSCLC cohort (no defining predicate yields a positive treatment effect).
judgments.rationale: A blanket assertion that pembrolizumab provides no PFS benefit in any NSCLC subgroup contradicts established guideline-level efficacy (e.g., PD-L1-high).

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib has no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup, including BRCA2-mutant patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib is not an established NSCLC therapy and lacks BRCA-defined guideline indication in lung cancer, so 'no benefit' is consistent with current paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_osimertinib has at most a small positive PFS effect (~+0.04 to +0.25 months) confined to alk_fusion == 1 patients; the canonical egfr_mutation subgroup shows no benefit.
judgments.rationale: Asserting osimertinib benefit only in ALK-fusion and none in EGFR-mutant patients is a cross-pathway, anti-guideline subgroup claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by approximately +4.6 months ONLY in patients with kras_g12c == 1 AND sex_female == 0 (male KRAS G12C+); the female-half of the KRAS G12C+ population (sex_female == 1) shows zero benefit. The complete subgroup definition is the conjunction of these two predicates.
judgments.rationale: Sex-stratified KRAS G12C sotorasib effect (males only, zero in females) is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The sotorasib/male-KRAS finding is not driven by lab-value imbalance: it persists when adjusting for hemoglobin, alkaline_phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, ldh, crp, nlr.
judgments.rationale: Robustness of the male-only KRAS G12C sotorasib effect to lab-value adjustment is a mechanistic/confounding-adjusted claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After exhaustive screening, no other treatment shows clinically meaningful heterogeneity. The dataset contains exactly one major treatment-effect heterogeneity signal: sotorasib in male KRAS G12C+.
judgments.rationale: Asserting the dataset contains exactly one heterogeneity signal (sotorasib in male KRAS G12C+) is a global, speculative claim beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Final summary: (a) treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by ~+4.6 months in patients with kras_g12c == 1 AND sex_female == 0; no effect elsewhere. (b) treatment_osimertinib has a small positive effect (~+0.04 to +0.23 months) confined to alk_fusion == 1; no effect in egfr_mutation+. (c) treatment_pembrolizumab has no positive effect in any subgroup. (d) treatment_olaparib has no positive effect in any subgroup, including brca2_mutation+. Baseline negative prognostic features include higher ecog_ps, stage_iv==1, has_brain_mets==1, squamous histology, current smoking, lower albumin, greater weight_loss_pct_6mo, sex_female==1, alk_fusion==1, brca2_mutation==1. Higher age_years is positively correlated with pfs_months in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: The combined claims — sex-restricted sotorasib effect, ALK-only osimertinib benefit, no pembrolizumab benefit, and prognostic roles for ALK/BRCA2/age — collectively contradict guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear relationship).
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic factor in oncology is a standard clinical consideration found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than male sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based survival differences are routinely discussed in lung cancer guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (monotonic decreasing).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a well-established prognostic factor in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than non-stage IV is foundational staging knowledge.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a poor prognostic factor is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs across smoking_status categories (former vs current vs never).
judgments.rationale: Smoking status influencing lung cancer outcomes is a well-known guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Squamous histology has shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
judgments.rationale: Squamous histology having different (often worse) outcomes than adenocarcinoma is standard NSCLC textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than egfr_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation status affecting PFS is a cornerstone of NSCLC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: kras_g12c=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C as a predictive/prognostic biomarker is now standard in NSCLC guidelines with sotorasib/adagrasib.

judgments.text: alk_fusion=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion status affecting PFS via targeted therapy is a guideline-level established association.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than stk11_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations are a well-established negative predictor of immunotherapy/treatment outcomes in NSCLC and appear in current guidelines and reviews.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status as a predictor of treatment response/PFS (especially for PARP inhibitors) is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: tmb_high=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: High TMB as a predictor of immunotherapy response and PFS is established and FDA-recognized (pembrolizumab pan-tumor TMB-high indication).

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months (linear relationship).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS as a continuous/threshold predictor of immunotherapy benefit and PFS is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is established across multiple guideline-endorsed indications.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA/HRD-associated cancers is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is the textbook standard of care.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor (and higher albumin associating with longer PFS) is a well-known general oncology prognostic principle.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker associated with shorter PFS is a textbook oncology association.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor is established across oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic marker is well-established in oncology literature and prognostic scores like GPS.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio is a widely recognized adverse prognostic biomarker in solid tumors.

judgments.text: Other routine labs (hemoglobin, alk phos, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) have negligible associations with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Routine labs other than those in established prognostic indices having negligible PFS association is consistent with standard clinical understanding.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_sotorasib and kras_g12c on pfs_months: sotorasib produces a much larger PFS gain in KRAS G12C+ patients than in KRAS G12C-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's selective benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is the FDA-approved indication and guideline standard.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_osimertinib and egfr_mutation on pfs_months: osimertinib produces a larger PFS gain in EGFR-mutant patients than in EGFR-wildtype patients.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's preferential benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on pfs_months: olaparib produces a larger PFS gain in BRCA2-mutant patients than in BRCA2-wildtype patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's enhanced efficacy in BRCA2-mutant cancers is the established companion-diagnostic indication.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and pdl1_tps on pfs_months: pembrolizumab produces a larger PFS gain at higher PD-L1 TPS.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy increasing with PD-L1 TPS is established in NSCLC guidelines and labeling.

judgments.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and tmb_high on pfs_months: pembrolizumab produces a larger PFS gain in TMB-high patients.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is an FDA-approved tissue-agnostic indication.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab is harmful (or less beneficial) in stk11_mutation=1 patients, even in PD-L1 high subset (negative interaction with STK11).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation conferring resistance to pembrolizumab even within PD-L1-high subsets is a mechanistic subgroup-conditional claim not yet codified in standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in patients with pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Three-way conditional adding STK11 wild-type to PD-L1≥50% and TMB-high goes beyond guideline biomarker criteria for pembrolizumab.

judgments.text: Osimertinib's lack of effect in EGFR+ patients persists when stratified by histology, smoking_status, has_brain_mets, stage_iv, ECOG, STK11, TMB and sex.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib lacks effect in EGFR+ patients contradicts established guideline consensus and the persistence-across-strata claim is non-standard.

judgments.text: Olaparib's lack of effect in BRCA2+ patients persists across subgroup strata (sex, histology, stage, ECOG, smoking).
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib lacks effect in BRCA2+ patients contradicts established PARP inhibitor guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The sotorasib treatment effect within KRAS G12C+ patients is larger in males (sex_female=0) than in females (sex_female=1) — a 3-way interaction sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female.
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific modification of sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C+ is not part of any guideline and represents a speculative three-way interaction.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs by sex (treatment_pembrolizumab × sex_female interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sex-by-pembrolizumab interaction is not used for treatment selection in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: Osimertinib effect on pfs_months differs by sex within EGFR+ (treatment_osimertinib × sex_female interaction in EGFR-mutant subset).
judgments.rationale: Sex-by-osimertinib interaction within EGFR+ is not part of guideline-based treatment selection.

judgments.text: Olaparib effect on pfs_months differs by sex within BRCA2+ (treatment_olaparib × sex_female interaction in BRCA2-mutant subset).
judgments.rationale: Sex-by-olaparib interaction within BRCA2+ is not part of PARP inhibitor guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males, sotorasib provides a +4 to +5 month PFS gain that persists after stratifying by stk11_mutation, tmb_high, has_brain_mets, and stage_iv.
judgments.rationale: Sex-restricted, magnitude-specific sotorasib benefit robust across multiple covariate strata is a speculative subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ females, no measured covariate (stk11, tmb_high, brain_mets, stage_iv, histology, smoking) recovers a sotorasib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claim that no measured covariate recovers sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ females contradicts the guideline-level expectation of benefit in all KRAS G12C+ patients.

judgments.text: In a male-only analysis, the treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction is large and positive (~+4.66).
judgments.rationale: Male-restricted sotorasib×KRAS G12C interaction magnitude is a sex-conditional effect not represented in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In a female-only analysis, the treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: Null interaction in a sex-restricted subgroup is a descriptive statistical observation, not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable linear model adjusting for all features, treatment_sotorasib has a large positive coefficient driven entirely by the 3-way interaction with KRAS G12C and male sex; the other three treatments (pembrolizumab, olaparib, osimertinib) have null adjusted coefficients.
judgments.rationale: A guideline would not specify that sotorasib benefit is confined to a 3-way KRAS G12C × male sex interaction while standard targeted therapies show null adjusted effects.

judgments.text: A T-learner causal forest (random forest treated/untreated models) followed by a regression tree on the patient-level CATE for treatment_sotorasib will identify KRAS G12C × male sex as the top splits, with mean predicted effect ~+4 to +5 months in that subgroup and near-zero elsewhere.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not stratify sotorasib benefit by male sex within KRAS G12C; a sex-conditional CATE of this magnitude is a non-standard subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Causal-forest CATEs for pembrolizumab, osimertinib, and olaparib will have small magnitudes and no coherent subgroup with a strong, biologically plausible benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no coherent biologically plausible benefit subgroup for pembrolizumab, osimertinib, and olaparib contradicts established guideline indications (PD-L1, EGFR, BRCA).

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by ~+4.6 months (95% CI excludes zero, p≈0) only in patients with kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (male). In all other patients (kras_g12c=0 of either sex, or kras_g12c=1 females), sotorasib has no detectable PFS effect. The female-suppressed subgroup is the variable whose unfavorable value abolishes the treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Sex-restricted (male-only) sotorasib efficacy within KRAS G12C is not part of any current treatment guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab has no detectable PFS benefit in any tested subgroup of this dataset (including pdl1_tps>=0.5, tmb_high, stk11_mutation=0, and combinations).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1-high/TMB-high NSCLC, so a blanket no-benefit claim contradicts established consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_osimertinib has no detectable PFS benefit even in EGFR-mutant patients or any further-restricted subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is a textbook standard; denying it in any subgroup contradicts guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib has no detectable PFS benefit even in BRCA2-mutant patients or any further-restricted subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant disease is guideline-established; denying it contradicts standard paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Age effects on PFS are well-known prognostic considerations in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association across the three ECOG strata 0/1/2).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status as a prognostic factor for PFS is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter mean pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring shorter PFS than non-stage-IV is textbook staging prognosis.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter mean pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases predicting shorter PFS is established standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic marker for survival/PFS is well-established in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is standard cancer prognosis knowledge.

judgments.text: Smoking_status differs in pfs_months across categories: current smokers have shorter pfs than former or never.
judgments.rationale: Claim that current smokers specifically have shorter PFS than former smokers on therapy is not a guideline-standard subgroup distinction.

judgments.text: Squamous histology has shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma histology.
judgments.rationale: Squamous NSCLC having different (often worse) PFS than adenocarcinoma is established histology-based prognosis.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than males.
judgments.rationale: Claim that females have shorter PFS than males contradicts typical guideline-level expectations and is a non-standard subgroup claim.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA-mutated cancers is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 (overall).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment assignment is correlated with the matching biomarker (e.g., osimertinib more likely in EGFR+; sotorasib more likely in KRAS G12C+; olaparib more likely in BRCA2+; pembrolizumab more likely in TMB-high).
judgments.rationale: Biomarker-directed treatment assignment is the standard of care and expected, not novel.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, treatment_sotorasib increases mean pfs_months relative to no sotorasib (positive effect).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C+ patients is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-negative patients, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of sotorasib effect outside KRAS G12C+ is the expected biomarker-restricted paradigm.

judgments.text: Within EGFR-mutated patients, treatment_osimertinib increases mean pfs_months relative to no osimertinib.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant disease is guideline-standard knowledge.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib increases mean pfs_months relative to no olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant cancer is guideline-standard knowledge.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months increases with PD-L1 TPS quartile (i.e., effect largest in Q4).
judgments.rationale: A monotonic dose-response across PD-L1 TPS quartiles with largest effect in Q4 is a specific quantitative gradient claim beyond the binary high/low guideline framing.

judgments.text: Within TMB-high patients, pembrolizumab increases mean pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TMB-high tumors is an established, FDA-approved, guideline-level indication.

judgments.text: The treatment_sotorasib by KRAS G12C effect is modified by sex_female: among KRAS G12C+ patients, sotorasib increases pfs_months only in males (sex_female=0), with no effect in females.
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific modification of sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C+ patients is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: In a multivariable linear model the three-way interaction treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female is negative (i.e., sex_female cancels the kras_g12c amplification of the sotorasib effect).
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction whereby female sex cancels KRAS G12C amplification of sotorasib effect is a speculative cross-feature claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib has no clinically meaningful effect on pfs_months in any binary subgroup (sex, stage, brain mets, biomarker, ECOG, smoking, histology) — all subgroup mean differences are near zero with no significance after multiple-testing scrutiny.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib has no effect in any subgroup (including BRCA-mutant) contradicts the established PARP inhibitor paradigm and is therefore non-standard.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2+ patients, olaparib has no effect even after stratifying by sex, stage, ECOG, or other biomarkers.
judgments.rationale: Asserting olaparib has no benefit even within BRCA2+ patients across all strata contradicts guideline consensus on PARP inhibitor activity.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab has no clinically meaningful effect on pfs_months in any single-feature subgroup (PD-L1 high, TMB high, never-smokers, ECOG, histology, etc.).
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab has no benefit in PD-L1 high or TMB high subgroups contradicts established immunotherapy biomarker guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is largest in the joint subgroup pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1.
judgments.rationale: Greatest pembrolizumab benefit in jointly PD-L1 high and TMB high patients aligns with established immunotherapy biomarker paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib has no clinically meaningful effect on pfs_months in any feature subgroup including EGFR-mutated patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib has no benefit in EGFR-mutated patients directly contradicts standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within EGFR-mutated patients, osimertinib has no effect even after stratifying by sex, stage, brain mets, smoking, or histology.
judgments.rationale: Asserting osimertinib has no effect in EGFR-mutant patients across all strata contradicts the established EGFR-TKI paradigm.

judgments.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable linear model (all features and treatments), the only significant treatment main effect is treatment_sotorasib (positive); pembrolizumab, olaparib, and osimertinib are not associated with pfs_months. Major prognostic factors are age (positive), ECOG (negative), stage IV (negative), brain mets (negative), albumin (positive), weight loss (negative), smoking_status (current worse), histology (squamous worse), sex (female slightly worse), LDH (slightly negative), and brca2_mutation (slightly negative).
judgments.rationale: The composite claim that only sotorasib is significant while pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib lack effects contradicts standard targeted therapy and immunotherapy guidelines.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, age_years remains a strong positive predictor of pfs_months (~+0.17 months per year), unchanged from the unadjusted estimate.
judgments.rationale: Older age as a strong positive predictor of PFS contradicts the standard prognostic understanding that older age and frailty typically confer worse outcomes.

judgments.text: Across an exhaustive screen of every (treatment x feature) two-way interaction with the standard adjustment set, the strongest interactions all involve treatment_sotorasib (with kras_g12c and with sex_female); no other treatment shows any interaction reaching p<1e-3 with any feature.
judgments.rationale: That sotorasib benefits KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the established indication and would appear in any current NSCLC guideline.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males, the sotorasib treatment effect is abolished by brca2_mutation=1 (i.e., sotorasib does not increase pfs_months in KRAS G12C+ male brca2_mutation+ patients).
judgments.rationale: A subgroup-conditional claim that BRCA2 mutation abolishes sotorasib efficacy specifically in KRAS G12C+ males is not in any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males, the sotorasib treatment effect is abolished by alk_fusion=1 (i.e., sotorasib does not increase pfs_months in KRAS G12C+ male alk_fusion+ patients).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion as a modifier abolishing sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C+ males is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: The complete sotorasib responder subgroup is: kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND alk_fusion=0; in this group sotorasib produces a large positive pfs_months effect (~+5 months), and no smaller subgroup within it improves the effect further.
judgments.rationale: Defining the complete sotorasib responder set by the conjunction of KRAS G12C+, male, BRCA2 wild-type, and ALK fusion-negative goes well beyond the standard KRAS G12C indication.

judgments.text: In a single multivariable model the 4-way interaction structure {sotorasib + kras_g12c + sex_female + brca2_mutation/alk_fusion} captures the entire treatment-effect heterogeneity for sotorasib: positive 2-way kras_g12c*soto coefficient, negative 3-way soto*kras_g12c*sex_female, negative 3-way soto*kras_g12c*brca2_mutation, and negative 3-way soto*kras_g12c*alk_fusion.
judgments.rationale: A specific 4-way interaction structure capturing all sotorasib heterogeneity via sex, BRCA2, and ALK is a mechanistic claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: A T-learner gradient-boosting model trained separately on sotorasib-treated and untreated patients yields per-individual treatment effects whose mean is +4.16 in (kras_g12c=1 & sex_female=0 & brca2=0 & alk=0) and ~0 elsewhere; a regression tree fit on these ITE values splits first on kras_g12c then on sex_female, with consistent ~+4 effects in KRAS+ males and ~0 effects in all other leaves.
judgments.rationale: Per-individual ITE estimates concentrated only in KRAS+ BRCA2-/ALK- males via T-learner with tree splits is a granular causal-ML claim not in standard practice.

judgments.text: The positive age-pfs_months association is real and not confounded by stage, ECOG, brain mets, or other features — adjusted age coefficient stays +0.17/yr with R^2 contribution >0.5.
judgments.rationale: A robust positive age-PFS association independent of stage, ECOG, and brain mets contradicts the usual expectation that older age is neutral or adverse for PFS.

judgments.text: Mean PFS rises monotonically across age quartiles (Q1 < Q2 < Q3 < Q4).
judgments.rationale: Monotonic increase in PFS across age quartiles is a non-standard epidemiologic claim not endorsed by guidelines.

judgments.text: Even allowing a triple interaction treatment_pembrolizumab*pdl1_tps*tmb_high in a fully adjusted model, no significant pembrolizumab benefit appears in any (PD-L1, TMB) cell.
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab provides no benefit in any PD-L1/TMB cell directly contradicts established guideline-level evidence of benefit in PD-L1-high or TMB-high patients.

judgments.text: Even allowing triple interaction treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation*sex_female in a fully adjusted model, no significant olaparib benefit appears in BRCA2+ patients of either sex.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib provides no benefit in BRCA2-mutant patients of either sex contradicts the established PARP inhibitor indication and is therefore a non-standard, speculative finding.

judgments.text: An adjusted model within EGFR+ patients (controlling for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, sex, albumin, weight_loss) shows no osimertinib benefit; and the osimertinib*egfr_mutation interaction in the full sample is also null.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's benefit in EGFR+ NSCLC is established guideline-level knowledge; a null finding in this dataset is a negative analytic result, not a novel biological/clinical claim.

judgments.text: There are no statistically significant pairwise treatment-by-treatment interactions on pfs_months (i.e., combinations of pembro/sotorasib/olaparib/osimertinib do not synergize or antagonize beyond their main effects).
judgments.rationale: Absence of pairwise synergy/antagonism across pembro/sotorasib/olaparib/osimertinib is a non-guideline, dataset-specific multi-drug interaction claim.

judgments.text: The negative sex_female prognostic effect (~-0.20 months) is concentrated in KRAS G12C+ patients (where it is largely the consequence of sotorasib working only in males). In KRAS G12C-negative patients, sex has no prognostic effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A sex-by-KRAS G12C-conditional sotorasib effect (benefit only in males) is a speculative subgroup-mechanistic claim not in current NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: Relative to never-smokers, current smokers have shorter pfs_months and former smokers have slightly longer pfs_months (small positive); the smoking effect persists after full adjustment.
judgments.rationale: A differential PFS effect of current vs former smokers (former slightly better) after full adjustment is not standard guideline content.

judgments.text: PD-L1 TPS (pdl1_tps) has no prognostic association with pfs_months either as a linear or quadratic term, after adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, sex, smoking, histology, albumin, and weight loss.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines treat PD-L1 TPS as predictive/prognostic in many settings; a fully null linear and quadratic prognostic effect after adjustment contradicts paradigm expectations.

judgments.text: TMB-high (tmb_high) has no prognostic association with pfs_months after the same adjustment.
judgments.rationale: A null prognostic effect of TMB-high after adjustment runs counter to the prevailing paradigm that TMB carries prognostic/predictive signal.

judgments.text: Histology (squamous vs adenocarcinoma) modestly modifies the prognostic value of KRAS G12C: squamous patients have slightly higher PFS conditional on KRAS G12C than adenocarcinoma patients (positive squamous*kras_g12c interaction).
judgments.rationale: A positive squamous-by-KRAS G12C interaction on PFS is a cross-pathway histology-genotype claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: There is no subgroup in which treatment_sotorasib lowers pfs_months: in KRAS-negatives, KRAS+ females, KRAS+ male brca2+, and KRAS+ male alk_fusion+, the sotorasib effect is zero (not negative).
judgments.rationale: Claiming sotorasib is never harmful across several specific genotype/sex subgroups including KRAS-negatives is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional safety/efficacy claim.

judgments.text: An exhaustive two-feature subgroup screen for the non-sotorasib treatments (pembro, olap, osim) finds no subgroup with a clinically meaningful positive treatment effect (>0.3 months) and very strong p-value (<1e-5); the few nominally significant cells are small magnitude (|diff|<0.7) and not corroborated by adjusted models.
judgments.rationale: An exhaustive two-feature subgroup negative result for pembro/olaparib/osimertinib is a dataset-level meta-claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: The age benefit on pfs_months is smaller in patients with stage_iv=1, has_brain_mets=1, or higher ecog_ps (i.e., poor-prognosis features attenuate the per-year age benefit).
judgments.rationale: Attenuation of a per-year age PFS benefit by stage IV, brain mets, or higher ECOG is a specific interaction pattern not codified in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The fully refined sotorasib responder definition is kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND alk_fusion=0; the adjusted treatment effect rises monotonically as each restriction is added (overall +0.32 → KRAS+ +2.51 → KRAS+ male +4.57 → KRAS+ male brca2- +4.73 → KRAS+ male alk- +4.75 → KRAS+ male brca2- alk- +4.91).
judgments.rationale: Restricting sotorasib response to KRAS G12C + male + BRCA2-wildtype + ALK-fusion-negative is a multi-way subgroup-conditional claim that goes beyond the guideline-level KRAS G12C indication.

judgments.text: After adjusting for the full sotorasib*kras_g12c*sex_female*brca2*alk_fusion structure, the main effects of treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib remain near zero (|coef|<0.01) and non-significant (all p>0.1) in the full adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Stating that pembrolizumab, olaparib, and osimertinib have null main effects in an unselected pooled cohort is consistent with standard knowledge that these agents work only in their biomarker-defined populations.

judgments.text: Within sotorasib non-responders, no treatment among pembro/olap/osim shows any signal of effect (all |diff|<0.03, p>0.2).
judgments.rationale: Asserting that pembrolizumab, olaparib, and osimertinib all lack any signal specifically within sotorasib non-responders is a cross-pathway subgroup claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the canonical sotorasib responder subgroup (KRAS+ male brca2- alk-), pembrolizumab is associated with a slightly shorter mean pfs_months (negative direction) — likely a chance/noise finding given that the same pembrolizumab effect is null in every other subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab trends toward shorter PFS specifically in the KRAS G12C+/male/BRCA2−/ALK− sotorasib-responder niche is a speculative subgroup-conditional interaction not present in any guideline.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months in this NSCLC cohort.
judgments.rationale: Older age is a well-established adverse prognostic factor in NSCLC and appears in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter pfs_months than male patients.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines generally describe female sex as associated with better, not worse, NSCLC outcomes, so this directional claim contradicts paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers (smoking_status='current' lower than 'former'/'never').
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a prognostic factor in NSCLC is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (worse performance status → worse PFS).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse PFS is foundational guideline-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 (metastatic) is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Metastatic (stage IV) disease having worse PFS than non-metastatic is definitional and guideline-standard.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an adverse prognostic factor in NSCLC is well established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with adenocarcinoma histology have longer pfs_months than patients with squamous histology.
judgments.rationale: Adenocarcinoma generally having better outcomes than squamous NSCLC is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation=1 patients have longer pfs_months than EGFR-wildtype patients.
judgments.rationale: EGFR-mutant NSCLC having longer PFS (with targeted therapy) is a core guideline-level association.

judgments.text: kras_g12c=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 (KRAS-driven tumors typically have worse prognosis).
judgments.rationale: KRAS mutations being associated with worse prognosis in NSCLC is a standard textbook association.

judgments.text: alk_fusion=1 patients have longer pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 (driver alteration with available targeted therapy).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion conferring longer PFS due to available targeted therapy is guideline-standard knowledge.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months (STK11/LKB1 loss is a known poor-prognostic marker).
judgments.rationale: STK11/LKB1 loss is a well-established poor-prognostic marker in NSCLC and is recognized in current oncology literature.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months in untargeted population.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 association with PFS in an untargeted population reflects established DNA-repair biology and known prognostic/predictive associations.

judgments.text: tmb_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months (high TMB usually predicts immunotherapy benefit and reflects DNA-repair deficiency).
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting longer PFS (typically via immunotherapy benefit) is a guideline-level established association.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting longer PFS on immunotherapy is a standard guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin as a favorable prognostic marker is well-established across oncology and appears in standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook association across many cancer types.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as an adverse prognostic factor is a textbook ECOG/performance-status correlate.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established (e.g., GPS/mGPS scores).

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as an adverse prognostic marker is widely established across solid tumors.

judgments.text: None of hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl have a marginal association with pfs_months at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: A null finding for routine labs lacking PFS association is consistent with known weak/absent prognostic signal and not a novel claim.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall vs untreated patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS overall is a well-established standard finding reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit is established in KRAS G12C NSCLC and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in indicated populations is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit is well-established standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps>=0.5 (PD-L1 high), treatment_pembrolizumab=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0; the interaction treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps is positive.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 high status predicting pembrolizumab benefit is a textbook biomarker association.

judgments.text: Among tmb_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0; the treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is an established guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: Among stk11_mutation=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 has reduced or no benefit relative to wildtype; the treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction is negative.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a negative modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is a mechanistic subgroup claim not yet codified in standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Among kras_g12c=1 patients, treatment_sotorasib=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (positive treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C predicting sotorasib benefit is the defining guideline indication.

judgments.text: Among brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation predicting olaparib benefit is a textbook PARP inhibitor association.

judgments.text: Among egfr_mutation=1 patients, treatment_osimertinib=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting osimertinib benefit is the canonical guideline indication.

judgments.text: A multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all clinical/biomarker/lab features (no treatments) explains a substantial share of variance (R^2>0.5), with the largest |coefficients| coming from stage_iv, ecog_ps, has_brain_mets, smoking_status, histology, kras_g12c (positive in this cohort), albumin_g_dl, sex_female, and age_years.
judgments.rationale: Standard prognostic regression — stage IV, ECOG, brain mets, albumin, age are well-established PFS predictors in any oncology textbook.

judgments.text: Adding the four treatments and their key biomarker interactions (pembro×pdl1_tps, pembro×tmb_high, pembro×stk11_mutation, sotorasib×kras_g12c, olaparib×brca2_mutation, osimertinib×egfr_mutation, osimertinib×has_brain_mets) to the multivariable model materially improves R^2, and only the sotorasib×kras_g12c interaction remains significant.
judgments.rationale: Claim that pembro×PD-L1, pembro×TMB, olaparib×BRCA2, and osimertinib×EGFR interactions all fail to reach significance contradicts guideline-level biomarker–drug pairings.

judgments.text: A treatment_sotorasib × {sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high, pdl1_high, smoking_status, histology} interaction screen will identify kras_g12c as by far the largest modifier; other apparent significant interactions (sex, smoking, alk_fusion, egfr, brca2) reflect the lower frequency of kras_g12c in those subgroups rather than independent effect modification.
judgments.rationale: While KRAS G12C predicting sotorasib is guideline knowledge, the specific claim that other apparent modifiers are confounded by KRAS G12C frequency is an analytical/statistical inference not in textbooks.

judgments.text: A treatment_pembrolizumab × {biomarker} interaction screen across the same modifier list will not identify any modifier with p<0.01 — pembrolizumab has no detectable subgroup with a positive PFS effect at conventional thresholds.
judgments.rationale: Asserting pembrolizumab has no biomarker-defined responder subgroup at p<0.01 directly contradicts established PD-L1 TPS and TMB-high indications in guidelines.

judgments.text: Heterogeneity scans for treatment_olaparib and treatment_osimertinib will not identify any modifier with p<0.01; in particular brca2_mutation and egfr_mutation will not modify their respective treatment effects.
judgments.rationale: Failure of BRCA2 to modify olaparib and EGFR to modify osimertinib effects contradicts the foundational biomarker indications in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, stk11_mutation status does NOT meaningfully modify the sotorasib effect (the sotorasib×kras_g12c×stk11 three-way interaction p>0.05).
judgments.rationale: STK11 co-mutation as a (non-)modifier of sotorasib within KRAS G12C+ patients is an emerging research question, not established guideline content.

judgments.text: The maximally enriched subgroup for sotorasib benefit is kras_g12c=1 alone; restricting further to stk11_mutation=0 yields effectively the same effect size (~+2.55 to +2.59 mo).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C alone defining the sotorasib-benefit subgroup is precisely the FDA label and guideline indication; the magnitude estimate is descriptive rather than novel.

judgments.text: Within pdl1_tps>=0.5 patients, the three-way interaction treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high × stk11_mutation will not yield a clinically meaningful or statistically robust subgroup of pembrolizumab benefit.
judgments.rationale: Three-way PD-L1×TMB×STK11 conditional pembrolizumab effect modeling goes beyond guideline single-biomarker selection criteria.

judgments.text: The compound subgroup (pdl1_high=1 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0) does not show a positive pembrolizumab effect at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: A compound PD-L1-high/TMB-high/STK11-wildtype subgroup failing to show pembrolizumab benefit contradicts the prevailing guideline expectation that this is the most-enriched responder profile.

judgments.text: The three-way interaction treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation × has_brain_mets is not significant — osimertinib does not preferentially extend PFS in EGFR+ patients with brain metastases.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines specifically favor osimertinib in EGFR+ patients with brain metastases due to CNS penetration, so a null three-way interaction is a guideline-discordant claim.

judgments.text: Treatment effects stratified by ecog_ps will preserve the sotorasib benefit (in the KRAS-mixed treated group) across ECOG levels and will not reveal benefit for any other treatment in any ECOG stratum.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C NSCLC and lack of benefit for unrelated agents across ECOG strata is consistent with standard guideline-level expectations.

judgments.text: FINAL pembrolizumab subgroup hypothesis: there is NO subgroup, including (pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0), in which treatment_pembrolizumab=1 produces a statistically significant longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 in this cohort. Direction is essentially null/slightly negative.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1-high, TMB-high, STK11-wildtype NSCLC; claiming no subgroup benefit contradicts established paradigm and is a non-standard, novel claim.

judgments.text: FINAL sotorasib subgroup hypothesis: in patients with kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib=1 produces substantially longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (~+2.55 months, ~80% longer median PFS). Restricting further by stk11_mutation=0 does not materially change the effect (+2.59 mo); KRAS G12C+ alone is the operative subgroup definition.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: FINAL olaparib subgroup hypothesis: no subgroup, including brca2_mutation=1, shows a significant positive treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support PARP inhibitor (olaparib) benefit in BRCA2-mutant cancers; asserting no subgroup benefit including BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts standard paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL osimertinib subgroup hypothesis: no subgroup, including egfr_mutation=1 and egfr_mutation=1 AND has_brain_mets=1, shows a significant positive treatment_osimertinib effect on pfs_months in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib is the guideline-standard first-line therapy for EGFR-mutant NSCLC including CNS disease; claiming no subgroup benefit contradicts established consensus.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear effect on PFS).
judgments.rationale: Age effects on PFS are standard prognostic considerations in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter pfs_months than male patients (sex_female=0).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based prognostic differences are well-established and routinely discussed in oncology literature.

judgments.text: Smoking status modifies pfs_months, with current smokers having shorter PFS than former or never smokers.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a prognostic factor (especially in lung cancer) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear effect).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a textbook prognostic factor with worse PS predicting worse outcomes.

judgments.text: Adenocarcinoma histology is associated with longer pfs_months than squamous histology.
judgments.rationale: Histology-based prognostic differences between adenocarcinoma and squamous are standard NSCLC knowledge.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse outcomes than earlier stages is foundational TNM staging knowledge.

judgments.text: Brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a poor prognostic factor is established guideline content.

judgments.text: Patients with egfr_mutation=1 have longer pfs_months than EGFR wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting better outcomes (with TKI therapy) is standard NSCLC guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_g12c=1 have longer pfs_months than KRAS G12C wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C historically associated with worse prognosis; claiming longer PFS is non-standard and would require subgroup/treatment context.

judgments.text: Patients with alk_fusion=1 have shorter pfs_months than ALK-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusions are typically associated with better outcomes on targeted therapy; claiming shorter PFS contradicts guideline consensus and is non-standard.

judgments.text: Patients with stk11_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than STK11 wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations are well-established as predictors of poor outcomes and immunotherapy resistance in NSCLC, appearing in current treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than BRCA2 wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2's prognostic and predictive role is well-established in guideline-level oncology practice across multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: Patients with tmb_high=1 have longer pfs_months than TMB-low patients.
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting better immunotherapy outcomes is a guideline-endorsed biomarker (FDA approval for pembrolizumab in TMB-high tumors).

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS is a standard guideline biomarker for predicting immune checkpoint inhibitor benefit.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic marker is a textbook association reflecting nutritional/inflammatory status.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a classical guideline-level association across many cancers.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a poor prognostic factor is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of systemic inflammation predicting worse outcomes is well-established in oncology literature and increasingly in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as a poor prognostic marker is a widely recognized and frequently cited association in oncology.

judgments.text: Routine labs (hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) each have measurable main effects on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming each of these routine labs individually has a measurable main effect on PFS goes beyond guideline consensus, which only highlights select labs (e.g., LDH, albumin) rather than the full panel.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is a well-established main effect documented in NSCLC and other guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit is established in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC per current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit is established in BRCA/HRD-related cancers per current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months increases with higher pdl1_tps (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction).
judgments.rationale: Greater pembrolizumab benefit with higher PD-L1 TPS is a guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: Within pdl1_tps >= 0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab superiority in PD-L1 ≥50% is a standard first-line NSCLC guideline indication.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is greater in tmb_high=1 patients (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enhanced ICI benefit with high TMB is recognized in guidelines (tissue TMB-high pembrolizumab indication).

judgments.text: Within tmb_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TMB-high tumors is a guideline-recognized association.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is reduced in stk11_mutation=1 patients (negative treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction, with stk11_wt patients benefiting more).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a negative predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is a mechanistic subgroup claim not yet codified in standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in kras_g12c=1 patients (large positive treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy concentrated in KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the defining guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib=1 produces substantially longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Olaparib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in brca2_mutation=1 patients (positive treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor (olaparib) benefit concentrated in BRCA-mutant patients is a well-established interaction in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib=1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA2-mutant cancer is standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Osimertinib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in egfr_mutation=1 patients (positive treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's selective benefit in EGFR-mutant disease is a textbook biomarker-treatment interaction.

judgments.text: Within egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib=1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib efficacy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: A multivariable OLS model of pfs_months on demographics, comorbidities, mutations, and labs (no treatments) explains a substantial fraction of PFS variance, with ECOG, stage IV, weight loss, albumin, smoking, and histology emerging as the largest standardized contributors.
judgments.rationale: A specific multivariable prognostic model with this exact set of dominant standardized contributors is a quantitative claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: Adding the four treatments and their pre-specified biomarker interactions to the multivariable model substantially increases R² beyond the clinical-only model, with sotorasib × kras_g12c being the dominant treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Quantitative claim that sotorasib×KRAS G12C dominates incremental R² over other biomarker-treatment pairs is a specific cross-pathway comparison not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across all binary/categorical modifiers (sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, EGFR, KRAS G12C, ALK, STK11, BRCA2, TMB high, PD-L1 high, smoking status, histology), at least one treatment_pembrolizumab × modifier interaction reaches p<0.05 with a clinically meaningful magnitude.
judgments.rationale: Claiming any pembrolizumab interaction beyond PD-L1/TMB (e.g., with sex, smoking, histology, STK11, brain mets) reaches significance goes beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Beyond kras_g12c, sotorasib's effect on pfs_months is modified by sex_female, smoking_status, and histology, with the strongest interaction being sotorasib × sex_female (males benefit more than females).
judgments.rationale: Sex, smoking, and histology as modifiers of sotorasib effect — particularly a male-favoring sex interaction — are not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: There exists at least one binary/categorical modifier under which treatment_olaparib has a significant heterogeneous effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Heterogeneous olaparib effects by modifiers other than BRCA status are speculative and not guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: There exists at least one binary/categorical modifier under which treatment_osimertinib has a significant heterogeneous effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Generic claim that some modifier alters osimertinib's PFS effect is too vague to exceed guideline-level knowledge that EGFR-TKI effects vary by subgroup.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in the compound subgroup pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0 (the canonical biomarker-favorable IO subgroup).
judgments.rationale: High PD-L1 plus high TMB with wild-type STK11 is the canonical guideline-endorsed IO-favorable subgroup, not novel.

judgments.text: Within egfr_mutation=1 patients, osimertinib produces larger pfs_months gains in patients with has_brain_mets=1 than has_brain_mets=0 (CNS-active TKI rationale; positive osi × egfr × brain_mets interaction).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's CNS penetration and superior intracranial activity in EGFR-mutant brain-metastatic NSCLC is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, sotorasib's pfs_months benefit is suppressed by stk11_mutation=1 (negative sotorasib × kras × stk11 three-way interaction).
judgments.rationale: A specific three-way KRAS G12C × sotorasib × STK11 negative interaction is a mechanistic co-mutation modifier not codified in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1, olaparib's effect on pfs_months differs by histology (treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation × histology three-way interaction).
judgments.rationale: Histology-dependent modification of olaparib benefit within BRCA2-mutant disease is a speculative three-way interaction beyond standard PARP-inhibitor guidance.

judgments.text: Each treatment's effect on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps, with better ECOG (0) patients deriving the largest absolute treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Better ECOG performance status predicting larger treatment benefit is a textbook prognostic/predictive principle across oncology.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab's effect on pfs_months is greater (more positive) in current/former smokers than never-smokers (a known IO-response correlate).
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a positive correlate of immunotherapy response in NSCLC is a well-established, guideline-discussed association.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab's effect on pfs_months differs by sex (sex_female × treatment_pembrolizumab interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based heterogeneity of pembrolizumab efficacy is debated in the literature but not part of standard guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, sotorasib's effect on pfs_months is concentrated in male patients (sex_female=0) and absent in female patients, i.e. sex_female suppresses the sotorasib effect (negative treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female three-way interaction).
judgments.rationale: A male-restricted sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C, with a negative three-way sex interaction, is a speculative subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: There is no subgroup definable from the available features in which treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months by a clinically meaningful and statistically significant margin; the largest pre-specified biomarker-favorable subgroup (pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0) shows no benefit.
