judgments.rationale: Asserting that even the canonical biomarker-favorable IO subgroup derives no meaningful pembrolizumab benefit contradicts guideline consensus and is a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: The complete subgroup in which treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months is kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (male sex), with an effect of approximately +4.6 months; female sex is the unfavorable feature that suppresses the treatment effect within KRAS G12C+ patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of sotorasib efficacy within KRAS G12C+ patients is not part of guideline consensus and represents a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: There is no subgroup definable from the available features (including brca2_mutation=1) in which treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months significantly.
judgments.rationale: Denying any olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant disease contradicts established guideline consensus that PARP inhibitors benefit BRCA-mutant cancers, making this a negation of standard knowledge rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: There is no subgroup definable from the available features (including egfr_mutation=1) in which treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months significantly.
judgments.rationale: Denying any osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant disease contradicts established guideline consensus that osimertinib is the standard of care for EGFR-mutant NSCLC, making this a negation of standard knowledge rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic factor for PFS is a standard clinical consideration discussed in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: sex_female == 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than sex_female == 0.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in lung cancer outcomes are well-established in standard oncology literature.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs across smoking_status categories (never, former, current); current smokers have the shortest PFS.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a prognostic factor in lung cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a foundational prognostic factor in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: Adenocarcinoma histology has longer pfs_months than squamous histology.
judgments.rationale: Histology-based prognostic differences between adenocarcinoma and squamous NSCLC are standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: stage_iv == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring worse PFS than earlier stages is foundational staging-based prognostication.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an adverse prognostic factor is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation == 1 patients have different (likely longer) pfs_months than egfr_mutation == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting better PFS (with TKI therapy) is canonical guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: kras_g12c == 1 patients have different pfs_months than kras_g12c == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C as a prognostic/predictive biomarker in NSCLC is now established in current guidelines with sotorasib/adagrasib.

judgments.text: alk_fusion == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion == 0 patients (since untargeted ALK fusion is aggressive).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion prognosis without targeted therapy being aggressive is standard textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stk11_mutation == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations are well-established as predictors of poor response/PFS to immunotherapy in NSCLC and appear in current guidelines/reviews.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation == 1 patients have shorter pfs_months than brca2_mutation == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation associations with treatment response (e.g., PARP inhibitor sensitivity) are standard guideline knowledge, and shorter PFS in unspecified context is a generic, non-novel claim.

judgments.text: tmb_high == 1 patients have longer pfs_months than tmb_high == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting longer PFS on immunotherapy is established and FDA-recognized (pembrolizumab tissue-agnostic indication).

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is positively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting longer PFS on immune checkpoint inhibitors is a foundational, guideline-endorsed biomarker association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is positively associated with pfs_months (better nutritional status → longer PFS).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor is a well-known, textbook association across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is negatively associated with pfs_months (LDH reflects tumor burden / hypoxia).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is standard and incorporated into staging/prognostic systems (e.g., melanoma AJCC, lymphoma IPI).

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is negatively associated with pfs_months (cachexia → shorter PFS).
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is negatively associated with pfs_months (systemic inflammation → shorter PFS).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established in oncology literature and prognostic scores (e.g., Glasgow Prognostic Score).

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as a negative prognostic marker is widely established across solid tumors and reviewed extensively in guidelines/literature.

judgments.text: Other routine labs (hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) are each individually associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Routine lab abnormalities (anemia, elevated ALP, liver/renal dysfunction, electrolyte derangements) as prognostic markers are standard, textbook-level associations.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is a well-established standard-of-care finding in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_sotorasib == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_sotorasib == 0.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving PFS in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_olaparib == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_olaparib == 0.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS in BRCA/HRD-positive cancers is a standard guideline-based finding.

judgments.text: Patients with treatment_osimertinib == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_osimertinib == 0.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib improving PFS in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is a textbook standard-of-care result.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is larger (more positive) in patients with higher pdl1_tps — i.e., positive treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction.
judgments.rationale: Greater pembrolizumab benefit with higher PD-L1 TPS is a well-established guideline biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup pdl1_tps >= 0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 TPS >=50% is a standard guideline-based indication.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is larger in patients with tmb_high == 1 — i.e., positive treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is an FDA-recognized, guideline-acknowledged biomarker interaction.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup tmb_high == 1, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 patients have longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TMB-high tumors is a recognized guideline-supported indication (tissue-agnostic approval).

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is smaller (more negative) in patients with stk11_mutation == 1 — i.e., negative treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a negative modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is a mechanistic/subgroup claim not yet codified in standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup stk11_mutation == 0 (STK11 wild-type), treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 patients have longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Restricting pembrolizumab benefit to STK11 wild-type as a conditional subgroup effect goes beyond current guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: The treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months is much larger in patients with kras_g12c == 1 — i.e., strong positive treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's efficacy in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is the established indication and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup kras_g12c == 1, treatment_sotorasib == 1 patients have substantially longer mean pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Improved PFS with sotorasib in KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the standard guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup kras_g12c == 0, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib is only indicated for KRAS G12C-mutant tumors, so lack of effect in non-mutant patients is established consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months is larger in patients with brca2_mutation == 1 — i.e., positive treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction.
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutant tumors is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup brca2_mutation == 1, treatment_olaparib == 1 patients have longer mean pfs_months than treatment_olaparib == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's superior efficacy in BRCA2-mutant patients is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The treatment_osimertinib effect on pfs_months is larger in patients with egfr_mutation == 1 — i.e., positive treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's efficacy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup egfr_mutation == 1, treatment_osimertinib == 1 patients have longer mean pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib == 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Longer PFS with osimertinib in EGFR-mutant patients is the established standard-of-care rationale.

judgments.text: A multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all clinical features (age, sex, smoking, ECOG, histology, stage, brain mets, mutations, biomarkers, labs) explains a large fraction of variance (R² > 0.5).
judgments.rationale: Claiming clinical features alone explain >50% of PFS variance is a strong quantitative claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Adding treatments and the named treatment×biomarker interactions (pembro×PDL1, pembro×TMB, pembro×STK11, sotorasib×KRAS, olaparib×BRCA2, osimertinib×EGFR, osimertinib×brain_mets) to the multivariable model meaningfully increases R² beyond the clinical-only model.
judgments.rationale: A specific bundled R² gain from adding treatment×biomarker interactions is a quantitative modeling claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, the treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction remains the dominant statistically significant treatment-biomarker term.
judgments.rationale: Asserting sotorasib×KRAS dominates all other treatment-biomarker interactions after full adjustment is a cross-pathway comparative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across all candidate modifiers (sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high, pdl1_high, smoking_status, histology), there exists at least one with a significant pembrolizumab × modifier interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 and TMB as pembrolizumab effect modifiers are established guideline biomarkers, so existence of some modifier is trivially expected.

judgments.text: Beyond kras_g12c, additional modifiers (e.g., sex_female, smoking_status, histology, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation, egfr_mutation) significantly modify the treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's effect modification by non-KRAS factors (sex, smoking, histology, ALK/BRCA2/EGFR) is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Specifically, the treatment_sotorasib effect is smaller in sex_female == 1 patients (negative treatment_sotorasib × sex_female interaction).
judgments.rationale: A sex-based attenuation of sotorasib efficacy is a speculative subgroup claim absent from current KRAS G12C treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Across all candidate modifiers, there exists at least one with a significant treatment_olaparib × modifier interaction on pfs_months (including but not limited to brca2_mutation).
judgments.rationale: BRCA modification of olaparib response is the canonical PARP inhibitor paradigm, making this existence claim guideline-standard.

judgments.text: Across all candidate modifiers (including egfr_mutation, has_brain_mets, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation), there exists at least one with a significant treatment_osimertinib × modifier interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation as the defining modifier of osimertinib response is core guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within the compound subgroup defined by pdl1_tps >= 0.5 AND tmb_high == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
judgments.rationale: A compound triple-conditional subgroup invoking STK11 wild-type status goes beyond standard PD-L1/TMB-based pembrolizumab guidance.

judgments.text: The 3-way interaction treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high × stk11_mutation is significant within the pdl1_high subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A formal 3-way pembrolizumab × TMB × STK11 interaction within PD-L1-high patients is a mechanistic cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup pdl1_tps >= 0.5 AND stk11_mutation == 0, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning pembrolizumab benefit on STK11 wild-type status reflects emerging research, not established guideline practice.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup egfr_mutation == 1 AND has_brain_mets == 1, treatment_osimertinib == 1 produces longer mean pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib == 0 (osimertinib is CNS-penetrant).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's superior CNS activity in EGFR-mutant patients with brain metastases is explicitly recognized in current NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: The 3-way interaction treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation × has_brain_mets on pfs_months is significant.
judgments.rationale: The enhanced osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant brain-metastatic patients underlying this interaction is already guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup kras_g12c == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0, treatment_sotorasib == 1 produces substantially longer mean pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib == 0.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established and guideline-endorsed, including the recognition that STK11 wild-type tumors derive benefit.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup kras_g12c == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 1, the treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months is smaller than in the kras_g12c == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0 subgroup (negative 3-way interaction treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × stk11_mutation).
judgments.rationale: A specific three-way interaction quantifying attenuation of sotorasib benefit by STK11 co-mutation goes beyond guideline-level statements and reflects a mechanistic/subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months remains positive and significant across all three ecog_ps strata (0, 1, 2).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not assert uniform sotorasib benefit across all ECOG strata including ECOG 2, where trial data are sparse.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs across ecog_ps strata; specifically, pembrolizumab is harmful (negative diff) in ecog_ps == 1.
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab is actively harmful specifically at ECOG 1 contradicts guideline consensus and is a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib and treatment_osimertinib have null effects on pfs_months in every ecog_ps stratum.
judgments.rationale: A blanket null effect of olaparib and osimertinib across all ECOG strata, ignoring biomarker selection, is not a standard guideline statement.

judgments.text: Within the smoking_status == 'never' subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 patients have similar pfs_months to treatment_pembrolizumab == 0 patients (no benefit in never-smokers).
judgments.rationale: Reduced pembrolizumab benefit in never-smokers (often EGFR/ALK-driven, lower TMB) is well established in NSCLC guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: Within the smoking_status == 'former' subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0 patients (largest expected benefit).
judgments.rationale: Greater pembrolizumab benefit in former/current smokers versus never-smokers is established consensus.

judgments.text: Within the sex_female == 0 (male) subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in male NSCLC patients is part of standard practice and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the sex_female == 1 (female) subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in female NSCLC patients is also standard guideline-endorsed practice.

judgments.text: Even using a 3-way interaction with histology (treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation × histology), the treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months remains null in every BRCA2/histology subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction asserting null olaparib effect across every BRCA2 × histology subgroup is a non-standard cross-pathway/subgroup claim beyond guideline scope.

judgments.text: FINAL — Pembrolizumab: there is NO subgroup of pdl1_tps, tmb_high, or stk11_mutation in which treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 produces a longer mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab == 0; the most-studied compound subgroup (pdl1_tps >= 0.5 AND tmb_high == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0) shows a non-significant difference.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1, TMB, and STK11 are well-established biomarkers for pembrolizumab response in NSCLC and appear in current guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL — Sotorasib: within the subgroup kras_g12c == 1, treatment_sotorasib == 1 produces a markedly longer mean pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib == 0; the effect is preserved when further restricting to kras_g12c == 1 AND stk11_mutation == 0 (no STK11 suppression).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C is the established biomarker for sotorasib and is the FDA-approved indication; STK11 co-mutation effects on KRAS-targeted therapy are discussed but the core claim is standard.

judgments.text: FINAL — Olaparib: within the subgroup brca2_mutation == 1, treatment_olaparib has no effect on pfs_months; the named biomarker hypothesis is refuted in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant response to olaparib is a guideline-level association; a null finding in one dataset does not constitute a novel hypothesis but rather a failed replication of established biology.

judgments.text: FINAL — Osimertinib: within the subgroup egfr_mutation == 1, treatment_osimertinib has no effect on pfs_months; the named biomarker hypothesis is refuted in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: EGFR-mutant response to osimertinib is guideline-standard first-line therapy; a null finding here is a failed replication, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months — patients with ecog_ps=1 and ecog_ps=2 have shorter PFS than ecog_ps=0.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a universally recognized prognostic factor in oncology guidelines and routinely associated with shorter PFS.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than non-stage IV is foundational staging dogma in every oncology textbook.

judgments.text: Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a poor prognostic factor for PFS is standard guideline knowledge across solid tumors.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
judgments.rationale: Hypoalbuminemia as a negative prognostic marker is well-established and appears in standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH is a textbook adverse prognostic factor incorporated into staging systems (e.g., melanoma AJCC, lymphoma IPI).

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss as adverse prognostic factors are established in oncology guidelines and prognostic scoring systems.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (inflammation hypothesis).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammation marker is well-established (e.g., Glasgow Prognostic Score) and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR as an adverse prognostic biomarker is widely established across cancer types in the literature and clinical use.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (typical clinical expectation).
judgments.rationale: Older age as a typical adverse prognostic factor is standard clinical expectation reflected in guidelines and geriatric oncology assessments.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than male sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in cancer outcomes are recognized in guidelines for several tumor types and represent a standard demographic prognostic consideration.

judgments.text: Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than never smokers, with former smokers intermediate.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a negative prognostic factor in NSCLC is well-established in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Squamous histology is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
judgments.rationale: Squamous histology having worse outcomes than adenocarcinoma is standard prognostic knowledge in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Patients with egfr_mutation=1 have longer pfs_months than wild-type patients (univariate, before considering treatment).
judgments.rationale: EGFR-mutant NSCLC having better PFS (largely due to TKI therapy) is established paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_g12c=1 have longer pfs_months than wild-type patients (univariate; expected if sotorasib effect leaks into the marginal estimate).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C benefit reflects the well-known sotorasib/adagrasib effect in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with alk_fusion=1 have shorter pfs_months than wild-type patients (univariate).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion patients are well known and their outcomes on targeted therapy are guideline-standard; a univariate worse-PFS claim is not a novel paradigm-breaking finding but a straightforward marginal observation.

judgments.text: Patients with stk11_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a negative prognostic/predictive marker (especially with immunotherapy) is now established in NSCLC literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as an independent negative prognostic marker for PFS in NSCLC is not part of standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with tmb_high=1 have longer pfs_months than tmb_high=0 (TMB as favorable marker).
judgments.rationale: High TMB as a favorable predictive/prognostic marker (especially for immunotherapy) is established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months (favorable marker).
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting better outcomes (with immunotherapy) is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as a negative prognostic factor is a well-established general oncology principle.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, or bun_mg_dl are associated with shorter pfs_months (organ dysfunction).
judgments.rationale: Organ dysfunction reflected by elevated liver/renal labs predicting worse outcomes is a standard prognostic observation in oncology.

judgments.text: Treatment with treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months (overall, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS in NSCLC is established first-line guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment with treatment_sotorasib is associated with longer pfs_months (overall, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Treatment with treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months (overall, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA/HRD-selected populations is well established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment with treatment_osimertinib is associated with longer pfs_months (overall, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib improving PFS in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, LDH, weight loss, age, sex, smoking, histology, mutations, PDL1, TMB, and routine labs, treatment_pembrolizumab still shows a positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab retaining PFS benefit after adjustment for prognostic covariates is consistent with established trial and real-world evidence.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_sotorasib still shows a positive marginal effect on pfs_months (i.e. the unadjusted effect is not entirely explained by patient mix).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib retaining a marginal adjusted PFS effect is consistent with its established activity in KRAS G12C NSCLC.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_olaparib still shows no overall effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib has no overall adjusted PFS effect across an unselected population is a dataset-specific negative finding not stated as such in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_osimertinib still shows no overall effect.
judgments.rationale: Asserting osimertinib has no overall adjusted PFS effect contradicts the guideline-level expectation and is a non-standard, dataset-specific claim.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit (interaction coefficient) increases with higher pdl1_tps — i.e. the slope of pfs_months on treatment_pembrolizumab is larger when pdl1_tps is high.
judgments.rationale: Greater pembrolizumab benefit with higher PD-L1 TPS is a well-established, guideline-endorsed predictive relationship.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is greater in tmb_high=1 patients than tmb_high=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is established in NSCLC and pan-tumor guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is less effective (smaller or negative interaction coefficient) in stk11_mutation=1 patients than in stk11_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a negative predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is a research-level biomarker hypothesis not yet codified in standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is less effective in egfr_mutation=1 patients than in egfr_mutation=0 (clinical expectation: oncogene-addicted disease).
judgments.rationale: Reduced immunotherapy efficacy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is well established and reflected in guideline preference for TKIs first-line.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months substantially more in patients with kras_g12c=1 than in kras_g12c=0 (large positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's selective benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is the textbook indication for the drug.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months substantially more in patients with egfr_mutation=1 than in egfr_mutation=0 (positive interaction; expected clinically).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's benefit restricted to EGFR-mutant disease is the canonical guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months substantially more in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in brca2_mutation=0 (positive interaction; expected clinically).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1 patients, treatment_sotorasib is associated with longer pfs_months than non-sotorasib (large positive subgroup mean difference).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the established indication in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=0 patients, treatment_sotorasib is NOT associated with longer pfs_months (null in the complement).
judgments.rationale: Lack of sotorasib benefit outside KRAS G12C is the basis for its biomarker-restricted approval and is guideline-standard.

judgments.text: Within egfr_mutation=1 patients, treatment_osimertinib is associated with longer pfs_months than non-osimertinib.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib superiority in EGFR-mutant disease is textbook first-line therapy.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than non-olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib activity in BRCA2-mutant patients is a guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect is largest in pdl1_tps>=0.5 patients (subgroup mean comparison).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS ≥50% as a predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is established in NSCLC guidelines (KEYNOTE-024).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect is positive in tmb_high=1 patients but null in tmb_high=0.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high status as a predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is well-established and FDA-approved as a tumor-agnostic indication.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect is most positive in patients without stk11_mutation (stk11=0).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations as a negative predictor of immune checkpoint inhibitor response in NSCLC is widely recognized in the literature and emerging guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect differs across smoking_status — positive (or larger) in current/former smokers (smoking-induced TMB) versus never smokers.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status correlating with checkpoint inhibitor benefit via TMB is an established association in NSCLC immunotherapy literature.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect differs by histology — positive in squamous histology compared with adenocarcinoma.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy across squamous vs adenocarcinoma histology is well-characterized in approved guideline indications (KEYNOTE-407 vs KEYNOTE-189).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect differs across ECOG strata — concentrated in ECOG 0/1 (fitter patients).
judgments.rationale: Restriction of immunotherapy benefit to ECOG 0-1 is standard guideline practice and trial inclusion criteria.

judgments.text: Within pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND stk11_mutation=0, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months (combining the two most-cited positive predictors).
judgments.rationale: Combined conditional benefit specifically in PD-L1-high AND STK11-wildtype patients is a cross-pathway compound subgroup claim not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: The triple interaction treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps × stk11_mutation is non-zero (i.e. STK11 modifies the PDL1-dependence of pembro effect).
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction where STK11 modifies the PD-L1 dose-response of pembrolizumab is a mechanistic claim beyond textbook descriptions of each marker independently.

judgments.text: Treatment effect heterogeneity for treatment_pembrolizumab is most strongly driven by some feature other than PDL1/TMB/STK11 — i.e. an exhaustive screen will reveal an unexpected modifier.
judgments.rationale: Claiming an unexpected non-canonical modifier dominates over PD-L1/TMB/STK11 contradicts established predictor hierarchy.

judgments.text: Treatment effect heterogeneity for treatment_olaparib has its strongest interaction with brca2_mutation (verifying clinical expectation).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as the primary modifier of olaparib benefit recapitulates the established PARP-inhibitor synthetic lethality paradigm.

judgments.text: Treatment effect heterogeneity for treatment_osimertinib has its strongest interaction with egfr_mutation (verifying clinical expectation).
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation as the dominant predictor of osimertinib benefit is canonical guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Sotorasib's KRAS-G12C-conditional benefit is itself modified by stk11_mutation — KRAS+STK11 co-mutation reduces the sotorasib benefit (positive triple interaction with opposite sign).
judgments.rationale: STK11 co-mutation attenuating sotorasib benefit in KRAS-G12C NSCLC is a speculative co-mutation-conditional claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ patients is preserved across ECOG strata (does not vanish at ECOG 2 even though absolute PFS is shorter).
judgments.rationale: Preservation of sotorasib relative benefit across ECOG strata including ECOG 2 is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: Within egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib benefit is concentrated in patients with adenocarcinoma histology and never-smoker status (classical EGFR-mutant clinical phenotype).
judgments.rationale: Concentration of EGFR-TKI benefit in adenocarcinoma never-smokers is the textbook clinical phenotype.

judgments.text: Within egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib benefit is reduced in patients with brain metastases.
judgments.rationale: Reduced osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant patients with brain metastases contradicts the guideline-recognized CNS activity of osimertinib and is a non-standard subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients without competing oncogenic drivers (egfr=0 & alk_fusion=0), treatment_olaparib has a positive effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Restricting olaparib benefit to BRCA2+ patients lacking EGFR/ALK drivers is a cross-pathway subgroup conditioning not present in guidelines (and olaparib in NSCLC is itself non-standard).

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib effect is larger in fit (ecog_ps=0) patients than in poor-PS (ecog_ps=2) patients.
judgments.rationale: PARP-inhibitor benefit modification by ECOG performance status is a speculative subgroup interaction not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: When all four treatments and their canonical biomarker interactions are entered jointly with full prognostic adjustment, only treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c remains significant (the other three biomarker-targeted interactions vanish).
judgments.rationale: Claiming that EGFR-osimertinib, ALK-targeted, and PD-L1-pembrolizumab interactions all vanish under joint adjustment while only KRAS-G12C-sotorasib persists contradicts established paradigm.

judgments.text: There is no joint subgroup of pdl1, tmb, stk11, and egfr in which treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months — i.e. an exhaustive small-feature subgroup search returns no positive treatment-effect cell for pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: An exhaustive negative finding that no PD-L1/TMB/STK11/EGFR subgroup benefits from pembrolizumab contradicts guideline-endorsed pembrolizumab indications.

judgments.text: TMB high is NOT prognostic after adjustment for histology, smoking and other covariates — its negative univariate association is confounded by histology/smoking distribution.
judgments.rationale: Asserting TMB-high is non-prognostic after histology/smoking adjustment, with confounding explanation, is a mechanistic claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Age is positively associated with pfs_months even after adjustment (the inverted age-PFS relationship is not an artifact of confounding by other features).
judgments.rationale: A positive age–PFS association after adjustment contradicts the standard oncology paradigm where older age and worse performance status correlate with shorter PFS.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for sotorasib: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by approximately 2.5 months only in patients with kras_g12c=1; the effect is NOT further restricted by stk11_mutation status, ecog_ps, sex, smoking_status, or histology.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit restricted to KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the established label indication and guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for pembrolizumab: there is no subgroup definable from the available features in which treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months meaningfully — the effect is null overall and in every clinically-motivated subgroup tested.
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab has no benefit in any definable subgroup contradicts guideline consensus that PD-L1-high or specific histologic subgroups derive meaningful benefit.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for osimertinib: treatment_osimertinib has NO measurable effect on pfs_months in this dataset — including in patients with egfr_mutation=1, regardless of histology, smoking_status, brain metastases, or ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: Asserting osimertinib has no effect even in EGFR-mutant patients contradicts established guideline consensus that osimertinib substantially prolongs PFS in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

judgments.text: Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for olaparib: treatment_olaparib has NO measurable effect on pfs_months in this dataset — including in patients with brca2_mutation=1, regardless of histology, smoking, or PS strata.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib has no effect even in BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts the guideline-endorsed PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers.

judgments.text: Overall conclusion: the strongest predictors of pfs_months in this dataset are ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, age_years, smoking_status, and histology. Among the four treatments, only treatment_sotorasib shows efficacy, restricted to the kras_g12c=1 subgroup (~+2.5 months PFS); treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_osimertinib, and treatment_olaparib show no measurable PFS benefit overall or in any tested subgroup.
judgments.rationale: The composite claim that pembrolizumab, osimertinib, and olaparib show no benefit in any biomarker-defined subgroup contradicts current treatment guidelines for these agents.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months across the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Age effects on PFS are well-described prognostic considerations in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months across the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Hypoalbuminemia as a poor prognostic marker is a well-established textbook association across cancers.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months across the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months across the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is established in multiple cancer staging systems and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months across the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS predicting better outcomes (with immunotherapy) is a guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months across the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio as a poor prognostic marker is widely reported and increasingly guideline-referenced.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months across the full cohort.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established in oncology literature.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring shorter PFS is fundamental staging-based prognostication.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a poor prognostic factor is standard textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is foundational guideline-level prognostication.

judgments.text: Squamous histology is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
judgments.rationale: Squamous NSCLC having worse PFS than adenocarcinoma in many treatment contexts is a well-known clinical pattern reflected in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status effects on outcomes are standard prognostic considerations discussed in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter pfs_months than males.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based outcome differences in lung cancer (often favoring females) are well-described in textbooks; the directionality here is the opposite of the textbook association but still falls within standard demographic prognostic discussion.

judgments.text: Patients with tmb_high=1 have longer pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: High TMB as a favorable predictor, particularly with immunotherapy, is established guideline knowledge (e.g., FDA tissue-agnostic pembrolizumab indication for TMB-high).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 increases mean pfs_months versus treatment_pembrolizumab=0 in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is a guideline-standard finding across multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib=1 increases mean pfs_months versus treatment_sotorasib=0 in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving PFS (in KRAS G12C NSCLC) is established and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib=1 increases mean pfs_months versus treatment_olaparib=0 in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS (in BRCA/HRD contexts) is guideline-standard knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib=1 increases mean pfs_months versus treatment_osimertinib=0 in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib improving PFS in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is a cornerstone guideline recommendation.

judgments.text: Treatment assignment appears randomized: biomarker prevalence does not differ between treated and untreated groups for any of the four treatments.
judgments.rationale: A claim that treatment assignment is effectively randomized with respect to biomarker prevalence is a dataset-specific structural observation, not a guideline/textbook fact.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib increases pfs_months specifically in patients with egfr_mutation=1 (positive interaction term in pfs_months ~ treatment_osimertinib * egfr_mutation).
judgments.rationale: The positive osimertinib × EGFR-mutation interaction is the canonical biomarker-treatment interaction featured prominently in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months specifically in patients with kras_g12c=1 (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant tumors is the established indication and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months specifically in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA2-mutant tumors is a standard guideline-endorsed biomarker-drug association.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months more in patients with tmb_high=1 (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit with high TMB is a well-established, FDA-approved tumor-agnostic indication.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months more in patients with high pdl1_tps (positive interaction term).
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is textbook and embedded in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, treatment_sotorasib increases mean pfs_months by ~2.5 months relative to no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: A positive sotorasib effect in KRAS G12C patients restates the standard indication.

judgments.text: Within the egfr_mutation=1 subgroup, treatment_osimertinib does not change mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib has no effect in EGFR-mutant disease contradicts guideline consensus and is a non-standard, novel claim.

judgments.text: Within the brca2_mutation=1 subgroup, treatment_olaparib does not change mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib lacks benefit in BRCA2-mutant disease contradicts established guideline evidence and is novel.

judgments.text: Within the tmb_high=1 subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab does not change mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no pembrolizumab benefit in TMB-high tumors contradicts the approved tumor-agnostic indication and is novel.

judgments.text: Within the alk_fusion=1 subgroup, treatment_osimertinib increases mean pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in ALK-fusion tumors is a cross-pathway claim not supported by guidelines and would be novel.

judgments.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, the sotorasib treatment effect is modified by stk11_mutation: sotorasib benefit is smaller in stk11_mutation=1 patients than in stk11_mutation=0 (negative 3-way interaction).
judgments.rationale: A three-way STK11-modifying effect on sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, the sotorasib treatment effect is larger in patients without brain metastases (has_brain_mets=0) than with brain metastases.
judgments.rationale: Reduced efficacy of targeted therapies including sotorasib in patients with brain metastases is a well-recognized clinical pattern in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, the sotorasib treatment effect differs by histology (adenocarcinoma vs squamous).
judgments.rationale: Differential sotorasib efficacy by histology within KRAS G12C is not an established guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 subgroup, the sotorasib treatment effect on pfs_months differs sharply between males and females, with substantially larger benefit in males.
judgments.rationale: A large sex-based difference in sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C NSCLC is not part of established treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The sotorasib effect modification by sex within KRAS+ is captured by a significant 3-way interaction in pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib * kras_g12c * sex_female: positive 2-way (sotorasib:kras_g12c) and negative 3-way (sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female).
judgments.rationale: A specific three-way interaction structure among treatment, KRAS status, and sex is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS+ males, the sotorasib treatment effect on pfs_months increases with age_years (positive sotorasib×age interaction).
judgments.rationale: An age-dependent increase in sotorasib benefit specifically within KRAS+ males is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The 4-way interaction treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female × age_years is significant (sex_female attenuates the kras+ × age sotorasib effect).
judgments.rationale: A four-way interaction involving treatment, KRAS, sex, and age is a highly speculative subgroup claim far beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There exists at least one feature that significantly modifies the pembrolizumab treatment effect on pfs_months (interaction p<0.05) when screened across all 20 candidate features.
judgments.rationale: It is well established that pembrolizumab efficacy is modified by features such as PD-L1, TMB, smoking, and STK11, so finding at least one moderator is expected.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab provides a positive treatment effect on pfs_months in at least one single-stratum subgroup (sex, smoking, ECOG, histology, stage_iv, brain mets, EGFR, KRAS, ALK, STK11, BRCA2, or TMB-high).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in subgroups like TMB-high, smokers, or PD-L1-high NSCLC is established standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: There exists at least one feature in the dataset (any candidate moderator) that significantly modifies the olaparib treatment effect on pfs_months at p<0.01.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib moderators in a general NSCLC-style dataset beyond BRCA/HRD are not established and would represent a novel signal.

judgments.text: Olaparib provides a positive treatment effect on pfs_months in at least one single-stratum subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant or HRD subgroups is a well-known guideline-level association.

judgments.text: There exists a subgroup (defined by any feature combination) in which osimertinib provides a positive treatment effect on pfs_months larger than 0.3 months.
judgments.rationale: Generic existence claim of any subgroup with a small positive osimertinib effect is trivially within standard expectations for an EGFR-targeted agent.

judgments.text: The osimertinib×egfr_mutation effect, if absent overall, may emerge in a sub-stratum defined by an additional feature (3-way interaction screen).
judgments.rationale: Proposing that the canonical osimertinib×EGFR effect is absent overall but emerges only in a 3-way stratum is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model adjusted for age, sex, ECOG, histology, stage IV, brain mets, lab markers, smoking, and biomarkers, only treatment_sotorasib retains a significant positive coefficient on pfs_months among the four treatments.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that in multivariable adjustment only sotorasib retains a positive PFS coefficient among four targeted/IO agents contradicts guideline-level expectations for osimertinib, pembrolizumab, and olaparib.

judgments.text: When the treatment-by-biomarker interactions are added to the multivariable model (sotorasib×kras_g12c, olaparib×brca2_mutation, osimertinib×egfr_mutation, pembrolizumab×tmb_high, pembrolizumab×pdl1_tps), only sotorasib×kras_g12c remains significant.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that of five canonical treatment×biomarker interactions only sotorasib×KRAS-G12C survives is a non-guideline, dataset-specific claim.

judgments.text: A T-learner CATE analysis (random forests fit separately to treated vs untreated, predict difference) followed by a depth-3 decision tree on the predicted CATE will (a) identify KRAS+ × male sex as the primary modifier of sotorasib effect; and (b) reveal essentially no clinically meaningful heterogeneity for pembrolizumab, olaparib, or osimertinib.
judgments.rationale: A CATE/decision-tree finding that KRAS+ × male sex is the primary modifier of sotorasib and that no heterogeneity exists for the other three agents is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 subgroup (KRAS+ males, n=3,508), treatment_sotorasib increases mean pfs_months by approximately 4.6 months versus no sotorasib, irrespective of histology.
judgments.rationale: A sex-conditional ~4.6-month PFS benefit of sotorasib in KRAS-G12C males irrespective of histology is not an established paradigm and would not appear in a treatment guideline.

judgments.text: Within KRAS+ females (n=2,860), no patient subgroup shows a clinically meaningful (>0.3 mo) sotorasib treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming complete absence of any sotorasib-benefiting subgroup among KRAS-G12C females contradicts the standard biomarker-only paradigm and is a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The dataset's strongest prognostic factor for pfs_months is age_years, with each additional year of age adding approximately 0.16 months of PFS in the multivariable model — opposite the usual real-world direction.
judgments.rationale: A positive age coefficient (~+0.16 months PFS per year) opposite the usual real-world direction is a non-standard, dataset-specific prognostic claim.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease and ECOG performance status are strong negative prognostic factors for pfs_months independent of treatment.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV and worse ECOG as negative prognostic factors independent of treatment is textbook oncology consensus.

judgments.text: Final synthesis: the only clinically meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity in this dataset is treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female, with positive effect concentrated in patients meeting all three predicates (kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0), magnitude ≈ +4.6 months PFS, additionally amplified by older age (+0.03 mo per year). No subgroup yields a positive treatment effect for pembrolizumab (with or without conditioning on tmb_high or pdl1_tps), olaparib (with or without brca2), or osimertinib (with or without egfr) in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: A synthesis restricting all meaningful heterogeneity to sotorasib×KRAS×male with age amplification and denying any benefit subgroup for pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib is a strongly non-guideline, speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (worse PFS for ECOG 1 and 2 vs 0).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status is a well-established standard prognostic factor for worse PFS in essentially all solid tumors and appears in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: stage_iv = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv = 0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having shorter PFS than non-stage IV is foundational TNM staging knowledge in standard oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets = 0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a poor prognostic factor for PFS is standard guideline-level knowledge in oncology.

judgments.text: histology = squamous is associated with shorter pfs_months than histology = adenocarcinoma.
judgments.rationale: Squamous histology generally carrying worse prognosis than adenocarcinoma in lung cancer is a well-known textbook association.

judgments.text: Current smoking_status is associated with shorter pfs_months than former or never smoking.
judgments.rationale: Current smoking status as a negative prognostic factor in cancer outcomes is well-documented in standard oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
judgments.rationale: A positive correlation between older age and longer PFS contradicts conventional wisdom and is not a guideline-level association.

judgments.text: sex_female = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than sex_female = 0.
judgments.rationale: Female sex predicting shorter PFS contradicts established oncology paradigms where female sex is typically associated with better or equivalent outcomes in most solid tumors.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin predicting longer PFS reflects the well-established prognostic role of nutritional status and is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as a negative prognostic factor (cancer cachexia) is foundational oncology knowledge appearing in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic biomarker is well-established in oncology guidelines across many tumor types.

judgments.text: Lab markers crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl have detectable univariate associations with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Routine lab prognostic markers like CRP, NLR, hemoglobin, and LFTs having univariate associations with PFS is a well-established generic prognostic finding.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months (typical NSCLC favourable prognosis).
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting favorable PFS in NSCLC is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: kras_g12c = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months (typical adverse prognosis).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C as an adverse prognostic marker in NSCLC is established paradigm knowledge.

judgments.text: alk_fusion = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months (typical favourable prognosis).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion association with favorable PFS is textbook NSCLC knowledge.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as an adverse prognostic/predictive marker (especially with immunotherapy) is well-established in NSCLC literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as an adverse PFS marker is not standard NSCLC paradigm and would not appear in current NSCLC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: tmb_high = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting longer PFS (especially with immunotherapy) is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS associating with longer PFS on immunotherapy is standard NSCLC paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS in NSCLC is standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving PFS in KRAS G12C NSCLC is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Overall PFS benefit of olaparib is well established in guideline-endorsed populations (e.g., BRCA-mutant ovarian/breast/prostate/pancreatic cancer).

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's overall PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with kras_g12c = 1 than in those with kras_g12c = 0 (positive interaction between sotorasib and kras_g12c).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib is specifically indicated for KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC; the predictive interaction is the basis for its approval.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with egfr_mutation = 1 than in those with egfr_mutation = 0.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting osimertinib benefit is the canonical biomarker-drug pairing in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with brca2_mutation = 1 than in those with brca2_mutation = 0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation predicting olaparib benefit is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 than in those with pdl1_tps < 0.5.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS >=50% predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is standard NSCLC guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab provides a larger PFS benefit in tmb_high = 1 patients than in tmb_high = 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is an FDA-approved tissue-agnostic indication and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect varies with histology (squamous vs adenocarcinoma).
judgments.rationale: Differential pembrolizumab efficacy by squamous vs adenocarcinoma histology is not a guideline-level predictive claim independent of PD-L1.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect varies with stk11_mutation (worse with STK11 co-mutation, established literature signal).
judgments.rationale: STK11 co-mutation as a negative modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is a research-literature signal, not a guideline-level recommendation.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is larger in male (sex_female=0) than in female (sex_female=1) patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C+ patients is a speculative subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is attenuated by alk_fusion = 1 (sotorasib effect is smaller or absent when ALK is also positive).
judgments.rationale: Speculative cross-pathway interaction claim that ALK fusion attenuates KRAS G12C-directed sotorasib benefit is not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is attenuated by brca2_mutation = 1.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 attenuating sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ patients is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not described in guidelines.

judgments.text: The sotorasib treatment effect is fully concentrated in the joint subgroup defined by kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; outside this subgroup the sotorasib effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A highly specific four-way joint subgroup defining the entirety of sotorasib benefit is far beyond guideline-level claims.

judgments.text: The three-way interaction treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female on pfs_months is significant and negative (sex_female cancels the sotorasib×kras_g12c benefit).
judgments.rationale: A sex-specific cancellation of the KRAS G12C × sotorasib effect is a speculative three-way interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment effects of pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib do not interact with sex_female (no sex-modified main effect for these drugs).
judgments.rationale: Absence of sex-based effect modification for pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib is consistent with current guideline practice that does not stratify by sex.

judgments.text: Treatment effects of pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib do not vary across any of the binary biomarkers (sex_female, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high) — i.e., these treatments are inactive throughout the cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claiming these established drugs are inactive across all biomarker subgroups (including EGFR for osimertinib, BRCA2 for olaparib, TMB/PD-L1 for pembrolizumab) contradicts well-established guideline associations.

judgments.text: Continuous prognostic biomarkers (age_years, pdl1_tps, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl) do not modify the pembrolizumab, olaparib, or osimertinib treatment effects (no continuous-feature × treatment interaction).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not generally specify continuous biomarker × treatment interactions for these drugs beyond known thresholds, so a null is consistent with standard paradigm.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 2-way binary-feature subgroup search will reveal at least one subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically significant (p<1e-3) treatment_pembrolizumab effect.
judgments.rationale: A specific 2-way binary subgroup with strong pembrolizumab benefit beyond standard PD-L1/TMB indicators would be a novel data-driven finding.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 2-way binary-feature subgroup search will reveal at least one subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically significant (p<1e-3) treatment_olaparib effect.
judgments.rationale: An exhaustively-discovered binary 2-way subgroup with significant olaparib benefit beyond BRCA status would go beyond guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 2-way binary-feature subgroup search will reveal at least one subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically significant (p<1e-3) treatment_osimertinib effect.
judgments.rationale: A 2-way binary subgroup with significant osimertinib benefit beyond EGFR mutation status alone would be a novel data-driven finding.

judgments.text: An exhaustive 2-way binary-feature subgroup search for treatment_sotorasib will identify the strongest sotorasib-benefit subgroup as kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (KRAS G12C+ males), with effect > +4 mo.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the strongest sotorasib benefit is restricted to KRAS G12C+ males is a speculative sex-conditional subgroup claim not supported by guidelines, which recommend sotorasib for KRAS G12C+ NSCLC regardless of sex.

judgments.text: A multivariable OLS for pfs_months including all features yields independent prognostic effects: ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, histology=squamous (negative); age_years, albumin_g_dl, former/never smoking (positive); with kras_g12c showing a positive coefficient and alk_fusion close to null after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: These are standard prognostic associations (ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, squamous histology, albumin, smoking status) well-established in NSCLC textbooks and guidelines.

judgments.text: When the sotorasib three-way interaction terms (sotorasib×kras_g12c, sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex_female, sotorasib×kras_g12c×alk_fusion, sotorasib×kras_g12c×brca2_mutation) are included in a multivariable model, the sotorasib×kras_g12c benefit is large (>+4 mo) and the female/alk/brca2 sub-interactions are large negative (each >|2 mo|), confirming the multi-feature subgroup definition under joint adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Three-way interactions specifying that sotorasib benefit is attenuated by female sex, ALK fusion, or BRCA2 mutation among KRAS G12C+ patients are speculative cross-pathway/subgroup claims not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males without ALK fusion or BRCA2 mutation, the sotorasib benefit is approximately +5 mo and is the largest single subgroup-treatment effect in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: A precisely-defined four-predicate subgroup (KRAS G12C+, male, ALK-negative, BRCA2-wildtype) yielding ~+5 mo sotorasib benefit is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Treatment effect heterogeneity for pembrolizumab, olaparib, and osimertinib does not concentrate in any plausible 3-way subgroup (e.g., pembro×PD-L1-high×TMB-high, olaparib×BRCA2×TMB-high, osimertinib×EGFR×never-smoker).
judgments.rationale: Asserting that no plausible biomarker-defined subgroup captures heterogeneity for pembrolizumab (PD-L1/TMB), olaparib (BRCA2/TMB), or osimertinib (EGFR/never-smoker) contradicts established guideline-level predictive biomarkers and is a non-standard negative claim.

judgments.text: FINAL — The complete treatment-effective subgroup for treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months is: kras_g12c = 1 AND sex_female = 0 AND alk_fusion = 0 AND brca2_mutation = 0. Within this subgroup the sotorasib effect on pfs_months is approximately +5 months; in any patient not meeting all four predicates the sotorasib effect is null. No analogous treatment-effective subgroup exists for treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, or treatment_osimertinib in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: A four-predicate treatment-effective subgroup definition for sotorasib combined with a sweeping null claim for pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib goes far beyond guideline consensus, which recognizes PD-L1, BRCA, and EGFR as predictive biomarkers.

judgments.text: In the ds001_nsclc cohort, the marginal distribution of pfs_months has a positive mean and a moderate spread (mean roughly 3 months, sd ~2).
judgments.rationale: A descriptive statistic about marginal PFS distribution is not a clinical claim and contains no novel association.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear trend) in the cohort.
judgments.rationale: Age and PFS associations in NSCLC are well-described demographic correlations covered in standard prognostic discussions.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female==1) have shorter pfs_months on average than male patients (sex_female==0).
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in NSCLC outcomes are widely documented in guidelines and epidemiologic literature.

judgments.text: smoking_status is associated with pfs_months, with at least one of the three categories (never/former/current) having a different mean PFS.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a prognostic/predictive factor in NSCLC (especially regarding driver mutations and outcomes) is established textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear trend).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status as a prognostic factor for shorter PFS/OS is a foundational, guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Adenocarcinoma histology is associated with longer pfs_months than non-adenocarcinoma (squamous) histology.
judgments.rationale: Histology-based prognostic differences between adenocarcinoma and squamous NSCLC are standard textbook content.

judgments.text: stage_iv==1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease predicting shorter PFS than earlier stage is the definitional basis of staging and fully guideline-level.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets==1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring worse PFS in NSCLC is a well-established prognostic factor in guidelines.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation==1 patients have a different (likely longer) pfs_months than egfr_mutation==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation status predicting different (typically longer on TKI) PFS is a cornerstone guideline association in NSCLC.

judgments.text: alk_fusion==1 patients have a different pfs_months than alk_fusion==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion as a predictive biomarker affecting PFS with targeted therapy is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: kras_g12c==1 patients have different pfs_months from kras_g12c==0 patients overall (marginal effect).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C status is a well-established prognostic and predictive biomarker in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation==1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stk11_mutation==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations are widely recognized in guidelines/literature as associated with worse outcomes and immunotherapy resistance in NSCLC.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation==1 patients have a different pfs_months from brca2_mutation==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status is a standard biomarker discussed in oncology guidelines for prognosis and therapy selection.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear trend).
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting better outcomes (especially with immunotherapy) is a guideline-level established association.

judgments.text: tmb_high==1 patients have longer pfs_months than tmb_high==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: High TMB as a favorable predictor of immunotherapy response and outcomes is established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor (and conversely higher albumin = better outcomes) is textbook oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker is a classic, guideline-recognized association across many cancers.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of poor prognosis is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as a poor prognostic marker is widely established across multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: Lower hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as a negative prognostic factor is well-established in oncology guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Liver-function panel labs (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl) are associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Abnormal liver function tests as prognostic markers (often reflecting metastatic burden) are standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Renal/electrolyte panel labs (creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) are associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Renal function and electrolyte abnormalities as prognostic indicators are routinely covered in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: After joint adjustment, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and adenocarcinoma histology each retain independent prognostic associations with pfs_months in the directions established marginally (negative for ECOG/stage IV/brain mets/weight loss, positive for adenocarcinoma).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status, stage IV, brain metastases, weight loss, and adenocarcinoma histology are all canonical prognostic factors in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: After joint adjustment, the apparent age and sex effects on pfs_months persist or change direction; specifically, female sex remains independently associated with shorter PFS.
judgments.rationale: Female sex is generally associated with better, not worse, PFS in NSCLC; an independent association with shorter PFS would contradict guideline consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab==1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab==0 patients on average.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is a guideline-endorsed standard of care in multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib==1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib==0 patients on average.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving PFS in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib==1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib==0 patients on average.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA-mutant and HRD cancers is a textbook/guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib==1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib==0 patients on average.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is a foundational guideline recommendation.

judgments.text: After adjustment for prognostic features and other treatments, treatment_sotorasib retains a positive independent association with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: An independent PFS benefit of sotorasib after adjustment is consistent with its established guideline-supported efficacy.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib do NOT have material independent main effects on pfs_months (effects ~0 / not clinically meaningful).
judgments.rationale: Null main effects of targeted/immunotherapy agents averaged across unselected populations is consistent with the well-established biomarker-driven paradigm and would be expected per guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_sotorasib==1 vs ==0 is concentrated in kras_g12c==1 patients (positive treatment-by-KRAS-G12C interaction).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C-restricted benefit of sotorasib is the established indication and appears in current NSCLC treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Among kras_g12c==0 patients, treatment_sotorasib has no association with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of sotorasib activity outside KRAS G12C is the established biomarker-restricted paradigm reflected in guidelines and FDA labeling.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_osimertinib==1 vs ==0 is concentrated in egfr_mutation==1 patients (positive treatment-by-EGFR interaction), as expected biologically.
judgments.rationale: EGFR-mutant restricted benefit of osimertinib is textbook standard-of-care and explicitly noted as 'expected biologically'.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_olaparib==1 vs ==0 is concentrated in brca2_mutation==1 patients (positive treatment-by-BRCA2 interaction), as expected biologically.
