judgments.rationale: BRCA-mutant restricted benefit of olaparib (PARP inhibitor synthetic lethality) is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is greater in patients with higher pdl1_tps (positive treatment-by-PD-L1 interaction).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS as a predictive biomarker for pembrolizumab benefit is a guideline-endorsed standard companion diagnostic.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is greater in tmb_high==1 patients (positive treatment-by-TMB-high interaction).
judgments.rationale: TMB-high as a predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is a recognized pan-tumor FDA-approved indication and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is reduced (less positive / negative interaction) in stk11_mutation==1 patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 as a negative predictor of immunotherapy benefit is a translational research finding not yet codified as a routine guideline-level biomarker for treatment selection.

judgments.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab differs between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.
judgments.rationale: Differential pembrolizumab benefit by adeno vs squamous histology in NSCLC is not an established guideline-level predictive distinction (KEYNOTE trials show benefit in both).

judgments.text: For each of the four treatments (pembrolizumab, sotorasib, olaparib, osimertinib), at least one feature in the dataset has a treatment-by-feature interaction effect on pfs_months at p<0.05 — to be discovered by systematic interaction screening.
judgments.rationale: An open-ended systematic interaction screen guaranteeing at least one significant treatment-by-feature interaction per drug is a data-driven discovery claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c==1 patients, the treatment_sotorasib pfs_months benefit is concentrated in male patients (sex_female==0); among female patients (sex_female==1), the sotorasib effect is essentially absent.
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific restriction of sotorasib benefit to males within KRAS G12C+ NSCLC is not an established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c==1 patients, the treatment_sotorasib pfs_months benefit is suppressed in alk_fusion==1 patients; the benefit is essentially restricted to alk_fusion==0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Co-occurring ALK fusion as a negative modifier of sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C+ disease is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c==1 patients, smoking_status (never vs ever) does NOT meaningfully modify the sotorasib effect.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status not modifying sotorasib effect in KRAS G12C+ NSCLC aligns with the standard understanding that KRAS G12C is itself the predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: In the joint subgroup defined by kras_g12c==1 AND sex_female==0 AND alk_fusion==0 (KRAS G12C+, male, ALK-fusion negative), treatment_sotorasib produces a markedly larger pfs_months benefit than in any individual KRAS G12C+ subset; in the complementary KRAS G12C+ population (any of: female, ALK+), the sotorasib effect is small or null.
judgments.rationale: A three-way subgroup (KRAS G12C+/male/ALK-negative) defining a markedly enriched sotorasib benefit is a speculative conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is heterogeneous: in patients with low weight_loss_pct_6mo (lowest tertile) AND non-stage-IV disease, pembrolizumab is associated with SHORTER pfs_months (small negative effect), whereas in higher-weight-loss / stage-IV strata the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A negative pembrolizumab PFS effect confined to low-weight-loss, non-stage-IV patients is a non-standard subgroup-conditional harm signal not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Even in carefully refined egfr_mutation==1 subgroups (e.g., adenocarcinoma + never-smoker; ALK-negative; ECOG==0 + no brain mets), treatment_osimertinib still has no detectable pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: Absence of osimertinib benefit even in refined EGFR-mutant subgroups contradicts established guideline consensus and is therefore a non-paradigm claim.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation==1, treatment_olaparib has no detectable pfs_months benefit even after stratifying on bun_mg_dl, ecog_ps, or histology.
judgments.rationale: Lack of olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant patients across stratifications contradicts established PARP inhibitor guideline consensus and is a non-paradigm claim.

judgments.text: FINAL — Sotorasib: treatment_sotorasib==1 increases pfs_months by approximately 4.6–4.9 months relative to treatment_sotorasib==0 in the subgroup defined by kras_g12c==1 AND sex_female==0 AND alk_fusion==0 (KRAS G12C-positive, male, ALK-fusion-negative). Outside this subgroup (any of: kras_g12c==0, sex_female==1, alk_fusion==1), the sotorasib effect is small or null.
judgments.rationale: Quantified sotorasib benefit restricted to KRAS G12C+/male/ALK-negative patients with null effect elsewhere is a speculative conditional effect not in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL — Pembrolizumab: across the entire cohort and within all clinically motivated biomarker subgroups (high pdl1_tps, tmb_high==1, stk11_mutation==0, adenocarcinoma, ECOG==0), treatment_pembrolizumab does not produce a positive pfs_months benefit in this dataset; the only signal is a small NEGATIVE effect (~−0.1 months) in lower-burden patients (low weight_loss_pct_6mo and/or stage_iv==0).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab failing across all standard biomarker-enriched subgroups, with a small negative signal in low-burden patients, contradicts guideline consensus and is non-paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL — Osimertinib: treatment_osimertinib does not produce a positive pfs_months benefit in any subgroup, including egfr_mutation==1 patients; this is biologically anomalous compared with real-world evidence.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib having no benefit in EGFR-mutant patients is explicitly anomalous versus guideline/real-world evidence and thus non-paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL — Olaparib: treatment_olaparib does not produce a positive pfs_months benefit in any subgroup, including brca2_mutation==1 patients; this is biologically anomalous compared with real-world evidence.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib lacks PFS benefit even in BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts established guideline consensus (PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers) rather than extending beyond it, and is framed as a dataset anomaly rather than a novel biological hypothesis.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years) is positively correlated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Age effects on PFS are commonly examined in standard prognostic analyses and not a novel cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter mean pfs_months than males (sex_female=0).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in outcomes are routinely reported and not beyond guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is established prognostic dogma in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Stage-IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV being associated with worse outcomes than earlier stages is foundational staging consensus.

judgments.text: Brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a poor prognostic factor is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Mean pfs_months differs across smoking_status groups (never/former/current).
judgments.rationale: Smoking status differences in NSCLC outcomes are well-established and routinely discussed in guidelines.

judgments.text: Adenocarcinoma histology has longer pfs_months than squamous histology.
judgments.rationale: Histology-based prognostic differences between adenocarcinoma and squamous NSCLC are standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: EGFR-mutant patients (egfr_mutation=1) have longer pfs_months than egfr_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: EGFR-mutant NSCLC having longer PFS on targeted therapy is core guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: KRAS G12C-mutant patients (kras_g12c=1) have longer pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: A general PFS advantage for KRAS G12C-mutant patients is not established guideline consensus and would be a non-obvious claim.

judgments.text: ALK-fusion-positive patients (alk_fusion=1) have shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: ALK-fusion-positive patients typically have favorable PFS on targeted therapy, so a shorter-PFS claim contradicts guideline consensus and is non-standard.

judgments.text: STK11-mutant patients (stk11_mutation=1) have different (any direction) pfs_months than stk11_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations are a well-established negative predictor of immunotherapy/PFS outcomes in NSCLC and appear in current guidelines and reviews.

judgments.text: BRCA2-mutant patients (brca2_mutation=1) have different (any direction) pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status as a predictor of PFS (especially with PARP inhibitors or platinum) is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: TMB-high patients (tmb_high=1) have different (any direction) pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting differential PFS (notably with immunotherapy) is established and FDA-recognized.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is correlated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS correlation with longer PFS on immunotherapy is a textbook biomarker association.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl is positively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor is a long-established clinical paradigm across oncology.

judgments.text: ldh_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is standard knowledge across many tumor types.

judgments.text: weight_loss_pct_6mo is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is a foundational oncology principle.

judgments.text: crp_mg_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: CRP as an inflammatory marker associated with worse cancer outcomes is well-established.

judgments.text: nlr is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a poor prognostic indicator is widely established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: hemoglobin_g_dl is positively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as a poor prognostic factor is a textbook association in oncology.

judgments.text: alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase is a well-established adverse prognostic marker (often reflecting liver/bone involvement) associated with shorter PFS across many oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: ast_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST reflecting hepatic dysfunction or metastatic burden is a standard negative prognostic factor in oncology.

judgments.text: alt_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT as a marker of hepatic involvement/dysfunction is a conventional adverse prognostic indicator.

judgments.text: total_bilirubin_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Hyperbilirubinemia is a classic marker of hepatic dysfunction and poor prognosis included in standard performance/organ-function assessments.

judgments.text: creatinine_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated creatinine reflecting renal dysfunction is a well-known adverse prognostic factor and dosing constraint in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: bun_mg_dl is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN as a marker of renal dysfunction or catabolic state is a standard, non-novel adverse prognostic marker.

judgments.text: sodium_meq_l is positively correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Hyponatremia is a well-established adverse prognostic factor in many cancers and appears in standard prognostic discussions.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is negatively (deviation from norm) correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Potassium derangements reflect overall metabolic instability and are recognized as adverse prognostic indicators in routine clinical assessment.

judgments.text: calcium_mg_dl is negatively (hypercalcemia) correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Hypercalcemia of malignancy is a textbook adverse prognostic feature associated with advanced disease and shorter survival.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (unadjusted, marginal effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is the established guideline-endorsed effect of immune checkpoint inhibition, not a novel finding.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (unadjusted, marginal effect).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving PFS in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 (unadjusted, marginal effect).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA/HRD-associated cancers is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 (unadjusted, marginal effect).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers and labs, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (positive adjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS after standard adjustment reflects established immunotherapy efficacy in guideline-recognized populations.

judgments.text: After adjusting for demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers and labs, treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (positive adjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: An adjusted main effect of sotorasib on PFS reflects its established role in KRAS G12C-mutant disease.

judgments.text: After adjusting for demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers and labs, treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 (positive adjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: An adjusted main effect of olaparib on PFS reflects its established guideline-recognized efficacy.

judgments.text: After adjusting for demographics, ECOG, stage, brain mets, smoking, histology, all biomarkers and labs, treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 (positive adjusted main effect).
judgments.rationale: An adjusted main effect of osimertinib on PFS is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months (i.e., the pembrolizumab effect differs by stage_iv).
judgments.rationale: A specific pembrolizumab × stage IV interaction on PFS is a subgroup-conditional claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × kras_g12c interaction on pfs_months (i.e., the pembrolizumab effect differs by kras_g12c).
judgments.rationale: Differential pembrolizumab efficacy by KRAS G12C status is a cross-pathway interaction beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × potassium_meq_l interaction on pfs_months (i.e., the pembrolizumab effect differs by potassium_meq_l).
judgments.rationale: A pembrolizumab effect modified by serum potassium is a speculative biomarker interaction absent from any guideline.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × age_years interaction on pfs_months (i.e., the pembrolizumab effect differs by age_years).
judgments.rationale: Age-based modification of pembrolizumab efficacy is not an established guideline-level predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab × weight_loss_pct_6mo interaction on pfs_months (i.e., the pembrolizumab effect differs by weight_loss_pct_6mo).
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as a predictive (not just prognostic) modifier of pembrolizumab benefit is not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a treatment_sotorasib × sex_female interaction on pfs_months.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

judgments.text: Patients with sex_female=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with sex_female=0.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based PFS differences are not guideline-defining; however the unconditional claim that females have worse PFS contradicts known better outcomes in EGFR-enriched female NSCLC populations and is not a paradigm claim — but as stated it's a generic demographic association without mechanistic novelty.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse PFS than earlier stages is textbook prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with has_brain_mets=0.

judgments.text: Patients with egfr_mutation=1 have higher mean pfs_months than those with egfr_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting better PFS (in the context of TKI therapy) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with kras_g12c=1 have higher mean pfs_months than those with kras_g12c=0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C as a targetable mutation associated with response to specific therapy is now established in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with alk_fusion=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with alk_fusion=0.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion alone is associated with better PFS on TKIs per guidelines; the unconditional 'lower PFS' claim is not a recognized paradigm but is a generic biomarker association without mechanistic novelty.

judgments.text: Patients with stk11_mutation=1 have higher mean pfs_months than those with stk11_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations are typically associated with worse outcomes and immunotherapy resistance in NSCLC, so a higher PFS claim contradicts guideline consensus and is non-standard.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 is not an established prognostic biomarker for PFS in standard NSCLC/oncology guidelines outside specific PARP-inhibitor contexts, making this association non-standard.

judgments.text: Patients with tmb_high=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with tmb_high=0.
judgments.rationale: High TMB is generally associated with better immunotherapy response, so a lower PFS claim runs counter to guideline-level expectations and is non-standard.

judgments.text: Patients with smoke_current=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with smoke_current=0.
judgments.rationale: Worse outcomes in current smokers versus non-smokers is a well-established prognostic association in lung cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with smoke_never=1 have higher mean pfs_months than those with smoke_never=0.
judgments.rationale: Better outcomes in never-smokers (often reflecting targetable driver mutations) is a standard, guideline-level association in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Patients with histology_squamous=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with histology_squamous=0.
judgments.rationale: Squamous histology being associated with worse PFS relative to non-squamous is well-established in oncology textbooks and guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with ecog_ge2=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with ecog_ge2=0.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status >=2 predicting worse outcomes is a foundational, guideline-level prognostic factor across oncology.

judgments.text: Patients with pdl1_high=1 have higher mean pfs_months than those with pdl1_high=0.
judgments.rationale: High PD-L1 predicting better outcomes (particularly with immunotherapy) is a standard, guideline-endorsed biomarker association.

judgments.text: age_years is positively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: pdl1_tps is positively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl is positively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: weight_loss_pct_6mo is negatively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: nlr is positively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: hemoglobin_g_dl is negatively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is negatively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: ast_u_l is positively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: alt_u_l is negatively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: total_bilirubin_mg_dl is negatively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: creatinine_mg_dl is negatively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: bun_mg_dl is negatively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: sodium_meq_l is positively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is positively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Potassium level as a PFS predictor is not established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: calcium_mg_dl is negatively associated with pfs_months.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is greater in patients with pdl1_high=1 (PD-L1 TPS>=0.5) than in those with pdl1_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 high status predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is established guideline knowledge in NSCLC and other cancers.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is greater in tmb_high=1 patients than tmb_high=0.
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is established (FDA tissue-agnostic approval) and in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months specifically in kras_g12c=1 patients but not in kras_g12c=0 patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit specifically in KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months specifically in egfr_mutation=1 patients but not in egfr_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit specifically in EGFR-mutant patients is the standard guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in brca2_mutation=1 patients but not in brca2_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant patients is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is larger in alk_fusion=1 patients than in alk_fusion=0 (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit being larger in ALK-fusion patients contradicts the guideline consensus that ALK-rearranged NSCLC responds poorly to immunotherapy, making this a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months is smaller in alk_fusion=1 patients than in alk_fusion=0 (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib targets KRAS G12C and ALK-fusion interactions are not part of standard guideline language, so a differential effect in ALK-fusion patients is a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months is larger in alk_fusion=1 patients than in alk_fusion=0 (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy modified by ALK fusion status is not an established paradigm and crosses unrelated pathways (PARP vs ALK), making it novel.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_osimertinib on pfs_months is larger in alk_fusion=1 patients than in alk_fusion=0 (interaction).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib is an EGFR TKI and ALK fusions are typically mutually exclusive with EGFR mutations; a larger osimertinib effect in ALK-fusion patients is not in any guideline and is a speculative cross-pathway interaction.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, age_years is positively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic covariate for PFS is a standard demographic adjustment routinely reported in oncology studies and guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, sex_female is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Sex as a prognostic covariate is a standard demographic adjustment and not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, ecog_ge2 is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Poor performance status (ECOG ≥2) being associated with worse PFS is a well-established prognostic finding in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, stage_iv is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease being associated with worse PFS is foundational oncology knowledge present in all staging-based guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, has_brain_mets is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases being associated with worse outcomes is a well-established prognostic factor in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, egfr_mutation is positively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation being associated with better PFS (largely via TKI responsiveness) is a guideline-level established finding in NSCLC.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, kras_g12c is positively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C as a positive PFS predictor in the era of sotorasib/adagrasib is discussed in current NSCLC guidelines as an actionable target with treatment benefit.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, alk_fusion is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion is established as a favorable predictive marker for ALK inhibitor response with excellent PFS, so a negative association contradicts guideline consensus and would be a notable novel finding.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, stk11_mutation is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: STK11/LKB1 mutations are well-established in current NSCLC literature and guidelines as conferring worse outcomes, particularly with immunotherapy.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, brca2_mutation is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2's prognostic direction is context-dependent and not a standard guideline-level negative PFS predictor across oncology, making a multivariable-adjusted negative association non-trivial.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, pdl1_tps is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS is canonically associated with better immunotherapy response and PFS, so a negative association contradicts guideline consensus.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, tmb_high is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: High TMB is generally a favorable predictive biomarker for immunotherapy response, so a negative PFS association runs counter to established paradigm.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, albumin_g_dl is positively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Higher serum albumin as a favorable prognostic marker reflecting nutritional/inflammatory status is a textbook association across oncology.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, ldh_u_l is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic factor is a long-established, guideline-level association in multiple malignancies.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, weight_loss_pct_6mo is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as an adverse prognostic factor is a textbook association captured in standard performance and prognostic indices.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, crp_mg_l is positively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: CRP elevation is conventionally associated with worse outcomes via systemic inflammation, so a positive association with PFS contradicts established prognostic understanding.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, nlr is positively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR as an adverse prognostic biomarker associated with worse PFS is well-established across solid tumors and appears in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: A negative main effect of pembrolizumab on PFS contradicts guideline consensus and would only be plausible as a context-specific finding, making it non-standard.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_sotorasib is positively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving PFS is the established guideline-supported effect in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_olaparib is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: A negative main effect of olaparib on PFS in an unselected cohort contradicts its established benefit in BRCA/HRD-positive subgroups and is not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_osimertinib is negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other covariates.
judgments.rationale: A negative main effect of osimertinib on PFS contradicts the established guideline benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC and is not a standard claim.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is reduced (or reversed) in stk11_mutation=1 patients compared to stk11_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations attenuating pembrolizumab benefit in NSCLC is a well-recognized association discussed in current guidelines and reviews.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is smaller (or negative) in smoke_never=1 (never-smokers) patients than in former/current smokers.
judgments.rationale: Reduced immune checkpoint inhibitor benefit in never-smokers (often EGFR-driven NSCLC) is a widely recognized clinical pattern reflected in guideline nuance.

judgments.text: Among the screened modifiers, the strongest interaction with treatment_pembrolizumab is on weight_loss_high: pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs between weight_loss_high=1 and weight_loss_high=0.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight-loss as the strongest modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not codified in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The strongest interaction with treatment_sotorasib in the full cohort is on kras_g12c (interaction coef +2.558); sotorasib effect differs between kras_g12c=1 and =0.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C status defining sotorasib activity is the canonical guideline-level biomarker-drug interaction.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1 patients, sotorasib effect on pfs_months is further modified by sex_female (top interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of sotorasib efficacy within KRAS G12C-mutant patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim absent from current guidelines.

judgments.text: The strongest interaction with treatment_olaparib is on albumin_low: effect of olaparib differs between albumin_low=1 and =0.
judgments.rationale: Albumin level as the strongest effect modifier for olaparib is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: The strongest interaction with treatment_osimertinib is on alk_fusion: effect of osimertinib differs between alk_fusion=1 and =0.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib targets EGFR, not ALK; suggesting ALK fusion modifies osimertinib effect contradicts standard paradigm and is more spurious than a recognized novel insight, but it does go beyond guideline knowledge so qualifies as a non-standard speculative claim.

judgments.text: In male, kras_g12c=1 patients (kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0), treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months relative to no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific benefit of sotorasib in KRAS G12C patients is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In female, kras_g12c=1 patients (kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=1), treatment_sotorasib does NOT improve pfs_months (effect ≈ 0 or negative).
judgments.rationale: Loss of sotorasib efficacy specifically in females with KRAS G12C is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 AND alk_fusion=0 patients, treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C patients without ALK fusion essentially restates the standard guideline indication, since ALK fusions are mutually exclusive with KRAS in practice.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 AND alk_fusion=1 patients, treatment_sotorasib does NOT improve pfs_months (alk fusion suppresses sotorasib benefit).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion suppressing sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C tumors is a cross-pathway speculative claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 patients, treatment_sotorasib substantially improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conditional subgroup (male, KRAS G12C, no ALK, no BRCA2) for sotorasib benefit is speculative and beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The strongest 2-way subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is ecog_0=0 AND weight_loss_high=0 (effect -0.116 mo).
judgments.rationale: ECOG and weight loss jointly defining the strongest pembrolizumab subgroup is a speculative interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The strongest 2-way subgroup for treatment_olaparib effect is stage_iv=1 AND histology_squamous=1 (effect -0.139 mo).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV plus squamous histology as the top olaparib effect-modifier subgroup is not a guideline-recognized predictor.

judgments.text: The strongest 2-way subgroup for treatment_osimertinib effect is alk_fusion=1 AND weight_loss_high=0 (effect +0.358 mo).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion combined with absence of weight loss as the top osimertinib subgroup contradicts the standard EGFR-driven indication and is speculative.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 patients, treatment_sotorasib substantially increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC without ALK fusion is established guideline knowledge (KRAS G12C and ALK fusions are essentially mutually exclusive).

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1 male patients, alk_fusion=1 abolishes the sotorasib benefit (effect drops to ~0 or negative).
judgments.rationale: A claim that ALK fusion specifically abolishes sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C male patients is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1 male patients, brca2_mutation=1 attenuates or abolishes the sotorasib benefit.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation attenuating sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C males is a mechanistic cross-pathway claim not established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: In male kras_g12c=1 patients without alk_fusion and without brca2_mutation, treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by ~5 months.
judgments.rationale: A specific quantitative ~5-month PFS benefit conditional on absence of both ALK fusion and BRCA2 mutation in male KRAS G12C patients is a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: The strongest positive 3-way subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab is pdl1_high=0 AND stage_iv=1 AND weight_loss_high=1, with effect +0.133 mo.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit being strongest in PD-L1 LOW, stage IV, weight-loss-high patients contradicts the guideline paradigm that PD-L1 high predicts pembrolizumab response.

judgments.text: The strongest positive 3-way subgroup for treatment_olaparib is ecog_0=1 AND age_old=1 AND albumin_low=1, effect +0.307 mo.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit being maximal in ECOG 0, older, low-albumin patients without reference to BRCA/HRD status is a novel non-canonical subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The strongest positive 3-way subgroup for treatment_osimertinib is alk_fusion=1 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND weight_loss_high=0, effect +0.386 mo.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit being strongest in ALK-fusion-positive patients contradicts the guideline paradigm (osimertinib targets EGFR; ALK fusions are typically mutually exclusive and treated with ALK inhibitors).

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=1 AND alk_fusion=0, treatment_sotorasib does NOT substantially improve pfs_months (sex restricts the responder population).
judgments.rationale: A sex-restricted responder population for sotorasib in KRAS G12C patients is not part of established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In egfr_mutation=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND pdl1_high=1 patients (canonical pembrolizumab responders), treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in EGFR/ALK wild-type, PD-L1 high NSCLC is the canonical guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: Patients with pdl1_tps>=0.6 derive a positive pfs_months benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 high (TPS ≥50-60%) predicting pembrolizumab benefit is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In egfr+ female (egfr+ female) patients, treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is guideline-standard and not modified by sex.

judgments.text: In egfr+ never-smoker (egfr+ never-smoker) patients, treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutations are enriched in never-smokers and osimertinib benefit in this group is well-established standard of care.

judgments.text: In egfr+ adeno (egfr+ adeno) patients, treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: EGFR-mutant adenocarcinoma responding to osimertinib is the canonical guideline indication.

judgments.text: In egfr+ ecog_0 (egfr+ ecog_0) patients, treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant patients with good performance status is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In brca2+ female patients, treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA2-mutant patients within this oncology context (not standard breast/ovarian/prostate/pancreatic guideline indication for olaparib stratified by sex) extends beyond textbook use.

judgments.text: In brca2+ adeno patients, treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib activity specifically in BRCA2-mutant adenocarcinoma histology is not a standard guideline-level subgroup claim.

judgments.text: In brca2+ ecog_0 patients, treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning olaparib benefit on ECOG 0 within BRCA2-mutant disease is a subgroup-specific claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: In stk11+ egfr+ patients, treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in STK11/EGFR co-altered tumors is a cross-pathway speculative claim with no guideline basis.

judgments.text: A tree-based heterogeneity search on sotorasib identifies a leaf with the largest predicted PFS gain: kras_g12c>0.50 & sex_female<=0.50 & histology_squamous>0.50 & age_years>66.15.
judgments.rationale: A KRAS G12C male squamous older-age subgroup with maximal sotorasib benefit is a speculative multi-feature interaction not in guidelines (sotorasib is approved in non-squamous NSCLC).

judgments.text: FINAL sotorasib subgroup hypothesis: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (male) AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; outside this subgroup the effect is null. Direction: positive.
judgments.rationale: Restricting sotorasib benefit to KRAS G12C males without ALK or BRCA2 alterations is a speculative multi-marker subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL pembrolizumab hypothesis: in this cohort, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT improve pfs_months overall; no clinically meaningful positive subgroup was found (incl. pdl1_high, tmb_high, never-smoker, ecog_0, kras_g12c, egfr-/alk-).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab lacking benefit overall and in PD-L1/TMB-high or never-smoker EGFR-/ALK- subgroups contradicts but directly addresses established guideline-level predictive biomarker paradigms, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: FINAL olaparib hypothesis: treatment_olaparib does NOT improve pfs_months overall; no clinically meaningful positive subgroup found, including brca2_mutation+, stk11+, or any 2/3-way combination.
judgments.rationale: The claim that olaparib fails to benefit BRCA2+ patients simply negates a standard guideline-level association without proposing a novel subgroup or mechanism.

judgments.text: FINAL osimertinib hypothesis: treatment_osimertinib does NOT improve pfs_months overall and does NOT improve pfs_months in egfr_mutation+ patients; no replicable positive subgroup was identified.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib failing to improve PFS in EGFR-mutant patients merely contradicts the well-established guideline association without offering a novel cross-pathway or mechanistic hypothesis.

judgments.text: In ds001_nsclc, older age (age_years) is associated with longer pfs_months in a multivariable adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Older age as an adverse prognostic factor in NSCLC is standard; the direction here (longer PFS with older age) is unusual but the hypothesis tests a routine demographic covariate covered in guidelines.

judgments.text: Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than male sex in a multivariable adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Sex as a prognostic covariate in NSCLC is a well-established demographic association discussed in standard references.

judgments.text: Current smoking status (smoking_status='current') is associated with shorter pfs_months than former smoking, in a multivariable adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status is a routine prognostic/predictive covariate in NSCLC and discussed in standard guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months in a multivariable adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is one of the most established prognostic findings in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease being associated with worse PFS than earlier stages is foundational TNM-based oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an adverse prognostic factor in NSCLC is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Squamous histology (histology='squamous') is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Squamous vs adenocarcinoma histology differences in outcome are established and discussed in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation status has a non-zero adjusted main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation status affecting NSCLC outcomes is a textbook biomarker association central to current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: kras_g12c=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: A claim that KRAS G12C confers longer PFS (independent of specific G12C inhibitor therapy) contradicts the typical adverse prognostic signal and would not appear as a standard guideline statement.

judgments.text: alk_fusion=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion status is a guideline-level predictive/prognostic biomarker in NSCLC and its association with PFS is standard knowledge.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stk11_mutation=0 in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations are a well-established marker of poor response/shorter PFS to immunotherapy in NSCLC and appear in current guideline discussions.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as a predictor of shorter PFS (rather than longer PFS with PARP inhibitors or platinum) is not standard guideline knowledge in this context.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with non-zero change in pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting altered PFS on immunotherapy is a foundational, guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: tmb_high=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than tmb_high=0 in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: High TMB associated with shorter (rather than longer) PFS contradicts the standard guideline expectation that high TMB predicts immunotherapy benefit.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a poor prognostic factor is well-established across oncology and routinely cited in textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook association across many cancers including melanoma and lung cancer.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a poor prognostic factor is a long-standing, guideline-level clinical observation.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with non-zero change in pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic inflammatory marker in cancer is well-established in the literature and clinical practice.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with non-zero change in pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as an adverse prognostic biomarker, including for immunotherapy outcomes, is widely recognized and discussed in current oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as an adverse prognostic factor is a textbook-level association in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflecting bone/liver metastatic burden is a well-established adverse prognostic marker across solid tumors and appears in standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with non-zero change in pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Renal function (creatinine) as a prognostic/performance covariate influencing PFS is a standard clinical consideration captured in routine prognostic scores.

judgments.text: Higher calcium_mg_dl is associated with non-zero change in pfs_months in adjusted model.
judgments.rationale: Hypercalcemia of malignancy is a textbook adverse prognostic marker associated with worse outcomes in advanced cancer.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 in unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS versus comparator is the established guideline-endorsed effect of PD-1 blockade in eligible patients.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 in unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib prolonging PFS in KRAS G12C–mutant NSCLC is the established guideline indication for the drug.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 in unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS in BRCA/HRD-positive cancers is a guideline-level established effect.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 in unadjusted comparison.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib prolonging PFS in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is a textbook standard-of-care finding.

judgments.text: Adjusted main effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is non-zero in multivariable model.
judgments.rationale: A non-zero adjusted main effect of pembrolizumab on PFS simply restates the known guideline-level benefit of PD-1 blockade.

judgments.text: Adjusted main effect of treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months is positive in multivariable model.
judgments.rationale: A positive adjusted main effect of sotorasib on PFS reflects its established guideline-endorsed activity in KRAS G12C–mutant disease.

judgments.text: Adjusted main effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months is non-zero in multivariable model.
judgments.rationale: A non-zero adjusted main effect of olaparib on PFS restates its established guideline-level PARP inhibitor benefit.

judgments.text: Adjusted main effect of treatment_osimertinib on pfs_months is non-zero in multivariable model.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib efficacy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is established first-line guideline-recommended therapy.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction on pfs_months is positive (greater pembrolizumab benefit at higher pdl1_tps).
judgments.rationale: Greater pembrolizumab benefit with higher PD-L1 TPS is a well-established guideline-based biomarker association.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction on pfs_months is positive (sotorasib benefit larger in KRAS-G12C+).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib is FDA-approved specifically for KRAS-G12C-mutant NSCLC, making this a standard known association.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction on pfs_months is positive (osimertinib benefit larger in EGFR+).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's greater benefit in EGFR-mutant disease is foundational guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction on pfs_months is positive (olaparib benefit larger in BRCA2+).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers is a textbook synthetic lethality association.

judgments.text: Among PD-L1 high patients (pdl1_tps>=0.5), treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 high (TPS>=50%) tumors is standard guideline-based indication.

judgments.text: Among PD-L1 low patients (pdl1_tps<0.5), treatment_pembrolizumab does not affect pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Limited pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 low patients is a well-known guideline-recognized pattern.

judgments.text: Among tmb_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high as a predictor of pembrolizumab benefit is an FDA-approved tumor-agnostic indication.

judgments.text: Among KRAS-G12C-positive patients, treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with substantially longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy in KRAS-G12C-positive patients is the established approved indication.

judgments.text: Among KRAS-G12C-negative patients, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Lack of sotorasib effect outside KRAS-G12C is expected from its mechanism and approved indication.

judgments.text: Among EGFR-mutated patients, treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutated NSCLC is established first-line guideline standard.

judgments.text: Among BRCA2-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA2-mutated cancers is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_pembrolizumab × nlr interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.009).
judgments.rationale: A specific negative pembrolizumab × NLR interaction on PFS is a mechanistic biomarker claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_pembrolizumab × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.036).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in advanced/stage IV disease is the standard guideline-supported context for its use.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_pembrolizumab × kras_g12c interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.049).
judgments.rationale: A negative pembrolizumab × KRAS G12C interaction conflicts with/extends current guidelines and is a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_pembrolizumab × sex_female interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.020).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of pembrolizumab PFS benefit is not part of guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_pembrolizumab × calcium_mg_dl interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.016).
judgments.rationale: Serum calcium as an effect modifier of pembrolizumab efficacy is not in any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_sotorasib × sex_female interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.585).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of sotorasib efficacy is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+2.535).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy specifically in KRAS G12C-mutant disease is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_sotorasib × smoke_current interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.254).
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a positive effect modifier of sotorasib PFS is not part of established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_sotorasib × alk_fusion interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.343).
judgments.rationale: A specific negative interaction between sotorasib and ALK fusion on PFS is not in guidelines; ALK fusions and KRAS G12C are typically mutually exclusive, so a quantitative interaction effect is a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_sotorasib × egfr_mutation interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.208).
judgments.rationale: A quantitative negative sotorasib × EGFR-mutation interaction on PFS is not standard guideline content; co-occurrence is rare and any interaction effect is a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_osimertinib × has_brain_mets interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.033).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's superior CNS/brain-metastasis activity (FLAURA) is established guideline-level knowledge, so a positive osimertinib × brain-mets interaction reflects standard paradigm.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_osimertinib × pdl1_tps interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.060).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS is not an established modifier of osimertinib efficacy in guidelines; a negative interaction would be a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_osimertinib × sodium_meq_l interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.004).
judgments.rationale: Serum sodium is not a recognized predictive biomarker for osimertinib PFS in guidelines; an interaction effect is a novel speculative claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_osimertinib × alk_fusion interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.055).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib is an EGFR TKI not indicated for ALK fusions; a positive osimertinib × ALK-fusion PFS interaction is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_osimertinib × alkaline_phosphatase_u_l interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.000).
judgments.rationale: Alkaline phosphatase is a general prognostic marker but not a guideline-recognized predictive modifier of osimertinib efficacy, so the specific interaction is novel.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_olaparib × sodium_meq_l interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.005).
judgments.rationale: Serum sodium is not a known predictive biomarker for olaparib PFS in any guideline, making this a novel subgroup interaction.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_olaparib × alkaline_phosphatase_u_l interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.000).
judgments.rationale: Alkaline phosphatase is not a guideline-recognized predictive modifier of olaparib efficacy, so a positive interaction with PFS is a novel claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_olaparib × weight_loss_pct_6mo interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.003).
judgments.rationale: While weight loss is broadly prognostic, a specific olaparib × weight-loss interaction effect on PFS is not in guidelines and constitutes a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_olaparib × stk11_mutation interaction on pfs_months is positive (interaction coefficient ~+0.027).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit modified by STK11 mutation status is not an established guideline association in any tumor type.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model, the treatment_olaparib × bun_mg_dl interaction on pfs_months is negative (interaction coefficient ~-0.002).
judgments.rationale: A BUN-dependent modification of olaparib PFS benefit is a speculative cross-pathway/biomarker claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS-G12C-positive patients, the treatment_sotorasib × sex_female interaction on pfs_months is large and negative (much smaller benefit in females).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based heterogeneity of sotorasib benefit within KRAS-G12C+ patients is not established in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS-G12C-positive patients, the treatment_sotorasib × age_years interaction is positive (slightly larger benefit at older ages).
judgments.rationale: Age-dependent enhancement of sotorasib benefit in KRAS-G12C+ disease is not a guideline-recognized effect modifier.

judgments.text: Among KRAS-G12C-positive males (kras_g12c=1, sex_female=0), treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with markedly longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving outcomes in KRAS-G12C+ patients is the established guideline indication, not novel.

judgments.text: Among KRAS-G12C-positive females (kras_g12c=1, sex_female=1), treatment_sotorasib does not affect pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming sotorasib has no effect specifically in KRAS-G12C+ females contradicts standard guideline use and is a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within KRAS-G12C-positive males, the treatment_sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is present in every age quartile (Q1-Q4).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit across age quartiles within KRAS-G12C+ patients restates the established indication without subgroup novelty.

judgments.text: After full adjustment plus a kras_male_sotor product term, the three-way interaction (treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × male) on pfs_months is positive and large (~+4.5 months).
judgments.rationale: A large positive three-way KRAS-G12C × sotorasib × male interaction is a speculative subgroup-conditional finding beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: After full adjustment plus a kras_female_sotor product term, the three-way interaction (treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × female) on pfs_months is null.
judgments.rationale: A null three-way KRAS-G12C × sotorasib × female interaction implying no benefit in females is a novel subgroup claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: A T-learner decision-tree uplift model identifies a top-quintile sotorasib uplift subgroup characterized by KRAS-G12C+ and male sex; observed PFS difference (on - off) in that subgroup is positive and large.
judgments.rationale: An uplift-model-identified KRAS-G12C+/male top-responder subgroup is a data-driven heterogeneity claim not present in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Best-supported treatment-effect subgroup: the treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in patients with kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0; outside this subgroup (either kras_g12c=0 OR sex_female=1) sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sex-restricted sotorasib benefit limited to KRAS G12C-positive males is not an established guideline-level claim and goes beyond the standard KRAS G12C biomarker association.

judgments.text: In a multivariable model restricted to pdl1_tps>=0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab adjusted main effect on pfs_months remains null (no PD-L1-stratum benefit).
judgments.rationale: A null pembrolizumab PFS effect within the PD-L1 TPS≥50% subgroup contradicts established guideline consensus that high PD-L1 expression predicts pembrolizumab benefit.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab × treatment_sotorasib interaction on pfs_months is non-zero (synergy or antagonism between concomitant therapies).
judgments.rationale: A pembrolizumab × sotorasib interaction on PFS is a speculative cross-pathway combination claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib × treatment_osimertinib interaction on pfs_months is non-zero.
judgments.rationale: A sotorasib × osimertinib interaction crosses KRAS and EGFR pathways in a way not addressed by standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Across the four annotated treatments, only treatment_sotorasib has a usable PFS benefit, and that benefit is restricted to patients with kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0; treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib have null adjusted PFS effects everywhere, including in their canonical biomarker subgroups (pdl1_tps>=0.5, brca2_mutation=1, egfr_mutation=1 respectively).
judgments.rationale: Claiming all canonical biomarker-matched therapies (pembrolizumab/PD-L1, olaparib/BRCA2, osimertinib/EGFR) lack benefit while only a sex-restricted sotorasib effect persists contradicts established guideline paradigms.

judgments.text: The dominant predictors of pfs_months in this cohort are age_years (positive), ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), has_brain_mets (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), kras_g12c (positive main effect), and weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative); these account for nearly all of the explained variance.
judgments.rationale: A positive age main effect on PFS and KRAS G12C as a positive prognostic main effect (independent of sotorasib) are not standard guideline-level prognostic claims.

judgments.text: Older age (higher age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Older age and worse PFS is a standard prognostic association documented in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have longer pfs_months than male patients (sex_female=0).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based prognostic differences in NSCLC (females faring better) are well-established in standard oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a foundational prognostic factor in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV disease.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring worse PFS than earlier stages is the basis of TNM staging and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a poor prognostic factor is established standard oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Adenocarcinoma histology is associated with longer pfs_months than squamous histology.
judgments.rationale: Adenocarcinoma typically having better outcomes than squamous NSCLC (partly due to targetable mutations) is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Smoking status is associated with pfs_months — specifically, current smokers have shorter pfs_months than never-smokers.
judgments.rationale: Current smoking status being associated with worse outcomes in lung cancer is a standard, guideline-level association.

judgments.text: EGFR mutation (egfr_mutation=1) is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting longer PFS (with appropriate targeted therapy) is the canonical example of a guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: KRAS G12C mutation (kras_g12c=1) is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claiming KRAS G12C mutation is associated with longer PFS in the overall cohort contradicts conventional prognostic understanding and is not a guideline-endorsed association.

judgments.text: ALK fusion (alk_fusion=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claiming ALK fusion predicts shorter PFS overall contradicts the established favorable prognosis with ALK-targeted therapy and is not guideline-consistent.

judgments.text: STK11 mutation (stk11_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a marker of poor outcomes and immunotherapy resistance in NSCLC is well-established in current guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: BRCA2 mutation (brca2_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation associations with cancer outcomes are well-documented in standard oncology references, though direction varies by context.

judgments.text: High tumor mutational burden (tmb_high=1) is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: High TMB predicting better response to immunotherapy and longer PFS is a guideline-level biomarker (e.g., FDA approval of pembrolizumab for TMB-high tumors).

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting better response to immune checkpoint inhibitors and longer PFS is a standard guideline biomarker.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic marker (higher = better outcomes) is well-established across oncology and incorporated into multiple prognostic scores.

judgments.text: Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook association across many cancer types and included in staging/prognostic systems.

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia and weight loss as adverse prognostic factors are standard textbook knowledge incorporated into performance status assessments.

judgments.text: Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established and incorporated into scores like the Glasgow Prognostic Score.

judgments.text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR as an adverse prognostic marker is extensively documented across cancer types and considered standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Routine chemistries (hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) each have nontrivial univariate associations with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Routine chemistry abnormalities (anemia, elevated alk phos, hepatic/renal dysfunction, electrolyte derangements) as prognostic markers are standard textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment with pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in oncology is well-established and appears in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment with sotorasib (treatment_sotorasib=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no sotorasib in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib is an approved targeted therapy with established PFS benefit in its indication.

judgments.text: Treatment with olaparib (treatment_olaparib=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib is a guideline-endorsed PARP inhibitor with documented PFS benefit.

judgments.text: Treatment with osimertinib (treatment_osimertinib=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no osimertinib in the overall cohort.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit is a textbook finding in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age_years, sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, histology, smoking_status, all biomarkers (egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high, pdl1_tps), and key labs (albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl), each treatment retains a positive main effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Claiming each treatment retains a positive main effect after adjustment for many covariates including biomarkers (rather than acting via biomarker-defined subgroups) is a non-guideline statistical claim.

judgments.text: Osimertinib increases pfs_months specifically in EGFR-mutant patients (egfr_mutation=1), with no benefit in EGFR-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib efficacy restricted to EGFR-mutant patients is core guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A positive osimertinib × EGFR-mutation interaction is the textbook predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: Sotorasib increases pfs_months specifically in KRAS G12C-mutant patients (kras_g12c=1), with no benefit in KRAS G12C-wild-type patients.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy is restricted to KRAS G12C-mutant patients per its label and guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A positive sotorasib × KRAS G12C interaction is the established predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab increases pfs_months more in tmb_high=1 patients than in tmb_high=0 patients (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: Greater pembrolizumab benefit in TMB-high patients is reflected in the tissue-agnostic TMB-high approval and guideline language.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab increases pfs_months more in patients with high pdl1_tps (>=0.5) than in low-pdl1 patients (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS ≥50% predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is established guideline-level knowledge in NSCLC.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps (continuous) interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A continuous PD-L1 × pembrolizumab interaction is a standard restatement of the well-known dose-response relationship in guidelines.

judgments.text: Olaparib increases pfs_months specifically in BRCA2-mutant patients (brca2_mutation=1), with no benefit in BRCA2-wild-type.
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit restricted to BRCA-mutant patients is established guideline consensus across multiple tumor types.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A BRCA2 × olaparib interaction is the textbook synthetic lethality paradigm, not novel.

judgments.text: STK11 mutation suppresses pembrolizumab efficacy: pembrolizumab increases pfs_months in STK11-WT patients but not in STK11-mutant patients (negative treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: STK11 as a negative predictor of immunotherapy benefit is emerging translational research, not yet standard guideline-level practice.

judgments.text: Within tmb_high=1 patients, the pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is concentrated in stk11_mutation=0 (i.e., positive 3-way pembrolizumab × tmb_high × stk11_mutation interaction such that tmb_high pembro benefit is suppressed when STK11 is mutated).
judgments.rationale: A three-way TMB-high × STK11 × pembrolizumab interaction is a speculative cross-pathway claim beyond current guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The pembrolizumab pfs_months benefit is concentrated in patients with good performance status (ecog_ps 0-1) versus ecog_ps=2.
judgments.rationale: Restriction of immunotherapy benefit to ECOG 0-1 reflects standard eligibility/efficacy expectations in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the EGFR-mutant subgroup, osimertinib has a larger pfs_months benefit when patients have no brain metastases (has_brain_mets=0) than when they do.
judgments.rationale: Differential osimertinib benefit by brain metastasis status within EGFR-mutant patients is a subgroup-conditional claim not established in guidelines (which actually emphasize CNS activity).

judgments.text: Within the EGFR-mutant subgroup restricted to ecog_ps<=1 and stage_iv=0, osimertinib increases pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib improving PFS in EGFR-mutant good-performance non-stage-IV patients restates the standard first-line indication.

judgments.text: A systematic treatment_pembrolizumab × feature interaction screen across binary and continuous features (sex_female, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high, adeno, smoke_current, age_years, ecog_ps, pdl1_tps, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl) will reveal at least one feature with a significant positive interaction (i.e., a subgroup with enhanced pembrolizumab benefit).
judgments.rationale: A broad interaction screen identifying novel predictive subgroups beyond PD-L1/TMB goes beyond established guideline biomarkers.

judgments.text: A systematic treatment_sotorasib × feature interaction screen will identify additional modifiers of sotorasib benefit beyond kras_g12c.
judgments.rationale: Systematic interaction screening for additional modifiers of sotorasib benefit beyond the canonical KRAS G12C biomarker would surface non-guideline modifiers.

judgments.text: A systematic treatment_olaparib × feature interaction screen will identify at least one feature with a significant interaction effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Beyond BRCA1/2 and HRD status, systematic interaction screening for olaparib PFS modifiers would surface non-guideline features.

judgments.text: A systematic treatment_osimertinib × feature interaction screen will identify at least one feature with a significant interaction effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Beyond EGFR-activating mutations, systematic interaction screening for osimertinib PFS modifiers would surface non-guideline features.

judgments.text: Within the KRAS G12C+ subgroup, the sotorasib pfs_months benefit is suppressed in females (sex_female=1): the treatment_sotorasib × sex_female interaction is large and negative within kras_g12c=1.
judgments.rationale: A sex-specific suppression of sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C+ patients is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Sotorasib substantially increases pfs_months in KRAS G12C+ AND male (sex_female=0) patients but has no effect in KRAS G12C+ AND female patients.
judgments.rationale: Restricting sotorasib benefit to KRAS G12C+ males with no effect in KRAS G12C+ females is a sex-conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months in KRAS G12C-wild-type patients regardless of sex.
judgments.rationale: Lack of sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C-wild-type patients is established guideline consensus, since the drug is indicated only for G12C-mutant disease.

judgments.text: Within the KRAS G12C+ subgroup, ALK fusion (alk_fusion=1) attenuates sotorasib benefit (negative treatment_sotorasib × alk_fusion interaction).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusions are not part of standard sotorasib response prediction; an attenuating interaction within KRAS G12C+ is a non-guideline cross-pathway claim.
