judgments.text: Within the KRAS G12C+ subgroup, BRCA2 mutation (brca2_mutation=1) attenuates sotorasib benefit (negative treatment_sotorasib × brca2_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as an attenuator of sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C+ is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the KRAS G12C+ subgroup, STK11 mutation (stk11_mutation=1) attenuates sotorasib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Although STK11 co-mutation is an emerging area of research in KRAS-mutant NSCLC immunotherapy resistance, a specific attenuating effect on sotorasib PFS is not yet guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the KRAS G12C+ subgroup, presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) attenuates sotorasib benefit.
judgments.rationale: A specific brain-metastasis-conditional attenuation of sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C+ is not established guideline content.

judgments.text: Within the KRAS G12C+ subgroup, smoking status modifies sotorasib benefit.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a modifier of sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C+ NSCLC is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among KRAS G12C+ AND male patients, additional restriction to alk_fusion=0 yields the largest sotorasib pfs_months benefit (over 4.7 mo).
judgments.rationale: A male-sex and ALK-fusion-negative subgroup driving maximal sotorasib benefit is a speculative cross-feature claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Among KRAS G12C+ AND male patients, additional restriction to brca2_mutation=0 yields a similarly large sotorasib pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-wildtype status as a conditional modifier of sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ males is not part of standard oncology consensus.

judgments.text: Among KRAS G12C+ AND male patients with no ALK fusion, the sotorasib pfs_months benefit exceeds 4.5 months and is robust to other adjustments.
judgments.rationale: A robust >4.5-month sotorasib PFS benefit specifically in KRAS G12C+/male/ALK-negative patients is a speculative subgroup claim beyond guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, histology, smoking, albumin, LDH, NLR, STK11, and ALK, sotorasib retains a large positive coefficient (>3.5 mo) within the KRAS G12C+ AND male subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A sex-conditional adjusted sotorasib effect persisting after multivariable adjustment is not a guideline-established finding.

judgments.text: An exhaustive subgroup search for olaparib across binary features (brca2_mutation, stk11_mutation, tmb_high, adeno, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ecog_ps, egfr_mutation) will fail to identify any subgroup with a positive olaparib pfs_months benefit (i.e., olaparib has no efficacy in any subgroup).
judgments.rationale: A blanket claim that olaparib has no benefit in any subgroup of this dataset contradicts established PARP inhibitor efficacy in BRCA-mutant disease and is a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: An exhaustive subgroup search for osimertinib across binary features (egfr_mutation, stk11_mutation, tmb_high, adeno, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, alk_fusion) will fail to identify any subgroup with a positive osimertinib pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib lacks benefit in any subgroup contradicts established EGFR-mutant NSCLC guideline evidence, making this a non-standard (and likely wrong) but non-paradigm claim.

judgments.text: An exhaustive subgroup search for pembrolizumab across binary features (pdl1_high, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, adeno, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation, smoke_current, smoke_former, smoke_never, sex_female) will fail to identify any subgroup with a meaningful positive pembrolizumab pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: A universal absence of pembrolizumab benefit across all subgroups including PD-L1-high contradicts guideline consensus and is not a standard claim.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, concurrent pembrolizumab does not modify the sotorasib pfs_months benefit (treatment_sotorasib × treatment_pembrolizumab interaction is null).
judgments.rationale: The absence of interaction between sotorasib and pembrolizumab in KRAS G12C+ patients is a mechanistic claim not codified in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within EGFR-mutant patients, concurrent pembrolizumab modifies the osimertinib effect on pfs_months (treatment_osimertinib × treatment_pembrolizumab interaction is non-null).
judgments.rationale: A pembrolizumab × osimertinib interaction modifying PFS in EGFR-mutant patients is a speculative cross-pathway claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for sotorasib on pfs_months: Sotorasib substantially increases pfs_months (~+4.6 months) in patients who are KRAS G12C+ AND male (sex_female=0). The benefit is completely suppressed by female sex (sex_female=1), and is absent entirely in KRAS G12C-wild-type patients regardless of sex. Within KRAS G12C+ males, ALK fusion (alk_fusion=1) and BRCA2 mutation (brca2_mutation=1) further attenuate the benefit; the cleanest responders are KRAS G12C+ AND male AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Claiming sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ is restricted to males and further attenuated by ALK fusion and BRCA2 mutation is a speculative multi-biomarker subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: For pembrolizumab, no subgroup defined by available biomarkers (pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, kras_g12c, egfr_mutation, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation, sex_female, smoking_status, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets) shows a positive pfs_months treatment effect; pembrolizumab is essentially neutral throughout the cohort, with several subgroups even showing a small negative point estimate.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines endorse pembrolizumab benefit (especially in PD-L1 high/TMB high NSCLC), so a claim of universal null effect across all biomarker subgroups contradicts established paradigm and is a non-standard assertion.

judgments.text: FINAL: For olaparib, no subgroup defined by available features (brca2_mutation, stk11_mutation, tmb_high, adeno, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ecog_ps, egfr_mutation) shows a positive pfs_months treatment effect; olaparib is essentially neutral throughout the cohort.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib is not a guideline-standard therapy for this NSCLC-like cohort and lacks an established predictive subgroup here, so a null finding aligns with conventional expectations.

judgments.text: FINAL: For osimertinib, no subgroup defined by available features (egfr_mutation, stk11_mutation, tmb_high, adeno, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, alk_fusion) shows a meaningful positive pfs_months treatment effect; osimertinib's effect is essentially null in EGFR+ patients and in EGFR-WT patients alike — the only weakly significant signal is osi_subgroup_alk_fusion_1 with effect 0.23 mo (p=0.018) and the within-EGFR+ pembro × osi interaction (coef +0.25, p=0.033), neither of which constitutes a clinically meaningful efficacy signal.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines establish osimertinib as highly effective in EGFR-mutant NSCLC, so claiming null efficacy in EGFR+ patients contradicts paradigm consensus and is a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in oncology is established and guideline-endorsed across multiple indications.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established and FDA-approved per guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers is established standard-of-care in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_osimertinib.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is the guideline-endorsed standard of care.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation positive patients have different mean pfs_months than egfr_mutation negative patients.
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation status as a PFS-relevant biomarker is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: kras_g12c positive patients have different mean pfs_months than kras_g12c negative patients.
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C as a prognostic/predictive biomarker is established in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: alk_fusion positive patients have different mean pfs_months than alk_fusion negative patients.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion status driving differential outcomes with targeted therapy is guideline-standard knowledge.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation positive patients have lower mean pfs_months than stk11_mutation negative patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations as a marker of worse outcomes (especially with immunotherapy) is well-established in NSCLC literature and increasingly in guidelines.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation positive patients have different mean pfs_months than brca2_mutation negative patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as a predictive/prognostic biomarker is established across multiple tumor types in guidelines.

judgments.text: tmb_high patients have higher mean pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: High TMB as a predictor of immunotherapy benefit is FDA-recognized and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher pfs_months (positive slope).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS predicting better outcomes on immunotherapy is a well-established guideline-level biomarker association in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Older age (higher age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Older age correlating with worse outcomes is a standard prognostic observation in oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have different mean pfs_months than male patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-based differences in NSCLC outcomes are well-documented in the literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Squamous histology patients have shorter mean pfs_months than adenocarcinoma patients.
judgments.rationale: Squamous histology having worse outcomes than adenocarcinoma is a standard known prognostic distinction in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Current smokers have shorter mean pfs_months than non-current smokers.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a prognostic and predictive factor in NSCLC is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Never smokers have higher mean pfs_months than ever smokers.
judgments.rationale: Never-smoker status conferring better outcomes (often via targetable driver mutations) is standard NSCLC paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status as a prognostic factor is foundational oncology knowledge in every guideline.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter mean pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease having worse outcomes than earlier stages is the basis of TNM staging and textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter mean pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring worse prognosis is a standard guideline-level prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic marker (nutritional/inflammatory status) is well-established across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH is a well-established negative prognostic marker across many solid tumors and appears in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss is a textbook adverse prognostic factor associated with shorter survival and PFS.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic marker is well-documented in oncology literature and inflammation-based scoring systems.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio is a widely recognized adverse prognostic biomarker in oncology.

judgments.text: Other labs (hemoglobin, alk_phos, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) have small/null associations with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: It is well known that routine labs like electrolytes and LFTs have weak/null prognostic value compared to inflammation/tumor-burden markers.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is greater (more positive) in pdl1_tps high patients than in pdl1_tps low patients (positive treatment x pdl1_tps interaction).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS-high status predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is a guideline-endorsed standard biomarker association.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is greater in tmb_high patients than in tmb_high=0 patients (positive treatment x tmb_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: Higher TMB predicting greater immune checkpoint inhibitor benefit is established and FDA-recognized (pembrolizumab tissue-agnostic TMB-high indication).

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is suppressed in stk11_mutation positive patients (negative treatment x stk11_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations as a marker of reduced benefit from PD-1 inhibitors in NSCLC is well-described in guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is greater in non-squamous adenocarcinoma patients (negative treatment x squamous interaction).
judgments.rationale: Differential pembrolizumab benefit favoring non-squamous over squamous NSCLC is not an established guideline-level predictive distinction (both histologies are approved indications).

judgments.text: The treatment_osimertinib effect on pfs_months is greater in egfr_mutation positive patients than in egfr_mutation negative patients (positive treatment x egfr_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting osimertinib benefit is the textbook standard-of-care biomarker association.

judgments.text: The treatment_osimertinib effect on pfs_months differs by alk_fusion status.
judgments.rationale: ALK fusions and EGFR mutations are typically mutually exclusive, so an osimertinib-by-ALK interaction is not a guideline-standard claim.

judgments.text: The treatment_osimertinib effect on pfs_months differs by has_brain_mets status.
judgments.rationale: Differential osimertinib PFS benefit by brain metastasis status is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: The treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months is greater in kras_g12c positive patients than in kras_g12c negative patients (positive treatment x kras_g12c interaction).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's enhanced benefit in KRAS G12C-positive patients is the established indication and guideline standard.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c positive patients, sotorasib increases pfs_months versus no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving PFS in KRAS G12C-positive patients is the approved, guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c negative patients, sotorasib has no measurable pfs_months effect.
judgments.rationale: Lack of sotorasib effect in KRAS G12C-negative patients reflects standard biomarker-based selection in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months is greater in brca2_mutation positive patients than in brca2_mutation negative patients (positive treatment x brca2_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Greater olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant patients is the established biomarker-defined indication in guidelines.

judgments.text: In brca2_mutation positive patients, olaparib increases pfs_months versus no olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS in BRCA2-mutated patients is a guideline-standard, FDA-approved indication.

judgments.text: In pdl1_tps>=0.5 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months versus no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 TPS>=50% patients is a well-established, guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: In tmb_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months versus no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TMB-high patients reflects the tumor-agnostic FDA approval and guideline recommendation.

judgments.text: In pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months versus no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: A combined PD-L1>=50% AND TMB-high subgroup-specific pembrolizumab effect is a cross-biomarker conditional claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: In stk11_mutation positive patients, treatment_pembrolizumab has lower (more negative) effect on pfs_months than in stk11_mutation negative patients.
judgments.rationale: STK11-mediated negative modulation of pembrolizumab benefit is an emerging biomarker hypothesis but not yet standard guideline consensus for treatment selection.

judgments.text: In pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND stk11_mutation=0 (clean PDL1-high) patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months versus no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning PD-L1-high pembrolizumab benefit on absence of STK11 co-mutation is a cross-pathway subgroup claim beyond current guideline recommendations.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 AND stk11_mutation=0 patients, sotorasib has a larger pfs_months effect than in kras_g12c=1 AND stk11_mutation=1 patients (negative sotorasib x stk11 interaction within KRAS+).
judgments.rationale: STK11 attenuation of KRAS G12C inhibitor (sotorasib) efficacy is a speculative co-mutation interaction not in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients on treatment_pembrolizumab only (no other treatment), mean pfs_months is higher than in patients on no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS over no treatment is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: In patients on treatment_sotorasib only (no other treatment), mean pfs_months is higher than in patients on no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib improving PFS over no treatment in its indicated population is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In patients on treatment_olaparib only (no other treatment), mean pfs_months is higher than in patients on no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS over no treatment is established in guidelines for its indicated populations.

judgments.text: In patients on treatment_osimertinib only (no other treatment), mean pfs_months is higher than in patients on no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib improving PFS over no treatment is a textbook/guideline association in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all features in a multivariable OLS, ecog_ps remains a strong negative predictor of pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status as a negative prognostic factor for PFS is textbook oncology consensus.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_sotorasib has a positive coefficient on pfs_months while treatment_pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib are near zero.
judgments.rationale: A claim that only sotorasib shows benefit while pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib are null after adjustment contradicts guideline expectations and is a non-standard cross-treatment pattern.

judgments.text: After adjustment, age_years has a positive coefficient on pfs_months in this dataset (counter-clinical-intuition).
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with longer PFS after adjustment runs counter to clinical intuition and is not a guideline-supported association.

judgments.text: At least one feature among {age_years, sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, all biomarkers, all labs} significantly modifies the treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: Asking whether any clinical/biomarker feature modifies pembrolizumab efficacy is a generic effect-modification screen consistent with standard predictive-biomarker thinking (PD-L1, TMB, etc.) found in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is more positive in patients with greater weight_loss_pct_6mo (positive treatment x weight_loss interaction).
judgments.rationale: A positive interaction where greater 6-month weight loss enhances pembrolizumab PFS benefit contradicts the well-established cachexia/weight-loss-as-poor-prognostic paradigm and is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs between stage_iv and non-stage_iv patients (positive treatment x stage_iv interaction).
judgments.rationale: Stage-based heterogeneity of immunotherapy benefit is a routinely examined subgroup analysis and aligns with standard staging-based treatment frameworks.

judgments.text: At least one feature among {all biomarkers, demographics, labs} significantly modifies the treatment_osimertinib effect on pfs_months at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: Screening for any modifier of osimertinib efficacy is a generic predictive-biomarker question consistent with established EGFR-TKI resistance/response literature in guidelines.

judgments.text: At least one feature beyond kras_g12c significantly modifies the treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: Claiming a non-KRAS-G12C feature modifies sotorasib efficacy goes beyond the guideline-level paradigm that KRAS G12C status is the defining predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: The treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months is greater in male (sex_female=0) than in female (sex_female=1) patients (negative treatment x sex_female interaction, marginally).
judgments.rationale: A sex-based differential benefit of sotorasib (greater in males) is not an established guideline finding for KRAS G12C inhibitors.

judgments.text: At least one feature significantly modifies the treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months at p<0.05.
judgments.rationale: Searching broadly for modifiers of olaparib efficacy reflects standard HRD/BRCA-context predictive-biomarker exploration already embedded in guidelines.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 patients, sotorasib increases pfs_months in MALE (sex_female=0) patients but has no effect in FEMALE (sex_female=1) patients.
judgments.rationale: A claim that sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C disease is restricted to males with no effect in females is a sex-conditional efficacy claim not supported by guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, the sotorasib pfs_months benefit is similar across smoking_status, ecog_ps, histology, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and stk11_mutation status.
judgments.rationale: Asserting uniform sotorasib benefit across STK11, PD-L1, TMB, and brain-mets subgroups contradicts emerging literature suggesting STK11/KEAP1 and other co-mutations modulate KRAS-G12C inhibitor response.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, the sotorasib pfs_months benefit is attenuated in alk_fusion=1 or brca2_mutation=1 patients (very small subgroups).
judgments.rationale: Attenuation of sotorasib benefit specifically in ALK-fusion or BRCA2-mutant KRAS-G12C patients is a speculative cross-pathway co-alteration claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In egfr_mutation=1 patients, treatment_osimertinib has no detectable pfs_months benefit in any single-feature subgroup (sex, smoking, brain mets, ECOG, histology).
judgments.rationale: Negative finding contradicting established osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant disease is a guideline-level reference point, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: In brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib has no detectable pfs_months benefit in any subgroup (sex, ECOG, histology, albumin).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers is guideline-standard; a null finding here just restates the known association as absent and isn't a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: In patients with no actionable driver (egfr_mutation=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND kras_g12c=0 AND brca2_mutation=0), treatment_pembrolizumab has no pfs_months benefit.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab use in driver-negative disease is standard practice; claiming no benefit overall is a guideline-adjacent observation, not a novel subgroup-conditional hypothesis.

judgments.text: Splitting by sex_female, treatment_pembrolizumab has no pfs_months benefit in male or female patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex-stratified pembrolizumab efficacy is a well-discussed topic in guidelines/meta-analyses; a simple null by sex isn't beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Splitting by ecog_ps, treatment_pembrolizumab has no pfs_months benefit at any ECOG level.
judgments.rationale: ECOG-stratified immunotherapy benefit (especially limited benefit at poor PS) is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, the treatment_sotorasib x sex_female interaction is strongly negative (i.e. sotorasib effect is much smaller in females).
judgments.rationale: A strong negative sotorasib-by-sex interaction within KRAS G12C is not part of guideline consensus and represents a novel subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, the sotorasib effect is captured fully by sex_female; no additional 3-way interaction with stk11_mutation is significant.
judgments.rationale: Claiming sex fully captures sotorasib heterogeneity with no STK11 three-way interaction is a specific cross-pathway mechanistic claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 patients (clean KRAS+ male), treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by approximately 5 months (large effect, p<<0.001).
judgments.rationale: A ~5-month PFS gain restricted to KRAS G12C males without ALK/BRCA2 alterations is a narrow subgroup-conditional effect not in any guideline.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=1 patients, treatment_sotorasib has no measurable pfs_months effect (delta ~ 0, p>0.5).
judgments.rationale: Complete absence of sotorasib effect in KRAS G12C females is a novel sex-specific resistance claim beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by ~+4.6 to +5.0 months only in patients who are kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0; in all other patients (kras_g12c=0 of any sex, or kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=1) sotorasib has no detectable pfs_months effect (delta < 0.05, p > 0.5).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit confined to KRAS G12C males with null effect elsewhere is a specific subgroup-conditional hypothesis not reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab has no detectable positive pfs_months effect in any tested subgroup, including pdl1_tps>=0.5, tmb_high=1, the joint pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1, no-actionable-driver, or stk11-stratified subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab has no PFS benefit even in PD-L1-high/TMB-high NSCLC contradicts established guideline consensus rather than extending beyond it, so it is not a novel hypothesis but a negative restatement of known biomarker-treatment relationships.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib has no detectable positive pfs_months effect in any tested subgroup, including brca2_mutation=1 alone or jointly with sex/ECOG/albumin.
judgments.rationale: Asserting olaparib has no PFS effect in BRCA2-mutant subgroups directly opposes the well-established guideline-level association between BRCA1/2 mutations and PARP inhibitor benefit, making it a negation of standard knowledge rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_osimertinib has no detectable positive pfs_months effect in any tested subgroup, including egfr_mutation=1 alone or jointly with sex/ECOG/brain mets/STK11/smoking.
judgments.rationale: Stating osimertinib has no PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant subgroups contradicts the textbook/guideline standard of EGFR-mutant disease responding to osimertinib, so it is a negation of established consensus rather than a novel hypothesis.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps=2 vs ecog_ps=0) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a foundational, guideline-level prognostic association across nearly all cancers.

judgments.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease being associated with shorter PFS than non-stage IV is a basic staging-based prognostic fact in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with has_brain_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases conferring worse PFS is a well-established prognostic feature documented in standard oncology textbooks and guidelines.

judgments.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have different pfs_months than male patients.
judgments.rationale: Sex as a prognostic factor for PFS is a generic demographic comparison without a specific mechanistic or subgroup-conditional claim, and not a novel oncology hypothesis.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not, on average.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is established guideline knowledge across multiple approved indications.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have longer pfs_months than those who do not, on average.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit is established in its approved KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC indication and reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than those who do not, on average.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS is established guideline knowledge in its approved BRCA/HRD-related indications.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have longer pfs_months than those who do not, on average.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib improving PFS is foundational guideline knowledge in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation+ patients have different pfs_months than egfr_mutation- patients (overall sample).
judgments.rationale: An overall PFS difference by EGFR mutation status is a standard, well-known prognostic/predictive association in lung cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: kras_g12c+ patients have different pfs_months than kras_g12c- patients (overall sample).
judgments.rationale: An overall PFS difference by KRAS G12C status is a standard known prognostic/predictive marker discussed in current NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: alk_fusion+ patients have shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion- patients (overall sample).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusions are a well-established targetable driver with known prognostic and predictive significance in NSCLC guidelines.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation+ patients have different pfs_months than stk11_mutation- patients (overall sample).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as a PFS modifier is an emerging biomarker (especially in immunotherapy contexts) but is not yet codified in standard treatment guidelines as a routine prognostic marker.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation+ patients have different pfs_months than brca2_mutation- patients (overall sample).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2's role in PFS outside of breast/ovarian/prostate/pancreatic PARP-inhibitor contexts is not a guideline-level association, making a generic PFS claim non-standard.

judgments.text: tmb_high+ patients have different pfs_months than tmb_high- patients (overall sample).
judgments.rationale: High TMB is an established predictive/prognostic biomarker for immunotherapy response included in current oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Low serum albumin is a textbook prognostic marker of poor outcomes across cancers.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH is a long-established adverse prognostic marker recognized in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines like GPS/mGPS.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: High NLR as an adverse prognostic marker is widely established across multiple cancer types.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months (positive Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting better outcomes on immunotherapy is a guideline-level established biomarker.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative Pearson correlation).
judgments.rationale: Older age associated with worse outcomes is a well-established prognostic factor in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Never-smokers (smoking_status='never') have longer pfs_months than current smokers (smoking_status='current').
judgments.rationale: Never-smoker status predicting better PFS (especially in NSCLC) is standard guideline-level knowledge tied to driver mutation enrichment.

judgments.text: Patients with adenocarcinoma histology have longer pfs_months than patients with squamous histology.
judgments.rationale: Adenocarcinoma versus squamous histology prognostic differences are textbook-level NSCLC knowledge.

judgments.text: Hemoglobin_g_dl is correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/hemoglobin as a prognostic factor for cancer outcomes is well-established in standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Liver enzymes (ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l) and renal markers (creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl) are correlated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Baseline liver and renal function markers are routinely recognized prognostic factors in oncology textbooks and guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months specifically in egfr_mutation+ patients (positive osimertinib*egfr_mutation interaction on log PFS), with little or no effect in egfr_mutation- patients.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit restricted to EGFR-mutant patients is the foundational guideline-level indication.

judgments.text: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months specifically in kras_g12c+ patients (positive sotorasib*kras_g12c interaction on log PFS), with little or no effect in kras_g12c- patients.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit restricted to KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the FDA-approved guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in brca2_mutation+ patients (positive olaparib*brca2_mutation interaction on log PFS), with little or no effect in brca2_mutation- patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant patients is a textbook PARP inhibitor synthetic lethality indication.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months more in pdl1_high (TPS>=0.5) patients than in pdl1_low patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab greater benefit in PD-L1 high (TPS≥50%) tumors is established guideline-level biomarker stratification.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months more in tmb_high+ patients than in tmb_high- patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab greater benefit with high TMB is an FDA-approved tissue-agnostic biomarker indication in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is less effective in stk11_mutation+ patients than in stk11_mutation- patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutations as a marker of resistance to anti-PD-1 therapy in NSCLC is well-established and appears in current literature and emerging guideline considerations.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab is less effective in never-smokers than in ever-smokers (negative interaction with never-smoker status).
judgments.rationale: Reduced immunotherapy efficacy in never-smokers (lower TMB, often EGFR/ALK driver-positive) is a standard, guideline-level concept.

judgments.text: After adjustment for all measured features in a multivariable OLS model on log(pfs_months+0.1), age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ldh_u_l, smoking status, histology, kras_g12c, alk_fusion remain independently associated with PFS, while pdl1_tps, tmb_high, egfr_mutation, stk11_mutation, and most chemistries are not.
judgments.rationale: The specific multivariable claim that PD-L1 TPS and TMB-high lose independent PFS association after adjustment for clinical/lab covariates—while KRAS G12C and ALK fusion retain it—is a dataset-specific finding that contradicts guideline expectations.

judgments.text: After adjustment, only treatment_sotorasib retains a positive PFS effect on log scale; treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib do not.
judgments.rationale: The claim that pembrolizumab, olaparib, and osimertinib show no adjusted PFS benefit while only sotorasib does contradicts established guideline efficacy for these targeted/IO agents and is a non-standard, dataset-specific assertion.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in the joint subgroup of pdl1_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1-high, STK11-wildtype patients reflects established knowledge about responsive IO subgroups.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in the joint subgroup of pdl1_high=1 AND tmb_high=1.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1-high and TMB-high patients aligns with well-established biomarker-driven IO response.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in the joint subgroup pdl1_high=1 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0 (the most clinically favorable immunotherapy candidates).
judgments.rationale: The triple-conditional claim (PD-L1 high AND TMB high AND STK11 wildtype) as the optimal IO subgroup is a specific multi-biomarker stratification not codified in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab effect is suppressed in pdl1_high+ AND stk11_mutation+ (STK11 confers resistance).
judgments.rationale: STK11-mediated resistance even in PD-L1-high patients is an established mechanistic finding in NSCLC immunotherapy literature.

judgments.text: Among all measured features, the strongest treatment-effect modifier of treatment_sotorasib is kras_g12c (consistent with its molecular target), and the second-strongest modifiers include sex_female and age_years.
judgments.rationale: While KRAS G12C as the key sotorasib modifier is expected, identifying sex_female and age_years as the second-strongest effect modifiers is a non-guideline, speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: No measured feature shows a clinically meaningful interaction with treatment_pembrolizumab; the largest interaction p-values per modifier exceed conventional thresholds.
judgments.rationale: The negative claim that no measured feature meaningfully modifies pembrolizumab effect contradicts established biomarker-based IO selection (PD-L1, TMB, STK11) and is a dataset-specific assertion.

judgments.text: No measured feature shows a clinically meaningful interaction with treatment_olaparib; including the brca2_mutation interaction itself.
judgments.rationale: Null/negative findings that no features modify olaparib effect (including BRCA2) restate the standard expectation that BRCA-mutant status is the established predictor; absence of novel modifiers is not itself a novel claim.

judgments.text: No measured feature shows a clinically meaningful interaction with treatment_osimertinib; including the egfr_mutation interaction itself.
judgments.rationale: Stating that no feature, including EGFR mutation, modifies osimertinib effect is a null finding against an established paradigm rather than a new biological claim.

judgments.text: A CART regression of treatment_sotorasib's estimated treatment effect (counterfactual: random forest trained on controls) on baseline features identifies a subgroup with very large positive sotorasib effect (>3 mo improvement) and a separate subgroup with no benefit.
judgments.rationale: Identifying a specific data-driven baseline-feature subgroup with >3-month sotorasib benefit versus a no-benefit subgroup goes beyond the guideline-level KRAS G12C indication.

judgments.text: CART subgroup analyses for treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib do NOT reveal subgroups with very large positive treatment effects; the across-leaf range of estimated effects is small.
judgments.rationale: A negative CART result reporting no large subgroup effects for pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib is a null finding consistent with current guideline-level use, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Within KRAS+ patients, sex_female modifies treatment_sotorasib effect: PFS gain from sotorasib is much larger in male KRAS+ patients than in female KRAS+ patients (negative sotorasib*sex_female interaction within KRAS+).
judgments.rationale: A sex-based modifier of sotorasib efficacy within KRAS+ patients (male >> female) is not part of standard KRAS G12C treatment guidance.

judgments.text: Within KRAS+ patients, treatment_sotorasib effect on log PFS is also modified by age_years and ecog_ps and has_brain_mets and albumin_g_dl, but the effect size of these modifiers is small relative to sex.
judgments.rationale: Claiming additional but smaller modulation of sotorasib effect by age, ECOG, brain mets, and albumin within KRAS+ patients is a multi-covariate subgroup claim beyond textbook KRAS G12C indications.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation does NOT meaningfully modify treatment_sotorasib effect within KRAS+ patients (interaction near zero).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines/literature suggest STK11 co-mutation tends to blunt KRAS-targeted/immunotherapy responses, so a null STK11 modifier of sotorasib effect is a non-trivial, non-consensus finding.

judgments.text: The three-way interaction treatment_sotorasib * kras_g12c * sex_female on log PFS in the full cohort is large and negative, confirming that sotorasib's benefit is concentrated in KRAS+ males.
judgments.rationale: A three-way KRAS G12C × sex × sotorasib interaction concentrating benefit in KRAS+ males is not established in any current guideline.

judgments.text: Within KRAS+ males, treatment_sotorasib gives a substantial PFS benefit (~4.5-4.8 months) that is consistent across ECOG, brain mets, STK11, and age strata.
judgments.rationale: Quantifying a ~4.5–4.8 month PFS benefit specifically in KRAS+ males that is consistent across ECOG/brain mets/STK11/age is a subgroup-specific claim beyond guideline-level efficacy statements.

judgments.text: Within KRAS+ females, treatment_sotorasib confers no PFS benefit across ECOG strata.
judgments.rationale: Asserting zero sotorasib benefit in KRAS+ females across ECOG strata contradicts the guideline assumption that KRAS G12C status alone drives benefit and is therefore novel.

judgments.text: FINAL subgroup hypothesis for treatment_sotorasib: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months by approximately 4.6 months in patients with kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (KRAS G12C-positive males); the benefit is essentially absent in kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=1 (KRAS G12C-positive females) and is essentially absent in kras_g12c=0 patients regardless of sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex-conditional sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C+ NSCLC (males benefit, females do not) is not in any current guideline and represents a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: FINAL subgroup hypothesis for treatment_pembrolizumab: no biomarker subgroup (including pdl1_high, tmb_high, stk11_mutation status, smoking status, or any combination of these) yields a clinically or statistically meaningful PFS benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab in this dataset; the adjusted treatment effect on log PFS is approximately 0 (p>0.9).
judgments.rationale: Claim that no biomarker subgroup—including PD-L1 high or TMB high—yields pembrolizumab benefit directly contradicts established guideline paradigms identifying PD-L1/TMB as predictive biomarkers.

judgments.text: FINAL subgroup hypothesis for treatment_olaparib: treatment_olaparib does not improve pfs_months even in the brca2_mutation+ subgroup; the olaparib*brca2_mutation interaction is near zero (p~0.97).
judgments.rationale: Claim that olaparib fails to benefit BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts the well-established PARP-inhibitor/BRCA synthetic-lethality paradigm enshrined in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL subgroup hypothesis for treatment_osimertinib: treatment_osimertinib does not improve pfs_months even in the egfr_mutation+ subgroup; the osimertinib*egfr_mutation interaction is near zero (p~0.43).
judgments.rationale: Claim that osimertinib does not improve PFS in EGFR-mutant patients contradicts the foundational guideline-level association of EGFR TKIs with EGFR-mutant NSCLC response.

judgments.text: Receipt of treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than not receiving it (mean PFS higher in treatment_pembrolizumab=1).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS is established standard-of-care across many oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Receipt of treatment_sotorasib is associated with longer pfs_months than not receiving it (mean PFS higher in treatment_sotorasib=1).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib is an approved KRAS G12C inhibitor with established PFS benefit in guideline-endorsed indications.

judgments.text: Receipt of treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than not receiving it (mean PFS higher in treatment_olaparib=1).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit is well-established in guideline-endorsed BRCA/HRD settings.

judgments.text: Receipt of treatment_osimertinib is associated with longer pfs_months than not receiving it (mean PFS higher in treatment_osimertinib=1).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib's PFS benefit in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is textbook standard-of-care.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative beta).
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is a foundational, textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 (negative beta).
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease conferring shorter PFS than earlier stage is a fundamental staging principle.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 (negative beta).
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an adverse prognostic factor is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive beta).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as an adverse prognostic marker is a classic, textbook association across cancers.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative beta).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is a foundational clinical oncology principle.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative beta).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook association across many malignancies.

judgments.text: Greater age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive beta).
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic factor is a standard clinical consideration discussed in oncology guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: sex_female=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than sex_female=0 (negative beta).
judgments.rationale: Sex differences in NSCLC outcomes are well-described in standard oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Squamous histology (histology=='squamous') is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-squamous (negative beta).
judgments.rationale: Squamous histology versus non-squamous is a foundational distinction in NSCLC treatment guidelines with known prognostic implications.

judgments.text: Smoking status: current smokers have shorter pfs_months than non-current smokers (negative beta on smk_current).
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as a prognostic and predictive factor in NSCLC is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Smoking status: never smokers have longer pfs_months than ever smokers (positive beta on smk_never).
judgments.rationale: Never-smoker status conferring different (often better targeted-therapy-eligible) outcomes is established guideline content.

judgments.text: kras_g12c=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 (positive univariate beta).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C historically considered adverse prognostic; a positive univariate PFS association would go beyond current guideline framing.

judgments.text: alk_fusion=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 (negative univariate beta).
judgments.rationale: ALK fusion is canonically associated with favorable response to ALK inhibitors and longer PFS, so a negative association contradicts guideline expectations and is non-standard.

judgments.text: egfr_mutation=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than egfr_mutation=0 (positive univariate beta).
judgments.rationale: EGFR mutation predicting longer PFS (with TKI therapy) is core NSCLC guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: tmb_high=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than tmb_high=0 (negative univariate beta).
judgments.rationale: High TMB is generally framed as favorable for immunotherapy response in guidelines; a negative PFS association is non-standard.

judgments.text: stk11_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stk11_mutation=0 (negative univariate beta).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation as an adverse prognostic/predictive marker (especially with immunotherapy) is increasingly established in NSCLC guideline-adjacent literature.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 (negative univariate beta).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as a negative prognostic factor is not a standard guideline association, but the simple univariate prognostic claim without context is a generic biomarker-outcome statement rather than a novel mechanistic insight beyond paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months (positive univariate beta).
judgments.rationale: Higher PD-L1 TPS predicting longer PFS is a well-established association in oncology guidelines, particularly for immunotherapy-treated NSCLC.

judgments.text: After adjustment for clinical features and biomarkers in a multivariable OLS model, treatment_sotorasib retains a positive association with pfs_months while treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib remain near-null on average.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit and the average null effects of targeted/IO agents in an unselected multivariable model reflect expected behavior when biomarker-matched therapies are diluted across populations, consistent with standard understanding.

judgments.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps>=0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab; this effect is larger than in patients with pdl1_tps<0.5 (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction).
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS >=50% predicting greater pembrolizumab benefit is a cornerstone guideline-level association in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Among patients with tmb_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab compared with tmb_high=0 (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: TMB-high status predicting greater benefit from pembrolizumab is an established association reflected in FDA labeling and guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients with stk11_mutation=1, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab compared with stk11_mutation=0 (negative treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: STK11 mutation predicting reduced pembrolizumab benefit is an emerging research finding not yet incorporated into standard treatment guidelines as an actionable biomarker.

judgments.text: Among never-smokers (smoking_status=='never'), treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab compared with ever-smokers (negative interaction with smk_never).
judgments.rationale: Reduced immunotherapy benefit in never-smokers is a well-recognized clinical observation reflected in guideline discussions of patient selection for IO.

judgments.text: Among patients with squamous histology, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab compared with non-squamous (positive interaction with hist_sq).
judgments.rationale: Squamous histology favoring pembrolizumab benefit aligns with established approvals and guideline recommendations for IO in squamous NSCLC.

judgments.text: Among patients with kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib is associated with longer pfs_months than no sotorasib; this effect is much larger than in kras_g12c=0 patients (positive treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction).
judgments.rationale: KRAS G12C mutation as the predictive biomarker for sotorasib response is the foundational guideline-level indication for the drug.

judgments.text: Among kras_g12c=1 patients, those with stk11_mutation=1 derive a smaller treatment_sotorasib benefit than those with stk11_mutation=0 (negative treatment_sotorasib × stk11_mutation interaction within kras_g12c=1).
judgments.rationale: STK11 co-mutation diminishing sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C-mutant patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not established in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients with brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib; this effect is larger than in brca2_mutation=0 (positive treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutant cancers is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Among patients with egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib is associated with longer pfs_months than no osimertinib; effect is larger than in egfr_mutation=0 (positive treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib efficacy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Among patients with alk_fusion=1, treatment_osimertinib is associated with longer pfs_months than no osimertinib; effect is larger than in alk_fusion=0 (positive treatment_osimertinib × alk_fusion interaction).
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib is not indicated for ALK-fusion disease; positive interaction with ALK fusion would contradict established paradigm.

judgments.text: For each treatment, screening every candidate modifier in pfs_months ~ treatment * modifier OLS reveals the strongest treatment-effect heterogeneity at the top of the list. We hypothesize that the systematic screen will surface biologically expected modifiers (kras_g12c for sotorasib, alk_fusion / egfr_mutation for osimertinib, brca2_mutation for olaparib, pdl1_tps / stk11 / smoking for pembrolizumab) at the top.
judgments.rationale: Recovering canonical biomarker-drug pairs via systematic screening simply reaffirms guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: For treatment_sotorasib, sex_female is a negative effect modifier (treatment_sotorasib × sex_female has negative beta).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based negative modification of sotorasib efficacy is not part of established KRAS G12C treatment paradigm.

judgments.text: For treatment_osimertinib, alk_fusion is a positive effect modifier (treatment_osimertinib × alk_fusion has positive beta).
judgments.rationale: Positive ALK-fusion modification of osimertinib effect contradicts the EGFR-specific indication and is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND stk11_mutation=0, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1-high, STK11-wildtype NSCLC aligns with known immunotherapy biomarker paradigm.

judgments.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps>=0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is suppressed by stk11_mutation=1 (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction in this subset, i.e., effect closer to zero or negative when STK11 mutated).
judgments.rationale: While STK11 as a negative modifier of IO is emerging, the conditional-on-PD-L1-high subgroup interaction is not yet guideline-standard.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months is reduced by stk11_mutation=1 vs stk11_mutation=0 (negative 3-way treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × stk11_mutation term).
judgments.rationale: A three-way KRAS G12C × STK11 × sotorasib interaction is a speculative mechanistic claim beyond current guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_sotorasib benefit is essentially confined to kras_g12c=1 patients regardless of STK11 status (+2.0 to +2.6 months in kras_g12c=1 subgroups; near zero in kras_g12c=0).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit confined to KRAS G12C-mutant patients is the established indication and basic guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, no remaining clinical or biomarker covariate (ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, nlr, crp_mg_l, stk11_mutation) modifies treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months in a statistically significant way.
judgments.rationale: Null finding within an established indication (PARP inhibitor in BRCA-mutant disease) showing no modifiers — does not advance beyond guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within egfr_mutation=1 patients, treatment_osimertinib has no overall effect on pfs_months (mean difference near zero in this subset), refuting expected biology.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib has no PFS effect in EGFR-mutant patients contradicts well-established guideline biology, making this a non-trivial (if implausible) departure from consensus.

judgments.text: Within egfr_mutation=1, no candidate modifier (ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, smk_never, hist_sq, stk11_mutation, tmb_high) significantly modifies treatment_osimertinib effect.
judgments.rationale: A negative result that no covariates modify osimertinib effect in EGFR-mutant patients aligns with current guideline-level practice of treating all EGFR-mutant patients similarly.

judgments.text: Within pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND stk11_mutation=0, no laboratory modifier (albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl) significantly modifies treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Null finding that lab values don't modify pembrolizumab effect in PD-L1-high/STK11-wildtype patients reflects standard guideline practice without a new modifier claim.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, lower albumin_g_dl is associated with reduced treatment_sotorasib benefit (negative treatment_sotorasib × albumin_g_dl interaction within KRAS+ — i.e., as albumin rises, sotorasib effect diminishes).
judgments.rationale: A continuous albumin × sotorasib interaction within KRAS G12C-mutant patients is a specific subgroup-conditional modifier not described in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, has_brain_mets=1 reduces treatment_sotorasib benefit (negative treatment_sotorasib × has_brain_mets interaction within KRAS+).
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as a specific negative modifier of sotorasib benefit within KRAS G12C subgroup is a subgroup-conditional claim beyond standard guideline content.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets=1 has a strong negative main effect on pfs_months across the cohort.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases having a strong negative prognostic effect on PFS is a well-established textbook finding.

judgments.text: has_brain_mets does not significantly modify treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_sotorasib, treatment_olaparib, or treatment_osimertinib effects on pfs_months overall.
judgments.rationale: Claiming brain metastases do not modify response to any of these targeted/immunotherapy agents contradicts common clinical expectations (e.g., CNS penetration concerns) and is a non-trivial cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: sex_female negatively modifies treatment_sotorasib effect on pfs_months (treatment_sotorasib × sex_female interaction is negative — females derive less PFS benefit from sotorasib than males).
judgments.rationale: A sex-by-sotorasib interaction with females deriving less benefit is a specific subgroup-conditional modifier not in current KRAS G12C treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Sex does not modify treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, or treatment_osimertinib effects on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Sex not modifying these treatment effects aligns with current guideline practice that does not stratify these therapies by sex.

judgments.text: Three-way: the sotorasib benefit is concentrated specifically in kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (males); the three-way treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female interaction is strongly negative, indicating the kras_g12c×sotorasib interaction itself is much smaller in females.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction where KRAS G12C/sotorasib benefit is sex-restricted (concentrated in males) is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: In kras_g12c=1 patients, treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months substantially (positive effect ≈ +2.5 months overall in the marker-positive group).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib does NOT increase pfs_months (effect near zero), refuting expected efficacy.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib has no efficacy in BRCA2-mutant cancer contradicts established guideline consensus, making it a novel (refutational) finding.

judgments.text: In egfr_mutation=1 patients, treatment_osimertinib does NOT increase pfs_months (effect near zero), refuting expected efficacy.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib lacks efficacy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC contradicts established guideline consensus and is a novel refutational claim.

judgments.text: In pdl1_tps>=0.5 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT increase pfs_months (effect near zero or slightly negative), refuting expected efficacy.
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab lacks efficacy in PD-L1-high tumors contradicts established guideline consensus and is a novel refutational claim.

judgments.text: Brute-force search across pairwise binary subgroups (each defined by two of {sex_female, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high, hist_sq, smk_never, smk_current}) will identify the largest treatment_sotorasib effect in the joint subgroup (sex_female=0 AND kras_g12c=1).
judgments.rationale: Identifying a sex-restricted KRAS G12C subgroup as the top sotorasib responder via brute-force search is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Brute-force search will not identify any pairwise binary subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 months) and statistically significant treatment_pembrolizumab effect.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no pairwise subgroup yields meaningful pembrolizumab benefit contradicts guideline expectations (e.g., PD-L1-high) and is a novel negative claim.

judgments.text: Brute-force search will not identify any pairwise binary subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 months) and statistically significant treatment_olaparib effect.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no pairwise subgroup yields meaningful olaparib benefit contradicts guideline expectations (e.g., BRCA-mutant) and is a novel negative claim.

judgments.text: Brute-force search will identify treatment_osimertinib subgroups defined by alk_fusion=1 (combined with another modifier) at the top of the ranked list, with effects ≈ +0.25 months.
judgments.rationale: Predicting osimertinib benefit concentrated in ALK-fusion subgroups crosses pathways and contradicts guideline biology, making it a novel cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: In marker-negative subgroups (kras_g12c=0, brca2_mutation=0, egfr_mutation=0, pdl1_tps<0.01), the corresponding targeted treatment provides no pfs_months benefit (all near-zero treatment effects).
judgments.rationale: Lack of targeted-therapy benefit in marker-negative patients is the established biomarker-selection paradigm reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND stk11_mutation=0 AND tmb_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab (positive effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 high, TMB high, STK11 wild-type NSCLC is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib retains a positive effect on pfs_months after adjustment for stk11_mutation, tmb_high, ecog_ps, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, has_brain_mets and the treatment_sotorasib × stk11 / × tmb_high interactions; the main treatment_sotorasib coefficient remains large and significant.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC, with awareness of STK11 as a modifier, is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib does not show a clinically meaningful positive effect in any subgroup defined by has_brain_mets, smk_never, tmb_high, or stk11_mutation.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib has no meaningful benefit in any EGFR-mutant subgroup contradicts established guideline consensus and is a speculative dataset-specific negative finding.

judgments.text: When pfs_months is regressed on treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps + treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation, neither interaction is statistically significant; the treatment_pembrolizumab effect at pdl1_tps=0 is essentially zero, and stratifying by PDL1 quintiles shows no monotonic dose-response of pembrolizumab benefit with PDL1.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no PD-L1 dose-response and null pembrolizumab effect at PD-L1=0 contradicts established TPS-stratified benefit and is a non-guideline claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by approximately +4.6 months relative to no sotorasib in the joint subgroup defined by kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (KRAS G12C-positive males); this benefit is essentially absent (≈0 months) in kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=1 (females), confirming that female sex is the unfavorable modifier that suppresses the kras_g12c-defined sotorasib treatment effect. No benefit in kras_g12c=0 regardless of sex.
judgments.rationale: Sex-specific suppression of sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-positive females is not an established guideline modifier and is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_osimertinib increases pfs_months by approximately +0.26 months in the alk_fusion=1 subgroup (with no benefit elsewhere); the expected egfr_mutation-defined benefit is absent in this dataset.
judgments.rationale: Claiming osimertinib benefit confined to ALK-fusion patients with no EGFR-mutant benefit contradicts standard oncology paradigms.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT meaningfully increase pfs_months in any subgroup defined by combinations of pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, smoking_status, histology, or other modifiers. Best-supported hypothesis is null effect across all clinically considered subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Universal null pembrolizumab effect across all PD-L1/TMB/STK11 subgroups contradicts guideline-endorsed benefit in PD-L1-high NSCLC.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib does NOT meaningfully increase pfs_months in any subgroup, including brca2_mutation=1; best-supported hypothesis is null effect.
judgments.rationale: Null olaparib effect including in BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts guideline-supported PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutated disease.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic factor for survival/PFS is well-established across oncology and appears in standard prognostic indices.

judgments.text: Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook association across many cancers.

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss percent (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss / cachexia predicting worse outcomes is a long-established prognostic factor in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Older age associated with longer PFS contradicts standard prognostic expectations and would not appear in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting shorter PFS is foundational guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Stage IV (stage_iv==1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Stage IV disease predicting shorter PFS is definitional and textbook.

judgments.text: Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets==1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Brain metastases as an adverse prognostic factor is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab improving PFS as a main treatment effect is established across multiple approved indications.

judgments.text: Sotorasib (treatment_sotorasib==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no sotorasib (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit is established in its approved KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC indication.

judgments.text: Olaparib (treatment_olaparib==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's PFS benefit in BRCA/HRD-related cancers is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Osimertinib (treatment_osimertinib==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no osimertinib (overall main effect).
judgments.rationale: Overall osimertinib benefit is established in EGFR-mutant NSCLC and reflects standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In EGFR-mutated (egfr_mutation==1) patients, osimertinib (treatment_osimertinib==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no osimertinib.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutated patients is a textbook first-line standard-of-care association.

judgments.text: In KRAS G12C-mutated (kras_g12c==1) patients, sotorasib (treatment_sotorasib==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C-mutated NSCLC is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: In BRCA2-mutated (brca2_mutation==1) patients, olaparib (treatment_olaparib==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated tumors is a well-established guideline-level biomarker association.

judgments.text: In PD-L1 high (pdl1_tps>=0.5) patients, pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 high tumors is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In tumor-mutational-burden-high (tmb_high==1) patients, pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab==1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in TMB-high tumors is FDA-approved (tissue-agnostic) and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_osimertinib x egfr_mutation interaction on pfs_months (osimertinib's PFS benefit is greater in EGFR-mutated than EGFR-wildtype patients).
judgments.rationale: Greater osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant vs wildtype is the basic biomarker rationale taught in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_sotorasib x kras_g12c interaction on pfs_months (sotorasib's PFS benefit is greater in KRAS G12C-mutated than KRAS G12C-wildtype patients).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's selective benefit in KRAS G12C vs wildtype is the established mechanism-based indication.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction on pfs_months (olaparib's PFS benefit is greater in BRCA2-mutated than BRCA2-wildtype patients).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's preferential benefit in BRCA2-mutant vs wildtype is the canonical synthetic lethality biomarker interaction.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_high interaction on pfs_months (pembrolizumab's PFS benefit is greater in PD-L1>=50% than <50% patients).
judgments.rationale: Greater pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 high vs low is the standard predictive biomarker relationship in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab x tmb_high interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: TMB-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is established in guidelines (FDA tissue-agnostic approval for TMB-high tumors).

judgments.text: At least one feature in the dataset shows a significant treatment_pembrolizumab x feature interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: A generic claim that some modifier of pembrolizumab benefit exists is too vague to go beyond guideline consensus, which already lists multiple predictive biomarkers.

judgments.text: At least one feature shows a significant treatment_sotorasib x feature interaction on pfs_months beyond kras_g12c (suggesting an additional modifier of sotorasib effect).
judgments.rationale: An additional modifier of sotorasib effect beyond KRAS G12C is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: At least one feature shows a significant treatment_olaparib x feature interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Modifiers of olaparib PFS benefit beyond known HRD/BRCA status are not part of standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: At least one feature shows a significant treatment_osimertinib x feature interaction on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Effect modifiers of osimertinib benefit beyond EGFR mutation status are not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Sotorasib has a strong negative interaction with sex_female on pfs_months, i.e. the sotorasib PFS benefit is concentrated in male (sex_female==0) patients.
judgments.rationale: A male-specific concentration of sotorasib PFS benefit is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within EGFR-mutated patients, restricting to ECOG<=1 increases the osimertinib PFS benefit (osimertinib coefficient is positive and larger than in the full EGFR-mutated subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Better PFS with osimertinib in EGFR-mutated patients with good performance status (ECOG<=1) reflects standard trial-eligible population captured in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated and ECOG<=1 patients, sotorasib is associated with longer pfs_months than no sotorasib (refined positive subgroup for sotorasib).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C patients with good performance status reflects the standard CodeBreaK trial population and guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within PDL1-high (pdl1_tps>=0.5), TMB-high (tmb_high==1), and ECOG<=1 patients, pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PDL1-high, TMB-high, ECOG<=1 patients combines established guideline-level predictive biomarkers.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2-mutated and ECOG<=1 patients, olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated patients with good performance status is the established guideline indication.

judgments.text: STK11-mutated (stk11_mutation==1) status attenuates pembrolizumab's PFS benefit in PD-L1 high (pdl1_tps>=0.5) patients (pembrolizumab effect is more positive in stk11==0 than stk11==1 within PD-L1 high).
judgments.rationale: STK11 co-mutation as a negative modifier of pembrolizumab benefit specifically within PD-L1 high NSCLC is a research-level subgroup-conditional claim not codified in standard treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In PD-L1 high (pdl1_tps>=0.5) and STK11-wildtype (stk11_mutation==0) patients, pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in PD-L1 high NSCLC is established guideline-level knowledge, and STK11-wildtype is essentially the standard population.

judgments.text: Restricting to patients receiving only one treatment (monotherapy), sotorasib (treatment_sotorasib==1, with no other treatment) is associated with longer pfs_months than no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's PFS benefit over no treatment in KRAS G12C NSCLC is established and reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: In monotherapy, pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab monotherapy improving PFS over no treatment is standard guideline knowledge in NSCLC.

judgments.text: In monotherapy, olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib improving PFS over no treatment in eligible (e.g., BRCA-mutant) cancers is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In monotherapy, osimertinib is associated with longer pfs_months than no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib monotherapy improving PFS over no treatment in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is the textbook standard of care.

judgments.text: In EGFR-mutated patients with ECOG<=1, osimertinib remains positively associated with pfs_months relative to no osimertinib.
judgments.rationale: Osimertinib benefit in EGFR-mutant patients with good performance status is the guideline-standard first-line indication.
