judgments.text: In KRAS G12C-mutated patients (kras_g12c==1), sotorasib's PFS benefit is the strongest treatment-effect signal in the dataset (very large positive coefficient).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib's strong PFS benefit in KRAS G12C-mutant NSCLC is the established targeted-therapy paradigm.

judgments.text: In BRCA2-mutated and ECOG<=1 patients, olaparib is positively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant patients with good performance status is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In PD-L1 high & STK11-wildtype & ECOG<=1, pembrolizumab is positively associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Triple-conditional benefit specifically restricted to PD-L1 high, STK11-wildtype, ECOG<=1 frames STK11 status as a guideline-level selection criterion, which is not yet standard.

judgments.text: Within EGFR-mutated patients, no clinical/molecular feature significantly modifies the (null) osimertinib effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Null subgroup-modification finding within EGFR-mutant osimertinib patients is a non-finding consistent with guideline-level expectation that EGFR-mutant NSCLC uniformly benefits, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated patients, baseline serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) modifies the sotorasib effect on pfs_months (interaction is non-zero).
judgments.rationale: A specific albumin-by-sotorasib interaction modifying PFS in KRAS G12C-mutant patients is not in current guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2-mutated patients, no feature significantly modifies the (null) olaparib effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Reporting no effect-modifier within BRCA2-mutant olaparib-treated patients is a null subgroup finding consistent with standard guideline use, not novel.

judgments.text: A regression-tree T-learner identifies a top-decile subgroup with a very large positive sotorasib treatment effect on pfs_months (consistent with the KRAS G12C+ subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Recovering the KRAS G12C+ subgroup as the sotorasib responder simply rediscovers the established biomarker indication.

judgments.text: A regression-tree T-learner does NOT identify any meaningful pembrolizumab-responsive subgroup with a clinically large positive treatment effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Failure of a tree learner to identify any pembrolizumab-responsive subgroup contradicts established PD-L1/TMB/MSI-H predictive biomarkers in guidelines.

judgments.text: A regression-tree T-learner does not identify any meaningful olaparib-responsive subgroup with a clinically large positive treatment effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Not finding an olaparib-responsive subgroup in a general population echoes the guideline restriction of olaparib to BRCA/HRD-defined patients.

judgments.text: A regression-tree T-learner does not identify any meaningful osimertinib-responsive subgroup with a clinically large positive treatment effect on pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Not finding an osimertinib-responsive subgroup in a general population echoes the guideline restriction of osimertinib to EGFR-mutant patients.

judgments.text: Adjusting for treatments and demographics, higher albumin_g_dl is independently associated with longer pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Higher serum albumin as a favorable prognostic factor for PFS is well-established across oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Adjusting for treatments and demographics, higher ldh_u_l is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker associated with shorter PFS is a standard, guideline-level oncology association.

judgments.text: Adjusting for treatments and demographics, greater 6-month weight loss is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: Greater weight loss (cachexia) predicting shorter PFS is a classical, textbook prognostic association in oncology.

judgments.text: After adjustment, the neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is independently associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: NLR as an independent prognostic biomarker for PFS is well-established in NSCLC and broader oncology literature/guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, pdl1_tps as a continuous predictor is independently associated with pfs_months.
judgments.rationale: PD-L1 TPS as a continuous predictor of outcomes is standard oncology paradigm in NSCLC.

judgments.text: Sotorasib's PFS benefit is modified by sex_female: the treatment effect is markedly smaller (or absent) in female (sex_female==1) patients than in males (sex_female==0).
judgments.rationale: Sex-based modification of sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C NSCLC is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Sotorasib's PFS benefit is modified by smoking_current: the treatment effect is larger in current smokers (smoking_current==1) than in non-current smokers - though once stratified within KRAS G12C+ this is small.
judgments.rationale: Smoking status as an effect modifier of sotorasib benefit beyond KRAS G12C selection is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab effect is not significantly modified by sex_female, age_years, or smoking status.
judgments.rationale: Lack of demographic effect modification of pembrolizumab benefit aligns with established understanding (a null finding consistent with guidelines).

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated (kras_g12c==1) and male (sex_female==0) patients, sotorasib (treatment_sotorasib==1) is associated with substantially longer pfs_months than no sotorasib (large positive treatment effect).
judgments.rationale: A male-restricted sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C patients is a subgroup claim not reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated (kras_g12c==1) and female (sex_female==1) patients, sotorasib (treatment_sotorasib==1) shows NO PFS benefit relative to no sotorasib (treatment effect indistinguishable from zero).
judgments.rationale: Absence of sotorasib benefit in female KRAS G12C patients contradicts current treatment paradigm and is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a strong negative three-way interaction between treatment_sotorasib, kras_g12c, and sex_female on pfs_months: the sotorasib benefit is concentrated in male KRAS-G12C-positive patients.
judgments.rationale: A three-way treatment-by-mutation-by-sex interaction concentrating sotorasib benefit in males is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated patients, BRCA2 mutation (brca2_mutation==1) substantially reduces the sotorasib PFS benefit (large negative interaction with sotorasib).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as a negative modifier of sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C patients is a novel cross-pathway hypothesis not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated, BRCA2-wildtype, male (kras_g12c==1 & brca2_mutation==0 & sex_female==0) patients, sotorasib provides a very large positive PFS benefit relative to no sotorasib.
judgments.rationale: A triply-conditioned subgroup (KRAS G12C+/BRCA2-WT/male) defining maximal sotorasib benefit is a speculative subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated, BRCA2-wildtype, male, ECOG<=1 patients, sotorasib provides a very large positive PFS benefit relative to no sotorasib (final refined positive sotorasib subgroup).
judgments.rationale: Sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C-mutated NSCLC is established; restricting to BRCA2-wildtype, male, ECOG<=1 is a routine subgroup slice without mechanistic novelty beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated female (sex_female==1) patients, sotorasib does NOT improve pfs_months relative to no sotorasib (negative subgroup defined by the variable that suppresses the treatment effect).
judgments.rationale: A sex-specific suppression of sotorasib efficacy in KRAS G12C-mutated females is not part of guideline consensus and represents a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within KRAS G12C-mutated and BRCA2-mutated patients, sotorasib does NOT improve pfs_months relative to no sotorasib (a second variable whose 'unfavorable' value suppresses the treatment effect).
judgments.rationale: A claim that BRCA2 mutation abrogates sotorasib benefit in KRAS G12C disease is a cross-pathway interaction not reflected in current guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: Marker-treatment 'mismatch' subgroups (e.g., osimertinib in EGFR-wildtype, sotorasib in KRAS-wildtype, olaparib in BRCA2-wildtype, pembrolizumab in PDL1<0.01) all show null PFS effects, confirming biological specificity for sotorasib but absence of biomarker-driven benefit for the other three drugs.
judgments.rationale: That biomarker-targeted drugs lack benefit in marker-negative populations (EGFR-wt, KRAS-wt, BRCA2-wt, PD-L1<1%) is the foundational rationale of biomarker-driven therapy and is standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide improving response rates in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's effect on response rate in prostate cancer is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel's activity in prostate cancer is a long-standing, guideline-endorsed standard.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib activity in prostate cancer (particularly HRR-mutated) is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA response in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer is established (VISION trial) and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's role in MSI-H/TMB-high prostate cancer is recognized in guidelines, and its general lack of broad activity is also well-known.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA reflecting greater disease burden and worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor performance status and worse outcomes is standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss predicting worse cancer outcomes is a well-established prognostic finding.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory marker associated with worse cancer outcomes is well-established prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker associated with worse treatment response is well-established across multiple solid tumors including prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age associated with reduced treatment response/tolerance is a standard clinical observation reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a marker of poor response to systemic therapy is well-documented in oncology literature and increasingly cited in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response is a fundamental, guideline-endorsed prognostic factor.

judgments.text: mcrpc=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Metastatic castration-resistant disease having lower response rates than non-mCRPC is established baseline knowledge in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutations are typically associated with improved (not lower) response to PARP inhibitors and platinum, so a lower response rate would contradict guideline expectations and warrant scrutiny.

judgments.text: ar_v7_positive=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to AR-targeted therapies is well-established in mCRPC guidelines.

judgments.text: msi_high=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high tumors are typically associated with better response to immunotherapy, so a lower overall response rate in prostate cancer would be a non-guideline, context-dependent finding.

judgments.text: psma_high=1 is associated with a different rate of objective_response than psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression predicting differential response, particularly to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy, is established in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: visceral_mets=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic feature with lower response rates is a well-established, guideline-recognized factor in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score reflecting more aggressive disease and predicting worse treatment response is established prostate cancer dogma.

judgments.text: After adjustment for ECOG, PSA, mcrpc, brca2, ar_v7, msi_high, albumin, weight loss, CRP, and other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide retains a positive association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A residual positive enzalutamide effect after adjustment for AR-V7, BRCA2, MSI, and disease burden markers is a specific multivariable claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_abiraterone has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A null effect of abiraterone after full adjustment contradicts the guideline expectation of benefit in mCRPC and is a non-trivial conditional claim.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_docetaxel has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Null effect of docetaxel after adjustment for biomarkers and comorbidity goes beyond textbook claims of chemotherapy benefit.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_olaparib has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A marginal (non-interaction) null for olaparib across the full population is a specific adjusted-model claim, not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_lu177_psma has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A fully-adjusted null main effect for Lu-177-PSMA is a model-specific claim not found in guidelines, which describe overall benefit in PSMA-positive mCRPC.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Null main effect of pembrolizumab after adjustment (including MSI status) is a conditional multivariable claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib increases objective_response specifically in brca2_mutation=1 patients (interaction term positive).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit specifically in BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge (PROfound, olaparib labeling).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response specifically in msi_high=1 patients (interaction term positive).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is the established tissue-agnostic FDA indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response specifically in psma_high=1 patients (interaction term positive).
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA benefit confined to PSMA-high disease is the established VISION-trial paradigm reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in ar_v7_positive=1 patients vs ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to enzalutamide is a well-established resistance biomarker documented in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response is also smaller in ar_v7_positive=1 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to abiraterone is established in guidelines alongside the enzalutamide finding from Antonarakis et al.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in mcrpc=1 patients vs mcrpc=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines actually establish enzalutamide efficacy in mCRPC; a negative interaction with mCRPC status contradicts standard paradigm rather than reflecting it.

judgments.text: The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response is similarly modified by mcrpc status (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Similarly, abiraterone is guideline-endorsed in mCRPC, so a negative mCRPC interaction is not a standard known association.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in brca2_mutation=1 patients vs brca2_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status as a modifier of enzalutamide response is not established guideline consensus; BRCA2 is linked to PARP inhibitor response, not AR-targeted therapy resistance.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in msi_high=1 patients vs msi_high=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a modifier of enzalutamide response is not standard paradigm; MSI-high prostate cancer is associated with immunotherapy response, not AR-therapy modification.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0; the three-way interaction enzalutamide:mcrpc:ar_v7_positive is positive.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction concentrating enzalutamide benefit in non-metastatic AR-V7-negative patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 subgroup, treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate by >50 percentage points relative to no enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: A >50 percentage point absolute response increase in a specific multi-marker subgroup is a speculative magnitude claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Adding brca2_mutation=0 to the (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0) subgroup further increases the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response, while the residual subgroup (brca2_mutation=1) shows essentially no enzalutamide effect.
judgments.rationale: Combining BRCA2 wild-type with mCRPC-negative and AR-V7-negative as an enzalutamide super-responder definition is a novel cross-pathway subgroup hypothesis.

judgments.text: Adding msi_high=0 to the (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0) subgroup yields the cleanest treatment_enzalutamide responder subgroup; objective_response rate on enzalutamide approaches 0.80 and the risk difference exceeds 0.62.
judgments.rationale: A four-marker subgroup yielding ~80% response rate and risk difference >0.62 is a highly specific speculative claim well beyond any guideline statement.

judgments.text: Each of the four predicates {mcrpc=0, brca2_mutation=0, ar_v7_positive=0, msi_high=0} is necessary for the enzalutamide effect: dropping any single predicate produces a stratum where adding back the unfavorable level (e.g., mcrpc=1) collapses the enzalutamide treatment effect to ~zero.
judgments.rationale: Claiming all four specific predicates are individually necessary for enzalutamide benefit is a speculative conjunctive subgroup claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup (mcrpc=0 & brca2=0 & ar_v7=0 & msi=0), visceral_mets does not further modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A guideline would not specify that visceral metastases fails to further modify enzalutamide effect specifically within this four-way favorable subgroup.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, psma_high does not further modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression as a non-modifier of enzalutamide response within a defined favorable stratum is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, ecog_ps does not meaningfully modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Conditional non-modification by ECOG within this specific multi-criteria favorable subgroup is not a standard guideline statement.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, gleason_score does not modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gleason score as a non-modifier of enzalutamide effect within this specific favorable subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml diminishes the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA reflecting greater disease burden and reduced response to AR-directed therapy aligns with established prognostic paradigms.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l diminishes the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic factor reducing response to systemic therapy in advanced prostate cancer is well established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl enhances the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin reflecting better performance status and improved treatment outcomes is standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: The negative psa_ng_ml × treatment_enzalutamide interaction is fully explained by membership in the favorable subgroup (mcrpc=0 & brca2=0 & ar_v7=0 & msi=0): within favorable patients PSA does not modulate the enzalutamide effect, and within unfavorable patients PSA also does not modulate the enzalutamide effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the PSA×treatment interaction is fully mediated by membership in a specific four-criteria favorable subgroup is a speculative cross-pathway mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: A random-forest T-learner on treatment_enzalutamide identifies a top-CATE quartile of patients with observed enzalutamide effect approaching +0.70 percentage points; the bottom-CATE quartile shows no benefit.
judgments.rationale: A specific causal-forest-derived CATE quartile structure with these effect magnitudes is a data-driven heterogeneity finding not present in guidelines.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_abiraterone is approximately zero, with no genuinely treatment-driven CATE quartile separation (apparent quartile differences track baseline prognosis, not treatment).
judgments.rationale: Null average treatment effect findings for a standard prostate cancer therapy are descriptive negative results, not a novel biological or clinical claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_docetaxel is approximately zero with no genuine treatment-driven heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A null ATE for docetaxel is a descriptive statistical negative result, not a novel oncology hypothesis.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_olaparib is approximately zero with no genuine treatment-driven heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A null ATE for olaparib without subgroup conditioning is a descriptive negative result, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_lu177_psma is approximately zero with no genuine treatment-driven heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A null ATE for Lu-177 PSMA is a descriptive negative result, not a novel mechanistic or subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_pembrolizumab is approximately zero with no genuine treatment-driven heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A null ATE for pembrolizumab is a descriptive negative result, not a novel oncology hypothesis.

judgments.text: Exhaustive search over all 2-feature binary subgroups (across {mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high}) does not identify any subgroup in which treatment_abiraterone meaningfully (>5 pp) and significantly (p<0.05) raises objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Failure to identify a 2-feature subgroup with abiraterone benefit is a negative analytic finding, not a novel claim that contradicts or extends guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Same exhaustive search yields no subgroup with treatment_docetaxel benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result for docetaxel is descriptive, not a novel mechanistic or cross-pathway hypothesis.

judgments.text: Same exhaustive search yields no subgroup with treatment_olaparib benefit on objective_response, including BRCA2-positive subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no olaparib benefit even in BRCA2-positive subgroups contradicts established guideline consensus that BRCA-mutant mCRPC responds to PARP inhibition, making it a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Same exhaustive search yields no subgroup with treatment_lu177_psma benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result for Lu-177 PSMA is a descriptive analytic finding, not a novel guideline-extending claim.

judgments.text: Same exhaustive search yields no subgroup with treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result for pembrolizumab (without specifically contradicting MSI-high benefit) is descriptive, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: When the full multivariable logistic model is augmented with one treatment_enzalutamide × feature interaction at a time, the strongest interactions remain mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high (all negative; effect-suppressors). Other features (visceral_mets, psma_high, ecog_ps, gleason_score) show only weak residual interactions after these four are accounted for.
judgments.rationale: Specific multivariable interaction structure identifying mcrpc, AR-V7, BRCA2, and MSI-high as the dominant effect-suppressors of enzalutamide response is a data-driven finding not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is concentrated in the subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND msi_high=0; in this favorable subgroup the response rate on enzalutamide is approximately 0.80 vs 0.17 off-treatment (risk difference ~+0.63). Outside this subgroup (any one of mcrpc=1, brca2_mutation=1, ar_v7_positive=1, msi_high=1), enzalutamide produces no meaningful change in objective_response (risk difference <+0.01).
judgments.rationale: A precisely defined four-feature favorable subgroup with ~63% absolute response difference for enzalutamide is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim beyond standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The favorable-subgroup enzalutamide effect on objective_response is robust to adjustment for the other five treatments and the strong prognostic factors (ECOG, PSA, weight loss, CRP, albumin).
judgments.rationale: Robustness of this specific composite favorable-subgroup enzalutamide effect to adjustment for other treatments and prognostic markers is a study-specific multivariable claim, not paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib is associated with a numerically lower objective_response rate within brca2_mutation=1 patients (a small negative direction effect, opposite to the mechanistic prior).
judgments.rationale: A negative direction signal for olaparib in BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts the established PARP inhibitor mechanistic paradigm and is therefore non-guideline.

judgments.text: There is no subgroup in which any of {treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab} is convincingly associated with a clinically meaningful change in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A blanket null claim across abiraterone, docetaxel, Lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab contradicts established efficacy of these agents in mCRPC and is a non-standard cross-pathway assertion.

judgments.text: sex_female is uniformly 0 in this prostate cohort and therefore uninformative.
judgments.rationale: Prostate cancer occurring essentially exclusively in males is trivially established and would appear in any textbook.

judgments.text: gleason_score does not predict objective_response in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: While Gleason score's prognostic role is well known, the absence of association with response in a single cohort is a mundane null finding rather than a paradigm-shifting claim — but more importantly Gleason as a non-predictor of treatment response is consistent with guideline framing.

judgments.text: There is a monotonic decrease in objective_response rate with increasing ecog_ps after adjustment for treatment_enzalutamide and other prognostic features.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting lower response/outcomes is standard, guideline-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is independently associated with lower objective_response after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is established textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is independently associated with lower objective_response after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker in advanced cancer is well established in guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: The single best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis in this cohort is: treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response by ~0.63 absolute (from ~0.17 to ~0.80) in patients with mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is ~0.
judgments.rationale: A highly conditional four-way subgroup (non-mCRPC, BRCA2 wild-type, AR-V7 negative, MSS) driving a ~0.63 absolute response increase to enzalutamide is a speculative cross-pathway claim not found in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: No other treatment in the cohort (treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab) shows a credible treatment effect on objective_response, in any tested subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Asserting null treatment effects across docetaxel, abiraterone, olaparib (in BRCA-mutant), Lu-177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab (in MSI-high) contradicts established guideline-supported activity of these agents and is not a standard textbook claim.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with treatment_enzalutamide=0.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_abiraterone=0.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone response in prostate cancer is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_docetaxel=0.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel response in prostate cancer is standard textbook chemotherapy knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_olaparib=0.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib response (especially in HRR-mutated prostate cancer) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_lu177_psma=0.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-positive mCRPC is now in guidelines (VISION trial).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab response in MSI-high/dMMR prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: mCRPC having lower response rates than hormone-sensitive disease is standard textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Claiming BRCA2-mutant patients have LOWER overall response rates contradicts the established paradigm that BRCA2 mutations predict PARP inhibitor sensitivity.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapy is established prostate cancer biomarker knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: Claiming MSI-high patients have LOWER response rates contradicts the established paradigm that MSI-high tumors respond to immune checkpoint inhibitors.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression as a predictive/prognostic biomarker in prostate cancer is well-established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases are a well-known poor prognostic factor associated with lower response rates across solid tumors.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is foundational oncology knowledge in every guideline.

judgments.text: Treatment_olaparib increases objective_response more in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in patients with brca2_mutation=0 (canonical PARP-inhibitor pairing).
judgments.rationale: Explicitly labeled as the canonical PARP inhibitor–BRCA2 pairing, which is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response more in patients with psma_high=1 than in patients with psma_high=0 (canonical PSMA-targeted radioligand pairing).
judgments.rationale: Explicitly the canonical PSMA-high predicts Lu-177-PSMA benefit pairing established by VISION and in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response more in patients with msi_high=1 than in patients with msi_high=0 (canonical immune-checkpoint MSI pairing).
judgments.rationale: Canonical MSI-high predicts pembrolizumab benefit, a tumor-agnostic FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed pairing.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response in ar_v7_positive=0 patients but provides no benefit (or reduced benefit) in ar_v7_positive=1 patients (AR-V7 resistance).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 splice variant as a resistance marker to enzalutamide is well-established in prostate cancer literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_abiraterone raises objective_response more in ar_v7_positive=0 patients than in ar_v7_positive=1 patients (AR-V7 resistance).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 resistance to abiraterone is the well-known companion finding to enzalutamide resistance, established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response in mcrpc=0 patients but provides no benefit in mcrpc=1 patients (castration-resistance modifies effect).
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide benefit is restricted to non-castration-resistant disease contradicts standard mCRPC indications and is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response in brca2_mutation=0 patients but provides no benefit in brca2_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status modifying enzalutamide (an AR-targeted therapy, not PARP inhibitor) response is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response in msi_high=0 patients but provides no benefit in msi_high=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide benefit restricted to MSI-low patients is not a standard guideline association.

judgments.text: At least one of the six treatments has a significant interaction with at least one of the six categorical biomarkers (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, psma_high, visceral_mets) on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Generic existence claim of any treatment-biomarker interaction is too vague to constitute a novel paradigm-shifting hypothesis.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide has a large positive effect on objective_response in patients jointly satisfying mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, and essentially no effect in any patient violating one or more of these conditions.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conjunctive predictive signature for enzalutamide response is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Each of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, msi_high=1 individually abolishes the enzalutamide treatment effect when other modifiers are favorable (i.e., the four predicates act conjunctively).
judgments.rationale: The conjunctive abolition pattern across four heterogeneous biomarkers is a non-standard mechanistic claim not in any textbook.

judgments.text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, at least one of the non-enzalutamide treatments (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab) raises objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, Lu-177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab all have established mCRPC activity, so this is guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment_olaparib raises objective_response in mcrpc=1 AND brca2_mutation=1 patients (canonical PARP-inhibitor indication).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC is the canonical PARP inhibitor indication explicitly in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_lu177_psma raises objective_response in mcrpc=1 AND psma_high=1 patients (canonical Lu-177 indication).
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA in PSMA-high mCRPC is the canonical approved indication.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA correlating with worse response is standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a favorable prognostic marker is well-established across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic marker is standard established oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia as a negative prognostic factor for treatment response is well-established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker associated with poorer response is standard textbook knowledge across many cancers.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a marker of poor response/prognosis is widely established in oncology literature and increasingly in guidelines.

judgments.text: age_years is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic/response-associated variable is a generic, well-known clinical association, not a novel finding.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, mcrpc, ecog_ps, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, albumin_g_dl, and psa_ng_ml remain independent predictors of objective_response, while non-enzalutamide treatments and most labs do not.
judgments.rationale: A specific multivariable model claiming these particular covariates remain independent predictors while specific treatments and labs do not is a dataset-specific, non-guideline claim.

judgments.text: Among the 6 treatments, at least one shows a significant interaction with at least one continuous feature (PSA, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin, ALP, age) on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming a specific treatment-by-continuous-biomarker interaction on response is a speculative heterogeneity claim not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7_positive=0 & brca2_mutation=0 & msi_high=0), the enzalutamide treatment effect remains large and approximately constant across psa_ng_ml deciles, visceral_mets status, ECOG, and gleason_score.
judgments.rationale: A subgroup-conditional claim that enzalutamide effect is constant across PSA deciles, visceral mets, ECOG, and Gleason within a defined biomarker-favorable subgroup is a specific cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: A T-learner (gradient boosting) trained separately on each treated arm will identify the same 4 modifiers (mcrpc, ar_v7, brca2, msi) as the dominant drivers of enzalutamide treatment-effect heterogeneity, and will not identify a coherent subgroup with meaningful uplift for any other treatment.
judgments.rationale: A methodological claim about T-learner heterogeneity drivers and absence of uplift subgroups for other treatments is a speculative ML-derived finding not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab raises objective_response in msi_high=1, mcrpc=0 patients (a more clinically intuitive subset).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is an established tumor-agnostic guideline indication, and restricting to non-mCRPC does not change its standard nature.

judgments.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup, at least one of the 5 non-enzalutamide treatments raises objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming a non-enzalutamide treatment raises response within a specifically defined enz-favorable biomarker subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: No treatment raises objective_response in mcrpc=1 patients, including the canonical pairings olaparib+BRCA2, lu177+PSMA-high, and pembrolizumab in mCRPC+AR-V7+ resistant disease.
judgments.rationale: Reports null findings for canonical guideline-endorsed pairings (olaparib/BRCA, Lu-177/PSMA, pembrolizumab/MSI), which is a dataset-specific negative result framed against standard paradigm rather than a new biological claim.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide effect is only present when ALL FOUR favorable conditions hold; relaxing any single condition (with the other three favorable) returns enzalutamide effect to ~0.
judgments.rationale: Claims enzalutamide benefit requires simultaneous satisfaction of four specific conditions (non-mCRPC, AR-V7-negative, BRCA2-wild-type, MSS) with no benefit if any one is relaxed — a four-way conjunctive predictor not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup, the enz absolute response benefit is approximately +0.6 across ECOG (0/1/2), visceral_mets (0/1), gleason (6-10), and psma_high (0/1), indicating no further multiplicative modifier.
judgments.rationale: A flat ~+0.6 absolute response benefit across ECOG, visceral mets, Gleason, and PSMA status within the favorable subgroup is a specific quantitative invariance claim not present in standard references.

judgments.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup, enzalutamide effect does not depend on psma_high status.
judgments.rationale: Asserts PSMA-high status does not modify enzalutamide response within the favorable subgroup, a specific non-modifier claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Among ALL 2-binary-feature subgroups (using mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2, ar_v7, msi_high, psma_high, high_psa, low_alb, high_crp, high_nlr, high_ldh, high_ecog), there exists a 2-feature subgroup in which abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, or pembrolizumab raises objective_response significantly (after considering multiplicity).
judgments.rationale: An existence claim that some 2-feature subgroup shows a significant positive effect for guideline-standard biomarker-drug pairings is essentially a restatement of established subgroup efficacy expectations.

judgments.text: A single composite indicator enz_fav := (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7=0 & brca2=0 & msi=0) explains the heterogeneity of enzalutamide effect on objective_response with a strong positive interaction term in a covariate-adjusted logit model.
judgments.rationale: Proposes a specific composite indicator combining four biomarkers as a unifying predictor of enzalutamide heterogeneity, which is a novel synthesized signature beyond paradigm.

judgments.text: After adjusting for ECOG, mCRPC, visceral mets, PSA, BRCA2, AR-V7, MSI-high, PSMA-high, albumin, weight loss, CRP, and concurrent enzalutamide, none of abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, or pembrolizumab has a significant effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claims that after multivariable adjustment, none of abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, Lu-177-PSMA, or pembrolizumab affect response — contradicts guideline-endorsed efficacy and is a non-trivial dataset-specific claim.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment, the canonical biomarker pairings olaparib*brca2_mutation, lu177*psma_high, and pembrolizumab*msi_high have positive interaction coefficients on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Positive interactions for olaparib×BRCA2, Lu-177×PSMA-high, and pembrolizumab×MSI-high are the canonical guideline-endorsed biomarker-drug interactions.

judgments.text: Treatment assignment is approximately independent of all baseline biomarkers (mcrpc, ar_v7, brca2, msi, psma_high, visceral_mets), implying that the absence of treatment effects (and the presence of the enzalutamide HTE) is not due to confounding by indication.
judgments.rationale: Claiming covariate balance / lack of confounding by indication is a methodological observation about the dataset, not a novel oncology hypothesis.

judgments.text: Adding all 4 enzalutamide x modifier interactions plus the 5 non-enz treatment main effects to a covariate-adjusted logit model: each enzalutamide x modifier interaction coefficient is large negative (~-1.5 to -2.2) and highly significant; main effects of the other 5 treatments remain non-significant.
judgments.rationale: Specific quantitative claim that all four enzalutamide×modifier interactions are large-negative (~-1.5 to -2.2) while five other treatments remain inert is a non-paradigmatic, dataset-specific multivariable pattern.

judgments.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup, ECOG, albumin, CRP, weight loss, and PSA remain independent prognostic predictors of objective_response (with enzalutamide as the dominant predictor), while LDH, NLR, visceral_mets, and PSMA-high are not.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional independent prognostic ranking within an enzalutamide-favorable strata is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: There exists a synergistic two-treatment combination interaction on objective_response (e.g., enz+doc, abi+ola).
judgments.rationale: A specific synergistic two-treatment combination interaction (e.g., enz+doc, abi+ola) on response is not established in mCRPC guidelines.

judgments.text: Among ALL 3-binary-feature subgroups (8 binary indicators, all 2^3 value combinations), there exists a subgroup in which a non-enzalutamide treatment raises objective_response with effect size >+0.10 and chance-corrected significance.
judgments.rationale: An existence claim about a 3-binary-feature subgroup where a non-enzalutamide treatment yields >+0.10 response is a speculative subgroup-conditional finding beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response by approximately +0.6 absolute (RR ~0.80 vs ~0.17) in patients jointly satisfying mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in any patient in whom one or more of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high is positive, treatment_enzalutamide has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response (point estimate <+0.025 in absolute terms).
judgments.rationale: A precise multi-biomarker conjunction (mCRPC=0 AND AR-V7- AND BRCA2- AND MSI-low) gating a ~+0.6 enzalutamide effect with null effect otherwise is far more specific than any guideline claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: None of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, or treatment_pembrolizumab has a detectable causal effect on objective_response in this cohort, in the overall population or in any clinically motivated biomarker subgroup (BRCA2+ for olaparib, PSMA-high for lu177, MSI-high for pembrolizumab, AR-V7+ for abiraterone).
judgments.rationale: A blanket null-effect claim across abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib (BRCA2+), lu177-PSMA (PSMA-high), and pembrolizumab (MSI-high) contradicts established guideline-level efficacy signals and is a non-standard composite assertion.

judgments.text: Sensitivity check: the nominal pembrolizumab signal in the (BRCA2+, high-albumin, visceral_mets+) subgroup does not generalize — within all BRCA2+ patients, pembrolizumab effect is +0.011 (p=0.69), and after adjustment p=0.74.
judgments.rationale: A specific non-generalization claim about a pembrolizumab signal in a (BRCA2+, high-albumin, visceral) subgroup is a granular sensitivity finding not in any guideline.

judgments.text: FINAL: Adverse prognostic factors for objective_response (independent of treatment) are mcrpc=1, ecog_ps high, brca2_mutation=1, ar_v7_positive=1, msi_high=1, low albumin_g_dl, high crp_mg_l, high weight_loss_pct_6mo, and high psa_ng_ml. visceral_mets, gleason_score, psma_high, age_years, ldh_u_l, nlr, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, and calcium are NOT independent predictors after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: ECOG, albumin, CRP, weight loss, PSA, mCRPC status, and known resistance biomarkers (AR-V7, BRCA2, MSI) being adverse prognostic factors—and routine labs being non-independent after adjustment—aligns with standard prostate cancer prognostic literature and guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's efficacy in prostate cancer is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone response in prostate cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel response in prostate cancer is well-established standard chemotherapy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib response, particularly in HRR-mutated prostate cancer, is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-positive mCRPC is now standard guideline knowledge (VISION trial).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's limited activity in prostate cancer (except MSI-H/dMMR) is established.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: mCRPC having lower response than hormone-sensitive disease is a foundational clinical paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic factor in prostate cancer is textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation generally predicts better PARP inhibitor response, so a lower overall response rate across treatments would be a non-guideline finding.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to AR-targeted therapy is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting response to immunotherapy is a well-established biomarker in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression predicting response (e.g., to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy in prostate cancer) is established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rates.
judgments.rationale: Poorer ECOG performance status correlating with worse treatment outcomes is a foundational, textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with lower objective_response rates.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score predicting worse prostate cancer outcomes is a core textbook prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA correlating with worse prostate cancer outcomes is a standard, guideline-level prognostic association.

judgments.text: Lower albumin_g_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor nutritional/inflammatory status predicting worse cancer outcomes is well-established in standard oncology practice.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse treatment response and survival is a textbook prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory marker predicting worse oncologic outcomes is widely established in the literature and prognostic scores.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a marker of tumor burden/aggressiveness predicting worse outcomes is a standard, guideline-incorporated prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio predicting worse cancer outcomes is a well-established prognostic biomarker across many tumor types.

judgments.text: Lower hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Anemia as an adverse prognostic marker in mCRPC is well-established and appears in standard prognostic nomograms.

judgments.text: age_years is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic/response-modifying factor in prostate cancer is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflecting bone metastatic burden and predicting worse outcomes is textbook mCRPC knowledge.

judgments.text: After controlling for all features and treatments, treatment_enzalutamide retains a significant positive effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A treatment-agnostic positive effect of enzalutamide that persists after adjusting for all other features and treatments is a specific cross-cohort claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab each have a near-zero average effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: The claim that abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, Lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab each have near-zero average effect after adjustment contradicts guideline expectations of meaningful average benefit for several of these agents.

judgments.text: After adjustment, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, ecog_ps, log(psa), log(crp), and weight_loss_pct_6mo remain independently negatively associated with objective_response, while albumin remains positively associated.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that this specific combined set of biomarkers (including AR-V7, BRCA2, MSI-H, CRP, weight loss, albumin) all retain independent associations after mutual adjustment is a multivariable claim beyond guideline-level statements.

judgments.text: After adjustment, gleason_score, age_years, hemoglobin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast/alt/bilirubin/creatinine/bun and the electrolytes have no independent association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A negative claim that Gleason score, LDH, alkaline phosphatase, and standard labs have no independent association after adjustment contradicts established prognostic models like Halabi where several of these are independent predictors.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib increases objective_response more in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in those with brca2_mutation=0 (PARP-inhibitor synthetic lethality).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor synthetic lethality with BRCA2 mutations is established guideline knowledge for mCRPC.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response more in patients with msi_high=1 than in those with msi_high=0 (immunotherapy in MSI-H tumors).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is a tumor-agnostic FDA-approved indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response more in patients with psma_high=1 than in those with psma_high=0 (PSMA-targeted radioligand).
judgments.rationale: Lu177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-high mCRPC is the established VISION-trial-based guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response less (or not at all) in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 than in those with ar_v7_positive=0 (AR-V7 confers AR-antagonist resistance).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 as a biomarker of resistance to enzalutamide is well-established in mCRPC guidelines and prostate cancer literature.

judgments.text: treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response less in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 than in those with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 conferring resistance to abiraterone is a well-known established association alongside enzalutamide resistance.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response substantially more in patients with mcrpc=0 (hormone-sensitive disease) than in patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant).
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide is standard in both mHSPC and mCRPC; claiming substantially larger response in hormone-sensitive disease as a differential effect is not a guideline-level paradigm and constitutes a speculative subgroup claim.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide effect is similar across visceral_mets status.
judgments.rationale: Comparable enzalutamide efficacy regardless of visceral metastases or ECOG status is consistent with existing trial subgroup analyses and guideline use.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide effect is similar across ecog_ps levels.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide is used across ECOG 0-2 with broadly similar benefit per existing trial data.

judgments.text: Within the joint subgroup ar_v7_positive=0 AND mcrpc=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces the largest absolute increase in objective_response, while in ar_v7_positive=0 AND mcrpc=1 the effect is essentially absent.
judgments.rationale: A specific joint AR-V7-/hormone-sensitive subgroup yielding the largest absolute response while AR-V7-/CRPC shows essentially no benefit is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Among all 6 treatments × 7 candidate modifiers (mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, ecog_ps), only treatment_enzalutamide shows large modifier interactions on objective_response, and its strongest negative modifiers are mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and msi_high.
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide is uniquely modifier-sensitive among six treatments with a specific multi-marker negative modifier set (including BRCA2 and MSI-high) is a speculative cross-pathway pattern not supported by guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the AR-V7- AND mcrpc=0 subgroup, brca2_mutation=1 abolishes the treatment_enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation abolishing enzalutamide benefit within an AR-V7-/hormone-sensitive subgroup is a speculative mechanistic interaction not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the AR-V7- AND mcrpc=0 subgroup, msi_high=1 abolishes the treatment_enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high abolishing enzalutamide benefit in an AR-V7-/hormone-sensitive subgroup is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the AR-V7- AND mcrpc=0 subgroup, psma_high status does NOT meaningfully modify the treatment_enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: A specific subgroup-conditional null modification by PSMA-high status on enzalutamide benefit is not a guideline-level claim and constitutes a speculative subgroup hypothesis.

judgments.text: Within the AR-V7- AND mcrpc=0 subgroup, visceral_mets does NOT meaningfully modify the treatment_enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: Stating that visceral metastases do not modify enzalutamide benefit in a non-mCRPC, AR-V7-negative subgroup is a null/negative subgroup claim consistent with standard expectations that enzalutamide works broadly in AR-V7-negative disease.

judgments.text: Within the AR-V7- AND mcrpc=0 subgroup, ecog_ps does NOT meaningfully modify the treatment_enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: A null finding that ECOG performance status does not modify enzalutamide benefit in AR-V7-negative non-mCRPC patients aligns with guideline consensus that enzalutamide is effective across performance status strata.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with smaller treatment_enzalutamide benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not specify that higher baseline PSA predicts diminished enzalutamide response on objective response rate; this quantitative biomarker-modifier claim goes beyond standard prescribing guidance.

judgments.text: Other continuous labs (albumin, LDH, hemoglobin, ALP, CRP, NLR, weight_loss, age, calcium) do not meaningfully modify the treatment_enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that routine labs (albumin, LDH, Hgb, ALP, CRP, NLR, weight loss, age, calcium) do not modify enzalutamide benefit is a generic null that matches the absence of guideline-endorsed lab-based selection for enzalutamide.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in a narrower subgroup defined by msi_high=1 plus one or more clinical features (e.g., mcrpc=0, ecog_ps=0, ar_v7_positive=0, visceral_mets=0).
judgments.rationale: While MSI-high predicting pembrolizumab benefit is established, the claim that benefit is concentrated in MSI-high patients further restricted by clinical features like non-mCRPC, ECOG 0, AR-V7-negative, or no visceral disease is a speculative subgroup refinement not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib benefit may emerge in BRCA2+ patients restricted by mcrpc status or AR-V7 status (e.g., BRCA2+ AND mcrpc=0).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support olaparib in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC broadly; restricting benefit specifically to BRCA2+ AND non-mCRPC or AR-V7-conditional subgroups is a non-standard, speculative cross-pathway refinement.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma benefit may emerge in PSMA-high patients restricted by mcrpc status, visceral_mets status, or ECOG status.
judgments.rationale: Although PSMA-high status is the established selector for Lu-177-PSMA, conditional restriction by mCRPC, visceral metastases, or ECOG status as effect modifiers is a speculative subgroup refinement beyond guideline criteria.

judgments.text: Within the subgroup ar_v7_positive=0 AND mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a very large absolute increase in objective_response (~0.62), while none of the other five treatments produces a meaningful effect within the same subgroup.
judgments.rationale: The specific multi-marker subgroup (AR-V7-, non-mCRPC, BRCA2-, MSI-low) showing a ~0.62 absolute response increase to enzalutamide while five other agents fail is a highly specific cross-treatment, multi-biomarker claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the joint enzalutamide-responsive subgroup, baseline psa_ng_ml does NOT further modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect (the earlier PSA × enz interaction was driven by PSA's correlation with mcrpc).
judgments.rationale: Attributing an apparent PSA × enzalutamide interaction to confounding by mCRPC status rather than a true biomarker effect is a mechanistic/causal disentanglement claim not present in standard references.

judgments.text: An exhaustive search over all 2-of-6 binary clinical/biomarker subgroups finds no subgroup in which abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, or pembrolizumab produces a statistically significant change in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A systematic null result across all 2-of-6 binary subgroups for abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, Lu-177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab contradicts established guideline-endorsed selectors (e.g., BRCA2 for olaparib, PSMA-high for Lu-177, MSI-high for pembrolizumab) and is a strong, non-standard claim.

judgments.text: An exhaustive search over all 3-of-6 binary clinical/biomarker subgroups (with adequate sample sizes) finds no subgroup in which abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, or pembrolizumab produces a statistically significant change in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A null finding from an exhaustive subgroup search is a methodological observation, not a novel biological claim that would appear in a guideline.

judgments.text: Pairs of negative biomarkers (mcrpc, ar_v7, brca2, msi_high, ecog_ps) act roughly additively (no large interaction) on baseline objective_response in untreated/non-enz patients.
judgments.rationale: Additivity of negative prognostic factors on baseline response is a generic statistical observation, not a guideline-level novel insight.

judgments.text: visceral_mets has no association with objective_response either overall or within mcrpc=0 or mcrpc=1 strata.
judgments.rationale: Reporting absence of association for visceral metastases with response, overall and within mCRPC strata, is a null descriptive finding rather than a novel paradigm-shifting claim.

judgments.text: Patients receive multiple concurrent treatments, and number of treatments is positively associated with objective_response (likely confounded by enzalutamide receipt).
judgments.rationale: Noting that more concurrent treatments correlate with response and is confounded by enzalutamide is a confounding/methodology observation, not novel oncology biology.

judgments.text: After fitting a logistic model with enzalutamide × {mcrpc, ar_v7, brca2, msi} interactions plus all other covariates, each interaction is strongly negative (effect-suppressing) and treatment_enzalutamide retains a large positive baseline effect among the 4-negative reference subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide's benefit is concentrated in a quadruple-negative (non-mCRPC, AR-V7−, BRCA2−, MSI−) reference subgroup with strongly negative interactions across all four markers is a speculative cross-pathway conditional effect not in guidelines.

judgments.text: A decision-tree summary of the data-driven enzalutamide treatment effect (T-learner with gradient boosting) selects mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, and brca2_mutation as the dominant splits and assigns mean ITE +0.59 to the leaf {mcrpc=0, ar_v7=0, brca2=0} and ITE near 0 to all other leaves.
judgments.rationale: A tree-based ITE model assigning a large enzalutamide benefit only to the {mcrpc=0, AR-V7=0, BRCA2=0} leaf and near-zero elsewhere is a speculative subgroup-conditional treatment-effect claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Each of the four conditions ar_v7_positive=0, mcrpc=0, brca2_mutation=0, and msi_high=0 is individually necessary: dropping any one of them and admitting the corresponding 'positive' patients shows essentially no enzalutamide benefit in the added stratum.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that each of four negative conditions (AR-V7−, non-mCRPC, BRCA2−, MSI−) is individually necessary for enzalutamide benefit is a novel multi-marker conjunctive claim not reflected in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the enzalutamide-responsive 4-negative core subgroup, adding any of the other five treatments to enzalutamide does not further increase objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no incremental benefit from adding any of five other therapies to enzalutamide within a specific quadruple-negative responsive subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional combination-therapy claim beyond guideline scope.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib has no benefit on objective_response even within BRCA2+ patients (adjusted logit coef = -0.27, p=0.05).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib lacking benefit even within BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts the established PARP inhibitor paradigm in BRCA-mutant disease and would not appear in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab has no benefit on objective_response even within MSI-high patients (adjusted logit coef = -0.006, p=0.99).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab lacking benefit in MSI-high patients contradicts the established tissue-agnostic MSI-high indication for pembrolizumab and is contrary to guideline consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma has no benefit on objective_response within PSMA-high or PSMA-high+mCRPC patients.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA is established for PSMA-positive mCRPC (VISION trial), so claiming no benefit in PSMA-high/mCRPC contradicts guideline consensus rather than offering a novel insight.

judgments.text: treatment_abiraterone has no benefit on objective_response within mCRPC patients.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's efficacy in mCRPC is a cornerstone of guideline-based therapy, so a 'no benefit' claim is simply contrary to established paradigm, not a novel mechanistic/subgroup insight.

judgments.text: treatment_docetaxel has no benefit on objective_response even within mCRPC + visceral_mets patients.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel benefit in mCRPC including visceral disease is standard guideline knowledge; asserting no benefit is contrarian rather than novel.

judgments.text: FINAL SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response by ~+0.63 absolute (from ~0.17 to ~0.80) in the joint subgroup ar_v7_positive=0 AND mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the treatment has no measurable benefit (the effect is suppressed by any of: ar_v7_positive=1, mcrpc=1, brca2_mutation=1, or msi_high=1).
judgments.rationale: A specific four-way conjunctive subgroup (AR-V7−, non-mCRPC, BRCA2 wild-type, MSS) driving a large enzalutamide response with effect suppression by any one of those alterations is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_enzalutamide=0.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's efficacy in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_abiraterone=0.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's response benefit in prostate cancer is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_docetaxel=0.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel is a guideline-recommended chemotherapy with known response activity in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_olaparib=0.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's activity (particularly in HRR-mutated prostate cancer) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_lu177_psma=0.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177 PSMA is an FDA-approved, guideline-incorporated therapy with known response rates in mCRPC.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's activity in MSI-H/dMMR prostate cancer is established in guidelines, though limited overall.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Castration-resistant disease having worse response than hormone-sensitive disease is textbook prostate cancer knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic factor is well-established standard oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Worse performance status predicting poorer treatment response is foundational oncology guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age associating with worse outcomes is a standard, well-known prognostic observation.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score is a well-established adverse prognostic factor in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status and treatment response is a standard biomarker association covered in prostate cancer guidelines (PARP inhibitor context).

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapy is established in mCRPC guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high is typically associated with better response to immunotherapy, so a lower objective response rate would be a non-standard, paradigm-divergent claim.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: A non-directional differential response by PSMA-high status outside of PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy context is not a guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin as a favorable prognostic/response marker is a standard, widely recognized clinical association.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic and response marker is a standard textbook oncology association.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: While CRP is a known prognostic inflammatory marker, its specific link to objective response rate (rather than survival) is not a guideline-level treatment-response biomarker.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse treatment response is a well-established clinical paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA correlating with worse outcomes/response in prostate cancer is a standard guideline association.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a poor prognostic/response marker is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Lower hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Anemia as a negative prognostic factor for treatment response is a standard, well-documented association in oncology.

judgments.text: Routine chemistry labs (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) are not univariately associated with objective_response.
