judgments.rationale: Routine chemistry labs not predicting response is the default null expectation, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit from treatment_enzalutamide is smaller (or absent) in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to enzalutamide is established prostate cancer biology found in guidelines and textbooks.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit from treatment_enzalutamide is smaller (or absent) in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: A negative interaction between BRCA2 mutation and enzalutamide response is not standard guideline knowledge and represents a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit from treatment_enzalutamide is smaller (or absent) in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status modifying enzalutamide response is not established in prostate cancer guidelines and represents a cross-pathway speculation.

judgments.text: Olaparib produces a larger benefit in objective_response in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients (positive interaction; expected from PARP-inhibitor biology).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation predicting olaparib benefit is foundational PARP inhibitor biology codified in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab produces a larger benefit in objective_response in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients (positive interaction; expected from immune-checkpoint biology).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting pembrolizumab response is a tumor-agnostic FDA-approved indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Lu177-PSMA produces a larger benefit in objective_response in psma_high=1 patients than in psma_high=0 patients (positive interaction; expected from radioligand mechanism).
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression predicting Lu177-PSMA response is established radioligand therapy biology reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Abiraterone benefit on objective_response is smaller in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction; expected from AR-V7 resistance biology).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced abiraterone response is well-established prostate cancer resistance biology in guidelines.

judgments.text: In the canonical-indication subgroup brca2_mutation=1 AND mcrpc=1, treatment_olaparib increases the rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's benefit in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC is established (PROfound trial) and appears in NCCN/ESMO prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: In the canonical-indication subgroup msi_high=1 AND mcrpc=1, treatment_pembrolizumab increases the rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in MSI-high/dMMR tumors including mCRPC is a tissue-agnostic FDA-approved indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: In the canonical-indication subgroup psma_high=1 AND mcrpc=1, treatment_lu177_psma increases the rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA (Pluvicto) efficacy in PSMA-high mCRPC is established by VISION trial and is in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: In mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases the rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel efficacy in mCRPC is a long-standing standard-of-care finding (TAX 327) in every prostate cancer guideline.

judgments.text: After adjustment for all features and treatments in a multivariable logistic regression, treatment_enzalutamide remains a strong positive predictor of objective_response while abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, and pembrolizumab show no significant main effect.
judgments.rationale: A multivariable claim that enzalutamide is the only treatment with a significant main effect while all other guideline-standard agents wash out is a non-guideline, dataset-specific pattern.

judgments.text: Among all 25 candidate effect modifiers, the largest interaction with treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is with mcrpc (negative), exceeding even the AR-V7, BRCA2, and MSI-high interactions.
judgments.rationale: Claim that mCRPC is a stronger negative effect modifier of enzalutamide than AR-V7, BRCA2, or MSI-high contradicts the canonical view that AR-V7 is the dominant enzalutamide-resistance marker.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide produces a much larger absolute increase in objective_response in mcrpc=0 patients than in mcrpc=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: While enzalutamide is approved across hormone-sensitive and castration-resistant disease, asserting a much larger absolute response benefit in non-mCRPC vs mCRPC as the dominant modifier is a non-guideline, dataset-specific quantitative claim.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide produces a clinically meaningful increase in objective_response only in patients who simultaneously have mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in all other strata the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conjunctive subgroup (mCRPC-negative AND AR-V7-negative AND BRCA2-wildtype AND MSI-low) defining the only enzalutamide-responsive stratum is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Defining favorable_for_enza := (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0) cleanly captures the entire enzalutamide-responsive subgroup: enzalutamide raises objective_response by ≥0.5 absolute inside this stratum and by essentially zero outside it.
judgments.rationale: Defining a composite 'favorable_for_enza' biomarker that cleanly partitions responders vs non-responders is a novel derived signature beyond guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within each of the 4 cells of (mcrpc × any-resistance-marker), only the (mcrpc=0, no-resistance) cell shows an enzalutamide treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: Restricting enzalutamide benefit to a single (non-mCRPC, no-resistance-marker) cell across a 2x2 stratification is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable enzalutamide subgroup, the absolute objective_response benefit from enzalutamide is preserved across ECOG levels 0, 1, and 2 (≥+0.55 in each) although somewhat attenuated at ECOG=2.
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional claim that enzalutamide's response benefit is preserved across ECOG 0-2 with attenuation at ECOG 2 is a granular interaction not stated in mCRPC guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher log(1+psa_ng_ml) further attenuates the enzalutamide effect on objective_response even after adjusting for mcrpc and the resistance markers.
judgments.rationale: Claim that higher baseline PSA attenuates enzalutamide's response effect after adjusting for mCRPC and resistance markers is a speculative effect-modification not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Adding abiraterone to enzalutamide does not increase the objective_response rate compared with enzalutamide alone (no enza+abi synergy).
judgments.rationale: Combination enzalutamide+abiraterone is not standard; the negative-synergy claim on objective response is a specific cross-pathway finding beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: None of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab show a Bonferroni-significant interaction with any of the 25 candidate features on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A global negative result across five treatments and 25 features under Bonferroni correction is a dataset-specific analytic claim, not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: visceral_mets does not modify the enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Negative effect-modification claim for visceral metastases on enzalutamide response is a specific subgroup non-interaction not articulated in guidelines.

judgments.text: albumin_g_dl does not meaningfully modify the enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a non-modifier of enzalutamide's response effect is a specific biomarker interaction claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: psma_high does not modify the enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high status as a non-modifier of enzalutamide response is a speculative biomarker-treatment interaction not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across all six treatments, no treatment except enzalutamide shows a clinically meaningful subgroup in which it improves objective_response by ≥0.05 absolute and reaches statistical significance.
judgments.rationale: Sweeping claim that only enzalutamide achieves a ≥0.05 significant subgroup benefit across six treatments is a cross-treatment comparative result beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Within any biomarker stratum (BRCA2, AR-V7, MSI, PSMA, mcrpc, visceral_mets), treatment_docetaxel shows no >+0.05 absolute increase in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Negative claim that docetaxel yields no >0.05 response gain in any of six biomarker strata is a specific subgroup non-effect not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable enzalutamide subgroup, declining albumin and rising PSA, CRP, and weight loss each independently lower objective_response, even on enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Worsening albumin, rising PSA/CRP, and weight loss are well-established adverse prognostic factors in mCRPC and would appear in standard prognostic discussions.

judgments.text: Adding any of (visceral_mets, ECOG, age, gleason, hemoglobin, NLR, LDH, alkaline_phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, Na, K, Ca) to the enzalutamide × (mcrpc, ar_v7, brca2, msi) subgroup definition does NOT identify any additional variable whose unfavorable level qualitatively suppresses the enzalutamide effect (i.e., reduces it from large to null) within the otherwise-favorable subgroup.
judgments.rationale: The claim that no clinical/laboratory covariate further modifies the enzalutamide benefit within an already-favorable molecular subgroup is a specific negative interaction finding not stated in any guideline.

judgments.text: FINAL CONSOLIDATED HYPOTHESIS: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response by approximately +0.63 absolute (from 0.17 to 0.80) ONLY in the subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially 0 (≈+0.008). No other treatment in this dataset shows a clinically meaningful efficacy signal in any subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support enzalutamide in mCRPC broadly; the claim that its benefit is confined to non-mCRPC, AR-V7–negative, BRCA2-wildtype, MSS disease (and absent elsewhere) is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup-conditional reversal not in standard oncology consensus.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide producing objective responses in prostate cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's efficacy in prostate cancer is well-established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel response in prostate cancer is a foundational, guideline-level fact.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib activity in HRR-mutated prostate cancer is established standard-of-care.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Lu177-PSMA response in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established (VISION trial, guideline-endorsed).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab response in MSI-H/dMMR or TMB-high prostate cancer is guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without brca2_mutation.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines indicate BRCA2 mutations predict better response to PARP inhibitors, not lower overall response, so an unconditional negative association would be novel/contrary.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without ar_v7_positive.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapy is well-established in prostate cancer literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high status have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without msi_high status.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status conventionally predicts higher, not lower, response (to immunotherapy), so a negative association would be a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high status have a different rate of objective_response than patients without psma_high status.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high status predicting differential response (notably to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy) is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without mcrpc.
judgments.rationale: Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer having lower response rates than non-mCRPC is established disease-state prognostic knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without visceral_mets.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic factor with lower response rates is standard prostate cancer guideline content.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a universally established oncology prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score conferring worse outcomes is foundational prostate cancer pathology textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with a different rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Age as a non-directional modifier of response is a generic demographic association, not a novel mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated PSA correlating with worse response in advanced prostate cancer is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin (better nutritional status) predicting better outcomes is a well-established prognostic association across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is established in prostate cancer risk stratification (e.g., Halabi nomogram).

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss/cachexia predicting worse cancer outcomes is textbook oncology prognostication.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory adverse prognostic marker in advanced prostate cancer is well-established in the literature and risk models.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High NLR as an adverse prognostic/predictive marker is well-established across oncology including prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as an adverse prognostic factor in mCRPC is standard and appears in prognostic nomograms (e.g., Halabi).

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase is a well-known adverse prognostic marker in prostate cancer, reflecting bone metastatic burden.

judgments.text: After adjustment for prognostic features in a multivariable logistic regression, treatment_enzalutamide is independently associated with higher objective_response while the other 5 treatments are not.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's efficacy in prostate cancer is guideline-established; superiority over comparators in a mixed cohort is expected, not novel.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in mcrpc patients than in non-mcrpc patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide works less well in mCRPC than non-mCRPC contradicts its primary indication and is a non-guideline subgroup claim.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in ar_v7_positive patients than in ar_v7_negative patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to enzalutamide is a well-recognized mechanism described in guidelines and reviews.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in brca2_mutation carriers than in non-carriers (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: A negative interaction between BRCA2 mutation and enzalutamide response is not established consensus and would not appear in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in msi_high patients than in msi-low patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Differential enzalutamide efficacy by MSI-high status is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is modified by psma_high status.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high status as a modifier of enzalutamide response is not established in guidelines; PSMA is used for imaging/radioligand selection, not AR-targeted therapy stratification.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is modified by visceral_mets.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases modifying enzalutamide-specific benefit (beyond general prognosis) is a subgroup-conditional claim not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller in patients with higher psa_ng_ml (negative continuous interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy declining with higher baseline PSA reflects established prognostic/burden-of-disease intuition in mCRPC and is consistent with guideline-level expectations.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib has a larger objective_response benefit in brca2_mutation carriers than in non-carriers (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Greater olaparib benefit in BRCA2 carriers is the canonical HRR-mutation indication and is enshrined in prostate cancer treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab has a larger objective_response benefit in msi_high patients than in msi-low patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy enrichment in MSI-high tumors is a tumor-agnostic FDA-approved indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma has a larger objective_response benefit in psma_high patients than in psma-low patients (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high patients is the established VISION-trial paradigm and an explicit eligibility criterion in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma's objective_response effect is smaller (or harmful) in visceral_mets patients than in non-visceral patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential Lu-177-PSMA response by visceral metastasis status as an effect modifier is not a guideline-level claim and goes beyond standard PSMA-expression-based selection.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab's objective_response effect is smaller (or harmful) in visceral_mets patients than in non-visceral patients.
judgments.rationale: A specifically reduced/harmful pembrolizumab effect in visceral-metastasis prostate cancer subgroups is not a recognized guideline biomarker and represents a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: After interaction screening of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab against all 6 binary biomarkers and 19 continuous features, none of these five treatments shows a robust effect-modifying covariate (no Bonferroni-significant interaction).
judgments.rationale: A global null finding that none of five major prostate cancer therapies shows any robust effect-modifying covariate contradicts established BRCA/MSI/PSMA predictive biomarker paradigms and is a non-trivial cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: In the joint subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly larger absolute increase in objective_response than in the complement subgroup where any of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, or msi_high is present.
judgments.rationale: A four-way joint-favorable subgroup (non-mCRPC, AR-V7-negative, BRCA2-wildtype, MSS) defining markedly enhanced enzalutamide benefit is a speculative composite-biomarker claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: In a logistic model with all four treatment_enzalutamide × biomarker interactions, each modifier (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high) independently and significantly reduces the enzalutamide log-odds effect, beyond the other three.
judgments.rationale: Independent additive negative modification of enzalutamide effect by all four biomarkers simultaneously in a joint model is a mechanistic multi-pathway claim beyond guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Relaxing any one of the four favorable-subgroup constraints (allowing mcrpc, or ar_v7_positive, or brca2_mutation, or msi_high) substantially reduces the absolute treatment_enzalutamide benefit relative to the four-way joint-favorable subgroup.
judgments.rationale: The claim that relaxing any single one of four favorable-subgroup constraints substantially erodes enzalutamide benefit is a speculative composite-subgroup interaction not represented in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the four-way favorable subgroup, no remaining clinical, biomarker, or laboratory variable further significantly modifies the (already large) treatment_enzalutamide effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no further effect modifiers exist within a specific four-way favorable subgroup is a dataset-specific subgroup-conditional finding not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Concomitant administration of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, or treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT alter the absolute treatment_enzalutamide benefit within the favorable subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that five specific concomitant agents do not alter enzalutamide's absolute benefit within a defined favorable subgroup is a speculative cross-treatment interaction claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients who are non-mcrpc AND ar_v7_positive lose the enzalutamide effect almost entirely (suppression by AR-V7 is not contingent on mcrpc).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 suppression of enzalutamide response in non-mCRPC (e.g., hormone-sensitive) disease is not standard guideline knowledge, which focuses AR-V7 on mCRPC.

judgments.text: A T-learner random forest CATE for treatment_enzalutamide, trained on all 25 baseline features, will rank the four binary modifiers (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high) as the dominant drivers of treatment-effect heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A claim about which features a T-learner random forest will rank as dominant CATE drivers is a methodological/data-specific prediction not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within the unfavorable subgroup (any of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high present), adjusted treatment_enzalutamide effect on log-odds of objective_response is at most modestly positive (clinically negligible).
judgments.rationale: A composite four-marker unfavorable subgroup yielding only modest enzalutamide benefit is a non-standard joint-conditional claim absent from textbooks.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is similar across ecog_ps strata in the overall cohort (constant absolute risk difference even though baseline response declines with ECOG).
judgments.rationale: Constant absolute treatment effect of enzalutamide across ECOG strata aligns with the standard guideline view that ECOG is prognostic but not strongly predictive of AR-targeted therapy benefit.

judgments.text: Independent prognostic features in the multivariable logistic model (adjusted for treatments and other features) include: ecog_ps↓, mcrpc↓, ar_v7_positive↓, brca2_mutation↓, msi_high↓, weight_loss_pct_6mo↓, crp_mg_l↓, psa_ng_ml↓, albumin_g_dl↑.
judgments.rationale: These prognostic associations (ECOG, mCRPC status, AR-V7, weight loss, CRP, PSA, albumin) are well-established standard prognostic markers in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Within the joint-favorable subgroup, lu177_psma still does not benefit psma_high vs psma_low patients (no PSMA-high-specific signal preserved when other modifiers are removed).
judgments.rationale: Claiming Lu177-PSMA provides no benefit to PSMA-high patients within a joint-favorable subgroup contradicts the standard PSMA-targeted therapy paradigm and is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: The complete best-supported treatment-effect subgroup in this cohort is: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive effect on objective_response (≈+63 percentage points absolute, ≈80% on-treatment vs ≈17% off-treatment) ONLY in patients who simultaneously satisfy mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this joint subgroup the absolute effect is ≤+1 pp and clinically negligible. None of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, or treatment_pembrolizumab shows a meaningful effect on objective_response in any subgroup probed.
judgments.rationale: A precise four-way joint biomarker subgroup defining a ~63 pp enzalutamide benefit with all other listed agents inert across subgroups is a highly specific dataset-derived claim well beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The six treatment indicators (treatment_enzalutamide, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab) differ in their unadjusted association with objective_response, and at least one treatment shows a non-trivial main effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Differential response across standard prostate cancer therapies is expected and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients commonly receive more than one of the six treatments, so unadjusted treatment-response comparisons are confounded by co-treatment.
judgments.rationale: Sequential/combination use of these agents in mCRPC is well known and confounding is a generic methodological observation, not a novel oncology claim.

judgments.text: mcrpc=1 (metastatic castration-resistant disease) is associated with lower objective_response rate than mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: It is established that mCRPC has worse response rates than hormone-sensitive disease.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 is associated with lower objective_response rate than brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines associate BRCA2 mutations with better (not worse) response to PARP inhibitors and platinum, so a lower overall ORR claim runs counter to standard expectation.

judgments.text: ar_v7_positive=1 is associated with lower objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity is a guideline-recognized marker of resistance to AR-targeted therapy and lower response.

judgments.text: msi_high=1 is associated with lower objective_response rate than msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status is a guideline-endorsed predictor of higher response (to pembrolizumab), so claiming lower ORR contradicts established consensus.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is a standard, textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA as a marker of greater disease burden and worse response is well established in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a favorable prognostic factor is a long-established, guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse outcomes is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a negative prognostic/predictive marker is well-established across solid tumors including prostate cancer.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all baseline features and other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains positively associated with objective_response while treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab show no significant adjusted main effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, and Lu-177-PSMA have no adjusted main effect contradicts guideline-level evidence of their efficacy in mCRPC.

judgments.text: visceral_mets and psma_high have no main effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases is a guideline-recognized poor prognostic factor, so claiming no main effect is contrarian to consensus.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by mcrpc (smaller benefit in mcrpc=1).
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide is guideline-endorsed in mCRPC; a smaller benefit specifically in mCRPC vs. earlier disease is a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by ar_v7_positive (smaller benefit in ar_v7=1).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity as a resistance marker to enzalutamide/abiraterone is a textbook association.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by brca2_mutation (smaller benefit in brca2=1).
judgments.rationale: Diminished enzalutamide benefit conditional on BRCA2 mutation is not a standard guideline claim.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by msi_high (smaller benefit in msi_high=1).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status modifying enzalutamide response is a cross-pathway claim not present in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by psa_ng_ml (smaller benefit at higher PSA).
judgments.rationale: While high PSA is broadly prognostic, a specific PSA-by-enzalutamide interaction is not a guideline-level predictive claim.

judgments.text: Among the 5 non-enzalutamide treatments, no single feature × treatment interaction reaches genome-wide significance equivalent (e.g., p<1e-4).
judgments.rationale: A meta-statistical null result about feature×treatment interactions across non-enzalutamide therapies is not a guideline-level statement.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab increases objective_response rate within msi_high=1 patients (classical immunotherapy biomarker hypothesis).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is the canonical FDA tumor-agnostic immunotherapy biomarker indication.

judgments.text: Olaparib increases objective_response rate within brca2_mutation=1 patients (classical PARP-inhibitor synthetic-lethality hypothesis).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA2-mutant prostate/ovarian/breast cancer is established guideline-level synthetic lethality.

judgments.text: Lu177-PSMA increases objective_response rate within psma_high=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Lu177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-high mCRPC is established (VISION trial) and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide effect on objective_response is largely abolished in ar_v7_positive=1 patients (AR-V7 resistance hypothesis).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 splice variant as a predictor of enzalutamide resistance is a well-known, textbook resistance mechanism.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide effect is concentrated in mcrpc=0 patients (hormone-sensitive setting), with little/no effect in mcrpc=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Restricting enzalutamide benefit to non-mCRPC (hormone-sensitive) with little/no effect in mCRPC contradicts guideline-supported mCRPC indications and is a non-standard subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Abiraterone has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response within ar_v7_positive=1 or mcrpc=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming abiraterone has no meaningful benefit in mCRPC patients contradicts established guideline use of abiraterone in mCRPC.

judgments.text: Docetaxel has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response in mcrpc=1 or in visceral_mets=1.
judgments.rationale: Asserting docetaxel lacks benefit in mCRPC or visceral metastatic disease contradicts established guideline-supported docetaxel indications.

judgments.text: Within mcrpc=0, the four binary biomarkers ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, and msi_high=1 each independently abolish enzalutamide benefit; psma_high and visceral_mets do not modify the enzalutamide effect.
judgments.rationale: A specific multi-biomarker conjunction (AR-V7, BRCA2, MSI-high each independently abolishing enzalutamide benefit only within non-mCRPC) is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: The enzalutamide-responsive subgroup is precisely defined by the conjunction (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0).
judgments.rationale: Defining the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup by a precise four-way biomarker conjunction is a speculative composite predictor not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup, ECOG and PSA continue to be prognostic but do not further modify the enzalutamide effect (effect-size remains ≥+60 pp absolute response).
judgments.rationale: Quantitative claim that ECOG/PSA remain prognostic but non-modifying within a narrowly defined responsive subgroup with ≥60pp absolute response is a specific, non-guideline subgroup-conditional assertion.

judgments.text: Among the 5 non-enzalutamide treatments, an S-learner gradient-boosting model on (features, treatment) reveals no subgroup with absolute conditional treatment effect (CATE) ≥ 0.05 spanning ≥1,000 patients.
judgments.rationale: A model-based negative claim that no ≥1,000-patient subgroup shows CATE ≥0.05 across five non-enzalutamide treatments is a speculative cross-treatment heterogeneity finding not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab increases objective_response rate in the subgroup defined by (albumin_g_dl ≥ median AND crp_mg_l ≤ median) — i.e., patients without inflammation/cachexia.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy conditioned on a composite nutritional/inflammatory phenotype (high albumin AND low CRP) is not part of standard guideline biomarker selection, which relies on PD-L1, MSI/dMMR, or TMB.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, abiraterone increases objective_response rate (modest magnitude).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not endorse abiraterone response prediction by BRCA2 status; this subgroup-conditional hormonal benefit claim is not part of standard prostate cancer paradigm.

judgments.text: Within visceral_mets=1 patients, lu177_psma decreases objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA is generally considered effective in visceral metastatic mCRPC per VISION-era guidance; a decreased response specifically in visceral disease contradicts the established paradigm and is not guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, olaparib decreases objective_response rate (opposite of expected biology).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant tumors are expected to respond to olaparib (PROfound); a decreased response in this subgroup directly opposes established PARP inhibitor biology and is non-paradigm.

judgments.text: Across continuous features, the pembrolizumab effect is best modified jointly by albumin and CRP rather than by either alone or by other continuous markers.
judgments.rationale: Identifying albumin+CRP jointly (over PD-L1, TMB, MSI, or single markers) as the dominant continuous effect modifier of pembrolizumab is a mechanistic claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1, pembrolizumab still has no benefit even when restricting to inflam_good=1.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high tumors are guideline-endorsed responders to pembrolizumab; claiming no benefit even within inflammation-favorable MSI-high patients contradicts established consensus.

judgments.text: Within the enzalutamide-non-responsive subgroup (mcrpc=1 OR ar_v7=1 OR brca2=1 OR msi=1), pembrolizumab continues to provide a benefit when inflam_good=1.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in an enzalutamide-resistant prostate-cancer subgroup conditioned on a favorable inflammatory state is not part of any current guideline, which restricts pembrolizumab in prostate cancer largely to MSI-H/dMMR/TMB-high.

judgments.text: Both predicates of the pembrolizumab-responsive subgroup are necessary: alb_high alone (with crp high) does NOT yield positive pembro effect; crp_low alone (with alb low) does NOT yield positive pembro effect.
judgments.rationale: The conjunctive necessity of both high albumin AND low CRP for pembrolizumab benefit is a specific interaction-level claim absent from textbook/guideline biomarker frameworks.

judgments.text: Within the pembrolizumab-responsive subgroup, PSMA, ECOG, AR-V7, mCRPC, BRCA2, MSI, and visceral mets do not appreciably modify the pembrolizumab effect (it is reasonably uniform across substrata).
judgments.rationale: Claiming uniformity of pembrolizumab effect across MSI, BRCA2, AR-V7, PSMA, ECOG, mCRPC, and visceral mets within a defined responsive subgroup contradicts the guideline view that MSI-H specifically drives response.

judgments.text: Docetaxel has no significant adjusted treatment-by-feature interaction with any of: visceral_mets, mcrpc, ecog_ps, psa_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score.
judgments.rationale: Asserting absence of effect modification of docetaxel by any of these standard prognostic clinicopathologic features (e.g., visceral mets, ECOG, LDH) is a non-trivial empirical claim not stated as such in guidelines.

judgments.text: Lu177-PSMA × visceral_mets interaction is statistically significant in the negative direction in adjusted logistic regression (lu177 effect is positive in visceral_mets=0 and negative in visceral_mets=1).
judgments.rationale: A negative Lu177-PSMA × visceral metastases interaction (harm in visceral disease) contradicts current guideline use of Lu177-PSMA in mCRPC including visceral disease and is not standard.

judgments.text: Abiraterone × brca2_mutation interaction is positive (modest) in adjusted logistic regression — abiraterone effect is more positive in BRCA2 mutated patients than in BRCA2 wild type.
judgments.rationale: Enhanced abiraterone benefit in BRCA2-mutated prostate cancer is consistent with known HRR-mutation sensitivity to AR-targeted therapy and PARP combinations and would appear in current guidelines/discussion.

judgments.text: Adjusted treatment effects of abiraterone and docetaxel are both essentially zero with no clinically meaningful subgroup of >1000 patients showing a 5+ pp benefit.
judgments.rationale: Claiming abiraterone and docetaxel both have essentially zero adjusted effect with no >1000-patient subgroup showing 5pp benefit contradicts established survival benefits in mCRPC/mHSPC and would not appear in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: enzalutamide is responsive only in the joint subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0); each of the four predicates is necessary, and the four together are sufficient for a +60+ pp absolute response benefit.
judgments.rationale: A highly specific four-way conjunctive subgroup (non-mCRPC AND AR-V7 negative AND BRCA2 wild-type AND MSS) being necessary and sufficient for a +60pp enzalutamide benefit is a speculative cross-pathway conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: pembrolizumab is responsive only in the joint subgroup (albumin_g_dl ≥ population median 3.8 AND crp_mg_l ≤ population median 3.34); both predicates are necessary; the effect is +6 pp absolute with adjusted t × subgroup interaction p~1e-4.
judgments.rationale: Restricting pembrolizumab response to a joint albumin-high/CRP-low inflammatory subgroup in prostate cancer is a speculative biomarker conjunction not in current guidelines (which use MSI-H/TMB/dMMR).

judgments.text: FINAL: olaparib's adjusted effect is approximately zero in the overall cohort and weakly NEGATIVE in BRCA2-mutated patients (interaction p=0.054); it has no responsive subgroup with positive effect.
judgments.rationale: A weakly negative olaparib effect in BRCA2-mutated prostate cancer directly contradicts the established PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC that is in every current guideline.

judgments.text: FINAL: lu177-PSMA's adjusted effect is approximately zero in the overall cohort and weakly NEGATIVE in visceral-mets+ patients (interaction p=0.01); it has no responsive subgroup with clinically meaningful positive effect.
judgments.rationale: Lu177-PSMA being net-negative in visceral-mets+ patients with no responsive subgroup contradicts guideline-endorsed VISION-based use of Lu177-PSMA in mCRPC.

judgments.text: FINAL: abiraterone has approximately zero overall effect; a small positive effect in BRCA2+ (interaction p=0.045) is the only signal — too small to define a clinically meaningful responsive subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone showing only a small positive signal in BRCA2+ patients aligns with the known HRR-mutation interaction with AR-targeted therapy already discussed in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: docetaxel has approximately zero overall effect and no responsive subgroup detectable from the available features.
judgments.rationale: Asserting docetaxel has approximately zero overall effect and no responsive subgroup contradicts its guideline-endorsed survival benefit in mHSPC and mCRPC.

judgments.text: All patients in this cohort have sex_female=0 (the cohort is exclusively male, as expected for prostate cancer).
judgments.rationale: An all-male prostate cancer cohort is a tautological demographic observation, not a novel biological or treatment claim.

judgments.text: After accounting for the four binary modifiers of enzalutamide (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high), the residual prognostic value of psa_ng_ml within the responsive subgroup is small (across PSA quartiles, the on-vs-off difference varies by <5 pp) — i.e., PSA is prognostic but not predictive for enzalutamide within this subgroup.
judgments.rationale: The claim that PSA is prognostic but not predictive for enzalutamide response specifically within the modifier-defined responsive subgroup is a subgroup-conditional distinction not established in mCRPC guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup, none of the other 5 treatments adds incremental response benefit (each shows on-vs-off difference within ±2 pp on top of enzalutamide use).
judgments.rationale: The assertion that no other systemic therapy adds incremental response on top of enzalutamide within this responsive subgroup is a cross-pathway non-additivity claim not stated in prostate cancer treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's effect on response in prostate cancer is well-established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel response in prostate cancer is a long-established standard treatment finding.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib response, particularly in HRR-mutant prostate cancer, is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA response in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established and FDA-approved guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in prostate cancer (especially MSI-H) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant) have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Lower response rates in castration-resistant versus hormone-sensitive disease is established prostate cancer paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: A lower overall response rate in BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts the established paradigm that BRCA2 mutations predict better response to PARP inhibitors and platinum.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapy is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: Lower response in MSI-high patients contradicts the established paradigm that MSI-high tumors respond better to immune checkpoint inhibitors.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases are a well-established adverse prognostic factor associated with lower response rates in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression predicting response to PSMA-targeted therapy (e.g., Lu-177-PSMA) is established in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic factor across all oncology.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA correlating with worse response is a well-known prognostic marker in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score predicting worse outcomes is a foundational established prognostic factor in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor nutritional/inflammatory status predicting worse response is well-established across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a poor prognostic factor is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory marker predicting worse cancer outcomes is well-established.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is established across multiple cancers including prostate.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio predicting worse outcomes is a well-established prognostic biomarker in oncology literature and increasingly in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with a different probability of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Age as a general prognostic/response factor is a routine clinical covariate, not a novel finding.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib increases objective_response specifically in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (predictive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated cancers is a guideline-established predictive biomarker relationship.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response specifically in patients with msi_high=1 (predictive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is a tumor-agnostic FDA-approved guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response specifically in patients with psma_high=1 (predictive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-high prostate cancer is the established VISION-trial guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response more strongly in patients with ar_v7_positive=0 than in ar_v7_positive=1 (negative predictive interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity as a negative predictor of enzalutamide response is a well-known established resistance biomarker.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response more strongly in patients with mcrpc=0 (hormone-sensitive) than in mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant).
judgments.rationale: Direct comparative claim that enzalutamide works better in hormone-sensitive than castration-resistant disease as a predictive interaction is not a standard guideline framing.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response only in the joint subgroup mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0; in any other (mcrpc, ar_v7) combination there is no clinically meaningful effect.
judgments.rationale: A joint two-biomarker subgroup restriction (mcrpc=0 AND AR-V7 negative) defining the only responders is a speculative cross-conditional claim beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: Among BRCA2-mutated patients, no available treatment increases objective_response above baseline.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no available treatment benefits BRCA2-mutated patients contradicts established PARP inhibitor guidelines and is a non-standard speculative claim.

judgments.text: Across the full feature set, the largest treatment-effect modifiers of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response are mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, and psa_ng_ml (each suppressing benefit).
judgments.rationale: Ranking a specific multi-feature modifier set (including MSI-high and PSA) as suppressors of enzalutamide benefit is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: For treatments other than enzalutamide, no feature × treatment logistic interaction reaches strong statistical significance after the screen.
judgments.rationale: A global negative claim that no other treatment shows significant feature interactions is a dataset-specific meta-finding not appearing in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, brca2_mutation=1 abolishes the treatment_enzalutamide benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation as a negative modifier abolishing enzalutamide benefit in non-metastatic, AR-V7-negative prostate cancer is not an established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, msi_high=1 abolishes the treatment_enzalutamide benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status abolishing enzalutamide benefit in this specific subgroup is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, psma_high does NOT modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression is not an established predictive biomarker for enzalutamide response, so a null modifier claim aligns with current paradigm.

judgments.text: Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, visceral_mets does NOT modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases are a prognostic factor but not an established effect modifier of enzalutamide benefit, consistent with guideline silence.

judgments.text: Within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0) base subgroup, continuous covariates (psa_ng_ml, ecog_ps, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l) do NOT modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on the logit scale.
judgments.rationale: Standard prognostic continuous covariates (PSA, ECOG, albumin, LDH, CRP) are not established predictive modifiers of enzalutamide effect, consistent with current paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response by a large absolute margin only in the joint subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the effect is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: A narrowly defined four-way joint subgroup as the exclusive locus of enzalutamide benefit is a speculative interaction claim well beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease), no biomarker subgroup shows a clinically meaningful treatment_enzalutamide benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no biomarker subgroup within mCRPC benefits from enzalutamide contradicts established guideline use of enzalutamide in mCRPC.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, the only treatment with a significant main effect on objective_response is treatment_enzalutamide; treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab have OR≈1.
judgments.rationale: Asserting null main effects for abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib (in BRCA), Lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab (in MSI-high) contradicts established prostate cancer treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: For each non-enzalutamide treatment, no single binary biomarker stratum shows a treatment effect on objective_response of magnitude >0.05 absolute and p<0.01.
judgments.rationale: A blanket negative claim across all non-enzalutamide therapies and biomarker strata contradicts guideline-level predictive associations like BRCA/olaparib and MSI-high/pembrolizumab.

judgments.text: For treatments other than enzalutamide, no 2-binary-feature subgroup shows a treatment effect on objective_response with |diff|>0.02 and p<0.01 (after the screen).
judgments.rationale: Claiming no 2-feature subgroup shows meaningful effects for other therapies contradicts established predictive biomarker subgroups in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib decreases objective_response in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (an adjusted predictive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib is established to increase (not decrease) response in BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer, so a predictive interaction in the opposite direction contradicts guideline consensus and is non-standard.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma decreases objective_response in patients with visceral_mets=1.
judgments.rationale: Reduced efficacy of Lu-177-PSMA in patients with visceral metastases is a recognized clinical observation reflected in VISION/TheraP subgroup analyses and treatment guidance.

judgments.text: Among the 16 cells defined by all combinations of (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high), only the (0,0,0,0) cell shows a treatment_enzalutamide vs no-enzalutamide objective_response difference greater than +0.10 absolute.
judgments.rationale: A claim that enzalutamide benefit is confined to a specific 4-way biomarker-negative cell (non-mCRPC, AR-V7-negative, BRCA2-wildtype, MSS) is a highly specific cross-pathway subgroup claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide × responder_subgroup interaction (where responder_subgroup = mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0) is highly significant after adjusting for age, ecog_ps, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, psma_high, albumin, ldh, weight_loss, crp, and nlr.
judgments.rationale: A covariate-adjusted interaction between enzalutamide and a composite four-biomarker 'responder' subgroup is a speculative multi-feature predictive claim beyond standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Treatment assignment for treatment_enzalutamide is balanced with respect to baseline covariates (age, mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, psma_high, ecog_ps, psa_ng_ml, albumin), implying that the observed enzalutamide subgroup effect is unlikely to be driven by confounding by indication.
judgments.rationale: A claim of covariate balance for enzalutamide across this specific multi-biomarker covariate set in this dataset, used to argue against confounding by indication, is a dataset-specific causal-inference assertion not part of guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: FINAL: The treatment_enzalutamide -> objective_response causal effect is concentrated almost entirely in the subgroup mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in this subgroup it raises the response rate from ~0.17 to ~0.80 (absolute diff ~+0.63). No other treatment in the dataset (treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab) shows a positive treatment-effect subgroup of comparable size or magnitude.
judgments.rationale: Concentrating enzalutamide benefit (response ~0.17→~0.80) in a quadruple-negative biomarker subgroup and asserting no comparable subgroup effect for any other listed therapy is a speculative, cross-pathway predictive claim well beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The dataset contains a binary outcome `objective_response` whose marginal rate is non-trivial (between 10% and 50%) and whose distribution differs across the six treatment indicators.
judgments.rationale: Describing the marginal distribution of an outcome variable is a basic dataset property, not a clinical claim.

judgments.text: All patients in this prostate-cancer cohort are male, so `sex_female` is constant and uninformative for any modeling.
judgments.rationale: Prostate cancer occurring essentially exclusively in males is textbook epidemiology.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_enzalutamide` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's efficacy in prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving `treatment_abiraterone`, `treatment_docetaxel`, `treatment_olaparib`, `treatment_lu177_psma`, or `treatment_pembrolizumab` do not have meaningfully different unadjusted response rates than patients not receiving each respective drug.
judgments.rationale: A null/no-difference claim across standard prostate cancer agents is not a novel mechanistic insight.

judgments.text: Patients with `mcrpc` = 1 (castration-resistant disease) have a substantially lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `mcrpc` = 0 (hormone-sensitive disease).
judgments.rationale: Castration-resistant disease having worse response than hormone-sensitive disease is standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher `ecog_ps` is associated with lower `objective_response`, monotonically across ECOG 0/1/2.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting worse response is textbook prognostic knowledge across oncology.

judgments.text: `visceral_mets` and `gleason_score` have negligible main effects on `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines treat visceral metastases and high Gleason as adverse prognostic factors, so claiming negligible main effects contradicts consensus and is non-trivial.

judgments.text: Patients with `brca2_mutation` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with brca2_mutation = 0.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines associate BRCA2 mutations with PARP inhibitor sensitivity and generally favorable targeted-therapy response, so a lower overall response rate is a non-guideline claim.

judgments.text: Patients with `ar_v7_positive` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than ar_v7_positive = 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapy is established prostate cancer biomarker knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with `msi_high` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than msi_high = 0 patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high tumors are guideline-recognized as predictors of better response to immunotherapy, so a lower overall response rate contradicts standard expectation.

judgments.text: `psma_high` has no main effect on `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression is not an established predictor of response to standard prostate cancer therapies in guidelines, but a null finding for PSMA on response is a routine negative result rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: Higher baseline `psa_ng_ml` is associated with lower probability of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA correlating with worse response is a well-established prognostic association in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher baseline `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher probability of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor outcomes is standard prognostic knowledge across oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` and higher `crp_mg_l` are associated with lower probability of `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia (weight loss) and elevated CRP/inflammation predicting worse response is established prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Standard chemistry/CBC labs (LDH, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, Na, K, Ca, NLR) and `age_years` show no main effect on `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: A null finding for routine labs is not a novel mechanistic or subgroup claim and contradicts well-known LDH/NLR prognostic effects only in an unsurprising direction for response endpoints.

judgments.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, `treatment_enzalutamide` retains a strong positive odds ratio for `objective_response`, while `treatment_abiraterone`, `treatment_docetaxel`, `treatment_olaparib`, `treatment_lu177_psma`, and `treatment_pembrolizumab` are not associated with response.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not assert enzalutamide is uniquely effective while abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, Lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab all lack response benefit in their indicated subgroups — this contradicts established efficacy of multiple agents.

judgments.text: The benefit of `treatment_enzalutamide` on `objective_response` is markedly larger in `mcrpc` = 0 (hormone-sensitive) patients than in `mcrpc` = 1 (castration-resistant) patients.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide having larger benefit in hormone-sensitive than castration-resistant prostate cancer aligns with established efficacy gradients reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of `treatment_enzalutamide` on `objective_response` is abolished in patients with `ar_v7_positive` = 1 (treatment x AR-V7 negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to enzalutamide is a well-known biomarker association discussed in current prostate cancer literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of `treatment_enzalutamide` on `objective_response` is abolished in patients with `brca2_mutation` = 1 (treatment x BRCA2 wild-type interaction).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not establish that BRCA2 mutation abolishes enzalutamide benefit; this is a speculative cross-pathway interaction beyond standard paradigm.

judgments.text: The benefit of `treatment_enzalutamide` on `objective_response` is abolished in patients with `msi_high` = 1 (treatment x MSI-low interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status abolishing enzalutamide benefit is not an established interaction in prostate cancer guidelines and represents a novel cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide does NOT interact with `psma_high` or `visceral_mets` for objective response (i.e., its benefit is similar in PSMA-high vs not, and in visceral-mets vs not, holding other things equal).
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression and visceral metastases are well-known prognostic/predictive considerations for AR-targeted therapy in prostate cancer, and the absence of interaction is a standard null finding rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: Among patients with `mcrpc` = 0, `brca2_mutation` = 0, `ar_v7_positive` = 0, and `msi_high` = 0, `treatment_enzalutamide` produces a very large absolute increase in `objective_response` rate (≥ +0.50).
judgments.rationale: A specific four-marker conjunction (non-mCRPC, BRCA2-wt, AR-V7-negative, MSS) defining a >50% absolute response gain to enzalutamide is a speculative subgroup rule that would not appear in any guideline.

judgments.text: Each of the four 'unfavorable' markers individually nullifies enzalutamide benefit: when only `mcrpc` = 1 (other three favorable) the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: Claiming mCRPC status alone fully nullifies enzalutamide benefit contradicts guideline use of enzalutamide in mCRPC and is a non-standard subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: When only `brca2_mutation` = 1 (other three favorable), the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation single-handedly abolishing enzalutamide benefit is not an established guideline concept and represents a novel cross-pathway interaction claim.

judgments.text: When only `ar_v7_positive` = 1 (other three favorable), the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity is widely recognized in guidelines and literature as a predictor of resistance to AR-targeted therapy including enzalutamide.

judgments.text: When only `msi_high` = 1 (other three favorable), the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status nullifying enzalutamide efficacy is not a recognized guideline association and is a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: A simple greedy decision tree trained to identify patients with high enzalutamide CATE (estimated by GBM meta-learner) recovers exactly the rule mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: The exact recovery of a four-marker conjunctive rule by a greedy tree on meta-learner CATEs is a specific mechanistic/algorithmic claim well beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: A logistic model with all four enz×marker interactions (mcrpc, brca2, ar_v7, msi) plus main effects shows a very large positive enzalutamide main coefficient (in the all-favorable cell) and four large negative interactions, confirming the four-component subgroup formula.
judgments.rationale: A four-way interaction model with large negative coefficients for each marker formalizing a conjunctive subgroup is a novel quantitative structural claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: `treatment_olaparib` does NOT improve `objective_response` in patients with `brca2_mutation` = 1 (no interaction with BRCA2).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated prostate cancer (PROfound), so claiming no interaction with BRCA2 contradicts established paradigm and is a novel/speculative negative claim.

judgments.text: `treatment_pembrolizumab` does NOT improve `objective_response` in patients with `msi_high` = 1.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is guideline-endorsed for MSI-high tumors (tissue-agnostic), so asserting no benefit in MSI-high patients is a non-standard claim that goes against consensus.

judgments.text: `treatment_lu177_psma` does NOT improve `objective_response` in patients with `psma_high` = 1.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177 PSMA is established for PSMA-positive mCRPC (VISION trial); a null effect in PSMA-high patients contradicts but doesn't extend guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: `treatment_abiraterone` does not improve `objective_response` in any of these candidate subgroups: `mcrpc` = 0, `mcrpc` = 1, `ar_v7_positive` = 0, `brca2_mutation` = 0, or in the joint hormone-sensitive favorable subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone is a guideline-recommended standard in both mHSPC and mCRPC; claiming no benefit across all these subgroups contradicts established paradigm in a non-trivial subgroup-conditional way.

judgments.text: `treatment_docetaxel` does not improve `objective_response` in any of the same candidate subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel is guideline-standard in mHSPC and mCRPC; a blanket null across these subgroups is a non-guideline, subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within the enzalutamide-favorable subgroup (`mcrpc`=0 & brca2=0 & ar_v7=0 & msi_high=0), no other treatment (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) further changes `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: A joint four-marker subgroup (mcrpc=0 & BRCA2- & AR-V7- & MSI-low) defining enzalutamide exclusivity with no incremental benefit from any other agent is not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within the enzalutamide-favorable subgroup, adding any other treatment to enzalutamide does not synergistically increase `objective_response` beyond the enzalutamide-only rate (~0.80).
judgments.rationale: Lack of synergy with enzalutamide within a specific four-marker favorable subgroup is a cross-treatment, subgroup-conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the enzalutamide effect on `objective_response` is preserved across ECOG strata (i.e., strong benefit even at ECOG 2).
judgments.rationale: Preservation of enzalutamide benefit at ECOG 2 within a specific molecular subgroup is a subgroup-conditional performance-status interaction not described in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within mcrpc=1 (CRPC) patients, no available treatment (enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) improves `objective_response`.
judgments.rationale: Claiming all six standard CRPC therapies fail to improve response in mCRPC contradicts guideline-established activity and is a strong cross-treatment claim.

judgments.text: After adjusting for treatment and other covariates, the strongest baseline prognostic factors for `objective_response` (in order of effect strength) are mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, ecog_ps, brca2_mutation, weight_loss_pct_6mo, msi_high, albumin_g_dl, psa_ng_ml, and crp_mg_l - all in the predicted directions.
judgments.rationale: These prognostic factors (CRPC state, AR-V7, ECOG, BRCA2, weight loss, albumin, PSA, CRP) and their directions are well-established prognostic variables in prostate cancer textbooks.

judgments.text: Two borderline subgroup signals - (a) `treatment_lu177_psma` lower response within visceral_mets=1, and (b) `treatment_pembrolizumab` lower response within visceral_mets=1 - do not survive multiple-testing scrutiny relative to the dozens of subgroup tests performed.
judgments.rationale: A meta-analytic claim that specific borderline visceral-mets subgroup signals for Lu-177 PSMA and pembrolizumab are multiple-testing artifacts is a methodological/subgroup statement not in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL refined heterogeneity statement for `objective_response`: `treatment_enzalutamide` produces a large positive effect on `objective_response` (absolute increase ≈ +0.63) ONLY in patients defined by ALL of the following: `mcrpc` = 0 AND `brca2_mutation` = 0 AND `ar_v7_positive` = 0 AND `msi_high` = 0. Outside this joint subgroup the effect is essentially zero.
judgments.rationale: A precise four-marker joint subgroup conferring an absolute +0.63 enzalutamide response with null effect elsewhere is a highly specific predictive-biomarker claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL heterogeneity statement for non-enzalutamide treatments on `objective_response`: none of `treatment_abiraterone`, `treatment_docetaxel`, `treatment_olaparib`, `treatment_lu177_psma`, or `treatment_pembrolizumab` has any reproducible subgroup of meaningful benefit on `objective_response` in this cohort - including in their canonical biomarker-defined subgroups (olaparib in BRCA2-mutated, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high) and including in the enzalutamide-favorable subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A blanket claim that olaparib in BRCA2-mutant, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, and Lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high subgroups all fail to produce meaningful response benefit directly contradicts guideline-endorsed biomarker-driven indications, making this a speculative cross-treatment negative claim that would not appear in any current oncology textbook.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide (univariable, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's effect on response in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone (univariable, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's association with response in prostate cancer is guideline-level established.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel (univariable, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel response in prostate cancer is well-established standard chemotherapy knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (univariable, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib response in HRR-mutated prostate cancer is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma (univariable, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA response in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established per VISION trial and guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (univariable, unadjusted).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab response in MSI-H/dMMR or TMB-high prostate cancer is guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: After adjusting for all features and the other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains positively associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A fully covariate- and co-treatment-adjusted independent effect of enzalutamide on response is not a standard guideline claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_abiraterone is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Adjusted independent effect of abiraterone controlling for other treatments and features goes beyond textbook consensus.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_docetaxel is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Adjusted independent effect of docetaxel after controlling for co-treatments and covariates is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_olaparib is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Adjusted independent olaparib effect across all patients (not just HRR-mutated) controlling for covariates exceeds guideline scope.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established (VISION trial) and appears in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is not a standard prostate cancer therapy outside MSI-H/dMMR/TMB-high subgroups, so a general association with response would go beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Metastatic castration-resistant disease having lower response than non-mCRPC is standard prognostic knowledge in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as an adverse prognostic/predictive factor for response in prostate cancer is well-established and guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than brca2-wildtype patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer is generally associated with better response to PARP inhibitors and platinum, so a lower overall response across treatments would contradict guideline expectation.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapy is established and appears in prostate cancer literature/guidelines.
