judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than msi_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting differential (improved) response to immunotherapy is established tumor-agnostic guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than psma_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high expression predicting differential response (to PSMA-targeted therapy) is established in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA as an adverse prognostic marker for treatment response in prostate cancer is well-established.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response is universally established across oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss predicting worse response and survival is standard guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a poor prognostic inflammatory marker is widely recognized in oncology practice.

judgments.text: age_years is associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic/predictive factor is a standard covariate considered in essentially all oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a marker of worse response/prognosis is textbook oncology knowledge across many tumors.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is smaller in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to enzalutamide is established in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is smaller in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 status modifying enzalutamide response is not standard guideline content; PARP inhibitors, not AR-targeted therapy, are the BRCA2-linked recommendation.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is smaller in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a negative modifier of enzalutamide efficacy is not part of established prostate cancer treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's enhanced efficacy in BRCA2-mutant tumors is a cornerstone guideline-level association.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab has a larger positive effect on objective_response in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's superior activity in MSI-high tumors is a tumor-agnostic FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed indication.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma has a larger positive effect on objective_response in psma_high=1 patients than in psma_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high status predicting better response to Lu-177 PSMA therapy is established and reflected in current prostate cancer guidelines (e.g., VISION trial).

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy in AR-V7-negative prostate cancer is a well-known established association in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity as a marker of resistance to enzalutamide is established oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Within mcrpc=0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy in non-metastatic/hormone-sensitive prostate cancer is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide does not meaningfully raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide does not meaningfully raise response in mCRPC contradicts established guideline use of enzalutamide in mCRPC.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: A BRCA2-specific lack of enzalutamide benefit is not standard guideline consensus and represents a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: An MSI-high-specific lack of enzalutamide benefit is not part of standard prostate cancer guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib raises objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA2-mutant prostate/breast/ovarian cancer is established and guideline-endorsed (PROfound, etc.).

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=0 patients, treatment_olaparib does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Lack of olaparib benefit in BRCA wild-type tumors is established standard knowledge.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab raises objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is established with a tissue-agnostic FDA approval and guideline endorsement.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=0 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: It is well-established that pembrolizumab efficacy is largely confined to MSI-high tumors and that MSS/MSI-low patients generally do not benefit, which is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within psma_high=1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma raises objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA is FDA-approved and guideline-recommended specifically for PSMA-positive mCRPC, so a positive response in PSMA-high patients is established paradigm.

judgments.text: Within psma_high=0 patients, treatment_lu177_psma does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Lack of benefit from Lu-177-PSMA in PSMA-negative disease is the established basis for requiring PSMA PET positivity for treatment selection.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml attenuates the positive effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: A continuous PSA-by-enzalutamide interaction attenuating response is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not codified in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps modifies the effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status as a specific effect modifier of enzalutamide response (beyond general prognostic effects) is not a guideline-established interaction.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl modifies the effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a specific modifier of enzalutamide treatment effect on objective response is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients with psa_ng_ml below the cohort median (~15.86), treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate by a larger margin than in patients with high PSA.
judgments.rationale: A median-split PSA threshold predicting differential enzalutamide benefit is a novel subgroup-conditional hypothesis not in standard practice.

judgments.text: In patients with psa_ng_ml above the cohort median, treatment_enzalutamide still raises objective_response rate but by a smaller margin.
judgments.rationale: Quantifying attenuated but preserved enzalutamide benefit above the cohort median PSA is a speculative subgroup claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than control.
judgments.rationale: A joint non-mCRPC and BRCA2-wildtype subgroup as a marker of enhanced enzalutamide response is a cross-pathway conditional hypothesis not in guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than control.
judgments.rationale: While AR-V7 negativity predicting AR-targeted therapy response is somewhat known, the joint non-mCRPC and AR-V7-negative conditioning is a speculative subgroup combination beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than control.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy in non-mCRPC (nmCRPC, mHSPC) settings is established and in guidelines; MSI-H status is not a standard modifier.

judgments.text: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a very large absolute increase in objective_response (around 60 percentage points) versus control.
judgments.rationale: A specific multi-biomarker conditional subgroup (non-mCRPC + AR-V7 negative + BRCA2 wild-type) with a precise ~60pp effect size is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a very large absolute increase in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Joint conditioning on mcrpc, AR-V7, and MSI status as a combined responder profile for enzalutamide is not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0 (the joint biomarker-favorable subgroup), treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate from approximately 17% to approximately 80%.
judgments.rationale: A 4-way biomarker-favorable subgroup with a specific 17%→80% objective response shift is a speculative quantitative subgroup claim beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: In patients who fall outside this 4-way favorable subgroup (i.e., have mcrpc=1 OR ar_v7_positive=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 OR msi_high=1), treatment_enzalutamide produces no clinically meaningful improvement in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the complement of a 4-way joint biomarker subgroup uniformly lacks enzalutamide benefit is a subgroup-conditional null not reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Restricting attention to mcrpc=1 patients (relaxing only the mcrpc favorable criterion) is sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: Contradicts established enzalutamide efficacy in mCRPC, which is a primary FDA-approved indication.

judgments.text: Restricting attention to ar_v7_positive=1 patients is sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity is a well-recognized marker of enzalutamide resistance referenced in oncology literature and guideline discussions.

judgments.text: Restricting attention to brca2_mutation=1 patients is sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation is not a standard predictor of enzalutamide resistance in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Restricting attention to msi_high=1 patients is sufficient to abolish the enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status is not an established biomarker for enzalutamide response/resistance in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 AND ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_olaparib does not raise objective_response (and may slightly lower it).
judgments.rationale: Contradicts established olaparib efficacy in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC (PROfound trial, FDA approval); claiming no benefit goes against guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 AND mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_olaparib does not raise objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Contradicts the PROFOUND-trial paradigm that olaparib improves outcomes in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC, so a null finding here would be unexpected relative to guidelines.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1 AND mcrpc=0 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not produce a clinically meaningful increase in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab has a tissue-agnostic indication for MSI-high tumors, so a subgroup-conditional null finding in MSI-high non-mCRPC patients departs from guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1 AND ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status is the established predictor of pembrolizumab response regardless of AR-V7, so claiming no benefit in this subgroup is a non-guideline subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Within psma_high=1 AND mcrpc=0 patients, treatment_lu177_psma does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA's approval is restricted to mCRPC, so a subgroup-specific claim about non-mCRPC PSMA-high patients is not addressed in guidelines and qualifies as speculative.

judgments.text: Within psma_high=1 AND visceral_mets=0 patients, treatment_lu177_psma does not raise objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: VISION established Lu-PSMA benefit in PSMA-positive mCRPC broadly; a visceral-mets-negative subgroup-conditional null is a speculative carve-out not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 AND ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_abiraterone raises objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: A BRCA2 × AR-V7-negative subgroup-conditional benefit of abiraterone is a cross-pathway claim not articulated in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients overall, treatment_abiraterone has a small positive effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not endorse BRCA2 mutation as a predictive biomarker for abiraterone response, so any directional effect claim here is beyond established paradigm.

judgments.text: Among the top two-feature subgroup combinations of treatment_docetaxel, no subgroup shows a clinically meaningful (>10pp) and statistically significant increase in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Current guidelines recognize no validated predictive biomarker for docetaxel response, so a null finding across subgroups aligns with established paradigm.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model containing all 6 treatments + the 5 plausible matched biomarker-treatment interactions, the enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive interaction is significantly negative.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity as a predictor of enzalutamide resistance (Antonarakis et al.) is well-established and would appear in modern prostate cancer treatment discussions.

judgments.text: In the same model, the olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction is not significantly positive (and may be slightly negative).
judgments.rationale: Contradicts the well-established BRCA2-olaparib synthetic lethality predictive interaction, making a null/negative interaction a guideline-discordant claim.

judgments.text: In the same model, the pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction is not significant.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting pembrolizumab response is well-established guideline knowledge, so a null interaction in this dataset is a standard negative finding rather than a novel claim.

judgments.text: In the same model, the lu177_psma x psma_high interaction is not significant.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high expression predicting Lu-177-PSMA response is the established basis for the therapy per current guidelines, so a non-significant interaction is a standard expected check, not novel.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logit including treatment_enzalutamide x mcrpc, x ar_v7_positive, x brca2_mutation, x msi_high, and x psa_ng_ml interactions, the mcrpc interaction is strongly negative.
judgments.rationale: A strongly negative mCRPC-by-enzalutamide interaction (i.e., enzalutamide working worse in mCRPC than non-mCRPC within the same multivariable model) is not part of guideline consensus and would be a notable subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: In the same model, the ar_v7_positive interaction with enzalutamide remains strongly negative.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to enzalutamide is well-established in prostate cancer literature and guidelines, so this is standard known biology rather than novel.

judgments.text: In the same model, the brca2_mutation interaction with enzalutamide remains strongly negative.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not establish BRCA2 mutation as a negative predictor of enzalutamide response; in fact PARP/AR combinations are favored in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC, so a strongly negative interaction would be a novel subgroup claim.

judgments.text: In the same model, the msi_high interaction with enzalutamide remains strongly negative.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status is not an established predictor of enzalutamide resistance in prostate cancer guidelines, so a strongly negative interaction would be a novel cross-pathway subgroup claim.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_abiraterone is not associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone is a guideline-endorsed active therapy in prostate cancer, so a fully adjusted null association with objective response contradicts guideline consensus and is a novel claim.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_docetaxel is not associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel has established activity in prostate cancer per guidelines, so a fully adjusted null association with objective response is a non-standard finding.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_olaparib is not associated with objective_response (overall).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib is guideline-endorsed for HRR/BRCA-mutant mCRPC, so an overall null association after adjustment is a non-standard, novel claim worth scrutiny.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_lu177_psma is not associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA has established efficacy in PSMA-positive mCRPC per recent guidelines, so a fully adjusted null association with objective response is a novel, non-consensus claim.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab is not associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab lacking overall response benefit in unselected prostate cancer is consistent with guideline-level knowledge that it is only indicated in biomarker-selected subgroups.

judgments.text: visceral_mets has no main effect on objective_response in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a prognostic but not response-predictive factor is standard guideline-level understanding.

judgments.text: Within patients with visceral_mets=1, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with a lower objective_response rate (vs no lu177_psma).
judgments.rationale: A subgroup-conditional negative effect of Lu-177-PSMA specifically in visceral metastasis patients is a speculative cross-conditional claim not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the 4-way favorable subgroup additionally restricted to psa_ng_ml ≤ 15.86, treatment_enzalutamide produces the largest absolute increase in objective_response observed in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: A 4-way subgroup with PSA threshold predicting maximal enzalutamide benefit is a speculative multi-covariate interaction not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: The final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup for objective_response is: in patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide raises the objective_response rate from approximately 17% to approximately 80% (a positive effect, ~63 percentage point absolute increase).
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide produces a ~63 percentage point response increase in a specific 4-biomarker-negative non-mCRPC subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional effect far beyond guideline expectations.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib does not raise objective_response in any subgroup tested, including the canonical brca2_mutation=1 target subgroup, where its effect is null to slightly negative.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib failing to benefit BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts established guideline paradigm and is a non-trivial negative claim.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab does not raise objective_response in any subgroup tested, including the canonical msi_high=1 target subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab failing to benefit MSI-high patients contradicts the established tissue-agnostic guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma does not raise objective_response in any subgroup tested, including the canonical psma_high=1 target subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA failing to benefit PSMA-high patients contradicts the established VISION-trial-based guideline paradigm.

judgments.text: treatment_abiraterone has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone having limited overall response effect (vs PSA/survival benefit) is consistent with known modest objective response rates in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_docetaxel has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel having limited objective response effect in prostate cancer is consistent with its known modest RECIST response rate in guidelines.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, higher psa_ng_ml remains negatively associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA reflects greater tumor burden and is a well-established negative prognostic/predictive marker in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, higher ecog_ps remains negatively associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting poorer treatment response is a foundational, guideline-level oncology principle.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, higher albumin_g_dl remains positively associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor nutritional/inflammatory status predicting worse response is standard, well-documented prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, higher weight_loss_pct_6mo remains negatively associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a negative predictor of treatment response is established consensus across oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's efficacy in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone is guideline-standard for prostate cancer with established response effects.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel is a guideline-standard chemotherapy for prostate cancer with known response effects.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's differential response in prostate cancer (especially HRR-mutated) is established and guideline-listed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA is FDA-approved for PSMA-positive mCRPC with established response data in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab response in prostate cancer (notably MSI-high subset) is a known guideline-level association.

judgments.text: BRCA2-mutated patients (brca2_mutation=1) have a lower objective_response rate than wildtype patients.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines associate BRCA2 with BETTER response to PARPi/platinum, not a uniformly lower overall ORR — this directional claim across all treatments is non-standard.

judgments.text: AR-V7-positive patients (ar_v7_positive=1) have a lower objective_response rate than AR-V7-negative patients.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance/lower response to AR-targeted therapy is a well-established biomarker association.

judgments.text: MSI-high patients (msi_high=1) have a different objective_response rate than MSI-stable patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting differential (improved) immunotherapy response is guideline-standard.

judgments.text: PSMA-high patients (psma_high=1) have a different objective_response rate than PSMA-low patients.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high expression predicting differential response (to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy) is established and in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a universally recognized prognostic factor included in every oncology guideline and trial eligibility criterion.

judgments.text: Castrate-resistant disease (mcrpc=1) is associated with lower objective_response than hormone-sensitive (mcrpc=0).
judgments.rationale: It is foundational prostate cancer paradigm that castrate-resistant disease has worse treatment response than hormone-sensitive disease.

judgments.text: Visceral metastases (visceral_mets=1) are associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastasis is a well-established adverse prognostic factor across prostate cancer guidelines and risk-stratification schemes.

judgments.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Age as a general adverse prognostic and tolerance factor is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Higher Gleason score is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gleason score is the canonical pathologic prognostic variable in prostate cancer and appears in every textbook.

judgments.text: Higher PSA (psa_ng_ml) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA correlating with worse disease burden and response is core prostate cancer paradigm.

judgments.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a nutritional/prognostic marker associated with better outcomes is well-established (e.g., used in standard prognostic nomograms).

judgments.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic indicator is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: CRP as an inflammatory adverse prognostic biomarker is widely recognized and incorporated into prostate cancer prognostic models.

judgments.text: Higher LDH, NLR, or alkaline phosphatase are associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: LDH, NLR, and alkaline phosphatase are all standard, guideline-referenced adverse prognostic biomarkers in advanced prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Olaparib improves objective_response specifically in BRCA2-mutated patients (treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutated tumors is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab improves objective_response specifically in MSI-high patients (treatment_pembrolizumab × msi_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is FDA-approved standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: Lu177-PSMA improves objective_response specifically in PSMA-high patients (treatment_lu177_psma × psma_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: Lu177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high mCRPC is established (VISION trial) and in guidelines.

judgments.text: AR-V7 expression abolishes the benefit of enzalutamide (treatment_enzalutamide × ar_v7_positive interaction with negative sign).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 as a resistance marker to enzalutamide is well-established in prostate cancer literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: AR-V7 modifies the benefit of abiraterone (treatment_abiraterone × ar_v7_positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 resistance to abiraterone is a well-known established association alongside enzalutamide resistance.

judgments.text: AR-V7 modifies the benefit of docetaxel (treatment_docetaxel × ar_v7_positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Preserved taxane efficacy regardless of AR-V7 status is established prostate cancer knowledge (Antonarakis et al.).

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for all clinical/biomarker/lab features, treatment_enzalutamide retains a strong positive effect on objective_response and the other five treatments do not.
judgments.rationale: A multivariable claim that only enzalutamide retains response benefit while five other active therapies do not is a speculative cross-treatment pattern not in guidelines.

judgments.text: BRCA2 mutation also abrogates the benefit of enzalutamide, separately from AR-V7 (treatment_enzalutamide × brca2_mutation interaction with negative sign).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation abrogating enzalutamide benefit independently of AR-V7 is not an established guideline association.

judgments.text: Other treatments (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab) show no significant interactions with any baseline feature at p<0.01 in screening (refined null hypothesis).
judgments.rationale: A global null claim that no baseline feature interacts with five major therapies is a speculative dataset-level pattern beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: AR-V7 and BRCA2 are independent resistance signals — enzalutamide benefit requires BOTH ar_v7=0 AND brca2=0.
judgments.rationale: Requiring joint absence of AR-V7 and BRCA2 for enzalutamide benefit is a novel compound-resistance hypothesis not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Castrate-resistance status (mcrpc) further restricts enzalutamide benefit — within AR-V7=0 & BRCA2=0, enzalutamide helps only mcrpc=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not condition enzalutamide benefit on castration-resistance status within AR-V7/BRCA2 wild-type subsets; this is a speculative three-way subgroup claim.

judgments.text: All treatments are stratified by mcrpc; only enzalutamide shows benefit, only in mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: The claim that enzalutamide uniquely benefits only non-mCRPC patients contradicts its established indication in mCRPC and is not guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Olaparib has no effect in BRCA2-mutated patients within mcrpc=1 (where it would be clinically targeted).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines and trial data (PROfound) support olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutated mCRPC, so a null effect in this canonical subgroup is a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Lu177-PSMA has no effect in PSMA-high mcrpc=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: VISION trial and guidelines establish Lu177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-high mCRPC, so claiming no effect contradicts established paradigm in a non-standard way.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab has no effect in MSI-high mcrpc=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is guideline-endorsed for MSI-high tumors (tissue-agnostic); claiming no effect in MSI-high mCRPC is a non-standard subgroup-specific assertion.

judgments.text: Abiraterone has no effect in mcrpc=1 stratified by AR-V7.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone is a standard mCRPC therapy; claiming no benefit stratified by AR-V7 in mCRPC is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Systematic interaction screening: only enzalutamide carries any feature-by-treatment interaction at p<0.01 against any baseline variable.
judgments.rationale: A systematic finding that only enzalutamide shows any feature-by-treatment interaction is a cross-pathway analytic claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: A T-learner ITE model + decision tree identifies the same enzalutamide-responsive subgroup defined by mcrpc=0, AR-V7=0, BRCA2=0 (and MSI-high=0).
judgments.rationale: An ITE-derived four-marker responder subgroup (mcrpc=0, AR-V7=0, BRCA2=0, MSI-high=0) for enzalutamide is a novel multi-marker signature beyond guideline biomarkers.

judgments.text: T-learner ITE for olaparib, pembrolizumab, lu177-PSMA, abiraterone, and docetaxel finds no consistent positive subgroup (all distributions essentially centered at zero).
judgments.rationale: A null ITE distribution across five standard prostate cancer therapies including olaparib and Lu177-PSMA contradicts established biomarker-driven benefit and is non-standard.

judgments.text: MSI-high status is a third independent suppressor of enzalutamide benefit (treatment_enzalutamide × msi_high interaction with negative sign), beyond AR-V7 and BRCA2.
judgments.rationale: A negative MSI-high × enzalutamide interaction as an independent suppressor is a mechanistic cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within mcrpc=0, enzalutamide produces a large response benefit ONLY in patients who are simultaneously AR-V7-negative, BRCA2-wildtype, AND MSI-stable.
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide benefit requires the joint absence of AR-V7, BRCA2 mutation, AND MSI-high specifically in non-mCRPC patients is a speculative multi-biomarker conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the clean subgroup (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7=0 & brca2=0 & msi_high=0), the enzalutamide benefit is constant across PSA quartiles (i.e., the global PSA-by-enzalutamide interaction is driven entirely by the AR-V7/BRCA2/MSI/mcrpc subgroup definition).
judgments.rationale: Asserting that a global PSA-by-treatment interaction is fully explained by a specific four-way biomarker subgroup is a non-guideline mechanistic claim about effect heterogeneity sources.

judgments.text: Within the clean enzalutamide-responsive subgroup, ECOG, albumin and weight loss continue to be independent prognostic factors but do not abrogate the treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: The claim that ECOG/albumin/weight loss remain prognostic but do not modify treatment effect within a specifically defined biomarker-clean subgroup is a subgroup-conditional finding beyond standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Adjusted (logistic) tests confirm null effects of olaparib in BRCA2+, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high, and abiraterone in mcrpc=1.
judgments.rationale: Null effects of olaparib in BRCA2+, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, and lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high contradict established guideline-level associations, making this a non-paradigmatic claim.

judgments.text: A logistic model restricted to mcrpc=0 with three biomarker-by-enzalutamide interactions cleanly captures the enzalutamide subgroup effect, with each biomarker individually β≈+3 for enzalutamide:negative-status.
judgments.rationale: Specific quantitative claim (β≈+3 per biomarker-by-enzalutamide interaction within mcrpc=0) is a granular mechanistic statement absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Enzalutamide: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive increase in objective_response (≈+62pp absolute, response 80% on enzalutamide vs 17% off enzalutamide) ONLY in the joint subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this joint subgroup the effect is essentially null.
judgments.rationale: A precise four-way joint subgroup driving an ~62pp enzalutamide benefit is a speculative interaction pattern not described in any prostate cancer guideline.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Olaparib: treatment_olaparib has no positive effect on objective_response in any biomarker-defined subgroup, including BRCA2-mutated patients (where canonically expected); the only borderline interaction is in the negative direction (olaparib slightly reduces response in BRCA2-mutated patients).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib being ineffective—or even harmful—in BRCA2-mutated patients directly contradicts the established PARP-inhibitor paradigm and is therefore non-standard.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Pembrolizumab: treatment_pembrolizumab has no positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup, including MSI-high patients (where canonically expected).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab having no benefit in MSI-high patients contradicts the tumor-agnostic guideline indication for MSI-high disease.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Lu177-PSMA: treatment_lu177_psma has no positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup, including PSMA-high mCRPC patients (where canonically expected).
judgments.rationale: Lu177-PSMA showing no benefit in PSMA-high mCRPC contradicts the VISION-trial-based guideline indication.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Abiraterone: treatment_abiraterone has no positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup, including AR-V7-negative or mCRPC patients.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone having no benefit in mCRPC or AR-V7-negative patients contradicts the established standard-of-care guideline use of abiraterone in mCRPC.

judgments.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS — Docetaxel: treatment_docetaxel has no positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup tested.
judgments.rationale: The claim that docetaxel lacks a positive response effect in any tested subgroup restates standard chemotherapy efficacy expectations and offers no subgroup-conditional or mechanistic insight beyond guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's improvement of response rates in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's efficacy improving response rates in prostate cancer is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel is a guideline-recommended chemotherapy with known response benefit in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's response benefit (in HRR-mutated prostate cancer) is established in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA's improvement in response rate for PSMA-positive mCRPC is established guideline knowledge post-VISION.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's modest response activity in select prostate cancer subsets (MSI-H/dMMR) is guideline-recognized, though broad benefit is not.

judgments.text: Higher age_years is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: A continuous age-response association in prostate cancer is not a standard guideline-level predictor of treatment response.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA correlating with worse outcomes/response is a well-known prognostic association in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as an adverse prognostic factor (Halabi nomogram) is established guideline/textbook knowledge in mCRPC.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic factor in mCRPC is established textbook/nomogram knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a negative prognostic factor for treatment response is well-established in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory biomarker associated with worse oncologic outcomes is a standard, well-known prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio is a widely recognized prognostic biomarker for response in many solid tumors including prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as a negative prognostic indicator for cancer treatment response is established in standard oncology practice.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase, often reflecting bone metastasis burden in mCRPC, is a well-established adverse prognostic marker in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with mcrpc=0.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic feature with reduced response rates is standard guideline-level knowledge in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with brca2_mutation=0.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) have lower objective_response rate than mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Castration-resistant disease being less responsive than hormone-sensitive disease is standard prostate cancer guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have lower objective_response rate than those without visceral metastases.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic factor with lower response rates is well-established in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is greater (more positive) in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in patients with brca2_mutation=0 (positive treatment x BRCA2 interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer responding preferentially to olaparib is established guideline knowledge (PROfound trial).

judgments.text: Within the brca2_mutation=1 subgroup, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response rate compared to no olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC is FDA-approved and guideline-endorsed standard of care.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is greater in patients with msi_high=1 than in patients with msi_high=0 (positive treatment x MSI-high interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high tumors responding preferentially to pembrolizumab is a tumor-agnostic FDA-approved indication in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the msi_high=1 subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is established standard of care across tumor types.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response is greater in patients with psma_high=1 than in patients with psma_high=0.

judgments.text: Within the psma_high=1 subgroup, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is greater in ar_v7_positive=0 than in ar_v7_positive=1 (negative interaction, ar_v7 abrogates enzalutamide benefit).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 as a resistance biomarker abrogating enzalutamide benefit is well-established and appears in guidelines/textbooks.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is greater in ar_v7_positive=0 than in ar_v7_positive=1.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 predicting resistance to abiraterone is well-established consensus knowledge.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide is smaller in patients with mcrpc=1 than mcrpc=0 (negative enzalutamide x mcrpc interaction).
judgments.rationale: A negative enzalutamide x mCRPC interaction (smaller benefit in metastatic CRPC) is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide is smaller in patients with higher ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: Specific quantitative interaction between ECOG PS and enzalutamide efficacy is a speculative subgroup claim beyond standard prognostic statements.

judgments.text: Treatment effects of all six treatments differ between mcrpc=0 and mcrpc=1.
judgments.rationale: Omnibus claim that all six treatment effects differ by mCRPC status is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide improving response in advanced prostate cancer is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_abiraterone is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone improving response in advanced prostate cancer is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_docetaxel is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel improving response in advanced prostate cancer is a standard guideline-level association.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_olaparib is associated with higher objective_response.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with higher objective_response.

judgments.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide improving response in advanced prostate cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: In a joint multivariable logistic regression containing all six treatments and prognostic covariates, treatment_enzalutamide retains an independent positive association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's independent benefit in prostate cancer is standard guideline content.

judgments.text: In the same joint model, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab each independently increase objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming all five additional agents (including pembrolizumab and Lu-177-PSMA) each independently increase response in an unselected prostate cancer population goes beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response decreases with increasing psa_ng_ml (negative enzalutamide x PSA interaction).
judgments.rationale: A continuous negative enzalutamide x PSA interaction on response is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response decreases with increasing ldh_u_l (negative enzalutamide x LDH interaction).
judgments.rationale: A continuous negative enzalutamide x LDH interaction on response is a speculative effect-modification claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2+ patients with ECOG performance status 0-1 (good PS), treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2+ mCRPC with good performance status reflects established guideline-based practice.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2+ patients with albumin_g_dl >= 3.5 (well-nourished), treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2+ patients with adequate albumin is essentially the guideline-endorsed PARP inhibitor indication.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2+ patients with both ECOG 0-1 AND albumin >= 3.5, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2+ patients with good PS and adequate nutrition is consistent with guideline use of PARP inhibitors.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients with ECOG 0-1, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is not a guideline-standard therapy for MSI-high prostate cancer specifically; the PS-restricted subgroup claim is speculative.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients with no visceral metastases, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning pembrolizumab benefit on MSI-high AND absence of visceral metastases in prostate cancer is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients with ECOG 0-1 AND albumin >= 3.5, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is established guideline consensus, and ECOG 0-1/albumin ≥3.5 are generic prognostic factors not a novel subgroup.

judgments.text: Within PSMA-high patients with no visceral metastases, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-high mCRPC is established (VISION/PSMAfore), and absence of visceral metastases is a known favorable prognostic feature, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Within PSMA-high patients with ECOG 0-1 AND albumin >= 3.5, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA in PSMA-high disease is guideline-endorsed, and ECOG 0-1/albumin ≥3.5 are standard generic prognostic factors.

judgments.text: Within PSMA-high patients with albumin >= 3.5 AND LDH < 250, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Conditioning Lu-177-PSMA benefit jointly on albumin ≥3.5 AND LDH <250 specifies a biomarker-defined subgroup not codified in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide markedly increases objective_response (vs ar_v7_positive=1 where no benefit is seen).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 negativity predicting enzalutamide response (and AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance) is well-established from Antonarakis et al. and reflected in clinical practice.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients with no visceral metastases, treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Combining AR-V7 negativity with absence of visceral metastases as a joint predictor of enzalutamide response is a cross-feature subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 negativity predicting abiraterone response is an established biomarker association from the same body of literature as enzalutamide.

judgments.text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel efficacy in mCRPC is foundational guideline-level knowledge (TAX327).

judgments.text: Within visceral_mets=1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel is preferentially used and active in visceral-metastatic CRPC per guidelines, so this is established consensus.

judgments.text: Within ECOG 0-1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel benefit in good performance status (ECOG 0-1) patients is standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There is a three-way treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation x ecog_ps interaction on objective_response (the olaparib-by-BRCA2 effect varies with performance status).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib's benefit in BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer is established, but ECOG-conditional modification of that benefit is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: There is a three-way treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high x ecog_ps interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is established, but performance-status modification of that benefit is not a guideline claim.

judgments.text: There is a three-way treatment_lu177_psma x psma_high x ecog_ps interaction on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high disease is established, but ECOG-conditional modification of that benefit is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: There exists at least one feature in {age_years,ecog_ps,mcrpc,visceral_mets,psa_ng_ml,gleason_score,brca2_mutation,ar_v7_positive,msi_high,psma_high,albumin_g_dl,ldh_u_l,weight_loss_pct_6mo,crp_mg_l,nlr,hemoglobin_g_dl,alkaline_phosphatase_u_l,ast_u_l,alt_u_l,total_bilirubin_mg_dl,creatinine_mg_dl,bun_mg_dl,sodium_meq_l,potassium_meq_l,calcium_mg_dl} that significantly modifies the effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response (treatment x feature interaction).

judgments.text: There exists at least one feature in {age_years,ecog_ps,mcrpc,visceral_mets,psa_ng_ml,gleason_score,brca2_mutation,ar_v7_positive,msi_high,psma_high,albumin_g_dl,ldh_u_l,weight_loss_pct_6mo,crp_mg_l,nlr,hemoglobin_g_dl,alkaline_phosphatase_u_l,ast_u_l,alt_u_l,total_bilirubin_mg_dl,creatinine_mg_dl,bun_mg_dl,sodium_meq_l,potassium_meq_l,calcium_mg_dl} that significantly modifies the effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (treatment x feature interaction).

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by (ar_v7_positive=0,mcrpc=0), treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide is guideline-endorsed for AR-V7-negative, non-castrate (mHSPC) prostate cancer with clear benefit over no treatment.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by (psma_high=1,visceral_mets=1), treatment_olaparib produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-associated mCRPC is established; conditioning on PSMA-high and visceral mets is a minor stratification but the underlying olaparib-response paradigm is guideline-level for HRR-mutant disease, and this hypothesis just restricts to a clinical subgroup without invoking a novel mechanism.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by (msi_high=1,mcrpc=0), treatment_pembrolizumab produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in MSI-high tumors is a tumor-agnostic guideline indication; restricting to non-mCRPC prostate cancer is a subgroup of an established paradigm.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by (msi_high=1,ar_v7_positive=1), treatment_lu177_psma produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than no treatment.
judgments.rationale: Claiming Lu-177-PSMA benefit is driven by MSI-high and AR-V7+ status crosses unrelated biomarker pathways (MMR/immune and AR splicing) with no guideline basis for predicting radioligand response.

judgments.text: Across {brca2_mutation, mcrpc, visceral_mets} 3-way subgroups, treatment_olaparib's largest treatment effect on objective_response defines its responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that olaparib's responder subgroup is defined by a specific 3-way intersection of BRCA2, mCRPC, and visceral mets goes beyond the guideline-level HRR-mutation indication into speculative subgroup-conditional effect modification.

judgments.text: Across {msi_high, mcrpc, visceral_mets} 3-way subgroups, treatment_pembrolizumab's largest treatment effect defines its responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Identifying a specific 3-way subgroup (MSI-high x mCRPC x visceral mets) as the pembrolizumab responder group beyond the established MSI-high indication is speculative subgroup-conditional refinement not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Across {psma_high, mcrpc, visceral_mets} 3-way subgroups, treatment_lu177_psma's largest treatment effect defines its responder subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support Lu-177-PSMA in PSMA-avid mCRPC broadly; pinpointing a specific 3-way subgroup (including visceral mets, which is often a negative prognostic factor) as the maximal responder set is a speculative effect-modification claim.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2+ patients, ECOG performance status, mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ar_v7_positive modify the (already-null) treatment effect of treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Effect modification of olaparib by ECOG/mCRPC/visceral mets/AR-V7 within BRCA2+ patients—especially framed against a null main effect—is a speculative interaction claim not present in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients, ECOG, mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ar_v7_positive modify the treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Cross-pathway effect modification of pembrolizumab by AR-V7 or visceral mets within MSI-high patients is a mechanistic/subgroup-conditional claim beyond the tumor-agnostic guideline indication.

judgments.text: Within PSMA-high patients, ECOG, mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ar_v7_positive modify the treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Effect modification of Lu-177-PSMA by AR-V7 status within PSMA-high patients invokes a cross-pathway interaction (AR splicing modulating radioligand response) not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: After adjusting for ecog_ps, visceral_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, age_years, and mcrpc, the treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction on objective_response is positive (BRCA2+ predicts olaparib benefit).
judgments.rationale: A positive olaparib x BRCA2 interaction on response after standard covariate adjustment restates the established guideline-level paradigm that BRCA2 mutation predicts olaparib benefit in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: After adjusting for the same prognostic covariates, the treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is established and guideline-endorsed (FDA tissue-agnostic approval).

judgments.text: After adjusting for the same prognostic covariates, the treatment_lu177_psma x psma_high interaction is positive.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-high prostate cancer is established by VISION trial and included in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within BRCA2+ patients with ECOG=0, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant patients is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Within MSI-high patients with ECOG=0, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high disease is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within PSMA-high patients with ECOG=0, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high prostate cancer is guideline-endorsed standard of care.

judgments.text: FINAL: Within the subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a large increase in objective_response (markedly higher than untreated patients in the same subgroup).
judgments.rationale: A large enzalutamide benefit specifically confined to non-mCRPC, AR-V7-negative, BRCA2-wildtype, MSI-stable patients is a speculative multi-covariate subgroup claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is suppressed (driven to zero) by ANY of the unfavorable covariate values mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, or msi_high=1; the strongest single suppressor is mcrpc=1.
judgments.rationale: The claim that mCRPC status is the dominant single suppressor of enzalutamide effect among several unfavorable markers is a cross-covariate mechanistic ranking not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: There is no biomarker-defined subgroup in this dataset in which treatment_olaparib increases objective_response among brca2_mutation=1 patients (in fact, the point estimate is slightly negative).
judgments.rationale: Asserting absence of any benefit (even slightly negative) for olaparib in BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts established guideline expectations and is a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: There is no biomarker-defined subgroup in this dataset in which treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response among msi_high=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no MSI-high subgroup benefits from pembrolizumab contradicts the established tissue-agnostic paradigm and is a non-guideline assertion.

judgments.text: FINAL: There is no biomarker-defined subgroup in this dataset in which treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response among psma_high=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no PSMA-high subgroup benefits from Lu-177-PSMA contradicts the VISION-based guideline paradigm and is a non-standard finding.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_abiraterone and treatment_docetaxel show no main effect and no biomarker-defined subgroup with meaningful benefit on objective_response in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: A null finding for abiraterone and docetaxel without any biomarker-defined responsive subgroup is consistent with established expectations rather than a novel mechanistic or subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: In the ds001_prostate cohort, the overall objective_response rate is non-zero and varies meaningfully across patient subgroups (i.e., this dataset has signal, not a constant outcome).
judgments.rationale: A general statement that the dataset has variable response signal is a basic data-quality observation, not a novel biological or clinical claim.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer is generally associated with HIGHER response to PARP inhibitors and platinum, so a lower response claim contradicts guideline consensus and is a non-standard subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting lower response (particularly to AR-targeted therapy) in mCRPC is well-established in guidelines and prostate cancer literature.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high prostate cancer is typically associated with HIGHER response to immunotherapy, so a lower response claim is non-standard and not reflected in guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a HIGHER objective_response rate than psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: High PSMA expression predicting better response (e.g., to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy like Lu-177-PSMA) is established in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a textbook prognostic association across essentially all oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: mcrpc=1 patients have LOWER objective_response than mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: mCRPC having lower response than non-castration-resistant disease is a standard, guideline-level prognostic distinction in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: visceral_mets=1 patients have LOWER objective_response than visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases predicting worse response and prognosis in prostate cancer is a well-established guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score predicting worse outcomes is a foundational, textbook prostate cancer prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age associated with lower treatment response/tolerance is a generic, well-known clinical association across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA reflecting greater tumor burden predicting worse response to prostate cancer therapy is a well-established prognostic association in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl predicts HIGHER objective_response (better nutritional status).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a marker of nutritional/performance status predicting better treatment response is a standard prognostic factor described in oncology textbooks.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l predicts LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: LDH as an established marker of tumor burden and worse outcomes is a standard prognostic factor across many oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo predicts LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss as a predictor of poor treatment response is a well-established clinical paradigm in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l predicts LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of systemic inflammation predicting worse outcomes is a well-known prognostic association in standard oncology literature.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) predicts LOWER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High NLR predicting worse outcomes is a widely recognized prognostic biomarker discussed in current oncology guidelines and reviews.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl predicts HIGHER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/lower hemoglobin predicting worse treatment outcomes is a standard prognostic factor in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l predicts LOWER objective_response (bone burden).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflecting bone metastatic burden and worse prognosis in prostate cancer is a textbook association.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response than treatment_enzalutamide=0.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide improving response in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_abiraterone=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone improving response in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in current treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_docetaxel=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel's activity in mCRPC is established standard-of-care chemotherapy and would appear in any prostate cancer treatment guideline.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: An unconditional response benefit from olaparib across an unselected population goes beyond guideline consensus, which restricts PARP inhibitor benefit to HRR-mutated subgroups.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA's response activity in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established (VISION trial) and appears in current guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
judgments.rationale: An unconditional response benefit from pembrolizumab in prostate cancer is not guideline-endorsed; benefit is restricted to MSI-H/dMMR or TMB-high subgroups.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib produces a LARGER absolute increase in objective_response in brca2_mutation=1 than in brca2_mutation=0 (PARP-inhibitor synthetic lethality hypothesis).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-selective benefit from olaparib via synthetic lethality is the textbook PARP inhibitor paradigm and appears in guidelines (PROfound).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab produces a LARGER absolute increase in objective_response in msi_high=1 than in msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: Enhanced pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is an established tissue-agnostic guideline indication.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma produces a LARGER absolute increase in objective_response in psma_high=1 than in psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: Greater Lu-177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high tumors is the established companion-diagnostic paradigm reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is SMALLER in ar_v7_positive=1 than in ar_v7_positive=0 (AR-V7 splice variant confers AR-targeted therapy resistance).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7-mediated resistance to enzalutamide is a well-established and guideline-discussed phenomenon (PROPHECY, Antonarakis studies).

judgments.text: The (lack of) effect of treatment_abiraterone differs by ar_v7_positive status.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 conferring resistance to abiraterone is the established cross-resistance paradigm for AR-targeted therapy and appears in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is SMALLER at higher ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: A specific ECOG-PS-conditional attenuation of enzalutamide response effect is not a standard guideline claim, even though poor PS generally portends worse outcomes.

judgments.text: The (null) effects of abi/doce/olaparib/lu177/pembro do not vary with ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: A null claim about ECOG not modifying treatment effects is not a guideline-level paradigm statement and is not a novel mechanistic hypothesis.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is LARGER in mcrpc=0 than in mcrpc=1 (the AR axis is still hormone-naive in mcrpc=0).
judgments.rationale: Greater AR-axis sensitivity in hormone-naive (mHSPC) vs castration-resistant disease is established prostate cancer paradigm reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: The other treatments show no mCRPC-by-treatment interaction.
judgments.rationale: A null claim of no mCRPC-by-treatment interaction for the remaining agents is not a paradigm-shifting or mechanistic hypothesis.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is LARGER in visceral_mets=0 than visceral_mets=1.
judgments.rationale: A specific enzalutamide-by-visceral-metastasis interaction effect modifier is a subgroup-conditional claim not codified in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_lu177_psma is SMALLER in visceral_mets=1 than visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Reduced Lu177-PSMA efficacy in visceral/PSMA-low disease is a recognized concern reflected in VISION-era guidance and selection criteria.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model controlling for all features and treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains the only treatment with a significant, positive coefficient.
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide is the sole treatment with an independent positive coefficient after full multivariable adjustment (implying olaparib, Lu177, pembro, docetaxel lack independent benefit) contradicts guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In the same multivariable model, mcrpc, ecog_ps, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, and psa_ng_ml retain independent NEGATIVE associations with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: ECOG, visceral/mCRPC status, BRCA2, AR-V7, MSI-high, weight loss, CRP, and PSA are well-known adverse prognostic/predictive markers in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Across a screen of treatment x feature logistic interactions, the strongest treatment effect-modifiers will be: (a) enzalutamide x mcrpc (negative), (b) enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive (negative), (c) enzalutamide x brca2_mutation (negative), (d) enzalutamide x msi_high (negative).
judgments.rationale: Predicting that enzalutamide benefit is specifically attenuated by BRCA2 and MSI-high (cross-pathway DNA-repair/immune effect modifiers on AR therapy) goes beyond established AR-V7 resistance dogma.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib remains associated with LOWER objective_response across ECOG strata.
judgments.rationale: Asserting olaparib yields LOWER response in BRCA2-mutant patients directly contradicts the established PROfound/guideline paradigm of PARP-inhibitor benefit in HRR-mutant mCRPC.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib remains associated with LOWER objective_response in BOTH visceral_mets=0 and visceral_mets=1.
judgments.rationale: Same contrarian claim against PARP-inhibitor benefit in BRCA2-mutant disease, stratified by visceral status, runs counter to current guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab effect is HIGHER in mcrpc=0 than in the overall MSI-high group (i.e., pembro benefit, if any, concentrates in hormone-sensitive MSI-high disease).
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high concentrates in hormone-sensitive (non-mCRPC) disease is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus, which simply endorses pembro for MSI-high tumors regardless of castration status.

judgments.text: Within psma_high=1, treatment_lu177_psma produces no meaningful response benefit in any (mCRPC, ECOG, visceral) subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines support Lu-177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high mCRPC; asserting no meaningful benefit across all subgroups contradicts established paradigm and is a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a LARGE positive RD (>20 percentage points) regardless of ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: A >20pp absolute response benefit of enzalutamide in AR-V7-negative patients independent of ECOG is a specific quantitative subgroup claim beyond textbook statements that AR-V7-negative disease is more likely to respond to AR-targeted therapy.

judgments.text: There is NO subgroup of brca2_mutation=1 patients (defined by ECOG and visceral_mets) in which treatment_olaparib produces a positive RD on objective_response of clinically meaningful magnitude.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that NO ECOG/visceral subgroup of BRCA2-mutant patients benefits from olaparib contradicts the guideline paradigm that BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer responds to PARP inhibition.

judgments.text: The entire treatment_enzalutamide benefit is concentrated in mcrpc=0 patients (no benefit in mcrpc=1) and is INDEPENDENT of visceral_mets status.
judgments.rationale: Confining enzalutamide benefit entirely to non-mCRPC patients while being independent of visceral mets is a speculative cross-stratum claim not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Neither treatment_docetaxel nor treatment_abiraterone shows clinically meaningful effect heterogeneity on objective_response across mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: A blanket claim of no heterogeneity for docetaxel or abiraterone across mCRPC/visceral/ECOG is a non-trivial cross-pathway negative claim beyond standard guideline statements.

judgments.text: Even in the most permissive subgroup (psma_high=1, ECOG=0, visceral_mets=0), treatment_lu177_psma does not produce a clinically meaningful positive RD on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claiming Lu-177-PSMA fails even in the most favorable PSMA-high, fit, non-visceral subgroup contradicts the established paradigm and is a strong novel negative claim.

judgments.text: The most plausible pembrolizumab benefit subgroup is msi_high=1 & ecog_ps<=1 & visceral_mets=0 (immunotherapy works best in fit, MSI-high, non-visceral patients), but the dataset is too underpowered to confirm.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab working best in MSI-high, fit, non-visceral patients reflects established immunotherapy paradigms (MSI-high benefit, better outcomes in fit/low-burden patients) that would appear in guidelines/textbooks.

judgments.text: There is NO 2-feature ECOG x visceral_mets cell within brca2_mutation=1 in which treatment_olaparib produces a positive RD with adequate sample size; the apparent absence of olaparib benefit in BRCA2+ patients is robust.
judgments.rationale: Asserting a robust absence of olaparib benefit across all ECOG x visceral cells within BRCA2+ patients contradicts the guideline paradigm endorsing PARP inhibitors in BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer.

judgments.text: A shallow decision tree fit on the treated subset of each treatment will identify, for treatment_enzalutamide, a subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 with response rate ~78% on enzalutamide vs ~17% off (RD ~+60 percentage points). For the other five treatments, no analogous high-RD subgroup will exist.
judgments.rationale: A specific multi-feature decision-tree subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND AR-V7-negative AND BRCA2 wild-type) with a quantitative ~60pp RD for enzalutamide, paired with no analogous subgroup for the other five drugs, is a speculative cross-pathway predictive claim beyond guideline consensus.
