judgments.text: FINAL best-supported treatment-effect subgroup for treatment_enzalutamide and objective_response: in patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0, treatment_enzalutamide HIGHER objective_response than no enzalutamide. Suppressors: mcrpc=1 and ar_v7_positive=1 each independently abolish the benefit.
judgments.rationale: Specific combinatorial subgroup requiring mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_negative with both factors as independent suppressors is a nuanced subgroup-conditional claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL best-supported subgroup for treatment_olaparib and objective_response: there is NO subgroup with a positive RD; the closest 'expected' subgroup brca2_mutation=1 actually shows a small NEGATIVE RD.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib failing to benefit BRCA2-mutated patients contradicts PROfound-based guideline consensus and would not appear in standard treatment recommendations.

judgments.text: FINAL best-supported subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab and objective_response: among msi_high=1 & mcrpc=0 patients, pembrolizumab is associated with HIGHER objective_response (29% vs 19%, n_on=34); evidence is suggestive but not statistically conclusive.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is established tissue-agnostic guideline knowledge and FDA-approved indication.

judgments.text: FINAL best-supported subgroup for treatment_lu177_psma and objective_response: NO subgroup with a clinically meaningful positive RD; the expected psma_high=1 subgroup is null. Modest negative interaction with visceral_mets.
judgments.rationale: Null effect of Lu-177-PSMA in PSMA-high patients contradicts VISION-based guideline expectation that PSMA expression predicts response.

judgments.text: FINAL best-supported subgroups for treatment_abiraterone and treatment_docetaxel and objective_response: NO subgroup with detectable positive RD; both treatments are essentially inert on this outcome in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claim that abiraterone and docetaxel are inert contradicts well-established guideline efficacy in prostate cancer and is not a textbook conclusion.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide improving response in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's effect on response in prostate cancer is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel response in prostate cancer is a long-standing standard chemotherapy with well-known efficacy.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib response in prostate cancer (especially HRR-mutant) is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA response in mCRPC is established post-VISION trial and included in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's role in MSI-high/TMB-high prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Lower response rates in mCRPC versus hormone-sensitive disease is standard clinical paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer is generally associated with better response to PARP inhibitors and platinum, so a lower overall response rate claim is non-standard.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapy is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high tumors typically respond better to immunotherapy, so a claim of lower overall response rate contradicts standard paradigm and is non-standard.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression as a predictive biomarker for PSMA-targeted therapy response is established in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic factor with lower response rates is a well-known standard prognostic feature in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response is a textbook prognostic factor across all oncology.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA correlating with worse outcomes in prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Gleason score as a prognostic factor for prostate cancer outcomes is foundational textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic/predictive factor is a standard demographic covariate considered in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as a poor prognostic indicator is established textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a prognostic marker reflecting nutritional status and inflammation is well-established in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: CRP as an inflammatory prognostic marker associated with worse cancer outcomes is established in the literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: LDH as a prognostic marker for tumor burden and worse outcomes is textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher hemoglobin reflecting absence of anemia is a well-established favorable prognostic marker in mCRPC and other cancers, included in standard prognostic nomograms.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio is a widely recognized adverse prognostic biomarker in prostate and other cancers, appearing in numerous reviews and prognostic models.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase is a classic adverse prognostic marker in mCRPC reflecting bone metastases and is part of standard prognostic frameworks.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is greater in patients with mcrpc=0 than in patients with mcrpc=1 (positive interaction with non-mCRPC, equivalent to negative treatment_enzalutamide x mcrpc interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide is guideline-approved across both metastatic hormone-sensitive and castration-resistant settings; a claim that benefit is specifically greater in non-mCRPC patients goes beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response differs between mcrpc=0 and mcrpc=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential abiraterone benefit by mCRPC status is a subgroup-conditional claim not reflected in current guidelines, which support its use across both mHSPC and mCRPC.

judgments.text: The treatment_docetaxel effect on objective_response differs between mcrpc=0 and mcrpc=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel is established in both mHSPC and mCRPC, and a differential effect by mCRPC status is not part of standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is greater in patients with ar_v7_positive=0 than in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 (negative treatment_enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to enzalutamide is a well-established association documented in major reviews and discussed in guidelines as a mechanism of AR-targeted therapy resistance.

judgments.text: The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response differs between ar_v7_positive=0 and ar_v7_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 status as a predictor of differential response to abiraterone is a well-known established association in the prostate cancer literature.

judgments.text: The treatment_olaparib effect on objective_response differs between ar_v7_positive=0 and ar_v7_positive=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 status as a predictive biomarker for olaparib response is not an established paradigm; olaparib's predictive biomarkers are HRR/BRCA alterations, making this a speculative cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is greater in patients with brca2_mutation=0 than in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (negative treatment_enzalutamide x brca2_mutation interaction).
judgments.rationale: While BRCA2 mutation predicts PARP inhibitor benefit, a specific claim that BRCA2 mutation reduces enzalutamide benefit is not established guideline consensus and is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: The treatment_olaparib effect on objective_response is larger (positive) in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in patients with brca2_mutation=0 (positive olaparib x brca2 interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer responding to olaparib is established guideline-level knowledge (PROfound trial).

judgments.text: The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response differs between brca2_mutation=0 and brca2_mutation=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential abiraterone response by BRCA2 status is not a standard guideline-level paradigm.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is greater in patients with msi_high=0 than in patients with msi_high=1 (negative treatment_enzalutamide x msi_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy modulation by MSI status is not part of established prostate cancer treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The treatment_pembrolizumab effect on objective_response is larger (positive) in patients with msi_high=1 than in msi_high=0 patients (positive pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high predicting pembrolizumab response is established tumor-agnostic guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The treatment_lu177_psma effect on objective_response is larger (positive) in patients with psma_high=1 than in psma_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high expression predicting Lu-177-PSMA response is established (VISION trial) guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response differs between psma_high=0 and psma_high=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide effect varying by PSMA expression is not a standard guideline association.

judgments.text: The treatment_lu177_psma effect on objective_response differs between visceral_mets=0 and visceral_mets=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Differential Lu-177-PSMA efficacy by visceral metastasis status is not part of established treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients with the joint subgroup mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in this 'responsive' joint subgroup the absolute response-rate increase exceeds 50 percentage points.
judgments.rationale: A specific joint four-variable subgroup with >50 percentage point enzalutamide response is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Outside this joint subgroup (i.e. at least one of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, msi_high=1), treatment_enzalutamide produces an essentially null effect on objective_response (less than 5 percentage points).
judgments.rationale: Claim of essentially null enzalutamide effect outside this specific joint subgroup is not standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within the responsive joint subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0), no other treatment besides treatment_enzalutamide produces a meaningful change in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claim that only enzalutamide works in this specific joint subgroup is a speculative cross-treatment comparison absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the responsive joint subgroup, the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is consistent (around +60 pp) across ECOG 0, 1, and 2 strata.
judgments.rationale: Claim of homogeneous ~60pp enzalutamide benefit across ECOG strata within a responsive subgroup is a specific subgroup-conditional effect not in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the responsive joint subgroup, the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is consistent (around +60 pp) across PSA tertiles.
judgments.rationale: Consistency of enzalutamide effect across PSA tertiles within a defined responsive subgroup is a non-guideline subgroup-level claim.

judgments.text: Across continuous features, the only continuous-feature-by-treatment interaction with p<0.01 is treatment_enzalutamide x psa_ng_ml (negative); no other treatment has any continuous-feature interaction at p<0.01.
judgments.rationale: A specific cross-treatment comparison asserting enzalutamide-PSA is the unique continuous interaction at p<0.01 is an exploratory analytic finding, not guideline content.

judgments.text: The apparent treatment_enzalutamide x psa_ng_ml interaction is mediated by mcrpc status: after adjusting for mcrpc and treatment_enzalutamide x mcrpc, the residual treatment_enzalutamide x psa_ng_ml interaction is non-significant.
judgments.rationale: Mediation of an enzalutamide-by-PSA interaction through mCRPC status is a mechanistic/statistical claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Systematic search for treatment_olaparib effect across single-feature subgroups (binary and quartile splits, n>=200 per subgroup) yields no subgroup with a robust positive treatment_olaparib effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A null finding that olaparib has no robust responsive subgroup contradicts the guideline-recognized HRR/BRCA-mutant benefit, making it a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Systematic search for treatment_pembrolizumab effect across single-feature subgroups yields no subgroup with a robust positive treatment_pembrolizumab effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A blanket null for pembrolizumab subgroup benefit conflicts with guideline-recognized MSI-H/dMMR or TMB-high responders, so this is a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Systematic search for treatment_lu177_psma effect across single-feature subgroups yields no subgroup with a robust positive treatment_lu177_psma effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Asserting no robust Lu-177-PSMA responsive subgroup runs counter to PSMA-positive selection in guidelines and is a non-standard analytic claim.

judgments.text: Systematic search for treatment_abiraterone effect across single-feature subgroups yields no robust positive subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone is a broadly effective mCRPC therapy without a guideline-mandated biomarker subgroup, so a generic null subgroup-search result aligns with standard expectations.

judgments.text: Systematic search for treatment_docetaxel effect across single-feature subgroups yields no robust positive subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel similarly lacks a guideline biomarker subgroup, so failing to find a robust positive subgroup is consistent with standard practice.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for all baseline features, treatment_enzalutamide is the only treatment with a significant non-null coefficient.
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide is the sole treatment with a significant adjusted coefficient over olaparib, pembrolizumab, Lu-177-PSMA, abiraterone, and docetaxel contradicts established guideline efficacy and is non-standard.

judgments.text: In a single multivariable logistic model that includes the four interaction terms treatment_enzalutamide x mcrpc, treatment_enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive, treatment_enzalutamide x brca2_mutation, treatment_enzalutamide x msi_high, all four interactions are negative and each one alone is sufficient to nearly cancel the enzalutamide main effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming that four distinct biomarker interactions (mcrpc, AR-V7, BRCA2, MSI-high) each independently nullify enzalutamide's main effect goes beyond guideline knowledge — only AR-V7 resistance is established, while BRCA2 and MSI-high as enzalutamide effect modifiers are not standard paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: In patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide raises the objective_response rate from approximately 17% to approximately 80% (absolute increase ~63 pp). Outside this joint subgroup (where any of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, msi_high=1 is present), treatment_enzalutamide has essentially no effect (<1 pp absolute change).
judgments.rationale: A specific joint-subgroup conditional claim that enzalutamide produces a ~63 pp response increase only when mcrpc, AR-V7, BRCA2, and MSI-high are all absent is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not found in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: For each of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab, no subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>=5 pp) and statistically robust treatment effect on objective_response could be identified in this cohort, including the biologically expected biomarker-defined subgroups (BRCA2+ for olaparib, MSI-high for pembrolizumab, PSMA-high for lu177_psma).
judgments.rationale: Asserting null biomarker-defined effects for olaparib in BRCA2+, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, and lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high directly contradicts established guideline-level associations, making the negative claim a non-standard, novel assertion about this cohort.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy in prostate cancer is well-established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone efficacy in prostate cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel response in prostate cancer is standard textbook chemotherapy knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib response, particularly in HRR-mutant prostate cancer, is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177 PSMA response in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established (VISION trial) and in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab response in MSI-high/dMMR prostate cancer is a known guideline indication.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer response differences (especially with PARP inhibitors) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapy is well-established in the literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting differential response (to immunotherapy) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high status predicting response to PSMA-targeted therapy is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Castration-resistant prostate cancer having lower response rates than hormone-sensitive disease is established prognostic knowledge in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases being associated with worse response and prognosis in prostate cancer is a well-established guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response is a textbook prognostic factor across oncology.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA being associated with worse outcomes in prostate cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin as a favorable prognostic marker is well-established in oncology textbooks and prognostic models.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor (cachexia) is standard textbook oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker in prostate cancer is well-established (e.g., in prognostic nomograms).

judgments.text: Age (age_years) is associated with objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Age as a prognostic/response factor is a routinely examined standard covariate, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score predicting worse outcomes is foundational prostate cancer guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjusting for prognostic features and other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide improving response in prostate cancer after adjustment is established guideline-level efficacy, not novel.

judgments.text: After adjusting for prognostic features and other treatments, abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, and pembrolizumab show no main-effect benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Null main effects after covariate adjustment is a generic statistical observation, not a novel oncology insight beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib produces a higher objective_response rate than no olaparib.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer responding to olaparib is established guideline knowledge (PROfound trial, FDA approval).

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab produces a higher objective_response rate than no pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high tumors responding to pembrolizumab is a tumor-agnostic FDA-approved indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Within psma_high=1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma produces a higher objective_response rate than no lu177-PSMA.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high prostate cancer responding to Lu-177-PSMA is established (VISION trial, FDA approval) and in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is attenuated (interaction) in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 compared to ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity attenuating response to enzalutamide is a well-established resistance mechanism described in guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is attenuated in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 compared to ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity attenuating response to abiraterone is a well-known resistance mechanism (Antonarakis et al.) reflected in clinical practice.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is attenuated in patients with mcrpc=1 compared to mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Claiming enzalutamide efficacy is specifically attenuated in mCRPC versus non-mCRPC contradicts standard use and is not a guideline claim.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is attenuated in patients with mcrpc=1 compared to mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Claiming abiraterone efficacy is specifically attenuated in mCRPC versus non-mCRPC contradicts standard use and is not a guideline claim.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is attenuated in patients with mcrpc=1 compared to mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: A specific attenuation of docetaxel benefit in mCRPC versus non-mCRPC is not a standard guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0; in any other combination of these two factors the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A conjunctive subgroup claim that enzalutamide benefit exists only when both mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_negative, with null effect otherwise, is a speculative cross-factor interaction beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is heterogeneous across binary biomarkers and clinical features; specifically brca2_mutation, msi_high, and psma_high may modify it.
judgments.rationale: Generic claim that enzalutamide effect varies across common biomarkers including BRCA2/MSI/PSMA is a broad hypothesis-generating statement consistent with standard precision-oncology framing, not a specific novel claim.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is also modified by continuous prognostic features (PSA, ECOG, albumin, etc.) when split at median.
judgments.rationale: Stating that treatment effect is modified by standard prognostic features (PSA, ECOG, albumin) split at median is a generic effect-modification hypothesis already implicit in guideline risk stratification.

judgments.text: Within the non-mCRPC AND non-AR-V7+ subgroup, treatment_enzalutamide retains a strong objective_response effect ONLY in patients with brca2_mutation=0; among brca2_mutation=1 patients in this subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: A specific conditional claim that within non-mCRPC/non-AR-V7+ patients, enzalutamide benefit is abolished by BRCA2 mutation is not in current prostate cancer guidelines and represents a novel subgroup interaction.

judgments.text: Within the non-mCRPC AND non-AR-V7+ subgroup, treatment_enzalutamide retains a strong objective_response effect ONLY in patients with msi_high=0; among msi_high=1 patients in this subgroup the effect is null.
judgments.rationale: Claiming MSI-high status nullifies enzalutamide benefit specifically within the non-mCRPC/non-AR-V7+ subgroup is a cross-pathway interaction not described in standard prostate cancer treatment paradigms.

judgments.text: Within the non-mCRPC AND non-AR-V7+ subgroup, the treatment_enzalutamide effect is similar in psma_high=1 and psma_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Stating PSMA-high vs low does not modify enzalutamide effect in this subgroup is a null/negative finding consistent with current absence of PSMA as an enzalutamide predictive biomarker in guidelines.

judgments.text: Treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive objective_response benefit specifically in patients who are mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in patients who fail any one of these four predicates, the effect of enzalutamide is null.
judgments.rationale: A four-way conjunctive predicate (non-mCRPC AND AR-V7- AND BRCA2-wt AND MSS) defining the only subgroup that benefits from enzalutamide is a highly specific multi-marker rule absent from any current guideline.

judgments.text: The enzalutamide benefit in the four-criterion subgroup remains large after adjusting for other prognostic features (age, PSA, ECOG, albumin, CRP, weight loss, visceral mets, PSMA, Gleason) and other concurrent treatments.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the four-criterion subgroup benefit persists after adjustment for prognostic covariates and concurrent therapies elevates the conjunctive predictive rule beyond confounding, which is novel beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient features; in particular, abiraterone may have a positive effect in some subgroup not visible in the marginal analysis.
judgments.rationale: Generic statement that abiraterone effect is heterogeneous and may show benefit in some subgroup is a standard hypothesis-generating claim, not a novel mechanistic or guideline-departing assertion.

judgments.text: Treatment_docetaxel effect on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient features and may be larger in specific subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Saying docetaxel effect varies by patient features and is larger in some subgroups is a generic effect-heterogeneity claim consistent with established practice.

judgments.text: Treatment_olaparib effect on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient features; in particular it may be different in BRCA2+ patients and may interact with renal/hepatic labs.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib differential efficacy in BRCA2+ patients is established guideline knowledge (PROfound/HRR indication), and noting interactions with renal/hepatic labs is a generic pharmacology expectation.

judgments.text: Treatment_lu177_psma effect on objective_response varies by visceral_mets; specifically it may be lower (or even harmful) in visceral_mets=1 patients.
judgments.rationale: Reduced PSMA-radioligand efficacy in visceral metastatic disease is a recognized clinical observation reflected in VISION trial subgroup analyses and treatment guidance.

judgments.text: Treatment_pembrolizumab effect on objective_response varies by patient features (CRP, albumin, alkaline phosphatase) and may show a positive subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab response prediction by CRP/albumin/alkaline phosphatase composite inflammatory-nutritional markers in this disease context is not guideline-established.

judgments.text: A logistic model that includes each treatment with main-effect terms and interaction terms with every prognostic feature reproduces the heterogeneity profile: enzalutamide has many strong feature interactions, while abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab have few or none.
judgments.rationale: A pattern in which enzalutamide alone exhibits broad prognostic-feature interactions while five other agents (including biomarker-matched ones) show none contradicts standard precision-oncology consensus.

judgments.text: Within the enzalutamide-responder subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0), abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab provide no additional benefit beyond enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: The claim that olaparib, pembrolizumab, and lu177-PSMA add no benefit over enzalutamide specifically within an mcrpc=0/AR-V7-/BRCA2-/MSI-low subgroup is not part of any guideline.

judgments.text: Outside the enzalutamide-responder subgroup, none of the six treatments (enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) provides a meaningful benefit.
judgments.rationale: Asserting universal futility of all six agents (including biomarker-matched therapies) outside a narrow predicate-defined subgroup contradicts established efficacy evidence and is not in guidelines.

judgments.text: A regression tree fit to the Tian-style transformed-outcome pseudo-response (response * (T - p_T)/(p_T*(1-p_T))) for treatment_enzalutamide partitions patients by mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, and brca2_mutation, with leaves having large positive enzalutamide effects (RD>+0.5) iff mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: A specific tree-derived predicate (mcrpc=0 AND AR-V7- AND BRCA2-) defining a >0.5 risk-difference enzalutamide responder leaf is a novel data-driven subgroup rule.

judgments.text: The presence of any one of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, or msi_high=1 is sufficient to suppress the enzalutamide benefit; the absence of all four is necessary for the benefit.
judgments.rationale: The four-predicate necessary-and-sufficient logical rule for enzalutamide benefit (including MSI-high as a suppressor) is not part of standard prostate cancer paradigm.

judgments.text: FINAL: In male prostate-cancer patients meeting ALL of mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive increase in objective_response (risk difference ≈ +0.63, OR ≈ 20 after adjustment); patients failing any one of these four predicates derive essentially no enzalutamide benefit.
judgments.rationale: A precise conjunctive four-feature predicate yielding RD≈+0.63/OR≈20 for enzalutamide, with no benefit otherwise, is far more specific than any guideline statement on enzalutamide selection.

judgments.text: FINAL: In this cohort, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab show no main effect on objective_response, no clinically meaningful subgroup with positive effect (including the canonical biomarker matches BRCA2/olaparib, MSI-high/pembrolizumab, PSMA-high/lu177-PSMA), and no benefit when added to enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Claiming complete absence of benefit—including in canonical biomarker-matched populations (BRCA2/olaparib, MSI-high/pembrolizumab, PSMA-high/lu177-PSMA)—directly contradicts established guideline-level evidence and is a novel (and likely cohort-specific) assertion.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide is a guideline-standard androgen receptor inhibitor with established response benefit in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone is a guideline-standard CYP17 inhibitor with established response benefit in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel is a guideline-standard chemotherapy with established response activity in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib has established response activity in HRR/BRCA-altered prostate cancer per current guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA is a guideline-approved radioligand therapy with established response benefit in PSMA-positive prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is not a standard prostate cancer therapy and a marginal response benefit unselected by MSI/TMB would be non-guideline.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with brca2_mutation=0 (testing whether brca2_mutation status is a marginal predictor of response).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation predicting differential response (via PARP inhibitor sensitivity) is well-established in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with ar_v7_positive=0 (testing whether ar_v7_positive status is a marginal predictor of response).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to AR-targeted therapy is a known biomarker association in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with msi_high=0 (testing whether msi_high status is a marginal predictor of response).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status predicting response (via immunotherapy eligibility) is an established tumor-agnostic guideline biomarker.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with psma_high=0 (testing whether psma_high status is a marginal predictor of response).
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high status predicting response is established in the context of PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response is a well-established prognostic factor in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) having worse response than non-mCRPC is established disease-state consensus.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as an adverse prognostic feature affecting response is standard guideline knowledge in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score (>=9) is associated with a lower objective_response rate than lower gleason_score (<=7).
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score predicting worse outcomes is foundational prostate cancer prognostic knowledge in any guideline or textbook.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Older age associated with reduced treatment response is a standard, widely recognized clinical observation.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA reflecting greater disease burden and worse response is established prostate cancer guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher objective_response rate (positive beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor nutritional/inflammatory status predicting worse response is a well-known prognostic factor.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic biomarker for response is established across oncology guidelines including prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse treatment response is a standard, textbook prognostic indicator.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a systemic inflammation marker predicting worse oncologic response is well-established prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR as a negative prognostic/predictive marker for response to systemic therapy is well-established across solid tumors and appears in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a higher objective_response rate (positive beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Anemia (low hemoglobin) is a long-recognized adverse prognostic factor associated with worse treatment response across oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase, often reflecting bone or liver metastatic burden, is a standard adverse prognostic marker for treatment response.

judgments.text: Higher ast_u_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST commonly reflects liver involvement or organ dysfunction and is a known adverse prognostic factor in oncology.

judgments.text: Higher alt_u_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated ALT similarly reflects hepatic dysfunction/metastatic burden and is conventionally associated with worse outcomes.

judgments.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated bilirubin reflects hepatic dysfunction and is a standard adverse prognostic factor that also limits chemotherapy dosing in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated creatinine reflects renal dysfunction, a recognized adverse factor that affects drug clearance and outcomes per standard practice.

judgments.text: Higher bun_mg_dl is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Elevated BUN reflects renal dysfunction or dehydration and is a conventional adverse prognostic indicator.

judgments.text: sodium_meq_l is associated with objective_response (non-zero beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Hyponatremia is a well-established adverse prognostic factor across multiple solid tumors and appears in standard oncology references.

judgments.text: potassium_meq_l is associated with objective_response (non-zero beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Serum potassium is not a recognized predictor of objective response in oncology guidelines, so any association would go beyond established consensus.

judgments.text: Higher calcium_mg_dl is associated with a lower objective_response rate (negative beta in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Serum calcium as a predictor of objective response is not a standard guideline-level biomarker, though hypercalcemia is a known prognostic marker.

judgments.text: Olaparib increases objective_response more in BRCA2-mutated patients than in BRCA2 wildtype (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit in BRCA-mutated tumors is a core guideline-level paradigm.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab increases objective_response more in MSI-high patients than in MSI-low patients (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is an FDA-approved, guideline-level indication.

judgments.text: Lu-177-PSMA increases objective_response more in PSMA-high patients than in PSMA-low patients (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-expressing tumors is the established VISION-trial paradigm and guideline indication.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's increase in objective_response is attenuated in AR-V7-positive patients (negative treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity as a resistance marker to enzalutamide is well-established in prostate cancer literature and practice.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's increase in objective_response is attenuated in BRCA2-mutated patients (negative treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
judgments.rationale: A specific BRCA2-mediated attenuation of enzalutamide response is not a standard guideline claim and goes beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's increase in objective_response is attenuated in MSI-high patients (negative treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status modifying enzalutamide response is not described in guidelines and represents a speculative cross-pathway interaction.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's increase in objective_response is attenuated in mcrpc=1 patients (negative treatment-by-mcrpc interaction).
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide is indicated across both mCSPC and mCRPC, so a specific attenuation by mCRPC status is not guideline-level consensus.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's effect on objective_response differs between ecog_ps>=2 and ecog_ps<2 (non-zero interaction).
judgments.rationale: ECOG PS is a general prognostic factor, but a specific differential treatment effect of enzalutamide by performance status is not a guideline-level claim.

judgments.text: Lu-177-PSMA's effect on objective_response is attenuated in patients with visceral_mets=1 (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Reduced Lu-177-PSMA benefit in visceral (especially liver) metastatic disease is a recognized finding from VISION subgroup data and is reflected in clinical practice considerations.

judgments.text: Lu-177-PSMA increases objective_response more in patients with gleason_score>=9 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional claim that Lu-177-PSMA efficacy is amplified specifically in Gleason >=9 disease is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab increases objective_response more in patients with gleason_score>=9 (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab is not a standard prostate cancer therapy and a Gleason-score-conditional response benefit is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response varies with psa_ng_ml (non-zero interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: A PSA-by-enzalutamide interaction on objective response is a speculative effect-modification claim not reflected in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response varies with sodium_meq_l (non-zero interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Serum sodium as an effect modifier of docetaxel response is a non-guideline, speculative interaction.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response varies with alt_u_l (non-zero interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: ALT-by-docetaxel interaction on response is not part of any standard oncology paradigm.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response varies with bun_mg_dl (non-zero interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: BUN as an effect modifier of olaparib response in prostate cancer is not in guidelines (which key on HRR/BRCA status).

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response varies with creatinine_mg_dl (non-zero interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine-by-enzalutamide interaction on response is a speculative cross-pathway claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response varies with creatinine_mg_dl (non-zero interaction term in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine-modulated Lu-177-PSMA efficacy is not established and would not appear in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide retains an independent positive association with objective_response after adjusting for age, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, gleason_score, all biomarkers, all labs, and other treatments (positive beta in multivariable logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide having an independent positive association with response in mCRPC is the established paradigm reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Other treatments (treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab) are not significantly associated with objective_response after adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Claiming abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, Lu-177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab all lack independent association with response contradicts guideline-supported efficacy of several of these agents.

judgments.text: After adjusting for treatment and demographics, mcrpc=1 retains an independent negative association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Worse response in mCRPC versus less advanced prostate cancer states is textbook/guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: After adjustment, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, and msi_high retain negative independent associations with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pooled negative associations for BRCA2 and MSI-high are counterintuitive given guideline expectations of positive response to PARP inhibitors and pembrolizumab respectively.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient subgroups defined by single binary features (any modifier with non-zero treatment-by-modifier interaction, found by systematic screening across all 30+ binarized features).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 status as a modifier of enzalutamide response is established in prostate cancer guidelines/literature.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient subgroups defined by single binary features (any modifier with non-zero treatment-by-modifier interaction, found by systematic screening across all 30+ binarized features).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 and prior AR-pathway exposure as modifiers of abiraterone response are established.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient subgroups defined by single binary features (any modifier with non-zero treatment-by-modifier interaction, found by systematic screening across all 30+ binarized features).
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel lacks an established predictive biomarker in guidelines, so any feature-driven heterogeneity beyond clinical factors would be novel.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient subgroups defined by single binary features (any modifier with non-zero treatment-by-modifier interaction, found by systematic screening across all 30+ binarized features).
judgments.rationale: BRCA1/2 / HRR-mutation status as a modifier of olaparib response is a guideline-level association.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient subgroups defined by single binary features (any modifier with non-zero treatment-by-modifier interaction, found by systematic screening across all 30+ binarized features).
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression as a modifier of Lu-177-PSMA response is the established guideline criterion.

judgments.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is heterogeneous across patient subgroups defined by single binary features (any modifier with non-zero treatment-by-modifier interaction, found by systematic screening across all 30+ binarized features).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high / dMMR (and TMB) as modifiers of pembrolizumab response are guideline-established.

judgments.text: Olaparib's effect on objective_response is concentrated in a two-feature subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A two-feature combination beyond BRCA/HRR status defining olaparib responders goes beyond current guideline single-biomarker selection.

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab's effect on objective_response is concentrated in a two-feature subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A two-feature combination beyond MSI-high defining pembrolizumab responders goes beyond current guideline single-biomarker selection.

judgments.text: Lu-177-PSMA's effect on objective_response is concentrated in a two-feature subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A specific two-feature subgroup driving Lu-177-PSMA response beyond PSMA-positivity alone is not established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's effect on objective_response is concentrated in a two-feature subgroup, with the largest effect among non-mCRPC, low-burden patients.
judgments.rationale: While enzalutamide is used across CRPC states, a specific two-feature interaction concentrating effect in non-mCRPC low-burden patients goes beyond standard guideline framing.

judgments.text: Abiraterone's effect on objective_response is concentrated in a two-feature subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not specify a particular two-feature subgroup that concentrates abiraterone's response benefit.

judgments.text: Docetaxel's effect on objective_response is concentrated in a two-feature subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Guidelines do not define a specific two-feature subgroup as concentrating docetaxel's response benefit.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's effect on objective_response is larger in lower-PSA quartiles than in higher-PSA quartiles.
judgments.rationale: PSA-quartile effect modification of enzalutamide response is not a guideline-endorsed predictive marker.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's effect on objective_response is larger in higher-albumin quartiles.
judgments.rationale: Albumin as an effect modifier (not just prognostic factor) for enzalutamide response is not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: Enzalutamide's effect on objective_response varies across LDH quartiles.
judgments.rationale: LDH-quartile-dependent variation in enzalutamide treatment effect is not part of guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by brca2_mutation AND ecog_le1, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response more than treatment_olaparib does in the complementary group (positive within-subgroup rate difference).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib benefit in BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer with good performance status reflects the established PROfound-era guideline indication.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by msi_high AND ecog_le1 AND albumin_ok, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response more than treatment_pembrolizumab does in the complementary group (positive within-subgroup rate difference).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors with good performance status is the standard tissue-agnostic guideline indication.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by psma_high AND albumin_ok, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response more than treatment_lu177_psma does in the complementary group (positive within-subgroup rate difference).
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high patients with adequate organ function recapitulates the VISION-based guideline eligibility.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by no_visceral, treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response more than treatment_enzalutamide does in the complementary group (positive within-subgroup rate difference).
judgments.rationale: Subgroup analysis suggesting enzalutamide works better in non-visceral disease aligns with known clinical patterns where AR-targeted therapies perform better in less aggressive, non-visceral mCRPC.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by no_visceral, treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response more than treatment_abiraterone does in the complementary group (positive within-subgroup rate difference).
judgments.rationale: Similarly, abiraterone benefit being greater in non-visceral disease is consistent with established understanding that AR-axis inhibitors perform better in less aggressive disease patterns.

judgments.text: In the subgroup defined by albumin_ok, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response more than treatment_docetaxel does in the complementary group (positive within-subgroup rate difference).
judgments.rationale: Adequate albumin (a marker of good performance status/nutrition) predicting better docetaxel response is a well-established prognostic association in mCRPC.

judgments.text: Among brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib does NOT increase objective_response (within-subgroup rate difference is non-positive); the effect is not concentrated in the canonical BRCA2-positive group.
judgments.rationale: Claiming olaparib does NOT benefit BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts established guideline consensus (PROfound trial) that PARP inhibitors are specifically indicated for BRCA-mutant mCRPC.

judgments.text: Among msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does NOT meaningfully increase objective_response (within-subgroup rate difference is near zero).
judgments.rationale: Claiming pembrolizumab does NOT benefit MSI-high patients contradicts the tumor-agnostic FDA approval and guideline consensus that immune checkpoint inhibitors are highly effective in MSI-high tumors.

judgments.text: Among psma_high=1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma does NOT meaningfully increase objective_response (within-subgroup rate difference is near zero).
judgments.rationale: Claiming Lu-177-PSMA does NOT benefit PSMA-high patients contradicts the VISION trial and guideline consensus requiring PSMA-positivity for this therapy's indication.

judgments.text: Even after adjustment for age, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, gleason_score, and key labs, the treatment_olaparib by brca2_mutation interaction term is not significantly positive.
judgments.rationale: An adjusted analysis showing no significant olaparib-by-BRCA2 interaction would contradict the established biomarker-driven indication and represents a counter-paradigm finding.

judgments.text: Even after adjustment for age, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, gleason_score, and key labs, the treatment_pembrolizumab by msi_high interaction term is not significantly positive.
judgments.rationale: An adjusted analysis showing no significant pembrolizumab-by-MSI interaction would contradict the established tumor-agnostic biomarker indication.

judgments.text: Even after adjustment for age, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, gleason_score, and key labs, the treatment_lu177_psma by psma_high interaction term is not significantly positive.
judgments.rationale: An adjusted analysis showing no significant Lu-177-PSMA-by-PSMA interaction would contradict the established biomarker-driven indication from VISION.

judgments.text: Patients receiving more concurrent treatments have a higher objective_response rate (positive correlation between number of treatments and response rate).
judgments.rationale: A claim that more concurrent treatments correlates with higher response rate is not a guideline-endorsed paradigm and likely reflects confounding rather than established clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving both treatment_enzalutamide and treatment_abiraterone have a different objective_response rate than patients receiving only one of them.
judgments.rationale: A multiplicative interaction between enzalutamide and abiraterone on objective response is not part of standard prostate cancer guideline consensus, which generally treats them as alternative AR-axis agents rather than combination partners with predictable response interaction.

judgments.text: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response only in the complete subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the rate difference is near zero. The unfavorable values mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, and msi_high=1 each independently suppress the enzalutamide treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: The claim that enzalutamide benefit is restricted to a specific four-way subgroup (non-mCRPC AND AR-V7-negative AND BRCA2-wildtype AND MSS) with each unfavorable factor independently suppressing the effect is a speculative cross-pathway conjunction that goes beyond guideline-level statements about AR-V7 resistance.

judgments.text: Within the non-mCRPC stratum, each of ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, and msi_high=1 individually nullifies the otherwise large treatment_enzalutamide benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: While AR-V7 positivity nullifying enzalutamide benefit is established, the assertion that BRCA2 mutation and MSI-high each independently abolish enzalutamide efficacy within non-mCRPC is not part of standard prostate cancer guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide efficacy in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone efficacy in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel efficacy in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in HRR/BRCA-mutant prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177 PSMA efficacy in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established guideline knowledge (VISION trial).

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity (in MSI-high/TMB-high prostate cancer) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have lower objective_response rate than brca2_mutation=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation is generally associated with higher, not lower, response to PARP inhibitors and possibly platinum, so a lower overall response rate would contradict guideline expectations.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have lower objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting poor response to AR-targeted therapies is well-established in prostate cancer literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have lower objective_response rate than msi_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status is typically associated with higher response to immunotherapy, so a lower overall response rate would be a non-guideline, unexpected finding.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than psma_high=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high status predicting differential response (notably to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy) is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age and reduced treatment response/tolerance is a standard prognostic association well-documented in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse response is a foundational prognostic factor in every oncology guideline.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score predicting worse outcomes in prostate cancer is core textbook knowledge and risk-stratification standard.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have lower objective_response rate than mcrpc=0 (hormone-sensitive) patients.
judgments.rationale: mCRPC having worse response than hormone-sensitive prostate cancer is a fundamental disease-state distinction in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have lower objective_response than visceral_mets=0 patients.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases conferring worse prognosis/response is a standard adverse prognostic feature in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml (log scale) is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA correlating with worse outcomes is a long-established prognostic marker in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as an adverse prognostic factor is well-established across oncology and incorporated in standard prognostic models.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic marker is a textbook prostate cancer prognostic factor (e.g., Halabi nomogram).

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reflecting bone disease burden and worse prognosis is standard prostate cancer guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Anemia/low hemoglobin as an adverse prognostic factor in mCRPC is established and included in standard prognostic nomograms.

judgments.text: Routine chemistry labs (ast, alt, total_bilirubin, creatinine, bun, sodium, potassium, calcium) are associated with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Routine chemistry labs as prognostic/response markers are standard clinical considerations and broadly known associations.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a negative prognostic and response marker is well-established in oncology literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio predicting poorer response is a widely recognized prognostic biomarker across cancers.

judgments.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse treatment response is standard clinical knowledge.

judgments.text: In a fully adjusted multivariable logistic model for objective_response, treatment_enzalutamide remains a strong positive predictor; mcrpc, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, ecog_ps, log_psa, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and crp_mg_l remain negative; albumin_g_dl remains positive; the other treatments do not contribute.
judgments.rationale: The specific multivariable adjusted pattern with enzalutamide retaining benefit while other treatments do not contribute, alongside this exact constellation of covariates, is a dataset-specific composite claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated (smaller or absent) in patients with brca2_mutation=1 compared with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Attenuation of enzalutamide benefit specifically in BRCA2-mutant patients is a subgroup-conditional cross-pathway claim not part of standard guideline consensus.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated in patients with ar_v7_positive=1 compared with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to enzalutamide is well-established in mCRPC guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated in patients with msi_high=1 compared with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: Attenuation of enzalutamide response by MSI-high status is a speculative cross-pathway interaction not in standard prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_olaparib is enhanced (positive) in patients with brca2_mutation=1 versus brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA2-mutant prostate/breast/ovarian cancer is established guideline-level knowledge (PROfound trial).

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_pembrolizumab is enhanced in patients with msi_high=1 versus msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-high tumors is FDA-approved tissue-agnostic indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of treatment_lu177_psma is enhanced in patients with psma_high=1 versus psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-high status predicting Lu-177-PSMA response is established and reflected in VISION trial data and current guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated in mcrpc=1 patients compared with mcrpc=0 (hormone-sensitive) patients.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's approval and efficacy spans both hormone-sensitive and castration-resistant prostate cancer, and differential benefit by castration-resistance state is established in guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide differs by visceral_mets status.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a prognostic/predictive modifier for AR-targeted therapy in prostate cancer is well-recognized in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide differs by ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a standard, guideline-recognized modifier of treatment benefit including for enzalutamide.

judgments.text: The objective_response effect of abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, or pembrolizumab is modified by mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ecog_ps.
judgments.rationale: Effect modification of standard prostate cancer therapies by disease state, visceral spread, or performance status is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is attenuated in patients with high psa_ng_ml (log scale).
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA reflecting greater disease burden and attenuated response to AR-axis therapy is a well-established clinical observation.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is modified by albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, or alkaline_phosphatase_u_l.
judgments.rationale: Specific interaction of enzalutamide response with a panel of labs like CRP, LDH, weight loss, and alkaline phosphatase as effect modifiers goes beyond standard guideline content into speculative biomarker territory.

judgments.text: The objective_response benefit of treatment_enzalutamide grows progressively as we restrict to non-mCRPC, then additionally to AR-V7-, then BRCA2 wild-type, then MSI stable. The fully restricted subgroup shows the largest treatment effect.
judgments.rationale: A progressively nested subgroup combining non-mCRPC, AR-V7-negative, BRCA2 wild-type, and MSI-stable as conferring maximal enzalutamide benefit is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Each non-enzalutamide treatment (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) shows a meaningful objective_response benefit in at least one clinically motivated subgroup (e.g., olaparib in BRCA2+/mCRPC, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, lu177 in PSMA-high).
judgments.rationale: Olaparib in BRCA2-mutant mCRPC, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, and Lu-177-PSMA in PSMA-high are all established guideline-endorsed indications.

judgments.text: Across an exhaustive single-feature interaction screen for treatment_enzalutamide, the strongest objective_response effect modifiers (in descending magnitude) are mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, log_psa, and ecog_ps; other features show no meaningful interaction.
judgments.rationale: Ranking a specific ordered set of effect modifiers for enzalutamide response (mcrpc > AR-V7 > BRCA2 > MSI > PSA > ECOG) as the dominant interactions is a speculative cross-pathway claim beyond guideline content.

judgments.text: Within the non-mCRPC subgroup, log_psa no longer modifies the objective_response effect of treatment_enzalutamide; the apparent log_psa interaction in iter 12 was driven by the strong correlation between mcrpc=1 and elevated PSA.
judgments.rationale: Recognizing that PSA elevation tracks with mCRPC status is a basic confounding observation, not a novel biological insight.

judgments.text: Within the non-mCRPC subgroup, the only remaining strong objective_response modifiers of treatment_enzalutamide are ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and msi_high.
judgments.rationale: Claiming AR-V7, BRCA2, and MSI-high are the dominant modifiers of enzalutamide objective response in non-mCRPC patients goes beyond guideline-level knowledge, especially for BRCA2 and MSI.

judgments.text: Each of the four conditions (mcrpc=0, ar_v7_positive=0, brca2_mutation=0, msi_high=0) is necessary for the enzalutamide objective_response effect: removing any one condition (knock-in of an unfavorable marker) collapses the treatment effect to ~0.
judgments.rationale: A conjunctive necessity claim—where any single unfavorable marker collapses the enzalutamide effect to null—is a specific subgroup-conditional mechanistic claim not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0), treatment_enzalutamide is associated with a very large odds-ratio increase in objective_response (OR >> 5). In the complement subgroup, the enzalutamide OR is approximately 1.
judgments.rationale: An OR >> 5 for enzalutamide in a four-way favorable composite subgroup, with OR~1 in the complement, is a speculative subgroup-conditional effect not reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: An exhaustive single-feature interaction screen for each non-enzalutamide treatment yields no replicable objective_response modifier; any p<0.05 hits are small in magnitude, in tiny subgroups, or in directions opposite to clinical expectation, consistent with multiple-testing noise.
judgments.rationale: A null finding from exhaustive interaction screening for other treatments restates standard multiple-testing caution rather than offering novel oncologic insight.

judgments.text: Within the unfavorable subgroup (any of mcrpc=1, brca2_mutation=1, ar_v7_positive=1, or msi_high=1), the conditional ORR (off any treatment) is dominated by mcrpc status, BRCA2 status, AR-V7 status, and MSI status; no treatment improves response.
judgments.rationale: Asserting that within the unfavorable subgroup no treatment improves response and ORR is dominated by molecular markers is a strong cross-treatment claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: In a joint logistic model that simultaneously includes enzalutamide × {mcrpc, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high} interactions and adjusts for log_psa, ecog_ps, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, and psma_high, all four enzalutamide interactions remain large and highly significant.
judgments.rationale: A joint multivariable model showing all four enzalutamide × marker interactions remain large after adjusting for clinical covariates is a specific quantitative claim not in textbooks.

judgments.text: A composite 'favorable' indicator (mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND msi_high=0; ~31% of cohort) interacts with treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response with very large magnitude.
judgments.rationale: A composite four-feature favorable indicator predicting a very large enzalutamide interaction is a non-guideline subgroup construct.

judgments.text: For each non-enzalutamide treatment, an exhaustive search across 22 candidate single- and pairwise-defined subgroups identifies no subgroup with a meaningful (>=5 pp) increase in objective_response on the treatment.
judgments.rationale: A negative exhaustive subgroup search across non-enzalutamide treatments reports absence of effect modifiers, which is a methodological non-finding rather than novel biology.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, adding any of the other treatments (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-PSMA, pembrolizumab) to enzalutamide does not further improve objective_response over enzalutamide alone.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no combination therapy adds benefit over enzalutamide monotherapy in the favorable subgroup contradicts standard combination paradigms (e.g., enzalutamide+olaparib in HRR-altered disease) and is a specific subgroup claim.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, no continuous lab feature meaningfully modifies the enzalutamide objective_response benefit.
judgments.rationale: Guideline-level knowledge does not specify that enzalutamide's objective response benefit is invariant across ECOG, visceral metastases, and PSA tertiles within a favorable subgroup defined by absence of mCRPC, AR-V7, BRCA2, and MSI-high.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the enzalutamide objective_response benefit is consistent across ECOG strata, visceral_mets strata, and PSA tertiles.
judgments.rationale: The claim that no continuous lab feature modifies enzalutamide benefit within the favorable subgroup is a specific subgroup-conditional negative finding not described in standard oncology references.
