judgments.text: Among unfavorable patients, every combination of one or more unfavorable markers (mcrpc, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high) is associated with a near-null enzalutamide objective_response effect (|diff| <= 10 pp), with no combination identifying a residual responsive subgroup.
judgments.rationale: A systematic null effect of enzalutamide across every combination of mCRPC, BRCA2, AR-V7, and MSI-high markers — with no responsive residual subgroup — is a cross-pathway claim not present in guidelines.

judgments.text: Final consolidated objective_response heterogeneity hypothesis: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive objective_response benefit (ORR rises from ~17% off-enzalutamide to ~80% on-enzalutamide; risk-difference +63 pp; OR ~19) confined to the subgroup defined simultaneously by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the treatment effect is null (OR ~1.06, p=0.054). No other treatment in the panel (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab) shows a clinically or statistically meaningful objective_response effect in any feature-defined subgroup tested.
judgments.rationale: A large enzalutamide ORR benefit confined to the simultaneous absence of mCRPC, AR-V7, BRCA2, and MSI-high (with null effects for all other panel agents in any subgroup) is a speculative multi-marker conditional claim well beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide improving response rates in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's effect on response rate in prostate cancer is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel's activity in prostate cancer is a long-standing, guideline-endorsed standard.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib activity in prostate cancer (particularly HRR-mutated) is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA response in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer is established (VISION trial) and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different rate of objective_response than those who do not.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab's role in MSI-H/TMB-high prostate cancer is recognized in guidelines, and its general lack of broad activity is also well-known.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA reflecting greater disease burden and worse outcomes is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Low albumin as a marker of poor performance status and worse outcomes is standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Cachexia/weight loss predicting worse cancer outcomes is a well-established prognostic finding.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an inflammatory marker associated with worse cancer outcomes is well-established prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as a poor prognostic marker associated with worse treatment response is well-established across multiple solid tumors including prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Older age associated with reduced treatment response/tolerance is a standard clinical observation reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: High neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a marker of poor response to systemic therapy is well-documented in oncology literature and increasingly cited in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response is a fundamental, guideline-endorsed prognostic factor.

judgments.text: mcrpc=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Metastatic castration-resistant disease having lower response rates than non-mCRPC is established baseline knowledge in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: brca2_mutation=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutations are typically associated with improved (not lower) response to PARP inhibitors and platinum, so a lower response rate would contradict guideline expectations and warrant scrutiny.

judgments.text: ar_v7_positive=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to AR-targeted therapies is well-established in mCRPC guidelines.

judgments.text: msi_high=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high tumors are typically associated with better response to immunotherapy, so a lower overall response rate in prostate cancer would be a non-guideline, context-dependent finding.

judgments.text: psma_high=1 is associated with a different rate of objective_response than psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression predicting differential response, particularly to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy, is established in current prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: visceral_mets=1 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic feature with lower response rates is a well-established, guideline-recognized factor in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score reflecting more aggressive disease and predicting worse treatment response is established prostate cancer dogma.

judgments.text: After adjustment for ECOG, PSA, mcrpc, brca2, ar_v7, msi_high, albumin, weight loss, CRP, and other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide retains a positive association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A residual positive enzalutamide effect after adjustment for AR-V7, BRCA2, MSI, and disease burden markers is a specific multivariable claim not stated in guidelines.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_abiraterone has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A null effect of abiraterone after full adjustment contradicts the guideline expectation of benefit in mCRPC and is a non-trivial conditional claim.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_docetaxel has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Null effect of docetaxel after adjustment for biomarkers and comorbidity goes beyond textbook claims of chemotherapy benefit.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_olaparib has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A marginal (non-interaction) null for olaparib across the full population is a specific adjusted-model claim, not a guideline statement.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_lu177_psma has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A fully-adjusted null main effect for Lu-177-PSMA is a model-specific claim not found in guidelines, which describe overall benefit in PSMA-positive mCRPC.

judgments.text: After full adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab has no association with objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Null main effect of pembrolizumab after adjustment (including MSI status) is a conditional multivariable claim not in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib increases objective_response specifically in brca2_mutation=1 patients (interaction term positive).
judgments.rationale: PARP inhibitor benefit specifically in BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer is established guideline knowledge (PROfound, olaparib labeling).

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response specifically in msi_high=1 patients (interaction term positive).
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit in MSI-high tumors is the established tissue-agnostic FDA indication and standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response specifically in psma_high=1 patients (interaction term positive).
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA benefit confined to PSMA-high disease is the established VISION-trial paradigm reflected in current guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in ar_v7_positive=1 patients vs ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to enzalutamide is a well-established resistance biomarker documented in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response is also smaller in ar_v7_positive=1 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to abiraterone is established in guidelines alongside the enzalutamide finding from Antonarakis et al.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in mcrpc=1 patients vs mcrpc=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Guidelines actually establish enzalutamide efficacy in mCRPC; a negative interaction with mCRPC status contradicts standard paradigm rather than reflecting it.

judgments.text: The treatment_abiraterone effect on objective_response is similarly modified by mcrpc status (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Similarly, abiraterone is guideline-endorsed in mCRPC, so a negative mCRPC interaction is not a standard known association.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in brca2_mutation=1 patients vs brca2_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status as a modifier of enzalutamide response is not established guideline consensus; BRCA2 is linked to PARP inhibitor response, not AR-targeted therapy resistance.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is smaller (or absent) in msi_high=1 patients vs msi_high=0 patients (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a modifier of enzalutamide response is not standard paradigm; MSI-high prostate cancer is associated with immunotherapy response, not AR-therapy modification.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0; the three-way interaction enzalutamide:mcrpc:ar_v7_positive is positive.
judgments.rationale: A three-way interaction concentrating enzalutamide benefit in non-metastatic AR-V7-negative patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within the mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 subgroup, treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response rate by >50 percentage points relative to no enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: A >50 percentage point absolute response increase in a specific multi-marker subgroup is a speculative magnitude claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Adding brca2_mutation=0 to the (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0) subgroup further increases the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response, while the residual subgroup (brca2_mutation=1) shows essentially no enzalutamide effect.
judgments.rationale: Combining BRCA2 wild-type with mCRPC-negative and AR-V7-negative as an enzalutamide super-responder definition is a novel cross-pathway subgroup hypothesis.

judgments.text: Adding msi_high=0 to the (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0) subgroup yields the cleanest treatment_enzalutamide responder subgroup; objective_response rate on enzalutamide approaches 0.80 and the risk difference exceeds 0.62.
judgments.rationale: A four-marker subgroup yielding ~80% response rate and risk difference >0.62 is a highly specific speculative claim well beyond any guideline statement.

judgments.text: Each of the four predicates {mcrpc=0, brca2_mutation=0, ar_v7_positive=0, msi_high=0} is necessary for the enzalutamide effect: dropping any single predicate produces a stratum where adding back the unfavorable level (e.g., mcrpc=1) collapses the enzalutamide treatment effect to ~zero.
judgments.rationale: Claiming all four specific predicates are individually necessary for enzalutamide benefit is a speculative conjunctive subgroup claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup (mcrpc=0 & brca2=0 & ar_v7=0 & msi=0), visceral_mets does not further modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A guideline would not specify that visceral metastases fails to further modify enzalutamide effect specifically within this four-way favorable subgroup.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, psma_high does not further modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression as a non-modifier of enzalutamide response within a defined favorable stratum is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim absent from guidelines.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, ecog_ps does not meaningfully modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Conditional non-modification by ECOG within this specific multi-criteria favorable subgroup is not a standard guideline statement.

judgments.text: Within the favorable subgroup, gleason_score does not modify the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Gleason score as a non-modifier of enzalutamide effect within this specific favorable subgroup is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml diminishes the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Higher PSA reflecting greater disease burden and reduced response to AR-directed therapy aligns with established prognostic paradigms.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l diminishes the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response (negative interaction).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic factor reducing response to systemic therapy in advanced prostate cancer is well established in guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl enhances the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response (positive interaction).
judgments.rationale: Higher albumin reflecting better performance status and improved treatment outcomes is standard prognostic knowledge.

judgments.text: The negative psa_ng_ml × treatment_enzalutamide interaction is fully explained by membership in the favorable subgroup (mcrpc=0 & brca2=0 & ar_v7=0 & msi=0): within favorable patients PSA does not modulate the enzalutamide effect, and within unfavorable patients PSA also does not modulate the enzalutamide effect.
judgments.rationale: Claiming the PSA×treatment interaction is fully mediated by membership in a specific four-criteria favorable subgroup is a speculative cross-pathway mechanistic claim.

judgments.text: A random-forest T-learner on treatment_enzalutamide identifies a top-CATE quartile of patients with observed enzalutamide effect approaching +0.70 percentage points; the bottom-CATE quartile shows no benefit.
judgments.rationale: A specific causal-forest-derived CATE quartile structure with these effect magnitudes is a data-driven heterogeneity finding not present in guidelines.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_abiraterone is approximately zero, with no genuinely treatment-driven CATE quartile separation (apparent quartile differences track baseline prognosis, not treatment).
judgments.rationale: Null average treatment effect findings for a standard prostate cancer therapy are descriptive negative results, not a novel biological or clinical claim beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_docetaxel is approximately zero with no genuine treatment-driven heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A null ATE for docetaxel is a descriptive statistical negative result, not a novel oncology hypothesis.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_olaparib is approximately zero with no genuine treatment-driven heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A null ATE for olaparib without subgroup conditioning is a descriptive negative result, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_lu177_psma is approximately zero with no genuine treatment-driven heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A null ATE for Lu-177 PSMA is a descriptive negative result, not a novel mechanistic or subgroup-conditional claim.

judgments.text: T-learner ATE for treatment_pembrolizumab is approximately zero with no genuine treatment-driven heterogeneity.
judgments.rationale: A null ATE for pembrolizumab is a descriptive negative result, not a novel oncology hypothesis.

judgments.text: Exhaustive search over all 2-feature binary subgroups (across {mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high}) does not identify any subgroup in which treatment_abiraterone meaningfully (>5 pp) and significantly (p<0.05) raises objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Failure to identify a 2-feature subgroup with abiraterone benefit is a negative analytic finding, not a novel claim that contradicts or extends guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Same exhaustive search yields no subgroup with treatment_docetaxel benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result for docetaxel is descriptive, not a novel mechanistic or cross-pathway hypothesis.

judgments.text: Same exhaustive search yields no subgroup with treatment_olaparib benefit on objective_response, including BRCA2-positive subgroups.
judgments.rationale: Claiming no olaparib benefit even in BRCA2-positive subgroups contradicts established guideline consensus that BRCA-mutant mCRPC responds to PARP inhibition, making it a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Same exhaustive search yields no subgroup with treatment_lu177_psma benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result for Lu-177 PSMA is a descriptive analytic finding, not a novel guideline-extending claim.

judgments.text: Same exhaustive search yields no subgroup with treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Negative subgroup-search result for pembrolizumab (without specifically contradicting MSI-high benefit) is descriptive, not a novel claim.

judgments.text: When the full multivariable logistic model is augmented with one treatment_enzalutamide × feature interaction at a time, the strongest interactions remain mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high (all negative; effect-suppressors). Other features (visceral_mets, psma_high, ecog_ps, gleason_score) show only weak residual interactions after these four are accounted for.
judgments.rationale: Specific multivariable interaction structure identifying mcrpc, AR-V7, BRCA2, and MSI-high as the dominant effect-suppressors of enzalutamide response is a data-driven finding not in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response is concentrated in the subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND msi_high=0; in this favorable subgroup the response rate on enzalutamide is approximately 0.80 vs 0.17 off-treatment (risk difference ~+0.63). Outside this subgroup (any one of mcrpc=1, brca2_mutation=1, ar_v7_positive=1, msi_high=1), enzalutamide produces no meaningful change in objective_response (risk difference <+0.01).
judgments.rationale: A precisely defined four-feature favorable subgroup with ~63% absolute response difference for enzalutamide is a speculative cross-pathway subgroup claim beyond standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: The favorable-subgroup enzalutamide effect on objective_response is robust to adjustment for the other five treatments and the strong prognostic factors (ECOG, PSA, weight loss, CRP, albumin).
judgments.rationale: Robustness of this specific composite favorable-subgroup enzalutamide effect to adjustment for other treatments and prognostic markers is a study-specific multivariable claim, not paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: treatment_olaparib is associated with a numerically lower objective_response rate within brca2_mutation=1 patients (a small negative direction effect, opposite to the mechanistic prior).
judgments.rationale: A negative direction signal for olaparib in BRCA2-mutant patients contradicts the established PARP inhibitor mechanistic paradigm and is therefore non-guideline.

judgments.text: There is no subgroup in which any of {treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab} is convincingly associated with a clinically meaningful change in objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A blanket null claim across abiraterone, docetaxel, Lu177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab contradicts established efficacy of these agents in mCRPC and is a non-standard cross-pathway assertion.

judgments.text: sex_female is uniformly 0 in this prostate cohort and therefore uninformative.
judgments.rationale: Prostate cancer occurring essentially exclusively in males is trivially established and would appear in any textbook.

judgments.text: gleason_score does not predict objective_response in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: While Gleason score's prognostic role is well known, the absence of association with response in a single cohort is a mundane null finding rather than a paradigm-shifting claim — but more importantly Gleason as a non-predictor of treatment response is consistent with guideline framing.

judgments.text: There is a monotonic decrease in objective_response rate with increasing ecog_ps after adjustment for treatment_enzalutamide and other prognostic features.
judgments.rationale: Worse ECOG performance status predicting lower response/outcomes is standard, guideline-level oncology knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is independently associated with lower objective_response after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss as an adverse prognostic factor is established textbook knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l is independently associated with lower objective_response after multivariable adjustment.
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as an adverse prognostic inflammatory marker in advanced cancer is well established in guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: The single best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis in this cohort is: treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response by ~0.63 absolute (from ~0.17 to ~0.80) in patients with mcrpc=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is ~0.
judgments.rationale: A highly conditional four-way subgroup (non-mCRPC, BRCA2 wild-type, AR-V7 negative, MSS) driving a ~0.63 absolute response increase to enzalutamide is a speculative cross-pathway claim not found in prostate cancer guidelines.

judgments.text: No other treatment in the cohort (treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab) shows a credible treatment effect on objective_response, in any tested subgroup.
judgments.rationale: Asserting null treatment effects across docetaxel, abiraterone, olaparib (in BRCA-mutant), Lu-177-PSMA, and pembrolizumab (in MSI-high) contradicts established guideline-supported activity of these agents and is not a standard textbook claim.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's effect on response in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's efficacy in prostate cancer is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel's activity in prostate cancer is a foundational, guideline-endorsed treatment association.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib response in HRR-mutant prostate cancer is established and guideline-endorsed.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA efficacy in PSMA-positive mCRPC is established (VISION trial) and now in guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in MSI-high/TMB-high prostate cancer is known and guideline-recognized, though limited.

judgments.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
judgments.rationale: Differential response between mCRPC and non-mCRPC disease states is a basic, well-established clinical paradigm.

judgments.text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases as a poor prognostic factor affecting response is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status predicting differential response (especially to PARP inhibitors) is established and guideline-recognized.

judgments.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to AR-targeted therapy is established and guideline-acknowledged.

judgments.text: Patients with msi_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with msi_high=0.
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status as a predictor of immunotherapy/treatment response is well-established and appears in standard oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with psma_high=0.
judgments.rationale: High PSMA expression predicting response (e.g., to PSMA-targeted therapy in prostate cancer) is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient in logistic regression).
judgments.rationale: Higher ECOG performance status predicting worse treatment response/outcomes is a textbook prognostic association.

judgments.text: Higher gleason_score is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Higher Gleason score as an adverse prognostic factor in prostate cancer is standard guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Older age_years is associated with a different objective_response rate.
judgments.rationale: Age as a factor in treatment response is a standard demographic covariate considered in oncology practice.

judgments.text: Higher psa_ng_ml (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Higher baseline PSA being associated with worse outcomes in prostate cancer is established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher albumin_g_dl (continuous) is associated with a positive change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Low albumin (poor nutritional status) predicting worse oncologic outcomes is a well-established prognostic marker.

judgments.text: Higher ldh_u_l (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Elevated LDH as an adverse prognostic biomarker is textbook knowledge across many cancers.

judgments.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Cancer cachexia/weight loss predicting worse treatment response and survival is established guideline knowledge.

judgments.text: Higher crp_mg_l (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Elevated CRP as a marker of inflammation predicting worse oncologic outcomes is well-established in the literature and guidelines.

judgments.text: Higher nlr (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Elevated NLR is a well-established negative prognostic/predictive biomarker for response to systemic therapy across many cancers.

judgments.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: A negative association between higher hemoglobin and objective response contradicts the standard expectation that anemia predicts worse outcomes, making this direction non-guideline.

judgments.text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (continuous) is associated with a positive change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: A positive association between elevated alkaline phosphatase (often reflecting bone/liver metastases) and objective response runs counter to the established negative prognostic meaning of high ALP.

judgments.text: Higher ast_u_l (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Elevated AST commonly reflects liver involvement or worse organ function and is conventionally linked to poorer treatment response.

judgments.text: Higher alt_u_l (continuous) is associated with a positive change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: A positive association between higher ALT and objective response is contrary to the usual expectation that hepatic dysfunction predicts worse outcomes.

judgments.text: Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Elevated bilirubin reflects hepatic dysfunction/liver involvement and is a well-known negative predictor of response and tolerability.

judgments.text: Higher creatinine_mg_dl (continuous) is associated with a positive change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: A positive association between higher creatinine and objective response is not part of standard guideline expectations, which generally treat renal dysfunction as neutral-to-negative.

judgments.text: Higher bun_mg_dl (continuous) is associated with a positive change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: A positive association between higher BUN and objective response is not an established guideline-level finding.

judgments.text: Higher sodium_meq_l (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Hyponatremia (lower sodium) is a recognized adverse prognostic marker, so a negative association of higher sodium with response would be contrary—but the established direction is that low sodium predicts worse outcomes, making 'higher sodium = worse response' a non-standard claim.

judgments.text: Higher potassium_meq_l (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Potassium level is not an established predictor of objective response in oncology guidelines, so any directional association is beyond paradigm consensus.

judgments.text: Higher calcium_mg_dl (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
judgments.rationale: Hypocalcemia/calcium as a continuous predictor of objective response in mCRPC is not a guideline-established biomarker.

judgments.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on all 6 treatments, all biomarkers, ECOG, Gleason, age, and all 16 lab covariates, multiple predictors show independent associations with response — most notably treatment_enzalutamide (positive), and mcrpc/brca2_mutation/ar_v7_positive/msi_high/ecog_ps/weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative).
judgments.rationale: Joint multivariable independent associations across labs (e.g., weight loss, calcium) with response, beyond known biomarker-treatment pairs, go beyond guideline-level claims.

judgments.text: Olaparib provides greater benefit in BRCA2-mutated patients (positive interaction on objective_response).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2-mutant prostate cancer benefiting more from olaparib is established guideline knowledge (PROfound).

judgments.text: Pembrolizumab provides greater benefit in MSI-high patients (positive interaction on objective_response).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high tumors responding preferentially to pembrolizumab is a tumor-agnostic guideline-level association.

judgments.text: Lu177-PSMA provides greater benefit in PSMA-high patients (positive interaction on objective_response).
judgments.rationale: PSMA-positive mCRPC benefiting from Lu177-PSMA-617 is established by VISION and reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: AR-V7 positivity reduces or abolishes the benefit of enzalutamide (negative interaction on objective_response).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to enzalutamide is a well-known established association.

judgments.text: AR-V7 positivity reduces or abolishes the benefit of abiraterone (negative interaction on objective_response).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting resistance to abiraterone is a well-known established association.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib increasing response within BRCA2-mutated mCRPC is guideline-established.

judgments.text: Within brca2_mutation=0 patients, treatment_olaparib affects objective_response.
judgments.rationale: A claim that olaparib affects response in BRCA2-wildtype patients goes beyond guideline consensus, which restricts benefit to HRR/BRCA-altered disease.

judgments.text: Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab benefit within MSI-high tumors is guideline-established (tumor-agnostic approval).

judgments.text: Within msi_high=0 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab affects objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab efficacy in MSI-stable patients contradicts the standard guideline tying response to MSI-high/dMMR status.

judgments.text: Within psma_high=1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA is approved/guideline-recommended specifically for PSMA-positive mCRPC, so this is the established indication.

judgments.text: Within psma_high=0 patients, treatment_lu177_psma affects objective_response.
judgments.rationale: PSMA-negative patients are excluded from Lu-177-PSMA labeling; claiming an effect there goes beyond guideline consensus.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity is the canonical marker of resistance to enzalutamide, so claiming increased response contradicts the established paradigm.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide affects objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide activity in AR-V7-negative patients is the standard expected behavior reflected in guidelines.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=1 patients, treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity is canonically associated with abiraterone resistance, so claiming increased response is paradigm-contradicting.

judgments.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_abiraterone affects objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone activity in AR-V7-negative patients aligns with established guideline expectations.

judgments.text: Within visceral_mets=1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel is standard of care for mCRPC including visceral disease, so efficacy here is established.

judgments.text: Within visceral_mets=0 patients, treatment_docetaxel affects objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel activity in non-visceral mCRPC is well-established standard-of-care knowledge.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by mcrpc (treatment-by-mcrpc interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: A negative enzalutamide-by-mCRPC interaction (less benefit in mCRPC vs earlier disease) is a subgroup-conditional quantitative claim not codified in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by ar_v7_positive (treatment-by-ar_v7_positive interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: AR-V7 positivity predicting reduced response to enzalutamide is well-established in prostate cancer guidelines and literature.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by brca2_mutation (treatment-by-brca2_mutation interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: A negative interaction between BRCA2 mutation and enzalutamide response is not standard guideline content; BRCA2 status is typically discussed in the context of PARP inhibitors, not as an effect modifier of enzalutamide.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by msi_high (treatment-by-msi_high interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: MSI-high status modifying enzalutamide response is not established in prostate cancer guidelines, where MSI-high primarily informs immunotherapy eligibility.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by ecog_ps (treatment-by-ecog_ps interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: ECOG performance status is a well-known prognostic and treatment-response modifier across virtually all oncology treatments and would appear in any guideline.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is modified by brca2_mutation (treatment-by-brca2_mutation interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation enhancing abiraterone response is a speculative cross-pathway claim not reflected in current prostate cancer treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is modified by psma_high (treatment-by-psma_high interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: PSMA expression as a negative effect modifier for abiraterone response is not part of standard guideline paradigms, where PSMA primarily guides radioligand therapy selection.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is modified by creatinine_mg_dl (treatment-by-creatinine_mg_dl interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine as a positive effect modifier of abiraterone response is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker, only a safety/dosing parameter.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is modified by bun_mg_dl (treatment-by-bun_mg_dl interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: BUN as a negative effect modifier of abiraterone efficacy is not described in prostate cancer guidelines or textbooks.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is modified by crp_mg_l (treatment-by-crp_mg_l interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: CRP as a positive predictive effect modifier (rather than negative prognostic marker) for abiraterone is a non-standard, speculative claim.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is modified by sodium_meq_l (treatment-by-sodium_meq_l interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: Serum sodium as an effect modifier of docetaxel response is not part of standard oncology paradigm or guideline content.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is modified by alt_u_l (treatment-by-alt_u_l interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: Subgroup-conditional claim that docetaxel response is modified by ALT levels is not a guideline-recognized predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is modified by alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (treatment-by-alkaline_phosphatase_u_l interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Alkaline phosphatase as a positive modifier of docetaxel response is prognostic at best in guidelines, not a recognized predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is modified by total_bilirubin_mg_dl (treatment-by-total_bilirubin_mg_dl interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Total bilirubin modifying docetaxel efficacy (beyond dose-adjustment safety guidance) is not an established predictive interaction in treatment guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response is modified by albumin_g_dl (treatment-by-albumin_g_dl interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Albumin as a treatment-effect modifier (rather than a general prognostic marker) for docetaxel response is not in standard guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is modified by bun_mg_dl (treatment-by-bun_mg_dl interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: BUN modifying olaparib response is not a known or guideline-recognized predictive biomarker interaction.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is modified by brca2_mutation (treatment-by-brca2_mutation interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 mutation status as a predictor of olaparib response is the canonical guideline-established biomarker for PARP inhibitor selection.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is modified by sodium_meq_l (treatment-by-sodium_meq_l interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Serum sodium as a modifier of olaparib efficacy is not a recognized predictive biomarker in oncology guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is modified by potassium_meq_l (treatment-by-potassium_meq_l interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: Serum potassium as a modifier of olaparib efficacy is not an established predictive interaction in guidelines.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is modified by age_years (treatment-by-age_years interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Age-modified olaparib efficacy beyond standard prognostic considerations is not a guideline-recognized predictive interaction.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response is modified by visceral_mets (treatment-by-visceral_mets interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases predicting reduced response to Lu177-PSMA is consistent with established VISION/TheraP-era guideline knowledge on PSMA-radioligand therapy.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response is modified by creatinine_mg_dl (treatment-by-creatinine_mg_dl interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Creatinine as a treatment-effect modifier for Lu-177 PSMA efficacy is not described in mCRPC guidelines, which mention renal function only as a safety/eligibility criterion.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response is modified by gleason_score (treatment-by-gleason_score interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Gleason score modifying Lu-177 PSMA response is not in current PSMA-radioligand guidelines, which use PSMA-PET avidity rather than Gleason for selection.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response is modified by crp_mg_l (treatment-by-crp_mg_l interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: CRP as a negative modifier of Lu-177 PSMA benefit is speculative and not part of guideline-based selection for radioligand therapy.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response is modified by brca2_mutation (treatment-by-brca2_mutation interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: BRCA2 status guides PARP inhibitor use in prostate cancer, not Lu-177 PSMA selection; BRCA2-conditional Lu-177 benefit is a non-guideline cross-pathway claim.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is modified by gleason_score (treatment-by-gleason_score interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Gleason score is not a recognized predictor of pembrolizumab benefit in prostate cancer; ICI selection rests on MSI-H/dMMR/TMB-H, not grade.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is modified by crp_mg_l (treatment-by-crp_mg_l interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: CRP-conditional ICI benefit is a research-level prognostic signal, not a guideline-recognized treatment-effect modifier for pembrolizumab in prostate cancer.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is modified by albumin_g_dl (treatment-by-albumin_g_dl interaction is positive).
judgments.rationale: Baseline albumin as a treatment-effect modifier for pembrolizumab is not in oncology guidelines; albumin appears only as a general prognostic marker.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is modified by ecog_ps (treatment-by-ecog_ps interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: Diminished ICI benefit in poor-PS patients is well-established and reflected in guideline recommendations restricting pembrolizumab to good performance status.

judgments.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is modified by visceral_mets (treatment-by-visceral_mets interaction is negative).
judgments.rationale: Visceral metastases are a general poor-prognostic factor, but a pembrolizumab-specific treatment-effect interaction in prostate cancer is not in current guidelines.

judgments.text: Confirmed in a joint model that for each treatment the top three modifier features from the screen retain significance after mutual adjustment, identifying which treatment-by-feature interactions are robust.
judgments.rationale: A joint-model identification of which treatment-by-feature interactions remain robust after mutual adjustment is a data-driven, multi-modifier finding not covered by guideline consensus.

judgments.text: There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Abiraterone's efficacy across mCRPC and high-risk subgroups is established standard-of-care prostate cancer guideline content.

judgments.text: There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Docetaxel benefit in mCRPC and visceral/high-volume disease is well-established guideline-level knowledge.

judgments.text: There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Olaparib efficacy in BRCA2-mutated prostate cancer is a textbook, guideline-endorsed association.

judgments.text: There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Lu-177-PSMA benefit in PSMA-high mCRPC is established via VISION and is in current guidelines.

judgments.text: There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Pembrolizumab activity in MSI-high tumors is a tumor-agnostic, guideline-level indication.

judgments.text: Within non-mCRPC patients alone, treatment_enzalutamide produces a substantially larger absolute increase in objective_response than in the overall population.
judgments.rationale: A claim that enzalutamide works substantially better specifically in non-mCRPC patients than overall is not standard guideline content.

judgments.text: Within non-mCRPC AND AR-V7-negative patients, treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response by more than +0.50 in absolute terms versus non-treated patients.
judgments.rationale: A specific >+0.50 absolute response increase confined to non-mCRPC AR-V7-negative patients is a speculative subgroup-conditional claim beyond guidelines.

judgments.text: Within non-mCRPC AND AR-V7-negative AND BRCA2-negative AND MSI-low patients (the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup), treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response from approximately 0.17 to approximately 0.80 — an absolute increase of about +0.63.
judgments.rationale: A precise four-way subgroup definition (non-mCRPC, AR-V7−, BRCA2−, MSI-low) with a quantitative ~0.17→0.80 response shift is a speculative cross-pathway claim not in any guideline.

judgments.text: Outside the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup (i.e., patients with mCRPC OR AR-V7+ OR BRCA2+ OR MSI-high), treatment_enzalutamide produces no clinically meaningful improvement in objective_response (absolute difference < 0.01).
judgments.rationale: Asserting near-zero enzalutamide benefit outside this composite subgroup is a strong cross-pathway negative claim beyond established paradigm.

judgments.text: In a joint adjusted logistic model, each of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and msi_high independently and significantly suppresses (negative interaction) the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response, after adjustment for ECOG, age, albumin, LDH, hemoglobin, visceral mets, weight loss, and CRP.
judgments.rationale: Independent negative interactions of mCRPC, AR-V7+, BRCA2+, and MSI-high on enzalutamide response in a joint adjusted model is a mechanistic multi-pathway claim not present in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive treatment effect on objective_response (absolute difference ≈ +0.626; rr_treated ≈ 0.80 vs rr_untreated ≈ 0.17) in the joint subgroup defined by ALL of: mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0. Each of mCRPC, AR-V7+, BRCA2+, and MSI-high independently suppresses the effect; in patients with any of these features, enzalutamide does not increase objective_response.
judgments.rationale: Claims a specific four-way joint subgroup (non-mCRPC AND AR-V7- AND BRCA2- AND MSI-stable) where enzalutamide produces a very large response effect, a cross-pathway conditional claim not found in guidelines.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_abiraterone shows no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response in any clinically defined subgroup tested (mCRPC, non-mCRPC, visceral mets, BRCA2 status, AR-V7 status, MSI status, PSMA status, or ECOG strata). All subgroup absolute differences |diff| < 0.025 with p > 0.05.
judgments.rationale: A null finding for abiraterone across standard subgroups is essentially restating absence of guideline-defined predictive biomarkers and does not propose a novel biological claim.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_docetaxel shows no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response in any clinically defined subgroup tested. All absolute differences |diff| < 0.01 with p > 0.5.
judgments.rationale: A blanket null result for docetaxel across standard subgroups does not advance beyond the established guideline understanding that docetaxel lacks a validated predictive biomarker.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_olaparib shows no positive effect on objective_response in any tested subgroup, including the canonical BRCA2-mutated subgroup, where the direction is actually slightly NEGATIVE (rr_treated 0.121 vs rr_untreated 0.153, diff -0.032, p≈0.06). Hence the canonical olaparib-BRCA2 effect is NOT supported in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Asserts that the canonical olaparib × BRCA2 benefit is absent and even directionally negative, directly contradicting established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_lu177_psma shows no positive effect on objective_response in any tested subgroup, including PSMA-high patients (diff = -0.001, p=0.88). In the visceral mets subgroup, lu177_psma shows a small NEGATIVE signal (diff = -0.024, p=0.04). The canonical Lu177-PSMA × PSMA-high benefit is NOT supported.
judgments.rationale: Claims the canonical Lu177-PSMA × PSMA-high benefit is absent (with a negative signal in visceral mets), contradicting established guideline consensus.

judgments.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab shows no significant effect on objective_response in any tested subgroup, including MSI-high patients (rr_treated 0.177 vs rr_untreated 0.176, diff +0.001, p=1.0). The canonical pembrolizumab × MSI-high benefit is NOT supported in this cohort.
judgments.rationale: Claims the canonical pembrolizumab × MSI-high benefit is absent, directly contradicting a tumor-agnostic guideline-endorsed association.

judgments.rationale: Enzalutamide's efficacy in prostate cancer is established standard-of-care knowledge in guidelines.

judgments.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response specifically in msi_high=1 patients (positive interaction).

judgments.text: ar_v7_positive=1 patients have a different overall objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0.

judgments.text: No single-feature subgroup shows a statistically significant ORR benefit for treatment_olaparib.
